details.routine: (two actions) The haunt uses one action to activate its gurgling gasp and then uses telekinetic interference for all of its other actions until it can use gurgling gasp again.

description.value:
Trigger Combat begins

On a failure, the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure). On a success, a creature is temporarily immune to gurgling gasp for 1 hour. The haunt cannot use gurgling gasp again for [[/gmr 1d6 #Recharge Gurgling Gasp]]{1d6 rounds}.
The haunt attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Trip] a living creature within 60 feet with a bonus of [[/r 1d20+8]]{+8}.

The triggering creature is pushed into an adjoining room, takes @Damage[1d8[bludgeoning]] damage, and lands @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. A creature who succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:19|traits:damaging-effect] save takes no damage and rolls out of the way of the trap, returning to the space they were in before stepping on the pressure plate.

details.description: A fire engulfs a @Template[square|distance:10]{10-foot-by-10-foot} section of forest, then spreads on each of its turns.
details.disable:
A successful @Check[athletics|dc:13|name:Smother Fire|traits:hazard,environmental,fire], @Check[nature|dc:13|name:Smother Fire|traits:hazard,environmental,fire], or @Check[survival|dc:13|name:Smother Fire|traits:hazard,environmental,fire] check from an adjacent square is sufficient to smother one 5-foot-square of fire; each attempt is an Interact action. Dousing the flames automatically extinguishes one or more sections of fire, with no check. Water typically clears a 5-foot square if the amount is small (such as that from create water or hydraulic push). Larger amounts of water, such as a full bucket, typically douse a 10-foot-by-10-foot area (or 4 squares in some other shape). Throwing a bucket of water on flames requires an Interact action. A waterskin doesn't contain enough water to put out even 1 square of fire.

Cold can also put out fire, but only if the cold can affect an area; cold is usually less effective than water, so a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Frost Vial (Lesser)]{Frost Vial} typically puts out only 1 square of fire, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Ray of Frost] is ineffective.

details.description:
The malevolence manifests a disturbing illusion meant to unsettle and erode intruders' resistances to the manor's dangers. Roll [[/r 1d12]] on the Xarwin Manor Phantasms table to determine what sort of illusion occurs; alternatively, pick an appropriate one from the table or create one of your own using the listed results as examples or inspiration.

Xarwin Manor Phantasms

[[/r 1d12]] Phantasm
1 A damp, ectoplasmic handprint appears on a window or wall, only to reach out of the surface briefly to try to choke a character. The hand vanishes a moment later, but the PC's left hand feels weirdly damp until it's wiped off.
2 A painless, bloodless incision manifests across a PC's forehead, completely encircling their skull over a few seconds; when the incision completes its circuit, the PC experiences a momentarily excruciating headache before the incision vanishes.
3 The PC catches a glimpse of a shadowy figure in a reflective surface; the figure raises an arm as if to point at them, yet the arm has no hand. When the PC turns to confront the image or blinks, the figure is gone.
4 The PC stumbles then notices that loose objects in the room shake, curtains swing, and floorboards shift. If a window is visible, the trees outside sway even though it's a windless day. As everything grows still, the PC gets the feeling that something immense just shifted somewhere down below. No other PC experiences this sensation.
5 The PC feels a sudden pain on their left wrist and is convinced that their hand has been cut off. Upon looking at the suspected wound, the pain vanishes and the hand looks fine.
6 The sky through a window appears to be fully night during the day, or the ceiling above suddenly fades away to reveal the night sky. The PC feels something looking back at them from somewhere between the stars, but as soon as they blink, look away, or draw attention to the night sky, the image reverts to normal.
7 The PC is suddenly overcome with the conviction that they've lived in this manor for decades and that the other PCs are trespassers, only for the feeling to vanish moments later.
8 The PC suddenly feels an overwhelming sense of betrayal from one of the other PCs-preferably the one with which they have the closest emotional ties. A heartbeat later, the feeling fades away.
9 The PC can hear what sounds like a small bird trapped in a wall, struggling for escape; no other PC can hear it. A few moments later, the PC feels that they're the trapped bird, and then the phantasm fades away.
10 The PC suddenly loses all senses and feels weightless for a split second. In the darkness, they get the creeping thought that something is about to give them the gift of sight, but they know that what they'll see will be awful. Then, everything returns to normal.
11 The PC is, for a few frightening moments, suddenly convinced that someone else is thinking for them and that they've been tricked into thinking they're in their own body when they're not.
12 The PC suddenly remembers an awful memory from their past or childhood, then feels a sense of relief as that memory is pulled from their minds. They can remember that something bad happened but not what it was.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:15|name:Exorcise Phantasm] or @Check[religion|dc:15|name:Exorcise Phantasm] (to exorcise the disquieting phantasm)

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure If the creature doesn't have the malevolence condition, the value of its malevolence condition increases by 1.

Critical Failure The creature increases its malevolence condition by 1.

The triggering creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until the trap is disabled or a creature succeeds at a [[/act force-open dc=12]]{Athletics} check to Force it Open. A creature who critically fails the check to Force it Open instead tightens the trap, dealing @Damage[(1d6+3)[piercing]] damage to the immobilized creature.

details.description: Gray mold covers a 10-foot by 25-foot section of the deck.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:13|name:Neutralize Moldy Deck|traits:poison,environmental,mechanical] to remove or neutralize the mold without disturbing it.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the mold's space or damages the mold. The mold can't use this reaction if the damage was fire or acid damage

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within the gray mold's area are exposed to gray mold.

Any clumsy condition from the gray mold persists after the poison's duration ends.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:15]

Maximum Duration 3 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d4[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[1d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} (1 round)

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 5-foot square surrounds an invisible rune on the wall or floor.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:15|name:Approach the Rune] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:16|name:Erase the Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank, counteract DC 15) to counteract the rune.
details.reset: Like its namesake creature, a phoenix rune is difficult to destroy. After it is triggered, a phoenix rune can immediately reset on a wall or floor within 120 feet, as long as it has not been disabled. When this happens, a PC who succeeds at a @Check[perception|dc:13|name:Spot the Phoenix Rune] check spots the shadowy form of a tiny phoenix "flying" along the wall or floor from the rune's old location to its new location.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the sensor area or fails any check to disable the phoenix rune.

Effect A white-hot talon of flame bursts upward from the phoenix rune, dealing @Damage[1d8[fire]] damage to a creature within the sensor area. This gout of fire is accompanied by the screech of a phoenix audible up to 500 feet away. Creatures within a 20-foot radius of the sensor area (including the triggering creature) must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:13|name:Screeching Talon Deafened|traits:auditory] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 round.

details.description: Loose handholds rip away from the walls to drop unsuspecting climbers into the chasm below.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:16] (trained) or @Check[thievery|dc:16] to anchor the climbing loops more securely

description.value:
Trigger A creature climbs across the chasm without knowing which of the loops are rigged

Effect The loops rip free, dropping the climber 20 feet into the chasm below and dealing @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage. The falling creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling if it succeeds at a @Check[flat|dc:11]; failure indicates that the edge grabbed rips away as well.

details.description: The PCs appear on a gigantic wall of hooked chains dangling in a gray swirling mist. The chains radiate life-chilling energy Cries of innocence and condemnation echo through the mist in Elven. A plane of light 10 feet above eddies and swirls as it dully reflects the PCs below, but the reflections warp and alter the PCs to have sallow complexions like the undead.
details.disable: A PC that has ascended to the plane of light (@Check[athletics|dc:13] check to Climb; on a critical failure, the PC falls 10 feet before being seized by a chain) can attempt a @Check[thievery|dc:18] check to disrupt the plane or a @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[occultism|dc:18] or @Check[religion|dc:18] check to ritually disrupt the haunting. Each success reduces the haunt's actions by one. When the haunt has no more actions, it is disabled and the PCs all return to area B3.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-08: The Haunted Corridor

details.reset: The haunt resets the next time the Soulshackle traps creatures in the library.
details.routine: (three actions) The chains rattle and sigh ominously around two different random creatures trapped in the haunt, dealing @Damage[(1d6+2)[void]] damage (@Check[will|dc:16|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger Hunted by Chains is disabled

Effect The chains rattle ominously, dealing @Damage[(1d6+2)[void]] damage to all creatures in the haunt (@Check[will|dc:16|basic] save). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: An assortment of leaves and loose dirt cover a sudden drop in elevation.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:12|name:Uncover Sinkhole|traits:environmental] to remove the covering foliage

publication.title: Pathfinder Bounty #2: Blood of the Beautiful

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the sinkhole

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes falling damage (@Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage). That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

details.description: The branches of this peculiar bush are heavy with ripening berries.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:16] (trained) to avoid gathering unripe berries.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-13: Within the Prairies

description.value:
Trigger A creature gathers unripe berries from the bush.

Effect A nearby branch warps into a viper's head and Strikes at the creature.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:16]

Maximum duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[(1d6+2)[poison]] damage (1 round)

details.description: Loose snow and ice have built up on a high surface, such as a tree branch or a rooftop. Its grip on the surface is tenuous, and it is likely to fall if the surface moves.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:19|name:Safely Dislodge the Snow] (trained) to safely dislodge the snow, or deal any amount of fire damage to destroy the hazard without triggering it

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves beneath where the snowfall is resting.

Effect Ice and snow fall on the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[(2d6+3)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save) and soaking their clothing. Until they change into fresh clothing or spend at least an hour in an area of normal or higher temperature, they treat cold environments as one step colder (for example, mild cold as severe cold).

details.description: The floorboards here are rotting and soft, giving way and dropping anyone above into the hole below.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:16|name:Reinforce Compromised Floorboards] (trained) to reinforce the compromised floorboards.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-03: Escaping the Grave

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto any square containing the sodden floorboards.

Effect All squares with floorboards give way. Each creature in any of the marked squares must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save.

Success The creature moves to the nearest safe square and avoids falling in.

Failure The creature falls 10 feet, takes 5 bludgeoning damage as a result of the fall, and is prone in the hole.

details.description: Several spice bombs disguised as rocks litter the ground.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:12] to remove the bombs without activating them or @Check[survival|dc:13] (trained) to render them inert.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on a spice bomb

Effect The spice bombs explode into clouds of spicy, choking gas that envelops the area. All creatures within 30 feet must succeed a @Check[fortitude|dc:14] save or take @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage. On a critical failure, the creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

details.description: A jagged spike of metal shoots from a hidden launcher.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:16|name:Disable Launcher|traits:mechanical,trap] to disable one of the four launchers or @Check[acrobatics|dc:12|name:Avoid Trip Line|traits:mechanical,trap] to step over a trip line (this doesn't disarm the trap, but avoids triggering it)

description.value:
Trigger A creature crosses one of the trip lines

Effect A spike launches along the trip line's path against the triggering creature, making a ranged Strike.

details.description: All lights in the room snap on, just as Lyari remembers the day of her death. A susurrus of accusations and a guilty verdict fill the air. Ghostly elven warriors disappear and reappear in a disorienting and implacable advance.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:18] to stand against the force of the verdict, or @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[occultism|dc:18], or @Check[religion|dc:18] to ritually disrupt the haunting. Each success reduces the haunt's actions by one When the haunt has no more actions, it is disabled and the Hunted by Chains haunt triggers.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-08: The Haunted Corridor

details.reset: The haunt resets the next time the Soulshackle traps creatures in the library.
details.routine: (two actions) The haunt makes vengeful judgment Strikes against three different random creatures trapped in this haunt. A spectral pool of blood forms beneath any creature knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] by this haunt.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs all enter the room

Effect Accusations of crimes against the crown are whispered throughout the room in Elven. Each living creature in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:16] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}. The haunt then rolls initiative.
Plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] on a critical hit

details.description: The rickety stairs groan and strain alarmingly if a Small or larger creature ascends them.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:16] to quickly jury-rig some supports to prevent the stairs from collapsing

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature attempts to climb the stairs;

Effect The stairs attempt a @Check[flat|dc:8] (if a Small creature triggered the hazard) or a @Check[flat|dc:16] (if a Medium or multiple Small creatures triggered it). On a failure, the stairs collapse. All creatures on the stairs fall and take @Damage[(2d6 + 3)[bludgeoning]] damage from the collapsing rubble (@Check[reflex|dc:16|basic]).

details.description: Plaster crumbles from this column moments before it collapses.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:16] (trained) to stabilize the column, @Check[athletics|dc:19] to push the column back into place

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the tiles adjacent to the column

Effect The column collapses. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:16|basic] save as debris falls on them, which deals @Damage[(2d6+3)[bludgeoning]] damage. The area adjacent to the collapsed column becomes difficult terrain.

details.description: The ice here is fractured due to the warming weather conditions and the inexorable flow of the ocean water below.
details.disable: 2 @Check[athletics|dc:19|name:Move Ice|traits:environmental] to move the ice sheets into a more stable position

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps onto the unstable ice

Effect The weight and momentum of the creatures causes the fractured ice to shatter and separate, leaving them stranded on a floating iceberg.

details.description: This open pit was once covered with branches- it's now visible, but its unstable edges are not obvious.

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 5 feet of the pit's edge

Effect The edge of the pit crumbles. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:19] save.

Critical Success The triggering creature steps back from the edge before it collapses.

Success The triggering creature falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Failure The triggering creature falls prone and slides into the pit, taking @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]] damage.

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 10-foot radius surrounds the central column of the room.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:15|name:Approach the Rune|traits:magical,trap] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Erase the Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device,magical,trap] (trained) to erase the rune, or @Check[athletics|dc:19|name:Remove Stones|traits:magical,trap] to remove enough stones from the pillar to disrupt the magic, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 15) to counteract the rune.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Intro #1: The Second Confirmation

details.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors

Effect The trap summons one of the creatures previously encountered in area B with the weak adjustment. The creature rolls initiative and remains for 7 rounds, after which the spell ends and the creature disappears. The summoned creature also disappears if a creature disables the trap before the duration expires. The summoned creature can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: Many small pits have been dug in the road and covered with leaves and dirt to disguise them.
details.disable: If spotted, the pits can be avoided by moving at one quarter of normal speed.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-04: Bandits of Immenwood

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into the affected area

Effect Any creature that walks through the affected area must attempt a @Check[acrobatics|dc:14|name:Ankle Trap|traits:damaging-effect,hazard,mechanical,trap] check. Creatures that fail this check take @Damage[1d4[bludgeoning]] damage and a –5-foot penalty to their Speed for 1 hour. A critical failure increases the damage to @Damage[2d4[bludgeoning]] damage and reduces the creature's Speed by half for 1 hour.

details.description: Many small pits have been dug in the road and covered with leaves and dirt to disguise them.
details.disable: If spotted, the pits can be avoided by moving at one quarter of normal speed.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-04: Bandits of Immenwood

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into the affected area

Effect Any creature that walks through the affected area must attempt a @Check[acrobatics|dc:14|name:Ankle Trap|traits:damaging-effect,hazard,mechanical,trap] check. Creatures that fail this check take @Damage[1d8[bludgeoning]] damage and a –5-foot penalty to their Speed for 1 hour. A critical failure increases the damage to @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning]] damage and reduces the creature's Speed by half for 1 hour.

stealth.details: (trained) to hear a child imitate an animal's roar

details.description: A scruffy stuffed bear animates, biting whoever disturbs it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Handle the Toy] (trained) to handle the toy without disturbing it or @Check[occultism|dc:21|name:Suppress the Memories] (trained) to suppress the memories suffusing the toy
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the toy

Effect The toy animates for a split second, biting the triggering creature with a ferocious jaws Strike.

details.disable: Slay Silaqui, or @Check[thievery|dc:17|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to pry the gem out of Silaqui followed by disrupting the gem's power by either breaking it (AC 15, Hardness 5, HP 25, BT 13) or using @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 15)

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-06: The Crashing Wave

details.routine:
All creatures within 40 feet of the vortex except Silaqui are drawn toward it. They must attempt either a @Check[fortitude|dc:14] save or an @Check[athletics|dc:14|traits:action:swim] check to Swim with a DC of 14, with the following results.

Success The creature is not moved.

Failure The creature is moved 10 feet closer to the center of the vortex. If this causes the creature to reach the center square of the vortex, they are exposed to Consuming Maelstrom.

Critical Failure As failure, except the creature is moved 20 feet closer to the center of the vortex.

description.value:
Trigger Silaqui rolls initiative

Effect The ritual vortex begins to draw in nearby creatures. The trap rolls initiative.

A creature that is pulled into the center square of the vortex (or that chooses to enter it) must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:17] save.

Critical Success The creature is ejected safely to a square of their choice within 10 feet of the vortex.

Success The creature is ejected safely to a square adjacent to the vortex.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(1d6+3)[electricity]] damage and is moved upward 40 feet.

Critical Failure As failure. Additionally, the creature is exposed the raw nature of the sea devil's frenzy and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round (@Check[will|dc:17|traits:mental] negates). This is a mental effect.

details.description: A net with soil and aquatic plants on it covers a 10-foot pit dug into the bottom of a body of water.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|name:Remove the Net|traits:mechanical,trap] to remove the net

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-20: The Lost Legend

details.reset: Others can fall into the pit, but the net entangles only the first creature to trigger the trap. The net must be reset manually.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks on the net.

Effect The triggering creature falls into a spiked pit and submerges, with the net collapsing to entangle the creature. Spikes in the pit deal @Damage[2d6[piercing]] damage. The net imposes a –10-foot penalty to the creature's speed. A creature must succeed at two DC 15 checks to Escape from the net; a critical success to Escape frees them immediately. A creature that can't breathe underwater may begin drowning. Any round the creature struggles to escape the net, the creature uses 2 rounds of air.

details.description: A sickly-sweet perfume wafts through the air outside the council building, assaulting the senses of all that inhale it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:17] to hold your breath through the perfume, @Check[occultism|dc:16] (trained) to draw a warding rune in the air to disperse the poison, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 15) to counteract the poison

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-12: Negotiations for the Star Gun

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the doors of the Segada council building

Effect All creatures within 10 feet are exposed to the butterfly's breath poison.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:18]

Onset 10 minutes

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (6 hours)

Stage 2 stupefied 1 (12 hours)

Stage 3 stupefied 1 (24 hours)

details.description: A massive swarm of bats flies throughout the room, hampering vision, attacking foes, and magically restoring Camazotz's followers.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:17] (trained) to disperse the swarm or @Check[religion|dc:15] (trained) to disrupt Camazotz's connection, which disperses the swarm
details.reset: The swarm disperses and returns to roost throughout the room 1 minute after all enemies leave the room. The swarm recovers 5 Hit Points per day thanks the power of Camazotz.
details.routine: (2 actions) The swarm uses its first action to move up to 30 feet to envelop as many enemies as possible within its @Template[type:burst|distance:30] space. All enemies in the swarm's space become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] while within the swarm. It then uses its second action to bite all enemies, dealing @Damage[(1d6+3)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic|options:area-effect] save) to all enemies in the space.

description.value:
Trigger A creature that's not a worshipper of Camazotz enters the room

Effect The hundreds of bats descend down into the room in a terrifying, shrieking mass. The swarm occupies a @Template[type:burst|distance:30] on the floor of the room. All enemies within this area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:17] save. On a failure, a creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The swarm then rolls initiative.

Requirements An ally is in the swarm's space

Trigger The swarm dealt damage to an enemy

Effect The swarm takes some of the blood it drank from an enemy and feeds it to an ally in its space. The ally restores Hit Points equal to the highest damage the swarm dealt to an enemy this turn.

details.description: The carving weeps. Its tears turn to a mist that covers the halls.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:17] (trained) to recite prayers for the dead, or @Check[dwarven-lore|dc:14] to speak the history of Raseri Kanton, or @Check[thievery|dc:20] to plug the holes in the carving

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature looks at the carved mourner

Effect The hallways fill with a thick mist. When the mist first appears, all living creatures in area A take @Damage[1d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic] save), and all undead creatures regain @Damage[1d6[healing]] Hit Points. All creatures within the mist become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed]. This mist lasts for 1 hour or until the haunt is disabled.

details.disable: A hero must succeed at a @Check[thievery|dc:18|traits:hazard|name:Disable Mechanism] check on the triggering floor tiles or the wall sockets in the western wall.
details.reset: The central spears reset after 1 minute.

description.value: When a creature moves into any of the squares in the outlined area, the trap shoots three spears from the western wall. It makes one ranged Strike against the westernmost creature in each row.

details.description: The frame splinters as the door flings open, the force hitting those nearby.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:17|name:Hold the Door Shut|traits:chaotic,hazard,magical,trap] (trained) to hold the door shut against the force, or @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Relieve Door Pressure|traits:chaotic,hazard,magical,trap] (trained) to deftly relieve pressure from the door.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-09: The Seven Secrets of Dacilane Academy

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches any part of the door.

Effect The door flies open with incredible force. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:17|traits:damaging-effect,chaotic,hazard,trap,magical] save as the door explodes outward into them, which deals @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is knocked 10 feet toward the window behind them.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is knocked 20 feet toward the window. The window shatters and the PC falls 5 feet before colliding with the ground, taking an additional @Damage[1d6[piercing]] damage from passing through the broken glass.

details.description: The equilibrium of the brown mold and lava river is disturbed, creating bubbles of molten stone that burst and spew burning debris.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:14|name:Clear mold] or @Check[survival|dc:14|name:Clear mold] or @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Clear mold] to clear your square and all adjacent squares of the mold; the churning lava does not target creatures in cleared squares with its spatter attack, but the hazard is not destroyed until every square has been cleared of mold.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-19: Enter the Pallid Peak

details.reset: If not completely destroyed, the mold spreads to one additional square within 10 feet of lava each day until it fully regenerates and regains its state of equilibrium. If every square is cleaned of mold, the hazard is destroyed.
details.routine: (1 action) On the its initiative, the churning lava targets a random creature in the area with a ranged spatter attack as a magma bubble bursts.

description.value:
Trigger A creature disturbs the mold, typically by walking through it

Effect The hazard makes a spatter attack against the triggering creature, and then rolls initiative as the lava begins to bubble.

The target must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:17] save as the lava rapidly cools and solidifies. On a critical hit, use the outcome for one degree of success worse than the result of the save.

Success The lava slides off the target with no additional effect.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1}. The target or an adjacent creature can take an Interact action to remove the stone, removing the condition.

Critical Failure As failure, except the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2}. Each Interact action to remove the stone reduces the value of the clumsy condition by 1.

details.description: The temperature suddenly drops as spectral forces gather in the area.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:19|name:Banish the Cold Spot] (trained) to banish the cold spot with ritual prayers or @Check[occultism|dc:20|name:Foil the Arrival of Outside Forces] (trained) to foil the arrival of outside forces
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the area

Effect The temperature drops as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Invisible] spirits gather around the living. Living creatures within 15 feet take @Damage[(2d4+5)[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic]). Creatures that critically fail their save are additionally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1}.

details.description:
Fluids from leaking beakers have eaten nearly through the supports holding up this cabinet.

This Broken Threshold for this trap is 5, not 10.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|name:Support Cabinet|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) to gently place something under the cabinet to support its weight.

description.value:
Trigger Someone touches or otherwise interacts with the cabinet

Effect The cabinet falls, splintering on impact. If someone attempts to break the cabinet but its Broken Threshold is not reached in one hit, it falls as well. The cabinet deals @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone beneath (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|traits:mechanical,trap] save).

details.description: The damaged ceiling is the only thing stopping the centipedes lairing above from falling through.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:19] (trained) to reinforce the ceiling with boards from the broken door.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-03: Escaping the Grave

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into the area beneath the centipede lair.

Effect Hearing potential prey, the centipedes begin scrabbling above, causing the ceiling to collapse and centipedes to rain down, dealing @Damage[(2d6+3)[bludgeoning]] damage to each creature in the area; each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:16|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the instability in the surrounding woodwork and precariously balanced masonry.

details.description: The room initially appears merely dilapidated, but on closer inspection it is positively liable to collapse at any moment.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:20] (untrained) to safely shore up the weaknesses in the masonry by replacing rotten wood with other assorted bric-a-brac, @Check[thievery|dc:17] (trained) to carefully place boards and the like to create bridges over structurally weak areas.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-16: A Lie Told to Strangers

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the hazardous area

Effect Bricks and broken wood fall on the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[(2d6+5)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic] save).

details.description: A section of splintery and rotten floorboards drop a creature 6 feet onto uneven rocks and piercing shards of wood.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:15] (trained) to replace or cover the weak sections of flooring, or @Check[survival|dc:15] (trained) to crumble the rotten floorboards from a safe distance
details.reset: Once this section of the porch has collapsed, creatures can still fall through the hole until the porch is Repaired.

description.value: If a creature walks on the area marked on the map, it falls through the weakened floorboards onto uneven rocks and sharp shards of wood, taking @Damage[1d4[piercing],1d6[bludgeoning]]{1d4 piercing damage and 1d6 bludgeoning damage}. The creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid the fall and the damage.

details.description: Skillet sputter and pots threaten to boil over, splashing their contents onto careless cooks or kitchen counters. The counters are covered with small dishes ready to add, along with 3 heavy cookbooks and a healthy coating of flour.
details.disable: @Check[cooking-lore|dc:14] (trained) or @Check[crafting|dc:17] to add the proper ingredients to finish the dish. @Check[diplomacy|dc:17] or @Check[occultism|dc:17] to communicate with students when to stir or adjust the heat on a dish. Each disable attempt requires two actions, and three successful checks are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-18: Dacilane Academy's Delightful Disaster

details.reset: The trap deactivates after 1 minute, destroying all of the food. If left untouched, it resets automatically in 24 hours.
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap uses each of its actions to make a splatter attack against a random creature in the kitchen. The trap never targets a student, as they are out of phase with it. For every successful disable attempt on the trap, it loses an action.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the room

Effect The hazard rolls initiative.

Trigger A creature fails an attempt to Disarm the trap

Effect The incorrect execution of the recipe releases a nauseating odor. The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:17] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

details.description: The thick green slime has been imbued with foul hag magic, making it cling to and grow over living creatures that touch it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17] (trained) to carefully push the slime away without causing it to grow, @Check[crafting|dc:17] (one action) to neutralize it with alchemical reagents, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 15 [DC 18 in Subtier 3-4]) to counteract the magic animating it. Casting a 1st-rank or higher spell with the water trait washes away the slime.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-22: Doom of Cassomir

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the slime covering the steps and upper landing.

Effect The slime rapidly climbs across the creature's body, grows cold, and congeals. Creatures assaulted by the slime take @Damage[(1d8+5)[cold]] damage(@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic] save); creatures that fail their save are also coated in slime, imposing a -5 foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds. The slime can't be easily cleaned away, and it requires ten minutes of bathing with clean water and soap to remove the slime.

details.description: A series of hidden launch tubes fire toxic darts at a 5-foot square.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:16] (trained)

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is applied to the floor tile

Effect The trap shoots 4 darts as a single attack.
Plus @Damage[1d10[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:16] negates the poison damage)

details.description: A large tree has been cut down and supported only by a rope.
details.disable: The trap cannot be disabled in advance.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-04: Bandits of Immenwood

description.value:
Trigger The squirrel swam finishes chewing through the rope at the beginning of its turn

Effect The tree drops 10 feet in front of the wagon, blocking the road. The tree deals @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone beneath the tree when it drops and pushes them out of its space in a random direction. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:18|traits:damaging-effect,hazard,mechanical,trap] save takes no damage and rolls out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, it can choose the direction. Mounted creatures do not attempt a save unless their mount attempts a save and fails. If a mounted creature's mount fails the save, the mounted creature can attempt its own @Check[reflex|dc:18|name:Mounted Deadfall|traits:damaging-effect,hazard,mechanical,trap] save by leaping off its mount, taking @Damage[1d4[bludgeoning]] damage from the fall. This damage is taken whether the save is successful or not.

details.description: Magical sensors detect when a creature enters the chamber.
details.disable:
@Check[diplomacy|dc:18|name:Convince the Spirit|traits:complex,haunt] to convince the spirit to calm the winds, @Check[athletics|dc:16|name:Raise Wooden Bench|traits:complex,haunt] to raise up a wooden bench to block the wind, or @Check[religion|dc:16|name:Speak a Rite to Calm Spirit|traits:complex,haunt] to speak an appropriate rite to calm the spirit.

Two successful checks are required to fully disable the haunt, and each attempt takes two actions. Each successful check removes one action from the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-02: The East Hill Haunting

details.reset: The storm continues raging for 1 minute unless disabled, and then dissipates, returning after 1 week.
details.routine: (2 actions) The haunt uses its first action to stir up a storm of debris against all creatures in the quadrant clockwise from where it last attacked. Creatures in the quadrant take @Damage[2d4[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:15|basic] save). For each additional action it has (at most 1 without adjustments from the scaling sidebar), the storm targets an additional quadrant, moving clockwise around the area. On the first round, it begins by attacking creatures in the northwestern quadrant.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the marked area of the haunt

Effect A ghostly figure manifests and conjures forth a telekinetic storm of debris from the area and then quickly disappears. The haunt rolls initiative.

details.description: A demonic presence animates the ship carrying demon pirates.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:17] (trained) to ward against demonic presence, @Check[arcana] (trained) or @Check[occultism|dc:17] (trained) to weaken the demonic possession, @Check[intimidation|dc:17] (trained) to scare it away, or @Check[sailing-lore|dc:14] (trained) to confuse it; two total successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.routine:
2 The demon launch uses its actions on one of the following options (GM's choice). It loses one action from its routine if a PC succeeds at an attempt to Disable it or it reaches its Broken Threshold.

  • Broadside Blast a The demon launch makes a Chthonian cannon Strike.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Sea Chantey]

Trigger The demon launch hits another ship and Encounter A begins

Each PC must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:17] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] ([[/act escape dc=17]]); on a critical failure, they're also impaled and take @Damage[(1d6+3)[piercing]] damage. The demon launch then rolls initiative.

All fiends within 60 feet gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and saves against fear effects; all non-fiends within 60 feet must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:17] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Sea Chantey]

details.description: A statuette of the deceased demon lord creates frightening illusions of insect swarms.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:17] (expert) or @Check[religion|dc:17] (trained) to deactivate the effigy or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 20) to dispel the statuette
details.reset: 1 minute as the illusory insects harass intruders before scuttling into the earth.

description.value:

Effect A swarm of illusionary insects bursts out of the grass in a @Template[cone|distance:60] from the Deskari effigy and seemingly crawl over the intruders, shrieking incessantly. All creatures in the area must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:16] save or become temporarily @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 minute (deafened and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute on a critical failure).

description.value:

details.description: Illusions cloaking this chamber cause its walls and all its contents to appear tilted, irregular, and in motion.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:17|name:Calculate the Room's Dimensions] (trained) to calculate the room's dimensions despite the illusions, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 17) to counteract the illusions
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the room

Effect Illusions cause the appearance of the room and the objects inside it to constantly warp, shift, bend, and distort for 1 minute. Each creature in the room while it's distorting must attempt a @Check[perception|dc:17] check.

Critical Success The creature disbelieves the illusions and is unaffected.

Success The creature disbelieves the illusions but still sees them. It treats the room as difficult terrain.

Failure The creature is fooled by the illusions. It treats the room as difficult terrain and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}. As long as it remains in the room, it can't reduce its sickened value below 1.

Critical Failure As failure, but each time the creature Archive attempts a move action, it must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] or they lose the action, and on a critical failure, it also falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

stealth.details: (expert); noticing the staff has a DC of 0.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-08: The Haunted Corridor

details.routine: (three actions) On its initiative, Duty's Soulshackle first uses Call to Serve if it's able to and spends any remaining actions making velstrac chains Strikes against its enemies.

description.value:
Trigger Lyari proposes how to free herself from Duty's Soulshackle.

Effect Duty's Soulshackle quivers in rage before unleashing more spectral chains. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Duty's Soulshackle creates a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pfs-season-7-bestiary.Actor.Withered Leaf of Talhindir] within 10 feet of itself. It then cannot use this action for [[/gmr 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds}.

details.routine: (3 actions) On its turn each round, two new 10-foot-square sinkholes, 5 feet deep, randomly appear on the map. Until a creature of size Small or larger steps in the area of one, it looks no different than the surrounding area. If a creature takes an action to seek and succeeds at a @Check[perception|dc:15] check, it notices the hazardous square.

description.value:
Trigger A creature of size Small or larger steps into an affected square or is standing on a square that becomes a sinkhole

Effect The ground beneath the creature gives way. The creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:15] Save.

Critical Success The creature is able to move to an adjacent square.

Success The creature grabs an edge.

Failure The creature falls into the pit and lands @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. It takes @Damage[2[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage. Due to roots in the pit wall, it is easy to climb out of, requiring only a @Check[type:athletics|dc:10] check.

details.description: Glowing red Daemonic runes blaze when blood falls on them.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Scratch Out Runes] (untrained) to scratch out enough runes to disrupt the magic or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 15) to counteract the runes

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-21: In Pursuit of Water

details.disable: Two successful @Check[religion|dc:17|options:disable-bloodmoon] or @Check[occultism|dc:17|options:disable-bloodmoon] checks, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 15)

description.value:
Trigger Combat begins with the creature it is attuned to

If a PC uses an action to taunt Bloodmoon with a successful @Check[deception|dc:15], @Check[diplomacy|dc:15], or @Check[intimidation|dc:15] check, Bloodmoon must spend an action monologuing on the next turn.

publication.title: Pathfinder Beginner Box: Secrets of the Unlit Star

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the hall

Effect Time weighs upon the creature, dealing @Damage[(2d6+5)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic] save). On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

details.description: A burst of abyssal energy ignites the sap in a nearby bruorsivi, which explodes in a burst of fiery splinters.
details.disable:
@Check[arcana|dc:17], @Check[occultism|dc:17], or @Check[religion|dc:17] to magically alter the runes, or @Check[thievery|dc:20] to physically alter them. A PC that can read Abyssal or Cyclops is better able to identify key runes, gaining a +1 circumstance bonus to these check (+2 if they speak both).

Each check requires two actions, and disabling the monolith requires three successful checks. A PC that critically succeeds earns two success. On a failed check, the attempting PC is exposed to the burning flames wreathing the monolith, taking @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage(@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic|name:Explosive Monolith]).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-14: Lost in Flames

details.routine:
(1 action) On the hazard's turn, randomly select a PC within 40 feet of the monolith. A tree near that PC explodes in a @Template[burst|distance:10], dealing @Damage[1d6[piercing],1d6[fire]]{1d6 piercing damage and 1d6 fire damage}. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:17|name:Explosive Monolith|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than the monolith's guardians approaches within 40 feet of the monolith

Effect the flames wreathing the monolith flare with incandescent light. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:17] save.

On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for one round.

On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for one round, then @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for one round.

The hazard rolls initiative.

details.disable: A hero must succeed at a @Check[thievery|dc:18|traits:hazard|name:Prevent Blocks from Falling] check on the triggering square or the ceiling to prevent the stone blocks from falling.

description.value: When a creature steps on the square closest to the north door, the trap drops blocks of stone all along the hallway. It makes one ranged Strike against each creature between the two doors.

details.description: A net filled with stones and a small anvil crashes down on someone who steps into the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17] to disarm the trap through a cracked-open door, or 3 Interact to open the door slowly without triggering the trap

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door without taking care to avoid triggering the trap;

Effect The net drops a dozen hefty stones and a small but quite hard anvil onto the creature, inflicting @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic]). The sound of this trap going off alerts Gurtlekep in area C8.

details.description: A pressure-sensitive floorboard connects to a beam holding up debris in the crawlway's ceiling.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|name:Disable Trap|traits:mechanical,trap,hazard] (trained) on the floorboard before the debris falls

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: The Fall of Plaguestone

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is placed on the floorboard.

Effect The ceiling in the crawlway collapses in the square where the passage turns, and also in the two adjacent squares. Anyone in these three squares takes @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]] damage and can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:14|traits:damaging-effect] save to reduce the damage. For the creature in the center square, where the trap is triggered, this is a basic save. Creatures in the two adjacent squares take no damage on a success or critical success as they avoid the falling debris entirely, but take full damage on a failed save, and take double damage on a critical failure. Creatures that take damage from the debris are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized], trapped under the crushing weight.

Clearing out a square of debris requires a successful @Check[athletics|dc:17|name:Clear Debris] skill check, with the following effects.

Critical Success The square is cleared in 1 minute.

Success The square is cleared in 5 minutes.

Failure The square is cleared in 10 minutes.

Critical Failure The ceiling above the square collapses further, dealing an additional @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]] damage to the character digging out the square and any character trapped in that square. This takes 1 minute and no progress is made.

details.disable: A hero must succeed at a @Check[athletics|dc:18] or @Check[thievery|dc:18] check to prevent the shelves from collapsing.

publication.title: Pathfinder Beginner Box: Secrets of the Unlit Star

description.value: When a creature attempts to touch the shelves or take anything from them, they collapse, sending debris throughout the room. The trap makes one melee Strike against each creature in the room.

details.description: The unstable rocks on the floor shift when stepped on, triggering rocks in the pile above to fall down across the pathway.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:17|name:Navigate Safe Path|traits:trap] (trained) to navigate a save path or @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Bolster Rocks|traits:trap] (trained) to bolster the rocks on the floor so they don't shift under a creature's weight. Two successful checks are necessary to ensure the route through the precarious pile of rocks is safe. Attempting each check requires two actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-10: Delve the Pallid Depths

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on one of the unstable rocks

Effect Rocks come crashing down onto the creatures below. The rockfall deals @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]] damage to each creature within 10 feet of the rock pile (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save).

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Failure The creature takes full damage, is knocked prone, and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its movement speeds for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is knocked prone, and takes a –20-foot circumstance penalty to its movement speeds for 2 rounds.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Rockfall Failure]

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Rockfall Critical Failure]

details.description: Sigils carved into the turquoise sphere atop the altar illuminate. Moments later, a rush of water flows into the hallway.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:17] (trained) to neutralize the tidal magic, or @Check[survival|dc:17] to predict the changing tides, or @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to scratch away the sigils; two total successes are required to disable the trap.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.routine:
(1 action) The magic controlling the tides malfunctions, causing the tides to change every few seconds. Water flows rapidly between areas B2 and B4. Each creature in area B2, area B4, or the hallway connecting them must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:17|traits:damaging-effect] save as the shifting tides buffet them, dealing @Damage[1d8[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is moved 5 feet along with the water.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is moved 10 feet along with the water.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the altar that contains the tidal control orb

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as water rushes down the hallway. Area B4 becomes flooded with 15 feet of water.

details.description: A haunting voice from the statue of a woman in servant's garb calls out to those in sight, welcoming them to stay. The statue then changes to the image of a gylou devil.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:14] (trained) to disrupt the unholy energy projecting from the statue, @Check[athletics|dc:17] to knock the statue off balance Three total successes using either skill disables the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-20: The Strings of Hell

details.reset: The statue returns to its human visage once no creatures are in the courtyard and it resets after [[/gmr 1d4+1]] rounds. Until it resets, a creature can destroy the statue with a @Check[athletics|dc:16] check, permanently disabling the hazard.
details.routine: (two actions) For its first action, the statue uses handmaiden's command. With its second action, it makes a stony tentacle Strike at the closest target within range; if there are no targets, the trap instead uses handmaiden's command again.

description.value:
Trigger A non-devil triggers or disables the slamming door hazard

Effect The statue takes on the visage of a handmaiden devil as a wave of unholy energy washes over the courtyard. Each creature in the courtyard must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:17|options:area-effect,inflicts:slowed] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for as long as it remains in the courtyard. The trap then rolls initiative.

The statue emits a supernaturally compelling command. Each creature in the courtyard must make a @Check[will|dc:17|options:area-effect,forced-movement] save, with results below.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is pulled 5 feet toward the statue.

Critical Failure The creature is pulled 5 feet toward the statue and must spend the first action on its next turn making a Step toward the statue.

stealth.details: (trained) or @Check[perception|dc:15|name:Notice Control Panel|traits:hazard,complex,fire,magical,trap] check to notice the control panel

Each check requires two actions.

The pillar cannot move at all if the track is destroyed. When the pillar moves, it deals @Damage[(1d6+3)[fire]] damage to any creatures whose space it moves through.

description.value:

details.description: Magical motion sensors detect when a creature enters the chamber.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17] (trained) to disable the sensor without triggering them, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 15) to counteract the sensors

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

details.reset: The trap resets after 10 minutes.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the chamber

Effect The rune detonates in a ball of flame that fills the chamber, dealing @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:16|basic] save).

details.description: The demon lord Urxehl manifests upon Golarion a small region of his Abyssal realm, filled with constantly burning forests lashed by fierce storms and unholy rain that only further feeds the flames. This is a mystical fire, fueled by the presence of Urxehl's minions. Any flames on the map emit concealing smoke in a 10-foot radius around the fire.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:17] or @Check[performance|dc:17] (trained) to remind the Finadar forest of its true self; or @Check[religion|dc:17], @Check[occultism|dc:17], or @Check[demon-lore|dc:17]{Demon Lore}(trained) to disrupt the connection to the imposed realm and extinguish one of the five sections of flames on the map. These checks can only be attempted by creatures adjacent to the section, and each attempt takes 2 actions. An extinguished fire still emits concealing smoke for 1 additional round but is no longer hot enough to deal damage. A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] spell or a spell with the water trait (2nd level; counteract DC 15) extinguishes a section of flames. If the PCs disable three of the five sections of the flames of Verakivhan, the hazard is too weakened to sustain itself, and the remaining flames disappear. If there are no demons or elementals remaining in the area, the hazard is automatically disabled.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-18: The Fanciful March of Urwal

details.routine: (3 actions) Any creature within the flames takes @Damage[(1d6+3)[fire]] damage, and any creature adjacent to the flames takes @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic|name:Flames of Verakivhan|traits:fire] save). Additionally, the flames call down a fiery rain that deals @Damage[(1d6+3)[fire]] damage to a random PC within the planar overlay (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic|name:Flames of Verakivhan|traits:fire] save). If any PCs attempted to disable the hazard that turn, the fiery rain chooses one of them as its target.

description.value:
Trigger A creature that is not a demon or elemental enters the planar overlay

Effect Flames ignite throughout the area. The hazard rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (trained); @Check[perception|dc:20|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap] when the bat swarm is active

details.description: A metal pincer trap lies hidden at the bottom of a weakened spot in the floor.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Jam Mechanism|traits:trap,mechanical] to jam the pincer's triggeer mechanism.
details.reset: The foot pincer must be reset manually before it can activate again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps onto a foot pincer

Effect The trap slams shut on the triggering creature's foot. The creature must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save or take @Damage[(1d6+5)[slashing]] damage and @Damage[1d4[bleed]].

details.description: Daemonic curses slither and crawl through malevolent runes carved in four stone pillars. HP is per-pillar.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Disable Thievery] (trained) to scratch out a pillar's runes, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 15) to counteract a pillar's magic, or @Check[athletics|dc:18|name:Disable Athletics|traits:action:force-open]{Athletics (Force Open)} to push a pillar over. Disabling a pillar requires two actions. Disabling all four pillars or slaying Sezruth disables the trap

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-21: In Pursuit of Water

details.routine:
Routine (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled pillar. On its initiative, the trap uses each action to afflict a different non-evil creature in the room with thirst. The creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:17|name:Foretold Ruin|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is temporarily immune to additional negative effects from the foretold ruin hazard's reaction for 24 hours. The hazard has no awareness of this immunity and continues to target this creature.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued] until they drink water. If the creature was already fatigued, they take @Damage[1d6[nonlethal,mental]]{1d6 nonlethal mental} damage.

Critical Failure The creature is fatigued until they drink water and takes 1d6 nonlethal mental damage. If the creature was already fatigued, they instead take @Damage[2d6[nonlethal,mental]]{2d6 nonlethal mental} damage.

description.value:
Trigger A non-evil creature enters the room.

Effect The trap casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantom Pain] on the creature, and then rolls initiative.

@Check[will|dc:17|name:Phantom Pain|traits:mental,nonlethal] save

stealth.details: (trained); @Check[perception|dc:20|traits:action:seek] (trained) to notice the construct limbs at the bottom of the pit.

details.description: The limbs of smashed constructs line the bottom of the fissure, beneath the water. They desperately grab anything that gets too close.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Immobilize Limbs] (trained) or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:16|name:Immobilize Limbs]{Engineering Lore} or @Check[azlant-lore|dc:16|name:Immobilize Limbs]{Azlant Lore} twice to immobilize the limbs, or @Check[deception|dc:18|name:Trick Limbs]{Deception} twice to trick the limbs into grasping each other.
details.routine: (2 actions) The grasping limbs try to Grapple all creatures in the water (Athletics [[/r 1d20 +9 #Athletics]]{+9}). They then tighten around all creatures they have grabbed or restrained, dealing @Damage[1d8[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic|name:Grasping Limbs]).

description.value:
Trigger A creature falls into the water

Effect The smashed constructs at the bottom of the water try to Grapple the triggering creature (Athletics [[/r 1d20+9 #Athletics]]{+9}). The trap rolls initiative.

details.description: When a concealed cord is tripped, several sharp stones fall onto the square containing the cord.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Cut the Trip Cord|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap] (trained) to safely cut the trip cord

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into the square containing the trip cord

Effect Falling rocks deal @Damage[4d8[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic]) to any creature in the square, possibly pinning them to the ground. On a failed save, the creature falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]; on a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] (DC 17 to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape]).

An adjacent creature can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:19] check to shove the rocks aside to free an immobilized creature.

details.description: Semitransparent sheets of webbing span the entryway, ready to capture small insects or hamper larger creatures that pass through.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:17|name:Dislodge Web] (trained) to dislodge it

description.value:
Trigger A creature that isn't a spider walks into the web

Effect The web wraps around the triggering creature's body, clinging to their limbs. The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save or take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all their Speeds until they @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] the web (DC 20). On a critical failure, the webbing also clings to the creature's face, making them @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}, and they can't attempt to reduce this condition until they Escape the web.

details.description: Semitransparent sheets of webbing are strategically placed in the basement, ready to capture small insects or hamper larger creatures that pass through.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:17] (trained) to dislodge a web

description.value:
Trigger A creature that isn't a spider walks into the web

Effect The web wraps around the triggering creature's body, clinging to its limbs. The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:20|options:inflicts:sickened] save or take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all its Speeds until it [[/act escape dc=20]]{Escapes} the web. On a critical failure, the webbing also clings to the creature's face, making it @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}, and it can't attempt to reduce this condition until it Escapes the web.

details.description: A pile of dust and bone meal, several inches deep and 10 feet across on each side, blocks the tunnel to the surface. It is suffused with the despair of the slowly dying, and summons vengeful skeletal hands to hunt down anyone who dares attempt to leave the area.
details.disable:
@Check[religion|dc:15|name:Administer Burial Rites|traits:haunt] (trained) to speak the proper burial rites, @Check[occultism|dc:15|name:Exorcise Spirits|traits:haunt] (trained) to exorcise the lingering souls, or @Check[intimidation|dc:17|name:Scare Away Spirits|traits:haunt] to scare the fearful souls away.

These actions have the verbal trait. Each check to disable the hazard requires 2 actions, and two total successes are required to disable the haunt.
details.reset: The haunt deactivates if no creatures remain within 30 feet but resets immediately thereafter unless disabled. If tthe haunt is disabled, it resets after 24 hours.
details.routine: (1 action) Spectral hands grasp at creatures in the area. Each creature within 30 feet of the dust pile takes @Damage[1d4[void]] damage (@Check[will|dc:17|basic|name:Grasping Hands] save; creatures that critically fail this saving throw are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round).

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 30 feet of the remains

Effect The haunt summons three @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Crawling Hand], which emerge from the pile of dust to attack nearby creatures. The hands roll initiative and remain for 7 rounds, after which the summoning spell ends and the hands disappear. The hands also disappear if someone disables the trap before the duration expires.

The summoned hands can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice how the pile might fall on a person disabling it; DC 0 to see the pile.

details.description: Beams of heavy and sharp wood rest against one another to form a precariously balanced cage tied to several trip lines and tension-bearing dowels.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17] (trained), @Check[crafting|dc:17] (trained), or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:17] (trained) to figure out how to safely dismantle the pile without it collapsing on anyone.

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

description.value:
Trigger Any of the four revelers within the hazard perform any action with the Move trait

Effect The pile collapses onto the four revelers, dealing @Damage[(1d6+5)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save) to each of them. All four also take @Damage[1d6[persistent,bludgeoning]] damage until they're pulled from the rubble with a @Check[athletics|dc:17] check or can [[/act escape dc=17]].

Trigger An attempt to disable the hazard from outside critically fails, or someone attempts to clamber into the pile of lumber

Effect The pile collapses outward, inflicting @Damage[(1d6+5)[bludgeoning]] damage to the four revelers (@Check[reflex|dc:15|basic] save), and @Damage[(2d6+5)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save) to all creatures in a 10-foot area surrounding the central 10-foot-square where the revelers were trapped.

details.description: Three shelves inside a dusty cabinet each hold a row of dolls infused with the Doll House's malevolent spiritual energy.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:16] (trained) or @Check[religion|dc:16] (trained) to calm the spirits of one of three shelves in the cabinet
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures have left the area or when the soulbound dolls in the northwestern cabinet are destroyed. After 1 hour, the haunt is ready to trigger again. Putting Thalleus's spirit to rest permanently destroys the haunt.
details.routine: (3 actions) For each shelf that remains, the haunt takes 1 action to target a living, non-construct creature in the room. Phantom dolls escape from the cabinet to rush that creature, clamber over them, and punch, kick, and bite them. The target takes @Damage[(1d6+2)[spirit]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger At least two living creatures are in the room for more than a minute

Effect The dolls in the cabinet begin to shake and shudder, filling the area with a feeling of overwhelming dread about what might happen next. Each living creature who can see the cabinet must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:20|options:inflicts:frightened] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: The machine is a cylindrical device ringed in blades with a devilish eye at its center. Gnome inventors created the mechanical device, which taps into the planes of earth and fre to create powerful blasts to damage fortress walls or decimate ranks of enemies. Unfortunately, the many miles of forest have not been kind and it's malfunctioning. The damaged jaws planar connections have weakened to a mere trickle and, when activated, it lashes out in an uncontrolled frenzy. The blades encircling it begin lashing out.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:15], @Check[crafting|dc:15], @Check[nature|dc:15], or @Check[thievery|dc:15] (trained) twice, to sever the connection to the planes of fre and earth. @Check[crafting|dc:13] or @Check[thievery|dc:13] (trained) once, to remove the pintle connecting the limber to the horses. A creature must be adjacent to the jaws to make either check.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-06: Brastlewark at War Part 1: The Gnome Defection

details.reset: The planar connection shuts down after 1 minute. The horses will tire and stop running after 1 minute.
details.routine: (three actions) On its turn each round, the horses spend one action to move 30 feet, dragging the limber carrying the jaws behind them in a randomly chosen direction. The horses will not move through other creatures. Players on the limber when it moves must attempt a @Check[acrobatics|dc:15] check when it moves or fall of. Removing the pintle will end the random movement. The jaws use 2 actions to Spit Fire or make melee strikes against an adjacent creature.

description.value:
Jaws Hardness 8

Jaws HP 30 (BT 15)

Trigger A Small or larger creature, moves within 10 feet of the jaws.

Effect One of the blades lashes out, making one Strike against the creature.

The jaws launch a magma ball (range 60 feet, @Template[burst|distance:10]), randomly, at the largest group of creatures, not including itself, it can catch in the blast dealing @Damage[2d4[bludgeoning],2d4[fire]|options:area-damage] damage. Each creature caught in the blast must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:15|options:damaging-effect,area-effect] save. The jaws cannot Spit Fire again for [[/gmr 1d4+1 #Recharge Spit Fire]] rounds.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, @Damage[1[persistent,fire]] damage, and is knocked prone.

details.description: A crossbow hidden inside the frog skull connects to a floor tile in the center of the chamber.

publication.title: Pathfinder Game Night: Dawn of the Frogs

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is applied to the floor tile

details.description: Shadowy chains reach out to ensnare creatures trapped in the mindscape.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:18] to avoid the chains, or @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[occultism|dc:18], or @Check[religion|dc:18] to ritually disrupt the haunting. Each success reduces the haunt's actions by one. When the haunt has no more actions, it is disabled and the Scaling the Chain haunt triggers.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-08: The Haunted Corridor

Each round that a creature starts its turn @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed], it takes @Damage[(2d4+1)[force]]{2d4+1 nonlethal force} damage. Spectral scars form on any creature knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] by this haunt.

Each living creature in the haunt must attempt a @Check[will|dc:16] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} The haunt then rolls initiative.

A small fissure opens, causing static electricity to build and, fueled by the increased presence of metal in the area, sparks begin to fly. Electrical discharges begin to break out reaching from objects to unlucky creatures.
details.disable: @Check[type:arcana,nature,thievery|dc:15] (trained) two times to counter the planar energies.

Trigger A non-elemental creature approaches within 20 feet;

Effect A bolt of electricity targets the creature for @Damage[2d4[electricity]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:15|basic] save. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: The souls of the ghouls' recent meals cry out from their pile of bones in the nearby tunnel, lamenting their fate.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:14] (trained) sets the spirits at ease, or @Check[deception|dc:17], @Check[diplomacy|dc:17], or @Check[intimidation|dc:17] to convince the spirits to not interfere right now. A single success disables the haunt. PCs may accrue additional successes beyond the first—each one convinces the haunt to use telekinetic interference once against ghouls in the haunt's range instead of the living creatures.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-99: Under the Eye of the Mantis

details.routine: (two actions) The haunt uses one action to activate its gurgling gasp and then uses telekinetic interference for all of its other actions until it can use gurgling gasp again.

description.value:
Trigger Combat begins

Effect The haunt rolls initiative.

The haunt emits a cacophony of the ghoul victims' death rattles, forcing all living creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:80]{80 feet} to attempt a @Check[will|dc:17|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened] save. On a failure, the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure). On a success, a creature is temporarily immune to gurgling gasp for 1 hour. The haunt cannot use gurgling gasp again for [[/gmr 1d6 #Recharge Gurgling Gasp]]{1d6 rounds}.
The haunt attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Trip] a living creature within 60 feet with a bonus of [[/r 1d20+9]]{+9}.

details.description: Solid ground gives way to a lake of bubbling mud that burns anyone who enters and routinely belches out sulfur.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:17|name:Remove Stones|traits:complex,environmental,poison] (trained) to find and remove the stones blocking the flow of water to the mud pot. The water decreases the viscosity of the mud, allowing steam and sulfur to escape gradually, rather than in violent bursts, so creatures can pass through unharmed. The hazard requires two successful checks to disable. Attempting the check takes two actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-10: Delve the Pallid Depths

For each success, the hazard takes a cumulative -1 penalty to attack rolls.
details.routine: (2 actions) The augdunars use an action to Strike one target with their jaws, and they use another action to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Trip] a different target (Athletics [[/r 1d20+9 #Athletics]]{+9}). If the augdunars have taken damage since their last turn, they instead use Lashing Hooves with their first action and then make either a jaws Strike or Trip attempt with their second action.

Trigger Algerschmick rolls initiative

An augdunar makes a hoof Strike and compares the attack roll result to the AC of up to two foes who are adjacent to each other.
