details.routine: (2 actions) The bar fight uses its first action to make a general mayhem Strike against all creatures in any area that has not been disabled. Make one Strike against each enemy in the area and roll the damage only once for all targets. The bar fight then uses its second action to use Port Peril specialty.

description.value: The bar fight creates an effect unique to the bar, located at any point within the hazard (even within sections that have been disabled) as long as the entire hazard has not been disabled. The DC for each effect is 15.
Damage can be bludgeoning, slashing or piercing.

Something about the bar fight has attracted a storm of bad luck, causing a character to bang their shin on a pushed-out bench or get hit on the head by a falling painting. The hazard casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Jealous Hex] on a creature. This effect is a Rivanti's Bar specialty.

stealth.details: -10 and no minimum proficiency to notice

details.description: An assorted crowd throws punches, flings bottles, and otherwise flies into chaos as a bar fight breaks out, clustering in six sections of one of Port Peril's drinking houses.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:17|name:Cow the Crowd] to cow a section of crowd or @Check[diplomacy|dc:20|name:Disperse the Crowd] to attempt to disperse it

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #4: Port Peril Pub Crawl

details.routine: (2 actions) The bar fight uses its first action to make a general mayhem Strike against all creatures in any area that has not been disabled. Make one Strike against each enemy in the area and roll the damage only once for all targets. The bar fight then uses its second action to use Port Peril specialty.

description.value: The bar fight creates an effect unique to the bar, located at any point within the hazard (even within sections that have been disabled) as long as the entire hazard has not been disabled. The DC for each effect is 15.
Damage can be bludgeoning, slashing or piercing.

Gaming chips, a money purse, or jewels from a broken necklace scatter across the ground. All creatures within a @Template[burst|distance:15] that could be enticed by material wealth must attempt a @Check[will|dc:15] saving throw or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for 1 round or until a creature has used an Interact action to scoop up the valuables. This effect is a Watchtower specialty.

details.description: A tripwire fastened to the gate snaps when the gate is opened, releasing a moderate thunderstone to the ground.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|name:Remove the thunderstone|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap] (trained) to remove the thunderstone or @Check[reflex|dc:18|name:Catch the falling thunderstone|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap] save to catch the falling thunderstone

publication.title: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powder Keg

description.value:
Trigger The front gate is opened

Effect A moderate thunderstone falls on the flagstones and activates, dealing @Damage[(2[splash])[sonic]] splash damage to each creature within 10 feet of the gate. Creatures in the area must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:17|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap] saving throw or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 round. Adjacent creatures can attempt to catch the falling thunderstone (see Disable).

details.description: One of the potions has decayed, putting it under immense pressure. Its contents have become poisonous. When handled improperly, the bottle bursts, sending glass shards flying everywhere.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Pressurized Bottle|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) to carefully place the bottle under a bucket or bowl.

description.value:
Trigger A creature tries to pick up or move the potion

Effect The bottle shatters into pieces that fill a @Template[burst|distance:5]. The burst deals @Damage[1[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic] save). A creature who takes any damage is exposed to fermented potion.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:19]

Maximum Duration 4 hours

Stage 1 @Damage[1d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} (1 hour)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} (1 hour)

Stage 3 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage and clumsy 2 (1 hour)

details.description: A patch of treacherous bog attempts to submerge creatures that step onto it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:15|name:Disturb Surface|traits:secret,action:sense-direction,exploration] (trained) to disturb the surface and make the quagmire plainly visible

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-08: Revolution on the Riverside

details.reset: The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it settles over the course of 24 hours
details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the quagmire pulls each creature within it 1 square toward the center of the quagmire and 1 step further submerged; a creature that is submerged up to its waist becomes submerged up to its neck, and a creature that is submerged up to its neck is pulled under and has to hold its breath to avoid suffocation.

A creature in the quagmire can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:17|name:Swim Out of Quagmire|traits:environmental,hazard,action:swim] check to Swim to either raise itself by 1 step if its submerged to its neck or worse, or to swim out of the quagmire if its submerged up to only its waist. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down 1 step. A creature that Swims out of the quagmire escapes the hazard and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] adjacent to the quagmire. Other creatures can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid] the creature, typically by using a rope or similar tool, or attempt to pull a creature within their reach out with a @Check[athletics|dc:17|name:Pull Out of Quagmire] check, with the same results as if the creature attempted the check.

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature walks into a quagmire.

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the quagmire up to its waist. The quagmire rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

details.description: A fire engulfs a @Template[square|distance:10]{10-foot-by-10-foot} section of forest, then spreads on each of its turns.
details.disable:
A successful @Check[athletics|dc:15|name:Smother Fire|traits:hazard,environmental,fire], @Check[nature|dc:15|name:Smother Fire|traits:hazard,environmental,fire], or @Check[survival|dc:15|name:Smother Fire|traits:hazard,environmental,fire] check from an adjacent square is sufficient to smother one 5-foot-square of fire; each attempt is an Interact action. Dousing the flames automatically extinguishes one or more sections of fire, with no check. Water typically clears a 5-foot square if the amount is small (such as that from @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Create Water] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Hydraulic Push]). Larger amounts of water, such as a full bucket, typically douse a 10-foot-by-10-foot area (or 4 squares in some other shape). Throwing a bucket of water on flames requires an Interact action. A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Waterskin] doesn't contain enough water to put out even 1 square of fire.

Cold can also put out fire, but only if the cold can affect an area; cold is usually less effective than water, so a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Frost Vial (Lesser)]{Frost Vial} typically puts out only 1 square of fire, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Ray of Frost] is ineffective.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-15: The Blooming Catastrophe

details.routine: On its turn, the fire spreads into a number of additional squares equal to half the number of squares the fire currently occupies, minimum 1 square. The GM determines the squares the fire spreads into. Any creature that ends its turn next to the flames takes @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage, and any creature within the flames takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:15|basic|name:Raging Forest Fire|traits:hazard,environmental,fire] save for both). A creature can take damage from flames only once per round.

details.description: This mirror reflects the viewer's deepest desires.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Shroud the Mirror|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to shroud the mirror without looking at it, or @Check[occultism|dc:17|name:Erect Mental Barriers] (trained) to erect mental barriers
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature looks into the mirror

Effect The triggering creature sees themselves enjoying their deepest desires in the mirror's reflection, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] (@Check[will|dc:20|options:inflicts:fascinated] negates). This fascination ends automatically when the mirror is broken or covered up.

details.description: The space above the flame in the ivory bowl wavers and the smell of brimstone assaults the senses.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:20] or @Check[religion|dc:20] three times to suppress the backlash of energy, or defeat Viro Ahala
details.reset: Once Viro Ahala has been defeated, this trap can't be reset.
details.routine: (3 actions) The hazard makes a ranged mindburn Strike, creates a Pulse of Fear, and bestows Vitality of Hell to Viro or one of his allies at random. If it's reduced to 2 actions, the hazard doesn't create a Pulse of Fear. If reduced to 1 action, the hazard only bestows Vitality of Hell.

description.value: Each of Viro's enemies in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] from the ivory bowl must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:18] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

Viro or one of his allies gains 1d6 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Vitality of Hell]

Trigger Viro Ahala rolls initiative

Effect Viro's lack of full attention unleashes the latent magical energy of the cave. The hazard creates a Pulse of Fear and then rolls initiative.

details.description: A rusty cage embedded in the ceiling drops down with a clang to try to entrap a creature that steps onto the pressure plate in the middle of the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:13] to harmlessly trigger the trap by nudging the pressure plate, @Check[thievery|dc:20] to stabilize the pressure plate so it doesn't trigger, or @Check[athletics|dc:20] to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] from the cage or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open] the cage once it falls
details.reset: The mechanism to manually reset this trap is located in area D15.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks under the cage and steps on the pressure plate

Effect The cage drops from the ceiling to trap the triggering creature, who must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:17|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature avoids the trap, and returns to the space it just left rather than entering the trap's space.

Success As Critical Success, but the falling trap delivers a glancing blow and @Damage[(1d6+3)[bludgeoning]] damage to the creature as it stumbles back.

Failure The falling trap lands on the triggering creature. A Medium or smaller creature becomes trapped inside the cage ([[/act escape dc=20]]). A Large or larger creature takes @Damage[(2d6+5)[bludgeoning]] damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] as the cage bounces off the creature's body, becoming destroyed in the process.

Critical Failure As Failure, but a Medium or smaller creature is also struck by the cage, knocked prone, and then takes @Damage[(2d6+5)[bludgeoning]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] by the cage as it lands on a limb.

details.description: An illusory cyclops in oracular robes cries out a warning, but then dissolves into a violent phantasmal cyclops, which calls out for the Blind Formless Chaos That Lies Behind the Stars to accept his sacrifice. It then plunges an obsidian athame into a creature's chest, summoning a foul entity.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:14] (trained) to repair Chamenaiti's magic, @Check[religion|dc:17] or @Check[occultism|dc:17] (trained) to offset the prayer, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 14) to counteract the warning

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.routine: a Rubbery crimson tentacles appear from a rip in the air and grasp at all creatures in the area, dealing @Damage[1d4[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger Sailing Mission 5 begins;

Effect The trap makes an athame Strike against a random creature, and then rolls initiative.

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 10-foot radius surrounds a shadowy 3-foot-diameter rune on the floor.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:14|name:Erase the Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device,magical,trap] (trained) to carefully erase the rune without triggering the sensors, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] or a spell with the Light trait (1st level; counteract DC 15) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Intro: Year of Shattered Sanctuaries

details.reset: The trap resets each night at dusk.

description.value:
Trigger A creature without the Shadow trait ends its turn in the cloud of magical sensors, and a member of the Onyx Alliance is within 60 feet

Effect This trap casts a minor version of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Mirror Image]: it creates a single illusory image that swirls about the nearest member of the Onyx Alliance within 60 feet, functioning in all ways as a mirror image cast by that creature. The image remains for 1 minute, or until it is destroyed or the trap is disabled. A creature can have up to three shadow doubles at one time; any additional doubles are instantly destroyed.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the air and plants wavering and shifting

details.description: Cross-planar seepage from the Oaksteward rebels' ritual causes shadowy warping effects to strain and damage living creatures in the forest clearing.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:17] (trained) to will the Material Plane's hold on the area back into firmness and disable the effect for 1 hour, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 15) to end the effect permanently
details.reset: This effect continues for 1 day, after which it ends and cannot be triggered again without another ritual.
details.routine:
Each creature in the clearing must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:17] save as the planar warping threatens to create microscopic tears inside their body. Fey, plant creatures, and druids are unaffected.

Critical Success As success, and the creature is healed by the primal magic, regaining @Damage[1d6[healing]] Hit Points.

Success The creature is unaffected and immune to the effect for 24 hours.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(1d6+3)[force]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d6+6)[force]] damage.

description.value:
Trigger A character enters the affected area

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as the field of planar warping surges, filling the forest clearing.

details.description: The shaitan observes the area from a distance, observing the battlefield as a game board. The ground trembles in response to the genie's enthusiasm.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[nature|dc:20] (trained) to contest the shaitan's control (requires two successes).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-14: Shattering Golden Chains

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets once all hostile creatures leave A3 or surrender.
details.routine: (2 actions) On each of the trap's actions, the trap either moves a willing creature on the ground up to 30 feet without provoking reactions or makes a hostile creature on the ground @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] for 1 round on a failed @Check[acrobatics|dc:20] check or @Check[reflex|dc:20] save.

description.value:
Trigger The shaitan sees a non-genie creature threatening an ally

Effect The trap rolls initiative and the shaitan takes control of the floor in A3, making it difficult terrain for hostile creatures while the trap is active.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice cracks spider-webbing across the window

details.description: The anger trapped within a structure shatters a window, showering glass in adjacent spaces.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:19|name:Suppress the Spiritual Energy] or @Check[religion|dc:19|name:Suppress the Spiritual Energy] (trained) to suppress the spiritual energy or @Check[diplomacy|dc:20|name:Soothe the Latent Anger] (trained) to soothe the latent anger

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the window

Effect The window shatters, dealing @Damage[(2d6+5)[slashing]] damage to creatures within 5 feet (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic]).

details.description: Singe's spirit lingers in the Whippoorwill's crow's nest, where it calls out warnings and shoots intruders.
details.disable: @Check[pathfinder-society-lore|dc:17] (trained) to persuade Singe that you are Pathfinders or @Check[religion|dc:17](trained) to perform rites to lay Singe to rest; two total successes are required to disable the haunt

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: d Singe uses its first action(s) to make a long gun Strike against a random creature in the area. It uses its final action for Lookout's Warning. If the PCs successfully use Pathfinder Society Lore to Disable the haunt, Singe loses an action to make a long gun Strike.

description.value:
Trigger One or more creatures steps onto the Whippoorwill's wreckage

Effect Singe makes a long gun Strike against a random creature in the area, uses Lookout's Warning, and then rolls initiative.

Singe lets out one of the following cries, in order (repeating from the top, if necessary). Everyone in the haunt's area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:17] save. On a success, the creature is unaffected; on a critical success, it also gains a +2 circumstance bonus on its next save vs. Lookout's Warning. Other effects depend on the specific cry below.

  • Thar She Blows: "Captain, look! A huge whale! It's coming toward us—fast!" On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for 1 round, looking for the whale; on a critical failure it's fascinated for 1 minute, and the condition doesn't end if Singe makes a Strike on an ally or uses Lookout's Warning.
  • No Quarter: "It's coming around again! It's relentless! Brace for impact!" On a failure, the creature is tossed off balance; it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] for 1 round. On a critical failure, it's also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].
  • Shark Bait: "Captain, it's hopeless! It's going to destroy the ship!" On a failure, a sense of doom fills the creature, which becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}. On a critical failure, the fear is overwhelming, and it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.
  • Going Down with the Ship: "Get below, captain, and secure the Forked Key! I'll try to give you time!" On a failure, the creature must spend one action putting an item it is holding away; if it is not holding anything, it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for one action. On a critical failure, it must spend three actions in a combination of putting away any items it is holding and fleeing.

details.description: Pressure-sensitive panels in the floor connect to a stone slab hidden in a hallway's ceiling.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|name:Disable Floor Panel] (trained) on the floor panels before the slab falls

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is placed on any floor tile.

Effect The door falls, closing off the hallway. The stone slab deals @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone beneath or adjacent to the slab when it drops and pushes them out of its space in a random direction. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:17|traits:damaging-effect] save takes no damage and rolls out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, they can choose the direction.

Lifting the fallen slab requires a successful @Check[athletics|dc:25|name:Lift a Slab Athletics|showDC:all] check.

Hitting the floor panels triggers the trap. The slab uses the same AC and saves as the trap, but it has Hardness 12, HP 48 (BT 24).

details.description: Pressure-sensitive panels in the floor connect to a stone slab hidden in a hallway's ceiling.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|name:Disable Floor Panel] (trained) on the floor panels before the slab falls

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-20: The Strings of Hell

details.reset: Once this trap is triggered, the area of the fallen slab is greater difficult terrain until the trap is reset, which requires 1 hour of labor.

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is placed on any floor tile.

Effect The door falls, closing off the hallway. The stone slab deals @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone beneath or adjacent to the slab when it drops and pushes them out of its space in a random direction. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:17|traits:damaging-effect] save takes no damage and rolls out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, they can choose the direction.

Lifting the fallen slab requires a successful @Check[athletics|dc:25|name:Lift a Slab] check.

Hitting the floor panels triggers the trap. The slab uses the same AC and saves as the trap, but it has Hardness 12, HP 48 (BT 24).

details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|traits:hazard|name:Disable Floor Panels] on the floor panels before the slab falls.

publication.title: Pathfinder Beginner Box: Secrets of the Unlit Star

description.value: When pressure is placed on a floor tile, the door falls, closing off the hallway. The stone slab deals @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone beneath or adjacent to the slab when it drops and pushes them out of its space in a random direction. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:17|options:damaging-effect] save takes no damage and rolls out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, they roll out of the way and can choose which direction. Lifting the fallen slab requires a successful @Check[athletics|dc:25] check. Hitting the floor panels triggers the trap. The slab uses the same AC and saves as the trap, but it has Hardness 12, HP 48 (BT 24).

details.description: The heavy gate slams shut when opened more than halfway
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|traits:action:disable-a-device|name:Loosen the Gate Springs] (trained) to loosen the gate's springs before it slams closed
details.reset: The trap can be reactivated (or deactivated, if it's active) by flipping a lever hidden in the nearby wall. A character Searching the area finds it with a successful @Check[perception|dc:18|traits:action:seek] check.

description.value:
Trigger The door is opened more than halfway

Effect The door slams shut and deals @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage to any creature in the way (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic]). On a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] by the closed door until it [[/act escape dc=17]]{Escapes}

details.description: With a rumble, this statue of a former Pathfinder slides across the schoolyard. Its legs pump up and down as it advances.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Hold Back Statue|traits:complex,hazard,magical,trap] to hold it back, @Check[crafting|dc:17|name:Break Statue|traits:complex,hazard,magical,trap] (trained) to break the statue by exploiting a flaw, @Check[occultism|dc:16|name:Dispel Planar Energies|traits:complex,hazard,magical,trap] (trained) to dispel the planar energies.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-09: The Seven Secrets of Dacilane Academy

details.routine: (3 actions) The trap uses its three actions for its Trample activity.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the trap.

Effect The statue Strides up to 30 feet in the direction of the triggering creature. It deals @Damage[(1d6+3)[untyped]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic|traits:complex,hazard,magical,trap] save) to each creature whose space it enters.

Medium or smaller, crushing feet @Check[reflex|dc:17|basic]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Trample]

details.description: The spirits of innumerable dryads and peaceful wood elementals, ripped from their bodies as they were turned into furniture, writhe and seethe, lashing out at both living and undead alike.
details.disable:
@Check[diplomacy|dc:14] (trained), @Check[nature|dc:14] (trained), or @Check[religion|dc:14] (trained) to set the spirits to rest or @Check[deception|dc:17], @Check[intimidation|dc:17], or @Check[occultism|dc:17] to convince the spirits that their eternal rest is coming soon.

A PC may make a Strike against AC 13 to destroy a single table or chair in the room, this functions as a successful check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid] another PC attempting to disable the haunt; two total successes are required to disable the haunt. If all of the tables and chairs are destroyed, the haunt is temporarily disabled until dawn of the next day.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-99: Under the Eye of the Mantis

details.routine: (two actions) The Haunted Hollows haunt uses table flip or chair toss Strikes at different creatures in the area, favoring those who are trying to disable the haunt first, but otherwise choosing randomly. The table flip Strikes originate with any table in the room, destroying the table in the process. The chair toss Strikes originate with any chair or bar stool in the room, destroying that object in the process. If the haunt is not disabled, these objects reform and return to their original positions at dawn of the next day.

description.value:
Trigger Any living or undead creatures approach within 30 feet of the haunt's locus (the center of the open balcony) inside the tavern

Effect The haunt makes a table flip Strike with a reach of 20 feet against a random creature in the area, and then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (trained) to recognize the plant among others

details.description: A large pitcher plant occupying a 5-foot space sits seemingly dormant with its dull petals open.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:15] (trained) to safely trigger the plant's reflexes, causing it to close and become dormant for one day, or @Check[nature|dc:17] (trained) to carefully coax the pitcher into quiescence
details.reset: Once the pitcher has eaten a spell, its petals turn dark pink and green before it closes. It goes dormant for one day per spell rank of the spell consumed. Once the pitcher finishes digesting, it opens again and slowly wilts. A second pitcher branches into an adjacent square when the first pitcher opens again.

description.value:
Trigger A spell's direct line of effect passes within a 5-foot-radius of the plant

Effect The pitcher attempts to counteract the spell (counteract rank 1, counteract modifier [[/r 1d20+10 #Counteract]]{+10}). On a success, the pitcher immediately consumes the spell as it passes over the plant.

details.description: A low trip wire connects to a lattice panel covered with wooden spikes.
details.disable: [[/act disable-device dc=15]]{Thievery} (trained) to snip the trip wire or jam the panel

description.value:
Trigger A creature triggers the trip wire

Effect A lattice panel lashed with wooden spikes swings shut across the passage. The triggering creature takes @Damage[3d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save). The panel provides lesser @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.other-effects.Item.Effect: Cover]{Cover} and prevents movement along the passage. The panel can be pushed open like a door with an Interact action, but it swings shut again unless wedged open with a heavy object.

details.description: A wooden trapdoor covers a pit that's 10 feet square and 20 feet deep.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:12|name:Remove Trap Door|traits:mechanical,trap] to remove the trapdoor

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #10: The Broken Scales

details.reset: Creatures can still fall into the trap, but the trapdoor must be reset manually for the trap to become hidden again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage plus @Damage[1d4[piercing]] damage from sharp implements at the bottom of the pit. That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

details.description: The enchanted fountain spews large quantities of grape juice as its marble tiers spin with increasing speed, flinging juice in all directions.
details.disable:
@Check[athletics|dc:16|name:Grapple a Tier|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap,action:grapple] to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple] a tier and halt its momentum, followed by @Check[crafting|dc:14|name:Disable the Pump Mechanism|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap,action:disable-a-device] or @Check[thievery|dc:14|name:Disable the Pump Mechanism|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap,action:disable-a-device] (while the tier is grabbed) to disable the pump mechanism through the gap that the previous checks left open. Grappling a tier requires 1 action. Each Crafting or Thievery check to disable a tier is a 2-action activity.

The fountain has three tiers, and fully disabling the fountain requires each tier to be disabled. For each disabled tier, the DC's to Grapple or disable another tier decrease by 2. If the creature critically succeeds at a check to disable a tier, it's immediately disabled, regardless of the number of checks that would otherwise be required due to Challenge Point adjustments.
details.routine: (1 action) The fountain accelerates its spinning, spraying juice 10 feet in all directions on its first turn, 15 feet in all directions on its second turn, and 20 feet in all directions on its third turn. Every square where it sprays juice becomes difficult terrain. At the start of its fourth turn, the fountain breaks, disabling the hazard and flinging juice 25 feet in all directions.

description.value:
Trigger Safa commands the fountain to start spinning at the beginning of Event 1: Safa's Arrival

Effect The fountain sprays juice in all directions, making every square adjacent to it difficult terrain. The trap rolls initiative.

Trigger A creature attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple] one of the fountain's tiers. Each tier of the fountain has one reaction per round

Effect The heavy, spinning marble has enough sheer force to crush the hands of those trying to stop it. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:15|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap] save before it rolls its check to Grapple the fountain.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to its next check to Grapple the fountain.

Success The creature takes no damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(1d6+3)[bludgeoning]] damage and takes a -2 circumstance penalty to its next check to Grapple the fountain.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d6+3)[bludgeoning]] damage and is flung 10 feet away from the fountain in a random direction, preventing its attempt to Grapple the fountain.

details.description: A large patch of moss ripples like the surface of a pond as spears of green plant matter jut outward.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:17] or @Check[occultism|dc:17] twice to cut off the unstable connection to the Plane of Wood, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 16) to counteract the planar magic
details.reset: If the trap isn't disabled, it becomes dormant for 10 minutes if there are no creatures on or within 10 feet of the moss patch at the start of its turn.
details.routine: (2 actions) For each action, the moss pond makes a spore spear Strike against a random non-plant creature on or within 10 feet of the moss pond.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more non-plant creatures step onto the moss pond

Effect The moss pond ripples oddly as its connection to the Plane of Wood intensifies. Each creature on the pond must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:17|options:area-effect,damaging-effect,inflicts:prone] save or be knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. A creature who critically fails the save also takes @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage from the fall. The pond then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: A fire engulfs a 5-foot-by-5-foot area, then spreads on each of its turns.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:17] to splash the barrels of gall ink on the fire or @Check[survival|dc:15] to smother the flames
details.routine: (1 action) On its turn, the fire spreads into a number of additional squares equal to half the number of squares the fire currently occupies, with a minimum of 1 square. You determine the squares the fire spreads into—typically those with the most flammable materials. Any creature that ends its turn next to the flames takes @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage, and any creature within the flames takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage. Both of these have a @Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save. A creature can take damage from flames only once per round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the printing house

Effect The flames swell at the influx of air and deal @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save). The fire then rolls initiative.

details.description: This bright green plant has long triangular leaves covered with fine hair-like stingers.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:17] to carefully navigate the plant and pull aside or cut away the nettles

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the stinging nettles or moves through a square containing stinging nettles

Effect The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:17] save if they're walking through the square or a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save if they touched the nettles.

Critical Success The creature avoids being stung by the plant's nettles.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} by the pain caused by brief contact with the nettles.

Failure The creature is sickened 1 by the pain caused by contact with the nettles and is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 8 hours from the lingering pain.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} by the pain caused by contact with the nettles and is also clumsy 1 for 24 hours from the lingering pain.

details.description: An immense, jagged piece of clear crystal 10 feet square and forty feet high juts up from the seafloor
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:13|name:Blunt Crystal] (trained) to blunt the crystal's edges without getting cut

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-12: The Burden of Envy

details.reset: The hazard still damages any creature or object that enters its space, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it settles over the course of 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature or object moves into the shard's space.

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[(2d6+5)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save).

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 10-foot radius surrounds an invisible wall or floor rune the size of the creature to be summoned.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:15|name:Approach Rune] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Erase Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 15) to counteract the rune
details.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors

Effect This trap summons a specific 1st-level creature, determined when the trap is created. The creature rolls initiative and remains for [[/br 2d6 #rounds]]{2d6 rounds}, after which the spell ends and the creature disappears. The creature also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. The summoned creature can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors covers four squares on the map. One of these hazards is in the squares directly above each of the four yellow runes on the map, and the other is in the squares directly above each of the four blue runes on the map.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:15] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:17] (trained) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 15) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-11: Flames of Rebellion

details.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors.

Effect This trap summons a specific level 1 creature, determined when the trap is created. The squares above the yellow runes summon a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.Fire Mephit], while the squares above the blue runes summon an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Imp]. The creature rolls initiative and remains for [[/br 2d6 #Rounds]]{2d6 rounds}, after which the spell ends and the creature disappears. The creature also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. The summoned creature can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: Gentle crying rises to become overwhelming wails. The fungal carvings begin to weep blood that pools around the pillars
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:17] (trained) to overpower the frightening cries with a greater sound or @Check[occultism|dc:15] or @Check[religion|dc:15] (trained) to ritually disrupt the haunting.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-08: The Haunted Corridor

details.reset: If the haunt is not dispersed, it resets after 1 day.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the carving of Viore Ciobanu

Effect The fungus statues weep blood, and their cries deal @Damage[(2d6+5)[mental]] damage to living creatures within the room (@Check[will|dc:17|basic|options:inflicts:frightened] save). On a failed save, creatures are also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure)

details.description: The floor wobbles with each step.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] to prop up the floor, or @Check[crafting|dc:17] (trained) to reinforce subflooring

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: immediate

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves on the unstable floor

Effect The floor tilts into a steep incline. Any creature on the tilting floor must succeed at either a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save or a Acrobatics check to [[/act balance dc=17]] (the creature chooses) or else take @Damage[(1d6+3)[bludgeoning]] damage as it slams into the wall.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage. It keeps its footing and can move freely across the floor this round.

Success The creature takes no damage but is somewhat off-balance; the floor is difficult terrain for the creature this round.

Failure The creature slides backward into the wall, taking full damage.

Critical Failure The creature slides backward into the wall with great force, taking double damage.

details.description: This basic trap is little more than a snare designed to slow down and distract enemies.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18]

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-07: Sewer Dragon Crisis

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the square where the trap is located

Effect The triggering creature's gets entangled in netting. The target must make a @Check[reflex|dc:16] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} until they @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] the net.

details.description: This basic trap is little more than a snare designed to slow down and distract enemies.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20]

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-07: Sewer Dragon Crisis

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the square where the trap is located

Effect The triggering creature's gets entangled in netting. The target must make a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} until they @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] the net.

details.description: A malfunction in the door's opening mechanism has caused unstable magic to pool within it, making it liable to release the sorrows it has collected from all who have mourned their lost homeland at its threshold.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:17] (trained) or @Check[deception|dc:20] to coax the door's magic into allowing access; the DC is reduced by 2 if someone in the room has said "Tar Khadurrm" in the last hour.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the door

Effect The last of the door's magic arcs outward. All creatures within @Template[burst|distance:10]{10 feet} of the door must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:17] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 hour.

details.description: A fire engulfs the western door and a @Template[square|distance:10]{10-foot-by-10-foot area} immediately east of it, then spreads on each of its turns.
details.disable:
Eliminating the hazard requires dousing the flames. Water typically clears a 5-foot square if the amount is small (such as that from @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Create Water] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Hydraulic Push]). Larger amounts of water, such as a full bucket, typically douse a @Template[square|distance:10]{10-foot-by-10-foot area} (or 4 squares in some other shape). Throwing a bucket of water on flames requires an Interact action. A waterskin doesn't contain enough water to put out even 1 square of fire. Cold can also put out fire, but only if the cold can affect an area; cold is usually less effective than water, so a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Frost Vial (Lesser)]{Frost Vial} typically puts out 1 square of fire, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Ray of Frost] is ineffective.

Fountain Circle, located outside town hall, has several working water pumps. These are far enough away that it's impractical for a PC to bring water back and forth. However, a PC might instruct the more steely nerved spectators to create a bucket brigade. It takes 15 spectators (which can include those rescued by councilors as well as by PCs) to set this up, and 2 rounds for the townspeople to line up and begin pumping water from Fountain Circle. As of the 3rd round, there is a bucket of water available for the PCs to use at the chamber's southern entrance at the beginning of each PC's turn. Members of a bucket brigade are lined up outside the chamber, and don't take damage from smoke inhalation.

If a PC does want to carry the water, it's 150 feet from the chamber's southern door to a pump, so it 6 actions are usually needed to reach the pumps or return. Filling a bucket requires an Interact action.

Other methods might also help control the fire, as you determine. For example, the PCs might be able to find something to barricade the fire with to control its path, or use a cloak to beat out some of the fire.
details.routine:
On its turn, the fire spreads into a number of additional squares equal to half the number of squares the fire currently occupies, with a minimum of 1 square. You determine the squares the fire spreads into-typically those with the most flammable materials. Any creature that ends its turn next to the flames takes @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage, and any creature within the flames takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage. Both of these have a @Check[reflex|dc:17|basic|traits:complex,environmental,fire,hazard] save. A creature can take damage from flames only once per round. (For simplicity, track damage only for the NPCs and their allies, not for the spectators; Turn 5 and Turn 7 have instructions regarding the spectators' health).

The fire has an additional effect on each of its turns after the first.

Turn 2 The chamber's northern door flies open and flames burst through, igniting the @Template[square|distance:10]{10-foot-by-10-foot area} in front of the door. All fires spread on subsequent turns, at the same rate listed above. Any fires that join together become one fire for this purpose.

Turn 4+ Creatures in the room take @Damage[1d6[untyped]] damage from smoke inhalation at the ends of their turns. Anyone who uses an Interact action to tie a wet rag around their nose and mouth (or uses another creative solution) halves the damage.

Turn 5 Spectators still inside the room fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] from burns and smoke inhalation.

Turn 7 Spectators still in the room die.

details.description: Pressure sensitive panels in the floor connect to a barrel of debris hidden in the hallway's ceiling
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Disable Floor Panels|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) on the floor panels before the barrel falls

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #10: The Broken Scales

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is placed on any floor tile

Effect The barrel of debris tips over and showers broken glass down. The glass deals @Damage[2d8[slashing]] damage and @Damage[1[bleed]] damage(@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|name:Dodge Falling Debris|traits:mechanical,trap] save) to all creatures within 10 feet.

details.description: A hair trigger rings an alarm and launches a small assortment of wooden spikes to impale anyone attempting to open the gate incorrectly.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15] (trained) to disable the thin trigger that snaps when attempting to open the gate.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to open the gate

Effect A loud alarm rings and set of wooden spikes swings up from below and over the gate, dealing @Damage[3d8[piercing]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save). If the creature takes damage from the spikes, they also become Grabbed by the gate. The creature can attempt to [[/act escape dc=17]]. If the creature succeeded at their basic Reflex save, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to their checks to Escape, as the spikes didn't fully impale them. If the creature critically failed their basic Reflex save, they take a –2 circumstance penalty to their checks to Escape, as the spikes are embedded particularly deep.

details.description: A large pile of treasure trembles precariously, threatening to collapse down into a crashing wave of coins and fine objects.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:16] (trained) to remove the right pieces from the treasure pile and induce a controlled collapse.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 5 feet of the treasure pile

Effect Treasure slides down the side of the triggering creature in a large wave. All creatures within 15 feet of the falling treasure must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:16] save as an onslaught of treasures bears down on them, dealing @Damage[(2d6+5)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is trapped in treasure, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until they @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] the collapsed treasure (DC 16).

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is buried in treasure, requiring another creature to dig them out of the treasure before they can Escape.

details.description: Barbed wire stretches across the passageway at the perfect height to tear into ankles.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17] (trained) to detach the wire

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into the trip wire

Effect The trip wire snaps free and coils around limbs. The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 hour.

details.description: Fire engulfs four 5-foot-by-5-foot sections of the library, then spreads erratically on each of its turns as burning books are carried about by the modified unseen servant.
details.disable: A successful @Check[athletics|dc:15], @Check[nature|dc:15], or @Check[survival|dc:15] check from an adjacent square is sufficient to smother one 5-foot- square of fire; each attempt is an Interact action. Dousing the flames automatically extinguishes one or more sections of fire, with no check. Water typically clears a 5-foot square if the amount is small (such as that from @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Create Water] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Hydraulic Push]). Larger amounts of water, such as a full bucket, typically douse a 10-foot-by-10-foot area (or 4 squares in some other shape). Throwing a bucket of water on flames requires an Interact action. A waterskin doesn't contain enough water to put out even 1 square of fire. Cold can also put out fire, but only if the cold can affect an area; cold is usually less effective than water, so a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Frost Vial (Lesser)]{Frost Vial} typically puts out only 1 square of fire, and ray of frost is ineffective.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-16: Dacilane Academy's First Great Prank War

details.routine: On its turn, the fire spreads into a number of additional squares equal to half the number of squares the fire currently occupies plus the number of books that the lurkers in light lit on fire this round, minimum 1 square. You determine the squares the fire spreads into, but any spread from lit books must affect a bookshelf; fires spread in this way do not need to spread into adjacent squares. Any creature that ends its turn next to the flames takes @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage, and any creature within the flames takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:15|basic] save for both). A creature can take damage from flames only once per round.

details.description: A ceramic urn containing a coiled viper tips and shatters when a tripwire is pulled.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to stabilize the urn, or @Check[athletics|dc:15] to carefully lower the heavy urn

description.value: When someone pulls the trip wire, the urn smashes against a creature in the doorway, making a shattering urn Strike against that creature, then releases a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Viper] into the square that contained the trap. The viper attacks any non-plant creature it detects.

details.description: Ornate full-length mirrors with fiendish filigree depict the image of Fasiel ibn Sazadin in villainous triumph over his enemies.
details.disable: @Check[stealth|dc:21] (trained) to approach a mirror without showing up in the reflection, followed by @Check[thievery|dc:19] to cover mirror or dispel magic (2nd level; counteract DC 19) to counteract the binding

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-14: Shattering Golden Chains

description.value:
Trigger The mirror breaks

Effect The mirror releases a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Raktavarna] rakshasa. The raktavarna rolls initiative and remains for 3 rounds, after which the spell ends and the rakshasa disappears. The summoned raktavarna can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

Trigger Fasiel is within 100 feet, this is the closest intact wicked reflections hazard, and a hostile creature's action would cause Fasiel to gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused], controlled, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed], petrified, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained], slowed, stunned, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] condition or would deal 20+ damage to Fasiel

Effect The mirror breaks and disrupts the action.

details.description: The wind grows fierce as a small planar fissure opens, blowing around leaves and creatures alike.
details.disable: @Check[type:arcana,nature,thievery|dc:15] (trained) two times to counter the planar energies.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #22: Friends in Need

details.routine:
(3 actions) On its turn each round, the wind begins blowing fiercely from a random direction. All creatures must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:15] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is moved 5 feet in the direction the wind is blowing.

Failure The creature is moved 10 feet in the direction the wind is blowing and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature is moved 10 feet in the direction the wind is blowing, is knocked prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.

description.value:
A non-elemental creature approaches within 20 feet

Effect All creatures within 20 feet must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:18] save; results are as if the hazard used its normal routine. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: The grove, broken and tormented due to the necromantic energy infecting the area it protected, manifests an illusion to warn others of the region's plight and plead for help. Pharasmin cleric and Pathfinder Seldrick Dralston trudges into the grove, injured and infected with some kind of evil corruption. He defiles the statue of Erastil in the center of the grove, and the golden light illuminating the grove ceases, revealing the black skies above. Purple magical energy emanating from a nearby tower seeps into the sacred grove, and the life within the grove's protection begins to die.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:18] (trained) or @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained) to convince the spirit of the wood the message has been understood

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-02: Return to the Grave

details.reset: The haunt returns every 10 minutes to retell its tale. The haunting of the sacred grove is permanently put to rest if the shrine is reconsecrated, which is impossible unless the evil forces in the nearby watchtower are defeated.

description.value:
Trigger At least 2 creatures step into the circle of trees

Effect The haunted grove creates a vision steeped in its despair to tell the tale of a cleric of Pharasma's fall to undead corruption and his subsequent desecration of the holy shrine, allowing necromantic energies to infest the grove. All creatures within the circle of trees must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature sees through the illusions entirely and is temporarily immune to the haunt for 1 hour.

Success The creature is unaffected by the strange images.

Failure The creature experiences a phantom pain from the Sacred Grove as it had suffered in the moment of Seldrick's fall. The haunt deals @Damage[2d6[mental]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is left with a lingering suspicion of others and can't benefit from Aid reactions for 24 hours.

details.description: A wooden crate bearing a chthonic sigil glows with a power contained within it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to open the crate with gradual release of its magical energy, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 15) to dispel the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the sigil on the crate

Effect The incomplete demonic pact causes the crate to explode. Bits of wood and energy from the Outer Rifts deal @Damage[(1d6+2)[bludgeoning],(1d6+2)[spirit]] damage to all creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10 feet} of it (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic]).

details.description: A complex device made of multicolored metal that resembles a combination of grinding gears, spider-like legs, and twitching blades, from which emits a constant soft ticking sound. All of the metal in the device appears to be rusted or corroded.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] to adjust the device's workings so they seize up and no longer function, or @Check[religion|dc:21] to focus prayers to a non-evil deity into the device to disrupt its ability to channel ambient faith
details.reset: The ancient apparatus reverts to its non-active mode and resets automatically at the end of any round in which there are no obvious targets to attack.
details.routine: (3 actions) The ancient apparatus emits beams of rust-colored light, spending an action to make ranged Strikes against three targets in area D3. The apparatus can target creatures as if it possessed darkvision, but has no ability to discern between friend or foe. It can't target a creature more than once, and if there are more than three targets, it selects its targets from those it can see in the room randomly. The ancient apparatus has only 1 action as long as its Hit Points are below its BT.

description.value:
Trigger The ancient apparatus takes damage or an attempt to disable it fails

Effect The ancient apparatus begins to grind and churn faster. Its soft ticking whirs up to a discordant buzzing sound, and the entire thing begins to glow with an unsettling rust-colored light. All living creatures in area D3 experience the unsettling taste of rust in their mouths and must succeed on a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}. The ancient apparatus then rolls initiative.

Those afflicted by rust creep develop uncomfortable rust-colored bruises on their flesh and endure full-body aches like those one might experience after a long workout. As the affliction progresses, their bodies—as well as the clothing and items they wear or carry—increasingly break down until a painful death occurs. If a character successfully resists contracting rust creep, or recovers from a case of rust creep, they are temporarily immune to future rust creep infections for 24 hours.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:15]

Stage 1 –1 status penalty to Athletics checks (1 day)

Stage 2 as stage 1 (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (1 day)

Stage 4 enfeebled 1 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1}, plus any armor, clothing and items you carry and that are of a level equal to or less than the disease become broken as the decay spreads to them (1 day; broken items remain broken)

Stage 5 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] (1 day)

Stage 6 unconscious (1 day)

Stage 7 death

details.description: The PCs appear on a gigantic wall of hooked chains dangling in a gray swirling mist. The chains radiate life-chilling energy Cries of innocence and condemnation echo through the mist in Elven. A plane of light 10 feet above eddies and swirls as it dully reflects the PCs below, but the reflections warp and alter the PCs to have sallow complexions like the undead.
details.disable: A PC that has ascended to the plane of light (@Check[athletics|dc:15] check to Climb; on a critical failure, the PC falls 10 feet before being seized by a chain) can attempt a @Check[thievery|dc:20] check to disrupt the plane or a @Check[arcana|dc:20], @Check[occultism|dc:20] or @Check[religion|dc:20] check to ritually disrupt the haunting. Each success reduces the haunt's actions by one. When the haunt has no more actions, it is disabled and the PCs all return to area B3.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-08: The Haunted Corridor

details.reset: The haunt resets the next time the Soulshackle traps creatures in the library.
details.routine: (three actions) The chains rattle and sigh ominously around two different random creatures trapped in the haunt, dealing @Damage[(2d6+2)[void]] damage (@Check[will|dc:18|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger Hunted by Chains is disabled

Effect The chains rattle ominously, dealing @Damage[(2d6+2)[void]] damage to all creatures in the haunt (@Check[will|dc:18|basic] save). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: Ceiling sprinklers spray in response to any fire in the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Neutralize Automatic Fire Suppression|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) to jam the sprinkler system or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:15|name:Disable Control Panel|traits:mechanical,trap]{Engineering Lore} (trained) on the control panel on the north wall to turn the system off.

description.value:
Trigger Fire damage is dealt in area A1

Effect Frozen fire retardant rains down on all creatures in area A1, dealing @Damage[(2d10+7)[cold]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic]). Any persistent fire damage in area A1 ends.

details.description: A large cylindrical metallic structure surrounded by dense cables and pipes, anointed with mystical runes which glow and hum vibrantly. Elemental energy arcs off three spikes that may have once been connected to some of the cables.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:18] (trained) to restart the machine's connection to an elemental plane, or @Check[thievery|dc:21] (trained) to find and deactivate the whichever rune is currently active; three total successes are required to disable the nexus. A spike can be disabled with a @Check[athletics|dc:21] (trained) check to rip it from the structure or a @Check[crafting|dc:18] (trained) check to reconnect some of the cables. If all 6 elemental energies are returned to the nexus, it is disabled.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-02: The Blackwood Lost

details.routine: (3 actions) The nexus spends each of its actions making an elemental wave Strike; each of these must be against a different creature who does not have the elemental trait. For each spike destroyed or disabled, the trap loses 1 action.

description.value:
Trigger A creature without the elemental trait approaches within 15 feet of the nexus or attacks an elemental within 15 feet of the nexus

Effect Unstable elemental energies surrounding the machine discharge abruptly from one of the spikes, sending waves of wind, gusts of metallic shards, jets of fire and other elemental bursts across the room in various directions. The hazard makes an elemental wave Strike against the triggering creature. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Trigger The nexus is broken or destroyed

Effect A ferocious gush of elemental energies bursts from the broken machine, dealing @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature within @Template[emanation|distance:15]{15 feet} (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic|options:area-effect] save). The trap is then disabled.

The damage of this Strike varies; no multiple attack penalty. Roll [[/gmr 1d6 #Damage Type]] for damage type.

1 cold (air)

2 bludgeoning (earth)

3 fire

4 electric (metal)

5 piercing (water)

6 slashing (wood)

stealth.details: (trained); DC 0 to notice the mirror without noting it is a trap

details.description: A dark shape moves in the surface of an ornate mirror, its eyes wide as blood seeps down its tormented face.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:16] (trained) to fully cover the mirror or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to dispel the mirror.
details.reset: 1 minute if no fresh blood has been spilled.
details.routine:
(2 actions; arcane, mental) The bloody figure in the mirror transforms to reflect a creature within 30 feet. The reflection bleeds profusely from the eyes, nose, and mouth, and the reflected creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18|traits:arcane,mental] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature bleeds from their mouth or nose.

Failure The creature bleeds from their eyes, mouth, and nose, and takes @Damage[1d10[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

description.value:
Trigger A creature spills blood in the room

Effect A ghastly figure of blood appears in the mirror's surface, forcing all creatures who can see it to attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature bleeds from their nose and mouth.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

details.description: Deceased Puddles residents lash out at living creatures.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:15] (trained) three times to banish the apparitions with prayers, or @Check[occultism|dc:18] (trained) once to commune with the spirits and convince them to pass on

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-04: An Enkindled Carnival

details.reset: The haunt re-forms after 1 hour. The haunt becomes disabled and doesn't reform 1 minute after the Puddles phantom is slain.
details.routine: (1 action) The haunt's screams send biting supernatural rain around the area, dealing @Damage[(2d6+2)[piercing]] damage to all living creatures within 90 feet (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save). Creatures who fail the save are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] by a red haze for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature comes within 30 feet of the haunt

Effect The haunt wails, cursing the living for allowing the Puddles residents to wallow in misery while Absalom's elites prosper. Each living creature within 90 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is temporarily immune to this bloodthirsty apparition's routine for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature shares the apparition's uncontrolled rage against the living. It makes a Strike against itself and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2}.

details.description: Four pressure plates beneath the floorboards activate dragon-headed fountains on either side of the room, which spray boiling water across the area between them.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Disable Pressure Plate|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to disable each pressure plate, @Check[thievery|dc:16|name:Jam Fountain|traits:action:disable-a-device] to jam each fountain, or @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Deflect Fountain's Spray|traits:action:disable-a-device] to deflect a fountain's spray

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

details.reset: Once a pressure plate has been activated, it must be reset manually.

description.value:
Trigger Weight is placed on one of the squares marked with "T" on the map, triggering a pressure plate

Effect The fountains spray boiling water across their own squares and the 10-foot-by-20-foot area between them (marked with dotted lines), dealing @Damage[(4d6+4)[fire]|options:area-damage] damage to any creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect] save).

details.description: This unassuming fungus leeches heat out of the air.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18|name:Remove Brown Mold|traits:environmental,fungus] (trained) to safely remove the mold
details.reset: After expanding, the brown mold can't grow again for 1 day.

description.value: 5 feet. Brown mold deals @Damage[2d6[cold]] damage to nearby creatures.

Trigger Fire comes within 5 feet of the brown mold.

Effect The brown mold expands into every square adjacent to its space. As it grows, it pulls more heat from its surroundings, dealing @Damage[(2d6+6)[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic] save) to creatures within 10 feet after it expands.

details.description: Combustible material can ignite and burn ferociously.
details.disable: @Check[mining-lore|dc:15] (trained) to spread inert material over the area or @Check[survival|dc:18] to pry up enough of the material to prevent a chain reaction.

description.value:
Trigger An effect in the area deals at least @Damage[8[electricity]] or @Damage[4[fire]]

Effect The coal residue rapidly ignites, dealing @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save). The area burns for 1 minute, during which creatures in the area are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed], other creatures are concealed to them, and creatures who end their turn in the area take @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage.

details.description: Around the edges of the pit, more rotting boards are ready to give way.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:16|name:Reinforce Beams] to reinforce the beams

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-01: The Absalom Initiation

details.reset: As the damage to the floor spreads, the areas adjacent to the new pit become new areas of this hazard.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks next to the pit.

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes falling damage (typically @Damage[3[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage) plus @Damage[4d6[piercing]] damage from landing on broken boards.

That creature can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling; the DC to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Climb] the walls or Grab an Edge is 18.

details.description: A hempen rope tied between trees emanates an invisible, cylindrical barrier that sounds an alarm when crossed.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to move the rope without setting of the alarm, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 16) to disable the rope's magic
details.reset: The alarm resets immediately. Multiple breaches can cause the alarm to sound in multiple places at the same time, though the alarm can still be triggered only once per round.

description.value:
Trigger An object or creature of at least 1 Bulk crosses an invisible vertical barrier extending 30 feet above and below the rope

Effect The rope squawks like an angry squirrel from the point where the barrier was breached. This alarm is as loud as a human scream and lasts for 1 minute. If an item or creature is still breaching the barrier at the end of this duration, the alarm immediately triggers again.

stealth.details: (or 0 if the trapdoor is disabled or broken)

details.description: A wooden trapdoor conceals a spike-filled pit 10 feet square and 20 feet deep. The DC to Climb out of the pit is @Check[athletics|dc:10|traits:action:climb].
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to jam the trapdoor open

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps onto the trapdoor (marked "T" on the map)

Effect The triggering creature falls into the pit. The creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling. Failing that, the creature falls 20 feet (taking @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] from the fall) and takes @Damage[3d6[piercing]] from the metal spikes jutting up from the pit's floor. A creature who takes damage from the spikes is exposed to tetanus.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:14]

Onset 10 days

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} (1 week)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} and can't speak (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] with spasms (1 day)

Stage 4 death

details.description: The crowd is riled up enough to start throwing garbage at the interlopers, but not riled up enough to risk their lives. The crowd fills nine contiguous squares but can be shaped to fit the streets. Characters can move through the crowd, but spaces occupied by the crowd are difficult terrain.
details.disable: After more people in the crowd have become shaken or taken damage than there are crowd leaders left standing, the crowd disperses. For abilities affecting an area, count each affected square as one member of the crowd.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-01: The Absalom Initiation

details.reset: After more people in the crowd have become shaken or taken damage than there are crowd leaders left standing, the crowd disperses. For abilities affecting an area, count each affected square as one member of the crowd.
details.routine: (3 actions) The crowd uses its first action to Stride away from any opponent adjacent to them (or to get within 30 feet), then uses each remaining action to make a trash Strike at a different target.

description.value: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (@Check[fortitude|dc:18] save negates sickened).

details.description: The crowd rushes forward, risking a stampede as everyone tries to fnd a safe exit.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:15] to calm the crowd, or @Check[intimidation|dc:18] or @Check[society|dc:18] to control the crowd's behavior

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-01: Intro to the Year of Immortal Influence

details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the crowd moves 5 feet toward the exit in area A1, then crushes any creature in a @Template[emanation|distance:10], dealing @Damage[(2d6+2)[bludgeoning]] damage. All targets must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save.

Failure The target takes full damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The target takes double damage, becomes prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger Lady Embersplitter announces that the victim has died

Effect The crowd begins moving around the bailey and deals @Damage[(2d6+2)[bludgeoning]] damage in a @Template[emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save), and then rolls initiative.

details.description: The 30-foot-long, 10-foot-wide bridge groans under the weight of those who cross it.

description.value:
Trigger A Medium or larger creature Strides 10 feet or more in the bridge's area

Effect All creatures on the bridge must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} until the beginning of its next turn.

Failure The creature falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature falls from the bridge, landing in the ravine 20 feet below.

Each time the decrepit bridge triggers its Perilous Sway after the first, attempt a @Check[flat|dc:5]. On a failure, the bridge collapses. All creatures on the bridge fall 20 feet into the ravine, taking falling damage normally (@Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage).

details.description: A 30-foot-wide rope bridge spans a 30-foot‑deep chasm between two crags on the mountainside.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:15] or @Check[thievery|dc:18] to secure the bridge in place.
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger One Large, two Medium, or three Small creatures move onto the rope bridge

Effect The bridge collapses and creatures on the bridge fall 30 feet, taking @Damage[15[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage. The triggering creatures can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling.

details.description: The wall bulges out in the shape of a howling humanoid face as it twists and forms tendrils reaching for nearby creatures. This haunt is formed when a murder victim is entombed within a wall before or after their death.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:17] (trained) to weaken the spirit, @Check[athletics|dc:18] (trained) to force the spirit's face back inside the wall, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:19] (trained) to talk down the spirit; two total successes across all skills are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area and resets immediately thereafter. If disabled, the haunt resets after 1 day. The haunt is permanently destroyed if the remains of the deceased are removed from the wall.
details.routine: (3 actions) The spirit attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple] up to two creatures within 10 feet with a [[/r 1d20+14 #Athletics]]{+14 Athletics modifier}, then squeezes each creature it has @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed], dealing @Damage[(1d10+4)[untyped]] damage. The haunt doesn't apply a multiple attack penalty to the second attempt to Grapple.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the wall where the corpse is entombed

Effect The spirit gasps and wails for aid while the wall takes on the shape of its face and roils, growing tendrils. All creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10 feet} must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:18] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} for as long as the haunt remains active. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: A trip wire strung 4 feet above the ground releases a branch studded with envenomed thorns.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Remove Trip Wire] (trained) to remove the trip wire.

publication.title: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On

description.value:
Trigger The trip wire is touched

Effect The trap's poisoned thorns attack the creature or object touching the trip wire.

details.description: A hollow statue filled with explosives is connected to a trip wire running along the adjacent door.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Safely Cut Wire|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to safely cut the wire

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the northern door without first disabling the trip wire

Effect The statue explodes, spraying shrapnel to the west in a @Template[cone|distance:5] and dealing @Damage[(1d10+7)[piercing],1d10[fire]]{1d10+7 piercing and 1d10 fire} damage to all creatures in the affected area (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save).

details.description: Wooden barrels marked with an oil-drop symbol catch fire and explode.
