details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20|name:Smother the Flames] to smother the flames without rupturing the barrels

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

description.value:
Trigger 4 rounds pass after the agents' arrival without anyone smothering them

Effect The barrels explode, dealing @Damage[(3d6+8)[fire]] damage to everything within a 20-foot radius (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|traits:fire] save).

details.description: A flawed ritual emits surges of shadowy energy and opens tiny breaches into the Shadow Plane.
details.disable:
Seven flaws have appeared in the ritual circle, dancing like shadowy flames. Each of Tavvar's three aides keeps one flaw under control as long as they are alive, while Tavvar herself staves off the ritual's implosion as listed in the ritual anchor ability below.

Each flaw can be repaired with a successful @Check[occultism|dc:22] or DC 18 (trained) check using a skill related to the flaw's original cause (see the table below). Regardless of success or failure, a PC who attempts an Occultism check to disable the hazard learns the cause of the flaws and the skills that can be used to overcome it.

Each success on a check to repair a flaw using these other skills reduces the DC of the Occultism check to repair a flaw by 2. Instead of using skill checks, the PCs can use force to stabilize the ritual. A PC who defeats a shadow wisp automatically repairs one flaw.

d4 Cause of Flaw Skills to Overcome
1 Harried (a "publish or perish" culture at the College of Mystery led to Tavvar cutting corners in the ritual's execution, so some runes aren't drawn quite right) @Check[arcana|dc:18] (trained) or @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained)
2 Misplaced Affection (Tavvar is from Nidal and subconsciously hopes the ritual will show that there is some good in her homeland; she included some Shadowtongue in the runes and it's enhancing the energy @Check[society|dc:18] (trained) or @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained)
3 Cloistered (Tavvar hasn't done much field work and made a dangerous mistake in which mushrooms she used in the ink for the runes) @Check[crafting|dc:18] (trained) or @Check[nature|dc:18] (trained)
4 Restrained (Tavvar's own hesitation and self-doubt has led to the ritual slipping out of her control) @Check[deception|dc:18] (trained) or @Check[diplomacy|dc:18] (trained)

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-01: The Absalom Initiation

details.routine:
(1 action) On each of its turns, the ritual lets out a surge of shadowy energy, dealing @Damage[2d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic] save) to each character who participated in ritual the past round (including Tavvar and her aides as well as any PC who attempted a skill check to disable the hazard). A non-participant can step in front of a participant as a reaction. This blocks the damage from the ritual caster, but the non-participant must then attempt the Fortitude save.

In addition to dealing damage, the ritual's surge of energy brings a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pfs-season-1-bestiary.Actor.Shadow Wisp] into the room. Wisps act at the end of the ritual's turn. If there are already two shadow wisps in the room, instead of creating a new wisp, the ritual restores @Damage[10[healing]] Hit Points to each wisp.

description.value: If Tavvar dies, the ritual immediately ends disastrously. With a loud pop, it explodes, filling the room with void energy before dissipating. This deals @Damage[2d6[void]] damage to all creatures in area B5 (@Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic|name:Flawed Ritual] save). The NPCs ritualists automatically critically fail their saving throws. The explosion ends the ritual but does not remove any @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pfs-season-1-bestiary.Actor.Shadow Wisp]{Shadow Wisps} that are already in the room.

details.description: A surge of abyssal energy creates a powerful wave pushing away from the monolith.
details.disable:
@Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[occultism|dc:18], or @Check[religion|dc:18] to magically alter the runes, or @Check[thievery|dc:21] to physically alter them. A PC that can read Abyssal or Cyclops is better able to identify key runes, gaining a +1 circumstance bonus to these check (+2 if they speak both).

Each check requires two actions, and disabling the monolith requires three successful checks. A PC that critically succeeds earns two success. On a failed check, the attempting PC is exposed to the burning flames wreathing the monolith, taking @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic|name:Flooding Monolith] save).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-14: Lost in Flames

details.routine:
(1 action) On the hazard's turn it sends a wave of water along the fork of the river (North, South, or East) that contains the nearest PC. Any creature along that fork in or on the surface of the water must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|name:Flooding Monolith] save.

The monolith's guardians are accustomed to these surges and are immune to this effect.

Critical Success The creature takes advantage of the undertow after the surge to reposition themselves. The creature can Step, ignoring difficult terrain caused by the water.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is pushed 10 feet away from the monolith.

Critical Failure The creature is pushed 15 feet away from the monolith and knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than the monolith's guardians approaches within 40 feet of the monolith

Effect the flames wreathing the monolith flare with incandescent light. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save.

On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for one round.

On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for one round, then @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for one round.

The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: Ghostly food appears on cracked plates as they float above the table. The faint outline of children can occasionally be seen as the ectoplasmic food is flung throughout the room.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:17] (trained) to weaken the spiritual energy in the room, @Check[acrobatics|dc:18] (trained) to pluck a floating plate out of the air, or @Check[intimidation|dc:19] (trained) to reprimand one of the ghostly children for their poor behavior; each successful check disables one of four plates
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the room or after all four plates are destroyed. After 1 hour, if at least one plate remains, the haunt reactivates. If disabled, the haunt resets after 1 day and the plates magically reassemble. The haunt is permanently destroyed when the attic whisperer in area D5 is destroyed.
details.routine: (4 actions) For each plate disabled or destroyed, the haunt has 1 fewer action. On each action, spectral food is flung off a plate at a different living creature in the room. This ranged Strike doesn't provoke reactions. A creature critically hit by a flung food Strike is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the set table

Effect A loud phantom dinner bell rings before the plates begin to tremble and hover. Each creature in the room takes @Damage[(2d6+2)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic] save). The haunt then rolls initiative.
no range increment or multiple attack penalty

details.description: The floor is highly waxed and slippery.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:16] or @Check[thievery|dc:16] to remove the slippery coating or any ability that turns the area into difficult terrain.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-05: Silver Bark, Golden Blades

details.reset: Manual, reapply the wax.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into the greased area

Effect The triggering creature must make a successful @Check[acrobatics|dc:14] check to avoid falling @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. The area is uneven ground.

details.description: This small toy-like device has a hidden mechanism meant to be activated by the user, but the centuries have eroded its magical-mechanical components to the point that they activate at the slightest touch.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to deactivate the mechanism; @Check[crafting|dc:16] (trained) to identify and repair the problem components; two successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-13: All That Glitters

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without a creature being within 5' of the handheld quaker.
details.routine:
At its initiative, to determine what the quaker does, roll [[/gmr 1d4]] and consult the following table:

1: No action

2: A small quake: the terrain in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] centered on the quaker shakes briefly. A creature in an affected square is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] (@Check[reflex|dc:18] save).

3: A medium quake: the terrain within the vault shakes constantly, transforming it into difficult terrain until its next turn. A creature in an affected square is knocked prone and takes @Damage[1d10[bludgeoning]] damage from the fall (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save).

4: A great quake: the terrain within the vault moves in waves, transforming it into greater difficult terrain until its next turn. A creature in an affected square is violently slammed to the ground, knocked prone, and takes @Damage[(1d10+4)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into a space with the handheld quaker or interacts with it

Effect The handheld quaker vibrates visibly and rolls initiative.

A creature within difficult terrain created by the quaker takes a –2 circumstance penalty to Reflex saves and actions with the Manipulate trait, including @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Disable a Device]{Disable Device}.

details.description: The spirits of community members linger on after their tragic deaths.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:20] (untrained) to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Request] that the spirits leave or @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained) to exorcise the spirits
details.reset: The haunt falls silent for 24 hours once all frightened creatures die or reduce their frightened condition's value to 0. During this time, a creature that isn't frightened can exorcise the spirits with a @Check[religion|dc:18] check, permanently destroying the haunt.
details.routine:
(1 action; auditory, emotion, fear, mental) The haunt attempts an Intimidation check with a [[/gmr 1d20+11 #Intimidation]]{+11} modifier against the Will DC of each @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened] creature as it bombards them with the voices of their dead loved ones.

Critical Success The creature takes @Damage[2d4[mental]] damage and its frightened condition increases by 1, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d4[mental]] damage.

Failure The creature is unaffected.

Critical Failure The creature's frightened condition is reduced by 1.

description.value:
Trigger A creature the spirits knew in life enters the haunted area

Effect The spirits manifest with a terrible wailing. Each creature in the area must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:20] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). Frightened creatures are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] for as long as they remain frightened. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: A 15-foot wide area of verdant-covered water attempts to suck creatures down into it.
details.disable: Two @Check[survival|dc:18] (trained) checks to find roots and tree limbs sturdy enough for everyone to use as escape paths ends the hazard's threat to all creatures in the area

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-99: Blessings of the Forest

details.reset: The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it accumulates leaf and greenery cover over the course of 24 hours.
details.routine:
(one action) On its initiative, the bog pulls down each creature within it. A creature that was submerged to its waist becomes submerged to its neck, and a creature that was submerged up to its neck is pulled under and must hold its breath to avoid suffocation.

A creature partially or fully submerged in the bog can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:16|traits:action:swim] check to Swim to either raise itself by one step (pulled under to submerged to its neck, or submerged to its neck to submerged to its waist), or to escape from the bog if it's submerged only up to its waist. This DC increases to @Check[athletics|dc:18|traits:action:swim] for creatures submerged up to their neck or worse. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down one step. Other creatures can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid], typically by using a rope or similar assistance (providing an automatic success), or attempt to pull a single affected creature out with their own Athletics check, with the same results as if the aiding creature attempted the check itself.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area of the bog.

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the bog up to its waist. The bog rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

stealth.details: (trained) or DC 0 if the trapdoor is disabled or broken

details.description: A wooden trapdoor covers a pit that's 10 feet square and 20 feet deep. Knives are arrayed at the bottom of the trap, impaling anyone who falls on them.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to remove the trapdoor

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: A Few Flowers More

details.reset: Creatures can still fall into the trap, but the trapdoor must be reset manually for the trap to become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes falling damage (@Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage) as well as @Damage[2d6[piercing]] damage from falling on to the knives. That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

details.description: Pebbles form into a hand the size of an ox, which reaches to crush anyone in the water.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to pick apart the pebbles, or @Check[warfare-lore|dc:15] to recognize and recreate the battle

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: 24 hours

description.value:
Trigger Three or more creatures enter the submerged passage

Effect The hand reenacts its final battle against the invading demon. It makes a crushing power Strike against all creatures in the passage.

details.description: The sigils on the door's frame glow with an amber light, which collects in a facet over the center of the door.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:14] (trained) to strategically destroy the runes, @Check[thievery|dc:15] to disrupt the beam from collecting the facet, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank, counteract DC 15) to dispel the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: The hostile architecture resets after being exposed to the light of a blue moon.
details.routine: a The hostile architecture fires its aversion beam on a creature that is sanctified holy. If there are no sanctified holy creatures, it instead fires its beam on the nearest non-demon creature.

description.value:
Trigger A non-demon creature comes within 15 feet of the hostile architecture

Effect The hostile architecture shines the aversion beam on the triggering creature, and then rolls initiative. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The target is immune to the trap for 1 minute.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing].

details.description: Patches of darkness envelop the rickety beds in this room, from which can be heard the sounds of gentle snoring from children.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:18] (trained) to ward off the darkness or @Check[deception|dc:19] or @Check[performance|dc:19] (trained) to pretend to be asleep; two total success are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the room but resets immediately thereafter. If disabled, the haunt resets after 1 day. The haunt is permanently destroyed when the attic whisperer here is destroyed.
details.routine: (1 action; darkness, occult) The haunt randomly chooses 1 living creature that it hadn't engulfed in darkness the previous round. Supernatural darkness wells over that creature, who must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save. On a failure, that creature is engulfed in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] of darkness centered on them and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 round. Any creatures in the emanation must attempt their own Will save or become blinded as well. Any non-magical light sources and magical light of 1st rank or lower the primary creature is holding or wearing cease to give off light for that duration.

description.value:
Trigger At least two living creatures enter the room

Effect The sounds of a soothing lullaby fill the air. All living creatures in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature gets slightly drowsy, taking a –1 status penalty to Perception checks for 1 round.

Failure The creature briefly nods off. They are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} and can't use reactions for 1 round after that.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2}.

details.description: The magical lodestone discharges bolts of electricity that also magnetize metal in the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] to unhook the pressure plates from the trap, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank, counteract DC 18) to counteract the trap

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

details.reset: At the end of a round in which there are no creatures standing on the floor or stuck to the lodestone, the trap deactivates. The vargouilles return to their alcoves, and the doors close. The trap resets automatically after 1 minute.
details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the trap exerts a strong magnetic pull on the doors, causing the north and west doors to open if they're closed, or causing the south and east doors to close if they're open. A character can open or close a door against this pull with a successful @Check[athletics|dc:18] check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open] the door. At the same time, any character in the room who wears primarily metal armor must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save (characters in medium metal armor suffer a -1 circumstance penalty to this save, and characters in heavy metal armor suffer a -2 circumstance penalty).

Critical Success The PC is unaffected.

Success The PC resists the pull but treats the room as difficult terrain.

Failure The PC treats the room as difficult terrain and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. A PC who's already prone suffers a critical failure instead.

Critical Failure As failure, but the PC is also pulled 5 feet toward the lodestone. A PC who is adjacent to the lodestone instead becomes stuck to it and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] as long as the trap continues its routine ([[/act escape dc=18]]).

description.value:
Trigger A single Medium or two or more Small creatures end their turn inside the room.

Effect A pulse of electricity discharges from the lodestone to strike all four doors, with smaller arcs of electricity lancing out to strike all creatures in the room. The south and east doors slam shut, while the north and west doors fly open, releasing two vargouilles into the room. All creatures in the room take @Damage[2d6[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic]). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: The hypnotic ticking of this metronome captures the attention of all who approach it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|name:Interfere with the Metronome's Mechanisms|traits:magical,trap] or @Check[crafting|dc:15|name:Interfere with the Metronome's Mechanisms|traits:magical,trap] to interfere with the metronome's mechanisms, @Check[athletics|dc:18|name:Prevent the Hand from Moving|traits:magical,trap] to grip the hand and prevent it from moving, or @Check[arcana|dc:18|name:Interpret the Magic Runes|traits:magical,trap] or @Check[occultism|dc:18|name:Interpret the Magic Runes|traits:magical,trap] to interpret the magic runes on the metronome and counteract its effects. Three successful checks are required to fully disable the metronome, ending its ticking.

description.value:
Trigger A creature starts the metronome

Effect During the next 10 minutes, any creature who approaches within 30 feet of the metronome, other than the one who activated it, must attempt a @Check[will|dc:16|traits:magical,trap] save, with the following effects.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected by the metronome and acts normally.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for as long as it can hear the metronome and remains within 30 feet of it.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} and is fascinated for as long as it can hear the metronome and remains within 30 feet of it. While fascinated in this way, the creature can't willingly move more than 30 feet away from the metronome until it stops ticking. The creature is then @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued] for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The creature is slowed 1 and is fascinated for as long as it can hear the metronome and remains within 30 feet of it. While fascinated in this way, the creature can't willingly move more than 30 feet away from the metronome until it stops ticking. The creature is then fatigued for 2 hours.

details.description: Mechanical jaws bolted to the stone beneath a camouflaged floor tile.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:16] (trained) on the jaw mechanism.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-13: A Gilded Test

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks on the tile.

Effect The jaws of the trap snap shut, Striking the triggering creature. The triggering creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until the trap is disabled or a creature succeeds at a [[/act force-open dc=14]]{Athletics} check to Force it Open. A creature who critically fails the check to Force it Open instead tightens the trap, dealing @Damage[(2d6+6)[piercing]] damage to the immobilized creature.

details.description: This mushroom ring forms a portal to the First World, allowing the Thorned Monarch's reinforcements into the Material Plane. When the PCs approach, a creature emerges. Creatures continue to emerge until the mushroom ring is disabled. Disabling the mushroom ring also causes any creatures that have emerged to disappear.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) or @Check[nature|dc:18] to rearrange the mushrooms to disrupt the portal (two actions); three successes are required to disable the portal

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-00: The King in Thorns

details.routine: (1 action) A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Pugwampi] emerges from the mushroom ring and defends it. All pugwampis use the mushroom ring's initiative.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs approach the mushroom ring

Effect A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Pugwampi] emerges from the portal to defend it, and the hazard then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: Either DC 18 or 0 if the eyes covering an area that obscures a pitfall are destroyed.

details.description: A layer of disembodied eyes obscures a pitfall in the floor that's either deep enough for a human to fall into, or narrow enough to cause a painful fall.
details.disable: This hazard can't be disabled, merely avoided.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into a large pit's square;

Effect The triggering creature falls into the pit but can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling. On a failure, a creature falls 20 feet into an eye-filled pit. The eyes cushion the fall so the 20-foot fall inflicts only @Damage[5[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage, but they also completely obscure vision; a character is effectively blind as long as they remain in the pit. The walls of the pit are slick, though a character can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Climb] them with a successful @Check[athletics|dc:25] check.

Trigger A creature walks into a small pit's square;

Effect The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save or fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] (on a critical failure, the creature also twists its ankle and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} until it receives any amount of healing).

details.description: Funerary inscriptions flicker and display different messages, causing ghostly ancestors to briefly appear and complain that their descendants have forgotten them.
details.disable: three @Check[diplomacy|dc:20], @Check[occultism|dc:20], or @Check[religion|dc:20] checks to rebuke the cantankerous spirits
details.routine: (2 actions) Ancestral spirits appear throughout areas D1 and D2, each fading after several rounds to be replaced by a different spirit.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of area D2

Effect The haunt rolls initiative as the ancestral spirits begin manifesting.

A spirit yells at a living creature, which must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2[persistent,mental]] damage. Until the persistent damage ends, the creature can perceive ghostly Dwarven inscriptions floating several inches in front of the various inscriptions throughout the park.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature takes @Damage[4[persistent,mental]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} until the persistent damage ends.

details.description: A nest of bones resembling an ant hill contains countless miniscule scavenger vermin with vicious, acid-dripping mandibles.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:18|name:Topple the Mound] (trained) to topple the mound and destroy it
details.reset: The hazard deactivates if there are no targets for its acid spew at the start of its turn. The hatchlings return to the nest, and the hazard resets. If damaged but not destroyed, the nest recovers @Damage[1d6[healing]] Hit Points per hour as the hatchlings repair it.
details.routine: (1 action) The hazard makes an acid spew Strike against the nearest creature.

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 15 feet of the nest

Effect A mass of skittering ostovite hatchlings crawls all over the nest, spewing acid in all directions. Each creature within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} of the nest takes @Damage[(1d10+4)[acid]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic]). A creature who fails the save also takes @Damage[1[persistent,acid]] damage. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: A menacing clockwork contraption emerges from this impressively large and heavy-duty oaken barrel. A spring-loaded launcher mechanism catapults explosive-laden rats, and nozzles spray flammable oil before igniting it.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:18] or @Check[thievery|dc:18] check to jam the mechanism of one of the spray nozzles. Every success disables one of the three oil nozzles, reducing the hazards number of actions by 1. After all three of the oil nozzles are disabled, there is no longer any oil surrounding the barrel for the ignition mechanism to burn, so the hazard can't deal damage. The ignition mechanism can be disabled with another @Check[crafting|dc:18] or @Check[thievery|dc:18] check. Each attempt to disable the hazard requires 2 actions.
details.routine: Routine (4 actions) Three nozzles located around the device spew oil, which coats objects and floats on water. As the nozzles fluctuate in pressure, they cover everything nearby in oil, then ignite the oil with built-in spark lighters. The hazard's first action spreads oil out to 5 feet, the second action spreads it out to 10 feet, and the third spreads it out to 20 feet. Creatures in the area can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save to avoid being covered in oil. Once the oil has spread, the spark plug activates.

description.value:
Trigger The lid of the barrel is removed;

Effect Removing the lid breaks a thin lever that initiates a chain reaction. The hoops snap, and the barrel's staves fall open like the petals of a flower. Four explosive rats are catapulted out in all directions, and the hazard rolls initiative.

The spark plug creates a fire that rapidly burns the oil-covered creatures and objects surrounding the barrel, dealing @Damage[(1d6+4)[fire]] damage and @Damage[1d4[persistent,fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save).

After catching fire, areas and creatures are no longer covered in oil.

details.description: A burst of pale gray spores sprays into the air.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18] to remove the mold without triggering the spores

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

description.value:
Trigger The moldy books are moved or the mold takes any damage;

Effect All creatures within 10 feet of the mold patch are exposed to phantom mold spores.

Any penalties to Perception from this poison persist for 1 hour after the poison's duration ends (this effect can be removed before then by any effect that neutralizes poison);

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:18];

Maximum Duration 4 rounds;

Stage 1 @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage;

Stage 2 @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage, and the victim takes a -1 status penalty on visual Perception checks as strange shadows and flittering movement appear in their peripheral vision;

Stage 3 as stage 2, but a -2 status penalty.

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 5-foot square surrounds an invisible rune on the wall or floor.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:18|name:Approach the Rune] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Erase the Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank, counteract DC 18) to counteract the rune.
details.reset: Like its namesake creature, a phoenix rune is difficult to destroy. After it is triggered, a phoenix rune can immediately reset on a wall or floor within 120 feet, as long as it has not been disabled. When this happens, a PC who succeeds at a @Check[perception|dc:16|name:Spot the Phoenix Rune] check spots the shadowy form of a tiny phoenix "flying" along the wall or floor from the rune's old location to its new location.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the sensor area or fails any check to disable the phoenix rune.

Effect A white-hot talon of flame bursts upward from the phoenix rune, dealing @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage to a creature within the sensor area. This gout of fire is accompanied by the screech of a phoenix audible up to 500 feet away. Creatures within a 20-foot radius of the sensor area (including the triggering creature) must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:16|name:Screeching Talon Deafened|traits:auditory] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 round.

details.description: A pile of volatile fireworks.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:18|name:Remove Lit Firework] (trained), @Check[engineering-lore|dc:16|name:Remove Lit Firework] (trained), or @Check[explosives-lore|dc:16|name:Remove Lit Firework] (trained) to safely remove the lit firework or @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Remove Lit Fuse|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to remove the lit fuse from the firework.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #5: The Dragon who Stole Evoking Day

description.value:
Trigger The pile of fireworks takes acid or fire damage.

Effect The fuse of one of the fireworks begins to burn and the pile of fireworks enters initiative immediately before the triggering creature or effect.

Trigger The pile of fireworks begins its turn.

Effect The pile of fireworks explodes, dealing @Damage[4d8[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic]) save to all creatures within 15 feet.

details.description: The spout trickling blood, sludge, and offal begins to draw upon your life energy, increasing the flow and spreading an even fouler smell within the chamber.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:17] or @Check[religion|dc:17] (trained) to counteract the fell energies drawing in life force to power this strange charnel flow or @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to disable the spout, stopping the flow at its source.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #15: In the Footsteps of Horror

details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger: A creature moves adjacent to the pit

Effect The remnants of a soul cage ritual draw upon the life energy of all living creatures in area A4 as well as the 10-foot hallway leading to the room. Each living creature in those areas must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic] save as the flow of vile liquid saps their very essence. The pull is more intense on more powerful creatures, dealing @Damage[1d6[void]] damage per level the creature has.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage. Additionally, they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} and cannot reduce the condition's value for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage. Additionally, they are sickened 1 and cannot reduce the condition's value until they leave the Nether Cathedral.

details.description: As ghostly, ectoplasm-dripping figures of dwarves work all around, the body of a living creature within the pit begins to ache with the pains of countless hours of back-breaking toil.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:21] (expert) to shame the pit by miming skilled artistry, or @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained) to exorcise the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

details.routine:
(1 action; divine, necromancy) As the dwarves continue to chant and grasp at the living, the pit infuses all living creatures inside it with the ache and pain a body endures after hours of constant toil. Each living creature in the pit must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|traits:divine,necromancy,damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature suffers no ill effect and is thereafter immune to the pit of toil's routine.

Success The creature suffers no ill effect.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d6[void]] damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[4d6[void]] damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued].

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the pit

Effect The surrounding dwarven shapes begin to chant in Dwarven, encouraging newcomers to join them as they grasp for them with their cold, incorporeal hands. The pit of toil rolls initiative.

details.description: This grotesque fungus releases poisonous spores when a creature comes near.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18] (trained) to safely remove the mold
details.reset: Unless entirely eradicated (typically with acid or fire), the mold regrows over the course of 2 weeks.

description.value: The mold expands when fed by blood or decay. If at any point a creature takes bleed damage while adjacent to the mold or a dying or dead body is adjacent to the mold, the mold grows to expand into every square adjacent to its current space. This can happen only once per day.

Frequency once per day

Trigger A creature touches the mold, hits it, or damages it

Effect The mold explodes in a burst of spores and mold clusters, dealing @Damage[(2d6+6)[poison]] damage to creatures within 10 feet of it, with a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic] save.
5 feet. A creature that enters or starts its turn in the aura takes @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage.

details.description: A cave painting of a great cat roars to life and leaps off the wall to attack.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:18|name:Quell the Spirit|traits:hazard,haunt] (trained) or @Check[religion|dc:18|name:Quell the Spirit|traits:hazard,haunt] (trained) to quell the spirit
details.reset: If the cave painting isn't destroyed, the haunt resets after 1 day.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 5 feet of the cave painting

Effect The haunt deals @Damage[4d8[slashing]] damage with semisolid claws (@Check[will|dc:18|basic]). On a failed save, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for 1 round (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and fleeing for 1 minute on a critical failure).

details.description: This dangerously unsteady pile of miscellaneous goods stands 7 feet high and 10 feet wide
details.disable:
@Check[thievery|dc:15|name:Excavate Safe Path|traits:action:disable-a-device] to carefully excavate a path through the wall without upsetting its balance

@Check[athletics|dc:17|name:Hold Up Unstable Portions] to hold up any unstable portions

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Little Trouble in Big Absalom

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to climb or remove an object from the wall or the wall suffers a forceful impact

Effect The wall collapses, dealing @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning]] damage to creatures on it or within 10 feet of it. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:18|traits:damaging-effect] save takes half damage and rolls out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, they take no damage and can choose the direction.

details.description: A crowd of carnival goers are spurred to panic by supernatural rain.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:17] (trained) to calm them down, @Check[intimidation|dc:18] (trained) to cow them, @Check[athletics|dc:20] (trained) to force them back

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-04: An Enkindled Carnival

description.value:
Trigger The PCs fail to overcome a task during the Dreaded Downpour event

Effect The crowd surges forward, dealing @Damage[(2d10+7)[bludgeoning]] damage to each PC. Each affected creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and takes a –1 circumstance penalty to their next skill check to overcome a task during the Dreaded Downpour event.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to their next skill check to overcome a task during the Dreaded Downpour event.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Surge Forward]

details.description: The room shakes violently, as it did during the Rending, and the spirits of dozens of dwarves make a mad dash for safety, their terror palpable.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:18|name:Calm the Spirits|traits:unique,complex,haunt], @Check[intimidation|dc:18|name:Calm the Spirits|traits:unique,complex,haunt], or @Check[religion|dc:18|name:Calm the Spirits|traits:unique,complex,haunt] to calm the panicked spirits, or @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Wrestle Spirits into Order|traits:unique,complex,haunt] to wrestle them into a semblance of order. Two successful checks are required to deactivate the haunt, and each attempt requires two actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-19: Enter the Pallid Peak

details.reset: Unless deactivated, the haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter. If deactivated, the haunt resets after 24 hours.
details.routine:
(1 action) Phantom dwarves trample toward the exit, dealing @Damage[(1d10+4)[bludgeoning]] damage to each living creature in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|name:Trampled by Spirits|traits:complex,haunt,unique] save). On a critical failure, a creature is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

A creature that attempts to Stand after being inside the haunt's area must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:11] check or else get knocked back down by the trampling spirits.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the lava

Effect The room rumbles and a blast of heat hits each creature in the area, followed by a surge of fear. The creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save, and the haunt rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is frightened 2 and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for 1 round.

stealth.details: (trained); @Check[perception|dc:19] (expert) to notice the water tank bulging under the immense pressure

details.description: Pipes extending from a pressurized water tank spray flesh-cutting jets of water at unwary passersby.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to safely puncture a pipe, or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:16]{Engineering Lore} (trained) to find and turn a pressure release valve; any combination of three punctured pipes or turned valves disables the trap
details.reset: The trap deactivates after 5 rounds. It cannot be reactivated until the reservoir is refilled, which takes an average of 5 days.
details.routine: (3 actions) For each pipe destroyed or disabled, the trap loses 1 action. On each of the trap's actions, the trap targets a random creature in the room with a water jet Strike.

description.value: Pipe Hardness 7, Pipe HP 30 (BT 15)

Trigger A creature steps on a floor space in this room

Effect Seals on the water pipes burst, issuing forth high-pressure water jets in random directions. The hazard makes a water jet Strike against the triggering creature. The hazard rolls initiative.

Trigger The water tank is broken or destroyed

Effect A torrent of water bursts from the shattered tank, dealing @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature within @Template[emanation|distance:15]{15 feet} (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect] save). The trap is then disabled.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (trained) to detect the change in pressure

details.description: A raging wind sucks creatures in the area toward the aiudara.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to recognize what's happening and craft a patch to block air from escaping through the portal
details.routine:
(1 action) The hazard uses its action to suck each creature (except the cairn wight) toward the aiudara. Each affected creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is pulled 5 feet toward the aiudara and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}. If the creature is already adjacent to the aiudara, it doesn't move but is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is pulled 10 feet toward the aiudara, falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], and is stunned 1. If the creature is already adjacent to the aiudara, it doesn't move but falls prone and is stunned 2.

description.value:
Trigger The aiudara appears here on Eox

Effect A jet of wind pulls creatures caught between the worlds toward the archway. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: Shards of rust extend from the door and crumble away to cling to the flesh and clothes of anyone who attempts to open or damage the door.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] to deface the hidden runes that allow the trap to activate, or @Check[religion|dc:21] to offer prayers to a non-evil deity to override the ancient influence of Xar-Azmak
details.reset: The rusted door resets automatically after 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A non-worshipper of Xar-Azmak attempts to open the door or causes damage to it with a melee Strike

Effect The triggering creature is exposed to rust creep as tendrils of rust-red energy seem to slither out of the rusty patches on the door to rasp strangely gently against exposed skin.

Those afflicted by rust creep develop uncomfortable rust-colored bruises on their flesh and endure full-body aches like those one might experience after a long workout. As the affliction progresses, their bodies—as well as the clothing and items they wear or carry—increasingly break down until a painful death occurs. If a character successfully resists contracting rust creep, or recovers from a case of rust creep, they are temporarily immune to future rust creep infections for 24 hours.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:15]

Stage 1 –1 status penalty to Athletics checks (1 day)

Stage 2 as stage 1 (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (1 day)

Stage 4 enfeebled 1 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1}, plus any armor, clothing and items you carry and that are of a level equal to or less than the disease become broken as the decay spreads to them (1 day; broken items remain broken)

Stage 5 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] (1 day)

Stage 6 unconscious (1 day)

Stage 7 death

details.description: Pressure plates on the floor near the north door cause rusty spikes to lance up out of the floor in area C3.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to disable the pressure plates.
details.reset: Turn the resetting wheel in the eastern Inner Guard Station (area C8b).

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the pressure plate

Effect The spikes spring up, Striking all creatures in the hallway. The floor becomes greater difficult terrain.

An infection introduced through open wounds, tetanus can produce stiffness, muscle spasms strong enough to break bones, and ultimately death.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:14]

Onset 10 days

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} (1 week)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} and can't speak (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] with spasms (1 day)

Stage 4 death

stealth.details: (trained) to hear the scratching beneath the floorboards

details.description: Illusory hands tear from the floorboards, clawing at their victim's legs and leaving real wounds.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:16|name:Exorcize the Spirits] (trained) to exorcize the spirits

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-01: The Absalom Initiation

details.reset: The underlying spirits remain, resetting the haunt every midnight, until the true story of what happened here becomes public.

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes through area C2 for the second time in 24 hours.

Effect Phantasmal rotting hands burst out from the floor under the target, tearing into their legs. The hands' appearance varies based on the reason for the haunting (see the table below).

The hands deal @Damage[4d6[slashing]] damage and @Damage[2d6[bleed]] (@Check[will|dc:17|basic] save). If a creature takes any damage from the haunt, the claws leave rending wounds on the target's legs, reducing their land Speed by 10 feet (minimum 5 feet) until the target is restored to its maximum HP.

Owners Haunt Manifestations
Archdevil Worshippers Scent of brimstone and sound of chanting in Infernal (a PC who understands Infernal or succeeds at a @Check[religion|dc:10|name:Recognize Chanting], @Check[hell-lore|dc:10|name:Recognize Chanting]{Hell Lore}, or @Check[mephistopheles-lore|dc:10|name:Recognize Chanting]{Mephistopheles Lore} check recognizes the chanting as a prayer to Mephistopheles)
Soulbound Dollmakers Flesh flaking off the rotting hands reveals smooth porcelain underneath (a PC who succeeds at a @Check[crafting|dc:10|name:Recognize Craftsmanship] or @Check[occultism|dc:10|name:Recognize Craftsmanship] check recognizes the craftsmanship as that of soulbound dolls)
Alchemical Experimenters A chemical scent overpowers the rot, and precise surgical cuts can be seen on the arms (a PC who succeeds at a @Check[crafting|dc:10|name:Recognize Cuts] or @Check[medicine|dc:10|name:Recognize Cuts] check realizes both are medical but unnecessary)
Slavers The hands still wear heavy manacles around the wrists
Crime Lords The hands are tattooed and some are cleanly missing fingers (a PC who succeeds at a @Check[society|dc:10|name:Recognize Marks] or @Check[underworld-lore|dc:10|name:Recognize Marks]{Underworld Lore} check recognizes these as marks of gangs active before the earthquake)
Misguided Pharasmins A scent of incense lingers as the haunt vanishes (a PC who succeeds at a @Check[religion|dc:10|name:Recognize Rites] or @Check[pharasma-lore|dc:10|name:Recognize Rites]{Pharasma Lore} check recognizes the scent from Pharasmin funerals)

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the runes concealed among the drawings

details.description: Shadowy caricatures of elves peel themselves from the floor and attack everyone in the room.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:16] to move through the room without touching any of the runes, followed by @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to carve them out of the wood without triggering them; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 16) to counteract the runes
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute. The drawings cannot pursue characters out of the room.
details.routine: (1 action) The drawings stab at each character in the room with spears drawn from sharp lines.

description.value:

Effect After waiting 1 round, the trap animates its shadowy stick figures and makes a painted spear Strike against each creature in the room. The trap rolls initiative.

details.description: Wisps of red energy and violent urges emanate from the crater.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:18] to overpower the warshard's will, @Check[religion|dc:18] to calm the belligerent miasma
details.reset: The residual energies reset after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature rolls initiative for combat within 100 feet of the crater

Effect An infectious battle frenzy threatens to overtake all creatures within 100 feet of the crater, driving them to lash out at anything nearby. Affected creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature resists the battle frenzy, but takes @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage from the roiling fury coursing through its blood.

Failure As success, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} as it fights off the frenzy.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round.

details.description: This hallway is designed to shrink as creatures traverse it, making access to the door at the hall's end impossible.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Jam the Scales|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to jam the scales built into the floor
details.reset: After 10 minutes without weight being placed on this hall's floor, this trap resets, returning the hall to its original size.

description.value:
Trigger 100 pounds of weight or more is placed on the hallway's floor

Effect The hall's last 40 feet of walls angle inward, causing the hall to shrink and contract down, reaching 6 inches in height and width at the hall's far end and completely blocking access to the hall's exit door for anything but a Tiny creature Squeezing.

details.description: Thick smoke fills this hallway, making it difficult to see and breathe.
details.disable: While a spell like @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Gust of Wind] can temporarily clear a path through the smoke, the hazard returns at the start of the next round as long as the fires in area A17 continue to burn; @Check[survival|dc:18] to know how best to cover your nose and mouth with a wet cloth to help alleviate the choking effects of the smoke.
details.reset: The smoke continues to affect anyone who enters area A16 until the fires in area A17 are extinguished.

description.value:
Trigger A creature that isn't holding their breath walks into the hallway or starts their turn in the hallway

Effect The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected by the smoke.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} by the smoke.

Failure The creature is sickened 1 and takes @Damage[1d6[nonlethal]] damage from choking on the smoke. A creature that falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] from the nonlethal damage begins to suffocate in [[/r {1d4+1} #rounds]]{1d4+1 rounds} if not dragged to safety.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} and @Damage[1d6[persistent,nonlethal]] damage.

details.description: Thick smoke fills this room, making it difficult to see and breathe.

details.reset: The smoke continues to affect anyone who enters area A16 until the fires in area A17 are extinguished.

description.value:

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} and @Damage[1d6[persistent,nonlethal]] damage.

The flames and heat deal @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:16|basic] save) to creatures who end their turns in these burning chambers as long as the fires continue.

Breaking the room's glass window before the fire is extinguished causes the fire within to flare up and deal @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage at the end of that round (rather than the standard 1d6), but once at least three of the burning rooms have their windows ventilated, the smoke in these rooms and in area A16 lessens, and the DC of the Fortitude save to resist its Choke effect is reduced to 11.

On a critical failure, the creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.
