details.description: A wooden trapdoor covers a pit that's 10 feet square and 20 feet deep. Sharp spikes line the bottom of the pit.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:14|name:Remove Trap Door|traits:mechanical,trap] to remove the trapdoor

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Intro #2: United in Purpose

details.reset: Creatures can still fall into the trap, but the trapdoor must be reset manually for the trap to become hidden again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage plus @Damage[2d6[piercing]] damage from sharp implements at the bottom of the pit. That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

details.description: Specialized spore pods burst open, expel toxic globules, and recharge from alchemical reservoirs.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:18] to add disruptive chemicals to the fungus's reservoir, @Check[nature|dc:18] (trained) to recognize how to constrict the chemicals' flow, or @Check[deception|dc:20] (trained) to spread spores that befuddle the fungus; three total successes are required to disable the trap.
details.routine: (3 actions) The spitter pods alternate between making Strikes and Reloading. The pods lose 1 action for each successful check to disable the trap.

description.value:
A non-fungus creatures enters area E4

Effect The spitter pod rolls initiative.

details.description: A massive bronze cauldron of molten candy bubbles on the stove, spraying sticky-sweet hot caramel in all directions.
details.disable: Breaking the pot disables it as soon as its rupture reaction is resolved.
details.routine:
(2 actions) On each of the pot's actions, it targets one creature hostile against Larmine within 30 feet of the pot, spitting a glob of hot molten candy that deals @Damage[(1d8+4)[fire]] damage; the target creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:16|options:damaging-effect,inflicts:slowed] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} as the sticky molten candy glues them to the floor.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2}, and takes @Damage[1d4[persistent,fire]] damage as the hot candy sticks to their exposed skin.

description.value:
Trigger Larmine rolls initiative

Effect With a wave of her hand, Larmine instantly brings her candy pot to a violent boil. The pot blows its top and targets all creatures hostile to Larmine within 20 feet of it, dealing @Damage[2d8[fire]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:16|basic|options:area-effect] save. On a critical failure, the targeted creature also takes @Damage[1d4[persistent,fire]|options:area-damage] damage as the hot candy sticks to their exposed skin. The spitting hot candy pot then rolls initiative.

Trigger The spitting hot candy pot Breaks

Effect The pot splits open, spilling its remaining contents in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] that deals @Damage[2d12[fire]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:20|options:area-effect,damaging-effect] save. The area covered by this reaction lingers as difficult terrain for the rest of the encounter.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes tangled in the sticky mess of hot candy, which imposes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until it leaves the area of the rupture's effect. Until the creature leaves the area of effect, it also takes @Damage[1d4[persistent,fire]|options:area-damage] damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] (DC 18 to [[/act escape dc=18]]), after which it takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds and @Damage[1d4[persistent,fire]|options:area-damage] damage as failure.

details.description: A half-dozen ghostly kobolds rise from the rubble in a howling vortex.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:18|name:Frighten the Spirits] (trained) to frighten the spirits with a threatening display, or @Check[religion|dc:21|name:Excorcise the Spirits] (trained) to exorcise the spirits
details.reset:
The haunt deactivates if there are no frightened creatures in area A7 at the start of its turn. The ghostly kobolds return to the rubble pile.

The haunt can't activate again for 1 hour.
details.routine: (1 action) The spirits swoop together toward one creature in area A7 who's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened], instilling feelings of betrayal and confusion. The target takes @Damage[(1d10+4)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:18|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters a square either fully or partially filled with rubble in the middle of the room

Effect Six ghostly kobolds surge out of the rubble with eerie yowls. Each creature in area A7 must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save with the following results. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected, is temporarily immune to Confusing Confrontation for 24 hours, and realizes that a display of intimidating dominance might quell the ghostly kobolds' assault.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 2 rounds and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}.

details.description: Bright, iridescent blue petals sprout from the cerulean center of this flower.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18] (trained) to safely move through the flowers without activating the pollen.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-13: Within the Prairies

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the flowers' space

Effect The pollen in the suncatcher blooms puffs out in a 5-foot burst. All creatures in the area must each attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is immune to the effects of suncatcher bloom pollen for 24 hours.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied] 1 for 1 minute.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied] 1 for 1 hour and immediately falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious]. They do not fall prone or release what they are holding, and they can still wake up normally with a successful Perception check. If they are still unconscious after 1 minute, they wake up automatically.

Critical Failure Same as failure, except the creature wakes up automatically after 1 hour instead of after 1 minute.

stealth.details: (trained), or DC 0 and no minimum proficiency if the surface is disturbed.

details.description: A 15-foot-wide patch of water and sand attempts to submerge creatures that step onto it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:16|name:Disturb Surface|traits:environmental] (trained) to disturb the surface

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Intro #1: The Second Confirmation

details.reset: The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it settles over the course of 24 hours.
details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the quicksand pulls down each creature within it. A creature that was submerged up to its waist becomes submerged up to its neck, and a creature that was submerged up to its neck is pulled under and has to hold its breath to avoid suffocation.

A creature in the quicksand can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:18|name:Swim out of Quicksand|traits:action:swim,environmental] check to Swim to either raise itself by one step if it's submerged to its neck or worse, or to move 5 feet if it's submerged only up to its waist. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down one step. A creature that Swims out of the quicksand escapes the hazard and is prone in a space adjacent to the quicksand patch. Other creatures can Aid the creature, typically by using a rope or similar aid, or attempt to pull the creature out with their own @Check[athletics|dc:18|name:Aid Another Creature in Quicksand] check, with the same results as if the creature attempted the check.

description.value:
Trigger A Huge or smaller creature walks onto the quicksand

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the quicksand up to its waist. The quicksand rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

details.description: All lights in the room snap on, just as Lyari remembers the day of her death. A susurrus of accusations and a guilty verdict fill the air. Ghostly elven warriors disappear and reappear in a disorienting and implacable advance.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:20] to stand against the force of the verdict, or @Check[arcana|dc:20], @Check[occultism|dc:20], or @Check[religion|dc:20] to ritually disrupt the haunting. Each success reduces the haunt's actions by one When the haunt has no more actions, it is disabled and the Hunted by Chains haunt triggers.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-08: The Haunted Corridor

details.reset: The haunt resets the next time the Soulshackle traps creatures in the library.
details.routine: (two actions) The haunt makes vengeful judgment Strikes against three different random creatures trapped in this haunt. A spectral pool of blood forms beneath any creature knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] by this haunt.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs all enter the room

Effect Accusations of crimes against the crown are whispered throughout the room in Elven. Each living creature in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}. The haunt then rolls initiative.
Plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] on a critical hit

stealth.details: (trained) to hear the spirit's grunt of exertion as it works to topple the furniture

details.description: A mischievous spirit pushes a bookshelf over onto a creature.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:16|name:Hold the furniture upright] to hold the furniture upright or @Check[occultism|dc:18|name:Foil the spirit's influence] (trained) to foil the spirit's efforts to influence matter
details.reset: If the haunt successfully damages a creature with Topple Furniture, the spirit laughs for [[/gmr 1d4 #Laughing Duration]]{1d4 rounds} as the haunt resets; each subsequent time the haunt activates, it must topple a different piece of upright furniture. If the haunt fails to damage a creature, the spirit wails in frustration and resets after pouting for [[/gmr 1d4 #Haunt Reset]]{1d4 hours}.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the furniture

Effect The spirit shoves the furniture over in an attempt to crush the triggering creature. The triggering creature takes @Damage[(2d10+7)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic]). On a failure, the creature is additionally knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

details.description: An oversized cauldron rigged as an elevator suddenly drops 30 feet if it's overloaded.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to lock the releasing mechanisms in place, or @Check[crafting|dc:20] to rig additional supports for the cauldron (this activity requires 10 minutes of work)

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

details.reset: Creatures can still fall down the shaft even after the cauldron is released, but the cauldron must be reset manually.

description.value:
Trigger More than one Medium or more than two Small creatures are inside the cauldron when it is activated to lower;

Effect The cauldron releases from its anchor points on the chains and drops 30 feet to area E1; any creatures within take 15 points of bludgeoning damage in the fall. A creature can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling by clinging to a chain.

details.description: Magical energy contained within the room surges in flashes of dark lightning.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:17] (trained) to restore some of the arcane wards, @Check[religion|dc:20] or @Check[occultism|dc:20] (trained) to quell the necromantic energy in the room.
details.reset: 10 minutes

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the room.

Effect The necromantic energy pulsing throughout the room branches out like lightning, inundating any creatures in the room or the three squares of hallway outside it. Each creature in those areas must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|traits:damaging-effect] save as they are suffused with dark energy, which deals @Damage[2d8[void]] damage, or @Damage[2d8[vitality]] damage to any creature with void healing.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 hour.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is enfeebled 1 for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is enfeebled 1 for 1 day.

details.description: Magical energy contained within the room surges in flashes of dark lightning.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:20] (trained) to restore some of the arcane wards, @Check[religion|dc:23] or @Check[occultism|dc:23] (trained) to quell the necromantic energy in the room.
details.reset: 10 minutes

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the room.

Effect The necromantic energy pulsing throughout the room branches out like lightning, inundating any creatures in the room or the three squares of hallway outside it. Each creature in those areas must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:21|traits:damaging-effect] save as they are suffused with dark energy, which deals @Damage[(2d8+5)[void]] damage, or @Damage[(2d8+5)[vitality]] damage to any creature with void healing.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 hour.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is enfeebled 1 for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is enfeebled 1 for 1 day.

details.description: Plaster crumbles from this column moments before it collapses.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:18] (trained) to stabilize the column, @Check[athletics|dc:21] to push the column back into place

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the tiles adjacent to the column

Effect The column collapses. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save as debris falls on them, which deals @Damage[(2d10+7)[bludgeoning]] damage. The area adjacent to the collapsed column becomes difficult terrain.

details.description: This unsteady pile of furniture is piled to the top of this usually high-ceilinged room. There looks to be some space to squeeze through to the other side, but even the slightest touch causes the stack to destabilize, and there's so much that once it begins to fall, it's difficult to stop.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:21] to knock the pile over in a safe direction or @Check[thievery|dc:19] (trained) to carefully rearrange the furniture to a stable configuration.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-13: All That Glitters

details.routine: On its initiative, the pile expands into an adjacent space, dealing creatures in that space @Damage[(1d6+4)[bludgeoning]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save, pushing any creatures in that space into an adjacent empty space, and making that space impassable until cleared. If no adjacent empty space is available, the pile buries the creature, suffocating it. Clearing a space or pulling a creature out requires a @Check[athletics|dc:15] check.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into a space adjacent to the furniture pile

Effect Part of the pile slides into a space the creature occupies, causing @Damage[(1d6+4)[bludgeoning]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save. The furniture pile rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the trap's trigger-thread attached to the door

details.description: Opening the door pulls a thread, which causes a plant mister to spray a cloud of poison into the triggering creature's face.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] to safely cut the thread

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to open the door

Effect Every creature within 10 feet of the door is exposed to violet venom poison.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:17];

Onset 1 minute;

Maximum Duration 6 rounds;

Stage 1 @Damage[1d6[poison]] plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round);

Stage 2 @Damage[1d6[poison]] plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} (1 round);

Stage 3 @Damage[2d6[poison]] plus enfeebled 1 (1 round)

details.description: Potion bottles, alembics, and potent ingredients violently explode when jostled and accidentally combined.
details.disable: Three @Check[acrobatics|dc:20|name:Sort and separate the ingredients|traits:alchemical,hazard,complex,environmental] or @Check[crafting|dc:20|name:Sort and separate the ingredients|traits:alchemical,hazard,complex,environmental] checks to sort and separate the bottles of volatile ingredients into groups

publication.title: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powder Keg

details.reset: The lab contains enough ingredients for this hazard to last 1 minute before all materials are expended. Alternatively, the hazard resets once Slick shrinks to regular size.
details.routine: (1 action) A random @Template[square|distance:10] in Gattlebee's laboratory explodes, dealing 2d6 damage to creatures and objects in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic|options:area-effect|traits:alchemical,hazard,complex,environmental]). The damage type is randomly determined each round: @Damage[2d6[acid]|options:area-damage]{acid}, @Damage[2d6[cold]|options:area-damage]{cold}, @Damage[2d6[electricity]|options:area-damage]{electricity}, @Damage[2d6[fire]|options:area-damage]{fire}, @Damage[2d6[slashing]|options:area-damage]{slashing}, or @Damage[2d6[sonic]|options:area-damage]{sonic}.

description.value:
Trigger Slick drinks Gattlebee's growth serum

Effect The giant lizard bumps into lab tables and workbenches, causing dangerous ingredients to intermix and detonate. The hazard rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (trained); @Check[perception|dc:18] to realize the pillars can move, noticing the pillar has a DC of 0.

details.description: A time-disrupting field shimmers around a 9-foot-tall pillar of obsidian and bronze that magically slides across the room.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[crafting|dc:18], @Check[occultism|dc:18], or @Check[thievery|dc:18]; three successes deactivates a pillar, as does destroying it.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-16: A Star's Journey

details.reset: If the pillar isn't destroyed, it resets 1 minute after making no time drain attacks.
details.routine: (two actions) The pillar Steps or Strides to get as close to as many targets as possible, then makes a time drain Strike against each creature within its reach.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attacks the pillar or approaches within 5 feet of it, or the other warp pillar activates

Effect The trap casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Slow] (@Check[fortitude|dc:18|traits:magical|options:item:type:spell]) on a random creature within 30 feet, then rolls initiative.

plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round on a hit (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2} for 1 round on a critical hit); no multiple attack penalty

details.description: Discreet webbing at throat level snags a creature that walks into it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18|name:Rearrange the Webbing|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) or @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Rearrange the Webbing|traits:mechanical,trap] (expert) to rearrange the webbing.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the square with the web tripwire.

Effect The web lurker noose makes a noose Strike against the triggering creature.

On a successful strike, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] and pulled off the ground ([[/act escape dc=22]]).

The target takes @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage at the end of each of its turns as long as it's caught in the noose.

details.description: Discreet webbing at throat level snags a creature that walks into it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18] (trained) or @Check[thievery|dc:18|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to rearrange the webbing

description.value: The target gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] condition is and pulled off the ground ([[/act escape dc=22]]).
If a creature steps into the square with the web trip wire, the web lurker noose makes a noose Strike against the creature.

stealth.details: (trained) to hear the whispers from a distance

details.description: Lost spirits whisper, reliving their last moments, ready to drag the living into their illusion.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:16|name:Supress the Spirits] (trained) to suppress the spirits or @Check[performance|dc:16|name:Drown Out the Spirits] (trained) to drown them out

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-01: The Absalom Initiation

details.reset: The underlying spirits remain, resetting the haunt every midnight, until what truly happened here becomes public.

description.value:
Trigger Multiple creatures remain in area C4 for more than 6 seconds.

Effect The whispers become more insistent until each character in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save.

On a failure, a creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round (or for [[/gmr 1d6+1 #rounds]]{1d6+1 rounds} on a critical failure). Both the audible whispers and the babbling of the confused are related to the haunting's origin.

Owners Whispers and Babbles
Archdevil worshippers Desperate praise of Mephistopheles
Soulbound dollmakers Pleading "don't make me go in the doll again"
Alchemical experimenters Frantic claims of not wanting to live forever anymore
Slavers Begging to be allowed to be released in multiple languages
Crime lords Promising not to mess with the family again
Misguided Pharasmins Sighing desires to be put to rest

details.description: A massive orca known as Abendego's Squall leaps from the water. Its mouth opens to snap against any part of the ship that it can reach.
details.disable: @Check[medicine|dc:20] (trained) to remove the harpoon, @Check[nature|dc:17] (trained) to calm the orca, or @Check[intimidation|dc:20] (trained) to scare the orca away; two total successes are required to subdue the orca

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: The orca returns to hunt down the Snapdragon after 8 hours.
details.routine:
d Abendego's Squall Leaps over the Snapdragon with each action. When it lands, it deals @Damage[1] damage to the Snapdragon. All creatures on the deck take @Damage[1d8[bludgeoning]] damage and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} until the end of its next turn.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} until the end of its next turn.

Speed 30 feet

description.value:
Trigger Abendego's Squall matches speed with the Snapdragon

Effect The orca leaps across the Snapdragon and tears its main mast from the deck in the process. This deals @Damage[30] damage to the Snapdragon. All creatures on deck take @Damage[1d8[bludgeoning]] damage from the shrapnel and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20|showDC:gm|basic] save. Abendego's Squall rolls initiative.

details.description: Hidden nozzles in the ceiling spray acid pumped in from a nearby reservoir to destroy the desk and all its documents, as well as anyone trying to retrieve those documents.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to disable one of the two acid-spewing nozzles or @Check[crafting|dc:21] (trained) to rig a makeshift plug for one of the two nozzles
details.reset: The trap deactivates after 1 minute. To be reset, the trap's reserve tanks must be refilled with acid.
details.routine: (1 action) The nozzles spray acid from the ceiling onto every creature adjacent to the desk. Each affected creature takes @Damage[4d6[acid]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|options:area-effect] save). For each broken nozzle, this damage is reduced by 2d6.

description.value:
Trigger A creature flips the switches on the underside of the desk in the wrong order or a creature attempts to open one of the desk drawers without first disabling the trap

Effect A hatch opens in the ceiling, releasing a string slime onto the desk. The string slime makes a pseudopod Strike against one creature within reach before rolling initiative. The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (DC 20 to find the cluster of aeon stones at the center of the nexus in the fog)

details.description: A small cluster of aeon stones floats two feet off the ground, orbiting by shards of shattered aeon stones that crackle with electricity.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Remove Aeon Stone|traits:magical,trap] (trained) to remove one nexus aeon stone from the center
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap loses one action each turn for each nexus aeon stone removed from its center. On each action, it shoots a jet of electrified, razor-sharp aeon stone shards at a different creature within 30 feet, dealing @Damage[1d8[piercing]] damage and @Damage[1d8[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|name:Aeon Nexus]).

description.value:
Trigger A creature who is not carrying a wayfinder with an aeon stone from the nexus slotted into it approaches within 15 feet of the aeon nexus

Effect The trap shocks the target, dealing @Damage[2d8[electricity]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic]), then rolls initiative.

details.description: Fire engulfs four 5-foot-by-5-foot sections of the library, then spreads erratically on each of its turns as burning books are carried about by the modified unseen servant.
details.disable: A successful @Check[athletics|dc:18], @Check[nature|dc:18], or @Check[survival|dc:18] check from an adjacent square is sufficient to smother one 5-foot- square of fire; each attempt is an Interact action. Dousing the flames automatically extinguishes one or more sections of fire, with no check. Water typically clears a 5-foot square if the amount is small (such as that from @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Create Water] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Hydraulic Push]). Larger amounts of water, such as a full bucket, typically douse a 10-foot-by-10-foot area (or 4 squares in some other shape). Throwing a bucket of water on flames requires an Interact action. A waterskin doesn't contain enough water to put out even 1 square of fire. Cold can also put out fire, but only if the cold can affect an area; cold is usually less effective than water, so a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Frost Vial (Lesser)]{Frost Vial} typically puts out only 1 square of fire, and ray of frost is ineffective.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-16: Dacilane Academy's First Great Prank War

details.routine: On its turn, the fire spreads into a number of additional squares equal to half the number of squares the fire currently occupies plus the number of books that the lurkers in light lit on fire this round, minimum 1 square. You determine the squares the fire spreads into, but any spread from lit books must affect a bookshelf; fires spread in this way do not need to spread into adjacent squares. Any creature that ends its turn next to the flames takes @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage, and any creature within the flames takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save for both). A creature can take damage from flames only once per round.

details.description: A fruit merchant's cart has been poorly secured. The slightest careless bump on the way past is enough to knock it over and topple the fruit it carries all over the street.
details.disable: If all the PCs succeed on a Perception check against the Stealth DC to spot the hazard, they're able to pass by that cart without triggering it. There's not enough time for PCs who succeed on this check to warn PCs who fail.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-03: Godsrain in a Godless Land

description.value:
Trigger A PC passes by the cart without succeeding on the Perception check against its Stealth DC

Effect The cart tips, causing an avalanche of apples that pour over the PCs and onto the street. PCs who failed the Perception check against the hazard's Stealth DC are caught in the hazard must make their choice of a Reflex or Fortitude save, DC 19. On a successful save, they dodge the worst of the applefall and come away unscathed; on a failed save, they're battered by the falling apples and walk away @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for the rest of the adventure.

Triggering this hazard also accrues 1 AP for the party.

stealth.details: (expert) to catch glimpses of ghostly figures drowning in the water below.

details.description: Ghostly arms rise up from the water to clutch and grab, while a geyser of leech-infested water sprays up into the central shrine.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20] to offer prayers to calm the haunt, or @Check[intimidation|dc:23] to threaten the unquiet spirits away

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: The haunt deactivates and resets at the start of any round in which the PCs aren't in the river, on the bridges, or in the shrines, but the leech swarms remain in the shrine for 10 minutes before retreating back into the water below.
details.routine: (3 actions) Ghostly arms flail about and attempt to grab creatures in the shrine or on either bridge. Each round, the haunt uses 3 actions to attempt to grapple a PC on the bridge or in the shrine. If a PC is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained], the haunt can use one of its actions to drag a PC into the river. A PC who's restrained in the river risks drowning.

description.value:
Trigger A creature swims more than halfway across the river, ends their turn in the bridge shrine, or sets foot onto the northern bridge

Effect Ghostly arms rise up from the river to grab and shake both bridges. Creatures standing on the bridge must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save or be knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. A blast of river water sprays up inside the shrine to deposit two brood leech swarms in the shrine. The haunt and the leech swarms then roll initiative.
Effect @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple]

details.description: A large cylindrical metallic structure surrounded by dense cables and pipes, anointed with mystical runes which glow and hum vibrantly. Elemental energy arcs off three spikes that may have once been connected to some of the cables.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:20] (trained) to restart the machine's connection to an elemental plane, or @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to find and deactivate the whichever rune is currently active; three total successes are required to disable the nexus. A spike can be disabled with a @Check[athletics|dc:23] (trained) check to rip it from the structure or a @Check[crafting|dc:20] (trained) check to reconnect some of the cables. If all 6 elemental energies are returned to the nexus, it is disabled.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-02: The Blackwood Lost

details.routine: (3 actions) The nexus spends each of its actions making an elemental wave Strike; each of these must be against a different creature who does not have the elemental trait. For each spike destroyed or disabled, the trap loses 1 action.

description.value:
Trigger A creature without the elemental trait approaches within 15 feet of the nexus or attacks an elemental within 15 feet of the nexus

Effect Unstable elemental energies surrounding the machine discharge abruptly from one of the spikes, sending waves of wind, gusts of metallic shards, jets of fire and other elemental bursts across the room in various directions. The hazard makes an elemental wave Strike against the triggering creature. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Trigger The nexus is broken or destroyed

Effect A ferocious gush of elemental energies bursts from the broken machine, dealing @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature within @Template[emanation|distance:15]{15 feet} (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|options:area-effect] save). The trap is then disabled.

The damage of this Strike varies; no multiple attack penalty. Roll [[/gmr 1d6 #Damage Type]] for damage type.

1 cold (air)

2 bludgeoning (earth)

3 fire

4 electric (metal)

5 piercing (water)

6 slashing (wood)

details.description: The three larger crystals on the table begin to vibrate and emit a swiftly growing peal of sound.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17] (trained) or @Check[occultism|dc:17] (trained) to disrupt the magic enchanting one of the crystals, or @Check[performance|dc:20] (trained) by a character who can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Counter Performance] to use sound to redirect the noise back into the crystal, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank, DC 18) to counteract a crystal.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: A Fistful of Flowers

details.routine: (3 actions) The hazard loses 1 action for each crystal that's disabled or destroyed. On each of the trap's actions, one of the crystals fires a blast of sound at a single Small or larger creature in the room. The target is randomly determined and takes @Damage[1d8[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic]); a target who critically fails this save is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 minute. At the end of the hazard's routine, attempt a @Check[flat|dc:10|name:Destroy a Crystal]. On a success, one of the crystals shatters and is destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than Darius or one of his homonculi approaches within 5 feet of the table or attempts to Interact with the crystals in any way other than to attempt to disable the hazard.

Effect The three large crystals on the table begin vibrating and humming. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: A 40-foot deep pit is obscured by a false floor set over a single balance point. Pressure on either side to the east or west causes occupants to fall into the depths below.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to attach the balanced stone floor piece to the actual floor and lock it in place, or @Check[crafting|dc:23] (trained) to jury-rig a quick bit of extra support to prevent the floor from tilting

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto one side of the false floor

Effect The triggering creature falls into the pit and takes @Damage[20[bludgeoning]] damage from the fall unless they [[/act grab-an-edge dc=23]]. It's a @Check[athletics|dc:20] check to climb the pit walls.

details.description: The fanged skull rune begins speaking in sweet, honeyed tones about eternal life.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Exorcise Spirit|traits:complex,haunt] (trained) to exorcise the spirit, @Check[religion|dc:21|name:Sever Spiritual Connection|traits:complex,haunt] or @Check[occultism|dc:21|name:Sever Spiritual Connection|traits:complex,haunt] (trained) to sever the connection between Hunger's Call and the temple, or @Check[athletics|dc:21|name:Crack the Wall|traits:complex,haunt] to crack the wall in several places; three total successes are required to disable the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Intro #2: United in Purpose

details.reset: The mosaic ceases its routine 2 rounds after all living creatures are out of sight of the stairs. After 6 hours the haunt is ready to trigger again.
details.routine: (3 actions) A pulsing red ball of energy flies from the wall and explodes in negative energy in the room. Anyone in the room takes @Damage[(1d10+4)[void]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|name:Avoid the Energy|traits:complex,haunt,void] save).

description.value:
Trigger A living creature bearing Hunger's Call moves within 30 feet of the exit stairs

Effect The creature's mind becomes hazy and foggy as an unseen voice says to drop the relic and walk away. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20|name:Resist Hunger's Possession|traits:haunt,complex] save, and the haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round.

Failure The target is dazzled for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The target loses control of their mind and on their next round must set Hunger's Call on the floor and move their speed in a direction away from the relic; this causes the relic to teleport itself back to area D1. If they have any actions remaining, the target loses those actions. At the end of their turn, the target regains control of their mind.

details.description: A crossbow hidden by the shrine's back curtains is ready to shoot. The loaded bolt is magically augmented with holy power to strike true against demons.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Disable Wire|traits:trap,mechanical] (trained) to disable the trigger wire.

description.value:
Trigger The holy text is touched or moved

Effect The trap shoots a bolt, aimed where a creature would stand in front of the altar.

details.description: Blood flows from the fanged skull rune as if it were an open wound.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Exorcise Spirit|traits:complex,haunt] (trained) to exorcise the spirit, @Check[religion|dc:21|name:Sever Spiritual Connection|traits:complex,haunt] or @Check[occultism|dc:21|name:Sever Spiritual Connection|traits:complex,haunt] (trained) to sever the connection between Hunger's Call and the temple, or @Check[crafting|dc:21|name:Break up the Mural|traits:complex,haunt] (trained) to break up the pattern of the mural; three total successes are required to disable the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Intro #2: United in Purpose

details.reset: The mosaic ceases its routine 2 rounds after all living creatures are out of sight of the stairs. After 6 hours the haunt is ready to trigger again.
details.routine: (3 actions) The bloody mosaic blasts shards of colored glass at anyone within 10 feet of the eastern wall, and they each take @Damage[1d10[slashing]] damage plus @Damage[1[bleed]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|name:Avoid the Glass Shards|traits:complex,haunt,slashing] save; critical success negates the bleed damage).

description.value:
Trigger A living creature bearing Hunger's Call moves within 30 feet of the exit stairs

Effect Whispering voices fill the triggering creature's mind with a compulsion for blood of the living. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20|name:Resist Hunger's Possession|traits:mental,occult,haunt,complex] save, and the haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2}.

Critical Failure The target loses control of their mind and on their next round must move to the nearest ally and make an unarmed bite attack. The ally is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] against this attack. The bite deals @Damage[1d4[piercing]] damage and a critical hit deals an additional @Damage[1[bleed]] damage. At the end of their turn, the target regains control of their mind.

details.description: A bloody image of Belcorra arises, emits a soul‑draining light, then inhales blood from living creatures in the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Wipe Away Blood] (trained) to wipe away enough of the bloodstain to disrupt the haunt's necromantic energies, or @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Excorcise Haunt] (trained) to exorcise the haunt
details.reset:
If the haunt is disabled or destroyed through damage, the bloody phantom of Belcorra sprays upward in a spiral, dousing the trap door leading to area A25 before draining upward through the keyhole, which reduces the Thievery DC to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Pick a Lock]{Pick the Lock} to @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Pick Trap Door|traits:action:pick-a-lock,skill,manipulate].

At the next sunrise, the blood drains back down to this room and the haunt resets. The haunt is destroyed permanently once Lasda Venkervale is rescued from area D9.
details.routine:
(1 action) The bloody image opens its mouth and appears to inhale, drawing blood from the body of one creature in its line of sight within 90 feet from the center of area A11. The creature targeted must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d10[bleed]].

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[bleed]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+6)[bleed]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} as long as it's bleeding.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature ends its turn in area A11 at night

Effect A ghastly image of Belcorra, seemingly composed of fresh blood, rises from the bloodstain on the floor and rotates in place like a lighthouse. Beams of cold blue light shine from Belcorra's eyes and open mouth, causing blisters and decay to spread across flesh. Living creatures within a @Template[burst|distance:30] from the center of area A11 must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20|traits:damaging-effect] save.

The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d8[void]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(1d8+6)[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+6)[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 round then dazzled for 1 round.

details.description: A bloody vortex violently draining into a crack of raw elemental energy, fueled by the curse of a sea devil oracle.
details.disable: Slay Silaqui, or @Check[thievery|dc:20|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to pry the gem out of Silaqui followed by disrupting the gem's power by either breaking it (AC 18, Hardness 10, HP 45, BT 23) or using @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 19)

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-06: The Crashing Wave

details.routine:
All creatures within 40 feet of the vortex except Silaqui are drawn toward it. They must attempt either a @Check[fortitude|dc:17] save or an @Check[athletics|dc:17|traits:action:swim] check to Swim with a DC of 17, with the following results.

Success The creature is not moved.

Failure The creature is moved 10 feet closer to the center of the vortex. If this causes the creature to reach the center square of the vortex, they are exposed to Consuming Maelstrom.

Critical Failure As failure, except the creature is moved 20 feet closer to the center of the vortex.

description.value:
Trigger Silaqui rolls initiative

Effect The ritual vortex begins to draw in nearby creatures. The trap rolls initiative.

A creature that is pulled into the center square of the vortex (or that chooses to enter it) must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save.

Critical Success The creature is ejected safely to a square of their choice within 10 feet of the vortex.

Success The creature is ejected safely to a square adjacent to the vortex.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[electricity]] damage and is moved upward 40 feet.

Critical Failure As failure. Additionally, the creature is exposed the raw nature of the sea devil's frenzy and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round (@Check[will|dc:20|traits:mental] negates). This is a mental effect.

details.description: A net with soil and aquatic plants on it covers a 10-foot pit dug into the bottom of a body of water.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Remove the Net|traits:mechanical,trap] to remove the net

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-20: The Lost Legend

details.reset: Others can fall into the pit, but the net entangles only the first creature to trigger the trap. The net must be reset manually.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks on the net.

Effect The triggering creature falls into a spiked pit and submerges, with the net collapsing to entangle the creature. Spikes in the pit deal @Damage[2d10[piercing]] damage. The net imposes a –10-foot penalty to the creature's speed. A creature must succeed at two DC 18 checks to Escape from the net a critical success to Escape frees them immediately. A creature that can't breathe underwater may begin drowning. Any round the creature struggles to escape the net, the creature uses 2 rounds of air.

details.description:
Several dozen spike snares are littered across this hex.

Treasure: Each time a PC triggers a trap, the trap is destroyed. If all the traps are disabled, materials to craft six spike snares can be salvaged.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:17] (made as an individual hexploration activity) to locate and disarm all the traps by hand.
details.reset: There are enough traps in this hex that this hazard can be triggered up to 5 times before all the traps are used up.

description.value:
Trigger Anyone Travels or Reconnoiters this hex

Effect If the characters are Traveling, one of them (determined randomly) steps in a trap. If the characters are Reconnoitering, [[/gmr 1d4]] of them (determined randomly) step in a trap. When a creature steps in a trap, the trap makes a bear trap Strike against the creature.

details.description: A rift to Hell crackles with unstable energy from the broken summoning circle as a squirming mass of fiends struggles to emerge A blood mist projection of a Hellknight angrily commands them.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:19], @Check[nature|dc:19], @Check[occultism|dc:19], or @Check[religion|dc:19] (2 actions) (trained) while within 15 feet to weaken the rift, dealing @Damage[3d4[spirit]|traits:holy] damage to it. This damage has the holy trait.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-01: Intro to the Year of Battle's Spark: Enough is Enough

details.routine: (1 action) The Hellknight commands the orts, and the trap lashes out at each enemy creature in its space and adjacent, dealing @Damage[(1d10+6)[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save) If no enemy is in reach, an ort emerges in an adjacent square.

description.value: All orts within 30 feet have +1 status bonus to hit and deal 1d4 extra fire damage on a hit.

Trigger The trap receives a critical hit or critically fails a saving throw

Effect One ort emerges adjacent.

Trigger The magic circle is broken

Effect The pile disgorges orts and then rolls initiative.

details.description: A magical glyph unleashes a blast of electricity when handled.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:20] (trained) to hurl the stone a safe distance before it activates, or @Check[thievery|dc:21] to delicately move the stone without triggering the glyph.

description.value:
Trigger A creature jostles or views the glyph

Effect An electric discharge shocks the triggering creature and all creatures within 10 feet, dealing @Damage[3d8[electricity]] damage to anyone in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save).

details.description: This bone-filled vault disgorges its contents the moment the vault door slides open.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[stealth|dc:20] (trained) to carefully open the vault door without disturbing the bones

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door to the vault

Effect The bones spill from the vault and into the adjacent corridor, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage to each creature within 10 feet of the door (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic]). The area becomes difficult terrain.

details.description: A sickly-sweet perfume wafts through the air outside the council building, assaulting the senses of all that inhale it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20] to hold your breath through the perfume, @Check[occultism|dc:19] (trained) to draw a warding rune in the air to disperse the poison, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 17) to counteract the poison

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-12: Negotiations for the Star Gun

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the doors of the Segada council building

Effect All creatures within 10 feet are exposed to the butterfly's breath poison.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:21]

Onset 10 minutes

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (6 hours)

Stage 2 stupefied 1 (12 hours)

Stage 3 stupefied 1 (24 hours)

details.description: Solid ground gives way to a lake of bubbling mud that burns anyone who enters and routinely belches out sulfur.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20|name:Remove Stones|traits:complex,environmental,poison] (trained) to find and remove the stones blocking the flow of water to the mud pot. The water decreases the viscosity of the mud, allowing steam and sulfur to escape gradually, rather than in violent bursts, so creatures can pass through unharmed. The hazard requires one successful checks to disable. Attempting the check takes two actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-10: Delve the Pallid Depths

details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the mud pot belches a cloud of sickening sulfur into the small cavern. Creatures other than sulfuric slimes within 10 feet must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:17] save or be exposed to sulfur poisoning.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than a sulfuric slime comes within 10 feet of the mud pot

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as a gaseous cloud explodes out of the mud. The triggering creature takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[acid]] damage @Check[reflex|dc:20|basic]

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:17]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d10[poison]] damage

Stage 2 @Damage[1d12[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[2d10[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened2} (1 round)

details.description: The frame splinters as the door flings open, the force hitting those nearby.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Hold the Door Shut|traits:chaotic,hazard,magical,trap] (trained) to hold the door shut against the force, or @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Relieve Door Pressure|traits:chaotic,hazard,magical,trap] (trained) to deftly relieve pressure from the door.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-09: The Seven Secrets of Dacilane Academy

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches any part of the door.

Effect The door flies open with incredible force. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:22|traits:damaging-effect,chaotic,hazard,trap,magical] save as the door explodes outward into them, which deals @Damage[5d8[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is knocked 10 feet toward the window behind them.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is knocked 20 feet toward the window. The window shatters and the PC falls 5 feet before colliding with the ground, taking an additional @Damage[2d6[piercing]] damage from passing through the broken glass.

details.description: When triggered, the room lurches suddenly on its side, causing those in the room to slide down toward the double line of spears on the west wall while the statues on the east slide down on a pulley-and-roller system to smash into the victims.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] to deactivate the triggering pressure plate near the northern door

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

details.reset: After 1 minute, the chamber rights itself and resets the trap.

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature approaches within 15 feet of the double door to area D9.

Effect The entire room rotates, its west half descending as its right half rises, forming a 45º slope down to the west. All creatures in the room fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] and slide toward the west wall (a creature adjacent to the north or south wall can attempt to arrest this fall with a DC 20 check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge]) and take @Damage[(2d6 + 5)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic]) as they land among the spears. At the same time, the warhammer-wielding statues to the east slide downward on chains to smash into the lower wall; each person in the trap is subjected to a sliding statue Strike.

details.description: A cheerful, wordless tune fills the room, compelling everyone to get to work.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Exorcise Spirits|traits:complex,haunt] (trained) to exorcise the spirits, or @Check[intimidation|dc:21|name:Take a Break|traits:complex,haunt] to convince the spirits it is time to take a break; three total successes are required to disable the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Intro #2: United in Purpose

details.reset: The ghostly workers cease their routine 2 rounds after all living creatures leave the room and reset after 6 hours.
details.routine: (3 actions) Ghostly workers appear in the room and begin abrading, scrubbing, and splashing cleaning chemicals on anyone in the area. Each creature in the room takes @Damage[(1d4+2)[slashing]] and @Damage[(1d4+2)[acid]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|name:Avoid Workers|traits:haunt,complex] save; roll once for both).

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the smiling face in the center of the north wall

Effect The whistling rises in volume to a painful pitch and deals @Damage[(1d8+4)[sonic]] damage. The affected creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20|traits:damaging-effect] save, and the haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The target takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}, and is deafened for 2 minutes.

details.description: The equilibrium of the brown mold and lava river is disturbed, creating bubbles of molten stone that burst and spew burning debris.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:17|name:Clear mold] or @Check[survival|dc:17|name:Clear mold] or @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Clear mold] to clear your square and all adjacent squares of the mold; the churning lava does not target creatures in cleared squares with its spatter attack, but the hazard is not destroyed until every square has been cleared of mold.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-19: Enter the Pallid Peak

details.reset: If not completely destroyed, the mold spreads to one additional square within 10 feet of lava each day until it fully regenerates and regains its state of equilibrium. If every square is cleaned of mold, the hazard is destroyed.
details.routine: (1 action) On the its initiative, the churning lava targets a random creature in the area with a ranged spatter attack as a magma bubble bursts.

description.value:
Trigger A creature disturbs the mold, typically by walking through it

Effect The hazard makes a spatter attack against the triggering creature, and then rolls initiative as the lava begins to bubble.

The target must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save as the lava rapidly cools and solidifies. On a critical hit, use the outcome for one degree of success worse than the result of the save.

Success The lava slides off the target with no additional effect.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1}. The target or an adjacent creature can take an Interact action to remove the stone, removing the condition.

Critical Failure As failure, except the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2}. Each Interact action to remove the stone reduces the value of the clumsy condition by 1.

details.description: Solvents and detergents spray from the ceiling and run down the walls where they combine in dangerous ways.
details.disable: @Check[engineering-lore|dc:20|name:Disable Control Panel|traits:mechanical,complex]{Engineering Lore} (trained) or @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Disable Control Panel|traits:mechanical,complex] (trained) to deactivate the control panel, or @Check[athletics|dc:21|name:Plug the Spouts|traits:mechanical,complex] or @Check[acrobatics|dc:21|name:Plug the Spouts|traits:mechanical,complex] to plug the spouts that release the chemicals; three successes are required to disable the trap.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Intro #2: United in Purpose

details.reset: The trap deactivates after 1 minute and resets in 1 hour.
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap fills the room with a cloud of caustic vapor and deals @Damage[(2d6+2)[acid]] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic|name:Cleaning Agent|traits:acid]).

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the room.

Effect The trap sprays cleaning chemicals at the triggering creature dealing @Damage[(1d8+4)[acid]] damage. The creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20|traits:acid,damaging-effect] save, and the trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded]{Blind} for 1 round.

details.description: A cloud of shimmering blue dust erupts from the chest and lingers in the air.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23] or @Check[medicine|dc:23] to recognize the distinct aroma as belonging to incense of reverie and take necessary precautions when opening the chest, such as holding one's breath and shielding the face from exposure.

description.value:
Trigger The chest is opened

Effect All creatures within 10 feet of the chest inhale the dust and become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued] (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save to negate both effects). The fascinated effect lasts for 1 minute, as the target enters a calm and dreamy stupor looking at into the receding dust mist. As usual, the fatigued condition lasts until the affected creature receives a full night's rest.

details.description: A tunnel through this steep barricade is rigged to collapse. If a kobold spends one action to throw a lever, the tunnel collapses on the next creature to pass through.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to carefully trigger the collapse without entering the tunnel or @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to brace the tunnel so it is safe to pass through.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-11: Flames of Rebellion

description.value:
Trigger A creature crawls into the tunnel in the collapsing barricade after the trap is activated with the lever

Effect The barricade collapses on the creature, who must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:22|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and escapes the barricade, emerging into a safe space on either side of it.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(1d6+2)[bludgeoning]] damage from collapsing debris and @Damage[(1d6+2)[slashing]] damage from broken glass before emerging on the opposite side of the barricade from the one they entered.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d6+4)[bludgeoning]] damage from collapsing debris and @Damage[(2d6+4)[slashing]] damage from broken glass before emerging on the opposite side of the barricade from the one they entered.

Critical Failure As a failure, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] beneath the barricade until they or another creature succeed at a @Check[athletics|dc:22|name:Escape Barricade] check to pull them free. They can still be targeted, touched, and otherwise interacted with by creatures on either side of the barrier.

details.description:
Fluids from leaking beakers have eaten nearly through the supports holding up this cabinet.

This Broken Threshold for this trap is 10, not 20.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Support Cabinet|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) to gently place something under the cabinet to support its weight.

description.value:
Trigger Someone touches or otherwise interacts with the cabinet

Effect The cabinet falls, splintering on impact. If someone attempts to break the cabinet but its Broken Threshold is not reached in one hit, it falls as well. The cabinet deals @Damage[6d8[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone beneath (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic|traits:mechanical,trap] save).

details.description: The damaged ceiling is the only thing stopping the centipedes lairing above from falling through.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:22] (trained) to reinforce the ceiling with boards from the broken door.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-03: Escaping the Grave

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into the area beneath the centipede lair.

Effect Hearing potential prey, the centipedes begin scrabbling above, causing the ceiling to collapse and centipedes to rain down, dealing @Damage[(3d8+4)[bludgeoning]] damage to each creature in the area; each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:19|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

details.description: Skillet sputter and pots threaten to boil over, splashing their contents onto careless cooks or kitchen counters. The counters are covered with small dishes ready to add, along with 3 heavy cookbooks and a healthy coating of flour.
details.disable: @Check[cooking-lore|dc:17] (trained) or @Check[crafting|dc:20] to add the proper ingredients to finish the dish. @Check[diplomacy|dc:20] or @Check[occultism|dc:20] to communicate with students when to stir or adjust the heat on a dish. Each disable attempt requires two actions, and three successful checks are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-18: Dacilane Academy's Delightful Disaster

details.reset: The trap deactivates after 1 minute, destroying all of the food. If left untouched, it resets automatically in 24 hours.
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap uses each of its actions to make a splatter attack against a random creature in the kitchen. The trap never targets a student, as they are out of phase with it. For every successful disable attempt on the trap, it loses an action.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the room

Effect The hazard rolls initiative.

Trigger A creature fails an attempt to Disarm the trap

Effect The incorrect execution of the recipe releases a nauseating odor. The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

details.description: This 20-foot-by-20-foot patch of ground is cobwebbed with cracks and fissures that crumble when a creature steps on it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:16] (untrained) to collapse safely; @Check[crafting|dc:20] (trained) to shore it up enough to pass over without collapsing it

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps onto the cracked earth

Effect The creature fractures the thin crust of earth and falls through. That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling, as long as it is adjacent to an edge. Otherwise it takes falling damage (typically @Damage[25[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage for a 50-foot drop). Some of these rifts eventually lead to the Darklands.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the worn flagstones which are crumbling away at the slightest pressure.

details.description: At a cursory glance the tunnel appears to be little more than a waterlogged chamber.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:17] (trained) to safely navigate the wet floor without stepping on any loose masonry or @Check[thievery|dc:19] (expert) to cause a controlled collapse without any danger to the PCs.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-16: A Lie Told to Strangers

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the hazardous area

Effect The flooring crumbles, breaking apart under the creature and sending them into the stagnant water below, dealing @Damage[(2d12+12)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save). That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

details.description: A large, wheeled cart with a multi-sectioned pole in the middle. Each section of pole has several shovels sticking out of it like makeshift arms.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) twice on the cart to dismantle core connected pieces, or @Check[nature|dc:20] or @Check[occultism|dc:20] (trained) check to temporarily disable the jinkins' curse and render the machine inert for an hour
details.routine: (3 actions) The digging machine spends an action to move 20 feet toward the nearest creature. If it starts next to a creature, it moves 20 feet toward the next closest creature. It then makes two slashing shovels attacks against any creature within 10 feet.

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 30 feet of the digging machine

Effect The digging machine rattles to life and rolls toward the triggering creature. It moves 30 feet toward the creature, attacking every creature within 10 feet with slashing shovels. The machine then rolls initiative.

details.description: The faint image of Shelyn performing for an enraptured audience distorts into a nightmare scene of a dead god tormenting the souls of her followers.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23] (trained) to offer prayers to counteract the blasphemies or @Check[performance|dc:20] (expert) to give the tormented souls a more positive performance to enjoy; two successes are required to disable it.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates at the end of the third consecutive round in which there are no living, sapient creatures in area A22, then resets automatically the next round.
details.routine: (3 actions) The sounds of the undead Shelyn's performance grow audible, bringing a somber, menacing dirge to the shrine. As undead Shelyn plays, creatures depicted in the mural die horrific deaths and send blasts of tormented spiritual energy spiraling out into the shrine. The haunt uses its three actions to make lifedrain Strikes at three different sapient targets in area A22, sending a tormented, phantasmal, and gory remnant spiraling out from the mural each time. A creature can only be targeted by a lifedrain Strike once per round.

description.value:
Trigger a PC approaches within 5 feet of the mural, or a PC Seeks the mural from anywhere in area A22

Effect The imagery on the mural distorts. Shelyn decays into an undead blasphemy, animating into unlife as she performs for an audience suddenly thrown into torment. One by one, the animals, fey, and humanoids in the mural die grisly deaths as the dirge slaughters them. Each PC in area A22 must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:20] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: The machine is a cylindrical device ringed in blades with a devilish eye at its center. Gnome inventors created the mechanical device, which taps into the planes of earth and fre to create powerful blasts to damage fortress walls or decimate ranks of enemies. Unfortunately, the many miles of forest have not been kind and it's malfunctioning. The damaged jaws planar connections have weakened to a mere trickle and, when activated, it lashes out in an uncontrolled frenzy. The blades encircling it begin lashing out.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[crafting|dc:18], @Check[nature|dc:18], or @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) twice, to sever the connection to the planes of fre and earth. @Check[crafting|dc:16] or @Check[thievery|dc:16] (trained) once, to remove the pintle connecting the limber to the horses. A creature must be adjacent to the jaws to make either check.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-06: Brastlewark at War Part 1: The Gnome Defection

details.reset: The planar connection shuts down after 1 minute. The horses will tire and stop running after 1 minute.
details.routine: (three actions) On its turn each round, the horses spend one action to move 30 feet, dragging the limber carrying the jaws behind them in a randomly chosen direction. The horses will not move through other creatures. Players on the limber when it moves must attempt a @Check[acrobatics|dc:18] check when it moves or fall of. Removing the pintle will end the random movement. The jaws use 2 actions to Spit Fire or make melee strikes against an adjacent creature.

description.value:
Jaws Hardness 15

Jaws HP 60 (BT 30)

Trigger A Small or larger creature, moves within 10 feet of the jaws.

Effect One of the blades lashes out, making one Strike against the creature.

The jaws launch a magma ball (range 60 feet, @Template[burst|distance:10]), randomly, at the largest group of creatures, not including itself, it can catch in the blast dealing @Damage[3d4[bludgeoning],3d4[fire]|options:area-damage] damage. Each creature caught in the blast must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18|options:damaging-effect,area-effect] save. The jaws cannot Spit Fire again for [[/gmr 1d4+1 #Recharge Spit Fire]] rounds.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, @Damage[1[persistent,fire]] damage, and is knocked prone.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the instability in the surrounding woodwork and precariously balanced masonry.

details.description: The room initially appears merely dilapidated, but on closer inspection it is positively liable to collapse at any moment.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:23] (untrained) to safely shore up the weaknesses in the masonry by replacing rotten wood with other assorted bric-a-brac, @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to carefully place boards and the like to create bridges over structurally weak areas.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-16: A Lie Told to Strangers

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the hazardous area

Effect Bricks and broken wood fall on the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save).

hp.details: (BT 30) to destroy the seal and disable the trap

stealth.details: (trained) or @Check[perception|dc:23|traits:action:seek] (trained) to notice scratches around the musical pipes and a slight give to the seal

details.description: Four dart-loaded pipes hidden in the diagonal walls are connected to a pressure plate under the brass seal in the room's center.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Disable Pressure Plate|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to disable the pressure plate, or four @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Plug Dart Launcher|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) checks to plug the dart launchers

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

details.reset: The trap runs out of darts after 4 rounds of firing and must be reset manually by dismantling the walls to reload the launchers. If at any point there are no longer more than two creatures placing weight on the floor, the trap ceases firing but remains armed, ready to resume firing its remaining darts once triggered again.
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action for each disabled launcher. On each of the trap's actions, it fires one dart at a creature in the center of the room.

description.value:
Trigger More than two Small or larger creatures step on the brass seal.

Effect The trap makes up to four dart Strikes against creatures standing on the seal, then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: A large tree has been cut down and supported only by a rope.
details.disable: The trap cannot be disabled in advance (unless the PCs are able to out stealth Vengeant Thorn and sneak up on him before the wagon reaches the target zone).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-04: Bandits of Immenwood

description.value:
Trigger Vengeant Thorn releases the rope when the wagon is 10 feet away from the target zone

Effect The tree drops 10 feet in front of the wagon, blocking the road. The tree deals @Damage[3d12[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone beneath the tree when it drops and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized]{Immobilizes} them. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:20|traits:damaging-effect,hazard,mechanical,trap] save takes half damage and is not immobilized, instead rolling out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, it can choose the direction. Mounted creatures do not attempt a save unless their mount attempts a save and fails. If a mounted creature's mount fails the save, the mounted creature can attempt its own @Check[reflex|dc:18|name:Mounted Deadfall|traits:damaging-effect,hazard,mechanical,trap] save by leaping off its mount, taking @Damage[1d4[bludgeoning]] damage from the fall. This damage is taken whether the save is successful or not.

details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:20] (trained) to disperse the force or @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained) to hide from the ominous presence's senses

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-04: To Seek the Heart of Calamity

details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature spends one minute in the haunted chamber

Effect A powerful force sweeps across the room, shoving all creatures within it toward the doorway. Each creature in the room must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is shoved through the doorway. They can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] as a reaction. If they fail, they fall to the ground below, taking the damage from the failure entry.

Failure The creature is pushed 10 feet into the empty air and falls to the ground 40 feet below. They cannot attempt to Grab an Edge.

Critical Failure As failure, except the creature also collides with several cables on the way down, taking an additional @Damage[(1d10 + 5)[bludgeoning]] damage.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the worn flagstones which are crumbling away at the slightest pressure.

description.value:

That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20] (trained) to ward against demonic presence, @Check[arcana|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[occultism|dc:20] (trained) to weaken the demonic possession, @Check[intimidation|dc:20] (trained) to scare it away, or @Check[sailing-lore|dc:18] (trained) to confuse it; two total successes are required to disable the hazard.

details.routine:
2 The demon ship uses its actions on one of the following options (GM's choice). It loses one action from its routine if a PC succeeds at an attempt to Disable it or it reaches its Broken Threshold.

  • Demonic List d Effect The ship tosses and turns. Each creature in the hazard's area must make a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save. On a failure, it is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]; on a critical failure it also takes @Damage[2d10[bludgeoning]] damage.
  • Heads Will Roll a It makes a bowsprit Strike.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Sea Chantey]

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps within 10 feet of the back of a diorama alcove

Effect The trap fires an apocalypse beam at that creature.

details.description: A giant squid rams the ship from below, using its beak to puncture the hull. It then wraps its tentacles around the mast, rocking the ship violently.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:20] to pull the tentacles from the mast, stopping the ship from rocking; two total successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-03: A Foot in the Door

details.routine: (two actions) On its initiative, the squid uses its first action to pull on the mast Every creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save On a failure, it falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] If a creature is prone when attempting the save, it slides 10 feet in a direction determined by the GM on a failure If this movement would force a creature off the ship, it can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] The squid uses its second action to make one tentacle attack

description.value:

details.description: The pile of bones transforms into a Large silver dragon, while the PCs appear to transform into dark, shadowy figures of alien physique. The dragon's flesh appears to slowly dissolve into lines of black smoke, which trail away toward the PCs and appear to be absorbed into their bodies. Once the dragon is stripped of flesh, its skeleton explodes in a shower of bone fragments.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Exorcise the Spirit|traits:haunt] (trained) to exorcise the spirit (two actions), or @Check[diplomacy|dc:23|name:Calm the Spirit|traits:haunt] (trained) to calm the spirit momentarily (two actions).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-01: Citadel of Corruption

details.reset: 1 minute, as the bone fragments slowly move back into a neat pile with the skull on top.
