description.value:
Trigger The wagon door is opened or the pods are disturbed

Effect A pod makes a pollen spray Strike against an adjacent creature or the creature that opened the door, the door falls off the wagon, and the trap rolls initiative.

A creature hit by the trap's pollen spray must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:20] save or it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round and takes a -2 status penalty to Perception checks and saves against mental effects for [[/gmr 1d4 #hours]] hours. On a critical failure, the penalty is instead -4.

details.description: A trapdoor covers a 10-foot-square pit that's 30 feet deep and has 5 feet of water at the bottom. Four water spouts in the walls connect to hidden water tanks. Each water spout extends out of a different wall, 6 inches from the top of the pit.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to seal each water spout, @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to open the trapdoor, or @Check[athletics|dc:22] to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.action-macros.Macro.Force Open: Athletics] the trapdoor
details.reset: The trap can be reset if the door is manually reengaged and the water tanks are refilled; it can be reset without draining the pit, but doing so renders the pit less effective.
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled water spout. On each of the trap's actions, a spout pours water, increasing the depth of the water by 5 feet. Once the pit is full of water, the pit stops using actions, but creatures in the pit begin drowning.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes damage from the fall, reduced by 5 feet for falling into the water (typically @Damage[12[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage). A creature can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling. The trapdoor then slams shut, and the hazard rolls initiative.

@Check[acrobatics|dc:20|name:Maneuver Through Fog|traits:complex,haunt] to maneuver through the fog, @Check[religion|dc:18|name:Ward off Spirit|traits:complex,haunt] to ward off the spirit, or @Check[stealth|dc:20|name:Move Through Fog Without Attracting Attention|traits:complex,haunt] to move through the fog without attracting attention.

Trigger Combat begins with the creature it is attuned to

If a PC uses an action to taunt Bloodmoon with a successful @Check[deception|dc:18], @Check[diplomacy|dc:18], or @Check[intimidation|dc:18] check, Bloodmoon must spend an action monologuing on the next turn.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

details.disable:
@Check[arcana|dc:20], @Check[occultism|dc:20], or @Check[religion|dc:20] to magically alter the runes, or @Check[thievery|dc:23] to physically alter them. A PC that can read Abyssal or Cyclops is better able to identify key runes, gaining a +1 circumstance bonus to these check (+2 if they speak both).

Each check requires two actions, and disabling the monolith requires three successful checks. A PC that critically succeeds earns two success. On a failed check, the attempting PC is exposed to the burning flames wreathing the monolith, taking @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|name:Explosive Monolith]).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-14: Lost in Flames

details.routine:
(1 action) On the hazard's turn, randomly select a PC within 40 feet of the monolith. A tree near that PC explodes in a @Template[burst|distance:10], dealing @Damage[2d6[piercing],2d6[fire]]{2d6 piercing damage and 2d6 fire damage}. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20|name:Explosive Monolith|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than the monolith's guardians approaches within 40 feet of the monolith

On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for one round.

On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for one round, then dazzled for one round.

The hazard rolls initiative.

Trigger A Medium or larger creature steps onto the middle of the bridge (any of the squares marked "T" on the map)

Effect The ropes on both sides of the bridge snap simultaneously, causing the bridge to collapse. Any creature on the bridge falls to the forest floor 50 feet below and takes @Damage[25[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage]. A creature within 10 feet of one end of the bridge can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18] saving throw to Grab a Ledge along the side of either A8 or A13.

If one side of the bridge has been disabled by resecuring the ropes, a character on any section of the bridge can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save to hang onto the bridge as one side of it falls. The dangling bridge thereafter functions as a ladder connecting either area A8 or A13 (whichever was secured) to the forest floor.

details.description: A tower of precariously stacked crates is rigged to fall when someone steps through a trip wire.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17] (trained) to carefully remove the trip wire.

description.value:
Trigger A creature trips the wire

Effect Several heavy crates come crashing down in the 10-foot square area marked on the map, dealing @Damage[(2d10+10)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature in that area (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save). A creature who critically fails the save is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

details.description: Unstable stalactites fall from the ceiling.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20|name:Safely remove Stalactite|traits:hazard,environmental] (trained) to remove a stalactite without triggering a fall

description.value:
Trigger A stalactite is damaged or touched

Effect The stalactite falls, making an attack against each creature underneath.

stealth.details: (trained; or 0 if the illusory floor is dispelled)

details.description: The floor in this chamber is an illusion, which conceals a 40-foot drop to the true floor below. Additionally, the area beneath the floor is magically silenced-no sound is audible within nor does sound leave this space.
details.disable: Succeeding on a @Check[perception|dc:21] check against the Stealth DC of 21 (including the check to find the trap) disbelieves the illusory floor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to dispel the illusory floor; dispel magic (2nd rank; counteract DC 20) to dispel the magical silence
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the illusory floor

Effect The triggering creature falls through the illusory floor and takes falling damage (typically @Damage[20[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage). That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling. The DC to [[/act climb dc=20]] the walls or [[/act grab-an-edge dc=20]] is 20.

details.description: The semitransparent image of a small Garundi woman at the podium suddenly begins lecturing on the nature of enchantment magic.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:17] to disrupt the lecture, @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to scratch out of one of the glyphs on the podium, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to counteract one of the runes; three total successes are required to disable the trap.
details.routine:
(1 action) (arcane, compulsion, enchantment, mental, nonlethal) The image lectures on incredibly advanced topics without pause, overwhelming the minds of those listening while compelling them to continue to pay attention. Each creature in the room must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:20|traits:arcane,compulsion,enchantment,mental,nonlethal] save and take @Damage[1d6[mental]]{1d6 nonlethal mental damage}, as well as the following effects.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} and takes half damage as it shakes off most of the mental load.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2} and takes full damage as it can't help but try and process all the information.

Critical Failure The creature is stunned for 1 round, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for 1 minute, and takes double damage as all its mental faculties are focused on the lecture. Damage from this hazard does not end the fascinated condition. A creature who is reduced to 0 Hit Points by the fascinating lecture enters a catatonic stupor until they regain Hit Points. If a creature spends 1 hour in the stupor, they automatically regain 1 Hit Point and awaken.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to touch the research papers on the podium.

Effect The papers are protected by a powerful ward of enchantment magic that lasts until the hazard is completely disabled; no creature can muster the will to touch them. All creatures within the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature must spend an action approaching one of the empty desks in the classroom.

Failure The creature must spend two actions approaching one of the empty desks in the classroom and taking a seat. They are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The creature spends its next turn approaching one of the empty desks in the classroom and taking a seat. It is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 1 hour and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for one minute.

details.description: The semitransparent image of a small Garundi woman at the podium suddenly begins lecturing on the nature of enchantment magic.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:20] to disrupt the lecture, @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to scratch out of one of the glyphs on the podium, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 20) to counteract one of the runes; three total successes are required to disable the trap.
details.routine:
(1 action) (arcane, compulsion, enchantment, mental, nonlethal) The image lectures on incredibly advanced topics without pause, overwhelming the minds of those listening while compelling them to continue to pay attention. Each creature in the room must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22|traits:arcane,compulsion,enchantment,mental,nonlethal] save and take @Damage[2d6[mental]]{2d6 nonlethal mental damage}, as well as the following effects.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} and takes half damage as it shakes off most of the mental load.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2} and takes full damage as it can't help but try and process all the information.

Critical Failure The creature is stunned for 1 round, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for 1 minute, and takes double damage as all its mental faculties are focused on the lecture. Damage from this hazard does not end the fascinated condition. A creature who is reduced to 0 Hit Points by the fascinating lecture enters a catatonic stupor until they regain Hit Points. If a creature spends 1 hour in the stupor, they automatically regain 1 Hit Point and awaken.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to touch the research papers on the podium.

Effect The papers are protected by a powerful ward of enchantment magic that lasts until the hazard is completely disabled; no creature can muster the will to touch them. All creatures within the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature must spend an action approaching one of the empty desks in the classroom.

Failure The creature must spend two actions approaching one of the empty desks in the classroom and taking a seat. They are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The creature spends its next turn approaching one of the empty desks in the classroom and taking a seat. It is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 1 hour and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for one minute.

details.description: The enchanted fountain spews large quantities of grape juice as its marble tiers spin with increasing speed, flinging juice in all directions.
details.disable:
@Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Grapple a Tier|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap,action:grapple] to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple] a tier and halt its momentum, followed by @Check[crafting|dc:18|name:Disable the Pump Mechanism|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap,action:disable-a-device] or @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Disable the Pump Mechanism|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap,action:disable-a-device] (while the tier is grabbed) to disable the pump mechanism through the gap that the previous checks left open. Grappling a tier requires 1 action. Each Crafting or Thievery check to disable a tier is a 2-action activity.

The fountain has three tiers, and fully disabling the fountain requires each tier to be disabled. For each disabled tier, the DC's to Grapple or disable another tier decrease by 2. If the creature critically succeeds at a check to disable a tier, it's immediately disabled, regardless of the number of checks that would otherwise be required due to Challenge Point adjustments.
details.routine: (1 action) The fountain accelerates its spinning, spraying juice 10 feet in all directions on its first turn, 15 feet in all directions on its second turn, and 20 feet in all directions on its third turn. Every square where it sprays juice becomes difficult terrain. At the start of its fourth turn, the fountain breaks, disabling the hazard and flinging juice 25 feet in all directions.

description.value:
Trigger Safa commands the fountain to start spinning at the beginning of Event 1: Safa's Arrival

Effect The fountain sprays juice in all directions, making every square adjacent to it difficult terrain. The trap rolls initiative.

Trigger A creature attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple] one of the fountain's tiers. Each tier of the fountain has one reaction per round

Effect The heavy, spinning marble has enough sheer force to crush the hands of those trying to stop it. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap] save before it rolls its check to Grapple the fountain.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to its next check to Grapple the fountain.

Success The creature takes no damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]] damage and takes a -2 circumstance penalty to its next check to Grapple the fountain.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+6)[bludgeoning]] damage and is flung 10 feet away from the fountain in a random direction, preventing its attempt to Grapple the fountain.

details.description: Reflections viewed in the water here appear monstrous and fiendish; a PC who views themself in these waters sees a distorted version of themself looking back at them, sneering and mocking.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20] to exorcise the haunt, or @Check[intimidation|dc:23] to turn the fear back on the haunt and overwhelm it with its own terror

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: The haunt resets at the end of the round automatically, ready to trigger again the next tome someone looks into the water.

description.value:
Trigger A creature looks at the water

Effect The creature sees its reflection on the water's surface distort into a fiendish version of itself; it must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:20] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} (and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for as long as it remains frightened on a critical failure).

details.description: Magical motion sensors detect when a creature enters the chamber.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to disable the sensor without triggering them, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to counteract the sensors

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the chamber

Effect The rune detonates in a ball of flame that fills the chamber, dealing @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save).

details.description: The demon lord Urxehl manifests upon Golarion a small region of his Abyssal realm, filled with constantly burning forests lashed by fierce storms and unholy rain that only further feeds the flames. This is a mystical fire, fueled by the presence of Urxehl's minions. Any flames on the map emit concealing smoke in a 10-foot radius around the fire.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:20] or @Check[performance|dc:20] (trained) to remind the Finadar forest of its true self; or @Check[religion|dc:20], @Check[occultism|dc:20], or @Check[demon-lore|dc:20]{Demon Lore}(trained) to disrupt the connection to the imposed realm and extinguish one of the five sections of flames on the map. These checks can only be attempted by creatures adjacent to the section, and each attempt takes 2 actions. An extinguished fire still emits concealing smoke for 1 additional round but is no longer hot enough to deal damage. A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] spell or a spell with the water trait (2nd level; counteract DC 17) extinguishes a section of flames. If the PCs disable three of the five sections of the flames of Verakivhan, the hazard is too weakened to sustain itself, and the remaining flames disappear. If there are no demons or elementals remaining in the area, the hazard is automatically disabled.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-18: The Fanciful March of Urwal

details.routine: (3 actions) Any creature within the flames takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[fire]] damage, and any creature adjacent to the flames takes @Damage[1d10[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|name:Flames of Verakivhan|traits:fire]). Additionally, the flames call down a fiery rain that deals @Damage[(1d10+6)[fire]] damage to a random PC within the planar overlay (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|name:Flames of Verakivhan|traits:fire] save). If any PCs attempted to disable the hazard that turn, the fiery rain chooses one of them as its target.

description.value:
Trigger A creature that is not a demon or elemental enters the planar overlay

Effect Flames ignite throughout the area. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: An invisible rune creates an invisible, spherical magical sensor with a 5-foot radius.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to erase the rune without triggering the sensor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2rd rank; counteract DC 16) to counteract the rune

description.value:
Trigger A living creature touches the sensor area.

Effect The rune detonates creating a flaming sphere in the sensor area. This is a 2nd-rank flaming sphere spell that deals @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic] save).

details.description: An invisible rune creates an invisible, spherical magical sensor with a 5-foot radius.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21] (trained) to erase the rune without triggering the sensor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2rd rank; counteract DC 18) to counteract the rune

description.value:
Trigger A living creature touches the sensor area.

Effect The rune detonates creating a flaming sphere in the sensor area. This is a 3nd-rank flaming sphere spell that deals @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save).

details.description: Daemonic curses slither and crawl through malevolent runes carved in four stone pillars. HP is per-pillar.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Disable Thievery] (trained) to scratch out a pillar's runes, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to counteract a pillar's magic, or @Check[athletics|dc:21|name:Disable Athletics|traits:action:force-open]{Athletics (Force Open)} to push a pillar over. Disabling a pillar requires two actions. Disabling all four pillars or slaying Sezruth disables the trap

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-21: In Pursuit of Water

details.routine:
Routine (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled pillar. On its initiative, the trap uses each action to afflict a different non-evil creature in the room with thirst. The creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20|name:Foretold Ruin|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is temporarily immune to additional negative effects from the foretold ruin hazard's reaction for 24 hours. The hazard has no awareness of this immunity and continues to target this creature.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued] until they drink water. If the creature was already fatigued, they take @Damage[1d6[nonlethal,mental]]{1d6 nonlethal mental} damage.

Critical Failure The creature is fatigued until they drink water and takes 1d6 nonlethal mental damage. If the creature was already fatigued, they instead take @Damage[2d6[nonlethal,mental]]{2d6 nonlethal mental} damage.

description.value:
Trigger A non-evil creature enters the room.

Effect The trap casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantom Pain] on the creature, and then rolls initiative.

@Check[will|dc:23|name:Phantom Pain|traits:mental,nonlethal] save

details.description: The font is hidden just under the water's surface. It spews foul brown sludge into the pristine waters, leaving them reeking and polluted as they move downstream. It can be reached without swimming, but not without getting wet.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:17] (trained) to draw upon the suppressed Primal energies of this place to cleanse the font, @Check[religion|dc:23] (expert) to suppress the void energies, @Check[thievery|dc:20] to undo the magic sigils allowing the font to operate; two total successes are required to disable the font.
details.reset: The foul font deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: (2 actions) The foul font uses its actions each turn to spew a single void geyser.

description.value: The foul font can take vitality damage, even though its an object and would normally be immune

Trigger One or more creatures touches the water within 10 feet of the foul font

Effect The font fires off a Void Geyser, causing a rain of foul water. The font then rolls for initiative.

The font shoots its foul brown sludge into the air, and it rains down upon all creatures within 15 feet that aren't completely submerged. Each of those creatures takes @Damage[3d8[void]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save). In addition, those that take damage from the font must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save.

Critical Success The target cannot be sickened by the font for 1 minute.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

Any creature that is submerged downstream (east) is automatically exposed to Spew Foulness at the start of its turn or when it enters those waters, except the damage is reduced with a @Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save instead of Reflex.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice a weird, faintly glowing ripple of the current beneath the water.

details.description: A swath of drowned spirits tries to pull someone down into the water to join them.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23] to calm the unquiet spirits in the waters for an hour, or light the Eternal Lantern (automatically disables the haunt)

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: The grasping currents quickly build up power and can trigger again after 10 minutes have passed.
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the grasping currents attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple] a swimming creature or capsize a rowboat by attempting an Athletics check with a [[/r 1d20+12 #Athletics]]{+12} modifier. A creature that's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] by the grasping currents is pulled underwater and risks drowning. To capsize a rowboat, the haunt's Athletics check is rolled against the pilot's Athletics or Sailing Lore DC, whichever is higher.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to cross the lake by swimming or piloting a boat

Effect Ghostly arms reach up to grab and clutch at the creature or the boat, causing a –2 penalty to the Athletics or Piloting check that triggered the haunt. The haunt then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (trained); @Check[perception|dc:23|traits:action:seek] (expert) to notice the construct limbs at the bottom of the pit.

details.description: The limbs of smashed constructs line the bottom of the fissure, beneath the water. They desperately grab anything that gets too close.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Immobilize Limbs] (trained) or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:19|name:Immobilize Limbs]{Engineering Lore} or @Check[azlant-lore|dc:19|name:Immobilize Limbs]{Azlant Lore} twice to immobilize the limbs, or @Check[deception|dc:18|name:Trick Limbs]{Deception} twice to trick the limbs into grasping each other.
details.routine: (2 actions) The grasping limbs try to Grapple all creatures in the water (Athletics [[/r 1d20 +12 #Athletics]]{+12}). They then tighten around all creatures they have grabbed or restrained, dealing @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic|name:Grasping Limbs]).

description.value:
Trigger A creature falls into the water

Effect The smashed constructs at the bottom of the water try to Grapple the triggering creature (Athletics [[/r 1d20+12 #Athletics]]{+12}). The trap rolls initiative.

hp.details: (if the grazing deer take this much damage, they aren't killed, but they do flee into the woods and don't return)

details.description: A herd of deer exits the woods to investigate the delicious-looking leshys.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:17|name:Befriend the Deer]{Diplomacy (with Wild Empathy)} to befriend the deer and convince them that leshys aren't for nibbling, or @Check[intimidation|dc:20|name:Scare the Deer Away]{Intimidation} to scare away the deer.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: A Fistful of Flowers

details.routine: (2 actions) The grazing deer takes one action to move about, and then one action to eagerly nibble at a randomly chosen PC. A character who spends three actions on their turn to Stride can avoid being nibbled by the grazing deer during the hazard's next turn. If all of the PCs do so, attempt a @Check[flat|dc:5|name:Escape the Deer]-on a success, the party escapes the deer and can proceed onward to Petalbrook, but they become delayed as a result of having to flee the pack of ravenous deer.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs emerge from the forest's edge

Effect A deer approaches the party and attempts a nibble Strike against a randomly determined PC. The grazing deer rolls initiative.

details.description: An eerie fog fills a lonely stretch of forest.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:22] (untrained) to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Request] that the spirits leave or @Check[religion|dc:20] (trained) to exorcise the spirits. All creatures and objects within the fog are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed], and all creatures and objects outside the fog are concealed to creatures within it.
details.reset: The haunt falls silent for 24 hours once all frightened creatures die or reduce their frightened condition's value to 0. During this time, a creature that isn't frightened can exorcise the spirits with a @Check[religion|dc:20] check, permanently destroying the haunt.
details.routine:
(1 action; auditory, emotion, fear, mental) The haunt attempts an Intimidation check with a [[/gmr 1d20+12 #Intimidation]]{+12} modifier against the Will DC of each @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened] creature as it fills their minds with haunting whispers.

Critical Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[mental]] damage and its frightened condition increases by 1, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}.

Success The creature takes @Damage[2d6[mental]] damage.

Failure The creature is unaffected.

Critical Failure The creature's frightened condition is reduced by 1.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the haunted area

Effect The spirits manifest, whispering in the minds of creatures in the fog. Each creature in the area must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). Frightened creatures are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] for as long as they remain frightened. The haunt then rolls initiative.

name: Hallways Full of Mostly Illusory Opossums

details.description: Several distressed opossums dressed in school uniforms and under the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Mirror Image] fly about the hallway, guided by four giggling sprites.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:20], @Check[deception|dc:20], or @Check[intimidation|dc:20] four times to convince one sprite to undo their spells; @Check[athletics|dc:22], @Check[diplomacy|dc:22] (with wild empathy) or @Check[nature|dc:22] to subdue or soothe the opossums; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to undo one sprites' worth of spells. Four successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-16: Dacilane Academy's First Great Prank War

details.routine: (4 actions) The hazard loses 1 action each turn for each Disable success. Each sprite takes one action to Strike a different randomly chosen PC.

description.value: @Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwarmMind]

Trigger A creature walks into the room

Effect The sprites scream "Surprise!," attempt a opossum flyby Strike, and roll initiative.

details.description: The spirits of innumerable dryads and peaceful wood elementals, ripped from their bodies as they were turned into furniture, writhe and seethe, lashing out at both living and undead alike.
details.disable:
@Check[diplomacy|dc:17] (trained), @Check[nature|dc:17] (trained), or @Check[religion|dc:17] (trained) to set the spirits to rest or @Check[deception|dc:20], @Check[intimidation|dc:20], or @Check[occultism|dc:20] to convince the spirits that their eternal rest is coming soon.

A PC may make a Strike against AC 16 to destroy a single table or chair in the room, this functions as a successful check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid] another PC attempting to disable the haunt; two total successes are required to disable the haunt. If all of the tables and chairs are destroyed, the haunt is temporarily disabled until dawn of the next day.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-99: Under the Eye of the Mantis

details.routine: (two actions) The Haunted Hollows haunt uses one table flip Strike or two chair toss Strikes at random creatures in the area, favoring those who are trying to disable the haunt first. The table flip Strikes originate with any table in the room, destroying the table in the process. The chair toss Strikes originate with any chair or bar stool in the room, destroying that object in the process. If the haunt is not disabled, these objects reform and return to their original positions at dawn of the next day.

description.value:
Trigger Any living or undead creatures approach within 30 feet of the haunt's locus (the center of the open balcony) inside the tavern

Effect The haunt makes a table flip Strike with a reach of 20 feet against a random creature in the area, and then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: Sophreista's ghost glows red, and shrieking in fury, she flies at her opponents.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:18] (trained) to subdue the spirit, @Check[religion|dc:18] to calm the spirit, @Check[deception|dc:16] or @Check[diplomacy|dc:16] to talk the spirit down; two total successes are required to disable the haunt
details.routine: (1 action) Sophreista lashes out at the nearest living creature, dealing @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save) and binding their fate to hers; a creature that critically fails their save is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1} (maximum @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 3}).

description.value:
Trigger The performance concludes with fewer than 8 Pathos Points

Effect Sophreista screams in rage; all living creatures in the area must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:20] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the runes carved into the lid.

details.description: The magical runes glow at any attempt to open this chest.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:19] (trained) to safely scratch out the runes or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 17) to counteract the runes.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-16: A Lie Told to Strangers

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the chest without speaking the command word

Effect The curse gives the target the sensation that countless ants are biting and stinging them for an entire day. The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 24 hours.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} for 24 hours.

details.description: A trapdoor in the floor conceals a stone chute.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Remove Trapdoor|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to remove the trapdoor or lock it in position using a catch hidden inside the room's doorframe

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor

Effect The triggering creature falls 30 feet down the shaft to area E33, taking @Damage[15[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage and landing @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in the ochre jelly's pit. The creature can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling.

The DC to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Climb] the walls or Grab an Edge is 22.

details.description: This fiery hot oven needs to be extra-large to keep up with the demands of the bakery—large enough for a Medium creature to crawl inside. Or be pushed.
details.disable: 1 Interact action and a @Check[athletics|dc:18] check to close the oven door after it's been opened.
details.reset: The oven door can be opened and closed freely, as described above, for the course of the entire combat. If left to its own devices, the oven burns through its fuel and goes out roughly 1 hour after it's lit.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to open the closed oven door without the protection of an oven mitt

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[(1d10+3)[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).

Trigger A creature moves or is forcibly moved into the open oven

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[3d10[fire]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic] save) and @Damage[2d6[persistent,fire]] damage. This damage repeats at the beginning of the creature's turn for as long as it remains inside the oven.

name: House Spirits With a Large Number of Regenerating Pies

hp.details: if the house spirits take this much damage, they aren't killed, but they do flee the school and don't return

details.description: A group of house spirits run around the lunchroom hurling pies at the walls and all the people in the room—and then the pies magically reconstitute and throw themselves again.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:20], @Check[deception|dc:20], or @Check[intimidation|dc:20] four times to convince the spirits to undo their spells, @Check[arcana|dc:18] or @Check[occultism|dc:21] to unravel a pie's enchantments, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to undo one pies' worth of spells. Three successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-16: Dacilane Academy's First Great Prank War

details.routine: (3 actions) The hazard regenerates any pies that it has thrown at a cost of one action per pie (to a maximum of 3 active pies). Each successful check to disable the hazard deactivates one of the pies, reducing the number of active pies by 1. After regenerating pies on its turn, the hazard throws pies whenever a creature triggers continually sur-piesed. For each pie Strike, roll [[/r 1d4]]. On a 1, the pie pan has nails driven into it by Numblekin and deals an additional @Damage[(1d10+6)[piercing]] damage. The nails make the pie's damage lethal, rather than nonlethal.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the room

Effect A pie from the room magically hurls itself at the creature and attempts a pie Strike, then the hazard rolls initiative.

Trigger A creature enters the room, attempts an action with the concentrate trait for the first time this turn, or ends their turn

Effect The hazard makes a pie Strike against the triggering creature.

details.description: A soot-blackened brazier glows with fiendish light, shadows fickering inside.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[occultism|dc:18], or @Check[religion|dc:18] while within 15 feet of the brazier to command it to stop, reducing its actions by 1 (or 2 on a critical success); @Check[thievery|dc:20] to fnd the hidden switch on the brazier that deactivates it; or 1st-rank spell, counteract DC 17, to dispel the shadow flames in one square, using any spell with the water or light traits.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-01: Intro to the Year of Battle's Spark: Enough is Enough

details.reset: The flames cease 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area, but after 1 hour, a well of darkness fills the brazier and it is ready to trigger again.
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap ignites shadowy flames in a @Template[line|distance:5]{5-foot square} within the area of the room A creature that moves into or ends its turn in this fire takes @Damage[(1d6+5)[fire]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic]) Increase the DC by 2 if the creature is in magical darkness (@Check[fortitude|dc:19|basic]).

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the area

Effect Shadows roil in the brazier. It casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Darkness] on itself, and then rolls initiative.

hp.details: per integrated chain gun; Plasma Lance Hardness 10, Plasma Lance HP 20 (BT 10)

stealth.details: (trained) to notice it's still functional; noticing the annihilator is automatic

details.description:
A gargantuan scorpion-like robot, covered in signs of damage, has been bolted into the canyon's rock walls.

Integrated Chain Gun Hardness 10, HP 40 (BT 20) per integrated chain gun

Plasma Lance Hardness 10, Plasma Lance HP 20 (BT 10)
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (expert) to successfully disable the annihilator at the control box on the northwest ledge, @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:15] (trained) to jam the mechanics of either of the two chain guns, or the "tail" mechanism where the plasma lance is mounted, preventing it from firing. Jamming the guns or tail requires first getting on top of the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] annihilator, which counts as difficult terrain. All attempts take 2 actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-24: Lightning Strikes, Stars Fall

details.routine: (2 Actions) When the plasma lance is ready, the annihilator uses it to attack as many PCs as it can, otherwise it attacks with its two integrated chain guns while the plasma lance recharges, spending one action for each of its two integrated chain guns that is still functional. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty. If Synthetic Khismar is destroyed, the annihilator attacks as many creatures as possible.

description.value:
The scorpion-like tail of the annihilator projects a @Template[line|distance:80] of burning plasma.

Creatures in the area take @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic]). The annihilator can't use the plasma lance again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Plasma Lance]]{1d4 rounds}.

details.description: The cauldron's ear-splitting keening calls forth spectral claws to assail ears and souls alike.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:22] to tip the cauldron, or @Check[occultism|dc:20] or @Check[religion|dc:20] (trained) to ward against the cauldron's effects. Any combination of three successful attempts is required to fully disable the haunt. When the haunt is disabled, the coven seeks vengeance.
details.reset: The haunt becomes inert (resuming its usual screaming) at the end of any round in which no creatures other than Kepgeda remain in her kitchen.
details.routine: (1 action) The spectral hands scurry about, grabbing at all of Kepgeda's foes in area E13. Each such creature takes @Damage[3d6[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:20|basic]) as the spectral claws assail them. A creature that takes mental damage from this effect doesn't reduce their frightened value at the end of their next turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than Kepgeda is within 15 feet of the cauldron for at least 1 round

Effect The screams increase in intensity, and a torrent of phantasmal, three-fingered hands with iron claws spews from the cauldron to assail creatures in area E13. Each creature in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23] save, with the following results. Kepgeda is immune to the haunt's effects, as she loves the cauldron's screams. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] as long as they remain frightened.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] as long as they remain frightened.

details.description: Barbed wire stretches across the passageway at the perfect height to tear into ankles.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to detach the wire

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into the trip wire

Effect The trip wire snaps free and coils around limbs. The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:23] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 hour. On a critical failure, the trip wire embeds into the creature's skin, making the creature clumsy 1 until it rests for 8 hours.

details.description: A magnetically charged platform draws in armored creatures, then flings them up into the sky, where they are struck by lightning.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Ground the Electricity|traits:electricity,magical,trap] (trained) to ground out the energy or @Check[arcana|dc:23|name:Neutralize the Magical Charge|traits:electricity,magical,trap] (trained) to neutralize the magical charge

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the platform

Effect All creatures within 10 feet of the platform must each attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23|traits:magical,trap] save. Creatures who are wearing metal armor or are made of metal suffer a -2 circumstance penalty to this save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is dragged 5 feet toward the platform.

Failure The creature is dragged onto the platform, which is quickly raised on chains 20 feet into the air. The creature is then struck by lightning for @Damage[(2d10+13)[electricity]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] on the platform ([[/act escape dc=20]]).

details.description: The air grows thick as the Planes of Metal and Air both bleed into the swamp once again. A small fissure opens, causing static electricity to build and, fueled by the increased presence of metal in the area, sparks begin to fly. Electrical discharges begin to break out reaching from objects to unlucky creatures.
details.disable: @Check[type:arcana,nature,thievery|dc:18] (trained) three times to counter the planar energies.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #22: Friends in Need

details.routine: (3 actions) On its turn each round, the electrical discharge randomly targets two creatures dealing @Damage[3d4[electricity]] damage. A targeted creature must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:18|basic] save.

description.value:
Trigger A non-elemental creature approaches within 20 feet;

Effect A bolt of electricity targets the creature for @Damage[3d4[electricity]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: At first, the tree looks small. Then the space around it warps to give the impression that it's a normalsized tree seen at a distance.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:20] or @Check[occultism|dc:20] twice to disperse the warped magic around the tree, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 19) to counteract the warped magic
details.reset: If the trap isn't disabled, it becomes dormant for 10 minutes if there are no creatures within 60 feet of it at the start of its turn.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap targets a random creature within 60 feet to try to bring it down to its size. That creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save. On a failure, the creature becomes Tiny in size until the beginning of its next turn. An affected creature's equipment shrinks with it but returns to its original size if removed. The creature's reach changes to 0 feet. This is a polymorph effect that has no effect on a Tiny creature. On a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} for the same duration.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 30 feet of the tree

Effect The tree seems to grow and shrink simultaneously. Each creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] saving throw. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] until the end of its next turn from the disorientation.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} as if unsure of the distance between places.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2}.

details.description: Ghostly smoke rising from an alchemical engine belches forth; the screeching sound of grinding gears echoes from the engine as it shutters to life.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:22|name:Eject the animating spirit|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] (trained) or @Check[engineering|dc:20|name:Eject the animating spirit|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] Lore (trained) to eject the animating spirit from its mechanical shell. Creatures gain a +2 circumstance bonus on their check if they use the airship's name, Harpy's Kiss, as part of their skill check, or a +4 circumstance bonus if they utter the name "Phera Wyndslow." The haunt remains active until its spirit is ejected, the haunt is destroyed, or there are no living creatures within 30 feet.

publication.title: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powder Keg

details.reset: The haunt deactivates and resets after 1 hour.
details.routine: (2 actions) The lonely machine spirit Belches Smoke or makes two red hot gear Strikes against two creatures within 30 feet.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the engine (marked with a "T" on the map)

Effect A loud whistle blows and all creatures within 30 feet take @Damage[2d6[sonic]] damage and become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic|traits:hazard,haunt,complex]; creatures aren't stunned on a success). The lonely machine spirit then rolls initiative.

The airship's engine issues forth a cloud of alchemical smoke in a @Template[cone|distance:30]. Creatures within the cone must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage and is sickened 2.

details.description: The stone slab tears free from its track with the ear-piercing sound of failing metal.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:20] (expert) to shore up the track so that it functions as intended, or @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to wedge the slab in place to immobilize it

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to move the slab;

Effect The slab rips free from its track and falls, slamming down in one direction while it kicks up twisted metal debris in the other. All creatures adjacent to the slab take 3d6 bludgeoning and @Damage[3d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic]).

details.description: Sigils carved into the turquoise sphere atop the altar illuminate. Moments later, a rush of water flows into the hallway.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:20] (trained) to neutralize the tidal magic, or @Check[survival|dc:23] to predict the changing tides, or @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to scratch away the sigils; two total successes are required to disable the trap.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.routine:
(1 action) The magic controlling the tides malfunctions, causing the tides to change every few seconds. Water flows rapidly between areas B2 and B4. Each creature in area B2, area B4, or the hallway connecting them must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20|traits:damaging-effect] save as the shifting tides buffet them, dealing @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is moved 10 feet along with the water.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is moved 20 feet along with the water.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the altar that contains the tidal control orb

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as water rushes down the hallway. Area B4 becomes flooded with 15 feet of water.

details.description: A cadre of evil spirits assails people's minds throughout the stretch of forest. The spirits first afflict their hosts with visceral images of their own deaths, then try to recreate their deaths by forcing their hosts to inflict similar wounds upon themselves. Creatures frightened by the haunt appear to have ephemeral masks with horns, tusks, and yellow eyes over their faces.
details.disable: Two @Check[occultism|dc:23|name:Exorcise the Haunt|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] or @Check[religion|dc:23|name:Exorcise the Haunt|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] (trained) to exorcise the spirits. A creature can either inflict 4 mental damage to the haunt or permanently lower the DC of checks to exorcise the spirits 1 by succeeding at a @Check[diplomacy|dc:20|name:Calm the Spirits|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] check to calm the spirits, a @Check[nature|dc:20|name:Ward Against the Spirits|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] check to use kami-specific warding incantations, a @Check[occultism|dc:20|name:Leverage Haunt Lore|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] check to leverage obscure haunt lore, or a @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Lesser Exorcism to Weaken the Haunt|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] check to use a lesser exorcism. Any creature currently frightened by the haunt is a potential target for inflicting damage to the haunt; mental and vitality damage only affect such a host creature if they choose to be affected, while force or similar damage harms both the haunt and the host equally.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-06: Lost on the Spirit Road

details.routine:
(3 actions) On its initiative, the haunt uses its first two actions to attempt to terrify a random target in its area that is not already @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]. The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20|name:Resist Terror|traits:emotion,fear,mental,hazard,haunt] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to this haunt for 24 hours.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target is frightened 1. The value of the frightened condition does not automatically decrease by 1 at the end of each of the target's turns. Instead, the target can attempt a @Check[will|dc:18|name:Reduce Frightened|traits:emotion,fear,mental,hazard,haunt] save at the end of each of their turns to remove the condition.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}. The value of the frightened condition does not automatically decrease by 1 at the end of each of the target's turns. Instead, the target can attempt a @Check[will|dc:18|name:Reduce Frightened|traits:emotion,fear,mental,hazard,haunt] save at the end of each of their turns to lower their frightened value by 1.

The haunt then spends an action to compels one of the creatures that it has frightened to harm itself with surrounding forest debris, its strength fueled by spirit possession. The creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:20|name:Resist Attacking Yourself|traits:emotion,fear,mental,hazard,haunt] save or attempt to Strike itself during its turn as its first action, using the haunt's statistics below. Note that frightened applies equally to the creature's attack roll and AC, and therefore does not affect the accuracy of the attack.

description.value:
Trigger Three or more creatures enter the haunt's area

Effect Spectral faces appear, and the haunt rolls initiative.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

details.disable:
@Check[thievery|dc:20|traits:complex,hazard|name:Disable Mechanism] on each corner mechanism (must disable all three working corners to disable the trap)

Each corner mechanism has hardness 8 and 15 HP
details.routine:
Each turn, the fountain uses 1 action. Roll a [[/gmr 1d10]] and look up the result on the following list to determine which action it uses that round.

  • 1-4 Single Target The statue spins about and sprays water at a single random target in the room. Roll a [[/gmr 1d4]] and count to the number you rolled, starting with the player on your left, to determine which hero gets attacked. The spray is a ranged Strike.
  • 5-7 Corner Spray The statue spins about, firing a spray of water at each corner of the room that still has a functioning corner mechanism. This hits any creature adjacent to the corner mechanism, dealing @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage. Each hero must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:15|basic|options:area-effect|traits:hazard], taking no damage on a critical success, half damage on a success, full damage on a failure, and double damage on a critical failure.
  • 8-9 Half Room The statue spins about, spraying half of the room with water. Roll any die. If the result is even, it sprays the west half; otherwise, it sprays the east half. This deals @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature in that half of the room, and each hero must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:15|basic|options:area-effect|traits:hazard] save.
  • 10 Full Room This works just like the half-room attack, but it targets everyone in the room.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice small animal skeletons among the mushrooms

details.description: The mushrooms emit powerful psychic blasts that overwhelm potential predators with exhausting psychedelic visions.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20] (trained) to navigate a safe path without triggering the mushrooms
details.reset: The mushrooms must rest for 24 hours before they can emit another Psychic Blast.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks through the mushrooms

Effect The mushrooms release a loud hum of psychic energy. The triggering creature takes @Damage[(2d8+8)[mental]] (@Check[will|dc:23|basic]). On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued].

details.description: A mold cloud puffs from spoiled spilled food.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:14|name:Remove Mold] (trained) to remove the mold from the food without triggering the spores.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-03: Escaping the Grave

description.value:
(inhaled, poison) The drained condition from the spores persists after the poison's duration ends.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:14]

Maximum Duration 2 rounds

Stage 1 1d6 poison damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 1d8 poison damage and drained 1 (1 round)

Trigger A creature moves into the mold's space or damages the mold. The mold can't use this reaction if it's in direct sunlight or if the damage was fire damage.

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within 10 feet are exposed to the mold's spores.

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 5-foot radius surrounds an invisible wall or floor rune the size of the creature to be summoned.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:18] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-11: No Time for Treason

details.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors.

Effect This trap summons a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Sod Hound]. The sod hound rolls initiative and remains for 7 rounds, after which the spell ends and the sod hound disappears. The sod hound also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. The summoned sod hound can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: A chill wind blows from a fissure in the cavern wall.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[nature|dc:18], @Check[occultism|dc:18], or @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained) to seal the rift, three successes total; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 17)
details.reset: The rift reopens after [[/gmr 2d10 #Years to reopen]] years. Killing the Path River Peeler permanently seals the rift.
details.routine:
(2 actions) The hazard uses its first action to repeat its Wind from the Netherworld reaction. If any patch of barrow claw remains, one of the patches chosen by the GM releases a cloud of spores. The spores fill the barrow claw's space and last until the beginning of the hazard's next turn. Creatures within the spores become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed], and all creature outside the spores become concealed to creatures within it.

A creature that enters the spores or starts their turn in the area takes @Damage[1d6[persistent,cold]] damage until they brush off the spores as an Interact action.

description.value:
Trigger The Path River Peeler enters the cave

Effect A gust of ice-cold wind from the Netherworld blasts from the rift in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15]. Creatures in the area take @Damage[2d4[cold]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic|options:area-effect] save). The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: Several dozen spear holes line the sides of the cavern walls, triggered by a false stone in the partially paved surface of the cavern floor. However, most of the spears used to arm the trap are broken and long forgotten on the dusty cavern floor.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to deactivate the trigger on the floor, or @Check[athletics|dc:23] to pry up the floor plate to dislodge it from the trigger.

description.value:
Trigger Downward pressure (such as by a footstep) is applied to the false stone

Effect The trap shoots several spears, making an attack against the triggering creature or object on the stone.

details.description: The Eternity Arch sparks with electricity, releasing bolts of lightning at each of the four largest time rifts.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[nature|dc:18], @Check[occultism|dc:18], or @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained) to seal each of the four time rifts, @Check[crafting|dc:20] (trained) to divert the lightning to another source, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 17) to counteract each time rift. Each attempt requires 2 actions. Skill checks to disable a time rift must attempted from adjacent to the rift.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled time rift. On each of the trap's actions, the Eternity Arch releases a bolt of lightning that activates that rift, dealing @Damage[2d6[electricity]] damage to all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:10] around the time rift (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save). It can release a bolt at each active time rift no more than once each turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of one of the time rifts

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[1d6[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save). The overloading arch rolls initiative.

details.description: Several swinging pendulums mounted with razor-sharp blades extend from the ceiling and slice into the room below.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] to jam the gears and prevent the trap from being activated

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

description.value:
Trigger This trap must be manually activated by a creature adjacent to the southwest gear stack;

Effect Blades swing down to strike at all creatures in area F2 before wrenching to a frozen halt against the opposite walls. All creatures in the central part of this room take @Damage[(6d6 + 3)[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic]).

details.description: A spirit appears and arrests the creature by clamping manacles around their wrists.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:18|name:Seize the manacles] to seize the manacles from the spirit, @Check[intimidation|dc:19|name:Order the spirit to stand down] (trained) to order the spirit to stand down, or @Check[occultism|dc:20|name:Exorcise the spirit] to exorcise the spirit
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the manacles

Effect The spirit attempts to lock the manacles around the triggering creature's wrists. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The spirit loosely places manacles around the creature's wrists. Removing the manacles requires a single action.

Failure The spirit clamps the manacles around the creature's wrists but fails to lock them. The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} until the manacles are removed as a 3-action activity.

Critical Failure The spirit clamps the manacles around the creature's wrists and locks them. The creature is off-guard and clumsy 1 until the manacles are removed ([[/act escape dc=23]], simple lock).

details.description: An old, dusty toy box creaks open and forcefully expels a series of cracked and broken toys.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:19] (trained) to exorcise the spirits of the toy box, @Check[athletics|dc:20] (trained) to hold the toy box closed momentarily, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:21] (trained) to convince the spirits that play time is over; three total success are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the room or if the toy box is destroyed. After 1 hour, if the toy box remains, the haunt reactivates. If disabled, the haunt resets after 1 day. The haunt is permanently destroyed when the attic whisperer in area D5 is destroyed.
details.routine: (1 action) The toy box belches forth a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] of toys, dealing 3d8 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (chosen randomly each time) with a @Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the toy box

Effect The toy box lid slowly opens with a groan, revealing a yawning chasm into nothingness. Each creature who can see the toy box must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 while the haunt is active.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 4}.

details.description: A trapdoor in the floor conceals a hidden chute, and a moving section of wall shoves victims into it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Jam the Trapdoor Shut|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to jam the trapdoor shut or activate a hidden bypass switch by twisting a fitting on the gas lamp

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor

Effect Anyone within 10 feet of the room's northern wall falls 40 feet down the shaft to area E33, taking @Damage[20[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage and landing @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in the ochre jelly's pit.

The victims can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling.

The DC to Grab an Edge is 20; the DC to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Climb] the walls is 22.

details.description: Magical motion sensors detect when a creature enters the chamber.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to disable the pressure plate

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

details.reset: The trap resets after 2 rounds. After 5 total attacks, it runs out of poisoned darts.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the pressure plate

Effect A poisoned dart shoots at the triggering creature.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:19]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d10[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d12[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} (1 round)

details.description: This ancient pile of bones appears stable, but the slightest disturbance topples them.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[stealth|dc:20] (trained) to shimmy past the bones without disturbing them, or deal any amount of damage to trigger the collapse from a distance

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves through the treacherous squares near the bone pile or damages the bones piled in one of the squares

Effect The bone pile collapses in a torrent of skeletal remains, spreading 5 feet in every direction from the bone pile's current area. This deals @Damage[(6d4+9)[bludgeoning]] damage to each creature within 5 feet of the bone pile (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic]). The area becomes difficult terrain.

details.description: One of the potions has decayed, putting it under immense pressure. Its contents have become poisonous. When handled improperly, the bottle bursts, sending glass shards flying everywhere.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Pressurized Bottle|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) to carefully place the bottle under a bucket or bowl.

description.value:
Trigger A creature tries to pick up or move the potion

Effect The bottle shatters into pieces that fill a @Template[burst|distance:5]. The burst deals @Damage[4[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save). A creature who takes any damage is exposed to fermented potion.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22]

Maximum Duration 4 hours

Stage 1 @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} (1 hour)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} (1 hour)

Stage 3 @Damage[2d8[poison]] damage and clumsy 2 (1 hour)

details.description: A spring-loaded glass jar containing purple dye and a thunderstone pops from the ground and explodes when a pressure sensor is triggered.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Remove the jar and thunderstone|traits:hazard,trap,mechanical] (trained) to remove the jar and thunderstone carefully or @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Reinforce the flagstone|traits:hazard,trap,mechanical] to reinforce the flagstone pressure sensor with an improvised wedge

publication.title: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powder Keg

description.value:
Trigger A creature or object weighing at least 50 pounds is placed on one of the flagstones running north to south on the west side of the yard (marked "T" on the map)

Effect A glass jar containing purple dye and a thunderstone pop from the ground between the flagstones. The sudden movement causes the thunderstone to clink against the jar and explode. All creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:10]{10-foot radius} take @Damage[2[sonic]] damage and must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:17|traits:hazard,trap,mechanical] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 round. In addition, each creature in that area is splattered with purple dye and showered with glass, taking @Damage[(1d4+2)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|traits:hazard,trap,mechanical]). Creatures splattered with purple dye take a -1 circumstance penalty to Charisma-based skill checks. Fully dying the affected hair or clothing removes this penalty. Dyed skin fades after a week but can be covered up with a successful @Check[deception|dc:12|traits:hazard,trap,mechanical,action:disguise]{Disguise check} and the appropriate cosmetics.

stealth.details: (trained) (or -10 and no minimum proficiency if the surface is disturbed)

details.description: A 15-foot-wide patch of water and sand attempts to submerge creatures that step onto it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18|name:Safely Disturb the Surface] (trained) to disturb the surface
details.reset: The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it settles over the course of 24 hours.
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the quicksand pulls down each creature within it. A creature that was submerged up to its waist becomes submerged up to its neck, and a creature that was submerged up to its neck is pulled under and has to hold its breath to avoid suffocation. A creature in the quicksand can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:20|traits:action:swim] check to Swim to either raise itself by one step if it's submerged to its neck or worse, or to move 5 feet if it's submerged only up to its waist. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down one step. A creature that Swims out of the quicksand escapes the hazard and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in a space adjacent to the quicksand patch. Other creatures can Aid the creature, typically by using a rope or similar aid, or attempt to pull the creature out with their own @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Aid Another Creature in Quicksand] check, with the same results as if the creature attempted the check.

description.value:
Trigger A Huge or smaller creature walks onto the quicksand

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the quicksand up to its waist. The quicksand rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

details.description: Magical sensors detect when a creature enters the chamber.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:21|name:Convince the Spirit|traits:complex,haunt] to convince the spirit to calm the winds, @Check[athletics|dc:19|name:Raise a Wooden Bench|traits:complex,haunt] to raise up a wooden bench to block the wind, or @Check[religion|dc:19|name:Speak an Appropriate Rite|traits:complex,haunt] to speak an appropriate rite to calm the spirit. Two successful checks are required to fully disable the haunt, and each attempt takes two actions. Each successful check removes one action from the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-02: The East Hill Haunting

details.reset: The storm continues raging for 1 minute unless disabled, and then dissipates, returning after 1 week.
details.routine: (2 actions) The haunt uses its first action to stir up a storm of debris against all creatures in the quadrant clockwise from where it last attacked. Creatures in the quadrant take @Damage[(2d4+4)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic]). For each additional action it has (at most 1 without adjustments from the scaling sidebar), the storm targets an additional quadrant, moving clockwise around the area. On the first round, it begins by attacking creatures in the northwestern quadrant.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the marked area of the haunt

Effect A ghostly figure manifests and conjures forth a telekinetic storm of debris from the area and then quickly disappears. The haunt rolls initiative.

details.description: A miniature makeshift catapult launches rocks when a creature triggers the trip wire.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:19] (trained) to disable the catapult without triggering the trap

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

description.value:
Trigger The trip wire is pulled or severed

Effect The catapult launches its load at the triggering creature.

details.description: The unstable rocks on the floor shift when stepped on, triggering rocks in the pile above to fall down across the pathway.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20|name:Navigate Safe Path|traits:trap] (trained) to navigate a save path or @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Bolster Rocks|traits:trap] (trained) to bolster the rocks on the floor so they don't shift under a creature's weight. Two successful checks are necessary to ensure the route through the precarious pile of rocks is safe. Attempting each check requires two actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-10: Delve the Pallid Depths

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on one of the unstable rocks

Effect Rocks come crashing down onto the creatures below. The rockfall deals @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage to each creature within @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10 feet} of the rock pile (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save).

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Failure The creature takes full damage, is knocked prone, and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its movement speeds for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is knocked prone, and takes a –20-foot circumstance penalty to its movement speeds for 2 rounds.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Rockfall Failure]

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Rockfall Critical Failure]

details.description: This trap appears to be an obvious 10-foot-deep pit with angled walls to prevent easy escape, but the true danger is represented not by the hole in the ground but from a set of loose stones in the ceiling. A knotted rope hangs from a beam above, seemingly to provide an option to swing across the pit, but any weight on the rope pulls down large, heavy boulders stacked far above.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to tie off the rope to a more solid support

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature or equivalent weight pulls on the rope

Effect All creatures within the pit or adjacent to the pit take @Damage[(4d6+10)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic] save) from falling rocks dislodged from above.

details.description: Rivulets of water begin to run from the circle of stones to gather in a growing puddle at the center.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (expert) to rub out the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] runes on the ceiling, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank, counteract DC 18) to counteract the trap
details.reset: The magic rejuvenates over 10 minutes, after which it is recharged and ready to activate again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area south of the ring of stones marked on the map

Effect A deluge of water rises up from the ring of stones, then flows forcefully down the passageway to drain into the pit at area F1b. Large and smaller creatures in the water's path must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20|traits:damaging-effect] save. The rushing waters vanish instantly upon striking the pit floor, but do leave everything down there sopping wet.

Critical Success The creature withstands the force of the rushing waters and is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d10[bludgeoning]] damage as the rushing waters slam them into a cave wall.

Failure The creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] by the rushing waters, takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]] damage, and is pushed by the water to the spiky pit trap and triggers the trap.
