Critical Failure As failure, but the creature can't attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] if they fall into the spiky pit trap.

details.description: As the large rusty door swings open, a mass of rusty spikes appear out of nowhere in the open doorway and launch violently into the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] to decouple the triggering rods that cause the spikes to extend, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to counteract the trap
details.reset: The trap automatically resets once the door is closed.

description.value:
Trigger a creature opens the door without uttering a prayer to Xar-Azmak first

Effect A mass of rusty spikes appears in the open doorway and then fire into area E1. The rusty spikes curve through the air to seek targets—all creatures in area E1 must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature avoids the spikes and takes no damage.

Success A spike grazes the creature, inflicting @Damage[2d10[piercing]] damage.

Failure A spike impales the creature, inflicting @Damage[(2d10+13)[piercing]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the spike also exposes the creature to tetanus.

An infection introduced through open wounds, tetanus can produce stiffness, muscle spasms strong enough to break bones, and ultimately death.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:14]

Onset 10 days

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} (1 week)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} and can't speak (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] with spasms (1 day)

Stage 4 death

details.description: A geyser of boiling water erupts at any character who attempts to take water from the oasis.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21] (trained) to concoct a mechanism for indirectly harvesting the water from a safe distance
details.reset: The geyser resets automatically and adapts to previous circumvention, requiring a new check to disable it each time.

description.value:
Trigger A creature dips a vessel or otherwise attempts to remove water from the oasis

Effect A jet of magical boiling water targets the triggering creature and affects all creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:5]{5 feet} of the target. Creatures caught in the burst take @Damage[(2d10+13)[fire]|options:area-damage] (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic|options:area-effect] save).

details.description: An illusory cyclops in oracular robes cries out a warning, but then dissolves into a violent phantasmal cyclops, which calls out for the Blind Formless Chaos That Lies Behind the Stars to accept his sacrifice. It then plunges an obsidian athame into a creature's chest, summoning a foul entity.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:18] (trained) to repair Chamenaiti's magic, @Check[religion|dc:20] or @Check[occultism|dc:20] (trained) to offset the prayer, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to counteract the warning

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.routine: a Rubbery crimson tentacles appear from a rip in the air and grasp at all creatures in the area, dealing @Damage[(1d8)[bludgeoning],(1d8)[acid]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger Sailing Mission 5 begins;

Effect The trap makes an athame Strike against a random creature, and then rolls initiative.

details.description: Ritual circles barely anchor the ancient stasis magic, which dissipates erratically when disturbed.
details.disable: @Check[hero-god-lore|dc:16]{Hero-God Lore} or @Check[occultism|dc:16] to convince the magic that it's fulfilled its objective; @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[nature|dc:18], or @Check[religion|dc:18] short-circuit the stasis magic; or @Check[thievery|dc:20] to disable the ritual circles.

description.value:
Trigger A creature physically contacts Merosyne

Effect The stasis affecting Merosyne is disrupted, but as the magic dissipates, it tries to escape into other nearby bodies, like electricity arcing toward nearby metal. Creatures other than Merosyne within 15 feet of the circles must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:21] save.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}. While drained by this effect, the PC takes a circumstance penalty equal to their drained value on all checks made as part of a reaction (such as Reactive Strike attack rolls).

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2}.

details.description: A cannon built of cast iron and infused with shadow magic, mounted on the side of Aslynn's flying ship.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:20] or @Check[thievery|dc:20] on the cannon's body to deactivate

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-08: Battle for Star's Fate

details.routine: (1 action) The cannon fires a blast that contains as many PCs as possible, choosing randomly in the event of a tie.

description.value:
Trigger The ship flies within 30 feet of Star

Effect The cannon rolls initiative.
to creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic])

details.description: The shaitan observes the area from a distance, observing the battlefield as a game board. The ground trembles in response to the genie's enthusiasm.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:23] (trained) or @Check[nature|dc:23] (trained) to contest the shaitan's control (requires two successes). Each success removes one of the traps actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-14: Shattering Golden Chains

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets once all hostile creatures leave A3 or surrender.
details.routine: (2 actions) On each of the trap's actions, the trap either moves a willing creature on the ground up to 30 feet without provoking reactions or makes a hostile creature on the ground @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] for 1 round (@Check[acrobatics|dc:23] check or @Check[reflex|dc:23] save negates).

description.value:
Trigger The shaitan sees a non-genie creature threatening an ally

Effect The trap rolls initiative and the shaitan takes control of the floor in A3, making it difficult terrain for hostile creatures while the trap is active.

stealth.details: (expert) to notice the floor sensor; DC 0 to notice the sphere

details.description: A gilded armillary sphere spins in place, shooting jeweled stars and planets at intruders.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (expert) to disable the sphere from rotating or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 20) to remove the enchantment.
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any creature moving within range of its sensor.
details.routine: (1 action) A jeweled planet shoots from the sphere, targeting a random creature within 30 feet. The creature takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the sphere, stepping on the floor sensor

Effect The sphere rotates and the trap rolls initiative.

details.description: A skeletal arm holding a sickle is concealed in the ceiling and connected to a mechanism in the sliding door below it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) on the door frame or ceiling groove, or Interact to operate a lever on the opposite side of the door (in areas D2 and D4, respectively)
details.reset: The trap resets after 15 minutes. Additionally, a creature can manually Interact with the lever on the opposite side of the door to reset the trap.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to open the door

Effect A skeletal arm swings a sickle from the ceiling, making a Strike against the triggering creature.

details.description: Singe's spirit lingers in the Whippoorwill's crow's nest, where it calls out warnings and shoots intruders.
details.disable: @Check[pathfinder-society-lore|dc:20] (trained) to persuade Singe that you are Pathfinders or @Check[religion|dc:20](trained) to perform rites to lay Singe to rest; two total successes are required to disable the haunt

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: d Singe uses its first action(s) to make a long gun Strike against a random creature in the area. It uses its final action for Lookout's Warning. If the PCs successfully use Pathfinder Society Lore to Disable the haunt, Singe loses an action to make a long gun Strike.

description.value:
Trigger One or more creatures steps onto the Whippoorwill's wreckage

Effect Singe makes a long gun Strike against a random creature in the area, uses Lookout's Warning, and then rolls initiative.

Singe lets out one of the following cries, in order (repeating from the top, if necessary). Everyone in the haunt's area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save. On a success, the creature is unaffected; on a critical success, it also gains a +2 circumstance bonus on its next save vs. Lookout's Warning. Other effects depend on the specific cry below.

  • Thar She Blows: "Captain, look! A huge whale! It's coming toward us—fast!" On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for 1 round, looking for the whale; on a critical failure it's fascinated for 1 minute, and the condition doesn't end if Singe makes a Strike on an ally or uses Lookout's Warning.
  • No Quarter: "It's coming around again! It's relentless! Brace for impact!" On a failure, the creature is tossed off balance; it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] for 1 round. On a critical failure, it's also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].
  • Shark Bait: "Captain, it's hopeless! It's going to destroy the ship!" On a failure, a sense of doom fills the creature, which becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}. On a critical failure, the fear is overwhelming, and it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.
  • Going Down with the Ship: "Get below, captain, and secure the Forked Key! I'll try to give you time!" On a failure, the creature must spend one action putting an item it is holding away; if it is not holding anything, it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for one action. On a critical failure, it must spend three actions in a combination of putting away any items it is holding and fleeing.

details.description: The floor wobbles with each step.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] to prop up the floor, or @Check[crafting|dc:20] (trained) to reinforce subflooring

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: immediate

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves on the unstable floor

Effect The floor tilts into a steep incline. Any creature on the tilting floor must succeed at either a @Check[reflex|dc:23] save or a Acrobatics check to [[/act balance dc=20]] (the creature chooses) or else take @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]] damage as it slams into the wall.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage. It keeps its footing and can move freely across the floor this round.

Success The creature takes no damage but is somewhat off-balance; the floor is difficult terrain for the creature this round.

Failure The creature slides backward into the wall, taking full damage.

Critical Failure The creature slides backward into the wall with great force, taking double damage.

details.description: With a rumble, this statue of a former Pathfinder slides across the schoolyard. Its legs pump up and down as it advances.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:23|name:Hold Back Statue|traits:complex,hazard,magical,trap] to hold it back, @Check[crafting|dc:20|name:Break Statue|traits:complex,hazard,magical,trap] (trained) to break the statue by exploiting a flaw, @Check[occultism|dc:18|name:Dispel Planar Energies|traits:complex,hazard,magical,trap] (trained) to dispel the planar energies.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-09: The Seven Secrets of Dacilane Academy

details.routine: (3 actions) The trap uses its three actions for its Trample activity.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the trap.

Effect The statue Strides up to 30 feet in the direction of the triggering creature. It deals @Damage[(1d10+6)[untyped]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|traits:complex,hazard,magical,trap] save) to each creature whose space it enters.

Medium or smaller, crushing feet @Check[reflex|dc:20|basic]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Trample]

details.description: A malfunction in the door's opening mechanism has caused unstable magic to pool within it, making it liable to release the sorrows it has collected from all who have mourned their lost homeland at its threshold.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[deception|dc:23] to coax the door's magic into allowing access; the DC is reduced by 2 if someone in the room has said "Tar Khadurrm" in the last hour.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the door

Effect The last of the door's magic arcs outward. All creatures within @Template[burst|distance:10]{10 feet} of the door must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:20] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 hour.

details.description: Loose debris covers a patch of what appears as solid ground in a 10 feet by 5 feet area. It's actually covering thin webbing strung over a 20 foot pit.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to deconstruct the webbing or @Check[acrobatics|dc:20] to pull the webbing apart without getting trapped or hit by rocks.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-13: All That Glitters

details.reset: 1 week

description.value:
Trigger A creature that isn't a spider walks onto the webbing

Effect The triggering creature falls in, taking @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save from rocks tumbling into the pit.

details.description: A wooden trapdoor covers a pit that's 10 feet square and 20 feet deep.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:12|name:Remove Trap Door|traits:mechanical,trap] to remove the trapdoor

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #10: The Broken Scales

details.reset: Creatures can still fall into the trap, but the trapdoor must be reset manually for the trap to become hidden again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage plus @Damage[3d8[piercing]] damage from sharp implements at the bottom of the pit. That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

details.description: The image of a ghostly warrior her holy symbol dripping blood and pulsing with negative energy, steps from the wall and seeks to kill any living creature.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Exorcise Spirit|traits:complex,haunt] (trained) to exorcise the spirit, @Check[religion|dc:21|name:Sever Spiritual Connection|traits:complex,haunt] or @Check[occultism|dc:21|name:Sever Spiritual Connection|traits:complex,haunt] (trained) to sever the connection between Hunger's Call and the temple, or @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Scratch Eyes out of the Mural|traits:complex,haunt] (trained) to scratch the eyes out of the mural; three total successes are required to disable the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Intro #2: United in Purpose

details.reset: The spectral warrior returns to the wall and ceases 2 rounds after all living creatures are out of sight of the stairs. After 6 hours the haunt is ready to trigger again.
details.routine: (3 actions) The warrior's blade sweeps through the bodies of any living creature within sight of the exit stairs, and they each take @Damage[(1d10+4)[slashing]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic|name:Avoid the Warrior's Blade|traits:complex,haunt,slashing] save). Undead creatures in the area gain 5 temporary hit points.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature bearing Hunger's Call moves within 30 feet of the exit stairs

Effect Blood pounds in the targe's body as their heart races uncontrollably. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20|name:Resist Racing Blood|traits:complex] save, and the haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

Critical Failure As failure, and the target's move speed is reduced by 10 for 1 minute.

details.description:
Four techno-organic "bush" structures featuring mini-acid pumps activate. The first lashes out with sharp "branches" as it activates, and then all of the bushes spit acid at movement nearby.

HP is 10 per bush with a BT of 5
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|traits:action:disable-a-device] or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:20|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) on each bush to collapse its acid pump into component parts (2 actions per bush).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-24: Lightning Strikes, Stars Fall

details.routine: (3 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each bush disabled. On each of the hazard's actions, one of the functional pumps spits acid at a creature within range. The bushes sense movement, only targeting creatures that moved from their space on their most recent turn. This trap does not attack any single creature more than once per round. It does not take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into a square adjacent to a bush

Effect The bush rakes its sharp branches across the creature, making a single branch Strike.

details.description: Discreet webbing at throat level snags a creature that walks into it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[thievery|dc:20] (expert) to rearrange the webbing.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-18: The Fanciful March of Urwal

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the square with the web tripwire.

Effect The web lurker noose makes a noose Strike against the triggering creature.

On a successful strike, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] and pulled off the ground ([[/act escape dc=22]]).

The target takes @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage at the end of each of its turns as long as it's caught in the noose.

details.description: A nozzle attached to a pressure sensor under the flagstone path sprays thick wads of smelly sap.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|name:Clog the nozzle|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap] (trained) to clog the nozzle or @Check[athletics|dc:14|name:Redirect the stream|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap] (trained) to redirect the stream away from the path; the Athletics check can be made at a distance with a thrown object, but a critical failure triggers the trap's Sudden Spray.

publication.title: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powder Keg

details.reset: 1 minute. The trap resets automatically twice before the supply of stink sap is exhausted.

description.value:
Trigger A creature or object weighing at least 50 pounds is placed on one of the flagstones north of the gate (marked "T" on the map)

Effect Pressurized sap sprays from the nozzle, coating everything in a @Template[cone|distance:15]. Creatures in the area that fail a @Check[reflex|dc:20|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap] save become affected by stink sap.

5 feet. Creatures and their equipment coated in the sap smell horrible. Creatures in the aura must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:16|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap] save. On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}, and on a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}. While within the aura, the creature takes a -2 circumstance penalty to saves to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is immune to stink sap for 1 minute. Creatures don't save against this effect in exploration mode and don't become temporarily immune, but creatures with stink sap on them must roll against their own aura when they roll initiative in encounter mode. The sap dissipates after 10 hours; spending 10 minutes scrubbing the sap off with soap and water reduces the duration by 1 hour.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Stink Sap]

stealth.details: (trained) or @Check[perception|dc:18|name:Notice Control Panel|traits:hazard,complex,fire,magical,trap] check to notice the control panel

details.description: A @Template[burst|distance:5] pillar of magical fire travels along a track throughout the room. The pillar emits bright light out to 20 feet.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:23|name:Deactivate Control Panel|traits:complex,fire,magical,trap,hazard] (trained) or @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Deactivate Control Panel|traits:complex,fire,magical,trap,hazard] (trained) once on the control panel deactivates the whole trap, or @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Deactivate Tracks|traits:complex,fire,magical,trap,hazard] (trained) twice on the track prevents the pillar from moving. Each check requires two actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-06: Struck by Shadows

details.reset: The trap deactivates once it no longer detects the presence of creatures in the room.
details.routine: (2 actions) The pillar Strides, following the track in the room. If the trap's track is broken, the pillar can still Stride, but its Speed is only 15 feet. The pillar cannot move at all if the track is destroyed. When the pillar moves, it deals @Damage[(1d10+6)[fire]] damage to any creatures whose space it moves through.

description.value:
Trigger Skyreach's defenses are active, and a creature who is not carrying a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Wayfinder] enters the room.

Effect A pillar of magical fire surges forth. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: A portion of Manaket's sewer system is under construction and has been left covered by only a few planks of wood since the Godsrain. After two weeks of wear, they're ready to collapse.
details.disable: If all the PCs succeed on a Perception check against the Stealth DC to spot the hazard, they're able to detour around the weakened boards without triggering it. There's not enough time for PCs who succeed on this check to warn PCs who fail.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-03: Godsrain in a Godless Land

description.value:
Trigger One or more PCs fail to spot the hazard and step onto the weakened boards

Effect The boards give way, dropping the PCs 5 feet into the open septic tank. The PCs can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge]; those who fail and fall into the tank become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} and cannot reduce this condition until after the Ambush! in Kassi's lab.

Triggering this hazard also accrues 1 AP for the party.

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors floats in the square above an invisible floor rune.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:18] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to disrupt the rune's summoning effect; alternatively, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 17) to counteract the rune
details.reset: The trap resets after 24 hours.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors

Effect The trap summons a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Cockatrice]. The cockatrice rolls initiative and remains for [[/gmr 2d6 #Summoning Duration]]{2d6 rounds}; after this duration, the spell ends and the cockatrice disappears. The cockatrice also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. The cockatrice can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: Gentle crying rises to become overwhelming wails. The fungal carvings begin to weep blood that pools around the pillars
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:20] (trained) to overpower the frightening cries with a greater sound or @Check[occultism|dc:18] or @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained) to ritually disrupt the haunting.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-08: The Haunted Corridor

details.reset: If the haunt is not dispersed, it resets after 1 day.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the carving of Viore Ciobanu

Effect Blood begins to rain from the ceiling. The cries from the statues deal @Damage[(2d10+13)[mental]] damage to living creatures within the room (@Check[will|dc:20|basic|options:inflicts:frightened] save). On a failed save, creatures are also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for 1 minute on a critical failure).

stealth.details: (or 0 if the trap door is disabled or broken) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Detect Magic]

details.description: Decorated smooth-hewn flagstone flooring gives way along hidden seams to a 20-foot square, 25-foot-deep pit festooned with spikes and blades of petrified wood in the darkness below.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to disable the trap door mechanism

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-12: The Burning of Greensteeples

details.reset: Creatures can still fall into the trap, but the trap door must be reset manually for the trap to become hidden again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trap door

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes falling damage (typically @Damage[10[bludgeoning]] damage). That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

When creatures fall into the pit, the petrified wood spikes along the floor grow to attempt to plunge into the triggering creature.

details.description: On the surface, a titanic flytrap appears to be a patch of the more common flytrap plant, but beneath murky waters it hides a far larger set of jaws, reaching 10 feet across and reinforced with woody branches and lined with paralytic hairs.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:21] (trained) to mislead the flytrap's sense of weight and pressure
details.reset: 1 hour (or longer, after a large meal)

description.value:
Trigger A Small or Medium creature moves into a square that is within reach of the flytrap's hidden jaws

Effect The flytrap's jaws snap shut, making a jaws Strike against the triggering creature.

The target is trapped by the flytrap's jaws, gaining the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] condition until it [[/act escape dc=21]]{Escapes}. Additionally, it is exposed to the titanic flytrap toxin from the hundreds of tiny hairs that line the inside of its leaves. If the flytrap's jaws Strike was a critical success, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its saving throws against this poison. At the end of each of target's turns that it remains grabbed, the target takes @Damage[2d8[acid]] damage.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:20]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[2d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[2d10[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] (1 round)

details.description: Pressure sensitive panels in the floor connect to a barrel of debris hidden in the hallway's ceiling
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Disable Floor Panels|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) on the floor panels before the barrel falls

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #10: The Broken Scales

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is placed on any floor tile

Effect The barrel of debris tips over and showers broken glass down. The glass deals @Damage[3d8[slashing]] damage and @Damage[2[bleed]] damage(@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|name:Dodge Falling Debris|traits:mechanical,trap] save) to all creatures within 10 feet.

details.description: A patch of treacherous bog attempts to submerge creatures that step onto it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18|name:Disturb Surface|traits:secret,action:sense-direction,exploration] (trained) to disturb the surface and make the quagmire plainly visible.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-08: Revolution on the Riverside

details.reset: The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it settles over the course of 24 hours
details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the quagmire pulls each creature within it 1 square toward the center of the quagmire and 1 step further submerged; a creature that is submerged up to its waist becomes submerged up to its neck, and a creature that is submerged up to its neck is pulled under and has to hold its breath to avoid suffocation.

A creature in the quagmire can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Swim Out of Quagmire|traits:environmental,hazard,action:swim] check to Swim to either raise itself by 1 step if its submerged to its neck or worse, or to swim out of the quagmire if its submerged up to only its waist. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down 1 step. A creature that Swims out of the quagmire escapes the hazard and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] adjacent to the quagmire. Other creatures can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid] the creature, typically by using a rope or similar tool, or attempt to pull a creature within their reach out with a @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Pull Out of Quagmire|traits:environmental,hazard,action:aid] check, with the same results as if the creature attempted the check.

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature walks into a quagmire.

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the quagmire up to its waist in its current square. The quagmire rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

details.description: The footing on this sloped ground appears to be stable at first glance, but the tiny rocks that cover it are loosely packed and prone to slipping.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20|name:Navigate Safe Path] (trained) to navigate a safe path

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the unstable ground.

Effect Rocks tumble and slip beneath its feet. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21|traits:damaging-effect] save as they tumble against the rocks, which deal @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage and falls prone in its space.

Failure The creature takes full damage, falls prone in its space, and tumbles down to the bottom of the slope.

Critical Failure As failure, but double damage.

details.description: The footing on this sloped ground appears to be stable at first glance, but the tiny rocks that cover it are loosely packed and prone to slipping.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20] (trained) to navigate a safe path

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-16: The Heart of the City

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the unstable ground

Effect Rocks tumble and slip beneath its feet. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21] save as they tumble against the rocks, which deal @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage and falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in its space.

Failure The creature takes full damage, falls prone in its space, and tumbles down to the bottom of the slope.

Critical Failure As failure, but double damage.

details.description: A large pile of treasure trembles precariously, threatening to collapse down into a crashing wave of coins and fine objects.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to remove the right pieces from the treasure pile and induce a controlled collapse.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 5 feet of the treasure pile

Effect Treasure slides down the side of the triggering creature in a large wave. All creatures within 15 feet of the falling treasure must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save as an onslaught of treasures bears down on them, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is trapped in treasure, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until they @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] the collapsed treasure (DC 20).

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is buried in treasure, requiring another creature to dig them out of the treasure before they can Escape.

details.description: At the unluckiest time, the shoddily supported tunnel wall collapses in on itself and floods the tunnel with dirt and stone.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:18] check to counteract the aura of unluck that pervades the tunnels for 1 minute, or @Check[survival|dc:20] to support the tunnel ceiling properly without triggering a collapse

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the tunnel

Effect The tunnel falls in, dealing @Damage[2d10[untyped]] damage to each creature in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save). Creatures in the area must spend at least 5 minutes digging themselves out to continue (@Check[survival|dc:20] halves this time).

stealth.details: (trained) Characters automatically detect this hazard when they open the door to the Grand Hall.

details.description: Concerned citizens shout slogans about the dangers of dealing with Cheliax and throw bricks at Chelaxian sympathizers.
details.disable: Succeed at two of the following checks in any combination: @Check[athletics|dc:20] (trained) to confiscate dangerous debris, @Check[diplomacy|dc:20] (trained) to calm the crowd, @Check[intimidation|dc:18] (untrained) to scare the crowd away, @Check[will|dc:22] (trained) to maintain a brave and confident demeanor; an effect such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Calm] that can target an area counts as one automatic success.
details.routine: (three actions) The unruly crowd uses an action to chant a Catchy Slogan if it is not already benefiting from the effect It uses Shake Confidence once per turn and uses its remaining actions for cobblestone Strikes.

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door to the Grand Hall

Effect The unruly crowd rolls Initiative.

All the hazards in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Catchy Slogan]

The unruly crowd targets a creature within 30 feet The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and immune to Shake Confidence for 24 hours

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}

Critical Failure The creature becomes frightened 2 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1}.

details.description: Giant mushrooms sprout from the ground, their caps reaching up to 15 feet high. Each giant mushroom flls up one 5-foot square and can appear anywhere within 20 feet of the center of the ring except adjacent to another giant mushroom. The ring can be disabled but not damaged before it activates.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:16] (trained) or @Check[occultism|dc:16] (trained) to repair the runes, @Check[survival|dc:20] (trained) to disturb the soil within the ring to weaken the mushrooms; one successful check to disable this hazard reduces the mushrooms' hardness to 0, and two total successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-12: Mischief in the Maze

details.reset: The giant mushrooms start to wilt and disappear 1 minute after all conscious living creatures have left the area. After 1 hour, the magic recharges, and the hazard is ready to trigger again.
details.routine: (two actions) A mushroom adjacent to one or more creatures uses Poison Spores. If there are no adjacent creatures, the mushroom vanishes in the ground and reappears in another square, making a Sprout attack. If no conscious creatures are within 20 feet of the center of the ring, the mushroom does nothing. A mushroom will not sprout underneath an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] creature. Each mushroom only acts once.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches a standing stone or enters the ring

Effect Two giant mushrooms sprout from the ground, each making a Sprout attack. The hazard then rolls initiative.
Adjacent creatures take @Damage[(1d4+3)[poison]|traits:nonlethal]{1d4+3 nonlethal poison} damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic] save).

A giant mushroom appears in a creature's square, dealing @Damage[(1d6+3)[bludgeoning]|traits:nonlethal]{1d6+3 nonlethal bludgeoning} damage and pushing the creature to an adjacent square. The creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:17] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and can choose the square to be moved to.

Success The creature takes half damage and can choose the square to be moved to.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is randomly pushed to an adjacent square.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage, falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], and is randomly pushed to an adjacent square.

details.description: An unstable section of limestone crumbles under the weight of anyone who steps on it.
details.disable: @Check[engineering-lore|dc:20], @Check[mining-lore|dc:20], or @Check[survival|dc:23] to determine the exact extent of the unstable ground and to optionally cause it to collapse safely into area A2 below

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature walks onto the weak limestone

Effect The ground collapses, and the triggering creature falls 15 feet into area A2 below. The creature can try to [[/act grab-an-edge dc=23]] to avoid falling, otherwise the fall inflicts @Damage[7[bludgeoning]] damage plus an additional @Damage[(4d6+3)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic] save) from falling rubble.

details.description: A ledge overlooks a yawning chasm.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:20] to tread lightly across the overlook onto more stable ground. Permanently disabling the hazard requires several hours of work and a successful @Check[crafting|dc:23] check (trained).

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature moves onto the weakened section of the overlook

Effect The ledge collapses. Any creature on the ledge that fails to [[/act grab-an-edge dc=20]] plummets 40 feet into the chasm below.

stealth.details: (trained) to feel an ominous presence in the room

details.description: An invisible force bats creatures aside, hurling them into a nearby wall.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:19|name:Disperse the Force] (trained) to disperse the force or @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Ward yourself from harm] (trained) to ward yourself from harm
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the haunted wall

Effect A powerful force sweeps across the room, shoving all creatures in the room toward the wall. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is pushed 5 feet toward the wall. If the creature would collide with the wall, they take @Damage[(1d10+5)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Failure The creature is pushed 10 feet toward the wall. If the creature would collide with the wall, they take @Damage[(2d10+10)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature is pushed 15 feet toward the wall. If the creature would collide with the wall, they take @Damage[(3d10+15)[bludgeoning]] damage and are knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

hp.details: to destroy the mechanisms in one wall

details.description: A series of rotating, serrated blades attached to long poles routinely slice out of grooves carved into the stone walls.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to disengage the mechanisms that operate the blades in one wall
details.reset: The trap deactivates when the room is empty and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (3 actions) For each wall in which the mechanisms have been disabled or destroyed, the hazard has 1 fewer action. On each action, the hazard makes a serrated blade Strike against one creature within 10 feet of the west, east, or south wall. Each set of wall blades acts once per turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature is within 10 feet of a trapped wall

Effect The hazard makes a serrated blade Strike against all creatures within 10 feet of the west, east, or south wall; these Strikes don't take a multiple attack penalty. The hazard then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: A web tripwire lets loose a bundle of boulders that fall on all targets within a 10-foot square.
details.disable: Survival (trained) or Thievery (expert) DC 20 to rearrange the webbing.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the square with the web tripwire.

Effect All creatures in the trap's 10-foot square take @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save).

details.description: The carving weeps. Its tears turn to a mist that covers the halls.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20] (trained) to recite prayers for the dead, or @Check[dwarven-lore|dc:17] to speak the history of Raseri Kanton, or @Check[thievery|dc:23] to plug the holes in the carving

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: The trap resets whenever a creature enters the Labyrinth for the first time.

description.value:
Trigger A creature looks at the carved mourner

Effect The hallways fill with a thick mist. When the mist first appears, all living creatures in area A take @Damage[2d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save), and all undead creatures regain @Damage[2d6[healing]] Hit Points. All creatures within the mist become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed]. This mist lasts for 1 hour or until the haunt is disabled.

details.description: The wind grows fierce as a small planar fissure opens, blowing around leaves and creatures alike.
details.disable: @Check[type:arcana,nature,thievery|dc:18] (trained) three times to counter the planar energies.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #22: Friends in Need

details.routine:
(3 actions) On its turn each round, the wind begins blowing fiercely from a random direction. All creatures must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is moved 5 feet in the direction the wind is blowing.

Failure The creature is moved 10 feet in the direction the wind is blowing and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature is moved 10 feet in the direction the wind is blowing, is knocked prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.

description.value:
A non-elemental creature approaches within 20 feet

Effect All creatures within 20 feet must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:18] save; results are as if the hazard used its normal routine. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: A hopper full of wooden sling bullets is attached to several launching devices, which spew the bullets at high velocity through various holes in the boards covering the window. A distinctive "click" and whirring sound indicate the trap has been sprung.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:reinforce the porch sensor|traits:complex,hazard,trap,mechanical] (trained) to reinforce the porch pressure sensor or @Check[crafting|dc:15|name:Cover the holes|traits:complex,hazard,trap,mechanical] (trained) to cover the holes launching the bullets

publication.title: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powder Keg

details.reset: The trap stops after 10 rounds or when the weight on the porch is removed, whichever comes first. The trap can operate for a total of 10 rounds before its ammunition is exhausted.
details.routine: (2 actions) The wooden bullets trap Strikes the two nearest creatures. It ignores @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] creatures as the bullets fly overhead.

description.value:
Trigger A creature or object weighing at least 50 pounds is placed on the porch (marked "T" on the map)

Effect A stream of wooden sling bullets flies from a launcher. The trap Strikes the two nearest creatures and then rolls initiative.

details.description: The grove, broken and tormented due to the necromantic energy infecting the area it protected, manifests an illusion to warn others of the region's plight and plead for help. Pharasmin cleric and Pathfinder Seldrick Dralston trudges into the grove, injured and infected with some kind of evil corruption. He defiles the statue of Erastil in the center of the grove, and the golden light illuminating the grove ceases, revealing the black skies above. Purple magical energy emanating from a nearby tower seeps into the sacred grove, and the life within the grove's protection begins to die.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:22] (trained) or @Check[religion|dc:22] (trained) to convince the spirit of the wood the message has been understood

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-02: Return to the Grave

details.reset: The haunt returns every 10 minutes to retell its tale. The haunting of the sacred grove is permanently put to rest if the shrine is reconsecrated, which is impossible unless the evil forces in the nearby watchtower are defeated.

description.value:
Trigger At least 2 creatures step into the circle of trees

Effect The haunted grove creates a vision steeped in its despair to tell the tale of a cleric of Pharasma's fall to undead corruption and his subsequent desecration of the holy shrine, allowing necromantic energies to infest the grove. All creatures within the circle of trees must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save.

Critical Success The creature sees through the illusions entirely and is temporarily immune to the haunt for 1 hour.

Success The creature is unaffected by the strange images.

Failure The creature experiences a phantom pain from the Sacred Grove as it had suffered in the moment of Seldrick's fall. The haunt deals @Damage[4d6[mental]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is left with a lingering suspicion of others and can't benefit from Aid reactions for 24 hours.

stealth.details: (trained) to determine the trees can move

details.description: A forest of fiendish trees swing their branches and shift their roots to bludgeon and trip creatures in the tainted grove (area A21).
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:22|name:Find a Safe Location|traits:hazard,complex,environmental] (trained) to find a location the trees can't reach or @Check[religion|dc:24|name:Cast Out the Fiendish Energy|traits:hazard,complex,environmental] to recite a prayer that temporarily casts out the fiendish energy inside a single tree in the grove, disabling it for 1 minute. A disabled or destroyed tree creates a 5‑foot‑square area in the grove that's safe from this hazard.
details.reset: The Abyss-warped trees cease moving 1 minute after no living creatures are in the tainted grove. They reset immediately. A disabled tree reanimates after 1 minute. A destroyed tree regrows after 1 month.
details.routine: (4 actions) The Abyss‑warped trees use their first action each round to lash their roots; each creature in the tainted grove must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:21|traits:hazard,complex,environmental] save or fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. The Abyss‑warped trees use their second, third, and fourth actions to make branch Strikes against up to three different creatures in the tainted grove.

description.value:
Trigger A creature ends its movement in the grove

Effect The trees throughout the tainted grove shift their roots wildly, making the area difficult terrain, then the Abyss-warped trees roll initiative.

Trigger The Abyss-warped trees critically hit a creature with a branch Strike

Effect The Abyss-warped trees loop their branches around the triggering creature, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed]{Grabbing} it ([[/act escape dc=21]]).

details.description: A wooden crate bearing a chthonic sigil glows with a power contained within it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to open the crate with gradual release of its magical energy, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to dispel the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the sigil on the crate

Effect The incomplete demonic pact causes the crate to explode. Bits of wood and energy from the Outer Rifts deal @Damage[(2d8+5)[bludgeoning],(2d8+5)[spirit]] damage to all creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10 feet} of it (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic]).

details.description: An invisible magic field around the strongbox releases a spray of acid when disturbed.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Bleed Away the Magical Field|traits:acid,evocation,magical,trap,hazard] (expert) to harmlessly bleed away the magical field or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 20) to dispel it.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the strongbox directly or with a held tool

Effect The trap deals @Damage[(4d8+10)[acid]] damage in a @Template[cone|distance:15] in the direction of the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic]).

details.description: A chest-sized puzzle box is covered in intricate designs, with no immediately obvious way to open it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:19] (trained), @Check[thievery|dc:18], and @Check[perception|dc:18] simultaneously by at least 3 characters; one of these checks can be replaced by a Strike against AC 19, and one can be replaced by targeting the box with a spell that has the electricity trait and deals at least 1 damage (this ignores the box's Hardness).
details.reset: The box resets immediately.

description.value:
Trigger A creature fails one of the disable checks

Effect An electric jolt shocks all creatures who were attempting to open the box. This deals @Damage[(2d8+5)[electricity]]{2d8+5 nonlethal electricity} damage, and each creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save.

details.description: Ceiling sprinklers spray in response to any fire in the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Neutralize Automatic Fire Suppression|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) to jam the sprinkler system or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:18|name:Disable Control Panel|traits:mechanical,trap]{Engineering Lore} (trained) on the control panel on the north wall to turn the system off.

description.value:
Trigger Fire damage is dealt in area A1

Effect Frozen fire retardant rains down on all creatures in area A1, dealing @Damage[(4d8+10)[cold]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic]). Any persistent fire damage in area A1 ends.

details.description: The double door is a false door; when triggered, the door slams downward like a drawbridge to crush anyone standing before it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to detach the trigger from the doors and lock them in place

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

description.value:
Trigger Someone attempts to open the doors, or the doors take enough damage to become broken;

Effect The heavy metal doors are spring-loaded to slam downward, dropping like a drawbridge to smash all creatures in a @Template[square|distance:10]{10-foot-square} before the doors. All creatures in this area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21|options:area-effect,damaging-effect,inflicts:immobilized,inflicts:prone] save.

Critical Success The creature lurches to the side and takes no damage-move the creature to a square of the creature's choice adjacent to the trap's area of effect.

Success The creature takes @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage and is pushed into an adjacent square just outside the trap's area.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[6d8[bludgeoning]] damage, is pushed into an adjacent square just outside the trap's area, and falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in that square.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[12d8[bludgeoning]] damage and is knocked prone. The heavy doors pin the creature to the ground, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized]{Immobilizing} it ([[/act escape dc=22]]). A non-immobilized character can lift the doors by Forcing them open with a successful @Check[athletics|dc:22] check so that an immobilized character can crawl to safety.

details.description: A phantasmal crimson worm appears to rampage outside the workshop, afflicting the area with phantom tremors, blistering heat, and cave-ins.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:22], @Check[occultism|dc:22], or @Check[religion|dc:22]; it takes three successful checks to weaken the haunt or erect supports that brace the ceiling. A PC who speaks Dwarven can attempt @Check[diplomacy|dc:22] (trained) checks to calm the phantasmal cave worm.
details.routine: (2 actions) Zogototaru uses 1 action to create a Tremor. With her other action, she either Exudes Heat or makes Rocks Fall, alternating between the two each round.

description.value: Zogototaru breathes fire across Guldrege. Even from a distance, this makes the area blisteringly hot, dealing @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage to all creatures (@Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic] save).
Zogototaru's thrashing appears to crack the roof, dropping debris on a random creature in the area. This deals @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).
Zogototaru's thrashing shakes the ground, knocking creatures @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in the area unless they succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save.

details.description: A large cylindrical metallic structure surrounded by dense cables and pipes, anointed with mystical runes which glow and hum vibrantly. Elemental energy arcs off three spikes that may have once been connected to some of the cables.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:22] (trained) to restart the machine's connection to an elemental plane, or @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) to find and deactivate the whichever rune is currently active; three total successes are required to disable the nexus. A spike can be disabled with a @Check[athletics|dc:25] (trained) check to rip it from the structure or a @Check[crafting|dc:22] (trained) check to reconnect some of the cables. If all 6 elemental energies are returned to the nexus, it is disabled.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-02: The Blackwood Lost

details.routine: (3 actions) The nexus spends each of its actions making an elemental wave Strike; each of these must be against a different creature who does not have the elemental trait. For each spike destroyed or disabled, the trap loses 1 action.

description.value:
Trigger A creature without the elemental trait approaches within 15 feet of the nexus or attacks an elemental within 15 feet of the nexus

Effect Unstable elemental energies surrounding the machine discharge abruptly from one of the spikes, sending waves of wind, gusts of metallic shards, jets of fire and other elemental bursts across the room in various directions. The hazard makes an elemental wave Strike against the triggering creature. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Trigger The nexus is broken or destroyed

Effect A ferocious gush of elemental energies bursts from the broken machine, dealing @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature within @Template[emanation|distance:15]{15 feet} (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save). The trap is then disabled.

The damage of this Strike varies; no multiple attack penalty. Roll [[/gmr 1d6 #Damage Type]] for damage type.

1 cold (air)

2 bludgeoning (earth)

3 fire

4 electric (metal)

5 piercing (water)

6 slashing (wood)

stealth.details: @Check[arcana|dc:22] or @Check[occultism|dc:22] to unravel the circle's meaning without triggering its reaction.

details.description: A magic circle made of royal icing seals the trap door to the gristmill's basement shut and plays a nasty trick on anyone who tries to open it without all three of its gumdrop keys.
details.disable: If the three magical gumdrops which act as this hazard's keys are placed at the correct points of the magic circle, its enchantment is disabled. Deciphering the correct locations requires a successful @Check[arcana|dc:20] or @Check[occultism|dc:20] check; on a critical failure, the mere act of placing the gumdrops activates the Malicious Metamorphosis reaction. Once the enchantment is disabled, the magic circles can be wiped away like any regular icing.
details.reset: If the trap door is closed without the magic circles being disturbed, the enchantment resumes as normal.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the trap door without first placing the three enchanted gumdrop keys in their proper arrangement

Effect The magic circles prevent the trap door from opening and maliciously transform a portion of the triggering creature's body, with effects based on the result of the triggering creature's @Check[fortitude|dc:18|options:forced-movement,inflicts:clumsy,inflicts:off-guard,inflicts:prone,inflicts:slowed] save.

Critical Success The triggering creature is blown 10 feet away from the trap door and knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], but otherwise unaffected.

Success The target is blown 10 feet away from the trap door, knocked prone, and has three fingers on one of their hands transformed into gingerbread. They cannot use weapons with the two-hand or twin traits for as long as the transformation persists.

Failure As success, except the transformation also extends to the creature's entire hand and a portion of the adjoining arm. The affected creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} for as long as the transformation persists.

Critical Failure As failure, except the transformation also applies to one of the triggering creature's legs. For the duration of the transformation, it takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all of its speeds, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard], in addition to the effects listed above.

details.description: A bladder of powdered peppers is secreted in a compartment above the door.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to disable the blade that bursts the bladder

description.value:
Trigger The door is opened

Effect The powder sprays in a burst that fills the square in front of the door and all adjacent squares in the orphans' dormitory. Any creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21] save.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is blinded for 1 minute.

details.description: Deceased Puddles residents lash out at living creatures.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained) three times to banish the apparitions with prayers, or @Check[occultism|dc:22] (trained) once to commune with the spirits and convince them to pass on

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-04: An Enkindled Carnival

details.reset: The haunt re-forms after 1 hour. The haunt becomes disabled and doesn't reform 1 minute after the Puddles phantom is slain.
details.routine: (1 action) The haunt's screams send biting supernatural rain around the area, dealing @Damage[4d6[piercing]] damage to all living creatures within 90 feet (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save). Creatures who fail the save are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] by a red haze for 1 round (or 1 minute on a critical failure).

description.value:
Trigger A living creature comes within 30 feet of the haunt

Effect The haunt wails, cursing the living for allowing the Puddles residents to wallow in misery while Absalom's elites prosper. Each living creature within 90 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is temporarily immune to this bloodthirsty apparition's routine for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature shares the apparition's uncontrolled rage against the living. It makes a Strike against itself and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2}.

details.description: The rope ladder rips free from its anchors and drops into the pit.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) or @Check[crafting|dc:22] (trained) to secure the ladder's anchors so they won't break

description.value:
Trigger More than 3 Small creatures or more than 1 Medium creature climbs the ladder at the same time

Effect The ladder's anchors rip free from the wall, dropping the ladder and anyone on it into area B1. Anyone on the ladder falls 50 feet, taking falling damage as appropriate. A creature at the top of the ladder can attempt a @Check[acrobatics|dc:21] check or Reflex save to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] and cling to the side of the pit in area A9. A creature lower on the ladder can attempt to grab a handhold on the shaft's wall, but in this case the check increases to @Check[acrobatics|dc:25].

details.description: A trapdoor, connected to floor sensors, opens in the section of floor marked on the map, while the surrounding floor suddenly tilts to a 45-degree angle and the fountains in the wall begin spraying at high pressure, making the tile slippery and pushing creatures toward the open chute.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Jam Trapdoor Mechanism|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to jam the mechanisms and keep the trapdoor from opening, or to locate the bypass switch hidden under a stair riser just outside the room

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

description.value:
Trigger Two or more creatures stand on the trapped spaces

Effect One round after the trap is triggered, all creatures in the room fall 40 feet down the shaft to area E36, taking @Damage[20[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage from the fall plus @Damage[2d6[piercing]] damage as they land in the spiked pit.

The creatures can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to snag the hammock or one of the hanging storage containers to avoid falling.

The DC to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Climb] the walls or Grab an Edge is 20.

details.description: The ceiling is unstable, and any Medium or larger creature that approaches within 5 feet of the collapsed tunnel triggers a cave-in.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Prop Up Ceiling] (trained) to prop up the ceiling in key locations prior to the cave-in.

publication.title: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On

description.value:
Trigger A Medium or larger creature approaches within 5 feet of the collapsed tunnel

Effect Massive stones in the ceiling fall, dealing @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage to the triggering creature and all creatures within 10 feet (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save).

Creatures that fail the saving throw are also buried and begin to suffocate. A buried creature can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Escape Falling Stones] check every round, breaking free on a success. Other characters who are not buried can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid] on this check.

details.description: The tunnel collapses, filling the passage with stone.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:25|name:Prop up ceiling] (trained) to prop up the tunnel ceiling without triggering a collapse

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the unstable tunnel

Effect The tunnel collapses in a cave-in that deals @Damage[(4d8+10)[bludgeoning]] damage to the triggering creature and all creatures in area A2 (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic]). On a failed save, the triggering creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] ([[/act escape dc=21]]). This collapse fully blocks the tunnel (area A2), making it impassable.

details.description: A 5-foot-square section in the ceiling is in danger of collapsing. A character can use a Crafting check instead of a Perception check to detect the hazard.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:21] (trained) to support the damaged area against a collapse

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters a square where the ceiling's structure is weakened

Effect Rocks fall in the square, dealing @Damage[(4d8+10)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save) to anyone in that square. The square also turns into greater difficult terrain.

details.description: A section of ceiling and iron cell bars collapse inward.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) or @Check[crafting|dc:25] (trained) to use a loose iron bar to shore up the ceiling.

description.value:
Trigger A Medium or larger creature moves through the cell at a faster pace than simply Stepping, or more than one Medium or larger creature enter the cell at the same time

Effect The ceiling and walls of the cell collapse, creating a thunderous roar. All creatures in the cell must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:25] save; the cell then becomes greater difficult terrain that can be passed through safely.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] by fallen rubble ([[/act escape dc=25]]).

Critical Failure The creature is buried in the rubble. They take @Damage[(3d10+19)[bludgeoning]] damage and are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] ([[/act escape dc=25]]). Until they escape, the creature must hold their breath or risk suffocating.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.other-effects.Item.Effect: Remaining Air]

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the glyph on the doorjamb

details.description: A loud pop sounds when this door is opened, followed immediately by a blast of colorful lights that washes over whoever opened the door.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to disable the glyph, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (1st rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract the glyph

description.value:
Trigger The door is opened

Effect The trap casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dizzying Colors] on the triggering creature (@Check[will|dc:21|traits:illusion,magical,incapacitation,visual,auditory,trap,visual]).

details.description: The crowd is riled up enough to start throwing chamberpots at the interlopers, but not riled up enough to risk their lives. The crowd fills nine contiguous squares but can be shaped to fit the streets. Characters can move through the crowd, but spaces occupied by the crowd are difficult terrain.
details.disable: After more people in the crowd have become shaken or taken damage than there are crowd leaders left standing, the crowd disperses. For abilities affecting an area, count each affected square as one member of the crowd.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-01: The Absalom Initiation

details.reset: After more people in the crowd have become shaken or taken damage than there are crowd leaders left standing, the crowd disperses. For abilities affecting an area, count each affected square as one member of the crowd.
details.routine: (3 actions) The crowd uses its first action to Stride away from any opponent adjacent to them (or to get within 30 feet), then uses each remaining action to make a chamber pot Strike at a different target.

description.value: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} (@Check[fortitude|dc:19] save negates sickened).

details.description: The crowd rushes forward, risking a stampede as everyone tries to fnd a safe exit.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:18] to calm the crowd, or @Check[intimidation|dc:21] or @Check[society|dc:21] to control the crowd's behavior

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-01: Intro to the Year of Immortal Influence

details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the crowd moves 5 feet toward the exit in area A1, then crushes any creature in a @Template[emanation|distance:10], dealing @Damage[(4d6)[bludgeoning]] damage. All targets must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save.

Failure The target takes full damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The target takes double damage, becomes prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger Lady Embersplitter announces that the victim has died

Effect The crowd begins moving around the bailey and deals @Damage[(4d6)[bludgeoning]] damage in a @Template[emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save), and then rolls initiative.

details.description: Spirits bound to sand-covered remains cascade down upon the living, imposing the weighty, helpless press of their final moments.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:18|name:Exorcise the Spirits|traits:complex,haunt,hazard] or @Check[occultism|dc:18|name:Exorcise the Spirits|traits:complex,haunt,hazard] (trained) to exorcise the spirits, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:22|name:Provide the Spirits with Comfort|traits:complex,haunt,hazard] to provide them with comfort. Each check requires 2 actions, which have the verbal trait, and three successful checks are required to address all ofthe spirits.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-09: The Secluded Siege

details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine:
(1 action) The haunt imposes the sensation of becoming buried beneath millions of grains of sand on all living creatures within the area. Each living creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21|traits:complex,haunt,hazard,damaging-effect] save as the sensation threatens to overcome them. The creatures take @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all its Speeds for 1 round.

Failure The creature takes full damage and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all its Speeds for 1 round. It also begins suffocating. The creature can begin breathing normally again if it leaves the area of the haunt or the haunt is disabled.

Critical Failure As failure, but the target takes double damage.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Crushing Spirits]

description.value:
Trigger A creature remains within the area occupied by the buried souls for 1 round or longer

Effect The haunt rolls initiative (if it hasn't already) as the spirits surge forth, swirling up sand in their wake.

details.description: The death of Thalleus Jongrim—in his bed, surrounding by ritualistic implements—repeats in ghostly form.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:21] (trained) to snuff out one of four ghostly candles or @Check[religion|dc:23] (trained) three times to calm what is left of Thalleus's fractured spirit
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures have left the room. One hour after this or being Disabled, the haunt is ready to trigger again. Putting Thalleus's spirit to rest permanently destroys the haunt.
details.routine: (1 action) The haunt recreates the moment when Thalleus dies, causing the ritual to go awry. This releases a wave of void energy, dealing @Damage[4d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] from the bed (@Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save). This damage is reduced by 1d8 for every ghostly candle that has been snuffed out.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature is adjacent to the bed

Effect Four ghostly candles appear around the bed—one at each corner—followed by a transparent image of a frail Thalleus reading from a tome in a wavering voice. Each living creature who can hear these words must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:23|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: This unassuming fungus leeches heat out of the air.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:22|name:Remove Brown Mold] (trained) to safely remove the mold

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: After expanding, the brown mold can't grow again for 1 day.

description.value: 5 feet. Enhanced brown mold deals @Damage[(1d10+6)[cold]] damage to nearby creatures.

Trigger Fire comes within 5 feet of the brown mold.

Effect The brown mold expands into every square adjacent to its space. As it grows, it pulls more heat from its surroundings, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic] save) to creatures within 10 feet after it expands.

details.description: A partially sawn tree collapses onto the path.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:21|name:Predict the tree's collapse] (trained) to predict and avoid the tree's collapse location or @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Prop the tree up] (trained) to prop it up.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the area of collapse

Effect The tree falls over, dealing @Damage[(4d8+10)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect]).

stealth.details: (trained) to hear the scratch of a quill upon paper

details.description: As a creature touches a hand-written letter, they witness the letter's author scribe it in the past; each pen stroke across the page carves the letter's text into the creature's flesh.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:24|name:Ward off the haunt] (trained) to ward off the haunt or @Check[thievery|dc:25|name:Dispose of the letter] (trained) to dispose of the letter with extreme care before the haunt can form
details.reset: 1 minute

description.value:
Trigger A creature reads the letter

Effect The haunt deals @Damage[(2d8+11)[slashing]] damage to the triggering creature as the words are carved into its flesh (@Check[will|dc:21|basic]). On a failure, the creature takes @Damage[2d8[bleed]].

details.description: A surge of abyssal energy creates a powerful wave pushing away from the monolith.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:22], @Check[occultism|dc:22], or @Check[religion|dc:22] to magically alter the runes, or @Check[thievery|dc:25] to physically alter them. A PC that can read Abyssal or Cyclops is better able to identify key runes, gaining a +1 circumstance bonus to these check (+2 if they speak both). Each check requires two actions, and disabling the monolith requires three successful checks. A PC that critically succeeds earns two success. On a failed check, the attempting PC is exposed to the burning flames wreathing the monolith, taking @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-14: Lost in Flames

details.routine:
(1 action) On the hazard's turn it sends a wave of water along the fork of the river (North, South, or East) that contains the nearest PC. Any creature along that fork in or on the surface of the water must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22|name:Flooding Monolith] save.

The monolith's guardians are accustomed to these surges and are immune to this effect.

Critical Success The creature takes advantage of the undertow after the surge to reposition themselves. The creature can Step, ignoring difficult terrain caused by the water.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is pushed 10 feet away from the monolith.

Critical Failure The creature is pushed 15 feet away from the monolith and knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than the monolith's guardians approaches within 40 feet of the monolith

Effect the flames wreathing the monolith flare with incandescent light. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save.

On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for one round.

On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for one round, then dazzled for one round.

The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: A pivoted section of a seemingly normal hallway becomes a chute that deposits victims into a more dangerous room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to prevent the floor from pivoting
details.reset: Provided no creature is on the slide, the trap resets after 1 minute, returning the hallway floor to its normal, unassuming state.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to open the door at the end of the hallway

Effect The section of floor marked on the map drops down, becoming a steep ramp. Creatures in the easternmost square fall 5 feet, creatures in the middle square fall 10 feet, and creatures in the western square fall 15 feet (DC 21 to [[/act grab-an-edge dc=21]]). In that order, all creatures who fall are moved to one of the squares immediately east of the marked squares in area A11. If any destination square is occupied, the creature in that square is pushed 5 feet to the east as the second creature takes its place, and both creatures take an additional @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]|traits:nonlethal]{2d6 nonlethal bludgeoning damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save).

details.description: A @Template[square|distance:10]{10‑foot‑by‑10‑foot} bonfire spreads to the surrounding forest on each of its turns.
