details.disable: A successful @Check[athletics|dc:22|name:Smother a square|traits:hazard,complex,environmental,fire], @Check[nature|dc:22|name:Smother a square|traits:hazard,complex,environmental,fire], or @Check[survival|dc:22|name:Smother a square|traits:hazard,complex,environmental,fire] check from an adjacent square is sufficient to smother one 5‑foot‑square of fire; each attempt is an Interact action. Dousing the flames automatically extinguishes one or more sections of fire, with no check. Water typically clears a 5‑foot square if the amount is small (such as from a spell like create water or hydraulic push). Larger amounts of water, such as a full bucket, typically douse a 10‑foot‑by‑10‑foot area (or 4 squares in some other shape). Throwing a bucket of water on flames requires an Interact action. A waterskin doesn't contain enough water to put out even 1 square of fire. Cold can also put out fire, but only if the cold can affect an area; cold is less effective than water, so a frost vial puts out only 1 square of fire, and a ray of frost is ineffective.
details.routine: On its turn, the fire spreads into a number of additional squares equal to half the number of squares the fire currently occupies (minimum 1 square). You determine the squares the fire spreads into. Any creature that ends its turn next to the flames takes @Damage[(2d8+5)[fire]] damage, or @Damage[(4d8+10)[fire]] damage if it ended its turn within the flames (@Check[reflex|dc:25|traits:hazard,complex,environmental,fire|basic] in either case). A creature can take damage from the forest fire only once per round.

description.value:
Trigger A brimorak urges the fire to spread out of control

Effect The bonfire becomes a forest fire and rolls initiative.

details.description: A thin layer of canvas conceals this 10-foot-square pit, the walls of which are studded with dagger-sized formian stingers. Creatures that fall into the trap risk being gouged by the poisonous barbs.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained)
details.reset: Creatures can still fall into the trap, but the canvas must be reset manually for the trap to become hidden again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the canvas

Effect The creature falls into the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed] trench and takes falling damage (typically @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage]). The creature is also gouged by three poisoned stingers as they fall. The creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:21]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d6[poison]] (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d6[poison]] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[1d6[poison]], clumsy 1, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round)

Stage 4 @Damage[2d6[poison]], clumsy 1, and enfeebled 1 (1 round)

details.description: The panicked crowd of gala attendees reacts to the unexpected attack.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:19], @Check[diplomacy|dc:21], or @Check[athletics|dc:24] to get guests to move out of harm's way; four total successes across all skills are required to disable the hazard
details.routine:
(1 action) The crowd reacts in one of the following ways, determined by a d12 die roll:

  • 1–2 Hunker Down: The crowd freezes in fear. It provides lesser cover to all creatures in the great room for 1 round.
  • 3–4 Run!: Determine a random direction. Each creature in the area must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:21] save or be moved 10 feet in that direction. On a critical failure, they're also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. This is forced movement.
  • 5–6 Hysteria: The crowd's dread is contagious. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). This is an emotion, fear, and mental effect.
  • 7–8 Wailing: The cries make it hard to concentrate. For 1 round, any creature in the area that attempts an action with the concentrate trait must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:4] or the action is disrupted. This is an auditory effect.
  • 9–10 Crushing Press: Members of the crowd shove and push, trying to find a way to safety. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[(2d8+5)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic] save).

  • 11–12 Wild Dismay: The chaos is overwhelming. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round. This is an emotion and mental effect.

description.value:
Trigger A creature rolls initiative

Effect The crowd stampedes, dealing @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone on the great room floor (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save), then rolls initiative.

details.description: Four magically treated gems used to store fragments of souls begin glowing with an eerie malevolence.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:22] (trained), @Check[occultism|dc:22] (trained), or @Check[nature|dc:22] (trained) to quiet one of the four grasping gems
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures have left the room. One hour after this or being Disabled, the haunt is ready to trigger again. Putting Thalleus's spirit to rest permanently destroys the haunt.
details.routine: (2 actions) During the haunt's first action, the grasping gems attempt an Athletics check (at a [[/r 1d20+4#Athletics]]{+14 modifier}) to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple] each non-@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] living creature in the room, rolling once and comparing the result to each target's Fortitude DC. For every gem that is disabled or destroyed, the haunt takes a cumulative –1 circumstance penalty to the check. For the haunt's second action, the gems' ghostly hands squeeze their victims. Each creature immobilized by the haunt takes @Damage[(2d8+5)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A living creature is adjacent to a grasping gem's shelf

Effect The room becomes awash with eerie light as ghostly hands reach out from all directions. Each living creature in the room must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24|options:area-effect,inflicts:immobilized] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The phantom hands interfere with the creature's movements. The creature takes a –2 status penalty to its initiative roll.

Failure The phantom hands hold the creature in place. The creature takes a –2 status penalty to its initiative roll and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] ([[/act escape dc=24]]) for the first round.

Critical Failure As failure, but if the creature attempts to Escape during the first round, it must roll twice and take the worse result. This is a misfortune effect.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Phantom Hands]

details.description: When a living creature crosses the midpoint of the trapped tunnel, necromantic magic partially animates the corpses buried in the walls and ceiling.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:24|name:Disrupt Magical Trigger|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to disrupt the magical trigger before the hazard rolls initiative, or @Check[religion|dc:18|name:Overwhelm the Area with Vitality Energy] (trained) to overwhelm the area with vitality energy and prevent the hazard from using its routine for 1 round; on the third successful Religion check, the necromantic energies are disrupted and the hazard is destroyed.
details.routine:
(1 action) Undead fists assail all creatures in the hall, dealing @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage. Creatures in the hall must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21].

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage, and its Speeds are reduced by 10 feet (to a minimum of 5 feet) for 1 round.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature crosses the midpoint of the tunnel

Effect The dead reach out from the walls and ceiling, moaning and spewing writhing maggots. Creatures in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}. It can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 while in the trap's area.

Critical Failure As failure, and the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] for 1 round.

stealth.details: (trained); DC 0 to notice the mirror without noting it is a trap

details.description: A dark shape moves in the surface of an ornate mirror, its eyes wide as blood seeps down its tormented face.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to fully cover the mirror or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 22) to dispel the mirror.
details.reset: 1 minute if no fresh blood has been spilled.
details.routine:
(2 actions; arcane, mental) The bloody figure in the mirror transforms to reflect a creature within 30 feet. The reflection bleeds profusely from the eyes, nose, and mouth, and the reflected creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21|traits:arcane,mental] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature bleeds from their mouth or nose.

Failure The creature bleeds from their eyes, mouth, and nose, and takes @Damage[2d8[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

description.value:
Trigger A creature spills blood in the room

Effect A ghastly figure of blood appears in the mirror's surface, forcing all creatures who can see it to attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature bleeds from their nose and mouth.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

details.description: This small toy-like device has a hidden mechanism meant to be activated by the user, but the centuries have eroded its magical-mechanical components to the point that they activate at the slightest touch.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to deactivate the mechanism; @Check[crafting|dc:20] (trained) to identify and repair the problem components; two successes are required to disable the hazard

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-13: All That Glitters

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without a creature being within 5' of the handheld quaker.
details.routine:
At its initiative, to determine what the quaker does, roll [[/gmr 1d4]] and consult the following table:

1: No action

2: A small quake: the terrain in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] centered on the quaker shakes briefly. A creature in an affected square is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] (@Check[reflex|dc:21] save).

3: A medium quake: the terrain within the vault shakes constantly, transforming it into difficult terrain until its next turn. A creature in an affected square is knocked prone and takes @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning]] damage from the fall (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save).

4: A great quake: the terrain within the vault moves in waves, transforming it into greater difficult terrain until its next turn. A creature in an affected square is violently slammed to the ground, knocked prone and taking @Damage[(2d8+6)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into a space with the handheld quaker or interacts with it

Effect The handheld quaker vibrates visibly and rolls initiative.

A creature within difficult terrain created by the quaker takes a –2 circumstance penalty to Reflex saves and actions with the Manipulate trait, including @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Disable a Device]{Disable Device}.

details.description: A 15-foot wide area of verdant-covered water attempts to suck creatures down into it.
details.disable: Two @Check[survival|dc:22] (trained) checks to find roots and tree limbs sturdy enough for everyone to use as escape paths ends the hazard's threat to all creatures in the area

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-99: Blessings of the Forest

details.reset: The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it accumulates leaf and greenery cover over the course of 24 hours.
details.routine:
(one action) On its initiative, the bog pulls down each creature within it. A creature that was submerged to its waist becomes submerged to its neck, and a creature that was submerged up to its neck is pulled under and must hold its breath to avoid suffocation.

A creature partially or fully submerged in the bog can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:20|traits:action:swim] check to Swim to either raise itself by one step (pulled under to submerged to its neck, or submerged to its neck to submerged to its waist), or to escape from the bog if it's submerged only up to its waist. This DC increases to @Check[athletics|dc:22|traits:action:swim] for creatures submerged up to their neck or worse. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down one step. Other creatures can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid], typically by using a rope or similar assistance (providing an automatic success), or attempt to pull a single affected creature out with their own Athletics check, with the same results as if the aiding creature attempted the check itself.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area of the bog.

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the bog up to its waist. The bog rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

details.description: Pebbles form into a hand the size of an ox, which reaches to crush anyone in the water.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to pick apart the pebbles, or @Check[warfare-lore|dc:18] to recognize and recreate the battle

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: 24 hours

description.value:
Trigger Three or more creatures enter the submerged passage

Effect The hand reenacts its final battle against the invading demon. It makes a crushing power Strike against all creatures in the passage.

details.description: The sigils on the door's frame glow with an amber light, which collects in a facet over the center of the door.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:17] (trained) to strategically destroy the runes, @Check[thievery|dc:18] to disrupt the beam from collecting the facet, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank, counteract DC 18) to dispel the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: The Hostile Architecture resets after being exposed to the light of a blue moon.
details.routine: a The hostile architecture fires its aversion beam on a creature that is sanctified holy. If there are no sanctified holy creatures, it instead fires its beam on the nearest non-demon creature.

description.value:
Trigger A non-demon creature comes within 15 feet of the hostile architecture

Effect The hostile architecture shines the aversion beam on the triggering creature, and then rolls initiative. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save.

Critical Success The target is immune to the trap for 1 minute.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing].

details.description: Compartments in the ceiling are rigged to drop a freezing alchemical liquid into the entire room if the door is opened without using the key.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Disable Ice Fall|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) on the door allows it to be opened without springing the trap.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: The Fall of Plaguestone

description.value:
Trigger The door is attacked or opened without using the key.

Effect A freezing alchemical slurry is dumped into the room, dealing @Damage[4d6[cold]] damage to everyone in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save).

details.description: A half‑dozen iron darts fire from spring‑loaded launchers concealed in the desk opposite the front door.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Disable the trigger|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to disable the trigger, or @Check[acrobatics|dc:18|name:Squeeze through the door|traits:action:squeeze] to squeeze through the partially opened door (this doesn't disable the trap but prevents it from triggering)
details.reset: The trap resets as soon as the door is closed. It has enough darts to trigger three times, after which, it must be reloaded with 20 darts before it can reset again.

description.value:
Trigger The front door is fully opened, Forced Open, or broken

Effect A hail of iron darts launches along a @Template[line|distance:20] from the desk through the doorway, dealing @Damage[(4d8+10)[piercing]] damage to each creature in the area of effect (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic]).

details.description: This unsteady pile of furniture is piled to the top of this usually high-ceilinged room. There looks to be some space to squeeze through to the other side, but even the slightest touch causes the stack to destabilize, and there's so much that once it begins to fall, it's difficult to stop.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:25] to knock the pile over in a safe direction or @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to carefully rearrange the furniture to a more stable configuration.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-13: All That Glitters

details.routine: On its initiative, the pile expands into an adjacent space, dealing creatures in that space @Damage[(2d8+5)[bludgeoning]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save, pushing them into an adjacent empty space, and making that space impassable until cleared. If no adjacent empty space is available, the pile buries the creature, suffocating it. Clearing a space or pulling a creature out requires a @Check[athletics|dc:18] check.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into a space adjacent to the furniture pile

Effect Part of the pile slides into a space the creature occupies, causing @Damage[(2d8+5)[bludgeoning]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save. The furniture pile rolls initiative.

details.description: The hypnotic ticking of this metronome captures the attention of all who approach it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Interfere with the Metronome's Mechanisms|traits:magical,trap] or @Check[crafting|dc:18|name:Interfere with the Metronome's Mechanisms|traits:magical,trap] to interfere with the metronome's mechanisms, @Check[athletics|dc:22|name:Prevent the Hand from Moving|traits:magical,trap] to grip the hand and prevent it from moving, or @Check[arcana|dc:22|name:Interpret the Magic Runes|traits:magical,trap] or @Check[occultism|dc:22|name:Interpret the Magic Runes|traits:magical,trap] to interpret the magic runes on the metronome and counteract its effects. Three successful checks are required to fully disable the metronome, ending its ticking.

description.value:
Trigger A creature starts the metronome

Effect During the next 10 minutes, any creature who approaches within 30 feet of the metronome, other than the one who activated it, must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18|traits:magical,trap] save, with the following effects.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected by the metronome and acts normally.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for as long as it can hear the metronome and remains within 30 feet of it.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} and is fascinated for as long as it can hear the metronome and remains within 30 feet of it. While fascinated in this way, the creature can't willingly move more than 30 feet away from the metronome until it stops ticking. The creature is then @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued] for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The creature is slowed 1 and is fascinated for as long as it can hear the metronome and remains within 30 feet of it. While fascinated in this way, the creature can't willingly move more than 30 feet away from the metronome until it stops ticking. The creature is then fatigued for 2 hours.

details.description: A mold cloud puffs from spoiled spilled food.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:16|name:Remove Mold] (trained) to remove the mold from the food without triggering the spores.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-03: Escaping the Grave

description.value:
(inhaled, poison) The drained condition from the spores persists after the poison's duration ends.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:16]

Maximum Duration 2 rounds

Stage 1 1d8 poison damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 1d12 poison damage and drained 2 (1 round)

Trigger A creature moves into the mold's space or damages the mold. The mold can't use this reaction if it's in direct sunlight or if the damage was fire damage.

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within 10 feet are exposed to the mold's spores.

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 5-foot radius surrounds an invisible wall or floor rune the size of the creature to be summoned.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:20] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 19) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-11: No Time for Treason

details.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors.

Effect This trap summons a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Sod Hound] with the elite adjustment. The sod hound rolls initiative and remains for 7 rounds, after which the spell ends and the sod hound disappears. The sod hound also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. The summoned sod hound can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: Linneus's grudge manifests as a haunt that isolates creatures in the bathroom and attempts to psychically drown them in one of three containers of water: the sink, the bath, or the wash bucket.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:22|name:Excorise the Spirit] (expert) to exorcise the spirit from one of the containers of water, or @Check[athletics|dc:23] or @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Overpower the Ghostly Force] (trained) to overpower the ghostly force sealing a container and drain it. All three containers must be disabled to disable the entire haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-13: Devil at the Crossroads

details.reset: The haunt deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine:
(one action) One creature within the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23] save as the haunt attempts to drown them.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure A ghostly version of the creature's face appears in the water of the sink, the bath, or the wash bucket. The creature immediately experiences the sensation of being underwater, and if it can't breathe water, it must begin to hold its breath to avoid suffocation. The haunt can affect only one creature per container remaining in the haunt. If the creature leaves the room, it's immediately freed.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature immediately loses [[/r 1d4 #Actions]]{1d4} actions' worth of air.

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door to the haunted chamber

Effect The manifestation slams the door shut. The force deals @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage to any creature in or adjacent to the door's space and pushes them into the room. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save takes no damage and rolls out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, it can choose the direction. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: This mushroom ring forms a portal to the First World, allowing the Thorned Monarch's reinforcements into the Material Plane. When the PCs approach, a creature emerges. Creatures continue to emerge until the mushroom ring is disabled. Disabling the mushroom ring also causes any creatures that have emerged to disappear.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) or @Check[nature|dc:22] to rearrange the mushrooms to disrupt the portal (two actions) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank, counteract DC 20) to counteract the magical properties; three successes are required to disable the portal

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-00: The King in Thorns

details.routine: (1 action) A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.Nuglub] emerges from the mushroom ring and defends it. All nuglubs use the mushroom ring's initiative.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs approach the mushroom ring

Effect A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.Nuglub] emerges from the portal to defend it, and the hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: The flammable oil catches flame and explodes, burning up in a ball of fire.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:25|name:Neutralize the oil|traits:hazard,environmental,fire] (trained) or @Check[crafting|dc:23|name:Neutralize the oil|traits:hazard,environmental,fire] to neutralize the oil without setting it alight

description.value:
Trigger An open flame is brought into area A4

Effect The oil on the walls explodes, dealing @Damage[(4d8+10)[fire]] damage to all creatures and objects in areas A4 and A3(@Check[reflex|dc:21|traits:hazard,environmental,fire|basic]).

details.description: A book unfurls and its case forms into a leathery locust, its pages folding into an overwhelming swarm.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] to tear up the leather locust, or @Check[arcana|dc:18] (trained) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 22) to render the magic inert.
details.reset: The paper locust swarm resets after 1 minute if there are no creatures to target.
details.routine:
(1 action) The swarm moves up to 30 feet to share the nearest creature's space, slicing into them with their paper wings and spurs. The targeted creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+5)[slashing]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d8[slashing]] damage and @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1}.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the bookshelf

Effect The enchanted book transforms into a swarm of origami locusts and one leather locust. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A pile of stolen and volatile fireworks.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:21|name:Remove Lit Firework] (trained), @Check[engineering-lore|dc:19|name:Remove Lit Firework] (trained), or @Check[explosives-lore|dc:19|name:Remove Lit Firework] (trained) to safely remove the lit firework or @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Remove Lit Fuse|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to remove the lit fuse from the firework.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #5: The Dragon who Stole Evoking Day

description.value:
Trigger The pile of fireworks takes acid or fire damage.

Effect The fuse of one of the fireworks begins to burn and the pile of fireworks enters initiative immediately before the triggering creature or effect.

Trigger The pile of fireworks begins its turn.

Effect The pile of fireworks explodes, dealing @Damage[6d8[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic]) save to all creatures within 15 feet.

details.description: The spout trickling blood, sludge, and offal begins to draw upon your life energy, increasing the flow and spreading an even fouler smell within the chamber.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:20] or @Check[religion|dc:20] (trained) to counteract the fell energies drawing in life force to power this strange charnel flow or @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to disable the spout, stopping the flow at its source.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #15: In the Footsteps of Horror

details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the pit

Effect The remnants of a soul cage ritual draw upon the life energy of all living creatures in areaA4 as well as the 10-foot hallway leading to the room. Each living creature in those areas must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic] save as the flow of vile liquid saps their very essence. The pull is more intense on more powerful creatures, dealing @Damage[1d6[void]] damage per level the creature has.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage. Additionally, they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} and cannot reduce the condition's value for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage. Additionally, they are sickened 1 and cannot reduce the condition's value until they leave the Nether Cathedral.

stealth.details: -10 and no minimum proficiency to notice

details.description: An assorted crowd throws punches, flings bottles, and otherwise flies into chaos as a bar fight breaks out, clustering in six sections of one of Port Peril's drinking houses.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:22|name:Cow the Crowd] to cow a section of crowd or @Check[diplomacy|dc:25|name:Disperse the Crowd] to attempt to disperse it

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #4: Port Peril Pub Crawl

details.routine: (2 actions) The bar fight uses its first action to make a general mayhem Strike against all creatures in any area that has not been disabled. Make one Strike against each enemy in the area and roll the damage only once for all targets. The bar fight then uses its second action to use Port Peril specialty.

description.value: The bar fight creates an effect unique to the bar, located at any point within the hazard (even within sections that have been disabled) as long as the entire hazard has not been disabled. The DC for each effect is 21.
Damage can be bludgeoning, slashing or piercing.

The sea splashes, a cask ruptures, or a patron from an aquatic ancestry uses a water-based ability, coating 4 contiguous 5-foot squares with the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Grease]. This effect is a Last Catch specialty.

stealth.details: -10 and no minimum proficiency to notice

details.description: An assorted crowd throws punches, flings bottles, and otherwise flies into chaos as a bar fight breaks out, clustering in six sections of one of Port Peril's drinking houses.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:22|name:Cow the Crowd] to cow a section of crowd or @Check[diplomacy|dc:25|name:Disperse the Crowd] to attempt to disperse it

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #4: Port Peril Pub Crawl

details.routine: (2 actions) The bar fight uses its first action to make a general mayhem Strike against all creatures in any area that has not been disabled. Make one Strike against each enemy in the area and roll the damage only once for all targets. The bar fight then uses its second action to use Port Peril specialty.

description.value: The bar fight creates an effect unique to the bar, located at any point within the hazard (even within sections that have been disabled) as long as the entire hazard has not been disabled. The DC for each effect is 21.
Damage can be bludgeoning, slashing or piercing.

Something about the bar fight has attracted a storm of bad luck, causing a character to bang their shin on a pushed-out bench or get hit on the head by a falling painting. The hazard casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Jealous Hex] on a creature. This effect is a Rivanti's Bar specialty.

stealth.details: -10 and no minimum proficiency to notice

details.description: An assorted crowd throws punches, flings bottles, and otherwise flies into chaos as a bar fight breaks out, clustering in six sections of one of Port Peril's drinking houses.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:22|name:Cow the Crowd] to cow a section of crowd or @Check[diplomacy|dc:25|name:Disperse the Crowd] to attempt to disperse it

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #4: Port Peril Pub Crawl

details.routine: (2 actions) The bar fight uses its first action to make a general mayhem Strike against all creatures in any area that has not been disabled. Make one Strike against each enemy in the area and roll the damage only once for all targets. The bar fight then uses its second action to use Port Peril specialty.

description.value: The bar fight creates an effect unique to the bar, located at any point within the hazard (even within sections that have been disabled) as long as the entire hazard has not been disabled. The DC for each effect is 21.
Damage can be bludgeoning, slashing or piercing.

Gaming chips, a money purse, or jewels from a broken necklace scatter across the ground. All creatures within a @Template[burst|distance:15] that could be enticed by material wealth must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] saving throw or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for 1 round or until a creature has used an Interact action to scoop up the valuables. This effect is a Watchtower specialty.

details.description: A spring-loaded net fires from the dummy, then is pulled tightly back in with magnets.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to jam the net or @Check[athletics|dc:25] to knock the dummy over

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the trap

Effect The creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:23] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] on a critical failure). The DC to Escape is [[/act escape dc=23]], or the net can be cut by doing 6 acid, fire, or slashing damage to it.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the bowl is the center point for a magical trap

details.description: The bowl releases a wave of negative energy when a living creature approaches without displaying a religious symbol of Venexus.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Remove the bowl|traits:hazard,magical,necromancy,void,trap] (expert) to remove the bowl without triggering the magic, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank, counteract DC 20) to counteract the runes

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the bowl without displaying a religious symbol of Venexus

Effect The bowl releases a wave of necromantic energy, dealing @Damage[(4d8+10)[void]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|traits:hazard,magical,necromancy,void,trap|basic]) to the triggering creature and all creatures in the area not displaying a religious symbol of Venexus. On a critical failure, a creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

details.description: A crowd of carnival goers are spurred to panic by supernatural rain.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:21] (trained) to calm them down, @Check[intimidation|dc:22] (trained) to cow them, @Check[athletics|dc:24] (trained) to force them back

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-04: An Enkindled Carnival

description.value:
Trigger The PCs fail to overcome a task during the Dreaded Downpour event

Effect The crowd surges forward, dealing @Damage[(4d8+10)[bludgeoning]] damage to each PC. Each affected creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and takes a –1 circumstance penalty to their next skill check to overcome a task during the Dreaded Downpour event.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to their next skill check to overcome a task during the Dreaded Downpour event.

details.description: Slender razor wire snags a creature, yanking its feet to knock it down.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:22] or @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to snip the wire without getting snared

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the square with the snare tripwire

Effect The snare makes a snare Strike against the triggering creature.
The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] ([[/act escape dc=23]]). On a critical hit, the target is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

details.description: The room shakes violently, as it did during the Rending, and the spirits of dozens of dwarves make a mad dash for safety, their terror palpable.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:18|name:Calm the Spirits|traits:unique,complex,haunt], @Check[intimidation|dc:18|name:Calm the Spirits|traits:unique,complex,haunt], or @Check[religion|dc:18|name:Calm the Spirits|traits:unique,complex,haunt] to calm the panicked spirits, or @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Wrestle Spirits into Order|traits:unique,complex,haunt] to wrestle them into a semblance of order. Two successful checks are required to deactivate the haunt, and each attempt requires two actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-19: Enter the Pallid Peak

details.reset: Unless deactivated, the haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter. If deactivated, the haunt resets after 24 hours.
details.routine:
(1 action) Phantom dwarves trample toward the exit, dealing @Damage[(2d8+5)[bludgeoning]] damage to each living creature in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic|name:Trampled by Spirits|traits:complex,haunt,unique] save). On a critical failure, a creature is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

A creature that attempts to Stand after being inside the haunt's area must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:11] check or else get knocked back down by the trampling spirits.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the lava

Effect The room rumbles and a blast of heat hits each creature in the area, followed by a surge of fear. The creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save, and the haunt rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is frightened 2 and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for 1 round.

stealth.details: (trained) to hear the scratching beneath the floorboards

details.description: Illusory hands tear from the floorboards, clawing at their victim's legs and leaving real wounds.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Exorcize the Spirits] (trained) to exorcize the spirits

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-01: The Absalom Initiation

details.reset: The underlying spirits remain, resetting the haunt every midnight, until the true story of what happened here becomes public.

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes through area C2 for the second time in 24 hours.

Effect Phantasmal rotting hands burst out from the floor under the target, tearing into their legs. The hands' appearance varies based on the reason for the haunting (see the table below).

The hands deal @Damage[(4d6+10)[slashing]] damage and @Damage[4d6[bleed]] (@Check[will|dc:21|basic] save). If a creature takes any damage from the haunt, the claws leave rending wounds on the target's legs, reducing their land Speed by 10 feet (minimum 5 feet) until the target is restored to its maximum HP.

Owners Haunt Manifestations
Archdevil Worshippers Scent of brimstone and sound of chanting in Infernal (a PC who understands Infernal or succeeds at a @Check[religion|dc:10|name:Recognize Chanting], @Check[hell-lore|dc:10|name:Recognize Chanting]{Hell Lore}, or @Check[mephistopheles-lore|dc:10|name:Recognize Chanting]{Mephistopheles Lore} check recognizes the chanting as a prayer to Mephistopheles)
Soulbound Dollmakers Flesh flaking off the rotting hands reveals smooth porcelain underneath (a PC who succeeds at a @Check[crafting|dc:10|name:Recognize Craftsmanship] or @Check[occultism|dc:10|name:Recognize Craftsmanship] check recognizes the craftsmanship as that of soulbound dolls)
Alchemical Experimenters A chemical scent overpowers the rot, and precise surgical cuts can be seen on the arms (a PC who succeeds at a @Check[crafting|dc:10|name:Recognize Cuts] or @Check[medicine|dc:10|name:Recognize Cuts] check realizes both are medical but unnecessary)
Slavers The hands still wear heavy manacles around the wrists
Crime Lords The hands are tattooed and some are cleanly missing fingers (a PC who succeeds at a @Check[society|dc:10|name:Recognize Marks] or @Check[underworld-lore|dc:10|name:Recognize Marks]{Underworld Lore} check recognizes these as marks of gangs active before the earthquake)
Misguided Pharasmins A scent of incense lingers as the haunt vanishes (a PC who succeeds at a @Check[religion|dc:10|name:Recognize Rites] or @Check[pharasma-lore|dc:10|name:Recognize Rites]{Pharasma Lore} check recognizes the scent from Pharasmin funerals)

details.description: The bridge creaks and strains alarmingly.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:25] to Repair the bridge as an individual hexploration activity.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more Medium creatures or a Large or larger creature crosses the bridge

Effect The bridge collapses. The triggering creature(s) fall into the river below, taking no damage from the fall itself due to the deep water, but taking @Damage[(2d8 + 5)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic]) from falling debris. For 1 round after the collapse, the waters below the bridge churn violently, requiring a @Check[athletics|dc:20] check to Swim.

details.description: A trip wire hidden in the dust of the midway point of the floor releases a large boulder to roll from the northwestern corridor toward A4, crushing anything it meets.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to set the trip wire to a different anchor point without changing the tension

description.value:
Trigger The trip wire is pulled or severed

Effect The boulder careens from its holding area to crush anything in its path. Anyone in area A3 path takes @Damage[(2d12+4)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save).

details.description: This trap is a net weaved from rope laid out beneath the jungle underbrush with mechanisms attached to some nearby trees; when triggered, it cases the target in the net and suspends them indefinitely.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:14] to cut the triggering length of rope

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-09: Equal Exchange - Skymetal Hoard

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on top of where the trap is located

Effect The triggering creature is caught in the rope net. The target must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] in the net and suspended in the air until they @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] from the net.

stealth.details: (trained) Characters automatically detect this hazard when they open the door to the Grand Hall.

details.description: Concerned citizens shout slogans about the dangers of dealing with Cheliax and throw bricks at Chelaxian sympathizers.
details.disable: Succeed at two of the following checks in any combination: @Check[athletics|dc:22] (trained) to confiscate dangerous debris, @Check[diplomacy|dc:22] (trained) to calm the crowd, @Check[intimidation|dc:20] (untrained) to scare the crowd away, @Check[will|dc:24] (trained) to maintain a brave and confident demeanor; an effect such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Calm] that can target an area counts as one automatic success.
details.routine: (three actions) The unruly crowd uses an action to chant a Catchy Slogan if it is not already benefiting from the effect It uses Shake Confidence once per turn and uses its remaining actions for cobblestone Strikes.

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door to the Grand Hall

Effect The unruly crowd rolls Initiative.

All the hazards in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Catchy Slogan]

The unruly crowd targets a creature within 30 feet The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and immune to Shake Confidence for 24 hours

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}

Critical Failure The creature becomes frightened 2 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1}.

details.description: A phantom conductor appears with a flourish, causing spectral instruments with razor-like strings to appear in the hands of those present. As the conductor gestures for the performance to begin, creatures are compelled to play the instruments.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:18] to perform so well the conductor releases you from your instrument, banishing it voluntarily; @Check[occultism|dc:22] (trained) or @Check[religion|dc:22] (trained) to banish one instrument; or @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) to sabotage one instrument; each instrument must be banished, sabotaged, or destroyed to disable the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates when all conjured instruments are destroyed or there has been silence for 1 minute. It resets after 1 hour.
details.routine: (1 action; enchantment, mental, occult) The conductor urges each creature within 30 feet to play the hazardous instrument it holds. Each creature that is compelled to play its instrument does so, and those who succeeded on their save can choose to do so. Playing the instrument, either from the haunt's routine or of a creature's own volition during an attempt to disable the haunt, deals @Damage[(1d6+3)[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|traits:enchantment,mental,occult] save), and the creature must attempt a @Check[performance|dc:18|traits:enchantment,mental,occult,damaging-effect] check. On a success, the conductor accepts the performance and dismisses that creature's instrument, and on a critical failure, the discordant sound and conductor's jeers deal an additional @Damage[(1d6+3)[sonic]] damage.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 15 feet

Effect The conductor conjures a hazardous spectral instrument into the hands of each sentient creature within 30 feet. Even a creature whose hands are full finds a spectral instrument superimposed over the other items it's carrying. Affected creatures must each attempt a @Check[will|dc:19] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The conjuration fails and no instrument appears in the creature's hands.

Success A conjured instrument appears in the creature's hands, but it isn't compelled to play it.

Failure A conjured instrument appears in the creature's hands and it's compelled to play it. The creature can't willingly put down the instrument while compelled to play.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature needs a critical success on its Performance in order for the conductor to be satisfied and dismiss its instrument.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice magical sensors hidden within four of the carvings' eyes

details.description: Illusions overwhelm the minds of particularly intelligent creatures, forcing them to relive every intellectual failure and shame they've ever suffered.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to carve out one eye sensor, @Check[arcana|dc:22] or @Check[occultism|dc:22] (trained) to blank out one's mind (disables trap for self only), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 20) to counteract one eye sensor
details.reset: The trap can function for 10 rounds, which don't need to be consecutive, before it must recharge for 24 hours.
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap uses each action to target a creature in the room with an Intelligence score of 10 or greater. A target must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22] save or take @Damage[(2d8+2)[mental]] damage. On a critical failure, the creature takes a –10-foot status penalty to Speed for 1 minute as they stagger under the weight of their failures. The trap loses 1 action for each eye sensor disabled or destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A creature with an Intelligence score of 10 or greater enters the room

Effect The trap fills the triggering creature's mind with shameful memories. The creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22] save or take @Damage[(2d8+2)[mental]]. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Two blades, each hidden in a 15-foot-long ceiling groove, are both connected to a trip wire.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Disable a Blade] (trained) to disable each blade
details.reset: The trap resets after 15 minutes.

description.value:
Trigger The trip wire is pulled or severed.

Effect Both blades swing down, each one Striking all creatures under the ceiling grooves.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: Two blades, each hidden in a 15-foot-long ceiling groove, are both connected to a trip wire.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|traits:hazard|name:Disable Blade] to disable each blade.

publication.title: Pathfinder Beginner Box: Secrets of the Unlit Star

description.value: When the trip wire is pulled or severed, both blades swing down, each one attacking all creatures under the ceiling grooves. The trap makes a melee attack with each blade with +17 to each attack roll. There's no multiple attack penalty on these attacks. On a hit, a blade deals 2d12+4 slashing damage. On a critical hit, the damage is doubled plus 6 extra damage.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: Arcs of primal energy emerge from the L-shaped stone wall at the center of each alcove.
details.disable:
1 @Check[crafting|dc:25] or @Check[thievery|dc:25] (expert) to rig up a dummy or other object to distract one of the trap's sensors, or

1 @Check[nature|dc:23] (trained) to temporarily befuddle one of the sensors with knowledge of primal magic.

Alternatively, inserting the correct key into an alcove's keyhole disables that alcove's sensor.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-16: The Perennial Crown Part 1, Opal of Bhopan

details.reset: The trap is powered by the Eternal Bloom. It resets after 5 minutes, at which time the doors to the vault slam shut once again.
details.routine:
(4 actions) The trap loses one action per magical sensor disabled. On its initiative, the trap uses each of its actions to target one creature adjacent to or within an alcove with a negative effect that corresponds to the season opposite the one depicted on the alcove's murals (@Check[fortitude|dc:24] to resist). For the purposes of this trap, creatures who are within an alcove's landscape count are within that alcove. The trap can target only one creature per alcove per round.

Spring Alcove (death) The decay of late autumn takes hold of the target.

  • Critical Success The trap has no effect.
  • Success The target takes @Damage[2d6[void]] damage.
  • Failure The target takes @Damage[4d6[void]] damage.
  • Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[4d6[void]] damage and @Damage[1d6[persistent,void]] damage. Effects that use vitality energy, such as the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Heal] spell, automatically end this persistent damage.

Summer Alcove (cold) Winter's bitter chill descends on the target.

  • Critical Success The trap has no effect.
  • Success The target takes @Damage[2d6[cold]] damage.
  • Failure The target takes @Damage[4d6[cold]] damage.
  • Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[4d6[cold]] damage;and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 hour.

Autumn Alcove (poison) Poisonous springtime pollen sprays at the target.

  • Critical Success The trap has no effect.
  • Success The target takes @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage.
  • Failure The target takes @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage.
  • Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

Winter Alcove (fire) Heat greater than the hottest summer afternoon engulfs the target.

  • Critical Success The trap has no effect.
  • Success The target takes @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage.
  • Failure The target takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage.
  • Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters one of the four alcoves

Effect The trap rolls initiative.

details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:20], @Check[nature|dc:20], @Check[occultism|dc:20], or @Check[religion|dc:20] on each of the shadow walls (disabling three of the four walls ends the hazard), or activating the orrery with the unlit star as noted in "The Orrery of Light."

publication.title: Pathfinder Beginner Box: Secrets of the Unlit Star

details.routine:
Each turn, the walls use 1 action. Roll a [[/gmr 1d10 #Routine Result]]{d10} and look up the result on the following list to determine which action the walls uses that round.

1–4 Single Target The walls lash out at a random hero in the room. Roll a [[/gmr 1d4 #Random Hero]]{d4} and count to the number you rolled, starting with the player on your left, to determine which hero gets attacked. The shadow lash is a ranged Strike with +10 to hit. On a hit, it deals @Damage[2d6[void]] damage (doubled on a critical hit as normal).

5–7 Wall Barrage The shadow arms lunge out from their respective wall, assaulting any one near an active wall. This hits any creature within 10 feet of an active wall (within 10 feet of a shadow hand on the map), dealing @Damage[2d6[void]|options:area-damage] damage. Each hero must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:15|basic|options:area-effect] save, taking no damage on a critical success, half damage on a success, full damage on a failure, and double damage on a critical failure.

8–9 Split Assault The shadows lash out against one half of the room. Roll [[/gmr 1d20 #Even Western Half, Odd Eastern Half]]{any die}. If the result is even, they assault the western half; otherwise, they assault the eastern half. This deals @Damage[2d6[void]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature in that half of the room, and each hero must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:15|basic|options:area-effect] save.

10 Overwhelming Assault Like the split assault attack, but it targets everyone in the room.

description.value: Note: This Strike can only be used with the Single Target result of the Shadow Walls' routine.

details.description: Sounds become muted in a @Template[line|distance:100|width:20]{20-foot-wide, 100-foot-long} segment of this street corridor.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:25] (expert) to twist the magic of the ley line around you, disabling the hazard for only you; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 23) to disable the hazard permanently

description.value:
Trigger A creature reaches the street's halfway point

Effect Each creature in the affected area must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:23] saving throw or be silenced as long as it remains in the area. A silenced creature makes no sound and can't use abilities with the sonic or auditory traits, including casting spells that don't have the subtle trait.

details.description: A wall socket loaded with a spear connects to a floor tile in one 5-foot square.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Disable Spear Launcher] (trained) on the floor tile or wall socket

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Quest #10: The Broken Scales

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is applied to the floor tile.

Effect The trap shoots a spear, making an attack against the creature or object on the floor tile.

details.description: Spikes lance out of the doorframe to skewer anyone who attempts to open the door.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Deactivate Trigger|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap,action:disable-a-device] (trained) to deactivate a trigger built into the door's handle

description.value:
Trigger The door is opened.

Effect The trap makes an attack against the creature that opened the door.

details.description: A piece of hide covered with a layer of dirt and gravel is suspended over a 20-foot pit filled with spikes.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to manually trigger the trap without falling in, or @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) to secure the hide so the trap doesn't activate
details.reset: Creatures can still fall into the trap, but the hide that covers it must be reset manually (a 10-minute activity) for the trap to become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature or other large weight (such as the rushing waters of the previous trap) moves onto the gravel-covered hide

Effect The triggering creature or object falls in and takes falling damage (@Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage) and is targeted by a spike's Strike. A falling creature can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] with a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save (this becomes a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save if rushing waters are pouring into the pit).

stealth.details: (trained) or @Check[perception|dc:26|traits:action:seek] (expert) to notice the control panel

details.description: A metal pole with three razor-sharp spinning blades is hidden in the floor, connected to trigger plates in up to eight floor tiles and a hidden control panel within 30 feet.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Disable Pillar|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) twice on the pillar, or @Check[thievery|dc:19|name:Disable Trap (Control Panel)|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) once on the control panel deactivates the whole trap. Breaking the control panel prevents anyone from disabling the trap using the control panel and prevents the trap from deactivating automatically.
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap uses its first action to make a spinning blade Strike against each adjacent creature, its second action to move straight in a random direction (roll [[/gmr 1d4 #Direction]] to determine the direction), and its third to make a spinning blade Strike against each adjacent creature. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on one of the trapped floor tiles

Effect The trap pops up in a grid intersection and makes a spinning blade Strike against one adjacent creature (if any), then rolls initiative.
10 feet

details.description: A set of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] spikes stabs into the feet of anyone on the landing or front stairs.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to unhook the pressure plate or @Check[crafting|dc:24] (trained) to block the spikes

description.value:
Trigger The door is opened while someone is standing on the stairs

Effect Spikes fire upward, dealing @Damage[(2d8+5)[piercing]] damage to all creatures standing on the landing or stairs (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). On a failure, the creature takes a –5 status penalty to its Speed for 1 hour; on a critical failure it's also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1}.

details.description: Large pressurized pipes connected to a trip wire are rigged to release a blast of steam on trespassers.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Disconnect the trip wire|traits:mechanical,trap,hazard]{Thievery (trained)} to disconnect the trip wire or a @Check[crafting|dc:24|name:Reduce the pressure|traits:mechanical,trap,hazard]{Crafting (expert)} to carefully reduce the pressure and prevent the blast

description.value:
Trigger The trip wire is pulled or severed, typically because a creature walked through the square with the trip wire

Effect Steam erupts from the pipes, dealing @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning],3d6[fire]]{3d6 bludgeoning damage and 3d6 fire damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|name:Avoid the erupting steam|traits:mechanical,trap,hazard]) to all creatures within 15 feet. Creatures that critically fail their save are knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

details.description: A ghostly host of god callers and their eidolons manifests to defend the barrow against intruders, and the strains of a battle song fill the air.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:22] to appear to be a defender of Storasta, @Check[diplomacy|dc:22] to demonstrate benign intentions, or @Check[occultism|dc:20] or @Check[religion|dc:20] to ward off the malevolent spiritual energies. Two successful checks are required to disable the haunt, and each attempt requires two actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-15: A Dirge for Sarkoris

details.reset: The haunt dissipates 1 minute after all living creatures leave area C1. Its spiritual energies re-form after 24 hours.
details.routine: (1 action) (holy, illusion, mental, occult) The haunt attacks what it believes is a demonic incursion, dealing @Damage[(1d8+3)[mental],1d8[spirit]|traits:holy]{1d8+3 mental and 1d8 spirit damage} to each living creature in the area (@Check[will|dc:21|basic|name:Storasta's Last Stand|traits:holy,illusion,mental,occult] save).

description.value:
Trigger One or more living creatures attempts to move past the entryway further into the barrow

Effect Each creature in the area feels their own form warping into that of a hideous demon and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected by the onslaught of illusions.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} and believes that it is infused with demonic energy, making it susceptible to holy effects (even if it is not unholy) for as long as it remains within the haunt's area.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature also gains weakness 5 to cold iron for the next 24 hours.

details.description: Bright, iridescent blue petals sprout from the cerulean center of this flower.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20] (trained) to safely move thorugh the flowers without activating the pollen.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-13: Within the Prairies

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the flowers' space.

Effect The pollen in the suncatcher blooms puffs out in a 5-foot burst. All creatures in the area must each attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:21] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is immune to the effects of suncatcher bloom pollen for 24 hours.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 hour and immediately falls unconscious. They do not fall prone or release what they are holding, and they can still wake up normally with a successful Perception check. If they are still unconscious after 1 minute they wake up automatically.

Critical Failure Same as failure, except the creature wakes up automatically after 1 hour instead of after 1 minute.

details.description: On the surface, a titanic flytrap appears to be a patch of the more common flytrap plant, but beneath murky waters it hides a far larger set of jaws, reaching 10 feet across, reinforced with woody branches and lined with paralytic hairs.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:22|name:Mislead the Flytrap's Senses] (trained) to mislead the flytrap's sense of weight and pressure
details.reset: 1 hour (or longer, after a large meal)

description.value:
Trigger A Small or Medium creature moves into a square that is within reach of the flytrap's hidden jaws

Effect The flytrap's jaws snap shut, making a jaws Strike against the triggering creature.

The target is trapped by the flytrap's jaws, gaining the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] condition until it [[/act escape dc=21]]{Escapes}. Additionally, it is exposed to the titanic flytrap toxin from the hundreds of tiny hairs that line the inside of its leaves. If the flytrap's jaws Strike was a critical success, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its saving throws against this poison. At the end of each of the target's turns that it remains grabbed, the target takes @Damage[3d6[acid]] damage.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:21]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] (1 round)

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the shelf's precarious instability

details.description: This stone bookshelf is badly compromised. It's just one push away from toppling over.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (expert) or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:22]{Engineering Lore} (trained) to shore up the shelf

description.value:
Trigger The shelf is attacked or touched forcefully (such as by a creature Shoved into it)

Any attack that fails to destroy the shelf triggers its Topple reaction

Effect The shelf tumbles over in the direction opposite its triggering creature, crashing onto the ground in a 10-foot-by-10-foot area. Creatures in the area take @Damage[(4d8+10)[bludgeoning]] (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic]). A creature that critically fails its save falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. The affected area becomes difficult terrain.

If the toppling shelf crashes into a space occupied by another toppling shelf, it triggers that shelf's Topple reaction. This can result in a chain reaction that causes multiple shelves to Topple, one after the other.

details.description: The former bank manager's ghostly presence tempts creatures to partake in a dubious meal.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:18|name:Recite a Prayer to Urgathoa] (trained) to say a prayer to Urgathoa and put the haunt temporarily to rest until it resets, @Check[occultism|dc:22|name:Commune with the Manager's Spirit] (trained) to commune with the bank manager's spirit and convince her to permanently pass on, disabling the haunt
details.reset: The haunt re-forms after 1 hour. The haunt becomes disabled and doesn't re-form if a creature eats a full meal at the desk-this can be any food, not just the "food" at hand.
details.routine: (1 action) The haunt sends a cloud of dust and bone shards hurtling about the room. The dust and bones batter all creatures in the room, dealing @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic]). Creatures who fail the save are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 round (or 1 minute on a critical failure).

description.value:
Trigger A creature looks over the contents of the desk

Effect The haunt tempts nearby creatures to eat from the plates and bowls arrayed on the desk by concealing it in an illusion of food the viewer finds delicious. Each creature in area D5 must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature sees through the illusion entirely and is temporarily immune to the haunt's routine for 1 minute.

Success The creature is not tempted by the food.

Failure The creature believes the food is appetizing and becomes driven to consume it. The creature takes @Damage[(4d4+4)[slashing]] damage as it chews the jagged bones and husks.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] for 1 round as the haunt continues to tempt it.

details.description: Plaster crumbles from this column moments before it collapses.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:22] (trained) to stabilize the column, @Check[athletics|dc:25] to push the column back into place

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the tiles adjacent to the column

Effect The column collapses. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save as debris falls on them, which deals @Damage[(4d8+10)[bludgeoning]] damage. The area adjacent to the collapsed column becomes difficult terrain.

details.description: The ground here is made of loosely packed soil that, when stepped upon, collapses.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:22] (trained) to reinforce the dirt so it's safe to walk on, or @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to collapse the trap from a safe distance
details.reset: Creatures can still fall into the pit, but the soil must be replaced for the trap to become hidden again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps onto any space in the dotted area on the map, or at least 30 pounds of pressure is put on the area

Effect The ground gives way. The triggering creature falls 20 feet down the hole, taking @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] and landing @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in area E10. A creature can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling.

details.description: The hatch atop the haunted furnace flips open and disgorges a pair of shrieking, burning ghosts.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:22|name:Cow a Spirit] (trained) to cow one of the vengeful spirits or @Check[religion|dc:25|name:Exorcise the Spirit] (trained) to exorcise the spirit. The haunt remains active until both spirits are cowed or exorcised, or until the furnace is destroyed.
details.reset: The haunt resets 1 hour after there are no creatures in the room.
details.routine: (3 actions) The burning ghosts lash at a random @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied] creature in the room (or any random creature, if no creatures in the room are stupefied).

description.value:
The target of the haunt's initial Ghostly Assault, as well as any creature later hit by a burning lash Strike, loses random memories, as if these thoughts were incinerated like pages in a burning book.

The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute while they forget random memories, as if these thoughts were incinerated like pages in a burning book.

Failure As success, but the stupefied 1 condition persists for 24 hours.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2}.

Trigger A living creature with an Intelligence score of 15 or higher enters the room, or any creature touches the furnace

Effect Burning ghosts burst from the furnace, exposing the triggering creature to the haunt's burn knowledge effect. The haunt rolls initiative.

details.description: An overwhelming feeling of being watched wells up in the minds of those in the room, an instant before an eerie red eye opens in the western wall.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:22|name:Appear Uninteresting|traits:haunt] (trained) to appear uninteresting to the watching eye (and thus be ignored by it) or @Check[religion|dc:22|name:Ward Against Being Seen|traits:haunt] (trained) to ward against being seen by or affected by the eye.
details.reset: The haunt becomes inert at the end of any round in which there are no @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened] creatures it can see. It stays dormant for 1 hour, after which point it resets.
details.routine:
(1 action) The eye glances about, and those it can see (whether in area C4 or outside of it) take @Damage[4d6[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:21|basic|traits:haunt] save) as fears of being watched impart ripples of pain.

A creature that takes mental damage from this effect doesn't reduce their @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened] value at the end of their next turn.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature remains in area C4 for at least 1 round

Effect An overwhelming wave of paranoia fills area C4. Each creature in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save, with the following results. An eerie red eye then opens in the center of the western wall, looking about the room, and the haunt rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} and feels like someone or something is watching them for as long as they remain frightened.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and treats no one as an ally as long as they remain frightened.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] as long as they remain frightened.

stealth.details: (trained) to hear the whispers from a distance

details.description: Lost spirits whisper, reliving their last moments, ready to drag the living into their illusion.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:19|name:Supress the Spirits] (trained) to suppress the spirits or @Check[performance|dc:19|name:Drown Out the Spirits] (trained) to drown them out

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-01: The Absalom Initiation

details.reset: The underlying spirits remain, resetting the haunt every midnight, until what truly happened here becomes public.

description.value:
Trigger Multiple creatures remain in area C4 for more than 6 seconds.

Effect The whispers become more insistent until each character in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save.

On a failure, a creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round (or for [[/gmr 1d4+2 #rounds]]{1d4+2 rounds} on a critical failure). Both the audible whispers and the babbling of the confused are related to the haunting's origin.

Owners Whispers and Babbles
Archdevil worshippers Desperate praise of Mephistopheles
Soulbound dollmakers Pleading "don't make me go in the doll again"
Alchemical experimenters Frantic claims of not wanting to live forever anymore
Slavers Begging to be allowed to be released in multiple languages
Crime lords Promising not to mess with the family again
Misguided Pharasmins Sighing desires to be put to rest

details.description: A massive orca known as Abendego's Squall leaps from the water. Its mouth opens to snap against any part of the ship that it can reach.
details.disable: @Check[medicine|dc:23] (trained) to remove the harpoon, @Check[nature|dc:21] (trained) to calm the orca, or @Check[intimidation|dc:23] (trained) to scare the orca away; two total successes are required to subdue the orca

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.routine:
d Abendego's Squall thrashes against the hull of the Snapdragon. With each action, it makes a flipper Strike against a PC adjacent to the railing. Whenever it makes a flipper Strike, it also deals its Strike damage to the ship whether or not the Strike was successful.

Speed 30 feet

description.value:
Trigger Abendego's Squall matches speed with the Snapdragon

Effect The orca leaps across the Snapdragon and tears its main mast from the deck in the process. This deals @Damage[30] damage to the Snapdragon. All creatures on deck take @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning]] damage from the shrapnel and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20|showDC:gm|basic] save. Abendego's Squall rolls initiative.

details.description: A flock of animated paper birds flit about to the playful sounds of an invisible flute, played by the invisible janni Mehryar.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:23] (trained) to create beautiful flying objects to complement the paper birds; @Check[acrobatics|dc:25] or @Check[performance|dc:22] (@Check[performance|dc:18] if using a flute) (trained) to perform along with the flute and paper birds (requires two successes); or one successful attack roll against the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Invisible] Mehryar (AC 23, @Check[perception|dc:23] to determine Mehryar's location)

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-14: Shattering Golden Chains

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if no hostile creatures remain outside.
details.routine: (3 actions) On each of the trap's actions, the flock of paper birds attacks a different creature. On a successful hit, each target must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:23] save. On a failure, they take @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]] damage.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the museum door without Fasiel's permission

Effect The trap rolls initiative as the paper birds attack. Wind and rain flurry through the area, applying a –2 circumstance penalty to ranged attacks and Perception checks, extinguishing non-magical fire, and impeding spells and abilities with the fire trait. Casters must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:6] or the action is disrupted.

stealth.details: (DC 23 to find the cluster of aeon stones at the center of the nexus in the fog)

details.description: A small cluster of aeon stones floats two feet off the ground, orbiting by shards of shattered aeon stones that crackle with electricity.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Remove Aeon Stone|traits:magical,trap] (trained) to remove one nexus aeon stone from the center
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap loses one action each turn for each nexus aeon stone removed from its center. On each action, it shoots a jet of electrified, razor-sharp aeon stone shards at a different creature within 30 feet, dealing @Damage[(1d8+3)[piercing]] damage and @Damage[(1d8+3)[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic|name:Aeon Nexus]).

description.value:
Trigger A creature who is not carrying a wayfinder with an aeon stone from the nexus slotted into it approaches within 15 feet of the aeon nexus

Effect The trap shocks the target, dealing @Damage[(2d8+4)[electricity]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic]), then rolls initiative.
