details.description: Many carnivorous plants that were once kept satiated now hunger for any meat they can get, even lashing out onto the path to get it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:25|name:Distract or Trick the Plants] (trained) to distract or trick the plants or @Check[nature|dc:22|name:Calm the Plants] (expert) to calm the plants

publication.title: Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within reach of the carnivorous plants' vines

Effect The vines wrap around the triggering creature. The creature must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] until it [[/act escape dc=22]]{Escapes}. Whether or not the creature is grabbed, the plants make a gnawing plants Strike against the creature.

details.description: A malfunction in the door's opening mechanism has caused unstable magic to pool within it, making it liable to release the sorrows it has collected from all who have mourned their lost homeland at its threshold.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23] (expert) or @Check[deception|dc:26] (trained) to coax the door's magic into allowing access; the DC is reduced by 2 if someone in the room has said "Tar Khadurrm" in the last hour.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the door

Effect The last of the door's magic arcs outward. All creatures within @Template[burst|distance:10]{10 feet} of the door must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:23] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 hour.

details.description: A large cylindrical metallic structure surrounded by dense cables and pipes, anointed with mystical runes which glow and hum vibrantly. Elemental energy arcs off three spikes that may have once been connected to some of the cables.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:23] (trained) to restart the machine's connection to an elemental plane, or @Check[thievery|dc:26] (trained) to find and deactivate the whichever rune is currently active; three total successes are required to disable the nexus. A spike can be disabled with a @Check[athletics|dc:26] (trained) check to rip it from the structure or a @Check[crafting|dc:23] (trained) check to reconnect some of the cables. If all 6 elemental energies are returned to the nexus, it is disabled.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-02: The Blackwood Lost

details.routine: (3 actions) The nexus spends each of its actions making an elemental wave Strike; each of these must be against a different creature who does not have the elemental trait. For each spike destroyed or disabled, the trap loses 1 action.

description.value:
Trigger A creature without the elemental trait approaches within 15 feet of the nexus or attacks an elemental within 15 feet of the nexus

Effect Unstable elemental energies surrounding the machine discharge abruptly from one of the spikes, sending waves of wind, gusts of metallic shards, jets of fire and other elemental bursts across the room in various directions. The hazard makes an elemental wave Strike against the triggering creature. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Trigger The nexus is broken or destroyed

Effect A ferocious gush of elemental energies bursts from the broken machine, dealing @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature within @Template[emanation|distance:15]{15 feet} (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect] save). The trap is then disabled.

The damage of this Strike varies; no multiple attack penalty. Roll [[/gmr 1d6 #Damage Type]] for damage type.

1 cold (air)

2 bludgeoning (earth)

3 fire

4 electric (metal)

5 piercing (water)

6 slashing (wood)

stealth.details: to notice a thickening in the fog and hear a whispered voice asking "is Garmon safe?"

details.description: Averlace's lingering sentiment wards her mentor's remains and her own.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained) to exorcise the haunt, or @Check[deception|dc:20] or Diplomacy to convincingly say Garmon is "safe"
details.reset: The haunt resets every night at midnight until the skeletal soldiers risen from Garmon and Averlace are destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the area of the haunt

Effect Averlace roars in anger or denial. All creatures within 30 feet must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save or take @Damage[3d6[mental]] damage and @Damage[3d6[sonic]] damage. On a failure, they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} for 1 hour (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on critical failure).

details.description: Enraged bees spread panic, causing the crowd to trample each other in a desperate attempt to escape.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:22] (expert) to calm the panicked crowd and guide them to safety, @Check[medicine|dc:20] (trained) to attend to victims' injuries, or @Check[nature|dc:21] (expert) to instruct the crowd on how best to protect themselves from bees. Alternatively, a PC able to communicate with insects can instead attempt a @Check[diplomacy|dc:18] (trained) check to calm and redirect the bees. Three successful checks are required to completely disable the hazard. The hazard is automatically disabled after 5 rounds or once both swarms are defeated.
details.routine: (3 actions) Panicked attendees suffer stings and accidentally injure one another in their fear and confusion. Increase the Casualties score by 1. This increases to [[/gmr 1d3 #Casualties]] if one swarm is stinging the crowd, and it increases to [[/gmr 1d3+1 #Casualties]] if both swarms are stinging the crowd.

details.description: The walls and ceiling begin bleeding more and more, and gouts of cursed blood spew from both decapitated statues' necks.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23] to exorcise the influence of Charg from a statue, @Check[thievery|dc:26] to dismantle the magical blood generator in a statue's neck, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd-rank, counteract DC 20) to render a statue inert; each statue must be disabled separately

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The blasphemous blood deactivates at the start of any round in which no creatures other than a sahkil are in area C13, then resets after 3 rounds.
details.routine:
(2 actions) The hazard sprays a sizzling gout of cursed blood from the neck stumps of each of the defiled statues of Pharasma for each of its actions. If a statue is disabled or destroyed, the hazard loses that corresponding action. Each gout targets a randomly determined non-sahkil creature in area C13, who must then attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save against the following effects. After dark, this trap gains 4 actions instead of 2, with each statue firing two gouts of blood. Regardless of how many gouts are fired, a creature can be targeted only once per round by these gouts.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes @Damage[2d6[spirit]] damage.

Failure The target takes @Damage[2d8[spirit]] damage, @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]] damage, and is exposed to the curse of death's blood.

Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[4d8[spirit]] damage, @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage, and is exposed to the curse of death's blood (and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to save against the curse).

description.value:
Trigger A non-sahkil creature moves more than 10 feet into area C13

Effect The walls and ceiling begin to weep blood at an alarming rate, causing the floor of area C13 to grow slick. A non-sahkil that moves across the slick floor is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] and must [[/act balance]] (@Check[acrobatics|dc:20]) as long as the hazard remains active. An area of effect with the cold trait created in this area freezes the blood on the floor, making it even more slippery (increasing the Acrobatics check to Balance to DC 22). The hazard then rolls initiative.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22]

Effect Your blood grows cold and imbued with death. Whenever you are struck by a critical hit, you take an additional @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]] damage, and as long as you are taking persistent bleed damage, you become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} as your body weakens under the lure of death's embrace. If you make your daily preparations at a location sanctified to Pharasma or Shelyn (such as area A9 after Shirail's phantom returns), you can attempt a new saving throw to end this curse.

details.description: An invisible solar fare causes this patch of unassuming ground cover to suddenly bloom into a radioactive feld.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:22] (trained) to remove a 5-foot square of jijioa, or 5 points of fire damage to destroy an affected area
details.reset: An hour after blooming, a jijioa patch goes dormant for 1 week.

description.value:
Trigger The jijioa patch is exposed to a solar fare

Effect The countless jijioa flowers suddenly bloom, magnifying the ambient heat of the sun a hundredfold. Creatures in the jijioa patch take @Damage[4d6[fire]] (@Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic]). Creatures that end their turn in the patch continue to take damage in this way as long as the jijioa is in bloom.

details.description: The faces carved on the wall appear to inhale, then exhale slithering tendrils of darkness to harm those who would despoil the tomb.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (expert) to disarm the trap's sensors in the chamber floor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 23) to counteract the faces. Breaking one of the faces on the walls reduces the trap's save DC, attack rolls, and its total actions by 1 per broken face; if all four faces are destroyed, the trap is disabled.
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 minute
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action per disabled face each turn. On each action, a different face spews a writhing breath of darkness from its mouth to a target in area C2. A creature can only be targeted once per round by the despairing breath, so if fewer than four creatures are in the room, the trap won't be able to take full advantage of all four of its actions.

description.value:
Trigger A non-flying living creature moves more than 10 feet into the area

Effect Each face inhales then exhales, and all living creatures in the area feel their strength and vitality being drawn out of them as tendrils of shadow from the faces latch onto their bodies. Each creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:22|options:area-effect,inflicts:enfeebled] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} on a critical failure).

No multiple attack penalty. A creature damaged by the despairing breath must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22|options:inflicts:frightened,inflicts:fleeing] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] on a critical failure); this effect has the fear trait.

details.description: A seep of mineral-rich water coats any unattended objects in a layer of calcite.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:20] (trained) to shatter the calcite using the resonant powers of the consonite crystals plus @Check[athletics|dc:20], @Check[nature|dc:20], or @Check[survival|dc:20] (trained) to dam the flow of water.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-13: Thick as Thieves

description.value:
Trigger An unattended inanimate object enters the path of the calcite flow

Effect The object gains a thin shell of calcite. Breaking the shell using physical damage will harm the object underneath.

details.description: Three giant centipede carcasses have wire springs that pull out and fling their sharpened legs around the room.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:23|name:Bend Carapace|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) to bend the centipede carapace so the wire can't pull out any more legs, or @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Cut Wires|traits:mechanical,trap] (expert) to cut the springy wires. After all three carcasses are disabled (regardless of the check used), the trap is deactivated. The trap automatically deactivates after 10 rounds.
details.routine: (1 action) Each creature within 30 feet of a centipede carcass that hasn't been disabled takes @Damage[6d4[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|name:Avoid Carapace Strike|traits:mechanical,trap] save).

description.value:
Trigger A carcass is touched or jostled

Effect Each creature within 30 feet of any centipede carcass takes @Damage[4d4[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|name:Avoid Carapace Strike|traits:mechanical,trap] save), and the area becomes difficult terrain due to the many sharpened legs on the ground. Each creature that Strides more than 10 feet through the area must succeed at a @Check[acrobatics|dc:20|name:Avoid Piercing Terrain|traits:mechanical,trap] check or take @Damage[1d4[piercing]] damage.

The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (expert) to notice the enticing effects of the pool

details.description: The swirling water in this pool entices anyone who can see or hear it to wander into its depths, drowning its victims.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:24] to scratch out the runes creating the effect, @Check[occultism|dc:20] (expert) or @Check[demon-lore|dc:20] (expert) to determine the password that identifies the party as believers, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd-rank; counteract DC 22); two total successes are required to deactivate the pool.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-16: The Heart of the City

details.routine:
(1 action; mental) The cleansing pool uses its first action to try and compel all creatures within 5 feet to enter into the pool. Creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20|traits:mental] save.

Critical Success The creature is immune to the pool's routine for 1 minute.

Success No effect.

Failure The creature gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] condition and attempts to enter the pool.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature must also use all of their actions on their next turn to try and pull one of their allies into the pool with them.

description.value:
Trigger a creature within 10 feet can see or hear the cleansing pool

Effect each creature within range must succeed a @Check[will|dc:22] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated], using all of their available actions to move toward and into the pool. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Trigger a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] creature enters the pool

Effect the waters rush up to encircle the creature, which must succeed on a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save or begin drowning.

details.description: A tunnel through this steep barricade is rigged to collapse. If a kobold spends one action to throw a lever, the tunnel collapses on the next creature to pass through.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) to carefully trigger the collapse without entering the tunnel.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-11: Flames of Rebellion

description.value:
Trigger A creature crawls into the tunnel in the collapsing barricade after the trap is activated with the lever

Effect The barricade collapses on the creature, who must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:25|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and escapes the barricade, emerging into a safe space on either side of it.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(1d8+3)[bludgeoning]] damage from collapsing debris and @Damage[(1d8+3)[slashing]] damage from broken glass before emerging on the opposite side of the barricade from the one they entered.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+7)[bludgeoning]] damage from collapsing debris and @Damage[(2d8+7)[slashing]] damage from broken glass before emerging on the opposite side of the barricade from the one they entered.

Critical Failure As a failure, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] beneath the barricade until they or another creature succeed at a @Check[athletics|dc:22|name:Escape Barricade] check to pull them free. They can still be targeted, touched, and otherwise interacted with by creatures on either side of the barrier.

details.description: Consonite forms needle-like stalactites that can easily break free if touched, setting off a chain reaction that threatens to pierce anyone who stands in the area.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:20] or @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Squeeze] or reach carefully through the gaps between the needles.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-13: Thick as Thieves

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches any consonite needle

Effect The needle snaps, and the vibrations resonate with some of the surrounding needles. The falling minerals deal @Damage[(2d8+7)[untyped]] damage to creatures within a @Template[burst|distance:5] surrounding the triggering individual (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save). The needle fall doesn't involve every consonite growth within these squares, so can be triggered again from the same location.

details.description: A cannon built of cast iron and infused with shadow magic, mounted on the side of Aslynn's flying ship.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:23] or @Check[thievery|dc:23] on the cannon's body to deactivate

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-08: Battle for Star's Fate

details.routine: (1 action) The cannon fires a blast that contains as many PCs as possible, choosing randomly in the event of a tie.

description.value:
Trigger The ship flies within 30 feet of Star

Effect The cannon rolls initiative.
to creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic])

details.description: A cannon built of cast iron and infused with shadow magic, mounted on the side of Aslynn's flying ship.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:24] or @Check[thievery|dc:24] to disrupt the cannon; two checks are required to disable it

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-17: Trapping the Hag's Claw

details.routine: (three actions) The cannon fires a blast that contains as many PCs as possible, choosing randomly in the event of a tie, then reloads.

description.value:
Trigger The ship flies within 30 feet of a PC

Effect The cannon reloads and rolls initiative.
@Damage[(2d8+5)[bludgeoning]] damage to creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save)

hp.details: to destroy the chains and disable the trap

stealth.details: (expert) to notice the blades embedded in the chains and the complex system of pulleys connecting them

details.description: Swinging bladed chains whip through the entire workshop.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) on the control panel on the raised platform deactivates the trap.
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap swings one slashing blade against every creature in the workshop as 1 action. The entire workshop is treated as difficult terrain because of the sheer number of slashing chains. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more creatures enter the workshop or end their turn in the workshop

Effect The trap makes a slashing blade Strike against the triggering creatures, then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (trained) to sense an ominous presence in the room

details.description: An invisible force shoves creatures backwards, hurling them through the chamber's doorway.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:23] (trained) to disperse the force or @Check[religion|dc:21] (trained) to hide from the ominous presence's senses

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-04: To Seek the Heart of Calamity

details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature spends one minute in the haunted chamber

Effect A powerful force sweeps across the room, shoving all creatures within it toward the doorway. Each creature in the room must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is shoved through the doorway. They can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:23] save to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] as a reaction. If they fail, they fall to the ground below, taking the damage from the failure entry.

Failure The creature is pushed 10 feet into the empty air and falls to the ground 60 feet below. They cannot attempt to Grab an Edge.

Critical Failure As failure, except the creature also collides with several cables on the way down, taking an additional @Damage[(2d8 + 6)[bludgeoning]] damage.

details.description: A whirlwind of four ephemeral, magical threads dispels the undead and shrouds the living in an endless slumber.
details.disable:
@Check[religion|dc:23|name:Dissipate One of the Threads|traits:hazard,magical,trap,complex] (expert) or @Check[performance|dc:21|name:Dissipate One of the Threads|traits:hazard,magical,trap,complex] (trained) to dissipate one of the four threads. Characters who failed or critically failed a Performance check to participate in Grandfather Eiwa's funeral in Chapter 1 have been able to reflect on the experience; these characters gain a +2 circumstance bonus to their Performance checks to disable this trap.

The trap is destroyed when all four threads are dissipated.
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after no living or undead creatures are within 100 feet, then resets.
details.routine: (4 actions) Each thread of energy passes through a random living or undead creature within 60 feet, dealing 4d6 damage (@Check[will|dc:22|basic|name:Death's Slumber Ward|traits:hazard,magical,trap,complex]) to the creature. A thread deals @Damage[4d6[void]] damage to living creatures and @Damage[4d6[vitality]] damage to undead creatures. Each thread passes through a different random creature, if possible. Each dissipated thread reduces the trap's number of actions by 1.

description.value:
Trigger A living or undead creature opens the trunk

Effect The ephemeral winds rise, sapping energy. All living and undead creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:60]{60 feet} must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2} on a critical failure). The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (trained) (or -10 and no minimum proficiency if the surface is disturbed)

details.description: A 15-foot-wide patch of water and sand attempts to submerge creatures that step onto it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:21|name:Safely Disturb the Surface] (trained) to disturb the surface

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-04: To Seek the Heart of Calamity

details.reset: The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it settles over the course of 24 hours.
details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the quicksand pulls down each creature within it. A creature that was submerged up to its waist becomes submerged up to its neck, and a creature that was submerged up to its neck is pulled under and has to hold its breath to avoid suffocation.

A creature in the quicksand can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:23|traits:action:swim] check to Swim to either raise itself by one step if it's submerged to its neck or worse, or to move 5 feet if it's submerged only up to its waist. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down one step.

A creature that Swims out of the quicksand escapes the hazard and is prone in a space adjacent to the quicksand patch. Other creatures can Aid the creature, typically by using a rope or similar aid, or attempt to pull the creature out with their own @Check[athletics|dc:23|name:Aid Another Creature in Quicksand] check, with the same results as if the creature attempted the check.

description.value:
Trigger A Huge or smaller creature walks onto the quicksand.

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the quicksand up to its waist. The quicksand rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

details.description: A flawed ritual emits surges of shadowy energy and opens tiny breaches into the Shadow Plane.
details.disable:
Seven flaws have appeared in the ritual circle, dancing like shadowy flames. Each of Tavvar's three aides keeps one flaw under control as long as they are alive, while Tavvar herself staves off the ritual's implosion as listed in the ritual anchor ability below.

Each flaw can be repaired with a successful @Check[occultism|dc:24] check or DC 20 (trained) check using a skill related to the flaw's original cause (see the table below). Regardless of success or failure, a PC who attempts an Occultism check to disable the hazard learns the cause of the flaws and the skills that can be used to overcome it.

Each success on a check to repair a flaw using these other skills reduces the DC of the Occultism check to repair a flaw by 2. Instead of using skill checks, the PCs can use force to stabilize the ritual. A PC who defeats a shadow wisp automatically repairs one flaw.

d4 Cause of Flaw Skills to Overcome
1 Harried (a "publish or perish" culture at the College of Mystery led to Tavvar cutting corners in the ritual's execution, so some runes aren't drawn quite right) @Check[arcana|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained)
2 Misplaced Affection (Tavvar is from Nidal and subconsciously hopes the ritual will show that there is some good in her homeland; she included some Shadowtongue in the runes and it's enhancing the energy @Check[society|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[religion|dc:20] (trained)
3 Cloistered (Tavvar hasn't done much field work and made a dangerous mistake in which mushrooms she used in the ink for the runes) @Check[crafting|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[nature|dc:20] (trained)
4 Restrained (Tavvar's own hesitation and self-doubt has led to the ritual slipping out of her control) @Check[deception|dc:20] (trained) or @Check[diplomacy|dc:20] (trained)

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-01: The Absalom Initiation

details.routine:
(1 action) On each of its turns, the ritual lets out a surge of shadowy energy, dealing @Damage[4d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic|name:Flawed Ritual] save) to each character who participated in ritual the past round (including Tavvar and her aides as well as any PC who attempted a skill check to disable the hazard). A non-participant can step in front of a participant as a reaction. This blocks the damage from the ritual caster, but the non-participant must then attempt the Fortitude save.

In addition to dealing damage, the ritual's surge of energy brings a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pfs-season-1-bestiary.Actor.Greater Shadow Wisp] into the room. Wisps act at the end of the ritual's turn. If there are already two shadow wisps in the room, instead of creating a new wisp, the ritual restores @Damage[10[healing]] Hit Points to each wisp.

description.value: If Tavvar dies, the ritual immediately ends disastrously. With a loud pop, it explodes, inflicting an additional surge of shadowy energy on every creature in area B5 before dissipating. This ends the ritual but does not remove any shadows that are already in the room.

details.description: A demonic presence animates the ship carrying demon pirates.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23] (trained) to ward against demonic presence, @Check[arcana|dc:23] (trained) or @Check[occultism|dc:23] (trained) to weaken the demonic possession, @Check[intimidation|dc:23] (trained) to scare it away, or @Check[sailing-lore|dc:21] (trained) to confuse it; two total successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.routine:
2 The demon ark uses its actions on one of the following options (GM's choice). It loses one action from its routine if a PC succeeds at an attempt to Disable it or it reaches its Broken Threshold.

  • Broadside Blast a The demon ark makes a Chthonian cannon Strike.
  • Demonic List d Effect The ship tosses and turns. Each creature in the hazard's area must make a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save. On a failure, it is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]; on a critical failure it also takes @Damage[4d8[bludgeoning]] damage.
  • Heads Will Roll a It makes a bowsprit Strike.
  • Sea Chantey a (auditory) All fiends within 60 feet gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and saves against fear effects; all non-fiends within 60 feet must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:20|traits:auditory] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Sea Chantey]

description.value:
Trigger The demon ark hits another ship and Encounter A begins

Effect The demon ark fires grapples at the Snapdragon. Each PC must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] ([[/act escape dc=22]]); on a critical failure, they're also impaled and take @Damage[(2d8+7)[piercing]] damage. The demon ark then rolls initiative.

All fiends within 60 feet gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and saves against fear effects; all non-fiends within 60 feet must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Sea Chantey]

Effect The ship tosses and turns. Each creature in the hazard's area must make a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save. On a failure, it is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]; on a critical failure it also takes @Damage[4d8[bludgeoning]] damage.

details.description: The ghosts of weeping, cowardly soldiers and the sound of claws scraping on the outer walls manifest.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:26] (trained) to stand firm against the coming terrors and show no fear or @Check[religion|dc:23] (expert) to exorcise the haunt; two total successes are required to disable the haunt

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates and then resets after 1 round in which no living creatures are within the watch post.
details.routine: (1 action) The sound of talons scratching on the outer walls of the room and glimpses of lean, shadowy shapes apping and scrambling at the arrow slits manifest. A PC who takes an action to [[/act seek]] these shapes and succeeds at a @Check[occultism|dc:21] check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Recall Knowledge] recognizes the shapes as nightgaunts. Each round on its turn, these shadowy nightgaunts reach impossibly long, thin, spade-tipped tails into the room through the arrow slits or down the stove's chimney to make a shadowy tail Strike against every creature in the room as 1 action. If the door to this area remains open, the tails have an easier time reaching inside and gain a +2 circumstance bonus on their attack rolls.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature ends their turn inside the watch post

Effect Ghostly human soldiers manifest in the central alcove, crying out in terror as they drop their weapons and beg for mercy. Each PC in area A3 becomes overwhelmed with the conviction that their own battle prowess will not save them from the danger rushing toward the building and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

A creature that takes damage from a shadowy tail is uncomfortably tickled by the slithering appendage and must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:21] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure).

details.description: The taxidermy monster lurches forward, howls, and exhales a blast of foul-smelling smoke.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] to pluck out the taxidermy's gemstone eyes
details.reset: The Devil's Breath resets automatically after an hour.

description.value:
Trigger A non-goblin creature approaches within 10 feet of the taxidermy Sandpoint Devil

Effect The taxidermy Sandpoint Devil lurches forward, opens its jaws, and emits a blood-curdling howl and a blast of foul-smelling gray smoke. All creatures in a @Template[cone|distance:30] directed south of the trap (which reaches all the way to the door leading out to the Pit) must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22|options:area-effect,inflicts:enfeebled] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 hour (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} for 8 hours on a critical failure).

details.description: A giant squid rams the ship from below, using its beak to puncture the hull. It then wraps its tentacles around the mast, rocking the ship violently.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:23] to pull the tentacles from the mast, stopping the ship from rocking; two total successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-03: A Foot in the Door

details.reset: The squid releases the ship for one round before regripping the mast and resetting the hazard The hazard no longer resets once the PCs defeat the naiads or they flee
details.routine: (two actions) On its initiative, the squid uses its first action to pull on the mast Every creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save On a failure, it falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] If a creature is prone when attempting the save, it slides 10 feet in a direction determined by the GM on a failure If this movement would force a creature off the ship, it can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] The squid uses its second action to make one tentacle attack

description.value:
Trigger The naiads board the ship

Effect Each creature standing on the deck must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save or fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: The pile of bones transforms into a Large silver dragon, while the PCs appear to transform into dark, shadowy figures of alien physique. The dragon's flesh appears to slowly dissolve into lines of black smoke, which trail away toward the PCs and appear to be absorbed into their bodies. Once the dragon is stripped of flesh, its skeleton explodes in a shower of bone fragments.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23|name:Exorcise the Spirit|traits:haunt] (trained) to exorcise the spirit (two actions), or @Check[diplomacy|dc:26|name:Calm the Spirit|traits:haunt] (trained) to calm the spirit momentarily (two actions).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-01: Citadel of Corruption

details.reset: 1 minute, as the bone fragments slowly move back into a neat pile with the skull on top.
details.routine:
Trigger A creature comes within 15 feet of the dragon skull

Effect All living creatures within 20 feet of the nest are peppered with bone shards and take @Damage[(4d8+10)[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|name:Dragon's Death|traits:haunt,divine,necromancy] save).

On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for [[/gmr 1d4 #hours]]{1d4 hours}.

details.description: Bodiless screams arise as the room rumbles and shakes, as if in the grip of an earthquake.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23] (expert) to exorcise the spirits or @Check[intimidation|dc:23] to stand resolute and show the spirits there's nothing to fear; three total successes are required to disable this haunt.
details.reset: The haunt deactivates once no living creatures are in the cave at the end of a round, then resets 1 hour later as the spiritual energy rebuilds to a breaking point.
details.routine:
(1 action; illusion, mental) A thunderous explosion rocks the room as the ceiling appears to split open and collapse. This cave-in is an illusion formed from the memories of the dead, but it can inflict real damage. All creatures in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22|traits:illusion,mental] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d10[mental]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[mental]] damage and believes the room is filling with rubble—they're @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} until the start of their next turn as they spend their energy navigating the illusory rockfall.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+12)[mental]] damage and believes they're pinned by rubble—they're @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until the start of their next turn.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature attempts to exit the room

Effect The cavern erupts into terrified cries as a deep rumbling fills the room. All creatures in area C12 must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: A massive ectoplasmic hand forms around a creature and squeezes it.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:24|name:Send the ectoplasm back] (trained) to send the ectoplasm back to the Ethereal Plane or @Check[acrobatics|dc:26|name:Move through the ectoplasm] to move through and disrupt the coalescing ectoplasm before it takes hold
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves over the haunt's area

Effect The hand forms out of ectoplasm and squeezes the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[(3d8+14)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic]). On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] ([[/act escape dc=22]]).

details.description: An obscuring fog covers the entirety of the hallway and northern room of the manor's second floor. Anyone moving through the area is subjected to an attack from a ghostly assailant.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:23|name:Maneuver Through the Fog|traits:complex,haunt] to maneuver through the fog, @Check[religion|dc:21|name:Ward Off the Spirit|traits:complex,haunt] to ward off the spirit, or @Check[stealth|dc:23|name:Move Through the Fog Without Attracting Attention|traits:complex,haunt] to move through the fog without attracting attention. Disabling the haunt requires two successful checks, and each attempt takes two actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-02: The East Hill Haunting

details.reset: The haunt lasts until disabled. It re-forms after 1 hour unless Eloise's ghost is put to permanent rest.
details.routine: (2 actions) The haunt uses an action to manifest an illusory shadow beside a creature ending its move in the fog, then attempts a spectral touch Strike against that creature. The shadow disappears after the Strike.

description.value: The area of the haunt is difficult terrain.

Trigger A creature enters the haunt's area

Effect A spectral figure manifests in the fog adjacent to the target and makes a spectral touch Strike against the creature. The haunt rolls initiative.

details.description: The enchanted pile of cookies and pastries arranged in this tasteful display case emit a supernaturally delicious aroma that compels those who fall under its smell to eat, eat, eat. No matter how many are consumed, the case always fills with more.
details.disable: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 20); destroying the case and its contents also shatters the enchantment and prevents further compulsion or poisonings.
details.routine: (1 action) On the hazard's turn, if any creature is within the range of eatable enticement that is neither compelled by nor immune to it, the enchanted sweets target that creature with the same effect. Otherwise, it targets all creatures who have consumed some of its contents with a gut-rotting magical poison. Any creature who has consumed some of the enchanted sweets takes @Damage[2d12[poison]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic] save.

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes within 5 feet of the open northern side of the glass display case

Effect The tantalizing smell of the enchanted sweets overrides the target's olfactory senses. The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24|options:inflicts:sickened] save. On a failure or critical failure, the enchanted sweets roll Initiative.

Critical Success The target is unaffected, immune to the enchanted sweets for 1 minute, and aware that the hazard was attempting to compel them.

Success The target is unaffected but remains unaware that the enchanted sweets were attempting to compel them.

Failure The target is compelled to remain beside the display case on its next turn and spend at least 2 of its actions consuming the enchanted sweets. It can repeat the Will save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. If compelled to consume the sweets for more than 2 consecutive rounds, the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The target is compelled to spend all of its actions consuming the enchanted sweets and immediately becomes sickened 1. It can repeat the Will save at the end of each of its turns, ending the compulsion on a success.

The sickened condition increases by 1 for each turn the target is compelled to spend all of its actions consuming sweets, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 3}.

details.description: A swarm of aggressive cuttlefish, octopuses, devil rays, and countless other species of sea life bite and bludgeon Timonithaea's enemies.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to remove the crown from the altar while avoiding being bitten by the swarming sea life; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 23) to disable the crown's enchantment, breaking Timonithaea's control over the sea creatures.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-03: A Foot in the Door

details.routine: (one action) The sea creatures attack, dealing @Damage[(1d8+3)[bludgeoning],(1d8+3)[piercing]] damage to each creature hostile to Timonithaea within the temple (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger The PCs enter the main temple chamber

Effect The chamber fills with sea creatures, then rolls initiative.

details.description: A chair on concealed tracks surges forward, knocking a creature into the chair's seat and trapping them in place with clamping armrests.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Disrupt the Tracks|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to disrupt the tracks
details.reset: The trap resets automatically over 2 rounds if the chair is unoccupied.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the track

Effect The chair rockets forward along the track and slams into the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[(3d8 + 15)[bludgeoning]] damage. A creature who succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:22|traits:damaging-effect] save takes no damage and moves out of the chair's path into a random adjacent square not containing the track. A creature who fails this saving throw is knocked into the chair's seat and captured by the armrests, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] on a critical failure; [[/act escape dc=26]]).

details.description: Time distorts along this hallway, making the air feel like sludge.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to disrupt the temporal distortions, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 21) to disperse the temporal energy

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the hall

Effect Time weighs upon the creature, dealing @Damage[(4d8+14)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic] save). On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 3 rounds.

details.description: A creature experiences the death of a priest carried off by griffons and fed to their chicks.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:22|name:Soothe the restless spirits|traits:hazard,haunt] (trained) to soothe the restless spirits, or @Check[religion|dc:24|name:Honor the deceased|traits:hazard,haunt] (trained) to recite a prayer honoring the deceased; two successful checks are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the Lea of Honored Souls. Ten minutes after deactivating, the haunt resets.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the gathering circle

Effect The triggering creature experiences the last moments of the priest who once tended the Lea of Honored Souls. Griffons swoop down from the sky and snatch the priest up in their claws, dealing @Damage[(1d8+7)[slashing]] damage. They're carried southwest across the valley to a mountain peak and dropped into a nest of young griffons. As the chicks devour the priest, the character takes @Damage[(1d8+7)[piercing]] damage and @Damage[2d8[mental]] damage. The affected creature must then attempt a @Check[will|dc:22|traits:hazard,haunt] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage from all damage dealt by the haunt.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and can't shake off the vision; they take @Damage[1d8[bleed]] and are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}-believing they're being eaten alive-as long as the bleed damage persists.

details.description: An invisible rune creates an invisible, spherical magical sensor with a 20-foot radius.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Erase the Rune] (expert) to erase the rune without triggering the sensor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 20) to counteract the rune

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the sensor area

Effect The rune detonates a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Fireball] centered on the triggering creature's square. This is a 3rd-rank fireball spell that deals @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).

details.description: An invisible rune creates an invisible, spherical magical sensor with a 20-foot radius.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (expert) to erase the rune without triggering the sensor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 20) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-09: Killer in the Golden Mask

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the sensor area

Effect The rune detonates a fireball centered on the triggering creature's square. This is a 3rd-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Fireball] spell that deals @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic]).

details.description: Beams of fire lance out of the flames within the Sihedron to sweep through the room unless a prayer to Abraxas is uttered in Thassilonian.
details.disable:
@Check[thievery|dc:23] to deface one of the small trigger runes on the walls, or @Check[religion|dc:23] to overwhelm the trap with prayers to a good deity

Bypass Prayers to Abraxas are carved into the walls in Thassilonian; speaking one of the prayers deactivates the trap for 1 hour.
details.reset: The trap resets automatically after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger The door to area B3 is opened or damaged.

Effect Two beams of fire lance out of the northern and western Sihedron, then swipe through the room. All creatures in area B2 take @Damage[6d10[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).

details.description: Rotating blades extend from the walls, floors, and ceiling of the surrounding halls when weight is removed from the trapped floor tile.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (expert) to disable the pressure plate

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

details.reset: The trap resets after 15 minutes.

description.value:
Trigger Weight is removed from the trapped floor tile

Effect Blades extend from the floor, ceiling, and walls of the surrounding hallways to eviscerate all creatures in the area, dealing @Damage[(2d10+7)[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save). Creatures that critically fail also take @Damage[1d4[bleed]] damage.

details.description: The flesh and leather sculpture of Azathoth seems to ripple and churn before eyes open across its surface—eyes that then open into mouths that extend long whipping tendrils of rasping filaments to bless with warped flesh.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] twice to pull away one of the two primary anchor points for the sculpture to cause the leather and bones to slump harmlessly to the floor, @Check[religion|dc:26] from a worshipper of a lawful or good faith to banish Azathoth's influence from the hazard, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank, counteract DC 20) to counteract the hazard
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after a worshipper of Azathoth spends 1 hour in prayer before it.
details.routine:
(2 actions; necromancy, occult) The eyes open like mouths to extrude a whipping tangle of filaments that lash out at a target in area F19 or within 10 feet of any entrance into the room. The filaments can't attack a single target more than once per round. A creature targeted by a filament must attempt a @Check[fortitude|traits:necromancy,occult|dc:22] save. The body of a creature slain by poison damage from this hazard melts into a heap and then splits open to allow a gibbering mouther to slither free; this creature attacks any non-worshipper of Azathoth it sees, or otherwise slithers off to area F20 to splash and gyre in the waters of the lake.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d6+7)[poison]] damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} as their flesh and muscles grow limp and saggy.

Critical Failure As failure, but @Damage[(4d6+14)[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2}.

description.value:
Trigger a non-worshipper of Azathoth approaches within 5 feet, or a non-worshipper of Azathoth attacks Ziradini in this room

Effect A dozen eyes blink open on the spherical shape to glare at the triggering creature, who must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: An invisible magical glyph on the guillotine's blade detects living creatures in the room, which causes the guillotine to fly off its hinges and attack.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Erase Glyph|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to erase the glyph (which requires a successful unarmed attack roll if the blade is already active) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 20) to counteract it. The trap has a secret bypass known only to Ralso and Pratchett: as long as the guillotine can see a creature in the room touching thumb and pinky together with an empty hand, it remains dormant.

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

details.reset: After 1 minute, the trap deactivates, though the door remains locked. The trap rearms itself each day at dawn.
details.routine: (1 action) The blade @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Fly]{Flies} up to 20 feet and Strikes any creatures whose squares it passes through. It cannot target the same creature more than once per round. The blade cannot be grabbed and doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the room

Effect One round after a creature enters the room, the door to the room slams shut and locks (two [[/act pick-a-lock dc=20]]{Thievery} checks to pick), and the guillotine blade detaches from its housing and begins flying around the room as the trap rolls initiative.
fly 20 feet

details.description: The characters witness Burning Mammoths walking this mountain pass in ages long past.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:26|name:Convince the spirits to remain dormant|traits:hazard,haunt] (trained) to convince the spirits to remain dormant, or @Check[religion|dc:26|name:Ritually silence the spirits|traits:hazard,haunt] (trained) to ritually silence the spirits
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A good-aligned spiritual descendant of the Burning Mammoth following enters the mountain pass

Effect Burning Mammoths from long ago appear alongside the party, walking the mountain pass as one united following. They walk in the footsteps of ancestors, heroes, and legends. Each creature that witnesses the vision gains a +1 status bonus to Will saving throws and all skill checks for 24 hours.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Ancestral Journey]

stealth.details: (trained) to smell the poison gas or hear it hissing

details.description: A spring slams and locks the room's door before four hidden gas vents begin pumping poison gas into the chamber.
details.disable: Four @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Block Gas Vents|traits:action:disable-a-device] checks to block the gas vents, or a @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Unlock Door|traits:action:pick-a-lock] check to unlock the door and escape

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

details.reset: The trap must be manually rearmed by unblocking the vents or replacing the poison gas tank in area E30.
details.routine:
(1 action) Each round on its initiative count, the trap pumps more toxic gas into the room.

Any breathing creature in the room takes @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage (a successful @Check[fortitude|dc:22|traits:poison,damaging-effect] save halves the damage taken). If a creature acts before the trap on the first round, it has the option of holding its breath to postpone taking damage from the trap-holding one's breath after the trap's first action has no effect, since the air in the creature's lungs is already tainted. Locating and blocking one of the hidden gas vents reduces the poison damage by 1d6-if all four are blocked, the trap is disabled, though the door remains locked. Otherwise, the trap functions for 1 minute before all the gas is expended and characters in the room cease taking damage, after which the trap ventilates the room for 1 additional minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens one of the drawers

Effect The door to the room slams shut and locks and the trap rolls initiative. (The trap can also be triggered manually or disarmed from area E30.)

details.description: A field of grass-like fungal filaments amid clusters of softly glowing, long-stalked mushrooms.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:22|name:Clear Patch of Mushrooms|traits:environmental,fungus,hazard] (expert) to remove a 5-foot-patch without triggering the mushrooms.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the mushrooms' space or damages the mushrooms. The mushrooms can't use this reaction if the damage was cold damage.

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within 10 feet are sprayed with itchy, distracting, glowing spores and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:22|traits:environmental,fungus,hazard] save. On a failed save, the creatures become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure) and take a -4 penalty to Stealth checks until the spores lose potency in 24 hours or are removed. This requires washing them off by bathing in water for a minute, a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Prestidigitation] spell, or a similar measure allowed by the GM.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Glowing Spores]

details.description: A strange shadowy figure churns in the waters at Goh's feet.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:21] to calm Goh enough that the haunt dissipates, @Check[religion|dc:23] to exorcise the haunt, or @Check[intimidation|dc:25] to "scare the shame" out of Goh

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: The haunt ends if Goh dies and doesn't reset. If Goh is dragged out of the water, the haunt ends at the end of its next turn but resets immediately the next time Goh enters the river.
details.routine:
(2 actions; emotion, fear, incapacitation, mental, occult) The haunt uses its first action to grapple with Goh and attempts to pull him underwater. Goh has a Fortitude DC of 19. In order to pull Goh underwater, the haunt must critically succeed at its grapple check against him with a result of 29 or higher to successfully restrain Goh. Goh is overwhelmed by terror and makes no attempt to escape, so it falls to the PCs to get him free from the haunt. If Goh is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] and pulled underwater, the haunt must continue to grapple him in following rounds. If the haunt manages to critically succeed at grappling Goh while he's restrained, the man dies from a combination of fright and drowning, at which point the haunt immediately ends. If Goh is pulled free and taken out of the water, the haunt loses one action and no longer attempts to grapple him. The haunt uses its second action to manifest the image of a drowned ghost, causing one creature it targets within 30 feet to attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save (the haunt always targets a creature who attempted to free Goh from its grip if possible).

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for as long as it remains frightened.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and fleeing as long as it remains frightened, or for 1 minute, whichever comes second.

description.value:
Trigger Goh steps into the Ceiba River

Effect A shadowy figure that looks like a swimming child appears to grab ahold of Goh's ankles and begins to pull him deeper into the water; Goh becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed]. The haunt then rolls initiative.
Effect @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple]

details.description: Hands of the buried dead rise from the ground, grabbing and tearing at creatures in the area to drag them underground.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:22] (trained) to ritually pray for the dead or @Check[occultism|dc:24] (trained) to exorcise the spirits' anger; two total successes are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area. After 1 hour, the haunt reactivates.
details.routine: (1 action) The grasping hands batter all creatures in the area, dealing @Damage[(2d6+7)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save). On a critical failure, a creature is dragged partially into the earth, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until it [[/act escape dc=26]]{Escapes}. If already immobilized, it's fully submerged and must hold its breath to avoid suffocation.

description.value:
Trigger At least two creatures enter the area

Effect The earth shifts wildly as the hands of the dead spring forth from the ground. The area becomes difficult terrain. Creatures within the area are knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] unless they succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:26] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: The carving weeps. Its tears turn to a mist that covers the halls.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23] (trained) to recite prayers for the dead, or @Check[dwarven-lore|dc:20] to speak the history of Raseri Kanton, or @Check[thievery|dc:26] (trained) to plug the holes in the carving

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: The trap resets whenever a creature enters the Labyrinth for the first time.

description.value:
Trigger A creature looks at the carved mourner

Effect The hallways fill with a thick mist. When the mist first appears, all living creatures in area A take @Damage[3d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save), and all undead creatures regain @Damage[3d6[healing]] Hit Points. All creatures within the mist become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed]. This mist lasts for 1 hour or until the haunt is disabled.

name: Hallways Full of Even More Mostly Illusory Opossums

details.description: Several distressed opossums dressed in school uniforms and under the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Mirror Image] fly about the hallway, guided by four giggling sprites.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:23], @Check[deception|dc:23], or @Check[intimidation|dc:23] four times to convince one sprite to undo their spells; @Check[athletics|dc:25], @Check[diplomacy|dc:25] (with wild empathy) or @Check[nature|dc:25] to subdue or soothe the opossums; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 21) to undo one sprites' worth of spells. Four successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-16: Dacilane Academy's First Great Prank War

details.routine: (4 actions) The hazard loses 1 action each turn for each Disable success. Each sprite takes one action to Strike a different randomly chosen PC.

description.value: @Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwarmMind]

Trigger A creature walks into the room

Effect The sprites scream "Surprise!," attempt a opossum flyby Strike, and roll initiative.

details.description: A 15-foot wide area of verdant-covered water attempts to suck creatures down into it.
details.disable: Two @Check[survival|dc:25] (trained) checks to find roots and tree limbs sturdy enough for everyone to use as escape paths ends the hazard's threat to all creatures in the area

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-99: Blessings of the Forest

details.reset: The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it accumulates leaf and greenery cover over the course of 24 hours.
details.routine:
(one action) On its initiative, the bog pulls down each creature within it. A creature that was submerged to its waist becomes submerged to its neck, and a creature that was submerged up to its neck is pulled under and must hold its breath to avoid suffocation.

A creature partially or fully submerged in the bog can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:23|traits:action:swim] check to Swim to either raise itself by one step (pulled under to submerged to its neck, or submerged to its neck to submerged to its waist), or to escape from the bog if it's submerged only up to its waist. This DC increases to @Check[athletics|dc:25|traits:action:swim] for creatures submerged up to their neck or worse. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down one step. Other creatures can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid], typically by using a rope or similar assistance (providing an automatic success), or attempt to pull a single affected creature out with their own Athletics check, with the same results as if the aiding creature attempted the check itself.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area of the bog.

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the bog up to its waist. The bog rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

details.description: A hidden pressure plate causes the secret doors to areas C2a to open.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] to render the pressure plate nonfunctional, or @Check[perception|dc:26] to locate a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] switch near the doors to area C3 that deactivates the pressure plate

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps onto the pressure plate

Effect The doors to the four C2a rooms open, allowing the goblin bat-dogs to roll Stealth for initiative instead of Perception.

name: House Spirits With an Absurd Number of Regenerating Pies

hp.details: if the house spirits take this much damage, they aren't killed, but they do flee the school and don't return

details.description: A group of house spirits run around the lunchroom hurling pies at the walls and all the people in the room—and then the pies magically reconstitute and throw themselves again.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:23], @Check[deception|dc:23], or @Check[intimidation|dc:23] four times to convince the spirits to undo their spells, @Check[arcana|dc:21] or @Check[occultism|dc:21] to unravel a pie's enchantments, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to undo one pies' worth of spells. Three successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-16: Dacilane Academy's First Great Prank War

details.routine: (4 actions) The hazard regenerates any pies that it has thrown at a cost of one action per pie (to a maximum of 4 active pies). Each successful check to disable the hazard deactivates one of the pies, reducing the number of active pies by 1. After regenerating pies on its turn, the hazard throws pies whenever a creature triggers continually sur-piesed. For each pie Strike, roll [[/r 1d4]]. On a 1, the pie pan has nails driven into it by Numblekin and deals an additional @Damage[(2d8+7)[piercing]] damage. The nails make the pie's damage lethal, rather than nonlethal.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the room

Effect A pie from the room magically hurls itself at the creature and attempts a pie Strike, then the hazard rolls initiative.

Trigger A creature enters the room, attempts an action with the concentrate trait for the first time this turn, or ends their turn

Effect The hazard makes a pie Strike against the triggering creature.

details.description: Magical sensors trigger clockwork cryomisters to expel frigid air, freezing intruders and potentially encasing them in a block of ice.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (expert) to disable the cryomisters, or @Check[arcana|dc:24] (expert) to trick the magical sensors, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 24) to counteract the sensors

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap resets after 10 minutes.
details.routine:
(1 action) On its turn, the trap fills the area with frigid air, which deals @Damage[(2d8+7)[cold]] damage to creatures in the area. Creatures damaged must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save. Clockwork soldiers and any creatures wearing a security badge are immune to this damage. The accumulating ice causes the floor in the area to become difficult terrain that lasts for 1 minute after the trap's routine ends.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage and increases its @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed] condition by 1.

Failure The target takes full damage and increases its slowed condition by 1.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and increases its slowed condition by 2.

Once a creature's actions are reduced to 0 by this slowed condition, the creature is completely encased in ice. The ice has Hardness 5 and 20 Hit Points, and its DC to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open] is DC 22. Breaking the ice frees the creature and reduces their slowed condition by 1. Each round a creature is encased in ice, they take @Damage[(1d8+7)[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area, and that creature is not a clockwork soldier and is not openly wearing a security badge

Effect The trap fills the area with frigid air, which deals @Damage[(2d8+7)[cold]] damage to creatures in the area (@Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic] save). Clockwork soldiers and any creatures openly wearing a security badge are immune to this damage. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A soot-blackened brazier glows with fiendish light, shadows fickering inside.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:21], @Check[occultism|dc:21], or @Check[religion|dc:21] while within 15 feet of the brazier to command it to stop, reducing its actions by 1 (or 2 on a critical success); @Check[thievery|dc:23] to find the hidden switch on the brazier that deactivates it; or 1st-rank spell, counteract DC 20, to dispel the shadow flames in one square, using any spell with the water or light traits

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-01: Intro to the Year of Battle's Spark: Enough is Enough

details.reset: The flames cease 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area, but after 1 hour, a well of darkness fills the brazier and it is ready to trigger again.
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap ignites shadowy flames in a @Template[line|distance:5]{5-foot square} within the area of the room. A creature that moves into or ends its turn in this fire takes @Damage[(2d6+5)[fire]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:19|basic] save). Increase the DC by 2 if the creature is in magical darkness (@Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic]).

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the area

Effect Shadows roil in the brazier. It casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Darkness] on itself, and then rolls initiative.

details.description: A recessed mechanism slowly pushes two iron doors closed, holding them in pace for 24 hours.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21] (expert)

details.description: The footing on this sloped ground appears to be stable at first glance, but the tiny rocks that cover it are loosely packed and prone to slipping.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:23] (trained) to navigate a safe path

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-16: The Heart of the City

description.value:
A creature steps on the unstable ground

Effect Rocks tumble and slip beneath its feet. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:24] save as they tumble against the rocks, which deal @Damage[(4d8+14)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage and falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in its space.

Failure The creature takes full damage, falls prone in its space, and tumbles down to the bottom of the slope.

Critical Failure As failure, but double damage.

details.description: Water splashes within an ornate 5-foot-tall amphora. The glossy exterior depicts images of a marid trapped in a cage of flames, the frightened creature waving their hands in warning.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:24] (trained) to scrape the flames away from the marid's image (requires two successes); @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 23) to disrupt the magical prison, disabling the trap for 10 minutes; or touch the amphora with Rayyan's seal to free the marid

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-14: Shattering Golden Chains

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 2 minutes pass without a creature inside the room.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap has one action per round by default, though it may gain more actions based on the encounter's scaling as listed in the sidebar. On each of the trap's actions, the amphora pours out water, increasing the depth of the water by 5 feet. Once the room is full of water, creatures in the room begin drowning, and the trap deals @Damage[(2d8+7)[bludgeoning]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic] save to each creature in the room on its action as the water pressure increases.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches anything in the room without Fasiel's permission

Effect The trap rolls initiative. A wall of water blocks the exits while the trap is active, making a shove attack ([[/r 1d20+19]]) toward the interior against any creature that moves into a doorway space.

details.description: A magnetically charged platform draws in armored creatures, then flings them up into the sky, where they are struck by lightning.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Ground the Electricity|traits:electricity,magical,trap] (trained) to ground out the energy or @Check[arcana|dc:26|name:Neutralize the Magical Charge|traits:electricity,magical,trap] (expert) to neutralize the magical charge

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the platform

Effect All creatures within 10 feet of the platform must each attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23|traits:magical,trap] save. Creatures who are wearing metal armor or are made of metal suffer a -2 circumstance penalty to this save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is dragged 5 feet toward the platform.

Failure The creature is dragged onto the platform, which is quickly raised on chains 30 feet into the air. The creature is then struck by lightning for @Damage[(4d8+14)[electricity]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] on the platform ([[/act escape dc=20]]).

details.description: The illusory scene of a furnished study covers the room's actual appearance. It flickers and fades, revealing a scorched and burnt chamber. A moment later, the screams of burning victims fill the room, and blazing skeletal arms reach from the ashes and charred walls to scrape and claw.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:26|name:Silence the Spirits|traits:action:coerce] to command the spirits haunting this room to be silent or @Check[religion|dc:23|name:Exorcise the Haunt] to exorcise the haunt
details.reset: 8 hours; destroying the tanglebones in area E3 destroys this haunt forever.
details.routine:
1 Action: If there are no targets within area B18 when the haunt takes its action, it can use its Lure reaction to try to get nearby victims back inside. The haunt manifests several burning skeletal arms that reach up from the ruined floors or out of the burnt walls to claw at every creature in area B18. These creatures must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:22|name:Lured to Ashes Fire|traits:damaging-effect,fire,necromancy,occult] save (creatures seated on a sofa take a -2 circumstance penalty to this saving throw).

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d8[fire]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d8[fire]] damage and gains weakness 5 to fire. This weakness persists until the creature is fully healed.

Critical Failure As failure, but @Damage[4d8[fire]] damage and weakness 10 to fire.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves more than 5 feet into the room

Effect All creatures in area B18 and all creatures within 10 feet of an open door or window adjacent to area B18 hear a compelling voice inviting them into the room to discuss a future move back to central Cheliax.

Each creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22|name:Lure to Ashes] save (creatures outside of area B18 gain a +2 circumstance bonus to this save). On a failure, the creature is compelled to move into area B18 and be seated on one of the sofas.

Creatures who succeed at the save aren't compelled and see the room as the burnt ruin that it is. The haunt then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: to spot the magical energies reverberating in the pillars

details.description: Starknife symbols on the six pillars glow and shoot across the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] on any four of the six pillars disables the entire trap (the trap is a 2nd-rank spell and the DC to dispel it is 21). Destroying any four of the six pillars also disables the trap.
details.reset: The trap deactivates 1 round after it has no target creatures and resets each day at sundown.
details.routine: (3 actions) For each of its actions on its turn, a carving on a different pillar glows and makes a magical starknife Ranged Strike against a random creature within range. Creatures might have cover from this Strike, such as from one of the pillars in the room.

description.value: If a creature passes the middle pair of pillars, both doors leading out of the room slam shut and lock; while the trap is active, the doors must be battered open with a successful @Check[athletics|dc:23|name:Batter Open Door|traits:action:force-open] check or sufficient damage (Hardness 12; HP 48 [BT 24]). One of the starknife carvings glows with light, detaches from the pillar, and then flies outward, making a magical starknife Ranged Strike against a random creature within range. The trap then rolls initiative with its Stealth modifier of +12

details.description: Ornate full-length mirrors with fiendish filigree depict the image of Fasiel ibn Sazadin in villainous triumph over his enemies.
details.disable: @Check[stealth|dc:27] (trained) to approach a mirror without showing up in the reflection, followed by @Check[thievery|dc:25] to cover mirror or dispel magic (3nd level; counteract DC 25) to counteract the binding

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-14: Shattering Golden Chains

description.value:
Trigger The mirror breaks

Effect The mirror releases a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.Dandasuka] rakshasa. The raktavarna rolls initiative and remains for 3 rounds, after which the spell ends and the rakshasa disappears. The summoned raktavarna can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

Trigger Fasiel is within 100 feet, this is the closest intact wicked reflections hazard, and a hostile creature's action would cause Fasiel to gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused], controlled, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed], petrified, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained], slowed, stunned, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] condition or would deal 20+ damage to Fasiel

Effect The mirror breaks and disrupts the action.
