hp.details: per integrated chain gun; Plasma Lance Hardness 10, Plasma Lance HP 26 (BT 13)

stealth.details: (trained) to notice it's still functional; noticing the annihilator is automatic

details.description:
A gargantuan scorpion-like robot, covered in signs of damage, has been bolted into the canyon's rock walls.

Integrated Chain Gun Hardness 10, HP 50 (BT 25) per integrated chain gun

Plasma Lance Hardness 10, Plasma Lance HP 26 (BT 13)
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (expert) to successfully disable the annihilator at the control box on the northwest ledge, @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:18] to jam the mechanics of either of the two chain guns, or the "tail" mechanism where the plasma lance is mounted, preventing it from firing. Jamming the guns or tail require first getting on top of the immobilized annihilator, which counts as difficult terrain. All attempts take 2 actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-24: Lightning Strikes, Stars Fall

details.routine: (2 actions) When the plasma lance is ready, the annihilator uses it to attack as many PCs as it can, otherwise it attacks with its two integrated chain guns while the plasma lance recharges, spending one action for each chain gun that is still functional. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty. If Synthetic Khismar is destroyed, the annihilator attacks as many creatures as possible.

description.value:
The scorpion-like tail of the annihilator projects a @Template[line|distance:80] of burning plasma.

Creatures in the area take @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic]). The annihilator can't use the plasma lance again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Plasma Lance]]{1d4 rounds}.

details.description: The party's memories of Osoyo coalesce into a titanic mirage in the storm clouds above.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:22] (trained) or @Check[arcana|dc:24] (trained) to divert the memory's rage; three total successful checks are required to disable the hazard
details.routine: (1 action) The memory of Osoyo gives a keening scream that shatters the layer of ice coating the deck of the Mermaid's Tears. Any creature on difficult terrain created by the memory's Glacial Gust takes @Damage[(2d8+7)[cold]] (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic]) from the icy shrapnel.

description.value:
Trigger The characters' shared vision during Event 15 subsides

Effect The memory of Osoyo unleashes a tremendous sigh of freezing wind, coating the deck of the Mermaid's Tears in ice in a @Template[cone|distance:60]. (The hazard can choose any point for the cone to originate.) The area becomes difficult terrain. Creatures in the area must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:26] save or take @Damage[1d6[persistent,cold]] as the freezing wind cuts to the bone. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: A dead body seems to ripple and bulge, as if something inside were shaking.
details.disable: @Check[medicine|dc:23] or @Check[thievery|dc:26] to carefully cut into the corpse and vent the worms harmlessly in a safe direction

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

description.value:
Trigger A creature jostles or touches the infested corpse

Effect The corpse bursts open, spraying hundreds of middenworm larvae in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20]. Creatures in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:25|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} and takes @Damage[2d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage from worm bites.

Failure The creature is sickened 1 and takes @Damage[4d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage plus @Damage[2d6[persistent,piercing]|options:area-damage] damage as the biting worms latch on.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} and the persistent piercing damage increases to @Damage[4d6[persistent,piercing]|options:area-damage] damage.

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 5-foot radius surrounds an invisible wall or floor rune the size of the creature to be summoned.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:21] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:23] (expert) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 20) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-11: No Time for Treason

details.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors.

Effect This trap summons a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pfs-season-3-bestiary.Actor.Living Landslide (PFS 3-11)]. The living landside rolls initiative and remains for 7 rounds, after which the spell ends and the living landside disappears. The living landside also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. The summoned living landside can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

stealth.details: (expert) to recognize that the scene before them is an illusion.

details.description: The illusion shatters, revealing the grove to be a lifeless scar and the seemingly peaceful loggers to be crawling aimlessly about the clearing, consumed by rage and lashing out at anything that comes near.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:22] (trained) to tame the primal energy, @Check[diplomacy|dc:23] (trained) to console the dryad, @Check[occultism|dc:24] (trained) to identify and heal the emotional wounds; three successes are required to disable the haunt.
details.reset: Unless Silene is destroyed, this haunt can recharge itself after 1 hour.
details.routine:
(1 action; emotion, mental, primal) Myristica bemoans her grove's fate, overwhelming foes with sorrow. Each creature in the grove must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23|traits:emotion,mental,primal] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round and takes @Damage[2d6[mental]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d6[mental]] damage, plus @Damage[2d6[persistent,mental]] damage. While taking this persistent damage, they are dazzled.

description.value:
Trigger At least two creatures enter the grove

Effect The illusion fades, and a sorrowful chill emanates from the giant tree. The haunt rolls initiative.

details.description: The Eternity Arch sparks with electricity, releasing bolts of lightning at each of the four largest time rifts.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) or @Check[arcana|dc:21] (trained), @Check[nature|dc:21] (trained), @Check[occultism|dc:21] (trained), or @Check[religion|dc:21] (trained) to seal each of the four time rifts, @Check[crafting|dc:23] (trained) to divert the lightning to another source, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 20) to counteract each time rift. Each attempt requires 2 actions. Skill checks to disable a time rift must attempted from adjacent to the rift.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled time rift. On each of the trap's actions, the Eternity Arch releases a bolt of lightning that activates that rift, dealing @Damage[2d8[electricity]] damage to all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:10] around the time rift (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save). It can release a bolt at each active time rift no more than once each turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of one of the time rifts

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[1d8[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save). The overloading arch rolls initiative.

details.description: The cracks scintillate and shift between the elemental planes, leaking perilous energy with each shift.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:24|name:Assemble a Seal|traits:environmental,magical] (trained) twice to assemble a seal using objects strongly grounded to the Material Plane, @Check[arcana|dc:22|name:Invoke Incantations|traits:environmental,magical] (trained) twice to invoke incantations to seal the cracks, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank, counteract DC 20) to counteract the cracks.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-16: Freedom for Wishes

details.reset: The cracks re-form after 1 hour.
details.routine:
(1 action) A surge of energy strikes a random non-mephit creature in the foyer. Roll [[/r 1d4]] to determine the element of the energy surge.

1-Energy from the Plane of Air: the creature takes @Damage[2d8[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|name:Planar Cracks|traits:air,electricity] save); on a failure, the creature is pushed back 10 feet toward the entryway. The hazard gains the air and electricity traits for this round.

2-Energy from the Plane of Earth: the creature takes @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|name:Planar Cracks|traits:earth] save); on a failed save, the creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. The hazard gains the earth trait for this round.

3-Energy from the Plane of Fire: the creature takes @Damage[(2d8+7)[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|name:Planar Cracks|traits:fire] save). The hazard gains the fire trait for this round.

4-Energy from the Plane of Water: the creature takes @Damage[2d8[cold]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|name:Planar Cracks|traits:water,cold] save); on a failed save, the creature's lungs fill with water, making them @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}. The hazard gains the cold and water traits for this round.

description.value:
Trigger A non-mephit creature enters the manor

Effect Raw elemental energy seeps into the Material Plane. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: A spring-loaded, poisoned needle is hidden near the drawer's keyhole. Disabling or breaking the trap does not disable or break the lock.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Disable Thievery] (expert) on the spring mechanism.

description.value:
Trigger A creature tries to unlock or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Pick a Lock]{Pick the Lock}

Effect The needle extends to attack the triggering creature.
plus Enhanced Hunting Spider Venom

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:23|basic]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d10[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d12[poison]] damage, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1}, and off-guard (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2}, and off-guard (1 round).

Note The clumsy condition caused by this venom persists for 24 hours before fading.

details.description: The ground around the lava pit is crumbling and unstable.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18] (trained) to weather the collapse safely; @Check[crafting|dc:20] (trained) to stabilize the ground

publication.title: Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the pit or within 5 feet of its edge

Effect The ground crumbles apart and the creature slides into the lava. That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling. Otherwise it falls 20 feet (taking 10 fall damage) and is exposed to lava, taking @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage per round of contact. Climbing the crumbling rock face requires a successful @Check[athletics|dc:20] check.

details.description: The floor wobbles with each step.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (trained) to prop up the floor, or @Check[crafting|dc:23] (expert) to reinforce subflooring

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: immediate

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves on the unstable floor

Effect The floor tilts into a steep incline. Any creature on the tilting floor must succeed at either a @Check[reflex|dc:26] save or a Acrobatics check to [[/act balance dc=22]] (the creature chooses) or else take @Damage[(2d8+7)[bludgeoning]] damage as it slams into the wall.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage. It keeps its footing and can move freely across the floor this round.

Success The creature takes no damage but is somewhat off-balance; the floor is difficult terrain for the creature this round.

Failure The creature slides backward into the wall, taking full damage.

Critical Failure The creature slides backward into the wall with great force, taking double damage.

stealth.details: or @Check[perception|dc:26|traits:action:seek] to feel a tingle in the air and smell a rich, chlorophyll-green fragrance with no discernible source

details.description: Primal magic becomes unpredictable near areas where Agyra lairs, sometimes to hazardous effect.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:22|name:Draw Off Excess Power] to negate the aura's effect for 1 round by temporarily drawing off the excess power and channeling it back into surrounding terrain, @Check[nature|dc:26|name:Disperse Lingering Magic] (expert) to completely disperse the lingering magic
details.reset: The hazard deactivates and immediately resets if it has no targets for 1 round; it also resets if Agyra slumbers for 1 week within 100 feet.
details.routine: (2 actions) On its initiative, the hazard gathers primal energy from the surroundings as its first action. Roll [[/gmr 1d6 #Energy Gathered]] to determine the type of energy gathered. The hazard uses its second action to replicate the associated spell (2nd rank, DC 23, spell attack modifier [[/r 1d20+15 #Spell Attack]]{+15}). The spell's target or targets are chosen randomly from creatures in the area. Any spell cast by this hazard is primal. 1 air (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Gust of Wind]); 2 electricity (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Thunderstrike]); 3 fire (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Blazing Bolt]); 4 poison (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Spider Sting]); 5 sonic (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Shatter]); 6 water (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Hydraulic Push])

description.value:
Trigger A character casts a primal spell within the hazard's area

Effect Each living creature in the area gains [[/r 2d4 #Temporary Hit Points]] temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute as life energy wells up around them, and the hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: Four magical prisms integrated into an aeon stone matrix fire energy beams at intruders. The trap doesn't target creatures wearing security badges, or in the custody of a creature wearing a security badge.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (expert) to disconnect one prism from the aeon stone matrix, or @Check[arcana|dc:21] (trained) to disrupt one prism's magical energy, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 23) to counteract one prism; four total successes are required to disable the trap.

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets when there are no viable targets in area A1. Damaged prisms are fully repaired after 24 hours.
details.routine: (4 actions) Each prism uses 1 action to make an energy ray Strike against the nearest creature who is not wearing a security badge or in the custody of a creature wearing a security badge. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty. This trap loses 1 action for each prism disabled or destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters area A1, and that creature isn't wearing a security badge and isn't in the custody of a creature wearing a security badge

Effect All four prisms light up, shedding dim light in 15 feet. The trap makes one energy ray Strike against the triggering creature. The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (trained); the DC to notice the protoplasmic extruder is 0

details.description: A spherical contraption of metal and reinforced hoses 20 feet in diameter, the protoplasmic extruder draws in the arcane slurry from Quantium's sewers and magically transforms the slurry into dangerous oozes.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:22|name:Disable Extruder|traits:complex,mechanical,magical,trap] (expert) twice or @Check[thievery|dc:24|name:Disable Extruder|traits:complex,mechanical,magical,trap] (expert) causes it to lose its action on its next turn. Two successes (or one critical success) disables it completely; @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 20) causes the protoplasmic extruder to lose its action on its next turn but never disables it.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-00: Origin of the Open Road

details.reset: The protoplasmic extruder must gather reagents from the arcane slurry of the sewers for 24 hours before it can be reactivated.
details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the trap creates an ooze in an adjacent unoccupied space. The first time the protoplasmic extruder uses this action, it creates a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.Gelatinous Cube] that acts immediately and thereafter acts on the protoplasmic extruder's initiative. In following rounds, the protoplasmic extruder instead creates @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Sewer Ooze]{Sewer Oozes} that act in the same manner.

Oozes created by the protoplasmic extruder are a nacreous green color; a gelatinous cube loses its transparent ability. All oozes created by the protoplasmic extruder are loyal to the Ohrlavis and act as they command.

The protoplasmic extruder can create no more than four oozes in total before it deactivates and resets.

description.value:
Trigger Bijan or Zaynap Ohrlavi can speak and rolls initiative when within 60 feet of the protoplasmic extruder.

Effect The protoplasmic extruder begins chugging more loudly and rolls initiative.

details.description: A magical mural threatens uncooperative inmates, causing mental trauma.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (expert) to deactivate the mural, or @Check[arcana|dc:26] (trained) to disrupt the mural's magic, or @Check[deception|dc:24] (trained) to pose as a superior and order the mural to deactivate, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 24) to counteract the mural

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: 1 hour.
details.routine: (1 action) On its turn, the trap threatens all living creatures in the room, dealing @Damage[(3d6+5)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:22|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature defies the prison's authority (such as by attacking a clockwork soldier or living graffiti)

Effect The mural commands the inmate to cease their defiance and surrender to authority. All living creatures in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} on a failure, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A seemingly ordinary hole in the forest floor exerts a strange gravity on creatures that come near.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:23] or @Check[occultism|dc:23] (expert) three times to disperse the unusual energy surrounding the hole, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract the engulfing magic
details.reset: If the trap isn't disabled, it becomes dormant for 10 minutes if there are no creatures within 20 feet of it at the start of its turn.
details.routine:
(2 actions) For each action, the pit chooses a different creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] to attempt to swallow. That creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save. On a failure, the creature is pulled 5 feet closer to the hole (10 feet on a critical failure). If this would pull the creature into the pit, that creature can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] as a reaction (DC 22). A creature who falls into the pit disappears into an extradimensional space that feels like being buried alive. A creature takes @Damage[(2d8+7)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save) at the end of each its turns in the extradimensional space. A creature in this extradimensional space is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1}; they can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] this extradimensional space with a successful @Check[athletics|dc:22], Nature, or Survival check. If they succeed, they appear in a random spot no more than 10 feet from the hole as if they had clawed their way from the dirt.

While this trap is active, all creatures treat movement away from the pit as difficult terrain.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 15 feet of the hole

Effect The ravenous pit seems to "inhale," attempting to pull creatures into itself. Each creature within 15 feet must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save. On a failure, the creature is pulled 5 feet closer to the hole (10 feet on a critical failure). The pit then rolls initiative.

details.description: A pressure plate in the hallway floor releases a latch, causing a ceiling panel to drop down. A +1 striking heavy crossbow contained in a steel box sprays bolts at any creatures in the hallway.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Disengage the reloading mechanism|traits:hazard,complex,mechanical,trap] (Expert) or @Check[crafting|dc:20|name:Disengage the reloading mechanism|traits:hazard,complex,mechanical,trap] (Trained) to disengage the reloading mechanism; the trap can also be disabled by the switch in area F2

publication.title: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powder Keg

details.reset: The trap runs out of ammo after it fires 5 bolts; it must be manually reloaded.
details.routine: (3 actions) The repeater crossbow trap reloads (2 actions) then Strikes a random creature in the hallway.

description.value:
Trigger An object or creature weighing at least 25 pounds steps on the pressure plate at the south end of the hallway

Effect A +1 striking heavy crossbow encased in a steel box drops from the ceiling and fires. The repeater crossbow trap Strikes a random creature in the hallway. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Giant mushrooms sprout from the ground, their caps reaching up to 15 feet high. Each giant mushroom flls up one 5-foot square and can appear anywhere within 20 feet of the center of the ring except adjacent to another giant mushroom. The ring can be disabled but not damaged before it activates.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:19] (trained) or @Check[occultism|dc:19] (trained) to repair the runes, @Check[survival|dc:23] (trained) to disturb the soil within the ring to weaken the mushrooms; one successful check to disable this hazard reduces the mushrooms' hardness to 0, and two total successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-12: Mischief in the Maze

details.reset: The giant mushrooms start to wilt and disappear 1 minute after all conscious living creatures have left the area. After 1 hour, the magic recharges, and the hazard is ready to trigger again.
details.routine: (two actions) A mushroom adjacent to one or more creatures uses Poison Spores. If there are no adjacent creatures, the mushroom vanishes in the ground and reappears in another square, making a Sprout attack. If no conscious creatures are within 20 feet of the center of the ring, the mushroom does nothing. A mushroom will not sprout underneath an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] creature. Each mushroom only acts once.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches a standing stone or enters the ring

Effect Two giant mushrooms sprout from the ground, each making a Sprout attack. The hazard then rolls initiative.
Adjacent creatures take @Damage[(1d6+6)[poison]|traits:nonlethal]{1d6+6 nonlethal poison} damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save).

A giant mushroom appears in a creature's square, dealing @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]|traits:nonlethal]{1d10+6 nonlethal bludgeoning} damage and pushing the creature to an adjacent square. The creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and can choose the square to be moved to.

Success The creature takes half damage and can choose the square to be moved to.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is randomly pushed to an adjacent square.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage, falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], and is randomly pushed to an adjacent square.

details.description: The effects of Hesla's profane ritual ripple outward through Deskari's Gullet. Physical tremors shake the cavern complex, while magical waves of energy affect those opposed to her actions.
details.disable: This hazard persists until Hesla's ritual is complete and her demonic forces defeated.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-14: Twice in Steel

details.routine: (two actions) The ground shakes, and waves of demonic energy surge through the cavern. Each PC must make a save against tremors and a save against demonic energy. Enemy combatants must only make the Reflex save against tremors; they are immune to the Demonic Energy effects.

description.value:
Trigger Combat begins within Deskari's Gullet

Effect The Ritual Effects roll initiative. Each combat indicates how often this hazard triggers during that combat.

Targets take @Damage[(1d8+4)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic] save) as rocks fall from the ceiling. On a critical failure, the target falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Targets take @Damage[(1d8+4)[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:23|basic] save). On a critical failure, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}. Holy creatures treat their save as one degree worse for the purposes of the sickened condition only.

details.description: A load of rocks, held up by a rope pulley, is dropped on the cavern's lower level.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25|name:Pin the pulley in place|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap] (trained) to pin the pulley in place

description.value:
Trigger A creature holding the rope pulley pulls it

Effect The net releases, dropping rocks on all creatures and objects in the lower half of area A15, dealing @Damage[(4d8+14)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|traits:hazard,mechanical,trap|basic]). The area then becomes difficult terrain.

details.description: An illusory cyclops in oracular robes cries out a warning, but then dissolves into a violent phantasmal cyclops, which calls out for the Blind Formless Chaos That Lies Behind the Stars to accept his sacrifice. It then plunges an obsidian athame into a creature's chest, summoning a foul entity.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:21] (trained) to repair Chamenaiti's magic, @Check[religion|dc:23] or @Check[occultism|dc:23] (trained) to offset the prayer, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 21) to counteract the warning

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.routine: a Rubbery crimson tentacles appear from a rip in the air and grasp at all creatures in the area, dealing @Damage[(1d6+3)[bludgeoning],(1d6+3)[acid]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger Sailing Mission 5 begins;

Effect The trap makes an athame Strike against a random creature, and then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (expert) to recognize that the scene before them is an illusion.

details.description: The illusion shatters, revealing the grove to be a lifeless scar and the seemingly peaceful loggers to be crawling aimlessly about the clearing, consumed by rage and lashing out at anything that comes near.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:22] (trained) to tame the primal energy, @Check[religion|dc:24] (trained) to suppress the haunt, @Check[diplomacy|dc:25] (trained) to console the dryad; three successes are required to disable the haunt.
details.reset: Unless Silene is destroyed, this haunt can recharge itself after 1 hour.
details.routine:
(1 action; emotion, mental, primal) Sarracenia's rage overwhelms up to two living creatures in the grove, each of which must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23|traits:emotion,mental,primal] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round.

Critical Failure For 1 round, the creature is confused, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1}, and gains a +4 status bonus to damage rolls.

description.value:
Trigger At least two creatures enter the grove

Effect The illusion fades, and a sorrowful chill emanates from the giant tree. The haunt rolls initiative.

details.description: Barbed wire stretches across the passageway at the perfect height to tear into ankles.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (expert) to detach the wire

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into the trip wire

Effect The trip wire snaps free and coils around limbs. The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:26] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 hour. On a critical failure, the trip wire embeds into the creature's skin, making the creature clumsy 1 until it rests for 8 hours.

stealth.details: (expert) to notice the floor sensor; DC 0 to notice the sphere

details.description: A gilded armillary sphere spins in place, shooting jeweled stars and planets at intruders.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21] (expert) to disable the sphere from rotating or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3nd rank; counteract DC 23) to remove the enchantment.
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any creature moving within range of its sensor.
details.routine: (1 action) A jeweled planet shoots from the sphere, targeting a random creature within 30 feet. The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+7)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the sphere, stepping on the floor sensor

Effect The sphere rotates and the trap rolls initiative.

details.description: Singe's spirit lingers in the Whippoorwill's crow's nest, where it calls out warnings and shoots intruders.
details.disable: @Check[pathfinder-society-lore|dc:23] (trained) to persuade Singe that you are Pathfinders or @Check[religion|dc:23](trained) to perform rites to lay Singe to rest; two total successes are required to disable the haunt

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: d Singe uses its first action(s) to make a long gun Strike against a random creature in the area. It uses its final action for Lookout's Warning. If the PCs successfully use Pathfinder Society Lore to Disable the haunt, Singe loses an action to make a long gun Strike.

description.value:
Trigger One or more creatures steps onto the Whippoorwill's wreckage

Effect Singe makes a long gun Strike against a random creature in the area, uses Lookout's Warning, and then rolls initiative.

Singe lets out one of the following cries, in order (repeating from the top, if necessary). Everyone in the haunt's area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save. On a success, the creature is unaffected; on a critical success, it also gains a +2 circumstance bonus on its next save vs. Lookout's Warning. Other effects depend on the specific cry below.

  • Thar She Blows: "Captain, look! A huge whale! It's coming toward us—fast!" On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for 1 round, looking for the whale; on a critical failure it's fascinated for 1 minute, and the condition doesn't end if Singe makes a Strike on an ally or uses Lookout's Warning.
  • No Quarter: "It's coming around again! It's relentless! Brace for impact!" On a failure, the creature is tossed off balance; it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] for 1 round. On a critical failure, it's also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].
  • Shark Bait: "Captain, it's hopeless! It's going to destroy the ship!" On a failure, a sense of doom fills the creature, which becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}. On a critical failure, the fear is overwhelming, and it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.
  • Going Down with the Ship: "Get below, captain, and secure the Forked Key! I'll try to give you time!" On a failure, the creature must spend one action putting an item it is holding away; if it is not holding anything, it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for one action. On a critical failure, it must spend three actions in a combination of putting away any items it is holding and fleeing.

details.description: A pressure-sensitive floor panel causes a spiked door to slam down into place from the ceiling, skewering anyone caught underneath.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (trained) to jam either the floor panel or the associated winch

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is placed on the associated floor tile

Effect The door falls, closing off the hallway. The door deals @Damage[(3d8+3)[bludgeoning],(3d8+3)[piercing]]{3d8+3 bludgeoning damage and 3d8+3 piercing} damage to anyone beneath or adjacent to the door when it falls, and it pushes them out of its space (away from the door, or in a random direction if the creature is directly beneath the door) as it falls. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save takes no damage and moves out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, they choose the direction.

Lifting the closed door requires a successful @Check[athletics|dc:25] check. Hitting the floor panels triggers the trap. The slab uses the same AC and saves as the trap, but it has Hardness 8 and 60 HP (BT 30).

details.description: The reflection in the mirror subtly twists and distorts, its expression taking on an unnerving sneer of malice.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23|name:Exorcise Spirit] (trained) to exorcise the spirit, or @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Cover the Mirror|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to quickly cover the mirror
details.reset: The haunt re-forms after 1 minute, manifesting in any mirror within its infused area (see Special below).

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 15 feet of the mirror, and the mirror is lit with dim or brighter light.

Effect Shadowy barbs impale the body of the reflected creature as the haunt makes a shadow barbs Strike.

A creature hit by the reflection's shadow barbs must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save as the haunt tries to draw a portion of its vital essence into the mirror. The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} on a failed saving throw, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2} on a critical failure.

Spectral reflections often infuse entire buildings, manifesting in any sufficiently large mirror. The example Hit Points and Hardness given represent a typical mirror reinforced by the haunt; at your discretion, the haunt might appear in reflective surfaces that are harder to destroy. Defeating a manifestation through damage destroys the surface, preventing the haunt from using it again. If this haunt appears in an area with many mirrors, consider giving PCs double or even triple the XP for a typical simple hazard.

details.description:
Four techno-organic "bush"; structures featuring mini-acid pumps activate. The first lashes out with sharp "branches" as it activates, and then all of the bushes spit acid at movement nearby.

HP is 14 per bush with a BT of 7
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23|traits:action:disable-a-device] or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:23|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) on each bush to collapse its acid pump into component parts (2 actions per bush).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-24: Lightning Strikes, Stars Fall

details.routine: (3 Actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each bush disabled. On each of the hazard's actions, one of the functional pumps spits acid at a creature within range. The bushes sense movement, and they only target creatures that moved from their space on their most recent turn. This trap does not attack any single creature more than once per round. It does not take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into a square adjacent to a bush

Effect The adjacent bush rakes its sharp branches across the creature, making a single branch Strike.

details.description: A taxidermy giant spider's abdomen opens up to spray the area in front of it with sticky webbing.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] to disable the pressure plate that triggers the sticky greeter.
details.reset: The sticky greeters must be reset manually.

description.value:
Web Hardness 5

Web HP 20

Immunities bludgeoning damage, critical hits, object immunities, piercing damage, precision damage

Trigger A creature steps on the pressure plate adjacent to the sticky greeter

Effect The sticky greeter lurches as its abdomen snaps forward, opens, and sprays the triggering creature with thick sheets of alchemical webbing. At the same time, the sticky greeter emits a high-pitched whistling sound. The creature must make a @Check[reflex|dc:26] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] and held in place, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] on a critical failure until it [[/act escape dc=22]]{Escapes} or the webbing binding it is destroyed.

details.description: Brightly polished copper chains rumble with distant thunder and statically charge the air.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (expert) to release the lock, or @Check[arcana|dc:25] or @Check[nature|dc:25] (expert) to harmlessly release the storm's energy

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-14: Shattering Golden Chains

details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to break or escape the chains

Effect The chains release a devastating blast of lightning that deals @Damage[(4d8+14)[electricity]] damage to all creatures in the room. Affected creatures must each attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 minute.

Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes deafened for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage. It also becomes deafened for 8 hours.

details.description: An invisible rune imprinted on a door latch releases a powerful electric discharge.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Scratch Out the Rune|traits:electricity,evocation,magical,trap,hazard] (expert) to scratch out the rune without allowing electricity to flow, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 21) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-06: Struck by Shadows

description.value:
Trigger A creature grasps the door latch directly or with a tool.

Effect The trap deals @Damage[(3d12+8)[electricity]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|traits:hazard,trap] save).

details.description: A mechanical sensor in the desk drawer releases a counterweight in the wall, which slams the door shut and opens the sleeping gas tank under the bed, allowing gas to fill the air-tight room with a hissing sound.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Disconnect the wire|traits:hazard,complex,mechanical,trap] (Trained) to disconnect the wire to the counterweight, preventing it from falling, or @Check[crafting|dc:20|name:Create a counteragent|traits:hazard,complex,mechanical,trap,alchemical,action:craft] (Alchemical Crafting) to create a counteragent to the sleeping gas

publication.title: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powder Keg

details.reset: The gas must be manually reloaded.
details.routine: (1 action) Each round on its initiative count, the trap pumps more sleeping gas into the room. Any breathing creature in the room is exposed to the toxin. If a creature acts before the trap on the first round, it has the option of holding its breath to postpone breathing in the poison-holding one's breath after the trap's first action has no effect, since the air in the creature's lungs is already tainted. The trap functions for 1 minute before all the gas is expended, after which it rapidly decays over the next minute. Opening the door to an adjacent room cuts the remaining time it takes for the gas to decay in half but exposes creatures in that room as well.

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the desk drawer without holding the release button under the desk

Effect The wooden door to the room slams shut and locks, and the trap rolls initiative.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22|traits:hazard,complex,mechanical,trap]

Maximum Duration 4 hours

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] with no Perception check to wake up (1 round)

Stage 3 unconscious with no Perception check to wake up (1 hour)

details.description: A summoning circle inlaid into the room's floor detects living creatures and summons a violent devil.
details.disable: @Check[perception|dc:18|name:Notice the Trap's Magic|traits:action:seek] to notice the trap's magic and avoid stepping on the floor outside the circle, followed by @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Break Summoning Circle|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to break the circle or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 23) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

details.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature steps on any part of the floor not enclosed by the summoning circle

Effect One round after a creature touches the triggering area, the door to the room slams shut and locks (two [[/act pick-a-lock dc=20]]{Thievery} checks to pick) and a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.Barbazu] devil appears in the summoning circle.

The devil rolls initiative and remains for [[/br 3d6 #rounds]]{3d6 rounds}, after which the spell ends and the devil disappears. The devil also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires.

The summoned devil can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: An invisible cloud of magical sensors detects living creatures in the room and summons fire elementals to slay the creatures.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:22|name:Approach Rune] (trained) to approach without triggering the trap, followed by @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Erase Summoning Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to erase the rune or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 20) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace

details.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the room

Effect Two rounds after a creature enters the room, the room's door slams shut and locks (two [[/act pick-a-lock dc=20]]{Thievery} checks to pick), and two @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Cinder Rat] fire elementals emerge from the floor.

The elementals roll initiative and remain for [[/gmr 3d6 #rounds]]{3d6 rounds}, after which the spell ends and the creatures disappear. The elementals also disappear if someone disables the trap before the duration expires.

The summoned elementals can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: Fires suddenly spring up along the rafters of the exposed roof as strange, monstrous shapes can be glimpsed in the sky above, which is suddenly filled with smoke.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:26] (trained) to extinguish fires swiftly or @Check[religion|dc:23] (expert) to exorcise the haunt; two total successes are required to disable the haunt

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates and then resets after 1 round in which no living creatures are within the schoolhouse.
details.routine: (4 actions) Ghostly figures flying in the skies above hurl bits of burning debris down into the three rooms of the schoolhouse below. The haunt uses its first three actions to target a random creature within the schoolhouse with a burning debris ranged Strike. A creature can't be targeted more than once per round—if there aren't enough targets for all 3 Strikes, the leftover Strike actions are wasted. On its fourth action, the haunt causes the fires in the building itself to flare up—all creatures inside the building take @Damage[2d8[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save). These flare-ups are the most extreme in area A4a; creatures in areas A4b or A4c gain a +2 circumstance bonus on this Reflex save.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature ends their turn inside the schoolroom

Effect Harrowing shrieks and frightening howls echo from the skies above as smoke plumes down into the rooms below. All creatures in the schoolhouse (areas A4 a–A4c) must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:21] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} from the smoke (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: Beams of magical energy fire from the Sihedrons to fan the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] to trick the basket on the door handle into thinking it's got a proper bead inside it, or @Check[stealth|dc:18] each round to avoid being detected by the trap
details.reset: Each trap resets automatically in 10 minutes.

description.value:
Trigger A creature ends its turn in the room.

Effect Beams of energy fan out of the Sihedrons on the east and west wall, causing 9d6 energy damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save) to all creatures in the room. The trap in area B4a deals @Damage[9d6[fire]] damage, the one in B4b @Damage[9d6[cold]] damage, and the one in B4c @Damage[9d6[poison]] damage.

stealth.details: (or 0 if the trap door is disabled or broken) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Detect Magic]

details.description: Decorated smooth-hewn flagstone flooring gives way along hidden seams to a 20-foot square, 25-foot-deep pit festooned with spikes and blades of petrified wood in the darkness below.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to disable the trap door mechanism

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-12: The Burning of Greensteeples

details.reset: Creatures can still fall into the trap, but the trap door must be reset manually for the trap to become hidden again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trap door

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes falling damage (typically @Damage[10[bludgeoning]] damage). That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

When creatures fall into the pit, the petrified wood spikes along the floor grow to attempt to plunge into the triggering creature.

hp.details: to destroy a single nozzle; Door Hardness 6, HP 22 (BT 11) to break open a door and escape the hallway

details.description: Nozzles hidden in the ceiling of the four corridors leading to the intersection spray jets of flame along their lengths when the trap is triggered.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|traits:action:disable-a-device] to disable a single nozzle, @Check[thievery|dc:21|traits:action:pick-a-lock] twice to unlock a door, or @Check[thievery|dc:25|traits:action:disable-a-device] to deactivate the trap from the control panel. A hero who isn't trained in Thievery automatically fails these checks.
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 10 minutes. After the trap is activated for the fourth time, it runs out of fuel and must be reset manually.
details.routine:
(4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled nozzle. On each action, a different nozzle fires a jet of flame down a different corridor, dealing @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage to each creature in the first 10 feet of the corridor past the intersection (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic]).

A creature standing in the corridor intersection can be hit by only one flame jet per round even if they're in the area of multiple flame jets. The creature attempts only one saving throw per round, but it takes a -2 circumstance penalty to its saving throw.

description.value: If a creature enters the corridor intersection, the four doors exiting this intersection close and lock, and the trap rolls Stealth (+13) for its initiative.

details.description: On the surface, a titanic flytrap appears to be a patch of the more common flytrap plant, but beneath murky waters it hides a far larger set of jaws, reaching 10 feet across and reinforced with woody branches and lined with paralytic hairs.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:24] (trained) to mislead the flytrap's sense of weight and pressure
details.reset: 1 hour (or longer, after a large meal)

description.value:
Trigger A Small or Medium creature moves into a square that is within reach of the flytrap's hidden jaws

Effect The flytrap's jaws snap shut, making a jaws Strike against the triggering creature.

The target is trapped by the flytrap's jaws, gaining the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] condition until it [[/act escape dc=24]]{Escapes}. Additionally, it is exposed to the titanic flytrap toxin from the hundreds of tiny hairs that line the inside of its leaves. If the flytrap's jaws Strike was a critical success, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its saving throws against this poison. At the end of each of target's turns that it remains grabbed, the target takes @Damage[3d8[acid]] damage.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:23]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[2d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[2d10[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] (1 round)

details.description: Nozzles connected to reservoirs of alchemist's fire are hidden just under the packed dirt of the floor, ready to spew burning jets when deployed.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to disable the nozzles or @Check[arcana|dc:26] (expert) or @Check[occultism|dc:26] (expert) to disable the nearby magical sensor
details.reset: The trap resets when an operator at the maze console in area A13 pulls the associated lever. The trap can be reset only once per minute.

description.value:
Trigger An operator at the maze console in area A13 pulls the associated lever

Effect Three hidden nozzles rise from the ground, and each makes a flame jet Strike against a creature in the area marked on the map. A creature can be hit more than once if there are fewer than three targets. On a critical hit, the target takes 1d10 persistent fire damage.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: A hidden vent in the ceiling is attached to a reservoir of freezing alchemical reagents that can create a bone-chilling cloud.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to jam the vent closed or @Check[arcana|dc:26] (expert) or @Check[occultism|dc:26] (expert) to disable the nearby magical sensor
details.reset: The trap resets when an operator at the maze console in area A13 pulls the associated lever. The trap can be reset only once per minute.

description.value:
Trigger An operator at the maze console in area A13 pulls the associated lever

Effect A vent opens in the ceiling, issuing forth a cloud of frigid fog that quickly fills the area marked on the map. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[6d8[cold]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:26|basic|options:area-effect] save). Creatures that fail the save also take @Damage[1d8[persistent,cold]|options:area-damage] damage (@Damage[2d8[persistent,cold]|options:area-damage] damage on a critical failure). The area remains cold for the next minute, dealing @Damage[2d6[cold]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect] save) to any creature who ends their turn in the area.

details.description: A dart launcher embedded in the ceiling holds a cartridge of darts tipped with deadly poison.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (trained) to disable the launcher or @Check[arcana|dc:26] (expert) or @Check[occultism|dc:26] (expert) to disable the nearby magical sensor
details.reset: The trap resets when an operator at the maze console in area A13 pulls the associated lever. The trap can be reset only once per minute.

description.value:
Trigger An operator at the maze console in area A13 pulls the associated lever

Effect A dart launcher emerges from the ceiling and makes a poison dart Strike against two different creatures in the area marked on the map. A single creature can't be targeted more than once.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: A cadre of evil spirits assails people's minds throughout the stretch of forest. The spirits first afflict their hosts with visceral images of their own deaths, then try to recreate their deaths by forcing their hosts to inflict similar wounds upon themselves. Creatures frightened by the haunt appear to have ephemeral masks with horns, tusks, and yellow eyes over their faces.
details.disable: Two @Check[occultism|dc:26|name:Exorcise the Haunt|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] or @Check[religion|dc:26|name:Exorcise the Haunt|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] (trained) to exorcise the spirits. A creature can either inflict 4 mental damage to the haunt or permanently lower the DC of checks to exorcise the spirits 1 by succeeding at a @Check[diplomacy|dc:22|name:Calm the Spirits|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] check to calm the spirits, a @Check[nature|dc:22|name:Ward Against the Spirits|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] check to use kami-specific warding incantations, a @Check[occultism|dc:20|name:Leverage Haunt Lore|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] check to leverage obscure haunt lore, or a @Check[religion|dc:22|name:Lesser Exorcism to Weaken the Haunt|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] check to use a lesser exorcism. Any creature currently frightened by the haunt is a potential target for inflicting damage to the haunt; mental and vitality damage only affect such a host creature if they choose to be affected, while force or similar damage harms both the haunt and the host equally.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-06: Lost on the Spirit Road

details.routine:
(3 actions) On its initiative, the haunt uses its first two actions to attempt to terrify a random target in its area that is not already @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]. The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22|name:Resist Terror|traits:emotion,fear,mental,hazard,haunt] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to this haunt for 24 hours.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target is frightened 1. The value of the frightened condition does not automatically decrease by 1 at the end of each of the target's turns. Instead, the target can attempt a @Check[will|dc:18|name:Reduce Frightened|traits:emotion,fear,mental,hazard,haunt] save at the end of each of their turns to lower their frightened value by 1.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}. The value of the frightened condition does not automatically decrease by 1 at the end of each of the target's turns. Instead, the target can attempt a @Check[will|dc:18|name:Reduce Frightened|traits:emotion,fear,mental,hazard,haunt] save at the end of each of their turns to lower their frightened value by 1.

The haunt then spends an action to compels one of the creatures that it has frightened to harm itself with surrounding forest debris, its strength fueled by spirit possession. The creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22|name:Resist Attacking Yourself|traits:emotion,fear,mental,hazard,haunt] save or attempt to Strike itself during its turn as its first action, using the haunt's statistics below. Note that frightened applies equally to the creature's attack roll and AC, and therefore does not affect the accuracy of the attack.

description.value:
Trigger Three or more creatures enter the haunt's area

Effect Spectral faces appear, and the haunt rolls initiative.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

details.description: The spirits of innumerable dryads and peaceful wood elementals, ripped from their bodies as they were turned into furniture, writhe and seethe, lashing out at both living and undead alike.
details.disable:
@Check[diplomacy|dc:20] (trained), @Check[nature|dc:20] (trained), or @Check[religion|dc:20] (trained) to set the spirits to rest or @Check[deception|dc:23], @Check[intimidation|dc:23], or @Check[occultism|dc:23] to convince the spirits that their eternal rest is coming soon.

A PC may make a Strike against AC 19 to destroy a single table or chair in the room, this functions as a successful check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid] another PC attempting to disable the haunt; two total successes are required to disable the haunt. If all of the tables and chairs are destroyed, the haunt is temporarily disabled until dawn of the next day.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-99: Under the Eye of the Mantis

details.routine: (two actions) The Haunted Hollows haunt uses one table flip Strike or two chair toss Strikes at random creatures in the area, favoring those who are trying to disable the haunt first. The table flip Strikes originate with any table in the room, destroying the table in the process. The chair toss Strikes originate with any chair or bar stool in the room, destroying that object in the process. If the haunt is not disabled, these objects reform and return to their original positions at dawn of the next day.

description.value:
Trigger Any living or undead creatures approach within 30 feet of the haunt's locus (the center of the open balcony) inside the tavern

Effect The haunt makes a table flip Strike with a reach of 20 feet against a random creature in the area, and then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the runes carved into the lid.

details.description: The magical runes glow at any attempt to open this chest.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to safely scratch out the runes or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 20) to counteract the runes.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-16: A Lie Told to Strangers

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the chest without speaking the command word

Effect The curse gives the target the sensation that countless ants are biting and stinging them for an entire day. The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23] save.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 24 hours.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} for 24 hours.

details.description: The font is hidden just under the water's surface. It spews foul brown sludge into the pristine waters, leaving them reeking and polluted as they move downstream. It can be reached without swimming, but not without getting wet.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:20] (trained) to draw upon the suppressed Primal energies of this place to cleanse the font, @Check[religion|dc:26] (expert) to suppress the void energies, @Check[thievery|dc:23] to undo the magic sigils allowing the font to operate; two total successes are required to disable the font.
details.reset: The void font deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: The void font uses its actions each turn to spew a single void geyser.

description.value: The foul font can take vitality damage, even though its an object and would normally be immune

Trigger One or more creatures touches the water within 10 feet of the foul font

Effect The font fires off a Void Geyser, causing a rain of foul water. The font then rolls for initiative.

The font shoots its foul brown sludge into the air, and it rains down upon all creatures within 15 feet that aren't completely submerged. Each of those creatures takes @Damage[5d6[void]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save). In addition, those that take damage from the font must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save.

Critical Success The target cannot be sickened by the font for 1 minute.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

Any creature that is submerged downstream (east) is automatically exposed to Spew Foulness at the start of its turn or when it enters those waters, except the damage is reduced with a @Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic] save instead of Reflex.

details.description: Sigils carved into the turquoise sphere atop the altar illuminate. Moments later, a rush of water flows into the hallway.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:23] (trained) to neutralize the tidal magic, or @Check[survival|dc:26] to predict the changing tides, or @Check[thievery|dc:26] (trained) to scratch away the sigils; two total successes are required to disable the trap.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.routine:
(1 action) The magic controlling the tides malfunctions, causing the tides to change every few seconds. Water flows rapidly between areas B2 and B4. Each creature in area B2, area B4, or the hallway connecting them must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22|traits:damaging-effect] save as the shifting tides buffet them, dealing @Damage[(2d8+5)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is moved 10 feet along with the water.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is moved 20 feet along with the water.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the altar that contains the tidal control orb

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as water rushes down the hallway. Area B4 becomes flooded with 15 feet of water.

details.description: A section of the cave floor collapses under a creature, plunging it 40 feet into a chasm filled with jagged stalactites.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20] to knock loose the unstable floor and reveal a traversable ledge to the south.

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature enters the marked portion of the map

Effect The triggering creature falls, taking @Damage[20[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage]{20} damage from the fall plus @Damage[3d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic]) from the stalagmites. A creature can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling, or succeed at a @Check[athletics|dc:20] check to climb out of the chasm.

details.description: A ghostly woman floats quietly through the shop, plucking hair and sending it slithering into the rafters or out to infest uncooked steamed buns.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:23] to calm the upset spirit down, or @Check[religion|dc:21] to convince the spirit to abandon the haunting via prayer. If the PCs use Hinode Akari's name or otherwise let the haunt know that they understand her story, checks to Disable this haunt gain a +2 circumstance bonus.

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.routine: (1 action) The haunt targets one creature in the shop at random, preferring targets who are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained]. The ghostly figure moves over to the victim and begins to strangle them with coils of her hair while she begins clawing through the target's hair as if ripping it out by the handful. The target takes @Damage[(2d8+7)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:22|basic] save) from enduring the painful sensations of being strangled and having their hair plucked out, even though they're not actually being physically strangled or losing their hair.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to interact with or speak to the haunt

Effect The ghostly woman shrieks and rises into the air as her hair grows swiftly, snaking through the shop to tangle into the rafters or weave through the seating area. All creatures in the shop must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: A wooden crate bearing a chthonic sigil glows with a power contained within it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) to open the crate with gradual release of its magical energy, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 21) to dispel the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the sigil on the crate

Effect The incomplete demonic pact causes the crate to explode. Bits of wood and energy from the Outer Rifts deal @Damage[(2d8+9)[bludgeoning],(2d8+9)[spirit]] damage to all creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10 feet} of it (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic]).

details.description: A golden guardrail sprays magical, flesh-eating acid throughout the stairwell.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (expert) to deactivate the trap's sensors, or @Check[arcana|dc:24] (expert) to disrupt the guardrail's magic, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 24) to counteract the guardrail

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap automatically deactivates after 1 minute. It resets in 1 hour.
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the trap continues to spray acid, dealing @Damage[(2d8+9)[acid]] damage to all living creatures on the stairs (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the south stairs

Effect The guardrail sprays flesh-eating acid, filling the entire stairwell. All living creatures on the stairs area take @Damage[(2d8+9)[acid]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic|options:area-effect] save). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A concealed acid fountain with three nozzles is connected to eight pressure pads in the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Disable the Fountain|traits:complex,mechanical,trap] (expert) on the fountain stops it from spraying acid, or @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Disable a Fountain Nozzle|traits:complex,mechanical,trap] (expert) disables one of the nozzles and reduces the number of actions the trap can take by 1. When the number of actions is reduced to 0, the fountain is disabled.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-00: Origin of the Open Road

details.reset: The trap deactivates after 1 minute, at which point its acid reservoir is empty. It can't reactivate until this reservoir is refilled.
details.routine: (3 actions) On its initiative, the trap makes an acid spray Strike against a random creature in the room for each of its actions.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on a pressure pad.

Effect The trap makes an acid spray Strike against a random creature in the room, although the initial damage is reduced by 8 as the acid eats through the concealing boxes. The trap then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: The faces of the original dwarven inhabitants of the city appear in the stone and scream in anger.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23] (trained) to ritually pray for the dead or @Check[occultism|dc:25] (trained) to exorcise the spirits' anger; a dwarf succeeds at a @Check[diplomacy|dc:22] or Intimidate check, asking or ordering the spirits to stand down.

publication.title: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm

details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 round after all living creatures leave the area. After 1 hour, the haunt reactivates. If the haunt is disabled by a dwarf succeeding at a Diplomacy or Intimidate check, the haunt is destroyed.
details.routine: (1 action) The faces continue to scream, dealing @Damage[5d6[sonic]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save) to all living creatures within 20 feet of the steps. On a critical failure, a creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 round. Deaf creatures are immune to this effect.

description.value:
Trigger At least two living creatures step on the staircase

Effect Dozens of mouths open on the stairs and eastern wall doors of the chamber and howl in anger and misery. Creatures in the area are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 2 rounds and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} unless they succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:27] save. Creatures who critically fail are deafened for 1 minute and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: A ghostly pair of teenagers-the Xarwin twins-cry out in pain as their brains are replaced into their heads. As if their trauma reverses, the initial pain fades and they express the desire to play.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:21|name:Distract the Haunt] (trained) to distract the haunt and entertain it until it becomes soothed or @Check[religion|dc:25|name:Exorcise Haunt] (trained) to exorcise the haunt
details.reset: The haunt resets automatically 1 hour after it's defeated or after no living creatures remain in the area to play with. Removing Marney's and Mira's brains from the brain collector statue in area E9 destroys the haunt.
details.routine: (2 actions) The architect (Mira Xarwin) uses Shackled Memories to cause a strange, house-like cage of solid ectoplasm to spring up around a randomly determined target. This house bears an uncanny resemblance to the home the target grew up in. The hunter (Marney Xarwin) then attempts a hunting shot Strike at that target.

description.value:
Trigger One round passes after a living creature opens the door to either of the children's rooms

Effect Both doors to the rooms open violently as the PCs experience visions of the twins having the tops of their heads cut away by unseen blades, followed by the ghastly sight of brains being placed into the empty skulls within. All creatures in either C14a, C14b, or in the hallway outside the rooms must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} by the pain.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+9)[mental]] damage and increases their malevolence value by 1.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d8+9)[mental]] damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} by the pain, and increases their malevolence value by 2.

The target believes a miniature version of their childhood home has built up around them, locking them in place. The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Shackled Memories for 24 hours.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] until the end of their next turn.

Failure The target is off-guard and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until the end of their next turn.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] for 1 minute or until they Escape.
The target imagines they're bleeding to death.

details.description: Hell hounds invisible to all but their lone target burn their chosen victim.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:24|name:Exorcise the Hounds|traits:complex,haunt,hazard] (expert) to perform an exorcism (with 2 actions)
details.reset: If their designated target dies or the hounds can't reach it in a turn, the hounds vanish into noxious smoke, but they manifest again elsewhere in the city after the next sunset.
details.routine: (2 actions) The haunt uses its first action to Stride (Speed 60 feet), then its second action for a jaws Strike against its designated target if it is in reach. If the target is not in reach, the haunt resets.

description.value:
Trigger A creature that doesn't openly wear the symbol of Asmodeus comes within 60 feet of the haunt's initial location.

Effect The hounds appear before the chosen target and begin to chase that creature, howling wildly. The haunt rolls initiative. Others cannot see or hear this baying and must @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Seek] to determine the hounds' location.

details.description: A chorus of Bellflower Network agents and allies chant about freedom from tyranny.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:21] (trained) to join the chant, or @Check[society|dc:25] to identify the Bellflower agents' tactics
details.routine: (1 action) The Bellflower chant continues their song. All non–Bellflower Network creatures in area A must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save. On a failure, the target is offguard until the end of its next turn.

description.value:
Trigger Zefiro becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained]

Effect The Bellflower chant swells from the assembled crowd. All grabbed, restrained, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] creatures can attempt to Escape as a free action. The Bellflower chant then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: The three powder kegs aren't particularly well hidden, but they are out of sight to casual observation.

details.description: Three bulky kegs of black powder placed at key locations in the temple are triggered to explode in unison, causing the entire structure to collapse into rubble and slide partially down the cliff face.
details.disable:
No special skills are required to disable this manually activated trap. As an Interact action, a PC can sweep aside some black powder to interrupt the fuse line, but this doesn't prevent Gerhard from activating a keg via blunderbuss. A keg that is removed from its position (either by being thrown over the cliff or merely carried away) retains its explosive potential, but as long as one of the three kegs are removed they no longer have the precise placement required to trigger a collapse-an explosion at this point merely inflicts fire damage in the appropriate areas but does not particularly damage the temple structure itself.

A keg's contents can be dispersed by scattering the gunpowder to the wind (most efficiently by pouring it over the cliff side) as a 2-action activity, or rendered permanently inert by pouring a gallon of water into the powder, inhibiting its explosive qualities. The kegs themselves can be destroyed with damage (note that the AC listed below for each keg takes into account the fact that Gerhard has wedged the kegs into nooks in the temple's stone flooring and walls), but note that any fire damage (including incidental fire damage from area effects like a fireball or Gerhard's blunderbuss) causes them to explode. At your option, other actions may be similarly effective at disabling this hazard.
details.routine: If Gerhard lights the black powder fuse, it burns at a speed of 10 feet each round; when the fuse reaches a keg 6 rounds later, the black powder bomb explodes. The bomb explodes immediately if he shoots a barrel with his blunderbuss.

description.value:
Trigger One of the three kegs takes any fire damage, including damage caused by a line of black powder burning up to a keg.

Effect The keg explodes, causing @Damage[5d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|traits:mechanical,trap]) in a @Template[emanation|distance:20]; this can cause an instantaneous chain reaction if either of the other kegs are in this area (note that as positioned, all three kegs are in range to trigger a chain reaction). Fire damage in overlapping areas isn't cumulative, but if all three kegs are in their initial positions when the explosion occurs, the temple itself collapses, causing @Damage[8d6[bludgeoning]] damage to all creatures in the temple (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|name:Temple Collapse|traits:mechanical,trap]).

details.description: A tiny spark of Nin's will drains the blood from living intruders.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (expert) to vandalize the space, or @Check[religion|dc:24] (expert) to pray to a god for passage, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 25) to counteract the magical energy

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap automatically deactivates after 1 minute. It resets in 1 hour.
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the trap continues to drain blood, dealing @Damage[(2d6+8)[void]] damage to all living creatures in the room (@Check[fortitude|dc:26|basic] save). On a failure, a creature increases the value of their drained condition by 1.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the room of their own accord

Effect The trap drains the blood from all living creatures in the room, which manifests as a bloody mist in the air. All living creatures in the room are dealt @Damage[(2d6+8)[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:26|basic] save). On a failure, a creature increases the value of their drained condition by 1. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Two blades, each hidden in a 15-foot-long ceiling groove, are connected to a trip wire.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:24] (trained) to disable each blade

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

details.reset: The trap resets after 15 minutes.

description.value:
