Trigger The trip wire is pulled or severed

Effect Both blades swing down, each one attacking all creatures under the ceiling grooves.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: Slender razor wire snags a creature, hauling them up into the air while causing deep, bloody wounds.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:25] (expert) or @Check[thievery|dc:25] (expert) to snip the wire without getting snared

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the square with the snare tripwire

Effect The bloodletting snare makes a snare Strike against the triggering creature.
The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] and pulled off the ground ([[/act escape dc=26]]). The persistent bleed can't be stopped while the creature is grabbed.

details.description: A stuffed owlbear twists toward intruders and breathes a gust of frigid air.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|traits:cold,evocation,magical,trap,hazard]{Thievery (expert)} on the owlbear's base

publication.title: Pathfinder One-Shot #4: Mark of the Mantis

details.reset: 10 minutes

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters either of the marked areas, or a creature remains in the room for 1 minute

Effect The trap breathes cold that envelops a @Template[burst|distance:15]{15-foot burst} centered on the triggering creature's square. This deals @Damage[(4d8+18)[cold]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|traits:cold,evocation,magical,trap,hazard] save), and a creature that critically fails the save becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 10 minutes due to frostbite.

stealth.details: (expert; or –10 and no minimum proficiency if the thicket has been recognized)

details.description: A 10-foot-wide patch of dead shrubs with wilted flowers steals the breath of the living.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:28] (expert) or @Check[survival|dc:28] (expert) to recognize the thicket for what it is and avoid it
details.reset: The thicket goes back into hibernation 1 round after there are no more targets to Strike in its reach. It then remains dormant for 24 hours.
details.routine: (3 actions) The breathless thickets patch attempts to Strike a creature in its reach. It then uses Suffocate if it has any creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed]; otherwise, it continues to Strike.

description.value:
Trigger A creature that breathes steps within 5 feet of a thicket

Effect The thicket makes a sinewy leaves Strike against the triggering creature. The hazard then rolls initiative.

If the breathless thicket hits a creature with its leaves, that creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] by the thicket. Unlike with a normal Grab, the creature isn't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized], but it can't move beyond the reach of the thicket's leaves. A creature can sever a branch by Striking against AC 18 and dealing at least @Damage[12[slashing]] damage. A branch doesn't have the thicket's Hardness, and damaging it doesn't deal damage to the thicket ([[/act escape dc=26]]).

Requirements The breathless thicket has @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] one or more creatures that breathe

Effect The dead flowers of the breathless thicket begin pulling oxygen from its victims' lungs. Each creature grabbed by the breathless thicket takes @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]|traits:nonlethal]{4d6 nonlethal bludgeoning} damage and must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:26] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and can immediately attempt to [[/act escape dc=26]] as a free action.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is stupefied 1 for 1 round, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}. For as long as it's grabbed by the thicket, the creature must hold its breath or suffocate.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature also falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] and starts suffocating. If it's no longer grabbed, the creature stops suffocating.

details.description: Shadows rise up from the empty tub, creeping along the floor, walls, and celling to blot out light.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:25] or @Check[nature|dc:25] (expert) to burn out the shadowy influence through prayer or meditation, @Check[arcana|dc:28] or @Check[occultism|dc:28] (trained) to use magic to block the shadowy influence from being able to manifest at all; three successes are required to disable the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates at the end of any round in which no living creature is in area A19, then resets 1 round later.
details.routine: (2 actions) A shadowy figure rises up from the bath, resembling the upper torso of a demonic humanoid with glowing eyes. On its first action, the haunt sweeps its many arms through area A19, dealing @Damage[4d6[void]] damage on all living creatures in the area (@Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic] save). On its second action, it either spawns an undead shadow from its mass, or it Sustains a currently spawned undead shadow; it can only have one shadow active at a time. This shadow has the minion trait, cannot leave area A19, and takes its two actions on the haunt's initiative. Void damage from the haunt doesn't heal a shadow minion.

description.value:
Trigger More than three living creatures end their turn in area A19 or any one creature ends their turn while standing within the empty bath

Effect Shadows seep out from the bath and swiftly coat the walls and ceiling, covering all entrances into A19 with sheets of shadow, plunging the area within into darkness. Non-magical light sources, such as a torch or lantern, do not emanate any light in the room while the haunt is active. This also suppresses magical light of rank 3 or lower. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: The countless bones and feathers on the floor churn into the air in a clattering swarm of partially reconstructed skeletal birds, whose mournful song fills the area as surely as do their slashing beaks and talons.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:23|name:Convince Carrion You Pose No Threat] to convince the carrion vortex that the PCs pose no ill intent or @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Exorcise the Haunt] to exorcise the haunt
details.reset: 24 hours; defeating the corrupted psychopomps in areas D5 and E4 destroys this haunt forever.
details.routine: (1 action; evocation, occult) The churning vortex of beaks and bones takes its action through area D2, clawing and pecking at any creature in the area. All creatures in area D2 take @Damage[(2d8+9)[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|traits:evocation,occult] save).

description.value:
Trigger Three or more creatures enter area D2, or anyone attempts to gather or destroy the bird remains

Effect The carrion vortex fills area D2 with the overwhelming cries of what sounds like thousands of whippoorwills, but this cacophony can't be heard beyond this room. Creatures in area D2 must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save. (worshipers of Pharasma or those with ties to psychopomps gain a +4 circumstance bonus to this save).The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature understands the dirge is also intended as a warning and a desperate request for absolution. The creature can sense the presence of the corrupted psychopomps in areas D5 and E4 as if under the effects of a locate spell, for 24 hours. As long as this effect persists, the character gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks made against the corrupted psychopomps in these areas.

Success As critical success, but the locate effect only lasts 10 minutes. The creature takes @Damage[2d8[mental]] damage from overwhelming feelings of sadness and despair.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[4d8[mental]] damage from overwhelming feelings of sadness and despair.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[6d8[mental]] damage from overwhelming feelings of sadness and despair, and as long as the haunt remains active and they remain in area D2, the character is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} as they're forced to do nothing but sob for one action every round.

details.description: A Hellknight on horseback shouts commands, beseeching all to return to order.
details.disable: @Check[Hellknight Lore|dc:21] (trained) to convince the Hellknight that she's adding to chaos, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:25] to request she stop yelling
details.routine: (3 actions) Cathilia uses her Hellknight's Orders Strike on creatures engaged in open fighting in area A2. She randomly chooses between targets in the likely case that there are multiple combatants.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than Zefiro opens the Museum's gates

Effect Cathilia shouts at the triggering creature. The target must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:27] save or have their action disrupted, and then Cathilia's meddling rolls initiative.

details.description: Two opposing mirrors spawn endless illusory duplicates of creatures in the hall.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Reposition Mirror|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to reposition each mirror, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 25) to counteract each mirror
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 day.
details.routine: (2 actions) This trap loses 1 action for each mirror disabled. Each mirror uses 1 action to Spawn a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-dark-archive.Actor.Reflection] of a creature reflected in the mirror. The hazard can have a maximum of four reflections spawned at once.

description.value:
Trigger A creature is reflected in the mirror

Effect The mirror creates a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-dark-archive.Actor.Reflection] of the triggering creature, which Steps out of the mirror and into the hall. The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (or 0 if a PC has analyzed the supports and discovered they are on the verge of collapse)

details.description: As a pillar collapses, the bridge itself groans and shakes, then crumbles into the river below.
details.disable: Several weeks of repair can rebuild the supports and remove the danger; at the GM's discretion, spells like @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Wall of Stone] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Wall of Force] can be used to augment the supports more quickly, although these spells are likely out of reach of the PCs at this point.

description.value:
Trigger A support is destroyed

Effect The bridge collapses. Any creature on the bridge between the red dotted lines falls 30 feet into water or onto land, taking damage as appropriate plus an additional @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic]) from falling debris; a creature within five feet of the red dotted line can try to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] (@Check[reflex|dc:24]) to avoid falling. A creature under the bridge-either directly below it or within ten feet of it-must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:24] save; a creature that's not directly under the bridge improves the result of their saving throw one degree.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d8 + 9)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d8 + 18)[bludgeoning]] damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(8d8 + 18)[bludgeoning]] damage, is knocked prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] by fallen rubble from the collapsed bridge.

details.description: Abyssal fire hits the stone beneath the ramp, causing a 15-foot-long section of it to collapse.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:28] (expert) to stabilize the masonry. Each attempt takes 2 actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-22: Breaking the Storm: Excising Ruination

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the collapse site

Effect The triggering creature falls off the Colossus and lands on the ground, taking @Damage[40[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage. They can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling (@Check[reflex|dc:22] save).

details.description: Churning vapors of glowing green mist fill a 30-foot-deep chasm.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:28] to counteract Kabriri's influence here by offering prayers to any non-evil deity and causing the mist to disperse, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 23) to counteract the mist for 24 hours
details.reset: The hazard resets automatically at the end of a turn and can trigger again the next round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature ends its turn within the mist

Effect The mist swiftly decays the flesh of all living creatures in the pit, inflicting @Damage[10d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:27|basic] save).

details.description: Bats and rats swarm around Ezio's enemies.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:24] or @Check[religion|dc:24] (trained) to scatter the vermin; two successes are required to disable it
details.routine: (1 action) The bats and rats bite all creatures in the room, doing @Damage[(2d8+3)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save). On a failure, the creature also takes @Damage[3[persistent,bleed]] damage.

description.value:
Trigger Ezio summons the swarms

Effect The swarms coalesce in nauseating fashion. Each creature in the room must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: A crew of anarchists rush to fight the dottari.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:21] to force them to back down, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:25] (expert) to request that they only injure the dottari
details.routine: (2 actions) The rioters lob bricks and other heavy objects at any dottari excruciator divisions as an action, dealing @Damage[(2d6+10)[bludgeoning]] damage to the dottari and any adjacent creatures (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A dottari excruciator division deals damage to a living creature

Effect The Crowncrusher riot throws a volley of homemade explosives that deals @Damage[2d10[fire]] damage to the triggering creature and all creatures within 10 feet of it (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] save), and then rolls initiative.
Common

details.description: Spirits bound to sand-covered remains cascade down upon the living, imposing the weighty, helpless press of their final moments.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:21|name:Exorcise the Spirits|traits:complex,haunt,hazard] or @Check[occultism|dc:21|name:Exorcise the Spirits|traits:complex,haunt,hazard] (trained) to exorcise the spirits, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:25|name:Provide the Spirits with Comfort|traits:complex,haunt,hazard] to provide them with comfort. Each check requires 2 actions, which have the verbal trait, and three successful checks are required to address all ofthe spirits.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-09: The Secluded Siege

details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine:
(1 action) The haunt imposes the sensation of becoming buried beneath millions of grains of sand on all living creatures within the area. Each living creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24|traits:complex,haunt,hazard,damaging-effect] save as the sensation threatens to overcome them. The creatures take @Damage[(2d8+7)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all its Speeds for 1 round.

Failure The creature takes full damage and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all its Speeds for 1 round. It also begins suffocating. The creature can begin breathing normally again if it leaves the area of the haunt or the haunt is disabled.

Critical Failure As failure, but the target takes double damage.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Crushing Spirits]

description.value:
Trigger A creature remains within the area occupied by the buried souls for 1 round or longer

Effect The haunt rolls initiative (if it hasn't already) as the spirits surge forth, swirling up sand in their wake.

stealth.details: but @Check[perception|dc:24|name:Notice Lights in Eyes|traits:complex,magical,fire,trap,hazard] to notice that the fiery lights within the skull's eye sockets seem to be looking around the room.

details.description: A pair of magical gems inside the dragon skull's eye sockets act as magical sensors with a 30-foot radius.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27|name:Pry Out the Embedded Gems|traits:complex,magical,fire,trap,hazard] (expert) two times to pry the gems embedded deep in the skull's eye sockets without triggering the sensor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank, counteract DC 22) twice to dispel both eyes.
details.routine: (2 actions) On its initiative, Dahak's skull fires up to two eye beams; it must target a different creature with each attack. The eye beams can't target creatures directly below the skull.

description.value: Perception [[/r 1d20+16 #Perception]]{+16}; @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.Darkvision], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.See the Unseen]

Trigger A living creature that is not a member of the Cinderclaws enters area C9

Effect One of the dragon skull's eye sockets shoots an eye beam at the triggering creature, then the hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: A magical pipe organ for controlling and bolstering the undead. Creatures that are trained in Performance can spend one action to attempt to play a tune.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25|name:Stopper the Pipes SC|traits:magical,necromancy] (expert) to stopper the pipes. Each pipe stoppered imparts a stacking -1 circumstance penalty to Performance checks with the organ. Once four pipes have been stoppered, the organ ceases to function.

description.value:
Trigger An adjacent creature attempts to play the organ

Effect The performer makes a @Check[performance|dc:21|name:Play the Dead Organ|traits:magical,necromancy] check.

Critical Success As success, and all other creatures who attempt to perform on the dead organ suffer a -1 status penalty to Play a Tune for 1 round.

Success Creatures within 100 feet of the organ are subjected to one of the following effects, as chosen by the performer:

  • Bolster (auditory, necromancy): Mindless undead within range gain a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 round.
  • Dance (auditory, move, necromancy): Mindless undead within range Stride up to 15 feet in the same cardinal direction. All targets move the same distance. If impeded, they move as far as possible, then stop.
  • Discordant Blast (auditory, evocation, sonic): All living creatures within 10 feet of the organ except the performer suffer @Damage[(2d8+9)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:24|basic|name:Discordant Blast|traits:auditory,evocation,magical,sonic,necromancy] save). On a failure or critical failure, a creature is also pushed 15 feet away from the organ.
  • Swing (attack, auditory, flourish, necromancy): Mindless undead within range make a Strike in the same relative cardinal direction. This Strike doesn't count toward the undead creatures' multiple attack penalty, and their multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike.
  • Weaken (auditory, necromancy): Mindless undead within range suffer a -1 status penalty to attack and damage rolls for 1 round.

Critical Failure The player alone experiences the effects of a discordant blast.

details.description: Phantasmal diners begin hurling phantom food and very real, sharp cutlery in a dangerous food fight that targets intruders.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:25] (trained) to threaten the ghostly guests into submission or @Check[religion|dc:28] (expert) to exorcise the haunt; two successes are required to disable it.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates at the start of any round in which Ilza is the only other creature present, then immediately resets.
details.routine: (1 action) On the haunt's initiative, the phantoms hurl ghostly food and real silverware through the room. All creatures other than Ilza who are in the area take @Damage[(2d8+9)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|options:area-effect] save) from the sharp forks and knives being thrown about.

items.name: Any Excuse for a Food Fight

description.value:
Trigger Ilza attacks the PCs

Effect Upon seeing Ilza's anger, the guests rise up in a flurry of activity, their voices suddenly loud and startling. Every PC in area D6 must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:24|options:area-effect,inflicts:off-guard] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] for the first round of combat. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: A 10-foot-tall wooden pole topped by a wooden carving of Dahak's head. Each dragon pillar's head has been treated with something to give it a distinct color, as indicated in the specific encounter, but regardless of color, plumes of smoke waft up from the mouth.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:26|name:Push the Pillar Over|traits:complex,magical,trap,hazard] (expert) to push the pillar over, or @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Erase Magic Runes|traits:complex,magical,trap,hazard] (expert) on the pillar to erase the magic runes that power it, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 22) to dispel the pillar's magic.
details.reset: The dragon pillar deactivates and resets as soon as it perceives no appropriate targets in range.
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the dragon pillar fires an eye beam at the closest target within 120 feet.

description.value: A dragon pillar can see through its empty eye sockets, and it can recognize Cinderclaws and their allies. A character disguised as a Cinderclaw can trick a dragon pillar into perceiving it as a Cinderclaw ally by using Deception to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Impersonate]. A dragon pillar always uses Perception for initiative.
Perception [[/br 1d20+16 #Perception]]{+16}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.Darkvision] 60 feet

While a target is controlled, it is treated as a Cinderclaw ally by the dragon pillar. The dragon pillar can control up to 3 targets at a time; any targets in excess who critically fail this saving throw are instead confused for [[/gmr 1d4+1 #Confused Rounds]]{1d4+1} rounds. A controlled creature can attempt a new Will save once every 24 hours to escape control, but doing so automatically causes the dragon pillar to attack it with a new eye beam to attempt to re-establish control. The control ends if the pillar is destroyed.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

details.routine: (6 actions) On each of the trap's actions, a crystal sphere that hasn't yet taken an action during the turn either flies 25 feet toward the closest creature in the Outer Ring (if no creature is within 25 feet), or it attacks a visible creature within 25 feet (the crystal spheres can sense targets via darkvision). If a crystal sphere attacks a target, that target takes @Damage[1d6[force]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic]) as the sphere hits them and bursts. A creature can be attacked by multiple spheres in a turn, but a sphere always targets the closest target. Once a sphere attacks and bursts, it vanishes but respawns at its spawn point at the start of the next turn and continues its routine. A sphere that has been destroyed or had its spawn point disabled vanishes and doesn't continue to respawn and attack, and the trap loses 1 action for each sphere destroyed or disabled in this way.

description.value:
Trigger Three or more creatures remain in the Outer Ring for 6 seconds or longer;

Effect Six spheres of crystal, each measuring 2 feet in diameter, manifest from thin air and begin quickly floating toward intruders. A sphere manifests at each of the points on the map indicated with a small numeral (labeled 1 through 6); the trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A tripline under the arch causes the keep's portcullis to slam down, sealing the entrance and alerting the denizens of the keep to the intruder's presence.

Any creature passing under the portcullis or standing below it must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:27] save. Once it falls, the portcullis can be lifted with a @Check[athletics|dc:30|traits:action:force-open] check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open]{Force it Open}, or via the winch.

Failure As critical success, but the creature takes a solid blow from the falling portcullis and takes @Damage[(4d8+18)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Failure The portcullis lands squarely on the creature, inflicting @Damage[(6d8+18)[piercing]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized]{Immobilizing} them. A Medium or smaller creature is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Success The hex bounces back to affect the gremlin horde. For 1 round, all creatures gain a +1 status bonus to checks to disable this hazard.

Success The item is unaffected.

Failure The item becomes unreliable for 1 round. If a creature attempts to Activate the item or Interact with it, it must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] or the action is wasted. If the item doesn't have an active use (such a mundane cloak), the creature wearing the item must instead immediately succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] or become tangled with the item and fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature wielding or wearing the item also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2}.

When the haunt rolls a critical hit to Strike a creature, the creature is instantly shunted to an empty space within 30 feet. The haunt chooses which space the creature is transported to.

description.value:
Trigger A non-demon creature comes within 15 feet of the hostile architecture

Critical Success The target is immune to the trap for 1 minute.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

details.description: Iron Taviah's cottage comes to life and tries to eat everyone in it. The hazard is divided into three areas: the basement becomes the cottage's stomach; the stairs act as the cottage's throat, becoming a steep, slippery incline that is greater difficult terrain; and the ground floor is the inside of the cottage's mouth. Creatures within the cottage can move around within it but can't exit without pulling the mouth open or slipping out while its teeth gnash. Characters who successfully escape the cottage are safe from the hazard.
details.disable: The hungry cottage can't be damaged or disabled, but the danger in each area can be temporarily mitigated. In the stomach: @Check[athletics|dc:17|traits:action:climb] (trained) to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Climb] atop the hoarded belongings and avoid contact with the acidic walls or floor for 1 round, or @Check[crafting|dc:25] (expert) as a 2-action activity to concoct an alchemical antacid that neutralizes acid damage dealt by the stomach for [[/gmr 1d4 #Rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. In the throat: @Check[athletics|dc:23] (trained) to hold the throat open for one round, allowing all characters in the stairs to use a Reflex save result one degree of success greater than their roll to avoid being pushed into the stomach, or @Check[nature|dc:23] or Survival as a 2-action activity to ease the throat's contractions and provide the same effect for [[/gmr 1d4 #Rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. In the mouth: @Check[athletics|dc:22|traits:action:force-open] to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open] a window or a door and forcibly expel one creature that's within 10 feet of an exit from the cottage, or @Check[athletics|dc:25] (expert) as a 2-action activity to jam furniture in place that prevents the teeth from gnashing for [[/gmr 1d4 #Rounds]]{1d4 rounds} and allows anyone in the cottage to move from it during that time.
details.reset: Once no creatures remain in the cottage, the hazard resets and returns to a normal cottage. The stairs and the basement are gone, however, as is Iron Taviah's body.
details.routine: The cursed cottage uses 3 actions: Acidic Assault in the stomach, Swallowing Staircase in the throat, and Gnashing Portal in the mouth (in that order). Checks to disable the areas might prevent these actions for a brief time.

description.value:
Trigger Iron Taviah is defeated in the cottage basement

Effect The basement floor absorbs Iron Taviah, and the cottage transforms into a hungry, semi-animate creature. The cottage rolls initiative.
The walls and floor of the stomach steam with digestive juices; all creatures in contact with these surfaces take @Damage[(2d8+4)[acid]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:24|basic]).

Creatures on the cottage's main floor are ground down as massive teeth appear from the floor and ceiling, gnash, and recede into the architecture, dealing @Damage[(2d8+4)[bludgeoning]] damage. Creatures in this area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:24] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and, if within 10 feet of an exit on the ground floor, can choose to exit the cottage.

Success The creature takes half damage and, if within 10 feet of an exit on the ground floor, can attempt to escape the cottage as the teeth retract by attempting a @Check[reflex|dc:24] save. On a success, the creature exits the cottage; on a critical failure, the creature falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Failure The creature takes full damage and is knocked prone.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is knocked prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.

Muscular swallowing contractions force creatures down the stairs and into the stomach. Creatures on the stairs must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:24] save or be pushed 15 feet down the stairs (and across the basement floor with any excess forced movement if they're pushed to the bottom of the stairs). Creatures who critically fail this Reflex save fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] at the end of this forced movement.

details.description: The sound of toppling trees and thunderous footsteps fills the air, as if some immense giant had taken notice and now slowly approaches through the forest.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:23] or @Check[religion|dc:23] to offer prayers against the evil to reduce Kugaptee's Anger's actions during its routine to 1 (see below) for 1 round; placing Sui-Jing's heart into her corpse at area A2 is the only way to disable this hazard

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.routine:
(2 actions) Darkness, fog and the sound of something immense striding just out of sight through the forest fills the air. The haunt's first action is to unleash a Terrifying Roar (@Check[will|dc:22] with the same effects as Terrifying Roar above). The second action is to infuse the trees of the entire mindscape with malevolent life, causing clawlike branches to lash out at each PC. These claws inflict @Damage[4d6[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save) to each character. A character standing on the path or in a clearing gets a +1 circumstance bonus to this saving throw, and the character who carries Sui-Jing's heart gains a +4 item bonus to this saving throw. On a PC's turn, they can take further action to protect themselves from the clawed branches. By Raising a Shield or by Dropping Prone, a PC gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their Reflex saving throw. A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] PC can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Take Cover] to gain the benefits of greater cover against the claws (increasing their circumstance bonus to this Reflex save to +4). A PC who Tumbles Through on their turn and succeeds at a @Check[acrobatics|dc:22] check gains a +2 status bonus to their Reflex saves against the clawed branches. A PC who succeeds at a @Check[stealth|dc:22] check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Sneak] improves the result of their Reflex save by one degree of success.

If a PC successfully used Nature or Religion to pray against the evil on the previous round, Kugaptee's Anger has only 1 action; randomly determine if it uses its Terrifying Roar or lashes out with trees with this action.

description.value:
Trigger A PC picks up Sui-Jing's heart

Effect A thunderous roar ripples through the forest. All of the PCs must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The character is unaffected and is temporally immune to Terrifying Roar for 24 hours.

Success The character is unaffected.

Failure The character is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The character's frightened condition increases by 2. If this would increase the character's frightened condition value above 4, the character is instead @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] until the end of their next turn.

details.description: The branches of the stone tree statue animate, extend, and lash out at intruders.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:25] to exorcise the haunt, or @Check[intimidation|dc:28] to appear overwhelmingly strong of body and mind so that the haunt recoils and fades

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: 1 hour
details.routine: (3 actions) Kugaptee's tree lashes out with its branches, using its actions to make three branch Strikes. Targets that are within reach but are outside of area E3 have cover from the surrounding archways. The haunt favors targets in area E3 but otherwise selects its targets randomly.

description.value:
Trigger An intruder begins their turn in area E3

Effect The stone statue animates, its branches whipping through the air as a horrifying roar rips through the monastery—a roar that seems to come from underground. All intruders within the monastery must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is frightened 2 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for as long as it remains frightened.

details.description: The chasm below shifts and ripples, transforming into a vast, gaping mouth filled with countless sharp fangs. A puckered, rasping tongue lances up from the noxious gulping depths below to lash and taste. Two words boom up from the vast throat below: "Iö Tchekuth!"
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:28|name:Exorcise Phantasm] or @Check[religion|dc:28|name:Exorcise Phantasm] (to exorcise the startling phantasm)
details.reset: The haunt resets after 24 hours and is destroyed permanently if Ioseff's ghost is put to rest.

description.value:
Trigger a creature crosses the chasm

Effect A vast, fanged maw appears to open below, and a long rasping tongue lurches up to attack the triggering creature, who must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save.

Critical Success The creature realizes the haunt is a frightening phantasm that can't actually hurt them and is unharmed.

Success The creature dodges the rasping tongue but falls from the bridge as a result, taking 10 damage from the fall (the creature can Grab an Edge with a successful @Check[reflex|dc:24|name:Grab an Edge|traits:damaging-effect] save).

Failure For a brief moment, the creature feels the awful touch of the rasping tongue as it brushes against them, licking away memories and causing the creature to become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours. The creature then falls (as success above).

Critical Failure The creature feels the tongue wrap around them and then experiences the awfulness of being pulled downward for what seems like days, but in fact, only an instant passes. The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 24 hours, during which time they can't remember their name. The creature also increases the value of their malevolence condition by 1 and then falls (as success above).

details.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 5-foot radius surrounds an invisible wall or floor rune the size of the creature to be summoned.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:23] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:25] (expert) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-11: No Time for Treason

details.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors.

Effect This trap summons a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pfs-season-3-bestiary.Actor.Living Landslide (PFS 3-11)] with the elite adjustment. The living landside rolls initiative and remains for 7 rounds, after which the spell ends and the living landside disappears. The living landside also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. The summoned living landside can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: Linneus's grudge manifests as a haunt that isolates creatures in the bathroom and attempts to psychically drown them in one of three containers of water: the sink, the bath, or the wash bucket.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:25|name:Excorise the Spirit] (expert) to exorcise the spirit from one of the containers of water, or @Check[athletics|dc:26|name:Overpower the Ghostly Force] or @Check[thievery|dc:26] (trained) to overpower the ghostly force sealing a container and drain it. All three containers must be disabled to disable the entire haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-13: Devil at the Crossroads

details.reset: The haunt deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine:
(one action) One creature within the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save as the haunt attempts to drown them.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure A ghostly version of the creature's face appears in the water of the sink, the bath, or the wash bucket. The creature immediately experiences the sensation of being underwater, and if it can't breathe water, it must begin to hold its breath to avoid suffocation. The haunt can affect only one creature per container remaining in the haunt. If the creature leaves the room, it's immediately freed.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature immediately loses [[/r 1d4 #Actions]]{1d4} actions' worth of air.

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door to the haunted chamber

Effect The manifestation slams the door shut. The force deals @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage to any creature in or adjacent to the door's space and pushes them into the room. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:25] save takes no damage and rolls out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, it can choose the direction. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: This mushroom ring forms a portal to the First World, allowing the Thorned Monarch's reinforcements into the Material Plane. When the PCs approach, a creature emerges. Creatures continue to emerge until the mushroom ring is disabled. Disabling the mushroom ring also causes any creatures that have emerged to disappear.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) or @Check[nature|dc:25] (trained) to rearrange the mushrooms to disrupt the portal (two actions) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank, counteract DC 23) to counteract the magical properties; three successes are required to disable the portal

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-00: The King in Thorns

details.routine: (1 action) A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.Korred] emerges from the mushroom ring and defends it. All korreds use the mushroom ring's initiative.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs approach the mushroom ring

Effect A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.Korred] emerges from the portal to defend it, and the hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: This unassuming fungus leeches heat out of the air.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:25|name:Remove Brown Mold] (trained) to safely remove the mold

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-98: Expedition Into Pallid Peril

details.reset: After expanding, the brown mold can't grow again for 1 day.

description.value: 10 feet. Enhanced brown mold deals @Damage[(2d8+7)[cold]] damage to nearby creatures.

Trigger Fire comes within 5 feet of the brown mold.

Effect The brown mold expands into every square adjacent to its space. As it grows, it pulls more heat from its surroundings, dealing @Damage[(4d8+14)[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:24|basic] save) to creatures within 15 feet after it expands.

stealth.details: @Check[perception|dc:22|traits:action:seek] to detect the subtle magical aura of the land

details.description: What seems like an innocent stretch of land assaults trespassers with terrible illusions.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:22], @Check[nature|dc:22], @Check[occultism|dc:22], or @Check[religion|dc:22] (by a hero with expert proficiency in that skill) three times to temporarily disrupt the magic, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank, DC 25)
details.reset: The terrible visions cease after all creatures have left the affected area, but the hazard is immediately ready to inflict its visions on the next creature to enter.
details.routine:
(1 action) Terrible visions assail the minds of all creatures who can see within the affected area. Each creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] saving throw.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+9)[mental]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d8+18)[mental]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 4}.

description.value: If a creature that can see moves 10 feet into the affected area, the hazard rolls Stealth (+16) for its initiative.

stealth.details: (trained) (or –10 and no minimum proficiency if the oozeshardling node's electric pulse reaction has been triggered)

details.description: A lump of bluish ooze shot through with what look like tiny silver shavings of metal. Once it has been activated, it glows with arcs of electricity that leap out at nearby targets.
details.disable: Two successful @Check[arcana|dc:21] or @Check[occultism|dc:21] (trained) checks on the oozeshardling to disperse its power, or @Check[acrobatics|dc:23] or @Check[thievery|dc:23] (expert) to remove the flecks of empowering warshard from within the oozeshardling. A failed Acrobatics or Thievery check to disable an oozeshardling results in the creature attempting the check taking @Damage[1d6[acid]] damage (@Damage[2d6[acid]] on a critical failure).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-07: A God Falls Where Magic Fails

details.reset: The oozeshardling node collapses and melds back into the oozeshard after 1 minute, or disappears entirely if the oozeshard is slain.
details.routine: (3 actions) The oozeshardling uses its first action to make an electric spark Strike against one random non-ooze creature within range, its second action is used to move straight in a random cardinal direction (roll [[/gmr 1d4 #Direction]] to determine the direction), and its third action to make a second electric spark Strike against a different random non-ooze creature within range. The oozeshardling doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters a space within 10 feet of the oozeshardling or the oozeshardling is created by the oozeshard's Spawn Oozeshardling Node ability

Effect The oozeshardling unleashes a shocking blast that deals @Damage[3d6[electricity]] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] around it (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save).

details.description: A book unfurls and its case forms into a leathery locust, its pages folding into an overwhelming swarm.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] to tear up the leather locust, or @Check[arcana|dc:21] (trained) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3nd rank; counteract DC 25) to render the magic inert.
details.reset: The paper locust tornado resets after 1 minute if there are no creatures to target.
details.routine:
(2 actions) The tornado uses each action to move up to 30 feet and share a creature's space, slicing into them with their paper wings and spurs. The targeted creatures must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:24] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+9)[slashing]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d8[slashing]] damage and @Damage[1d10[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1}.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the bookshelf

Effect The enchanted book transforms into a tornado of origami locusts and one leather locust. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Cloying violet mist billows from the undergrowth, filling the heads of any creatures inside with maddening visions of dark moonless nights, twisted malignant trees, and steaming rivers of blood.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:28] (expert) to find a path through the Grand Hall that doesn't disturb the undergrowth

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves through the undergrowth

Effect One round later, the hall fills with violet fog that imposes a -2 circumstance penalty to Perception checks to all creatures within it and exposes anyone who enters it to phantasmagoric fog.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Phantasmagoric Fog]

Saving Throw @Check[will|dc:24]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] and stupefied 1 (1 round)

Stage 3 confused and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} (1 round).

details.description: The echo of diabolic bells reverberates with a bone-shaking clamor.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:27|name:Recite Against the Power of Hell|traits:auditory,complex,haunt,sonic,hazard] (expert) to break the curse with recitations against the power of Hell (2 actions), or @Check[performance|dc:22|name:Perform an Opposing Composition|traits:auditory,complex,haunt,sonic,hazard] (expert) to Perform an opposing composition
details.reset: The bells cease ringing if there are no living creatures within 30 feet.
details.routine: (1 action) The bells toll, dealing @Damage[4d6[sonic]] damage to each living creature within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} (@Check[fortitude|dc:24|basic|name:Phantom Bells Toll|traits:auditory,complex,haunt,sonic,hazard] save).

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 30 feet.

Effect Sounds of creaking metal fill the area as the phantom bells begin to swing and the haunt rolls initiative.

stealth.details: or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Detect Magic] to notice the heavy divine magic in the area

details.description: Three daeodon corpses, animated and enraged by Sezelrian's divine intervention, trample anything within the ritual circle.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:21] or @Check[religion|dc:21] to disrupt the magic animating the spirits, @Check[athletics|dc:25] to hold down one of the daeodons, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd-rank, counteract DC 25). 3 successes are required.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-15: Cleansing the Flame

details.reset: The hazard resets within 24 hours if the cultists of Sezelrian still remain in the area.
details.routine: (three actions) The animated daeodons trample all creatures within the circle, dealing @Damage[(2d8+9)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area of the ritual circle

Effect The outline of the circle alights with fire dealing @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage to all creatures within it (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A hidden string connected to precariously stacked bricks causes the cubby to collapse in on itself when the string is pulled.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Cut String|traits:action:disable-a-device] to cut the string without setting off the trap

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the sickle in the cubby

Effect The heavy bricks above the cubby collapse, leaving the sickle unscathed but dealing @Damage[(4d6+10)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save) to the triggering creature.

details.description: A 10-foot-long, 5-foot-thick column of stone thuds to the ground, just in front of the bridge. It immediately starts to roll down the bridge, quickly picking up speed.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] check to create a wedge to immobilize the column, @Check[athletics|dc:21] check to immobilize the column until the end of the creature's next turn, a creature can Climb over an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] column with a successful @Check[athletics|dc:18] check.

publication.title: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm

details.routine: (1 action) The rolling stone cylinder trap uses Crushing Roll.

description.value:
A lever at the top of the south tower is pulled

Effect A thick column of stone drops from above the gate, then uses crushing roll. The rolling stone cylinder trap rolls initiative.

Requirements The rolling stone cylinder is not @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized]

Effect The rolling stone cylinder trap moves its Speed west along the bridge. Creatures it moves past or lands on take @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]] damage and are forced to move west to the first available space (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic]). Creatures who critically succeed at the Reflex save are not damaged but are still moved west. If the rolling stone tries to move into a non-creature obstruction, such as a wall or barricade, it deals bludgeoning damage to the obstruction and only continues moving if the obstruction is destroyed, otherwise the trap stops.

stealth.details: (expert), or 0 if the grate is open

details.description: The rusty grate covers a pit that's 10 feet in diameter and 40 feet deep.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Harmlessly Trigger Trap] to harmlessly trigger the trap by nudging the gate or the dangling winch, or @Check[thievery|dc:24|name:Stabilize Grate] to stabilize the grate so it doesn't collapse.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the grate

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes falling damage (@Damage[20[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage). That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

Whether or not the creature Grabs an Edge, the rusty winch above crashes down atop the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[3d10[bludgeoning]] damage and dislodging its grip (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save). The creature can still try to Grab an Edge again.

details.description: Arcs of primal energy emerge from the L-shaped stone wall at the center of each alcove.
details.disable:
@Check[crafting|dc:28] or @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) to rig up a dummy or other object to distract one of the trap's sensors, or @Check[nature|dc:26] (trained) to temporarily befuddle one of the sensors with knowledge of primal magic.

Alternatively, inserting the correct key into an alcove's keyhole disables that alcove's sensor.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-16: The Perennial Crown Part 1, Opal of Bhopan

details.reset: The trap is powered by the Eternal Bloom. It resets after 5 minutes, at which time the doors to the vault slam shut once again.
details.routine:
(4 actions) The trap loses one action per magical sensor disabled. On its initiative, the trap uses each of its actions to target one creature adjacent to or within an alcove with a negative effect that corresponds to the season opposite the one depicted on the alcove's murals (@Check[fortitude|dc:27] to resist). For the purposes of this trap, creatures who are within an alcove's landscape count are within that alcove. The trap can target only one creature per alcove per round.

Spring Alcove (death) The decay of late autumn takes hold of the target.

  • Critical Success The trap has no effect.
  • Success The target takes @Damage[(2d6+2)[void]] damage.
  • Failure The target takes @Damage[(4d6+4)[void]] damage.
  • Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[(4d6+4)[void]] damage and @Damage[1d6[persistent,void]] damage. Effects that use vitality energy, such as the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Heal] spell, automatically end this persistent damage.

Summer Alcove (cold) Winter's bitter chill descends on the target.

  • Critical Success The trap has no effect.
  • Success The target takes @Damage[(2d6+2)[cold]] damage.
  • Failure The target takes @Damage[(4d6+4)[cold]] damage.
  • Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[(4d6+4)[cold]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 hour.

Autumn Alcove (poison) Poisonous springtime pollen sprays at the target.

  • Critical Success The trap has no effect.
  • Success The target takes @Damage[(2d6+2)[poison]] damage.
  • Failure The target takes @Damage[(4d6+4)[poison]] damage.
  • Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[(4d6+4)[poison]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

Winter Alcove (fire) Heat greater than the hottest summer afternoon engulfs the target.

  • Critical Success The trap has no effect.
  • Success The target takes @Damage[(2d6+2)[fire]] damage.
  • Failure The target takes @Damage[(4d6+4)[fire]] damage.
  • Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[(4d6+4)[fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters one of the four alcoves

Effect The trap rolls initiative.

details.description: Exposed clockwork gears spin at high speeds.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Disable the engine|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to disable the engine, or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:26|name:Disengage the drive shaft] to disengage the drive shaft. Either method disables the trap but also shuts down the engine, disabling the Second Kiss's propulsion.

description.value:
Trigger A creature Strides in the engine nacelle

Effect The creature's clothing or limbs get caught in the spinning gears and axles of the engine. The creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:24] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+9)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d8+18)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature's clothing is pulled into the clockworks. The creature takes @Damage[(4d8+18)[bludgeoning]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained]. Until the creature [[/act escape dc=24]]{Escapes}, it takes @Damage[(2d8+9)[bludgeoning]] damage each round as it's pulled into the spinning rotors. A creature wearing clothing can automatically Escape by doffing its caught clothing; this destroys the doffed clothing.

details.description: An invisible rune creates knives of force if someone tries to bypass the lock.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) to silently erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract the rune

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts a Thievery check to Pick the Lock

Effect Knives of force deal @Damage[(4d8+18)[slashing]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). On a failure, the creature also takes a –1 penalty to Dex-based skills for 1 hour.

details.description: Necromantic runes inscribed on the cage bars and lock drain the vitality of living creatures who interact with the cage.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25|name:Drain the rune's power|traits:hazard,magical,necromancy,void,trap] (expert) to drain the runes' power harmlessly or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract the rune

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the cage bars, door, or lock directly or with a tool

Effect The trap deals @Damage[(4d8+18)[void]] damage to the triggering creature, creatures inside the cage, and all creatures adjacent to the cage (@Check[fortitude|dc:27|traits:hazard,trap|basic]). On a failed save, a creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

details.description: Daffodils spit acidic nectar at non-plant creatures in the area.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (expert) or @Check[survival|dc:25] (trained) to remove the plant's motion sensing filaments
details.reset: The daffodils replenish their nectar and filaments after 24 hours.

description.value:
Trigger A non-plant creature comes within 10 feet

Effect The daffodils spit nectar at all non-plant creatures within 10 feet, dealing @Damage[(4d8+18)[acid]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] save).

details.description: A ghostly host of god callers and their eidolons manifests to defend the barrow against intruders, and the strains of a battle song fill the air.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:25] to appear to be a defender of Storasta, @Check[diplomacy|dc:25] to demonstrate benign intentions, or @Check[occultism|dc:22] or @Check[religion|dc:22] to ward off the malevolent spiritual energies. Two successful checks are required to disable the haunt, and each attempt requires two actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-15: A Dirge for Sarkoris

details.reset: The haunt dissipates 1 minute after all living creatures leave area C1. Its spiritual energies re-form after 24 hours.
details.routine: (1 action) (illusion, mental, occult) The haunt attacks what it believes is a demonic incursion, dealing @Damage[(1d8+9)[mental]] damage and @Damage[1d8[good]] damage to each living creature in the area (@Check[will|dc:24|basic|name:Storasta's Last Stand] save).

description.value:
Trigger One or more living creatures attempts to move past the entryway further into the barrow

Effect Each creature in the area feels their own form warping into that of a hideous demon and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected by the onslaught of illusions.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} and believes that it is infused with demonic energy, making it susceptible to holy effects (even if it is not unholy) for as long as it remains within the haunt's area.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature also gains weakness 5 to cold iron for the next 24 hours.

details.description: Planar winds summoned from beyond sweep through the room, song and sound carried adrift on their current. This air elemental is trapped on the Material Plane and can't escape.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:24|name:Duplicate the Melody|traits:environmental,magical] (expert) twice to duplicate the melody and appease the elemental, or @Check[arcana|dc:27|name:Guide the Elemental|traits:environmental,magical] (expert) and then @Check[diplomacy|dc:24|name:Guide the Elemental|traits:environmental,magical] (trained) to guide the elemental through a planar tear elsewhere in the manor. Each attempt to Disable the hazard requires two actions, and each successful check to Disable removes one action from the hazard's routine.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-16: Freedom for Wishes

details.reset: The melody remains for 1 minute, after which it disperses into several holes in the walls. It returns after an hour unless disabled.
details.routine: (2 actions) For each action in the routine, the melody makes a solid refrain Strike against one creature in its area. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters area B3

Effect Winds surge into the room. The hazard rolls initiative.

When a creature fails a Performance check to Disable the melody, the elemental plays along before taking over the performance. Creatures in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save to resist becoming fascinated by the melody.

Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is then temporarily immune to Mesmerising Duet for 1 hour.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for 1 round; this fascination does not end when the creature or its allies take damage from the hazard.

Critical Failure The creature is fascinated for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}; this fascination does not end when the creature or its allies take damage from the hazard.

details.description: Discordant music, dance, and poetry combine to build a deep suspicion and distrust of strangers.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:26] to counteract the performance or @Check[religion|dc:27] or @Check[occultism|dc:27] to disrupt the spiritual energy. Three total successes are needed to disable the trap.
details.reset: A new production of The Enmity Cycle must be arranged; this process usually takes at least 1 week.
details.routine:
(1 action) The performance continues to sow seeds of enmity, discord, and distrust on those in its area. All creatures in attendance other than Zridi must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and is immune to the effects for 1 day.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} for 2 rounds.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} for 1 minute and is certain the source of this fear is their fellow citizens, especially those from places other than their hometown. For 1 week the target suffers a –2 penalty to all Diplomacy skill checks against people from other Thuvian cities and regains the frightened 2 condition whenever they are in one of those cities.

Critical Failure As failure, but the target becomes a carrier for The Enmity Cycle for the next week. The target is compelled to travel to a city other than where they were exposed and recite as many lines from the anbarit as they can recall. Those who hear or witness the performance are treated as if they had attended a performance of The Enmity Cycle, but with a @Check[will|dc:23] save

description.value:
Trigger Zridi spends an action to activate The Enmity Cycle while in area F2

Effect All creatures other than Zridi in F2 take @Damage[(2d8+9)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:25|basic] save). The trap then rolls initiative.

hp.details: BT 29 to smash a 5-ft-sq hole in the wall; Spout H 13; Spout HP 20 (BT 10)

details.description: Magically hardened sheets of obsidian drop down over the room's exits while the machinery within taps into wells of magma to begin spewing lava-and monsters-into the maze.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) to disable the main pressure plate at area H4a and deactivate the trap entirely (this causes the obsidian walls to retract up into the ceiling as well), @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) to disable one of the three lava spouts, or Athletics DC 28 (trained) to [[/act force-open dc=28]] a fallen obsidian wall

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

details.reset: The Last Crucible deactivates and resets 10 minutes after it has no target creatures (because the creatures either left area H4 or died). Any thoqquas that still live sense the magma river at area H6 and eventually make their way north to that area to swim away.
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap loses 1 action per disabled spout each turn. On each action, a different spout located at one of the three areas marked H4c spews a gout of lava at the closest non-thoqqua target in its line of sight; these spouts use magical sensors that have darkvision and a Perception DC of 25. Originally, agents of the abbot would come through here after the trap was triggered, deactivate it, and clean out the hardened blobs of lava and dead bodies that remained, but the amount of time it would take for this trap to clog tunnels with cooled lava makes tracking that slow accretion over the course of this adventure unnecessary.

description.value:
Trigger Four or more Small or larger creatures walk onto the pressure plate at area H4a;

Effect A magically hardened sheet of obsidian drops down like a portcullis in the western and northern entrances to this room that lead to areas H3 and H5, respectively. Any creatures standing in one of these squares takes @Damage[(4d6 + 4)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage from the falling wall (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|options:area-effect]) and then decides what side of the wall they want to be on. At the same moment, four thoqquas that have spent centuries in hibernation awaken and emerge from vents into area H4b. The trap then rolls initiative, as do the thoqquas.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: Fashionable performers and revolutionaries rappel from the surrounding buildings on scarves.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:21] to turn away from the explosions, or @Check[crafting|dc:25] to identify the exact moment the fireworks will explode
details.routine: (1 action; visual) The Thin Wisps continue to launch fireworks until the dottari excruciator divisions are defeated. Each round, all creatures must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:24|options:area-effect,inflicts:dazzled,inflicts:deafened,inflicts:blinded|traits:visual] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 round (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] and deafened for 1 round on a critical failure).

description.value:
Trigger A non-dottari creature takes damage

Effect Thin Wisps fire off a series of fireworks as they rappel from the eaves on scarves. All creatures in area A must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save. On a failure, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] until the end of its next turn (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] and deafened on a critical failure). The hazard then rolls initiative.

hp.details: Furnace BT 28, Vent Hardness 10, Vent HP 18 (BT 9)

details.description: The smoking furnace begins to hiss and click, and its vents swivel and adjust to spew toxic fumes in the direction of intruders.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) to render one of the vents useless, or @Check[athletics|dc:25] to wrench and bend a vent until it no longer functions

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Crown of the Kobold King

details.reset: The trap resets after 1 minute, during which it sees no creatures to target.
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap loses 1 action for each disabled vent. On each of the trap's actions, a vent spews a gout of boiling toxin at a living creature in sight of it. It can't target a single creature more than once per round, so if there aren't enough viable targets for all 3 actions, the trap loses those unspent actions.

description.value:
Trigger A visible living creature enters the room, or a Stealthy creature fails to remain @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] from the toxic furnace with a @Check[stealth|dc:25] check (the trap's sensors have darkvision and lifesense);

Effect One of the toxic furnace's vents swivels to aim at the triggering creature and then spews a gout of boiling toxin at the target. The toxic furnace then rolls initiative.

details.description: An unspeakable miasma of foul-smelling vapor with shrieking faces barely visible in the steam bubbles up from the soup.
details.disable: @Check[cooking-lore|dc:19] to neutralize the haunted soup with the addition of a few select spices from nearby shelves, or @Check[religion|dc:25] to exorcise the haunt

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: The haunt resets 1 round after the lid is replaced on the cooking pot.

description.value:
Trigger A non-nindoru creature takes the lid off the cooking pot

Effect All creatures within 15 feet of the pot must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:27] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}. The triggering creature instead must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save to resist the compulsion to dip their face into the stew and swallow a mouthful of the stuff (this effect has the emotion and mental traits). On a failure, the triggering creature takes @Damage[5d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:24|basic] save).

details.description: Abyssal fire rains from the sky.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:28], @Check[nature|dc:28], @Check[occultism|dc:28], or @Check[religion|dc:28] (trained) twice or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 22) to cause a temporary, local disruption to the Disciple of Urxehl's ritual. Checks to disrupt the firestorm require two actions. Defeating the Disciple of Urxehl also disables the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-22: Breaking the Storm: Excising Ruination

details.reset: The hazard activates when the PCs reach Event: Rain of Fire, area A1, and area A9. If the PCs defeat the Disciple of Urxehl, the hazard is permanently destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A creature who doesn't worship Urxehl walks into the hazard's area

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as bolts of profane fire begin to target the intruders.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: Abyssal fire rains from the sky.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:31], @Check[nature|dc:31], @Check[occultism|dc:31], or @Check[religion|dc:31] (trained) twice or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 24) to cause a temporary, local disruption to the Disciple of Urxehl's ritual. Checks to disrupt the firestorm require two actions. Defeating the Disciple of Urxehl also disables the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-22: Breaking the Storm: Excising Ruination

details.reset: The hazard activates when the PCs reach Event: Rain of Fire, area A1, and area A9. If the PCs defeat the Disciple of Urxehl, the hazard is permanently destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A creature who doesn't worship Urxehl walks into the hazard's area

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as bolts of profane fire begin to target the intruders.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (trained) Characters automatically detect this hazard when they open the door to the Grand Hall.

details.description: Concerned citizens shout slogans about the dangers of dealing with Cheliax and throw bricks at Chelaxian sympathizers.
details.disable: Succeed at two of the following checks in any combination: @Check[athletics|dc:25] (trained) to confiscate dangerous debris, @Check[diplomacy|dc:25] (trained) to calm the crowd, @Check[intimidation|dc:23] (untrained) to scare the crowd away, @Check[will|dc:28] (trained) to maintain a brave and confident demeanor; an effect such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Calm] that can target an area counts as one automatic success.
details.routine: (three actions) The unruly crowd uses an action to chant a Catchy Slogan if it is not already benefiting from the effect It uses Shake Confidence once per turn and uses its remaining actions for cobblestone Strikes.

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door to the Grand Hall

Effect The unruly crowd rolls Initiative.

All the hazards in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Catchy Slogan]

The unruly crowd targets a creature within 30 feet The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and immune to Shake Confidence for 24 hours

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}

Critical Failure The creature becomes frightened 2 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1}.

details.description: Tumor-like growths erupt from the isle as it responds to psychic attack, spewing black lightning and fire in every direction.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:26|name:Reshape the Dream] (trained) to reshape the substance of the dream, or @Check[performance|dc:30|name:Invoke Desna's Blessing] (trained) or @Check[religion|dc:28|name:Invoke Desna's Blessing] (trained) to invoke Desna's blessing through hymns or prayers. Each check to disable the hazard is a two-action activity; two total successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-17: Dreams of a Dustbound Isle

details.routine: (1 action) On the hazard's initiative, a boil erupts from the ground and bursts. This blast strikes as many enemies as possible in a @Template[burst|distance:15] anywhere on the isle, dealing @Damage[4d8[force]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] or @Check[will|dc:24|basic], target's choice) to enemies in the area. Aslynn avoids targeting the nightmares with the blast.

description.value:
Trigger Sarnia's psychic intrusion begins

Effect The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: The depictions of heroic deeds transform as the lights dim, becoming testaments to cowardice and failure.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:25] to push back against the haunt with your own memories of bravery or @Check[religion|dc:28] to exorcise the haunt; three successes are required to disable it.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates at the start of any round in which there are no living humanoids in the room, then resets 1 minute later.
details.routine:
(2 actions; emotion, fear, mental) On its initiative, the haunt's first action is to randomly select one humanoid in the room. All of the depictions of Eragayl in the artwork transform to depict that humanoid, who becomes the focus of the haunt. On its second action, the haunt's depictions animate, showing the targeted humanoid as they are slowly destroyed by what they fear most. All humanoids in the room must make a @Check[will|dc:24|options:inflicts:frightened,inflicts:paralyzed|traits:emotion,fear,mental] save, with the target taking a –2 status penalty to the save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d6+1)[mental]|traits:emotion,fear,mental] damage from the conviction that they'll be remembered as a coward and a failure.

Failure As success, but @Damage[(4d6+2)[mental]|traits:emotion,fear,mental] damage, and the creature increases its frightened condition value by 1 to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 4}.

Critical Failure As failure, but @Damage[(8d6+4)[mental]|traits:emotion,fear,mental] damage. If the creature is already frightened 4, they become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] with fear until the start of the haunt's next turn.

description.value:
Trigger A humanoid ends their turn in the room

Effect The room's built-in light sources lower to dim illumination (although any light sources carried by PCs are unaffected), and the depictions of the heroics in the dioramas and statues transform into depictions of fear and cowardice. All humanoids in the room must make a @Check[will|dc:24|options:inflicts:frightened] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (expert) to detect the magical sensor; noticing the wheel has a DC of 0

details.description: An ornate wheel set into a wall-divided into six segments with colored runes on each-is controlled by a magical sensor that detects any creature within 100 feet in front of it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Stop Wheel from Spinning|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) on the wheel to stop it from spinning, @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Erase Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to erase each rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract each rune
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any creature moving within range of its sensor.
details.routine:
(2 actions) On its initiative, the trap uses its first action to spin, then stops. Roll 1d6 to determine which segment is topmost when the wheel stops spinning. The wheel uses its second action to replicate the spell listed for that segment (3rd rank, DC 24, spell attack modifier +14). This spell's target is centered on or otherwise includes the nearest creature in the area. This increases the spell's range to 100 feet if necessary. Any spell cast by this trap is arcane.

1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Sleep]

2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Paralyze]

3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Lightning Bolt] (100-foot line)

4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Blindness]

5 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Caustic Blast]

6 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Enfeeble]

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the sensor's detection area.

Effect The wheel begins to spin and rolls initiative.

details.description: The paintings suddenly start to bleed-Ioseff Xarwin from the left wrist and the other three Xarwins in a line cutting across their brows-as the viewers receive glimpses of the traumas the family endured.
