details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:25|name:Exorcise Painting] to exorcise one of the paintings (a critical success exorcises all of the paintings at once) or @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Cover a Painting|traits:action:disable-a-device] to quickly cover a painting with a cloak or remove it and turn it to face the wall
details.reset: 1 hour; putting Xarwin's ghost to rest destroys this haunt forever.
details.routine:
(4 actions) The haunt uses up to 4 actions to afflict bleeding wounds on different creatures in area B22. These bloody wounds manifest where the target would have felt pain from Inflict Agony, regardless of whether they succeeded in resisting that effect or not. A creature can't be targeted more than once by this routine. Each creature affected must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24|traits:emotion,fear,mental,necromancy,occult] save.

The haunt loses 1 action for each painting that's disabled or destroyed.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(1d8+3)[slashing]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} by the conviction that these wounds were caused by a loved one.

Failure As success, but @Damage[(2d8+6)[slashing]] damage, @Damage[1d6[bleed]], and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure As failure, but @Damage[(4d8+12)[slashing]] damage, @Damage[2d6[bleed]], and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}. Increase the value of the character's malevolence condition by 1.

description.value:
Trigger At least four characters are in area B22, or someone touches or attempts to damage a painting

Effect All characters in area B22 must attempt a @Check[will|dc:27] save to resist sudden, agonizing pain (the PC with the highest Intelligence feels this pain in the left wrist, while the others feel it in their heads). On a failure, a PC becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} from the agony (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: Abendego's Squall leaps from the water. Its mouth opens to snap against any part of the ship that it can reach.
details.disable: @Check[medicine|dc:23] (expert) to remove the harpoon, @Check[nature|dc:25] (trained) to calm the orca, or @Check[intimidation|dc:27] (expert) to scare the orca away; two total successes are required to subdue the orca

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.routine:
d Abendego's Squall thrashes against the hull of the Snapdragon. With each action, it makes a flipper Strike against a PC adjacent to the railing. Whenever it makes a flipper Strike, it also deals its Strike damage to the ship whether or not the Strike was successful.

Speed 30 feet

description.value:
Trigger Abendego's Squall matches speed with the Snapdragon

Effect The orca leaps across the Snapdragon and tears its main mast from the deck in the process. This deals @Damage[30] damage to the Snapdragon. All creatures on deck take @Damage[(4d8+4)[bludgeoning]] damage from the shrapnel and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:25|showDC:gm|basic] save. Abendego's Squall rolls initiative.

details.description: High winds and forest fires intensified by Abyssal energy batters everything in the surge's path.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:27|name:Redirect the Storm Surge|traits:rare,fire,environmental,hazard,magical], @Check[nature|dc:27|name:Redirect the Storm Surge|traits:rare,fire,environmental,hazard,magical], @Check[occultism|dc:27|name:Redirect the Storm Surge|traits:rare,fire,environmental,hazard,magical], or @Check[religion|dc:27|name:Redirect the Storm Surge|traits:rare,fire,environmental,hazard,magical] (trained) to redirect the storm surge or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank, counteract DC 23) to nullify it.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-24: Breaking The Storm: Parting Clouds

description.value:
The storm of wind and fire suddenly surges over everyone in the area. When this hazard activates, roll [[/r 1d10]] to determine the effect and type of damage.

  • 1-2 Firestorm Embers and burning debris gets whipped around as the wind suddenly shifts direction, dealing @Damage[1d10[bludgeoning],(1d10+13)[fire]]{1d10 bludgeoning damage and 1d10+13 fire damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|name:Avoid Firestorm] save).
  • 3-4 Battering Debris Wreckage rains down throughout the area, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic|name:Avoid Battering Debris] save).
  • 5-6 Abyssal Energy The storm blows about debris that has absorbed Abyssal energies. Creatures who come into contact with this debris are exposed to Abyssal plague.
  • 7-8 Sharp Rain The storm picks up various objects, some of which are particularly sharp. The storm deals @Damage[1d10[bludgeoning],(1d10+13)[piercing]]{1d10 bludgeoning damage and 1d10+13 piercing damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|name:Avoid Sharp Rain] save).
  • 9-10 Soot Storm Large amounts of soot blow in on the wind. The soot moves with supernatural malice, seeking out the lungs of any creatures caught nearby. Any creature that needs to breathe must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:25|name:Avoid Soot Storm|traits:inhaled] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The sickened condition can't end or be reduced until a creature has spent 10 minutes breathing clean air.

Level 7

A creeping malaise overcomes the afflicted, whose skin develops a sickly pallor.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:25|name:Resist Abyssal Plague]

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 3} (1 day)

The target can't recover from the drained condition from Abyssal plague until the disease is cured.

details.description: A massive tangle of brambles weaves through a skeleton clad in armor.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:27] to delicately unthread the vines from the skeleton without triggering them; four successes are required to disable the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

description.value:
Trigger A person attempts to damage the vines or to Interact with the skeleton or its armor

Effect The thorny vines suddenly animate, and in so doing, make it appear that the skeleton is rising to attack. The vines lash out, and all creatures in area B2b or within 20 feet above take @Damage[8d6[slashing]] damage from the thorns (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic] save). A creature that fails the save also takes @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]] damage (@Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage on a critical failure).

details.description: A flock of animated paper birds flit about to the playful sounds of an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Invisible] flute, played by the invisible janni Mehryar
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:26] (trained) to create beautiful flying objects to complement the paper birds; @Check[acrobatics|dc:28] or @Check[performance|dc:25] (@Check[performance|dc:21] if using a flute) (trained) to perform along with the flute and paper birds (requires two successes); or one successful attack roll against the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Invisible] Mehryar (AC 25, @Check[perception|dc:25] to determine Mehryar's location)

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-14: Shattering Golden Chains

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if no hostile creatures remain outside.
details.routine: (3 actions) On each of the trap's actions, the flock of paper birds attacks a different creature. On a successful hit, each target must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:26] save. On a failure, they take @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the museum door without Fasiel's permission

Effect The trap rolls initiative as the paper birds attack. Wind and rain flurry through the area, applying a –2 circumstance penalty to ranged attacks and Perception checks, extinguishing non-magical fire, and impeding spells and abilities with the fire trait. Casters must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:6] or the action is disrupted.

details.description: Six metal talons concealed in the walls swing out and rake across the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25|name:Disable a Talon|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to disable each talon
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger one of the pressure plates is depressed

Effect Six talons swing out from the walls, each striking a different random creature in the room. No creature can be struck by more than one talon until every creature in the room has been attacked by at least one talon.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (trained); DC 0 to spot the balanced boulders

details.description: A weight-sensitive plate buried underneath sand pulls a boulder over a cliff ledge, causing it to fall on targets within the entrance.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:27|name:Disable the Plate|traits:mechanical,trap] (expert) or @Check[thievery|dc:25|name:Disable the Plate|traits:mechanical,trap] (expert) to find and disconnect the plate from the tipping mechanism

publication.title: Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind

details.reset: Resetting the boulders in the trap is a labor-intensive project that takes 2 hours.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area without taking care to avoid the hidden trigger plate

Effect Creatures within 10 feet of the cave entrance take @Damage[(4d10+18)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|name:Dodge Falling Rocks|traits:mechanical,trap] save).

details.description: A seep of mineral-rich water coats any unattended objects in a layer of calcite.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:23] (trained) to shatter the calcite using the resonant powers of the consonite crystals plus @Check[athletics|dc:23], @Check[nature|dc:23], or @Check[survival|dc:23] (trained) to dam the flow of water.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-13: Thick as Thieves

description.value:
Trigger An unattended inanimate object enters the path of the calcite flow

Effect The object gains a thin shell of calcite. Breaking the shell using physical damage will harm the object underneath.

details.description: These animal snares have been repurposed to discourage unwanted intruders. When a creature steps onto the snare, jaws with sharpened teeth close around the creature's leg.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:26] (trained) to step clear the snare before it springs, or @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to trigger the trap harmlessly

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the space containing the trap

Effect The trap's jaws snap shut on the creature's leg, dealing @Damage[(4d8+14)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save). On a failure, the creature becomes caught in the trap and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] ([[/act escape dc=22]]).

details.description: The ritual power in this room, corrupted by necromantic energy, slams purifying water into all "unclean" creatures that try to leave.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:25|name:Ceremonially purify everyone] (expert) to ceremonially purify everyone in the room, by pouring water from a tub over their hands, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 25) to counteract the corrupted ritual magic.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-14: The Tomb Between Worlds

details.reset: 1 minute

description.value:
Trigger A living creature steps out of the room.

Effect Water sprays out of the tubs. Every creature in the room or within 10 feet of the doors takes @Damage[(4d10+18)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] save) and the water casts 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] with a counteract modifier of [[/r {1d20+15} #Counteract Check]]{+15} against the highest-rank spell active on each target (or all spells on a target that critically fails its Reflex save).

details.description: Several clockwork humanoid figures armed with spinning blades move throughout the area.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to deactivate the clockworks from the control panel. Two successes are needed to deactivate the trap.
details.reset: The trap deactivates after 4 rounds and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (1 action) The clockwork dancers target each creature on the stage within 10 feet of a pillar by a spinning blade Strike.

description.value:
Trigger A creature ends its turn in F2

Effect Clockwork figures spring out from the pillars and attack anyone on the stage with a spinning blade Strike. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Consonite forms needle-like stalactites that can easily break free if touched, setting off a chain reaction that threatens to pierce anyone who stands in the area.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:23] (trained) to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Squeeze] through the caps between the needles.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-13: Thick as Thieves

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches any consonite cluster

Effect The needles snap, and the vibrations resonate with some of the surrounding clusters. The falling minerals deal @Damage[(2d10+9)[untyped]] damage to creatures within a @Template[burst|distance:5] surrounding the triggering individual (@Check[reflex|dc:22] save). The needle fall does not involve every consonite growth within these squares, so can be triggered again from the same location.

details.description: A cannon built of cast iron and infused with shadow magic, mounted on the side of Aslynn's flying ship.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:28] or @Check[thievery|dc:28] to disrupt the cannon; two checks are required to disable it

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-17: Trapping the Hag's Claw

details.routine: (three actions) The cannon fires a blast that contains as many PCs as possible, choosing randomly in the event of a tie, then reloads.

description.value:
Trigger The ship flies within 30 feet of a PC

Effect The cannon reloads and rolls initiative.
@Damage[(2d8+9)[bludgeoning]] damage to creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save)

details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27] (master) to painstakingly remove the rune without triggering the magic, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 25) to counteract the rune.

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes the threshold

Effect Each living creature within 30 feet must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:23] save or be subjected to the dragon's curse A cursed creature takes a –2 status penalty to Fortitude saves, and any natural or magical healing it receives is halved The curse remains until removed by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cleanse Affliction] or similar magic

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Curse the Intruders]

details.description: Enchanted caltrops blend in with the surrounding masonry.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] to safely clear the path.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-22: Breaking the Storm: Excising Ruination

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks on to the Drandlesticks

Effect The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[acrobatics|dc:27] check or take @Damage[(1d4+7)[piercing],2d6[precision,piercing]]{1d4+7 piercing damage, 2d6 precision damage}, and @Damage[2d4[bleed]]. A creature taking persistent bleed damage from the Drandlesticks takes a –10-foot penalty to its Speed.

Additionally, a creature damaged by the Drandlesticks is targeted by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Command] spell (@Check[will|dc:25|traits:magical,enchantment,mental] save) with orders to release everything it's holding; if the creature fails the save and drops what it's holding, there is a [[/r {1dc}#Heads(1): blown over the edge]]{50% chance} that the high winds blow the object or objects off the edge of the Colossus.

stealth.details: (trained) to spot the runes etched into the door's frame

details.description: Magical runes electrocute anyone who crosses the door's threshold.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:26] (trained) to jump through the door before the runes trigger, @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) to scratch out the runes, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 24) to counteract the runes
details.reset: 1 minute

description.value:
A creature moves through the doorway

Effect Arcs of lightning flash across the threshold. The triggering creature takes @Damage[(4d8+18)[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic] save). On a failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 minute. On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 minute and then dazzled for an additional minute.

stealth.details: (trained) (or –10 and no minimum proficiency if the oozeshardling node's electric pulse reaction has been triggered)

details.description: A lump of bluish ooze shot through with what look like tiny silver shavings of metal. Once it has been activated, it glows with arcs of electricity that leap out at nearby targets.
details.disable: Two successful @Check[arcana|dc:22] or @Check[occultism|dc:22] (trained) checks on the oozeshardling to disperse its power, or @Check[acrobatics|dc:24] or @Check[thievery|dc:24] (expert) to remove the flecks of empowering warshard from within the oozeshardling. A failed Acrobatics or Thievery check to disable an oozeshardling results in the creature attempting the check taking @Damage[1d6[acid]] damage (@Damage[2d6[acid]] on a critical failure).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-07: A God Falls Where Magic Fails

details.reset: The oozeshardling node collapses and melds back into the oozeshard after 1 minute, or disappears entirely if the oozeshard is slain.
details.routine: (3 actions) The oozeshardling uses its first action to make an electric spark Strike against one random non-ooze creature within range, its second action is used to move straight in a random cardinal direction (roll [[/gmr 1d4 #Direction]] to determine the direction), and its third action to make a second electric spark Strike against a different random non-ooze creature within range. The oozeshardling doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters a space within 10 feet of the oozeshardling or the oozeshardling is created by the oozeshard's Spawn Oozeshardling Node ability

Effect The oozeshardling unleashes a shocking blast that deals @Damage[3d6[electricity]] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] around it (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save).

details.description: A raging spectral inferno arises out of thin air, strengthening all undead creatures within its area. This haunt most often arises from the charred remains of a group of three people who burned to death, whether in a terrible accident or a deliberate execution, and their unavenged souls burn with rage.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:27|name:Calm the Spirit's Rage] (expert) to temporarily calm the rage of one of the three spirits, or @Check[religion|dc:30|name:Exorcise Spirit] (trained) to exorcise one of the spirits; three total successes are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: The flames cease 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area, but after 1 hour, the anger and pain simmer up and the haunt is ready to trigger again.
details.routine: (1 action) Phantom flames rage across the haunted area, dealing @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage to each living creature within the area (@Check[will|dc:23|basic] save). Undead creatures in the area are infused with flames for the following round. They gain the fire trait and immunity to fire, and all their attacks deal an additional @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage. Objects in the area are unaffected.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the remains of a victim of the original fire

Effect Memories of the pain suffered by the fire's past victims assault the triggering creature's mind. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save, and the haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 3}, and it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] for as long as it remains within the haunt's area and for 3 rounds thereafter.

details.description: Time distorts along this hallway, making the air feel like sludge.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27] (trained) to disrupt the temporal distortions, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 25) to disperse the temporal energy

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the hall

Effect Time weighs upon the creature, dealing @Damage[(4d10+18)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic] save). On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 4 rounds.

details.description: Reality suddenly and violently transforms, shifting from a harmless patch of terrain into something truly deadly as the mindscape draws from memories of pain and agony, revisiting old wounds.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:27] to cause the hazard to fade away without triggering it, or @Check[survival|dc:30] to sense what parts of reality are in flux and to avoid the area

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

description.value:
Trigger A creature Interacts with the hazardous object, or steps into the hazardous area

Effect A sudden blast detonates, centered on the triggering creature's square. This blast fills a @Template[burst|distance:20] and inflicts @Damage[(4d10+18)[untyped]] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). The type of damage is chosen by the GM from physical (bludgeoning or piercing), or from acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. The description of how this explosion manifests should vary to match the type of damage inflicted, but should also draw from the triggering creature's past, as if memories of old wounds had suddenly come back with a vengeance. An explosive manifestation that deals piercing damage might be conveyed as a sudden blast of spikes radiating up out of the ground (stemming from a creature's memory of the time it fell into a spiked pit and almost died). One that deals fire damage might involve the sound of a dragon's roar as the air erupts into flames (coming from a creature's memory of being nearly incinerated by a dragon). A moment later, all evidence of the hazard's manifestation (save for the damage it causes to the creatures in its area) vanish.

stealth.details: (trained) to find the desk switch; @Check[perception|dc:28|traits:action:seek] (expert) to find the trapdoor and charge

details.description: A black‑powder charge fastened to a trapdoor (marked with a "T" on the map) blows a hole in a steam tunnel, filling the room with superheated steam.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to disarm the desk switch; @Check[thievery|dc:28|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to disarm the trapdoor charge
details.routine:
(1 action) The trapdoor emits a jet of superheated steam. Each creature in the room must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:28] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(1d10+3)[fire]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+6)[fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+6)[fire]] damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued], and is blinded for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger The desk switch is pressed or the trapdoor is opened

Effect The charge on the door explodes. All creatures within 10 feet take @Damage[(2d10+6)[fire],(1d10+3)[piercing]]{2d10+6 fire damage and 1d10+3 piercing damage}, and @Damage[1d6[bleed]] (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic]). The room is filled with steam, making all creatures within the room @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed]. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: The spirits of innumerable dryads and peaceful wood elementals, ripped from their bodies as they were turned into furniture, writhe and seethe, lashing out at both living and undead alike.
details.disable:
@Check[diplomacy|dc:23] (trained), @Check[nature|dc:23] (trained), or @Check[religion|dc:23] (trained) to set the spirits to rest or @Check[deception|dc:27], @Check[intimidation|dc:27], or @Check[occultism|dc:27] to convince the spirits that their eternal rest is coming soon.

A PC may make a Strike against AC 22 to destroy a single table or chair in the room, this functions as a successful check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid] another PC attempting to disable the haunt; two total successes are required to disable the haunt. If all of the tables and chairs are destroyed, the haunt is temporarily disabled until dawn of the next day.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-99: Under the Eye of the Mantis

details.routine: (two actions) The Haunted Hollows haunt uses table flip or chair toss Strikes at different creatures in the area, favoring those who are trying to disable the haunt first, but otherwise choosing randomly. The table flip Strikes originate with any table in the room, destroying the table in the process. The chair toss Strikes originate with any chair or bar stool in the room, destroying that object in the process. If the haunt is not disabled, these objects reform and return to their original positions at dawn of the next day.

description.value:
Trigger Any living or undead creatures approach within 30 feet of the haunt's locus (the center of the open balcony) inside the tavern

Effect The haunt makes a table flip Strike with a reach of 20 feet against a random creature in the area, and then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (expert) to spot the sockets in the floor where the portcullis drops; @Check[perception|dc:28|traits:action:seek] (expert) to spot the floor trigger

details.description: An iron portcullis drops from the ceiling when a pressure plate is triggered.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to disable the floor trigger, or @Check[athletics|dc:27] or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:27] to jam the portcullis in the ceiling

description.value:
Trigger The pressure plate is depressed

Effect The portcullis falls, closing off the hallway. Any creature under the portcullis when it drops takes @Damage[(4d10+18)[piercing]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized]. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:26] save takes no damage and rolls away in a random direction; on a critical success, they can choose the direction.

If the trap was triggered by a creature moving north with a Speed of at least 10 feet, they're considered to be under the portcullis when it triggers. Creatures moving south when triggering the portcullis aren't considered to be under the portcullis when it triggers.

details.description: After a battle concludes, the battlefield shimmers strangely as dead bodies twitch, destroyed objects shake, and feelings of déjà vu grow overwhelmingly strong.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:27] to cause the hazard to fade away before it manifests, or @Check[deception|dc:30] to pretend to ignore the growing continuity break and proceed without fear, causing it to collapse in on itself.

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

description.value:
Trigger The PCs conclude an encounter in which a battle took place

Effect The mindscape starts to revert the results of the battle, shifting things back to the way they were at the start of the encounter. The reversion takes 1 round to complete, during which the PCs can attempt to disable the hazard or take any other actions to prepare for facing the same encounter over again. At the end of the round, the encounter resets and initiative is rolled again; all foes and environmental elements return to the condition they were in just before the fight began, but the PCs themselves do not.

details.description: A blast of energy damages all creatures within 20 feet, with particularly severe damage to fiends.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27|name:Identify Button Sequence|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to identify the proper sequence of buttons in the divots, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 25) to suppress the magical effect for 1 hour
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger The door is touched without first pressing the correct sequence of buttons

Effect A blast of energy ripples outward from the door, dealing @Damage[4d10[force]] damage to all creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:20]{20 feet} and forcing them to make a @Check[fortitude|dc:25] save.

Fiends treat the result of their saving throw as one degree worse.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}; while it is sickened, it's also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1}.

details.description: Rotating blades extend from the walls, floors, and ceiling of the surrounding halls when weight is removed from the trapped floor tile.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27] (expert) to disable the pressure plate

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

details.reset: The trap resets after 15 minutes.

description.value:
Trigger Weight is removed from the trapped floor tile

Effect Blades extend from the floor, ceiling, and walls of the surrounding hallways to eviscerate all creatures in the area, dealing @Damage[(4d8+10)[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic] save). Creatures that critically fail also take @Damage[1d6[bleed]] damage.

details.description: A swarm of aggressive cuttlefish, octopuses, devil rays, and countless other species of sea life bite and bludgeon Timonithaea's enemies.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27] (trained) to remove the crown from the altar while avoiding being bitten by the swarming sea life, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 27) to disable the crown's enchantment, breaking Timonithaea's control over the sea creatures.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-03: A Foot in the Door

details.routine: (one action) The sea creatures attack, dealing @Damage[(1d10+4)[bludgeoning],(1d10+4)[piercing]] damage to each creature hostile to Timonithaea within the temple (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger The PCs enter the main temple chamber

Effect The chamber fills with sea creatures, then rolls initiative.

details.description: An invisible rune creates an invisible, spherical magical sensor with a 20-foot radius.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (expert) to erase the rune without triggering the sensor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 23) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-09: Killer in the Golden Mask

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the sensor area

Effect The rune detonates a fireball centered on the triggering creature's square. This is a 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Fireball] spell that deals @Damage[8d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic]).

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the creatures hovering in the trees

details.description: Hovering amid the branches of an acacia tree, a swarm of wood wisps attempt to gather the papers that have been caught in its leaves and thorns.
details.disable: Diplomacy @Check[diplomacy|dc:21] (trained) to make a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Request], @Check[athletics|dc:21] (trained) to climb the tree and help gather papers manually, or @Check[performance|dc:23] (trained) to demonstrate how the wisps can help without destroying the papers.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-13: Thick as Thieves

details.routine: (3 actions) The wisp swarm loses one action for each successful check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Disable a Device]{disable} it. The wisps swarm to work on each of their actions, making a headbutt Strike against any creature within 10 feet of the tree and destroying [[/br 2d4 #Pages]] pages of notes.

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 10 feet of the tree or directly addresses the wood wisps

Effect The wood wisp swarm animatedly tries to help the group gather the papers, destroying [[/br 2d4 #Pages]] pages, and then rolls initiative.

details.description: Spectral hag claws reach out from the walls of the gem-like chamber to grab the PCs' souls and pull them into the trap's onyx facets.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (expert) or @Check[crafting|dc:25](expert) to find the weak spot and crack each facet, or dispel magic (4th level; counteract DC 23) to counteract each facet; the hazard has a number of facets equal to the number of PCs.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-11: Prisoners of the Electric Castle

details.routine:
The hazard gains a number of actions equal to the number of PCs; it loses 1 action each round for each facet that has been disabled. Each claw tears at a different PC, who takes @Damage[(2d10+9)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:27|basic] save). On a failure, they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] by the claw ([[/act escape dc=25]]). On a critical failure, or on a failure if they are already grabbed, they have their soul ripped from their body, which falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious]. Each round on their turn, the soul can attempt a @Check[intimidation|dc:25], @Check[occultism|dc:25], or @Check[survival|dc:25] check to fortify itself and return to its body as a three-action activity. The possible outcomes are as follows.

Critical Success The soul returns to their body, which loses the unconscious condition.

Success The soul returns to their body, which loses the unconscious condition, but gains the grabbed condition.

Failure The soul makes no progress toward escape.

Critical Failure The soul makes no progress toward escape and the target takes @Damage[(2d10+9)[mental]] damage until they are reduced to 0 Hit Points, after which they remain in captivity until the trap is defeated.

Each claw focuses on its target until the target is reduced to 0 Hit Points, after which it takes no action on its turn unless another facet is disabled or its target regains Hit Points.

description.value:
Trigger Pathfinder agents enter the electric castle

Effect Spectral hag claws emerge from the walls. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Pressure plates trigger hidden mauls in the walls of the pits that swing up to slam creatures and knock them off the span.
details.disable: @Check[society|dc:23|name:Locate the hidden switch] (expert) to locate the hidden deactivation switch or @Check[thievery|dc:30|name:Jam the mechanics] (trained) to jam the mechanics on the span.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-14: The Tomb Between Worlds

details.reset: The trap resets over the course of the round and is ready to swing again 1 round later. It can be manually reset if Disabled.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves onto the span.

Effect The mauls swing up, making an attack against the triggering creature.

If struck by the Mauls strike, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:25] save or be knocked off the span into a pit. The target can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

details.description: Water splashes within an ornate 5-foot-tall amphora. The glossy exterior depicts images of a marid trapped in a cage of flames, the frightened creature waving their hands in warning.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (trained) to scrape the flames away from the marid's image (requires two successes); @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 25) to disrupt the magical prison, disabling the trap for 10 minutes; or touch the amphora with Rayyan's seal to free the marid

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-14: Shattering Golden Chains

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 2 minutes pass without a creature inside the room.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap has one action per round by default, though it may gain more actions based on the encounter's scaling as listed in the sidebar. On each of the trap's actions, the amphora pours out water, increasing the depth of the water by 5 feet. Once the room is full of water, creatures in the room begin drowning, and the trap deals @Damage[(2d10+9)[bludgeoning]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic] save to each creature in the room on its action as the water pressure increases.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches anything in the room without Fasiel's permission

Effect The trap rolls initiative. A wall of water blocks the exits while the trap is active, making a shove attack ([[/r 1d20+22]]) toward the interior against any creature that moves into a doorway space.

details.description: A 5-foot-square metal vault covered with knobs lies concealed within a stone block. A lever on the stone block slides the vault into view. When the vault is exposed-whether it's slid out or whether the stone is smashed open-the trap activates.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:24|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to find and press hidden knobs in a specific order (three successes are required to disable the trap, which also opens it); @Check[crafting|dc:24] (expert) to sabotage the vault's mechanisms, reducing its actions by 1 (when the trap's actions are reduced to 0, it is disabled)
details.routine: The keystone uses its 3 actions to Electrify, Spin, and then Launch Darts. When the trap loses an action, roll randomly to determine which action it loses.

description.value:
Keystone Hardness 15

Keystone HP 60 (BT 30)

Trigger The vault is exposed

Effect The vault makes a poison dart spray Strike at a random creature within 30 feet. The trap then rolls initiative.

The metal vault crackles with electricity. In air, this would damage only creatures touching it; in water, the trap deals @Damage[2d8[electricity]] damage to all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:10] (@Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic]).

The vault fires a stream of poisoned darts. The trap attacks all creatures in a @Template[line|distance:30] with its poison dart spray Strike; roll [[/r 1d8]] to determine the line's direction. These Strikes don't have a multiple attack penalty.
The vault spins quickly for 1 round. While it Spins, checks to disable the trap have a -2 circumstance penalty, and it Launches Darts at all creatures within 30 feet of it, rather than in a line.

details.description: Influenced by the last prayers of the dead, creatures experience extreme claustrophobia and batter at the walls and floor, desperate to escape, see the sun, and breathe fresh air—but their prayers go unanswered.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:26] (expert) to disrupt the haunt through prayer, or @Check[deception|dc:26] or @Check[diplomacy|dc:26] DC 27 (expert) to convince the spirits you'll free them from this place

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The haunt reforms after 1 day.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the altar, corpse, or statue

Effect The triggering creature batters their fists and body into the floor and walls, dealing @Damage[(4d10+18)[bludgeoning]] damage to themself (@Check[will|dc:25|basic] save). All creatures who witness this become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} unless they succeed at a @Check[will|dc:25] save.

details.description: A magical magnet hidden behind a panel in the corner of the room pulls not metal but living creatures toward it, heedless of any obstacles in the way.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:29|name:Deactivate Panel|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to deactivate the panel, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract the panel's magic
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets 1 minute after there are no creatures touching the iron bars.
details.routine:
(1 action) The trap pulls up to four creatures that have already triggered its Magnetize the Living ability toward one of the far corners of the room, possibly pressing them into the iron bars. If all the iron bars between an affected creature and the magnetic panel remain intact, the creature takes @Damage[(2d10+10)[bludgeoning]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic|options:inflicts:restrained] save and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] on a failure ([[/act escape dc=23]]).

If an iron bar has been broken, the creature takes @Damage[(1d10+5)[bludgeoning]] damage instead, and if two consecutive bars have been broken the creature takes no damage (but might still be restrained on a failed save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches any of the iron bars in this area or touches a creature already affected by the trap

Effect The magical panel hidden in the far corner of the chamber (either the western or the eastern corner, depending on which side of the room the creature was closest to when it triggered the trap) "magnetizes" the triggering creature and violently pulls it toward the corner, automatically dealing @Damage[(2d10+10)[bludgeoning]] damage to the creature.

The trap then rolls initiative if it hasn't already done so.

details.description: The strange fog layer on the walls swirls and churns, then suddenly fires several beams of dark mist into the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27] (master) to disrupt some of the hidden runes that focus the trap's energies, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank, counteract DC 25) or any spell with the Light trait to counteract the trap
details.reset: The lightlock takes 1 minute to restore its energy, after which it can be triggered again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature carrying a light source ends their turn in area F8

Effect The walls fire beams of chilling dark fog at the triggering creature, who takes @Damage[10d6[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic] save). The beams also attempt to counteract any light source the triggering creature carries. Non-magical light sources of 7th level or less are automatically extinguished. Against other light sources, the lightlock casts a 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] spell with a counteract modifier of [[/r 1d20+14 #Counteract]]{+14} to attempt to counteract the light.

stealth.details: (trained) to identify as dangerous quicksand; +20 (expert) to recognize its magical properties

details.description: This 15-foot-wide patch of destabilized sand attempts to submerge creatures who wield magic. A desert tree with dense bright green foliage, pale pink blooms, and at least one carnivorous red-chested magpie is always nearby.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:26] (expert) to feed magic into the whorl and temporarily stabilize it for a few minutes; feed the whorl a number of spellcasters and magic items with a combined 10 levels (for instance, a 4th-level cleric with two 3rd-level magic items) to stabilize it for 1 week; or feed the whorl a number of spellcasters and magic items with a combined 20 levels to stabilize it permanently. Spellcasters and magic items fed to the whorl are devoured whole.
details.reset: The hazard continues to suck in anyone with magic until it is satiated (see Disable above). Magpies leave any satiated whorls.
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the whorl pulls down any creature within it that can cast spells or that is wielding a magic item. A creature submerged to the waist becomes submerged to the neck, and a creature submerged to the neck is pulled under and must hold its breath to avoid suffocation. If a creature has no spells or magic items, the mana whorl is merely difficult terrain. A creature caught in a mana whorl can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:23] check to Swim to either raise itself by one step if submerged to the neck or worse, or to move 5 feet if submerged only to the waist. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down by one step. A creature that Swims out of the whorl escapes the hazard and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in a space adjacent to the whorl. Other creatures can Aid the creature, typically by using a rope or similar aid, or attempt to pull the creature out with their own @Check[athletics|dc:23] check, with the same results as if the creature attempted the check. If a creature has only magic items and no ability to cast spells, dropping all the items releases that creature, and it can move through the mana whorl as normal difficult terrain.

description.value:
Trigger A creature who can cast spells, or who is wielding a magic item, walks into the mana whorl

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the whorl to its waist. The whorl rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

details.description: Hidden jets expel gas that quickly freezes, causing limited damage to nearby books.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27] (expert) to deactivate the trap without its control key or @Check[crafting|dc:26] to create and apply a chemical that neutralizes the gas.

Creatures who fail or critically fail the saving throw take @Damage[2d8[persistent,cold]] damage for up to 3 rounds. The affected area is filled with mist that functions as obscuring mist for 3 rounds.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Catchy Slogan]

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-14: The Tomb Between Worlds

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches or disturbs the umbral vise.

Effect Natron sprays out of the tub, infusing all creatures adjacent to the tub with void energy and desiccating their internal organs. Each creature takes @Damage[2d10[void],2d10[poison]]{2d10 void damage and 2d10 poison damage} (@Check[fortitude|dc:29|basic] save). On a failure, the internal damage makes the creature more susceptible to other effects that would cause it to mummify, such as Mummy Rot. For 1 day, the first time it is exposed to such an effect, it uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its saving throw.

details.description: The Eternity Arch sparks with electricity, releasing bolts of lightning at each of the four largest time rifts.

details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled time rift. On each of the trap's actions, the Eternity Arch releases a bolt of lightning that activates that rift, dealing @Damage[2d10[electricity]] damage to all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:10] around the time rift (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). It can release a bolt at each active time rift no more than once each turn.

description.value:

details.description: A curse is imbued on an entryway's threshold.

details.reset: The trap resets when the door is shut.

description.value:

A cursed creature takes a -2 status penalty to Fortitude saves, and any natural or magical healing it receives is halved. The curse remains until removed by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cleanse Affliction] or similar magic.

details.description: The cracks scintillate and shift between the elemental planes, leaking perilous energy with each shift.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Assemble a Seal|traits:environmental,magical] (expert) twice to assemble a seal using objects strongly grounded to the Material Plane, @Check[arcana|dc:26|name:Invoke Incantations|traits:environmental,magical] (trained) twice to invoke incantations to seal the cracks, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank, counteract DC 22) to counteract the cracks.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-16: Freedom for Wishes

details.reset: The cracks re-form after 1 hour.
details.routine:
(1 action) A surge of energy strikes a random non-mephit creature in the foyer. Roll [[/r 1d4]] to determine the element of the energy surge.

1-Energy from the Plane of Air: the creature takes @Damage[(2d10+3)[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|name:Planar Cracks|traits:air,electricity] save); on a failure, the creature is pushed back 10 feet toward the entryway. The hazard gains the air and electricity traits for this round.

2-Energy from the Plane of Earth: the creature takes @Damage[(2d10+3)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|name:Planar Cracks|traits:earth] save); on a failed save, the creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. The hazard gains the earth trait for this round.

3-Energy from the Plane of Fire: the creature takes @Damage[(2d10+9)[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|name:Planar Cracks|traits:fire] save). The hazard gains the fire trait for this round.

4-Energy from the Plane of Water: the creature takes @Damage[(2d10+3)[cold]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|name:Planar Cracks|traits:water,cold] save); on a failed save, the creature's lungs fill with water, making them @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}. The hazard gains the cold and water traits for this round.

description.value:
Trigger A non-mephit creature enters the manor

details.description: The effects of Hesla's profane ritual ripple outward through Deskari's Gullet. Physical tremors shake the cavern complex, while magical waves of energy affect those opposed to her actions.
details.disable: This hazard persists until Hesla's ritual is complete and her demonic forces defeated.

description.value:
Trigger Combat begins within Deskari's Gullet

Targets take @Damage[(1d10+4)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] save) as rocks fall from the ceiling. On a critical failure, the target falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Targets take @Damage[(1d10+4)[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:27|basic] save). On a critical failure, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}. Holy creatures treat their save as one degree worse for the purposes of the sickened condition only.

If its grasp was already weakened, they drop the item.

details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:25] (trained) to repair Chamenaiti's magic, @Check[religion|dc:27] or @Check[occultism|dc:27] (trained) to offset the prayer, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 25) to counteract the warning

description.value:

(1 action; mental) The cleansing pool uses its first action to try and compel all creatures within 5 feet to enter into the pool. Creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23|traits:mental] save.

Critical Success The creature is immune to the pool's routine for 1 minute.

Success No effect.

Failure The creature gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] condition and attempts to enter the pool.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature must also use all of their actions on their next turn to try and pull one of their allies into the pool with them.

Trigger a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] creature enters the pool

Effect the waters rush up to encircle the creature, which must succeed on a @Check[fortitude|dc:25] save or begin drowning.

publication.title: Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind

Effect Hissing, illusionary snakes leap from the door. Each living creature within 30 feet is subjected to the curse of potent poison. If a living creature opened the door, that creature must roll its saving throw against the curse twice and take the worse result (this is a misfortune effect).

Curse of Potent Poison (uncommon, curse, magical) CURSE 5

This curse magically speeds poison through your body.

Saving Throw @Check[will|dc:20|name:Curse of Potent Poison|traits:uncommon,magical]

Effect You take a -1 status penalty to Fortitude saves and you gain weakness 3 to poison (if you already have weakness to poison, increase it by 3). The curse remains until removed by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cleanse Affliction] or similar magic.

A creature that fails their save is pushed back 10 feet (15 feet on a critical failure).

@Damage[3d6[cold],3d6[void]|options:area-damage]

@Damage[2d6[cold],2d6[void]|options:area-damage]

@Check[crafting|dc:25] or @Check[thievery|dc:25] (master) to ground the device, or @Check[arcana|dc:25], @Check[nature|dc:25], @Check[occultism|dc:25], or @Check[religion|dc:25] (master) to safely vent the magical energy; three total successes in any combination to disable

details.routine: (3 actions) The shadow beacon makes three electric bolt Strikes at random creatures within 100 feet. One of these Strikes targets either Deg or Nizca, and the other two target PCs. The trap loses 1 action for each successful disable.

description.value:
Trigger The fourth round of combat begins

Effect The shadow beacon malfunctions, releasing a shock wave of energy. All creatures within 100 feet of the shadow beacon must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:29|options:area-effect,inflicts:prone] save or be knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. Creatures who are flying are instead knocked down 15 feet on a failure, taking @Damage[(1d10+5)[bludgeoning]] damage and landing prone if they collide with a solid object during this movement. The trap then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: Singe's spirit lingers in the Whippoorwill's crow's nest, where it calls out warnings and shoots intruders.
details.disable: @Check[pathfinder-society-lore|dc:27] (trained) to persuade Singe that you are Pathfinders or @Check[religion|dc:27](trained) to perform rites to lay Singe to rest; two total successes are required to disable the haunt

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: d Singe uses its first action(s) to make a long gun Strike against a random creature in the area. It uses its final action for Lookout's Warning. If the PCs successfully use Pathfinder Society Lore to Disable the haunt, Singe loses an action to make a long gun Strike.

description.value:
Trigger One or more creatures steps onto the Whippoorwill's wreckage

Effect Singe makes a long gun Strike against a random creature in the area, uses Lookout's Warning, and then rolls initiative.

Singe lets out one of the following cries, in order (repeating from the top, if necessary). Everyone in the haunt's area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:27] save. On a success, the creature is unaffected; on a critical success, it also gains a +2 circumstance bonus on its next save vs. Lookout's Warning. Other effects depend on the specific cry below.

  • Thar She Blows: "Captain, look! A huge whale! It's coming toward us—fast!" On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for 1 round, looking for the whale; on a critical failure it's fascinated for 1 minute, and the condition doesn't end if Singe makes a Strike on an ally or uses Lookout's Warning.
  • No Quarter: "It's coming around again! It's relentless! Brace for impact!" On a failure, the creature is tossed off balance; it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] for 1 round. On a critical failure, it's also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].
  • Shark Bait: "Captain, it's hopeless! It's going to destroy the ship!" On a failure, a sense of doom fills the creature, which becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}. On a critical failure, the fear is overwhelming, and it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.
  • Going Down with the Ship: "Get below, captain, and secure the Forked Key! I'll try to give you time!" On a failure, the creature must spend one action putting an item it is holding away; if it is not holding anything, it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for one action. On a critical failure, it must spend three actions in a combination of putting away any items it is holding and fleeing.

stealth.details: (expert) to notice little wisps of smoke start to curl up from the unlit candles on the skulls.

details.description: A line of skulls adorned with black candles sit on the floor, and use darkvision to spot intruders. The red arrows on the map indicate the direction the skulls are looking.
details.disable: @Check[stealth|dc:27] to move through the room unseen by the skulls (this does not disable the trap but does prevent it from triggering), @Check[thievery|dc:27] three times to "blind" an adjacent skull by deftly scraping hidden runes away from the inside of the eye sockets, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank, counteract DC 25) to counteract the trap
details.reset: If there are no visible targets, the trap deactivates at the end of its turn, then automatically resets.
details.routine: (6 actions) On its initiative the trap uses its first action to rotate a skull that can't see targets a full 360 degrees, stopping as soon as the skull spots a target. It repeats this action until all six skulls are looking at targets, then uses its remaining actions to shoot beams of fire from their eyes. The trap can only target a creature once per round, so if there are fewer targets than actions, any additional actions possessed by the trap are lost. The trap loses one action for each skull that is destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks into view of the skulls and is spotted by them

Effect The candles atop all six skulls flare to light and the skulls shriek out fearsome, mind-numbing howls. All creatures in area F6, the stairs, or the eastern trough must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] as long as they remain frightened.

Critical Failure As failure, but once the creature is no longer frightened, the previous minute of memories are erased—likely removing knowledge of the secret door at the top of the stairs in area E7a. All that remains are strange lingering fears about returning to the area.

stealth.details: (trained) to discern the snake's mane from the surrounding vegetation

details.description: A tangle of feathery green vines appears innocuous but fires a spray of acidic barbs at anything that disturbs it.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:27] (expert) to strip the barbs from the vines, rendering them harmless, or @Check[stealth|dc:30] (trained) to move through the snake's mane without disturbing it
details.reset: 1 hour, as the vine grows a new set of barbs

description.value:
A creature enters an area containing snake's mane or the snake's mane takes damage

Effect The vine sprays a hail of acidic barbs in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10]. Any creature in the area takes @Damage[(4d10+18)[acid]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save). On a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 minute.

Note: A saving throw for this ability was not provided by Paizo. The DC presented here is a hard DC for the hazard's level based on the GM Core hazard building rules.

details.description: A flickering humanoid shape with four gold eyes, clad in red robes with black sleeves and wielding a torch that burns with red fire and smoke, strides purposefully across the ground, creating distortions in reality around it that cause wood to twist and decay and sends agonizing pain through the flesh.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:23] (expert) to disrupt the haunt's concentration in the living world and break its link to the mindscape, @Check[religion|dc:27] (expert) to banish the energy from the mindscape, or @Check[occultism|dc:30] (expert) to turn the haunt's energy back against itself to cancel it out

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: If both hazards are disabled or destroyed, a new one forms adjacent to a randomly determined character still inside the crumbling building after [[/gmr 1d4+2 #Rounds]] rounds. Once all three NPCs are rescued or exorcised, these hazards don't reset.
details.routine: (3 actions) The spiritual disruptor's first action each round is always to create a pulse of rippling energy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] that causes the surrounding structure to appear to age rapidly and become distorted, causing the ground in this area to become difficult terrain. If the spiritual disruptor is adjacent to a living target, it attempts to disrupt that target's life energy by making a Strike with its smoking torch. If the spiritual disruptor isn't adjacent to a living target, it Strides toward the closest target. If it has multiple adjacent targets, the spiritual disruptor favors higher-level targets, selecting randomly from multiple adjacent equal-level targets.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs enter or otherwise interact with the building and its contents

Effect A spiritual disruptor manifests at both locations marked A4. Both hazards then roll initiative.

details.description: A sportlebore hides amid the victuals on a plate of food or within a pack of trail rations.
details.disable: A creature that notices the sportlebore can remove it from the food it is hidden within automatically as an Interact action.

description.value:
Trigger A creature eats food the sportlebore is hidden within

Effect The sportlebore rapidly multiplies in the host's stomach, exposing the host to sportlebore infestation

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (1 hour)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} (1 hour)

Stage 4 @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic] save) as the host painfully vomits out a sportlebore swarm and returns to stage 1

details.description: A sportlebore hides amid the victuals on a plate of food or within a pack of trail rations.
details.disable: A creature that notices the sportlebore can remove it from the food it is hidden within automatically as an Interact action.

description.value:
Trigger A creature eats food the sportlebore is hidden within

Effect The sportlebore rapidly multiplies in the host's stomach, exposing the host to sportlebore infestation

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (1 hour)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} (1 hour)

Stage 4 @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic] save) as the host painfully vomits out a sportlebore swarm and returns to stage 1

details.description: The sand conceals a rune that summons an invisible stalker into this yard.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:25|name:Approach Rune] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[arcana|dc:25|name:Erase Rune] (expert) or @Check[thievery|dc:27|name:Erase Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 25) to counteract the rune
details.reset: The trap resets after 24 hours.

description.value:
Trigger A creature that isn't openly bearing an Aspis Consortium badge crosses the center of the yard

Effect The trap summons an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Phade]{Invisible Stalker}. The invisible stalker rolls initiative and remains for [[/gmr 2d6 #rounds]]{2d6 rounds}, after which the spell ends and the invisible stalker disappears. The invisible stalker also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires.

The invisible stalker can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: A field of warm mist covers an open plain. Pockets of highly pressurized steam lie @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] in the earth; pressure releases jets of steam.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:26] (expert) twice to navigate a safe path

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-24: Lightning Strikes, Stars Fall

description.value:
Trigger A creature fails a skill check to navigate through the steaming field

Effect Several jets of pressurized steam erupt, dealing @Damage[(2d6+6)[bludgeoning],(2d6+6)[fire]]{2d6+6 bludgeoning damage and 2d6+6 fire damage} to creatures within 30 feet of the ground (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic]).

details.description: A hardy, innocuous-looking green mold grows over surfaces, slowly absorbing minerals.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:26] (trained) to scrape away the mold without touching it
details.reset: 1 hour, as the mold settles back across the surface.

description.value:
Trigger A creature with bones touches the mold or steps into an area covered with it

Effect The mold shudders and slithers onto the triggering creature as it attempts to absorb calcium through their skin. The creature attempts a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:25] save.

Critical Success The target shakes the mold off and is unaffected.

Success A small patch of mold clings to the target and deals @Damage[(2d10+9)[piercing]] damage.

Failure The mold clings to the target. The target takes @Damage[(4d10+18)[piercing]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

Critical Failure The mold envelops the target. As failure, except the target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} until it's healed to its Hit Point maximum.

Note: A DC was not provided for this ability by Paizo. The DC present here is a hard DC for the hazard level according to the GM Core hazard building Tables.

details.description: Brightly polished copper chains rumble with distant thunder and statically charge the air.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (expert) to release the lock, or @Check[arcana|dc:27] or @Check[nature|dc:27] (expert) to harmlessly release the storm's energy

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-14: Shattering Golden Chains

details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to break or escape the chains

Effect The chains release a devastating blast of lightning that deals @Damage[(4d10+18)[electricity]] damage to all creatures in the room. Affected creatures must each attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:26] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 minute.

Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes deafened for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage. It also becomes deafened for 8 hours.

details.description: Just as a fight begins, time suddenly seems to break in the area, causing some elements to freeze in place and others to slow down or speed up, imparting a disjointed and nightmarish sensation upon those present.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:27] to cause the hazard to fade away and release all those affected by it from the hazard's effects, or @Check[intimidation|dc:30] to shock a single adjacent creature out of the distortion and free them from the hazard's effects.

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

description.value:
Trigger Initiative is rolled at the start of a combat where the PCs face a type of foe they have fought before

Effect As the combat begins, everything suddenly feels distorted and dreamlike. Just as in dreams, time itself seems to stretch or compress in ways that transform everything into a nightmare. All creatures within a @Template[burst|distance:30] from the first creature to act in combat must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] saving throw as, for a brief moment, time appears to stand still.

Critical Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Quickened] 1 for 1 minute; it can only use the extra action each round for actions with the move trait.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3} as it perceives time to have stopped entirely for it. When it recovers from the stun, it is slowed 1 for 1 minute.

Note: A DC was not provided for this ability by Paizo. The DC present here is a hard DC for the hazard level according to the Gamemastery Guide hazard building Tables.

details.description: An incorporeal Sarkorian deity manifests before the party and challenges their resolve. Only aspirants whose motives are pure and whose hearts are in tune with the natural world can hope to overcome this "test of the god caller."
details.disable: Three @Check[nature|dc:27] or @Check[occultism|dc:27] (trained) checks can disable the haunt
details.reset: 8 hours
details.routine:
(3 actions) The Sarkorian divinity commands a swarm of ghostly animals to overwhelm creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:10] in the well. The haunt can select up to three different points to target, spending 1 action per burst. Creatures in an affected take @Damage[4d6[force]] and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save. The haunt loses 1 action for each successful check to disable the haunt.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] until the beginning of the haunt's next turn.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage, is off-guard until the beginning of the haunt's next turn, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger Sakuachi makes an offering at the well shrine

Effect The Sarkorian deity inhabiting the well surges to life, manifesting as a large, ghostly beast surrounded by throngs of smaller animals. The rush of spiritual energy washes over everyone in the well, dealing @Damage[8d6[force]] (@Check[will|dc:25|basic]).

details.description: A shadow the size of an island appears in the water beneath the ship.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:27], @Check[athletics|dc:27], or @Check[diplomacy|dc:27] or @Check[sailing-lore|dc:23] to help the crew of the Tide Hunter escape the Ketos; three total successes are required to disable the hazard
details.reset: If three or more of its tentacles are destroyed, the Ketos releases the Tide Hunter and disappears into the ocean's depths to nurse its wounds. The monster does not return to this area for 24 hours.
details.routine: (3 actions) The Ketos uses its actions to make tentacle Strikes against creatures aboard the Tide Hunter or within 30 feet of the ship.

description.value:
Trigger The Tide Hunter enters the Ketos's hunting grounds

Effect The Ketos uses its eight tentacles to seize the ship, holding it in place. The hazard then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (expert) to hear whispered prayers in Dwarven to Torag

details.description: The lingering essence of the dwarven worshippers who established this shrine demand deference to Torag and punish those who don't offer it.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:27] (trained) to calm the lingering essence, @Check[religion|dc:23] (expert) to perform the required obeisance, or @Check[thievery|dc:30] (expert) to disrupt and redirect the essence harmlessly.
details.reset: The haunt deactivates as soon as no living creatures are in area A4, then resets after 1 hour has passed. It can be permanently destroyed by clearing the fortress of all creatures that don't follow Torag and rededicating it to the god with a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Consecrate] ritual.
details.routine:
(3 actions; divine, enchantment, mental) Ghostly images of chanting dwarves float through the room, drifting between the creatures in the chamber. As they float, they use their 3 actions to target up to three different creatures in the room, touching their brows and filling that person's head with overwhelming but entirely mental choirs exalting the glory of Torag. The haunt targets @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] creatures before other creatures, and a single creature cannot be targeted more than once per round. A targeted creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25|traits:divine,enchantment,mental] save; prone creatures take a -2 penalty on this saving throw.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d10[mental]] damage from the cacophonous choir in their head.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+9)[mental]] damage from the cacophonous choir in their head.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(3d10+18)[mental]] damage from the cacophonous choir in their head and is knocked prone.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves more than 10 feet into the room.

Effect A great weight settles on all creatures in area A4, and they must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected by the haunt and is temporarily immune to it for 24 hours.

Success The creature feels pressed down by the weight-it takes a 10-foot penalty to all its speeds for as long as it remains in the room.

Failure The creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature is knocked prone and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] for 1 round.

details.description: When the bin is opened, it collapses, spilling out the infested corpse within.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:23] (expert) to reinforce the bin so it doesn't collapse (although if the PCs then search the remains, they still trigger the hazard), @Check[religion|dc:27] (master) to exorcise the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

description.value:
Trigger The seed bin is opened, or the body within is jostled or searched

Effect The body spills out and splits open, releasing an agonizing scream. All creatures in area C4 must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:29] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] as long as they remain frightened on a critical failure). The ghoulish gardeners in area C7 come to investigate soon thereafter. The creature who triggered the haunt must also succeed at a @Check[will|dc:29] save with the following effects as it takes a blast of spore-like seeds to the face.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected by the infestation.

Success The creature believes it inhaled some of the stuff, and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

Failure As success, but the creature now fears that it's infected by the corrupt seeds, and imagines it can feel plants growing inside itself. This is all in the creature's mind, but for the next 8 hours, the sensation makes it @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2}.

Critical Failure As failure, but the clumsy condition has an unlimited duration.

stealth.details: (trained) to spot the rune inside the lock

details.description: A rune inscribed on the inside of the lock releases a burst of flame that burns the thief's hands and their tools.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (expert) to scratch out the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 24) to counteract the runes

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Pick a Lock]{Pick the Lock} or fails to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Disable a Device]{Disable the Device}

Effect The rune triggers, releasing a burst of flame from the lock. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:25] saving throw.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+9)[fire]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d10+18)[fire]] damage and their fingers are burned, imposing a –1 circumstance penalty to Thievery checks for 1 hour.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Burned Fingers]

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature's thieves' toolkit is broken.

details.description: A trip wire halfway up the stairs releases half a dozen spherical thunderstones that bounce down the stairs and explode.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27|name:Safely Cut the Trip Wire|traits:mechanical,hazard,trap] (expert) to safely cut the trip wire

publication.title: Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God

description.value:
Trigger The trip wire is disturbed

Effect Several thunderstones bounce down the stairs and explode. Each creature within 10 feet of the lower half of the stairs takes @Damage[6d10[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:24|basic|traits:hazard,trap,mechanical] save). Creatures that fail this save are additionally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 minute; creatures that critically fail this save are instead deafened for 1 hour.

details.description: The green crystals begin to pulse more brightly with light in the presence of living creatures. These fluctuations in illumination from different crystals eerily match the pace of the breathing and rhythm of the heartbeats of those in the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (expert) to create a chain reaction among the crystals by striking them in precise spots that cause them to shatter and harmlessly discharge their energy, or @Check[performance|dc:27] (expert) to use a musical instrument or singing to resonate the crystals and discharge their toxic energy harmlessly
