details.reset: The toxic crystals become dormant once no living creatures are in range, but can activate again as soon as another living creature comes in range.
details.routine:
(1 action) (light, poison) Each round on their action, the toxic crystals emit a shrill humming sound and flash brightly with green light. All creatures in area E14 or within the first 10 feet of either passage leading out of this area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|traits:light,poison|dc:25] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[2d8[poison]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[4d8[poison]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[4d8[poison]] damage, becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1}, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature comes within 20 feet of the green crystals

Effect The crystals pulse with nauseating light. All creatures in area E14 or within the first 10 feet of either passage leading out of this area must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:25] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 3} on a critical failure).

details.description: A raging storm covers the mountain lake as spring water levitates from the lake to fall back down in a driving spiral of steaming rain. In the depths of the storm, barely visible, is a tiny koi egg surrounded by three bands of water.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:27], @Check[religion|dc:27], @Check[nature|dc:27], or @Check[occultism|dc:27] to purify the magic corrupting the ascension ritual, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:30] or @Check[performance|dc:30] to soothe the spirit within the koi egg and give it the strength to fight off the corruption itself; four total successes are required to purify the ritual, each of the first three successes purifying one ring of water and the final one halting the ritual itself. If the cursed comb has not been removed from the ritual site, then succeeding on this final check merely halts the ritual; if the cursed comb has been removed from the ritual site, then succeeding on this final check completes the ascension ritual.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-11: The Godsrain and the Dragon

details.reset: The turbulent koi egg can attempt to hatch again, restarting the ascension ritual, after 1000 years of Xalreonshin's care.
details.routine: (1 action) Torrential water descends from the sky, taking the shape of a dragon that smashes into one creature within 60 feet of the egg. The dragon always targets the creature that is closest to the egg, dealing @Damage[2d10[bludgeoning],2d10[fire]]{2d10 bludgeoning damage and 2d10 fire} damage in a spray of boiling water (@Check[fortitude|dc:26] save). Creatures that fail the save are pushed 10 feet away. Creatures that have the earth trait, are carrying an item with the earth trait, or are otherwise strongly tied to elemental earth (such as an earth elemental sorcerer or kineticist) get the result one degree of success better than the outcome of their save, as the power of elemental earth overcomes water.

items.name: Immune to All Damage (Until Final Ring is Purified)

description.value:
Trigger Xalreonshin rolls initiative, three rounds pass after the PCs begin speaking with Xalreonshin, or a creature other than Xalreonshin comes within 40 feet of the egg

Effect The turbulent koi egg churns the lake surface, ejecting water into the sky. A 1-mile-radius area centered on the egg becomes filled with heavy rain and strong wind imposing a -2 circumstance penalty to Perception checks and requiring flying creatures to [[/act maneuver-in-flight dc=22]].

details.description: Faceless statues struggle against each other to stay above the black dust.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:24|name:Convince the Statues] (expert) to urge the statues to work together, @Check[intimidation|dc:27|traits:action:coerce|name:Coerce the Statues] (expert) to Coerce the statues away, or @Check[occultism|dc:30|name:Oppose Psychic Influence] (trained) to oppose the psychic influence. Each check to disable the haunt is a two-action activity; three total successes are required to disable the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-17: Dreams of a Dustbound Isle

details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: (1 action) On the haunt's initiative, creatures in the area sink deeper into the dust as statues grasp at them. Creatures in the area take @Damage[(2d10+9)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:25|basic|options:inflicts:confused] saving throw). On a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round. Creatures @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] by the haunt take a -1 circumstance penalty to their saving throw.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature spends 1 round in the area

Effect A whispered shout carries through the air: "I have a face! I have a name! My sisters may be forgotten, but I will reclaim what's mine" Sinking statues desperately grasp at creatures in the area, struggling to stay above the surface. The haunt attempts an [[/r 1d20+18 #Grapple]]{Athletics check (with a +18 modifier)} to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple] each creature in the area. On a success, a creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] ([[/act escape dc=28]]). The haunt then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (expert) to sense an oppressive aura of sorrow

details.description: Influenced by the guilt and regrets of the dead, creatures are wrapped in weighted chains that embody their own sins.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:26|name:Disrupt the haunt] (expert) to disrupt the haunt through prayer or @Check[deception|dc:27|name:Openly defy your regrets] (expert) to openly defy your regrets and banish the haunt. Creatures who haven't committed sins or already made amends for their transgressions are immune to the effects of this haunt.
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area

Effect Each creature within 30 feet becomes wrapped in heavy spectral chains forged from its own sins and regrets. Each must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] until it [[/act escape dc=25]]{Escapes}.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is overwhelmed with guilt, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 1 day.

details.description: As the door is opened, unsettling whispering radiates outward, worming its way into the mind of the one who dared intrude.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27] (master) to deface the subtly hidden triggering runes around the door's circumference, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 23) to counteract the trap
details.reset: The trap resets over the course of a round and is ready to whisper at the next person who attempts to open the door 1 round later.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to open the door or damages the door with a Strike or spell

Effect The door whispers into the triggering creature's ear, speaking in one of the following three languages: Shoanti, Thassilonian, or Varisian. If the target creature speaks any of these languages, it hears the whispers in the tongue it is most comfortable speaking. If the target does not speak these languages, the trap's whispers have no effect. If the target understands the whispers, the trap casts suggestion on the target (@Check[will|dc:25] save), suggesting they "stand guard before the door and fight any who attempt to pass by it to enter the chamber beyond until Mistress Liralarue's arrival." Once, the whispers also alerted Liralarue, but as she is long gone now, the PCs don't need to fear the arrival of a powerful runelord's apprentice.

stealth.details: (expert) to note stirrings in the wind

details.description: Hurricane-force winds rip through the area as Agyra flies past.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:27|name:Crouch and Contort] (expert) or @Check[acrobatics|dc:27|name:Crouch and Contort] (expert) to crouch and contort out of the wind's way (disables the hazard for only yourself), @Check[survival|dc:30|name:Point Out a Safe Space] to point out a safe space from the winds for up to 3 creatures.

description.value:
Trigger Agyra flies overhead for at least 120 feet in a straight line

Effect Supernaturally powerful winds batter the area in a line that is @Template[line|distance:120|width:30]{120 feet long and 30 feet wide and 30 feet tall}. Each creature in the line must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:29] save. Moving above ground against the direction of the line is greater difficult terrain for 1 minute.

Critical Success The creature is pushed 10 feet along the line.

Success The creature takes @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]] damage and is pushed 15 along the line.

Failure The creatures takes @Damage[10d6[bludgeoning]] damage, is pushed 30 feet along the line, and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[20d6[bludgeoning]] damage, is pushed 45 feet along the line, is knocked prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} for 1 round.

If the line overlaps a body of water, the winds cause massive waves that deal @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] damage to creatures in the water or within 15 feet of the waterline (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic] save). On a critical failure, a creature is also swept 30 feet away from the waterline and 15 feet beneath the water's surface.

details.description: The hatchet embedded atop the desk wiggles, vibrates, and then flies into the air, accompanied by a rage-filled cry of a woman.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:27|name:Soothe the Haunt's Wrath] to soothe the haunt's wrath or @Check[religion|dc:25|name:Exorcise the Haunt] to exorcise the haunt
details.reset: 24 hours; putting Xarwin's ghost to rest destroys this haunt forever.
details.routine:
(3 actions; evocation, occult) The haunted hatchet uses an action to move to a square adjacent to a creature within 25 feet, attempts a wrathful hatchet Strike against an adjacent creature, or attempts to Escape from being immobilized or restrained. If there are no creatures within 25 feet, the hatchet moves in a random direction.

If it ends its turn and there are no creatures within 25 feet, the hatchet falls to the ground, and the haunt ends.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 5 feet of the hatchet or more than 3 creatures enter area C10

Effect The furious shriek of Asethanna's rage resonates throughout the room, and all creatures within 25 feet of the hatchet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature feels as if the wrathful haunt is particularly enraged at them. The creature becomes frightened 2; their frightened condition doesn't decrease automatically as long as the haunt remains active, and the hatchet gains a +2 circumstance bonus to all wrathful hatchet Strikes it attempts against the creature.

details.description: A wooden crate bearing a chthonic sigil glows with a power contained within it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28] (trained) to open the crate with gradual release of its magical energy, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (2nd rank; counteract DC 24) to dispel the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the sigil on the crate

Effect The incomplete demonic pact causes the crate to explode. Bits of wood and energy from the Outer Rifts deal @Damage[(2d10+11)[bludgeoning],(2d10+11)[spirit]] damage to all creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10 feet} of it (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic]).

details.description: Tumor-like growths erupt from the isle as it responds to psychic attack, spewing black lightning and fire in every direction.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:28|name:Reshape the Dream] (trained) to reshape the substance of the dream, or @Check[performance|dc:32|name:Invoke Desna's Blessing] (trained) or @Check[religion|dc:30|name:Invoke Desna's Blessing] (trained) to invoke Desna's blessing through hymns or prayers. Each check to disable the hazard is a two-action activity; two total successes are required to disable the hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-17: Dreams of a Dustbound Isle

details.routine: (1 action) On the hazard's initiative, a boil erupts from the ground and bursts. This blast strikes as many enemies as possible in a @Template[burst|distance:15] anywhere on the isle, dealing @Damage[5d8[force]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] or @Check[will|dc:26|basic], target's choice) to enemies in the area. Aslynn avoids targeting the nightmares with the blast.

description.value:
Trigger Sarnia's psychic intrusion begins

Effect The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: The gold religious symbol of a former priest of Sarenrae is haunted by the priest's tormented soul.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:28] to bring the spirit to its senses, or @Check[religion|dc:24] (expert) to banish it
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A non-good creature approaches within 10 feet of the religious symbol

Effect The Sarenite summons a burst of spiritual flame in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30-foot radius}. Creatures in the area take @Damage[10d8[spirit]] damage (@Check[will|dc:26|basic]). Creatures who fail this save are also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}, and creatures who critically fail are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for 1 round.

details.description: A spring-loaded blade hidden in the door's handle stabs anyone attempting to turn it, dealing unholy wounds that bleed profusely. Disabling or breaking the trap does not disable or break the lock.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:31] (expert) on the spring mechanism

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

description.value:
A creature tries to unlock or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Pick a Lock]{Pick the Lock}

Effect A hidden blade stabs the triggering creature, making a blade Strike. Then, the trap summons 2 bulbous blood oozes, which attack living creatures.

stealth.details: (trained); @Check[perception|dc:28] (expert) to find the precise location of the steam vents

details.description: Hidden vents emit scalding hot steam made from a particularly acrid solution, irritating the eyes and throat of anyone who inhales it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|name:disable|dc:29] (trained) to block a vent, or @Check[crafting|name:disable|dc:27] (trained) to disable the heating element within the vent that turns the liquid into steam

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-20: The Rakshasa's Court

details.reset: The steam vents cease to operate 1 minute after all creatures have left the area and go into a maintenance cycle for 1 hour. After this time, they can be triggered again.
details.routine: (4 actions) Each of the four steam vents uses a single action each to emit superheated steam, which spreads for 60 feet along the wall from its location and extends out from the wall by 30 feet, dealing @Damage[(2d10+11)[untyped]] damage to each creature in their range (@Check[fortitude|dc:26] save). For each disabled vent, the trap loses 1 action each turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area of the trap

Effect All four vents rapidly heat and let out steam for 5 seconds, filling room with a painfully acrid smell. Each creature in the room must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:26] save, and the trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded].

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 3}, and, struck by a coughing fit, falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

details.description: A 20-foot deep, funnel-shaped pit with dozens of wooden spikes lining its walls.
details.disable: Yanking the cloth away is a simple way to expose the danger to allow someone to merely avoid it by walking around its edges, but it cannot be quickly disabled.
details.reset: Creatures can still fall into the trap once it has been exposed, but the camouflaged cover must be reset manually for the trap to become hidden again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto one of the pits

Effect The triggering creature falls and rolls through several spikes as it slides to the bottom. This inflicts @Damage[5[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage from the fall itself down the steep slope (the falling PC can't attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] due to the lack of defined edges to the pit's walls), but the falling PC must also attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:26|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The character avoids all of the spikes and takes no additional damage.

Success The character takes @Damage[(2d10+11)[piercing]] damage from the spikes.

Failure The character takes @Damage[(4d10+22)[piercing]] damage from the spikes.

Critical Failure The character takes @Damage[(4d10+22)[piercing]] damage from the spikes plus @Damage[2d6[bleed]] damage. While the character is taking this bleed damage, they are also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} from the pain.

details.description: The rubble shifts and the cavern ceiling collapses.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:28] (expert) or @Check[survival|dc:31] (trained) to prop up the cavern ceiling without triggering a collapse.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves the rubble

Effect The cavern collapses in a cave-in that deals @Damage[(4d8+22)[bludgeoning]] damage to the triggering creature and all creatures in the cavern (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save).

details.description: Abyssal fire hits the stone beneath the ramp, causing a 15-foot-long section of it to collapse.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:31] (expert) to stabilize the masonry. Each attempt takes 2 actions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-22: Breaking the Storm: Excising Ruination

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the collapse site

Effect The triggering creature falls off the Colossus and lands on the ground, taking @Damage[50[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage. They can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling (@Check[reflex|dc:24] save).

details.description: Allies appear to shed their disguises and reveal themselves to be malevolent monsters.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:28|name:Confound the Haunt] (expert) twice to confound the haunt with your own deceptions, or @Check[occultism|dc:28|name:Create a Ward Against Influence] (trained) twice to create a ward against the haunt's mental influence
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine:
(1 action; illusion, incapacitation, occult) The haunt continues to confound victims' senses and inspire them to commit violence against each other. Each creature in the haunt's area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is confused for 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more creatures enter the haunt's area

Effect Each creature sees the forms of nearby creatures shift and change, appearing to transform into fiendish or aberrant beings with a thirst for blood. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature sees through the illusions entirely and is temporarily immune to the haunt's routine for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected by the strange images.

Failure The creature believes the illusions to be true; if they become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] by the haunt's routine, they can't attempt flat checks to end the confused condition when they take damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is left with a lingering suspicion of others and can't benefit from Aid reactions for 24 hours.

details.description: A hall's four walls become fluid and elastic when a creature reaches the hall's midpoint.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Stabilize Wall|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to stabilize each wall, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 26) to counteract each wall
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour.
details.routine: (4 actions) This trap loses 1 action each round for each wall that has been disabled. Each wall uses 1 action to slam back and forth rapidly, pummeling one creature in the hall with a wall Strike. On a hit, the target is additionally knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. The walls distribute the attacks as evenly as possible among creatures in the hall, and the Strikes don't apply the trap's multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature reaches the hall's midpoint

Effect The hall's walls lose their rigidity, becoming fluid and elastic. The hall becomes difficult terrain, and each creature in the hall is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] unless they succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:26] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Discordant music, dance, and poetry combine to build a deep suspicion and distrust of strangers.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:28] to counteract the performance or @Check[religion|dc:28] or @Check[occultism|dc:28] to disrupt the spiritual energy. Two total successes are needed to disable the trap. The mechanical head can automatically disable the trap with two uses of Inspired Recitation.
details.reset: The hazard stops when there are no conscious creatures in area E3 and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine:
(1 action; emotion, fear, mental, occult) The echoes continue to reverberate, tearing at those in the area and filling them with distrust of others. All creatures in the area take @Damage[(2d10+11)[mental]] damage and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:28|traits:emotion,fear,mental,occult,damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and is immune to the effects for 1 minute.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} by feelings of distrust and anxiety.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more characters enter area E3 and make any sound at all

Effect Sounds or speech are amplified and garbled into a terrible, shrieking cry. All creatures in the area take @Damage[(2d10+11)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:28|basic] save). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A loved one, tied to a post with thick ropes, suddenly lurches to life and begins to shriek in anguish as soon as one of the PCs comes within 20 feet.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:31] (expert) to remove the small runes carved on the pole that focus the magic, or @Check[occultism|dc:28] (trained) to recognize the occult illusions woven into the trap and disable the underlying magic
details.reset: A cruel welcome trap resets at midnight.
details.routine:
(1 action; illusion, mental, occult, visual) The cruel welcome's head lifts, and it gazes at the closest PC within 20 feet; if there are no PCs in range, the trap does nothing, its turn ends, and it deactivates until midnight. The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26|traits:illusion,mental,occult,visual] save.

Critical Success The PC is unaffected. For the next 24 hours, the PC gains a +4 circumstance bonus to all saving throws against cruel welcome traps.

Success The PC recognizes the face of the tormented figure on the pole as that of a loved one, and the anguish of the sight results in @Damage[4d10[mental]] damage. For the next 24 hours, the PC gains a +4 circumstance bonus to all saving throws against cruel welcome traps.

Failure As success, but @Damage[(4d10+22)[mental]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but @Damage[(8d10+44)[mental]] damage, and the PC becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours.

description.value:
Trigger A PC approaches within 20 feet of the trap

Effect The rag-wrapped figure cries out in pain. The triggering PC must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A magical pipe organ for controlling and bolstering the undead. Creatures that are trained in Performance can spend one action to attempt to play a tune.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Stopper the Pipes SC|traits:magical,necromancy] (expert) to stopper the pipes. Each pipe stoppered imparts a stacking -1 circumstance penalty to Performance checks with the organ. Once four pipes have been stoppered, the organ ceases to function.

description.value:
Trigger An adjacent creature attempts to play the organ

Effect The performer makes a @Check[performance|dc:24|name:Play the Dead Organ Keyboard|traits:magical,necromancy,keyboard] check.

Critical Success As success, and all other creatures who attempt to perform on the dead organ suffer a -1 status penalty to Play a Tune for 1 round.

Success Creatures within 100 feet of the organ are subjected to one of the following effects, as chosen by the performer:

  • Bolster (auditory, necromancy): Mindless undead within range gain a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 round.
  • Dance (auditory, move, necromancy): Mindless undead within range Stride up to 15 feet in the same cardinal direction. All targets move the same distance. If impeded, they move as far as possible, then stop.
  • Discordant Blast (auditory, evocation, sonic): All living creatures within 10 feet of the organ except the performer suffer @Damage[(2d8+9)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:24|basic|name:Discordant Blast|traits:auditory,evocation,magical,sonic,necromancy] save). On a failure or critical failure, a creature is also pushed 15 feet away from the organ.
  • Swing (attack, auditory, flourish, necromancy): Mindless undead within range make a Strike in the same relative cardinal direction. This Strike doesn't count toward the undead creatures' multiple attack penalty, and their multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike.
  • Weaken (auditory, necromancy): Mindless undead within range suffer a -1 status penalty to attack and damage rolls for 1 round.

Critical Failure The player alone experiences the effects of a discordant blast.

details.description: The skull of a defeated demilich is haunted by souls of the demilich's victims.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:26], @Check[occultism|dc:26], @Check[nature|dc:26], or @Check[religion|dc:26] to counteract the skull's Broken Gems
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the skull

Effect The spirits haunting the skull activate one of the skull's ruined gemstones, casting one of the following four spells determined randomly:

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cast into Time], @Check[fortitude|dc:26|traits:divination]
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Chain Lightning], @Check[reflex|dc:26|traits:electricity,evocation|basic] (@Damage[8d12[electricity]] damage)
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Fireball] (6th), @Check[reflex|dc:26|traits:fire,evocation|basic] (@Damage[12d6[fire]] damage)
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Spellwrack], @Check[will|dc:26|traits:abjuration,curse,force] (@Damage[2d12[persistent,force]] or @Damage[(2*2d12)[persistent,force]] damage)

The saving throw DC for any spell is 26.

details.description: A locked, wrought iron fence with bars spaced inches apart is magically electrified.
details.disable: Crafting or @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) to disconnect the fence from its magitech power source

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap automatically resets. If deactivated or disabled, the trap doesn't reset.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the fence directly or with a tool or weapon

Effect The fence deals @Damage[6d12[electricity]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic]).

details.description: Passing over an invisible rune on the floor between the north and south doors triggers a dancing burst of electricity.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Bleed away trap energy|traits:complex,magical,trap] (expert), @Check[arcana|dc:28|name:Bleed away trap energy|traits:complex,magical,trap] (expert), or @Check[occultism|dc:28|name:Bleed away trap energy|traits:complex,magical,trap] (expert) to harmlessly bleed away the electrical energy from the rune. Once the trap has been activated, the electrical energy is stronger, so three successful checks (of any combination of the relevant skills) are necessary to deactivate it, but these checks can be attempted from anywhere in the room.

publication.title: Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind

details.reset: The pulses stop as soon as no creatures are in the hallway, and then reset after 1 hour.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap deals @Damage[4d10[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|name:Avoid Electrical Shock|traits:complex,magical,trap,arcane,electricity,evocation] save) to all creatures in the room. A creature that fails the save is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2} on a critical failure). A creature that isn't touching the water treats the result of its saving throw as one degree of success better.

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes over the invisible underwater rune between the north and south doors

Effect The trap deals @Damage[4d10[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|name:Avoid Electrical Shock|traits:complex,magical,trap] save) to creatures touching the water, and then rolls initiative.

details.description: A raging storm covers the mountain lake as spring water levitates from the lake to fall back down in a driving spiral of steaming rain. In the depths of the storm, barely visible, is a tiny koi egg surrounded by three bands of water.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:28], @Check[religion|dc:28], @Check[nature|dc:28], or @Check[occultism|dc:28] to purify the magic corrupting the ascension ritual, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:31] or @Check[performance|dc:31] to soothe the spirit within the koi egg and give it the strength to fight off the corruption itself; four total successes are required to purify the ritual, each of the first three successes purifying one ring of water and the final one halting the ritual itself. If the cursed comb has not been removed from the ritual site, then succeeding on this final check merely halts the ritual; if the cursed comb has been removed from the ritual site, then succeeding on this final check completes the ascension ritual.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-11: The Godsrain and the Dragon

details.reset: The turbulent koi egg can attempt to hatch again, restarting the ascension ritual, after 1000 years of Xalreonshin's care.
details.routine: (1 action) Torrential water descends from the sky, taking the shape of a dragon that smashes into one creature within 60 feet of the egg. The dragon always targets the creature that is closest to the egg, dealing @Damage[(2d10+2)[bludgeoning],2d10[fire]]{2d10+2 bludgeoning damage and 2d10 fire} damage in a spray of boiling water (@Check[fortitude|dc:28] save). Creatures that fail the save are pushed 10 feet away. Creatures that have the earth trait, are carrying an item with the earth trait, or are otherwise strongly tied to elemental earth (such as an earth elemental sorcerer or kineticist) get the result one degree of success better than the outcome of their save, as the power of elemental earth overcomes water.

items.name: Immune to All Damage (Until Final Ring is Purified)

description.value:
Trigger Xalreonshin rolls initiative, three rounds pass after the PCs begin speaking with Xalreonshin, or a creature other than Xalreonshin comes within 40 feet of the egg

Effect The turbulent koi egg churns the lake surface, ejecting water into the sky. A 1-mile-radius area centered on the egg becomes filled with heavy rain and strong wind imposing a -2 circumstance penalty to Perception checks and requiring flying creatures to [[/act maneuver-in-flight dc=24]].

details.description: Vicious spirits of the two owlbears, Eirikk, and the rest of the bandits rise and resume their final battle.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:24] (expert) three times to empathize with the bestial spirits that comprise the haunt and to calm them, or @Check[religion|dc:28] (expert) two times to exorcise the haunt and disperse its energy.
details.reset: The haunt resets after 24 hours; it can be permanently destroyed if it is either disabled or destroyed after the Beast itself has been slain.
details.routine: (3 actions; emotion, fear, mental) The spectral violence churns and flashes through the room, targeting three separate creatures with ghostly claws, beaks, swords, and arrows. At the end of the haunt's turn, it takes its Spectral Uprising reaction.

description.value: @Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

Trigger More than three Small or larger living creatures are in area B7 at the end of a turn.

Effect Spectral figures of the bandits and owlbears manifest in a sudden and overwhelming battle of endless violence. All living creatures in area B7 must make a successful @Check[will|dc:28] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure).

details.description: Moving too quickly through the bed of pods causes them to shudder, emit eerie gurgling sounds, and then burst.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:28] (expert) (4 checks) to slice through all four of the larger shared root systems to render the dangerous pods inert, or inflict enough damage to the pods on the first attempt to reach the hazard's BT (damage that doesn't reach the hazard's broken threshold causes it to trigger)

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature moves at normal speed through the area, or the area takes less than 33 points of damage from any source

Effect The pods explode into a burst of toxic foliage, mold, and poisonous vapors. All creatures in area C3 must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:28] save, while all creatures in the ten-foot-wide tunnel leading south must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:24] save.

Critical Success The character takes no damage.

Success The character takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning],1d10[poison]] damage from the explosion.

Failure The character takes @Damage[(2d10+11)[bludgeoning],2d10[poison]] damage from the explosion and also takes @Damage[1d10[persistent,poison]] damage as the toxins in the fungus and vapor continue to work in the character's flesh. Being restored to full hit points automatically ends this persistent poison damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the character is also affected by Lamashtu's bloom and automatically starts at Stage 1 of the affliction, bypassing the regular onset period entirely.

While some NPCs become afflicted by Lamashtu's bloom curse during Parts 1 and 2 of this chapter, during Part 3 the PCs may be exposed to it as well. While the bloom is itself a curse, it functions mechanically more like a disease, and as such features both of these affliction traits. As long as the curse persists, a creature cannot recover from the disease, and removing the curse does not automatically remove the disease element of the affliction. Both elements must be dealt with individually before a victim suffering from Lamashtu's Bloom is safe. As with all elements of this curse, slaying the bloom of Lamashtu in area C10 automatically removes this affliction.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:23]

Onset 1 week

Stage 1 no ill effects (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 enfeebled 1 and @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage (8 hours)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] and @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage (10 minutes)

Stage 5 death

Special a creature that dies from Lamashtu's bloom explodes in a blast of First World foliage, releasing a monster. This creature is typically a low-intelligence beast or dragon between 7th and 9th level, depending on the GM's whim.

hp.details: to destroy the bricks and disable the trap;

stealth.details: (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:31] (master) to notice the cold fog rising from the floor

details.description: The white brick floor radiates ice-cold air.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:24] (expert) to erase a rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 24) to dispel a rune; all three runes must be erased or dispelled to disable the trap.
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (3 actions) With each action, the trap attempts a frost jet Strike against any creature standing on the white bricks. The trap loses 1 action per disabled or dispelled rune.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the white bricks

Effect The trap makes a frost jet Strike against the triggering creature and rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

A creature that takes persistent cold damage from the trap's frost jet Strike must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|options:inflicts:clumsy,inflicts:fatigued] save.

Critical Success The creature isn't affected and is immune to this frostbite effect for 24 hours.

Success The creature isn't affected.

Failure The creature can't stop shivering and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1}.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued].

details.description: A spirit musician rises out of an instrument and plays a frantic, bone-shaking melody.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:26|name:Bring the music to a close|traits:action:perform] (trained) to bring the spirit's music to a close or @Check[occultism|dc:28|name:Banish the spirit] check (expert) to banish the spirit
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the instrument

Effect The musician's bone-shaking musical performance deals @Damage[(4d10+22)[sonic]] damage to creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} (@Check[will|dc:30|basic]).

details.description: Religious sigils of Scal carefully carved into the doorframe capture Tokainen's suppressed anger and frustration, unleashing it in a destructive explosion when a creature attempts to open the door.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (expert) to scratch out the religious iconography, @Check[religion|dc:24] (trained) to pray for safe passage, @Check[diplomacy|dc:28] (expert) to soothe the emotions powering the magical ward, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 24) to counteract the magical energy

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap resets after 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the door directly or with a tool; Bypass key

Effect The trap explodes in a 15-foot radius burst, dealing @Damage[5d10[fire]] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save). Creatures damaged by the trap must additionally succeed at a @Check[will|dc:26] save (against a mental effect), becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 minute on a failure.

details.description: The xulgath corpse splits open to release a cloud of tiny ectoplasmic crystals which, when they are inhaled, disorient and devitalize living creatures.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:26|name:Render the cloud inert|traits:hazard,environmental] or @Check[occultism|dc:26|name:Render the cloud inert|traits:hazard,environmental] (expert) to render the crystal cloud inert

publication.title: Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God

details.reset: The crystals regrow into a number sufficient to form another dangerous cloud after 1 week.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches or disturbs the corpse containing the growth of crystals

Effect All creatures within 10 feet of the corpse are exposed to the ghost crystal cloud.

Creatures with the ethereal trait are immune

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:26|traits:environmental,hazard]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d10[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d10[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[3d10[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 3} (1 round)

details.description: Among the many mirrored reflections lurk three trapped souls. By attacking reflections, these spirits cause wounds to appear on the bodies of those reflected in the mirror. If a trapped soul kills its victim, the soul escapes the mirror, replaced by its victim's soul.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:24] (expert) to realign a mirror so a trapped soul can't hurt the reflections, @Check[religion|dc:28] (expert) to banish a trapped soul, or @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) to cover a mirror quickly enough to keep the trapped soul inside it; each successful attempt to disable the haunt reduces its number of actions by 1
details.routine: (3 actions) Each trapped soul makes a reflective rip Strike against the reflection of a creature in the room; each trapped soul must attack a different target.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more creatures enter the room

Effect Three trapped souls appear in mirrors throughout the room, menacing the reflections of the triggering creatures. The triggering creatures must each succeed at a @Check[will|dc:28] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

A creature damaged by the haunt's reflective ripple Strike is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} until 1 minute after it leaves the room. This enfeebled condition is cumulative, up to enfeebled 4. If the creature dies from reflective rip, its soul is contained within the mirror until the mirror is broken and a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.Specter] emerges.

details.description: Ethereal hands stretch out from the walls and floor, attempting to restrain nearby creatures.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:30|name:Exorcise Spirits] (master) to exorcise the spirits or @Check[intimidation|dc:32|name:Scare Away the Hands|traits:action:demoralize] (expert) to scare away the hands

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the area marked on the map

Effect Misty blue hands stretch out from the hallway's stone tiles and grasp at the triggering creature, which must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:26|traits:damaging-effect] save to avoid becoming hindered or harmed by the ethereal hands.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2} for 1 round and takes @Damage[(2d10+10)[void]] damage.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2} for 1 round and takes @Damage[(4d10+15)[void]] damage.

details.description: A 15-foot wide area of verdant-covered water attempts to suck creatures down into it.
details.disable: Two @Check[survival|dc:28] (trained) checks to find roots and tree limbs sturdy enough for everyone to use as escape paths ends the hazard's threat to all creatures in the area

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-99: Blessings of the Forest

details.reset: The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it accumulates leaf and greenery cover over the course of 24 hours.
details.routine:
(one action) On its initiative, the bog pulls down each creature within it. A creature that was submerged to its waist becomes submerged to its neck, and a creature that was submerged up to its neck is pulled under and must hold its breath to avoid suffocation.

A creature partially or fully submerged in the bog can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:26|traits:action:swim] check to Swim to either raise itself by one step (pulled under to submerged to its neck, or submerged to its neck to submerged to its waist), or to escape from the bog if it's submerged only up to its waist. This DC increases to @Check[athletics|dc:28|traits:action:swim] for creatures submerged up to their neck or worse. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down one step. Other creatures can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Aid], typically by using a rope or similar assistance (providing an automatic success), or attempt to pull a single affected creature out with their own Athletics check, with the same results as if the aiding creature attempted the check itself.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area of the bog.

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the bog up to its waist. The bog rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

details.description: Pebbles form into a hand the size of an ox, which reaches to crush anyone in the water.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28 (expert) to pick apart the pebbles, or @Check[warfare-lore|dc:24] (trained) to recognize and recreate the battle

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: 24 hours

description.value:
Trigger Three or more creatures enter the submerged passage

Effect The hand reenacts its final battle against the invading demon. It makes a crushing power Strike against all creatures in the passage.

details.description: The illusion of a normal, if dirty, pond is broken by clammy hands reaching up from the depths. While they never break the surface, travelers feel compelled to reach for the grasping fingers and willingly walk to their doom.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:28], @Check[diplomacy|dc:28] or @Check[religion|dc:28] (expert) to calm the spirits; two successes required, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 28) to dispel the magic.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-14: Twice in Steel

details.reset: When there are no living humanoids within 30 feet of the pond, the hands recede into the depths and the pond resets.
details.routine: (1 action) The pond attempts to Grab all PCs in the water with a [[/gmr 1d20+18 #Athletics]]{+18} Athletics bonus. Any @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] PCs take @Damage[(4d10+22)[void]|traits:unholy] (unholy) damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] save). All PCs within 30 feet must then make a save against Call of the Depths.

description.value:
Trigger A creature is within 30 feet of the Horrible Pond

Effect All PCs must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save, with results as described below. The pond then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The PC is unaffected

Success The PC feels compelled to reach for the grasping hands, but is otherwise unaffected.

Failure The PC is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} until the end of the pond's next turn and must use their first action on their next turn to move half their speed toward the pond.

Critical Failure The PC is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Controlled] and must use all their actions to walk into the pond, or swim in the pond if they are already in the water.

details.description: The sigils on the door's frame glow with an amber light, which collects in a facet over the center of the door.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:22] (trained) to strategically destroy the runes, @Check[thievery|dc:24] to disrupt the beam from collecting the facet, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank, counteract DC 24) to dispel the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: The Hostile Architecture resets after being exposed to the light of a blue moon.
details.routine: a The hostile architecture fires its aversion beam on a creature that is sanctified holy. If there are no sanctified holy creatures, it instead fires its beam on the nearest non-demon creature.

description.value:
Trigger A non-demon creature comes within 15 feet of the hostile architecture

Effect The hostile architecture shines the aversion beam on the triggering creature, and then rolls initiative. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save.

Critical Success The target is immune to the trap for 1 minute.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing].

details.description: A stretch of fence is electrified by a hidden Stasian coil.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Disconnect the coil from the fence|traits:electricity,hazard,trap,mechanical] (expert) to carefully disconnect the coil from the fence or @Check[crafting|dc:30|name:Jury-rig a grounding device|traits:electricity,hazard,trap,mechanical] (master) to jury-rig a grounding device to nullify the shock
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the fence directly or with a tool or weapon

Effect The fence deals @Damage[7d12[electricity]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|traits:electricity,mechanical,trap,hazard] save).

details.description:
Variant confounding betrayal

Allies appear to shed their skin, transforming into either giant worm monsters or masses of wriggling worms.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) twice to suppress the trap's trigger, or @Check[occultism|dc:28] (expert) to create a ward against the trap's mental effects.

publication.title: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm

details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine:
(1 action, illusion, incapacitation, occult) The haunt continues to confound victims' senses and inspire them to commit violence against each other. Each creature in the haunt's area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26].

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is confused for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is confused for 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more creatures enter the haunt's area.

Effect Each creature sees the forms of nearby creatures shift and change, appearing to transform into fiendish or aberrant beings with a thirst for blood. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save.

Critical Success The creature sees through the illusions entirely and is temporarily immune to the haunt's routine for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected by the strange images.

Failure The creature believes the illusions to be true; if they become confused by the haunt's routine, they can't attempt flat checks to end the confused condition when they take damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is left with a lingering suspicion of others and can't benefit from Aid reactions for 24 hours.

hp.details: Iron Maiden Hardness 12; Iron Maiden HP 46 (BT 23)

details.description: A pressure plate hidden on the floor before an iron maiden causes the torture device to snap shut and trap the triggering creature inside.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Disable Pressure Plate|traits:action:disable-a-device] to disable the pressure plate
details.reset: The pressure plate must be reset manually.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps onto the trap's pressure plate (indicated on the map)

Effect The front of the iron maiden slams shut with incredible force and locks itself. The triggering creature takes @Damage[(4d10+10)[piercing],2d6[bleed]]{4d10+10 piercing damage and 2d6 persistent bleed damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|options:inflicts:immobilized] save; if the creature critically succeeds, they avoid the trap completely). The triggering creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized]. The victim can end their immobilization only once a creature outside the iron maiden unlocks the device (requiring two successful DC 28 Thievery checks to [[/act disable-device dc=28]]) or breaks the iron maiden, after which the trapped creature must succeed at a DC 25 check to [[/act escape dc=25]] the trap's spike-lined interior.

details.description: The smells and sights of fresh baked goods compel those who experience them to take part in a grisly feast.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:28] (trained) to draw upon memories of leaner times and counteract the temptation of the promised meal or @Check[religion|dc:31] (expert) to exorcise the haunt; 2 successes are required to disable it, and once the haunt is disabled, the food on the table is revealed to be foul, rotting, and moldy.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates at the start of any round in which there are no living creatures in area D2, then reactivates 1 minute later.
details.routine:
(1 action; mental) On its initiative, the haunt exudes mental compulsions to all living creatures in the room, driving them to take part in the offered feast. Each living creature in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save with the following results. Food consumed during this hazard's routine is automatically replenished at the start of the next round.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is compelled to approach the table of food and be seated. On the creature's turn, it Strides as often as needed to reach the table, then Interacts with a chair to be seated. If it has any remaining actions, it can use them normally (such as by Interacting again to stand up from being seated at the table).

Failure As success, but the creature must spend at least one of its actions this turn (if any are available after potentially approaching and taking a seat) Interacting with the food to eat some of it. Eating the food afflicts the creature with Rancid Breakfast.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature must use any actions not used to approach the table or be seated at it to Interact and eat food.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters area D2

Effect The sight and scent of the baked goods grows nearly irresistible. The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:26|options:inflicts:stupefied] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure) for 24 hours. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:26] (this increases to @Check[fortitude|dc:28] if a creature spent two actions eating or @Check[fortitude|dc:30] if a creature spent three actions eating)

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} (1 round) and @Damage[1d6[poison]]

Stage 2 clumsy 1 (1 hour) and @Damage[2d6[poison]]

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} (24 hours) and @Damage[4d6[poison]]

Stage 4 clumsy 2 (unlimited duration) and @Damage[6d6[poison]]

hp.details: moderate cover behind the bars from creatures outside the cell

details.description: The skeletal remains in the cells clatter up to their feet and reach out toward visitors as they call out, inviting them to embrace the addiction of imprisonment.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:26] (trained) to trick one of the skeletons into thinking they're free, or @Check[religion|dc:23] (expert) to exorcise a haunted skeleton; each success disables one of the four haunted skeletons.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates at the end of any round in which there are no living creatures in the room. It then resets 1 minute later.
details.routine:
(4 actions) Each of the four skeletons targets a living creature in area C37, reaches their arms out toward the target, and in a mournful yet strangely compelling voice invites the target to join them in imprisonment forever, promising blissful enlightenment in return for an eternity spent behind bars. The skeletons attempt to target a different living creature, but if there are more skeletons than targets, they randomly target identical creatures. Targeted creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26|options:damaging-effect,inflicts:paralyzed,inflicts:slowed,inflicts:stupefied] save, with a cumulative status penalty of –1 for each additional skeleton targeting them (to a maximum penalty of –3 if all four skeletons target a single creature), and the following results. This is an incapacitation and mental effect.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is tempted to remain in the room, takes @Damage[2d6[mental]] damage, and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} until the end of its next turn.

Failure The creature is compelled to remain in the room, takes @Damage[5d6[mental]] damage, and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] until the end of its next turn.

Critical Failure The creature is compelled to remain in the room, takes @Damage[10d6[mental]] damage, becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} with an unlimited duration, and becomes paralyzed until the end of its next turn.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature ends their turn inside of area C37

Effect The skeletons rise up in a horrifying clatter. All living creatures in area C37 must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26|options:inflicts:paralyzed] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] for 1 round (or 1 minute on a critical failure, with a new save at the end of their turn to recover). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: The haze conceals a rune that summons a krooth in front of the door to area B10.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:24|name:Approach without triggering the trap|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Erase the rune|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap] (expert) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 24) to counteract the rune.

publication.title: Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God

details.reset: The trap resets after 24 hours.

description.value:
Trigger A non-xulgath, non-demon creature that isn't carrying a religious symbol of Zevgavizeb crosses from the north part of the hall to the south part of the hall

Effect The trap summons a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Krooth]. The krooth rolls initiative and remains for [[/br 2d6 #rounds]]{2d6 rounds}, after which the spell ends and the krooth disappears. The krooth also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. Unlike most summoned creatures, the krooth can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions.

details.description: A rune hidden in the door's decorative carvings delivers a telepathic jolt, draining will and muddling the mind.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (expert) to scratch out the rune without triggering a mental assault, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 24) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap resets after 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the door directly or with a tool; Bypass key

Effect The trap deals @Damage[6d10[mental]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[will|dc:26|basic] save). On a failure, the target is additionally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 3} on a critical failure).

details.description: Invisible but highly flammable natural gas has built up beneath the ground, where geological pressure can ignite it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:28|name:Diffuse the Flames|traits:environmental,hazard] to dig around the vent in a way that diffuses the fumes

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 5 feet of the flue

Effect A fiery geyser of flaming gas explodes from the ground. Creatures within 5 feet of the flue take @Damage[4d10[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic]); creatures that fail the save also take @Damage[4d10[persistent,fire]] damage.

details.description: This mushroom ring forms a portal to the First World, allowing the Thorned Monarch's reinforcements into the Material Plane. When the PCs approach, a creature emerges. Creatures continue to emerge until the mushroom ring is disabled. Disabling the mushroom ring also causes any creatures that have emerged to disappear.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:24|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) or @Check[nature|dc:28] (expert) to rearrange the mushrooms to disrupt the portal (two actions) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank, counteract DC 26) to counteract the magical properties; three successes are required to disable the portal

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-00: The King in Thorns

details.routine: (1 Action) A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Elananx] emerges from the mushroom ring and defends it. All elananxes use the mushroom ring's initiative.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs approach the mushroom ring

Effect An @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Elananx] emerges from the portal to defend it, and the hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: A metal lattice forged from warshards feeds into a dark purplish crystal that pulses with energy. If the configuration of the lattice changes, so does the energy the device emits.
details.disable: Three [[/act disable-device dc=27]] (see Disabling the Focus under Event 1 on page 18).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-17: The Devil in the Details

details.routine: (3 actions) The Nahyndrian Focus uses Select Condition to determine which condition it is going to inflict. It then uses Inflict twice, usually on two different targets. If the focus is broken, it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1}, and can only Inflict once per turn.

description.value:
Trigger Yollen activates the Nahyndrian Focus

Effect The Nahyndrian Focus shifts into attack mode and rolls initiative. All creatures without the unholy trait in within @Template[emanation|distance:60]{60 feet} must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:31|basic] save against @Damage[(4d10+22)[spirit]] damage.

The Nahyndrian Focus rolls a [[/gmr 1d4 #Condition]] to randomly decide whether it is going cause the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled], or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied] condition when it uses Inflict until the end of its turn. After the first round, it changes the random roll die to [[/gmr 1d6 #Condition]] as it does not pick the same condition it had in the previous round.

An unhealthy beam shoots out from the focus. One creature without the unholy trait within 60 feet of the Focus must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:31] save.

Critical Success The target becomes immune to Inflict for 1 minute.

Success The targe gains the condition from Select Condition with a value of 1 for 1 minute.

Failure As success, but with a value of 2.

Critical Failure As success, but with a value of 3.

details.description: The voices of the nameless dead intrude into the minds of the living.
details.disable: @Check[perception|dc:26] to discern a name spoken by the nameless dead, or @Check[religion|dc:28] (expert) to put the nameless dead to rest. Speaking three names aloud disables the haunt. A creature that speaks any other name aloud must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:28] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round.
details.reset: If the haunt is disabled, divine light washes through the area. Living creatures in the area regain @Damage[4d8[healing]] Hit Points, and undead creatures in the area take @Damage[4d8[vitality]] damage. The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: (1 action; auditory, divine, emotion, fear, mental) Spirits of the dead whisper their names into the minds of creatures in the room, dealing @Damage[(2d10+11)[mental]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[will|dc:28|basic|options:area-effect] save). A creature that fails the save is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the room

Effect An illusory wall appears across the room's entrance. A creature who succeeds at @Check[perception|dc:30] check disbelieves the illusion, allowing them to leave the area. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: A hidden rune on the floor under the filth, just past the threshold, triggers an ersatz ghost.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Remove Rune|traits:magical,trap] (expert) to remove the rune without triggering it, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 26) to dispel the rune
details.reset: The trap automatically resets after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature moves over the rune

Effect A frightful moan echoes through the room, and a hoarse telepathic voice shouts, "Get out!"

This shout deals @Damage[6d12[mental]] damage to creatures in the room (@Check[will|dc:26|basic] save). Creatures who fail this saving throw must immediately leave the room and can't willingly reenter it for 1 minute (1 hour on a critical failure).

details.description: Magical symbols carved in the doorframe magically ward this door; anyone attempting to pick the lock is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] and wracked with pain. Disabling or breaking the trap does not disable or break the lock.
details.disable:
@Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) to scratch out the magical symbols, @Check[arcana|dc:28] (expert) to safely siphon the magical energy, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 26)

Bypass Anyone other than Mirida turns the door handle three times to the right and once to the left in quick succession.

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap automatically resets. It doesn't reset if the trap is disabled.

description.value:
Trigger A creature tries to unlock or Pick the Lock

Effect The trap deals the triggering creature @Damage[(2d10+11)[mental]] damage (nonlethal) (@Check[will|dc:30|basic] save) and @Damage[1d6[persistent,mental]] damage (nonlethal). A creature that fails their save is additionally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] for as long as they suffer the persistent mental damage.

details.description: When any creature other than a devil enters the light, the magic interwoven in the light holds the creature in place and rings an alarm.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Distort or Diffuse the Light|traits:magical,trap] (master) to distort or diffuse the light, keeping the light cone intact but preventing the trap from triggering, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 26) to dispel the light, leaving the room in darkness
details.reset: The stasis magic in the light builds up over the course of an hour, after which the trap can trigger again.
details.routine: (1 action) All @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] creatures in the room take @Damage[1d10[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:26|basic|traits:magical,trap] save). Creatures who fail also become paralyzed for 1 round but can use a reaction to mentally fight off this stasis; creatures who do so take @Damage[5d10[mental]] damage mental damage but are no longer paralyzed.

description.value:
Trigger A non-devil creature moves into the light

Effect The light expands to fill the room, and each nondevil creature within the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save. A high-pitched chime sounds in the barracks (area D15), audible in this room as well. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for [[/gmr 1d4 #Duration Stupefied]]{1d4 rounds} and paralyzed for 1 round.

hp.details: to destroy the control panel and disable the trap

stealth.details: (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:31|traits:action:seek] (master) to notice the control panel

details.description: Countless holes to launch poison darts from line a long hallway with a hidden control panel on the far end.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Disable Trap (Control Panel)|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) on the control panel deactivates the trap.
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap launches one dart against every creature in the gallery as 1 action. Because it launches darts continuously, the trap can also use the Continuous Barrage free action (see below) to launch darts at each creature during that creature's turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the hallway or ends its turn in the hallway

Effect The trap makes a poisoned dart Strike against the triggering creature, then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

Trigger A creature within the active gallery finishes an action

Effect The trap makes a poisoned dart Strike against the triggering creature.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 3} (1 round)

details.description: A fast-acting toxin that's almost invisible to the eye coats the first and seventh skull on this display shelf.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:31] to identify the toxin and delicately wipe it free before burning the tainted cloth; on a critical success, the character can pinpoint two areas where the skull can be safely carried and, perhaps, used as an improvised thrown weapon to poison a creature.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes in contact with the skull

Effect The contact poison turns the exposed flesh a deep scarlet color that persists for 24 hours and also afflicts the creature with nettleweed residue.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:27|traits:poison]

Onset 1 minute

Maximum Duration 6 minutes

Stage 1 @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage (1 minute)

Stage 2 @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage (1 minute)

Stage 3 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage (1 minute)

details.description: Flowers carved into the door emit colorful pollen when the door is touched.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (expert) to deface the flower carvings, @Check[nature|dc:25] (expert) to neutralize the pollen, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 26) to counteract the conjuration
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger The door is touched, or the lock is opened

Effect The flowers spray poisonous pollen in a @Template[cone|distance:30]. A creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save, with the following effect.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+11)[poison]] damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2}.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(3d10+16)[poison]] damage and @Damage[1d10[persistent,poison]] damage, becomes enfeebled 2, and takes a –2 status penalty to Perception checks for [[/gmr 1d4 #hours]]{1d4 hours}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d10+22)[poison]] damage and @Damage[1d10[persistent,poison]] damage, becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 4}, and takes a –2 status penalty to Perception checks and saves against poison for 24 hours.

stealth.details: or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Detect Magic] to notice the heavy divine magic in the area

details.description: Three daeodon corpses, animated and enraged by Sezelrian's divine intervention, trample anything within the ritual circle.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:24] or @Check[religion|dc:24] to disrupt the magic animating the spirits, @Check[athletics|dc:28] to hold down one of the daeodons, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th-rank, counteract DC 28). 3 successes are required.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-15: Cleansing the Flame

details.reset: The hazard resets within 24 hours if the cultists of Sezelrian still remain in the area.
details.routine: (three actions) The animated daeodons trample all creatures within the circle, dealing @Damage[(2d10+11)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area of the ritual circle

Effect The outline of the circle alights with fire dealing @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage to all creatures within it (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Ominous storm clouds gather overhead, pulsating with flashes of blue-white lightning.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23] (trained) to petition Gozreh for safe passage or @Check[arcana|dc:28], @Check[nature|dc:28], or @Check[occultism|dc:28] (expert) to disperse the storm's magical energies. A PC whose voice is exceptionally loud (such as using the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Bullhorn] cantrip) can also attempt to Disable this hazard with a @Check[diplomacy|dc:25] check to convince the Gozren priests that they mean no harm; the priests make a second prayer to dissipate the hazard on the following round, though they're unable to stop the sargassum heaps.
details.reset: The storm clouds disperse once no creature other than those designated by Katlanthe are atop the plateau.
details.routine:
(1 action) (divine, electricity) A bolt of lightning strikes a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] atop the plateau of the GM's choosing. Creatures in the area take @Damage[(2d10+9)[electricity]|traits:divine|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|traits:divine,electricity] save). On a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}. Creatures designated by Katlanthe within the area are infused with divine energy for 1 round. They gain the electricity trait and immunity to electricity damage, and all their attacks deal an additional 1d6 electricity damage.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Rage of the Sky Father]

description.value:
Trigger A creature not designated by Katlanthe approaches within 10 feet of the circle of stone columns

Effect The storm clouds release a boom of thunder. Creatures atop the plateau take @Damage[(2d10+9)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:26|basic] save). On a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: Riftcarver is the chthonic artifact weapon of Deskari, the slain demon lord of the locust host and plague of the Worldwound (now known as the Sarkoris Scar). Unfettered from its creator, it now wreaks havoc on the enemies of its current master, Hesla Embersplitter.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:24] (expert), @Check[demon-lore|dc:24] (trained), or @Check[religion|dc:24] (expert) to remind Riftcarver that it should not serve an interloper; @Check[intimidation|dc:28] (trained), @Check[nature|dc:28] (trained), or @Check[occultism|dc:28] (trained) to temporarily make Riftcarver's spirit cower; @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank, counteract DC 25); three total successes are required to disable the hazard. The first time Riftcarver is taken away from Hesla Embersplitter against her will (via disarm, a spell, or some other method), it also counts as one success. If Hesla Embersplitter is rendered @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] or killed, the hazard immediately ends.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-20: The Overthrow of Ambition

details.routine: (three actions) Riftcarver lives up to its name, using all three of its actions to carve a new rift in a random direction (rolling [[/gmr 1d8]] to determine that direction). Hesla Embersplitter is immune to the effects of these rifts while wielding Riftcarver.

description.value:
Trigger An enemy of Hesla Embersplitter rolls for initiative
