Effect Riftcarver calls out with thousands of voices to infest the minds of the righteous. Any creature within 60 feet with the holy trait must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:30|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure). Riftcarver then rolls initiative.

A line of acid slices through creatures and terrain alike, creating a rift that is 20 feet high, 100 feet long, and 1 inch thick. This rift provides cover. The rift deals @Damage[5d8[acid]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature that is in its space when it is first created, that attempts to pass through it, or that ends its turn inside the rift, with a @Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|options:area-effect] save. A creature that succeeds at this save when the chasm is created is pushed to the nearest space on the side of its choice. Creatures trying to move through the rift fail to do so if they critically fail their save, ending their movement next to the rift.

details.description: Riftcarver is the chthonic artifact weapon of Deskari, the slain demon lord of the locust host and plague of the Worldwound (now known as the Sarkoris Scar). Unfettered from its creator, it now wreaks havoc on the enemies of its current master, Hesla Embersplitter.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:27] (expert), @Check[demon-lore|dc:27] (trained), or @Check[religion|dc:27] (expert) to remind Riftcarver that it should not serve an interloper; @Check[intimidation|dc:32] (trained), @Check[nature|dc:32] (trained), or @Check[occultism|dc:32] (trained) to temporarily make Riftcarver's spirit cower; @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank, counteract DC 28); three total successes are required to disable the hazard. The first time Riftcarver is taken away from Hesla Embersplitter against her will (via disarm, a spell, or some other method), it also counts as one success. If Hesla Embersplitter is rendered @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] or killed, the hazard immediately ends.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-20: The Overthrow of Ambition

details.routine: (three actions) Riftcarver lives up to its name, using all three of its actions to carve a new rift in a random direction (rolling [[/gmr 1d8]] to determine that direction). Hesla Embersplitter is immune to the effects of these rifts while wielding Riftcarver.

description.value:
Trigger An enemy of Hesla Embersplitter rolls for initiative

Effect Riftcarver calls out with thousands of voices to infest the minds of the righteous. Any creature within 60 feet with the holy trait must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:33|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure). Riftcarver then rolls initiative.

A line of acid slices through creatures and terrain alike, creating a rift that is 20 feet high, 100 feet long, and 1 inch thick. This rift provides cover. The rift deals @Damage[6d8[acid]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature that is in its space when it is first created, that attempts to pass through it, or that ends its turn inside the rift, with a @Check[reflex|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect] save. A creature that succeeds at this save when the chasm is created is pushed to the nearest space on the side of its choice. Creatures trying to move through the rift fail to do so if they critically fail their save, ending their movement next to the rift.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the smoke intensifying and filling with the tortured faces of lost souls

details.description: Smoke and fire explode up around the boundaries of the storm, accusatory screams and threats of violence echoing over the winds and crackling of flames. Broken weapons and shields litter the ground and Shoanti warriors emerge from the smoke, blood running down their faces as they prepare for battle.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:26] to redirect the anger of the haunt back onto itself, @Check[intimidation|dc:28] to goad the Shadefield spirits into attacking someone outside of the ritual circle, or @Check[religion|dc:28] to assuage the spirits in the area. Four successes are needed to disable the haunt long enough for the ritual to be completed.
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the Shadefields but resets immediately. The haunt is only permanently disabled when Diskah Night Watcher is put to rest. Otherwise, the haunt reforms within 24 hours of being Disabled.
details.routine: (4 actions) The ancestor storm uses 1 action to release a pulse of void energy, restoring @Damage[10[healing]] Hit Points to each Shadefield spirit in the area, before using its next 2 actions to make an arrows of rage Strike against all non-Shoanti creatures within 60 feet of the cairn. The haunt then uses its 4th action to use Calling the Blood on one random leader participating in the ritual. The haunt loses an action for each Shadefield spirit killed, and when there are no Shadefield spirits remaining, the haunt uses its next action to summon a new Shadefield spirit into the battle.

description.value:
Trigger The ritual begins

Effect The ancestor storm howls in fury and pain. This deals @Damage[(2d10+11)[spirit]] damage to all non-Shoanti creatures in the area, who must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

The ancestor storm randomly chooses a Shoanti ritualist to distract. The ritualist must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] by the voices of their ancestors for 1 round, increasing the DCs to Disable the haunt by 2 for the duration.
The hazard makes an arrows of rage Strike against all non-Shoanti creatures within 60 feet of the cairn.

details.description: Six long blades, hidden in grooves in the walls and floor, zigzag through different parts of this hallway when any pressure plate in the hallway intersection is depressed; there are so many plates it's impossible to avoid them when moving through the room. The blades retreat into the floor and move through the hidden grooves before swinging out from the wall again in a different location.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Disable Each Blade] (expert) to disable each blade, or utter the magical passphrase (which only Chafkhem knows) to deactivate the trap for 10 minutes
details.reset: The trap resets when no creatures remain in area E20. Damaged or destroyed blades aren't repaired when the trap resets.
details.routine: (7 actions) The trap spends 1 action for each of its blades; a blade makes a scythe Strike against each creature in its region. With its final action, the trap uses Scythe Shuffle. Reduce the number of actions the trap can take by 1 for each destroyed blade.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps in the 15-foot-by-25-foot area where the branching hallways connect

Effect The trap uses Scythe Shuffle; each blade makes a scythe Strike against each creature in its region, then it uses Scythe Shuffle again. The trap then rolls for initiative.

The blades travel erratically throughout the hallway's branches, out of sight under the floors or behind the walls. For each blade, roll [[/gmr 1d4 #Direction Scythe Blade]]{1d4} to determine the region in which it next makes scythe Strikes. A creature can [[/act seek dc=22]] to learn clues about blades in the region they're currently occupying. On a success, the creature knows how many blades are currently in its region.

1. Main intersection (the 15-foot-by-25-foot area where the hallways connect, as marked on area E20)

2. North branch (from the main intersection to the secret door to area E14)

3. Central hall (from the main intersection to the secret door to area E24)

4. South branch (from the main intersection to the wall shared with area E25)
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: Several hidden nozzles spray colorless sleeping gas into the seating area.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) to crimp shut the primary feeding tube for the gas, or @Check[perception|dc:31] (expert) to discover the hidden bypass switch next to the door to area A3 that shuts the trap off

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is applied to any of the steps in the middle of the seating area, or applied to the manual trigger on the stage. This manual trigger can activate the trap even if its @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] bypass switch is engaged

Effect Sleeping gas is silently sprayed into the seating area. All creatures in the area must roll a @Check[will|dc:26] save to resist its effects. The gas persists in the area for 3 rounds before becoming inert. Any creature who ends their turn in this area during these 3 rounds must attempt a new @Check[will|dc:26] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and realizes that the area has been gassed.

Success The creature is unaffected but doesn't notice the gas.

Failure The creature feels a bit drowsy and lethargic, and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued] for 1 minute. If the creature is already fatigued, it instead falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious]. If it's still unconscious after 1 minute, it wakes up automatically.

Critical Failure The creature falls unconscious. If it's still unconscious after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically.

stealth.details: (trained) (or –10 and no minimum proficiency if the oozeshardling node's electric pulse reaction has been triggered)

details.description: A lump of bluish ooze shot through with what look like tiny silver shavings of metal. Once it has been activated, it glows with arcs of electricity that leap out at nearby targets.
details.disable: Two successful @Check[arcana|dc:23] or @Check[occultism|dc:23] (trained) checks on the oozeshardling to disperse its power, or @Check[acrobatics|dc:25] or @Check[thievery|dc:25] (expert) to remove the flecks of empowering warshard from within the oozeshardling. A failed Acrobatics or Thievery check to disable an oozeshardling results in the creature attempting the check taking @Damage[1d6[acid]] damage (@Damage[2d6[acid]] on a critical failure).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-07: A God Falls Where Magic Fails

details.reset: The oozeshardling node collapses and melds back into the oozeshard after 1 minute, or disappears entirely if the oozeshard is slain.
details.routine: (3 actions) The oozeshardling uses its first action to make an electric spark Strike against one random non-ooze creature within range, its second action is used to move straight in a random cardinal direction (roll [[/gmr 1d4 #Direction]] to determine the direction), and its third action to make a second electric spark Strike against a different random non-ooze creature within range. The oozeshardling doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters a space within 10 feet of the sparking oozeshardling or the sparking oozeshardling is created by the divine oozeshard's Spawn Oozeshardling Node ability

Effect The sparking oozeshardling unleashes a shocking blast that deals @Damage[5d6[electricity]] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] around it (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save).

details.description: Ghostly soldiers wielding crossbows manifest within the towers that flank the gatehouse and rain bolts down upon intruders.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:31|traits:haunt,hazard] (expert) to cow the archers into fleeing or @Check[religion|dc:28|name:Banish the Spirits|traits:haunt,hazard] (expert) to temporarily banish the spirits
details.reset: The haunt vanishes permanently if the graveknights are destroyed. Otherwise, it returns after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attacks the graveknights in area G1 or enters the castle

Effect The haunt makes a spectral bolt Strike against the triggering creature, then rolls initiative.

(1 action) The hazard fires one bolt at every creature in a 30-foot-by-30-foot area in front of the gate as 1 action. Because the spectral archers fire bolts continuously, the haunt can also use the Continuous Barrage free action (below) to fire bolts at each creature during that creature's turn.

Trigger A creature in front of the gate finishes an action

Effect The haunt makes a spectral bolt Strike against the triggering creature.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (expert; @Check[perception|dc:31] to detect the hidden bypass button)

details.description: A pile of excrement and rotten food is launched at whoever opens the trunk without first depressing a hidden button on the side of the trunk.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (expert) to unhook the launch spring

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the trunk or breaks open the trunk; Bypass @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] button

Effect The trap deals @Damage[(4d10+22)[poison]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[fortitude|dc:26|basic] save). On a failure, the target is additionally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 4}. A creature can spend one Interact action to scrub the filth from their eyes, removing the blinded condition. A creature can't reduce the value of their sickened condition below 1 until they fully wash the filth off, a process which requires 5 minutes and suitable supplies.

details.description: Planar winds summoned from beyond sweep through the room, song and sound carried adrift on their current. This air elemental is trapped on the Material Plane and can't escape.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:27|name:Duplicate the Melody|traits:environmental,magical] (expert) twice to duplicate the melody and appease the elemental, or @Check[arcana|dc:30|name:Guide the Elemental|traits:environmental,magical] (expert) and then @Check[diplomacy|dc:27|name:Guide the Elemental|traits:environmental,magical] (trained) to guide the elemental through a planar tear elsewhere in the manor. Each attempt to Disable the hazard requires two actions, and each successful check to Disable removes one action from the hazard's routine.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-16: Freedom for Wishes

details.reset: The melody remains for 1 minute, after which it disperses into several holes in the walls. It returns after an hour unless disabled.
details.routine: (2 actions) For each action in the routine, the melody makes a solid refrain Strike against one creature in its area. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters area B3

Effect Winds surge into the room. The hazard rolls initiative.

When a creature fails a Performance check to Disable the melody, the elemental plays along before taking over the performance. Creatures in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save to resist becoming fascinated by the melody.

Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is then temporarily immune to Mesmerising Duet for 1 hour.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fascinated] for 1 round; this fascination does not end when the creature or its allies take damage from the hazard.

Critical Failure The creature is fascinated for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}; this fascination does not end when the creature or its allies take damage from the hazard.

details.description:
Feeling the presence of creatures on its backside, the Granite Ox swishes its stone tail to swat them away.

Escaping The PCs overcome this hazard by moving out of the area that the Ox's tail can reach. A PC escapes (and is not subject to tail Strikes) by ascending the Ox's sloped back, traveling a distance based on the result of the PC's Reflex save from the Shaken Loose hazard. PCs can Climb up the Ox's back with a successful @Check[athletics|dc:24] check or—by finding and scrambling up improvised paths—a @Check[acrobatics|dc:26], @Check[perception|dc:26], or @Check[survival|dc:26] check. With a successful @Check[acrobatics|dc:20] check, a flying PC can ascend half their fly Speed (or their full Speed on a critical success). PCs can attempt to Disable the hazard whether or not they're in range of the tail.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:26] to scramble out of the way, @Check[deception|dc:24] or @Check[intimidation|dc:24] (trained) to draw the Ox's attention. The DC of the flat check for this round (see Routine) is increased by 4 on a success, or by 8 on a critical success. Increasing the flat check DC to 21 or higher buys the party enough time to move up the Ox's back, disabling the hazard.
details.reset: Once all of the PCs moved outside the tail's striking range, the hazard is effectively disabled. The hazard reactivates if any PC returns to that area.
details.routine: (1 action) The Granite Ox makes a tail Strike against one creature in the area. If the attack succeeds, the GM rolls a @Check[flat|dc:9]. On a success, the hazard makes another Strike. The hazard continues making Strikes until the GM fails the flat check.

description.value:
Trigger A creature lands near the base of the Granite Ox's tail

Effect The Granite Ox makes a tail Strike against the triggering creature. The hazard then rolls initiative.
Plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round; no multiple attack penalty

details.description: Ghostly images of your heart's desire appear and beckon you toward them.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:25] or @Check[religion|dc:25] (expert) to disrupt the images; @Check[occultism|dc:26] (expert) to shout a countering chant; @Check[intimidation|dc:27] (expert) to scare the images away; @Check[will|dc:28] save (expert) to refuse to listen

publication.title: Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea

details.routine:
(1 per PC) The hazard targets a PC and uses cruel temptation

Special The hazard continues each round until defeated, and the hallway's end cannot be reached until the hazard is stopped.

description.value:
Trigger A PC moves down the exit hallway

Effect The ghostly images turn cruel and hungry, grasping and slashing at the PC

details.description: The demigod statues in the alcoves seem to shift, their heads turning to watch as intruders pass by.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:31] (to scrape away hidden runes that grant the statues the ability to watch), @Check[religion|dc:28] (to pray for protection from the attention of Abyssal lords), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 26) to counteract the trap
details.reset: Once the Watching Lords are triggered, the trap needs an hour to recharge its energies, after which point it can be triggered again.

description.value:
Trigger A non-chaotic-evil creature approaches within 20 feet of the doors to area G4

Effect The statues appear to hiss and growl, and a moment later, four @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Brimorak] demons appear in the hallway—two near the entrance to area G1, and two near the entrance to area G4. The four demons attack at once, rolling initiative as normal. They fight to the death, but vanish after 1 minute has passed.

details.description: The white-and-blue ice is less structurally sound than it looks.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:31] or @Check[survival|dc:31] (expert) to spread out and prevent the ice from cracking further, or @Check[acrobatics|dc:31] or @Check[athletics|dc:31] (trained) to get off the ice safely as it cracks.
details.reset: The river freezes over every night, restoring this hazard. The hazard disappears once the vernal equinox passes.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more creatures step onto the ice in area M8

Effect The ice beneath the party cracks. All creatures in area M8 must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:30] save. On a failure, they fall into the freezing water, whereupon they must succeed at a basic @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save against @Damage[(4d10+22)[cold]] damage. On a critical failure, the creature must start holding their breath or risk drowning as their muscles lock and they begin to sink. A creature must spend an action to Swim to the shore and must then succeed at a @Check[athletics|dc:30] check to Climb up out of the water.

details.description: Unwelcome guests who touch this hatch are cursed.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:28] (expert) to drain the magic from the curse, @Check[thievery|dc:31] (master) to unweave the curse's bindings, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank, counteract DC 28) to counteract the curse
details.reset: The trap resets when the hatch is shut.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the hatch

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[(3d10+22)[force]] damage and is knocked back 10 feet. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[will|dc:30] save takes half damage and isn't moved. On a failure, the creature is additionally cursed. A cursed creature takes a –2 penalty to Will saves, and whenever the cursed creature becomes affected by a spell, the creature takes @Damage[1d10[force]] damage. The curse remains until removed by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cleanse Affliction] or similar magic.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Curse Trespassers]

details.description: The brewing equipment begins to shudder and shake, and thick foamy sludge starts to bubble up out of the various pipes, tuns, and barrels—sludge that contains blinking eyes, screaming mouths, and twitching fingers among its turbulent mass!
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:28] (master) to swiftly dismantle key parts of the contraption containing the vile brew, or @Check[religion|dc:31] (expert) to exorcise the haunt; three total successes in all are needed to disable the haunt

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates at the start of any round in which no living creatures are in the room; it then reactivates 1 minute later.
details.routine: (3 actions) On its initiative, the churning awfulness extrudes a long, slimy tendril of clotted blood, tangled hair, and jagged broken teeth. These long, whiplike lengths then make three vile lash Strikes on three randomly determined targets in the room. With their reach and ability to manifest from any part of the machinery, these Strikes can target any creature in the room. They only target a creature more than once if there's not enough targets to split their attention.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to use the brewing equipment, or at least one living creature spends 1 minute in the room

Effect The brewing equipment rattles to life as a hideous mash of what looks like clotted blood and body parts surges up and out of the machinery, pushing a nauseating stench into the room. All creatures in area C46 must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:26|options:inflicts:sickened] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:26|options:damaging-effect,inflicts:drained,inflicts:enfeebled]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage, enfeebled 2 (1 round), and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}

details.description: Blasts of fire and smoke pour out of the tunnel walls to coalesce into the burning form of Dahak himself; a PC who succeeds at a @Check[religion|dc:20|name:Recognize Depiction of Dahak|traits:hazard,complex,fire,magical,trap,action:recall-knowledge] check to Recall Knowledge notes that the fiery dragon closely resembles classical depictions of the draconic god of destruction.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:26|name:Counteract Dahak With Prayers|traits:complex,fire,magical,trap,hazard] (expert) to utter prayers to a non-evil deity to counteract Dahak's presence (prayers to Apsu allow this check to be made if the character is merely trained in Religion), @Check[thievery|dc:30|name:Divert the Hazard's Energy Back|traits:complex,fire,magical,trap,hazard] (expert) to divert the hazard's energies back upon themselves, or a successful @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 26).
details.reset: The vision of Dahak deactivates and resets automatically once no creatures remain within the Huntergate way station.
details.routine: (2 actions) The vision of Dahak manifests next to the creature that is closest to the center of the way station, then uses its Breath Weapon in a direction that catches the most possible targets in its area.

description.value:
Trigger A creature is within the way station.

Effect The vision of Dahak appears in a 15-foot space adjacent to that creature and rolls initiative.

The vision of Dahak unleashes a blast of fire from its burning maw, creating a @Template[cone|distance:60] that deals @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage to all creatures within (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|traits:magical,trap,hazard] save).

details.description: This figurine of a charging triceratops was crafted from the dinosaur's femur and contains its spirit.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:24] (expert) to banish the dinosaur's spirit with a primal incantation, @Check[intimidation|dc:28] to scare it off with a frightening display, or @Check[survival|dc:26] (expert) to calm the spirit
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the figurine

Effect A spectral triceratops appears around the figurine and charges toward the triggering creature, affecting a line 30 feet long. Creatures in this area take @Damage[(4d10+22)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic]).

details.description: Poisonous mold spores assault nearby creatures.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:26|name:Remove the Mold] (expert) to remove the mold without triggering the spores

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the mold's space or damages the mold. The mold can't use this reaction if it's in direct sunlight or if the damage was fire damage.

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10 feet} are exposed to yellow mold spores.

Any drained condition from the spores persists after the poison's duration ends.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:26]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[3d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 3} (1 round)

details.description: Abendego's Squall leaps from the water. Its mouth opens to snap against any part of the ship that it can reach.
details.disable: @Check[medicine|dc:23] (expert) to remove the harpoon, @Check[nature|dc:23] (trained), or @Check[intimidation|dc:23] (expert) to scare the orca away; two total successes are required to subdue the orca

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-00: Salt of the Ocean

details.reset: The Abendego's Squall returns to hunt down the Snapdragon after 8 hours.
details.routine:
d The Abendego's Squall Leaps over the Snapdragon with each action. When it lands, it deals @Damage[4] damage to the Snapdragon. All creatures on the deck take @Damage[(3d10+40)[bludgeoning]] and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:28] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} until the end of its next turn.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} until the end of its next turn.

Speed 30 feet

description.value:
Trigger Abendego's Squall matches speed with the Snapdragon

Effect The orca leaps across the Snapdragon and tears its main mast from the deck in the process. This deals @Damage[30] damage to the Snapdragon. All creatures on deck take @Damage[(3d12+4)[bludgeoning]] damage from the shrapnel and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:28|showDC:gm|basic] save. Abendego's Squall rolls initiative.

details.description: High winds and forest fires intensified by Abyssal energy batters everything in the surge's path.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:33|name:Redirect the Storm Surge|traits:rare,fire,environmental,hazard,magical], @Check[nature|dc:33|name:Redirect the Storm Surge|traits:rare,fire,environmental,hazard,magical], @Check[occultism|dc:33|name:Redirect the Storm Surge|traits:rare,fire,environmental,hazard,magical], or @Check[religion|dc:33|name:Redirect the Storm Surge|traits:rare,fire,environmental,hazard,magical] (trained) to redirect the storm surge or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank, counteract DC 26) to nullify it.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-24: Breaking The Storm: Parting Clouds

description.value:
The storm of wind and fire suddenly surges over everyone in the area. When this hazard activates, roll [[/r 1d10]] to determine the effect and type of damage.

  • 1-2 Firestorm Embers and burning debris gets whipped around as the wind suddenly shifts direction, dealing @Damage[1d10[bludgeoning],(1d10+13)[fire]]{1d10 bludgeoning damage and 1d10+13 fire damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic|name:Avoid Firestorm] save).
  • 3-4 Battering Debris Wreckage rains down throughout the area, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic|name:Avoid Battering Debris] save).
  • 5-6 Abyssal Energy The storm blows about debris that has absorbed Abyssal energies. Creatures who come into contact with this debris are exposed to Abyssal plague.
  • 7-8 Sharp Rain The storm picks up various objects, some of which are particularly sharp. The storm deals @Damage[1d10[bludgeoning],(1d10+13)[piercing]]{1d10 bludgeoning damage and 1d10+13 piercing damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic|name:Avoid Sharp Rain] save).
  • 9-10 Soot Storm Large amounts of soot blow in on the wind. The soot moves with supernatural malice, seeking out the lungs of any creatures caught nearby. Any creature that needs to breathe must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:28|name:Avoid Soot Storm|traits:inhaled] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The sickened condition can't end or be reduced until a creature has spent 10 minutes breathing clean air.

Level 9

A creeping malaise overcomes the afflicted, whose skin develops a sickly pallor.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|traits:disease|name:Resist Abyssal Plague|dc:28]

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 3} (1 day)

The target can't recover from the drained condition from Abyssal plague until the disease is cured.

stealth.details: (expert) to notice the residual animating magic in the fang

details.description: The fang on the altar leaps up to stab at insufficiently zealous creatures.
details.disable: The haunt is disabled once there aren't any creatures in the room that haven't sufficiently proclaimed their zeal (as described above).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-13: Ancient Beyond Imagining

details.reset: If the fang is not destroyed, it returns to the altar and the haunt resets immediately.
details.routine: (two-actions) The fang leaps around the room, flying up to 20 feet and making a fang Strike against a random creature in the room.

description.value:
Trigger A creature interacts with the items in this room without first proclaiming their zeal

Effect The haunt makes a fang Strike against the triggering creature, then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: The wind intensifies and the seeping fog rushes forward in a stampede of ghostly orcs screaming wordless battle cries, weapons raised to slaughter their foes.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:28] (expert) to funnel the magic powering the mist back into itself, or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to exorcise some of the trapped souls and weaken the storm; three total successes in any combination are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area of Fort Whiteskull but resets immediately. The army of mist resets 1 day after being disabled if the ancestor storm isn't put to rest.
details.routine: (3 actions) The army of mist uses 3 actions to make frozen fists Strikes against three random creatures, favoring non-orcs or anyone who attempted to Disable it within the past round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the haunt's area

Effect The orc warriors roar in fury, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] save) to all living creatures within the haunt, then rolls initiative. Creatures that critically fail their saves are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} until Umbra's Last Stand is quelled.

details.description: Animated door slams into trespassers.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:30] or @Check[occultism|dc:30] (master) to soothe the psychic impressions or @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) to jam the door open
details.reset: The trap resets automatically.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature passes through the door

Effect The door slams into the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[(4d10+26)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save).

details.description: The scene of the arid Cinderlands melts away as the water of the river refills and green grass spreads across the land. The sounds of battle can be heard everywhere and all allies have vanished, murderous orcs from the One Eye Hold taking their place.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) twice to weaken the haunt or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 27) to counteract the illusions
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the Shadefields but resets immediately. The battle illusion resets 1 day after being disabled if the ancestor storm isn't put to rest.
details.routine:
(1 action; illusion, incapacitation, mental, occult) The haunt continues the illusion, making victims feel as though they've been abandoned and are surrounded by attackers on all sides. Each creature in the haunt's area must make a @Check[will|dc:28|traits:illusion,incapacitation,mental,occult] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is hostile toward the supposed One Eye orcs and must spend all actions moving toward and attacking One Eye orcs. This hostility lasts for 1 minute, until the haunt is disabled, or until they fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious]. They cannot voluntarily stop attacking those they perceive as enemies.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more creatures enter the haunt's area

Effect Each creature sees the forms of nearby allies shift and fade, replaced with hostile One Eye orcs. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature sees through the illusions and is temporarily immune to the haunt's routine for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature believes the illusions are true; if they become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] by the haunt's routine, they can't attempt flat checks to end the confused condition when they take damage.

Critical Failure As failure and the creature is filled with feelings of abandonment and betrayal. When the creature rolls against the haunt's routine, they must roll twice and take the worse result. This is a misfortune effect.

details.description: A spectral assailant drives a stake into a dying vampire's heart; the vampire collapses and releases a surge of void energy.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:28] (expert) to drain away the haunt's void energy
details.reset: Unless disabled, the haunt materializes again on the next moonrise.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the vampire's corpse

Effect A surge of void energy fills the room as the corpse lurches as though staked. The haunt deals @Damage[10d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage to creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} of the corpse; this damage heals undead creatures for the same amount. Living creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30|options:area-effect,damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}. This drained condition is reduced by 1 after 10 minutes.

Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes drained 1.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2}.

details.description: Enchanted carvings of animals transform any creatures who damage the door into the animal they harmed.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:26] (expert) to handle the animal carvings or @Check[thievery|dc:33] (master) to scratch out the animal carvings or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 28) to counteract the spell
details.reset: automatic

description.value:
Trigger A creature damages the door

Effect The trap casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Bestial Curse] on the triggering creature (@Check[fortitude|dc:32|traits:arcane,curse,polymorph,transmutation] save); on a critical failure, the duration is 2 hours.

stealth.details: (or 0 if the trapdoor is disabled or broken) or detect magic

details.description: An iron trapdoor covers an infinitely deep 10-foot-square pit.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Remove Trapdoor|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to remove the trapdoor
details.reset: The trap still causes creatures to fall forever if they fall in, but the trapdoor must be reset manually for the trap to become Hidden again.

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor

Effect The triggering creature falls in and continues to fall, potentially forever. That creature can try to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling. The DC to Climb the walls or Grab an Edge is 26. The pit contains many handholds, so the falling creature can try to Grab an Edge again every 6 seconds. If the creature succeeds, it can start to Climb out from that point (though it might be a very long climb, depending on how far the creature fell). Since the creature falls endlessly, it can rest and even prepare spells while falling, though items dropped while falling are usually lost forever.

hp.details: to destroy the beams holding the side stones and disable the trap

details.description: Several large boulders are separated by wooden support beams, entrapping a woman and three young children within.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) on the support beams to separate them enough to allow Mayor Eliana's family to escape without being crushed.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: The Scourge of Sheerleaf

details.routine: (3 actions) The trap uses its first action to slam a hanging stone into the main beam of the trap, and its last two actions to launch splinter Strikes against two Medium or smaller creatures. The main beam can withstand four rounds of this before splintering.

description.value:
Trigger A Medium or smaller creature steps into the room

Effect A wooden beam explodes as a rock drops onto it, shooting large splinters in all directions and making a splinter Strike against every creature in the room. The trap then rolls initiative.

Trigger The main beam has been struck by the hanging stone four times

Effect The main beam abruptly shatters into pieces and the boulders of the trap collapse, dealing @Damage[12d8[bludgeoning]] damage to all creatures held within.

details.description: Spectral undead converge on any living creatures to rip into and devour them.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:28] to push past or run away, or @Check[occultism|dc:28] or @Check[religion|dc:28] (expert) to exorcise the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after there are no bleeding creatures left in M2. If disabled, it resets after 1 day. Destroying Illcayna is the only way to permanently destroy this haunt.
details.routine: (1 action) The haunt converges upon all creatures currently bleeding in area M2. The targeted creatures must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] save against @Damage[3d6[spirit],3d6[void]] damage from the spirits pulling and gnawing on their souls. On a critical failure, the creature also suffers @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the square of the town center

Effect All creatures within area M2 take @Damage[3d10[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: Pressure plates in the hall trigger the release of caustic gas.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:28] (master) to neutralize the acid or @Check[thievery|dc:30] (master) to disable the pressure plates
details.reset: A 10-minute Interact activity and a new supply of caustic gas.

description.value:
Trigger A creature in contact with the ground travels down this hall

Effect Caustic gas fills the hall, dealing @Damage[(4d10+26)[acid]] damage to all creatures in area D5 (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save).

details.description: The ritual power in this room, corrupted by necromantic energy, slams purifying water into all "unclean" creatures that try to leave.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:28|name:Ceremonially purify everyone] (expert) to ceremonially purify everyone in the room, by pouring water from a tub over their hands, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 28) to counteract the corrupted ritual magic.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-14: The Tomb Between Worlds

details.reset: 1 minute

description.value:
Trigger A living creature steps out of the room.

Effect Water sprays out of the tubs. Every creature in the room or within 10 feet of the doors takes @Damage[(4d10+26)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save) and the water casts 5th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] with a counteract modifier of [[/r {1d20+18} #Counteract Check]]{+18} against the highest-rank spell active on each target (or all spells on a target that critically fails its Reflex save).

details.description: The pain of over 300 souls crying out for recognition and revenge pierces through the room, as a spiritual whirlwind fills the space.
details.disable: @Check[medicine|dc:30] or @Check[society|dc:30] to determine 287's first or surname through the journal text or @Check[occultism|dc:32] or @Check[religion|dc:32] to connect with the spiritual energy bound to the journal to find her name. Two successes are needed, one for each part of 287's name.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-11: The Hidden Current

details.reset: The haunt vanishes permanently if 287's name is revealed.
details.routine: (1 action) The haunt makes a howling wail Strike against all living creatures in area A13 as 1 action.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs start investigating the journal for 287's name

Effect The victims all beg for attention and recognition, lashing out at the PCs when they remain focused. The haunt makes a howling wail Strike against all living creatures and then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: Animated cabinet imprisons intruders. The cabinet has two compartments; each can hold a single Medium creature or two Small-sized creatures or smaller.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (expert) twice on cabinet; @Check[crafting|dc:28] or @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) to remove each or two doors
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour.
details.routine:
(3 actions) The trap uses its first action to move directly toward the nearest living creature, its second action to make a door Strike against each adjacent creature, and its third action to Batter. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

Speed 15 feet

description.value:
Trigger A living creature moves adjacent to the cabinet

Effect The cabinet animates and makes a door Strike against one adjacent creature. The hazard then rolls initiative.

The target is trapped inside the cabinet's interior, gaining the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] condition until it [[/act escape dc=32]]{Escapes}, another creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open]{Forces Open} the cabinet door (DC 32), or that cabinet door is destroyed. Each of the two cabinet interiors can contain one Medium creature, or two Small-sized creatures or smaller.
The cabinet shakes, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage to all creatures it has @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed].

details.description: A complex geometrical ward with mathematical equations woven into its lines magically seals the door, harming anyone who touches it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (master) to sabotage the ward, @Check[geometry-lore|dc:25]{Geometry} or @Check[mathematics-lore|dc:25] (trained) to calculate the solution to the complex equations, @Check[crafting|dc:28] (expert) to manipulate the ward's precise underlying schematics, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 26) to counteract the magical ward

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap automatically resets after being triggered. If disabled, the ward does not reset.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the door directly or with a tool

Effect The ward's equations spiral and swirl, drawing the mind into an inescapable conundrum. The triggering creature is dealt @Damage[(4d10+26)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:32|basic] save). On a failure, the target is additionally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 4} on a critical failure.

stealth.details: (trained) (or –10 and no minimum proficiency if the oozeshardling node's electric pulse reaction has been triggered)

details.description: A lump of bluish ooze shot through with what look like tiny silver shavings of metal. Once it has been activated, it glows with arcs of electricity that leap out at nearby targets.
details.disable: Two successful @Check[arcana|dc:25] or @Check[occultism|dc:25] (trained) checks on the oozeshardling to disperse its power, or @Check[acrobatics|dc:27] or @Check[thievery|dc:27] (expert) to remove the flecks of empowering warshard from within the oozeshardling. A failed Acrobatics or Thievery check to disable an oozeshardling results in the creature attempting the check taking @Damage[1d6[acid]] damage (@Damage[2d6[acid]] on a critical failure).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-07: A God Falls Where Magic Fails

details.reset: The oozeshardling node collapses and melds back into the oozeshard after 1 minute, or disappears entirely if the oozeshard is slain.
details.routine: (3 actions) The oozeshardling uses its first action to make an electric spark Strike against one random non-ooze creature within range, its second action is used to move straight in a random cardinal direction (roll [[/gmr 1d4 #Direction]] to determine the direction), and its third action to make a second electric spark Strike against a different random non-ooze creature within range. The oozeshardling doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters a space within 10 feet of the divine oozeshardling node

Effect The divine oozeshardling unleashes a shocking blast that deals @Damage[5d6[electricity]] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] around it (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save).

details.description: Enchanted caltrops blend in with the surrounding masonry.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] to safely clear the path.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #2-22: Breaking the Storm: Excising Ruination

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks on to the Drandlesticks

Effect The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[acrobatics|dc:30] check or take @Damage[(1d4+11)[piercing],2d6[precision,piercing]]{1d4+11 piercing damage, 2d6 precision damage}, and @Damage[2d4[bleed]]. A creature taking persistent bleed damage from the Drandlesticks takes a –10-foot penalty to its Speed.

Additionally, a creature damaged by the Drandlesticks is targeted by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Command] spell (@Check[will|dc:28|traits:magical,enchantment,mental] save) with orders to release everything it's holding; if the creature fails the save and drops what it's holding, there is a [[/r {1dc}#Heads(1): blown over the edge]]{50% chance} that the high winds blow the object or objects off the edge of the Colossus.

details.description: Runes hidden in the door's carvings blast lightning down the hall.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (master) to scratch out the runes, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 28) to counteract the runes
details.reset: automatic

description.value:
Trigger A creature damages the door, fails to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open] the door, or fails a check to realign the stonework keys;

Effect Electricity fills the hallway and affects all creatures inside it, who must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:28] save.

Critical Success No effect.

Success The trap deals @Damage[(1d12+5)[electricity]] damage.

Failure The trap deals @Damage[(2d12+10)[electricity]] damage, and the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The trap deals @Damage[(4d12+20)[electricity]] damage, and the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 1 hour.

details.description: The ghostly spirit of a polar bear stands on its hind legs and roars.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:26] (expert) to calm the spirit, @Check[intimidation|dc:30] (master) to cow the spirit, or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to contain the spiritual corruption (each successful check reduces its number of actions by 1)
details.reset: The bear spirit calms after no creatures are in the room for 1 minute but is ready to lash out again after 10 minutes.
details.routine: (3 actions) The bear moves around the room at an alarming speed, seemingly teleporting, and Strikes a random creature with its spiritual claws.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the polar bear spirit

Effect The polar bear spirit roars. Each creature in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:28] save. The enraged polar bear spirit rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[6d6[sonic]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[12d6[sonic]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}.

details.description: The incense burners begin to smoke, filling the air with strangely nostalgic scents.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:26] (expert) to enter a state akin to lucid dreaming so as to unweave the nightmare from within, or @Check[intimidation|dc:30] (master) to stand resolute against the nightmares and turn the fear back upon itself
details.reset: The hazard resets when Etward dreams in this room.
details.routine:
(2 actions; cold, mental, occult) The haunt uses its first action to light incense burners again, forcing any creature that isn't already @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] to save against that effect once more. Its second action is to cause any unconscious creatures in the room to experience horrific, vivid dreams about being lost in the arctic during a blizzard, while enormous furred figures— saumen kar—lunge at them through the snow to attack repeatedly. Each unconscious creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:28|traits:cold,mental,occult] saving throw. If at the end of the round there are no unconscious creatures in the room, Etward's nightmare ends and the trap deactivates.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and wakes up.

Success The creature remains unconscious and takes @Damage[1d10[cold],1d10[mental]]. This damage does not wake the creature, and those who are awake can see the unconscious creature thrash as if in the throes of a nightmare while their body rimes over with layers of frost. The creature can fight against the bitter cold and monstrous shapes by attempting a @Check[will|dc:28|traits:cold,enchantment,mental,occult] save as a three-action activity on its turn— on a success, the creature wakes up.

Failure As success, but @Damage[(1d10+6)[cold],(1d10+6)[mental]], and with a @Check[will|dc:32|traits:cold,mental,occult] save to wake up.

Critical Failure The creature remains unconscious and takes @Damage[(2d10+12)[cold],(2d10+12)[mental]].

description.value:
Trigger A creature lies down on the bed, or a living creature spends more than 3 rounds inside this room

Effect Incense swiftly fills the room (even if the incense burners have been removed or were destroyed). All creatures in area A12 must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} by the incense's scent.

Failure The creature falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious]. If it's still unconscious after 1 minute, it wakes up automatically.

Critical Failure As failure, but if it's still unconscious after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically.

details.description: Magic runes carved into a doorframe connect a door to the Plane of Air when opened, blowing creatures down the connecting hall.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30|name:Scratch Out Runes|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) twice to scratch out the runes, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 30) to counteract the planar runes; @Check[athletics|dc:32|name:Shut the Door] to shut the door if it's open (using Athletics only stops the trap, it doesn't fully disable it)
details.reset: The trap resets when the door is shut.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap uses 1 action to blow powerful winds down the hall. Each creature in the hall must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save with the same results as Gust. If this forced movement would cause a creature to collide with a solid object or fall downstairs or out a window, that creature takes an additional @Damage[(2d10 + 11)[bludgeoning]] damage (or @Damage[20[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage due to a fall).

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door

Effect The hall is buffeted by powerful winds for as long as the door remains open. A creature must succeed at a @Check[athletics|dc:32] check to move toward the door. A creature who fails at this check is pushed back 5 feet and falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Additionally, the triggering creature is blasted by a powerful gust of wind and must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is pushed back 5 feet.

Failure The creature is pushed back 10 feet.

Critical Failure The creature is pushed back 10 feet and knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

details.description: An alchemical bombs explodes when the rubble is shifted, filling the hall with flame.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:30] (master) to nullify the bomb's alchemical components or @Check[thievery|dc:28] (master) to disable the bomb

description.value:
Trigger A creature in contact with the ground travels down this hall

Effect The bombs explodes, dealing @Damage[(6d8+12)[fire]] damage to the triggering creature and all creatures within 20 feet (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save). Additionally, if the triggering creature fails their saving throw, they take @Damage[1d8[persistent,fire]] damage.

stealth.details: (trained) to notice the creatures hovering in the trees

details.description: Hovering amid the branches of an acacia tree, a swarm of wood wisps attempt to gather the papers that have been caught in its leaves and thorns.
details.disable: Diplomacy @Check[diplomacy|dc:23] (trained) to make a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Request], @Check[athletics|dc:23] (trained) to climb the tree and help gather papers manually, or @Check[performance|dc:25] (trained) to demonstrate how the wisps can help without destroying the papers.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-13: Thick as Thieves

details.routine: (3 actions) The wisp swarm loses one action for each successful check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Disable a Device]{disable} it. The wisps swarm to work on each of their actions, making a headbutt Strike against any creature within 10 feet of the tree and destroying [[/br 2d4 #Pages]] pages of notes.

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 10 feet of the tree or directly addresses the wood wisps

Effect The wood wisp swarm animatedly tries to help the group gather the papers, destroying [[/br 2d4 #Pages]] pages, and then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (expert) to notice the incipient collapse; noticing the decorations has a DC of 0.

details.description: Large metal moons and spears break apart and fall when a creature moves beneath them.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) or @Check[crafting|dc:28] (expert) to prevent the decorations from falling.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #7-15: Within Antiquated Halls

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks through the space under the hanging decorations

Effect The little bit of disturbance is all the cracked decorations need to come dislodged. They fall, their sharp metal edges dealing @Damage[(6d10+15)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save) to those underneath them.

details.description: A smoke and fire manifestation of the Sandpoint Devil emerges from the pit with a blast of foul-smelling smoke.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:30] (master) to soothe and calm the unquiet fears left behind by the slain adventurers or @Check[religion|dc:30] (master) to hinder the haunt with prayers of protection (two successes from either skill disables the haunt)
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: (1 action; evocation, fire, occult) The smoking vision of the Sandpoint Devil appears to hover over the pit, its wings of fire flapping as its head lowers to exhale a @Template[cone|distance:30] of fire into the room, targeting as many creatures as possible. Note that the geist can be targeted by this blast of fire as well, but remember that its resistance applies to the fire. All creatures in the area take @Damage[7d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic|traits:fire,occult] save). A creature that critically fails this saving throw is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}; this effect has the emotion, fear, and mental traits.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the pit's edge

Effect A smoky, burning manifestation of the Sandpoint Devil appears to rise from the pit, causing a plume of brimstone-scented smoke to billow into the room. All creatures in area H6 must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:28] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: Eight pillars composed of jagged bones spin, damaging anyone adjacent who doesn't worship Zon-Kuthon.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] to disassemble one pillar, @Check[religion|dc:22] to ritually purify one pillar, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 26) once to counteract the magic binding all the bone pillars together, deactivating the whole trap

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute. The trap is automatically destroyed if all the velstracs on this floor are killed.
details.routine: (8 actions) Each of the functioning bone pillars makes a bone spur Strike against an adjacent creature. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty. This trap loses one action for each disabled or destroyed pillar.

description.value:
Trigger A creature who doesn't worship Zon-Kuthon and who isn't granted immunity by a velstrac enters the area

Effect The bones composing the pillars begin to spin, then makes a bone spur attack against one creature adjacent to any pillar. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Animated awls, hammers, nails, and other tools hurl themselves off the tool bench at intruders.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:33] (master) to soothe the psychic echoes, @Check[religion|dc:32] (expert) to overwhelm the area's energy, or @Check[occultism|dc:32] (expert) to counter the psychic echoes; on the third successful check, the psychic echoes are disrupted, and the trap is destroyed.
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap launches one tool against every living creature within 15 feet as 1 action. Because it flings tools continuously, the trap can also use the Continuous Barrage free action to fling tools at each creature during that creature's turn.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature comes within 15 feet of the trap

Effect The trap makes a tool Strike against the triggering creature. The trap then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

Trigger A creature within 15 feet finishes an action

Effect The trap makes a tool Strike against the triggering creature.

details.description: When the western secret door is opened or any of the trapdoors in the ceiling are forced open, the trap is triggered. As soon as this occurs, the portcullises to the east and west come crashing down, each consisting of solid iron sheets that form watertight seals. At the same time, the dozen 5-foot-wide trapdoors open in the ceiling. These holes are the mouths of winding tunnels that connect to a second pair of apertures on the submerged side of the island outside, allowing the waters of the Little Sellen River to plunge down into this room, possibly damaging or knocking @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] those in the square portion of the room itself (but not in the adjoining hallway) and eventually flooding the entire chamber.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (this increases to @Check[thievery|dc:30] once the trap is activated) to disable one of the 12 trapdoors and seal it shut; disabling all 12 of the trapdoors completely deactivates the trap, but a critical failure on any attempt causes the trap to trigger; @Check[thievery|dc:24] to disable one of the watertight portcullises and prevent it from falling; magic such as shape stone or wall of stone or even lock can also be used to disable portions of this deadly trap at the GM's discretion
details.reset: The trap must be reset with a major engineering effort.
details.routine:
(12 actions) The trap loses 1 action for each of its 12 ceiling trapdoors that are disabled. On each of the trap's actions, it pours water into the room, filling it to a depth of one inch. If all 12 trapdoors are open, it thus fills the room at a rate of 1 foot per round. If one of the portcullises is breached, the trap fills the room at half the rate, and if both are breached, it fills at one quarter the rate. Once the cascade of water begins, it's easy enough for a PC to avoid the downpour, but any creature that deliberately enters the cascade is subjected to the trap's Rushing Water reaction. If a creature is struck by Rushing Water while already underwater, the result of its Reflex save is automatically improved one degree of success.

The flooding ends and the trap deactivates once the area is filled to the ceiling-at this point, the water's surface is at the level of the river, and at the top of the stairs leading down from areas D4 and D6. A Medium or smaller creature can try to Swim to freedom through one of the 5-foot-wide tunnels leading to the river from a trapdoor, but doing so requires navigating 200 feet of flooded tunnels.

description.value:
Trigger The secret door to the west or any trapdoor is opened.

Effect The 12 ceiling trapdoors open along with the two secret doors to the cyclops zombie alcoves, releasing river water and zombies into the room. Any creature standing in the central 40-by-40 foot section of the main room must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:28] save; the trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]] damage from the cascade of water.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage from the cascade of water.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage from the cascade of falling water and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (master) to painstakingly remove the rune without triggering the magic, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 28) to counteract the rune.

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes the threshold

Effect Each living creature within 30 feet must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:26] save or be subjected to the dragon's curse A cursed creature takes a –2 status penalty to Fortitude saves, and any natural or magical healing it receives is halved The curse remains until removed by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cleanse Affliction] or similar magic

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Curse the Intruders]

details.description: A caustic green film clings to the ceiling above, watching for prey to pass beneath it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:33|name:Peel the Slime Off the Ceiling] (expert) to carefully peel the slime off the ceiling without touching it
details.reset: 1 hour, as the slime feasts and then slowly creeps back up to the ceiling

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks beneath the slime.

Effect The green slime drops on top of the creature, attempting to dissolve it into a nutritious slurry. The target must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:28] save.

Critical Success The target leaps out of the way, and it is unaffected.

Success A small amount of the slime splashes onto the target. The target is drained 1.

Failure The slime lands on its target. The target is drained 1, and this condition value increases by 1 at the end of its turn each round until the slime is removed. If the target reaches drained 4, the next time its drained value would increase, it dies and collapses into a slurry of nutrients. A slime covering a target can no longer be removed through Survival checks, and damage dealt to the slime is also dealt to the target (applying the target's immunities, weaknesses, and resistances rather than those of the green slime).

Critical Failure The slime completely coats its target. This has the same effect as a failure, except the target is immediately drained 2, becomes drained 4 after 1 round, and dies after 2 rounds.

hp.details: HP is per facet; BT is 22

details.description: Spectral hag claws reach out from the walls of the gem-like chamber to grab the PCs' souls and pull them into the trap's onyx facets.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) or @Check[crafting|dc:28](expert) to find the weak spot and crack each facet, or dispel magic (5th level; counteract DC 26) to counteract each facet; the hazard has a number of facets equal to the number of PCs.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-11: Prisoners of the Electric Castle

details.routine:
The hazard gains a number of actions equal to the number of PCs; it loses 1 action each round for each facet that has been disabled. Each claw tears at a different PC, who takes @Damage[(2d10+13)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:30|basic] save). On a failure, they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] by the claw ([[/act escape dc=28]]). On a critical failure, or on a failure if they are already grabbed, they have their soul ripped from their body, which falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious]. Each round on their turn, the soul can attempt a @Check[intimidation|dc:28], @Check[occultism|dc:28], or @Check[survival|dc:28] check to fortify itself and return to its body as a three-action activity. The possible outcomes are as follows.

Critical Success The soul returns to their body, which loses the unconscious condition.

Success The soul returns to their body, which loses the unconscious condition, but gains the grabbed condition.

Failure The soul makes no progress toward escape.

Critical Failure The soul makes no progress toward escape and the target takes @Damage[(2d10+13)[mental]] damage until they are reduced to 0 Hit Points, after which they remain in captivity until the trap is defeated.

Each claw focuses on its target until the target is reduced to 0 Hit Points, after which it takes no action on its turn unless another facet is disabled or its target regains Hit Points.

description.value:
Trigger Pathfinder agents enter the electric castle

Effect Spectral hag claws emerge from the walls. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Pressure plates trigger hidden mauls in the walls of the pits that swing up to slam creatures and knock them off the span.
details.disable: @Check[society|dc:30|name:Locate the hidden switch] (expert) to locate the hidden deactivation switch or @Check[thievery|dc:33|name:Jame the mechanics] (expert) to jam the mechanics on the span.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-14: The Tomb Between Worlds

details.reset: The trap resets over the course of the round and is ready to swing again 1 round later. It can be manually reset if Disabled.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves onto the span.

Effect The mauls swing up, making an attack against the triggering creature.

If struck by the Mauls strike, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:28] save or be knocked off the span into a pit. The target can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

details.description: A magical flame changes color in response to Fort Ozem's alert status, with a @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10-foot radius}.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:30], @Check[nature|dc:30], @Check[occultism|dc:30], or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to snuff out the flame with opposing magic; each success reduces the flame's color one step (from red to blue, blue to yellow, and so on). A critical success reduces the flame's color two steps. A success while the flame is white permanently disables the hazard.
details.reset: The hazard resets automatically each round. After 1 hour, the flame reverts to its default status of a white flame.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the radius of the flame's light or starts its turn in the radius

Effect Iomedae's Flame flares, producing an effect determined by the flame's current color. Followers of Iomedae treat the result of all saving throws against the flame's effects as one step better. The effects are cumulative; a creature that enters the radius of the red flame also triggers the effect of the white, yellow, and blue flames.

White Flame The flame casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Revealing Light] (DC 28).

Yellow Flame The flame casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Fear] (DC 28) on each creature in the light's radius.

Blue Flame The flame flickers as winds buffet creatures in the area. Any creature in the flame's light takes @Damage[8d4[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic|options:area-effect] save). This damage has the nonlethal trait against living creatures.

Red Flame The flame releases a sudden pulse of intense heat. Any creature in the flame's light takes @Damage[4d6[fire],4d6[spirit]|options:area-damage|traits:holy]{4d6 fire damage and 4d6 spirit damage} (@Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic|options:area-effect] save). This damage has the holy trait.

details.description: An invisible rune creates an invisible, spherical magical sensor with a 20-foot radius.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) to erase the rune without triggering the sensor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 26) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #4-09: Killer in the Golden Mask

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the sensor area

Effect The rune detonates a fireball centered on the triggering creature's square. This is a 5th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Fireball] spell that deals @Damage[10d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic]).

stealth.details: (trained) to detect the magical runes in the maze; noticing the maze itself has a DC of 0

details.description: Invisible runes in the maze disorient those within it and cause its mirrored walls to shift about.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Stop the mirrors|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap] (expert) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 28) to stop the mirrors' shuffling; @Check[occultism|dc:28|name:Dispel the minotaur|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap] or @Check[religion|dc:28|name:Dispel the minotaur|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap] (expert) to dispel the minotaur

publication.title: Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any creature in the maze.
details.routine:
(3 actions) The trap uses its first action to magically shuffle the mirrors, rendering markings or maps of the maze ineffective. Creatures in the maze that fail a @Check[will|dc:26|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap] save are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} until they leave the maze. If the target fails additional saves against this ability, the condition value increases by 1 (to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 4}).

For its second action, the trap's ghostly minotaur attacks a random creature in the maze with its spectral gore.

For its third action, the trap makes another spectral gore attack against a random creature.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the maze

Effect Creatures in the maze can't escape it except by use of teleportation magic or as described in Trapped in the Maze. The trap rolls initiative.

Once each turn after it has taken a move action within the maze, each target in the maze can spend 1 action to attempt a @Check[perception|dc:26|name:Escape the Maze|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap] or @Check[survival|dc:26|name:Escape the Maze|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap] check to escape it. A group traveling the maze together is treated as a single target; no more than one creature in the group can attempt this check each round, but accompanying creatures can Aid this check. The possible outcomes follow. A target attempting to leave the maze the same way it entered uses the outcome for one degree of success better than the result of its roll when attempting this check (failure to success, for example).

Critical Success The target escapes the maze.

Success The target is on the right path to the exit. If the target was already on the right path, it escapes the maze.

Failure The target makes no progress toward escape.

Critical Failure The target makes no progress toward escape, and if it was on the right path, it no longer is.

details.description: Anyone looking into the hole has memories sucked from their head.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:29] (master) to feed the pit false thoughts that plug it, or @Check[occultism|dc:28] (expert) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 27) to drain the pit's magic
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour if it's not disabled.

description.value:
Trigger A creature looks into the pit

Effect Ephemeral energies pour from the creature's head, and the creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 1 hour and loses 1 minute of recent memories (a memory of the GM's choosing within the last 24 hours).

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 3}.

details.description: A flurry of scattered flower petals flies through the area, as psychic impressions of pain, sorrow, and being trapped assail the living—Mirida's scattered memories.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:31] or @Check[religion|dc:31] (expert) to weaken the haunt, @Check[deception|dc:29] or @Check[diplomacy|dc:29] (expert) to soothe the haunt's sorrow; three total successes in any combination are required to disable the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The petals disperse after 1 minute and reset after 1 day.
details.routine: (1 action) The flower petals surge through the room, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save). A creature that fails is additionally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 4} on a critical failure).

description.value:
Trigger Two or more creatures enter the area
