Effect Fragments of Mirida's scattered thoughts and memories manifest in the room as flower petals, the sounds of weeping, whimpers of pain, and shrieks echo through the area, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[mental]|options:area-damage] damage to all intelligent creatures in the area (@Check[will|dc:32|basic] save). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: Negative-energy charged natron sprays out of the tub and starts to mummify nearby creatures.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30|name:Safely remove the vise] (expert) to safely remove the umbral vise from the tub without triggering the natron or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 28) to counteract the ritual energy.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-14: The Tomb Between Worlds

details.reset: The trap resets after 24 hours.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches or disturbs the umbral vise.

Effect Natron sprays out of the tub, infusing all creatures adjacent to the tub with void energy and desiccating their internal organs. Each creature takes @Damage[(2d10+4)[void],(2d10+4)[poison]]{2d10+4 void damage and 2d10+4 poison damage} (@Check[fortitude|dc:32|basic] save). On a failure, the internal damage makes the creature more susceptible to other effects that would cause it to mummify, such as Mummy Rot. For 1 day, the first time it is exposed to such an effect, it uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its saving throw.

details.description: The evercursed crystals suddenly extinguish if they are disturbed, taking the PCs' sight away with their failing light.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (master) to subtly sift the ashes aside in a way where the curse simply fades without discerning a target or @Check[occultism|dc:33] (expert) to know how to harmlessly disperse the malign magic

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

description.value:
Trigger A creature disturbs the ashes in the fireplace or attempts to manipulate any of the evercursed crystals

Effect The crystals burn out, becoming non-magical stones, and the triggering creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:32|options:inflicts:dazzled,inflicts:blinded] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature's vision blurs and dims, and they become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 24 hours.

Failure As success, but with an unlimited duration.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] with an unlimited duration.

details.description: The sky suddenly darkens with storm clouds rumbling overhead, the faces of the dead forming in the clouds above as their voices wail along the high winds.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:28] (expert) to funnel the magic powering the weather back into itself, or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to exorcise some of the trapped souls and weaken the storm; three total successes in any combination are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the Shadefields but resets immediately. The occult weather resets 1 day after being disabled if the ancestor storm isn't put to rest.
details.routine: (4 actions) The occult weather intensifies, with winds picking up speed and lightning and thunder sounding overhead. The occult weather uses 3 actions to make lightning Strikes against three random creatures, favoring orcs or anyone who attempted to Disable it within the past round, then uses Wind of Screams as its final action.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the haunt's area

Effect The clouds darken and thunder roars, filled with the wails and screams of those killed in these lands. The shrieks of the spirits deal @Damage[(3d6+13)[sonic]] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] save). The haunt then rolls initiative.

The occult weather intensifies the wind blowing around the area, buffeting all creatures within a @Template[type:line|distance:60]. Each creature in the line must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save or be knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], taking @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]] damage.

details.description: The Eternity Arch sparks with electricity, releasing bolts of lightning at each of the four largest time rifts.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (expert) or @Check[arcana|dc:28], @Check[nature|dc:28], @Check[occultism|dc:28], or @Check[religion|dc:28] (expert) to seal each of the four time rifts, @Check[crafting|dc:30] (trained) to divert the lightning to another source, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 26) to counteract each time rift.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Special #3-99: Fate in the Future

details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled time rift. On each of the trap's actions, the Eternity Arch releases a bolt of lightning that activates that rift, dealing @Damage[2d12[electricity]] damage to all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:10] around the time rift (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save). It can release a bolt at each active time rift no more than once each turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of one of the time rifts

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[1d12[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save). The overloading arch rolls initiative.

details.description: A metal spire bursts from the ground, skewering the target and knocking aside other creatures.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:30] or @Check[occultism|dc:30] (expert) to seal the planar breach, or @Check[nature|dc:28] (master) to magically reinforce the ground and prevent a spire's emergence.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the area

Effect A 60-foot-tall metal spire rises from the ground, puncturing the triggering creature and dealing @Damage[(4d10+26)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save). On a critical failure, the creature is additionally impaled, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] until it [[/act escape dc=30]]{Escapes}. A creature that Escapes is 60 feet in the air. Additionally, all creatures within 10-feet of the spire take @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save). On a failure, they're knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

stealth.details: (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:27|name:Notice Sluice Gates|traits:complex,mechanical,trap,hazard] (expert) to notice the six cleverly hidden sluice gates while they are closed

details.description: Six sluice gates open to allow thousands of gallons of water to begin flooding the room. The water churns through the room and flows out through three chutes on the south end of the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Close a Sluice|traits:complex,mechanical,trap,hazard] (expert) twice to close a sluice (on a critical success, a sluice is closed immediately)
details.reset: The trap resets if all six sluices are closed. If all the water has drained when this occurs, the water takes many months to refill.
details.routine:
(6 actions) The trap loses 1 action per disabled or destroyed sluice. On each of its actions, the water rushing from the sluices flows through the room, creating a river of churning water that fills the room to several inches and makes the floor of the chamber into difficult terrain. The water level never rises above this level in the room, as it drains out of the chutes to the south and begins to flood the quarry pit (area J2). On the trap's second turn, the water starts filling the four slave pits in area J2. Each of the trap's actions fills the pits 4 inches (2 feet per round if all sluices are functional). If the slave pits fill to their total depth of 12 feet and the grates over the slave pits aren't open, the slaves begin to drown, dying if they aren't freed in 5 rounds. Once the slave pits are full, the water continues to slowly fill the central area over the course of an hour until it empties the reservoir and floods area J2 to a depth of 15 feet.

Other options beyond disabling or destroying the sluices could prevent the flooding of the quarry pit at your discretion.

description.value:
Trigger A creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Interact]{Interacts} to throw either the primary lever or backup lever.

Effect The trap rolls initiative.

details.description: A shockwave of energy surges out from the room's center.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:29] (expert) to fool the wards, @Check[thievery|dc:31] (master) to disable the wards, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 28) to counteract the wards
details.reset: Automatic

description.value:
Trigger A creature that isn't a Rivethun practitioner enters the room

Effect The trap deals @Damage[(5d4+12)[force]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[fortitude|dc:32|basic] save). On a failed save, they're additionally knocked back 10 feet.

details.description: Sargassum heaps that have only started to grow have no intelligence, but are incredibly tenacious and can reflexively grab onto nearby prey. They obtain nutrients by drowning prey in the water they grow in.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:32] (expert) to rip the reflexive tendrils off the bud or @Check[thievery|dc:32] (expert) to trigger the tendrils beforehand and keep them occupied.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-17: The Devil in the Details

details.reset: 1 hour if the bud pile has something in its grasps. 1 round if the bud pile has nothing in its grasps.

description.value:
Trigger a creature moves into a square adjacent to the bud pile or into the square the bud pile occupies

Effect The sargassum bud pile whips out numerous tendrils, attempting to grab the creature. The target must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:28] save.

Critical Success The target dodges the tendril and is unaffected.

Success A couple of tendrils latch on to the target. The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed]. The target can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] by attempting an Athletics or Acrobatics skill check of DC 28.

Failure Several tendrils grab the target. The target becomes grabbed and must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] or fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. If the target cannot breathe in water, they begin to suffocate. The DC to Escape increases to 30.

Critical Failure As failure, except the DC to Escape increases to 35.

stealth.details: (the tar lake is blatantly obvious, and its effects occur near the end of each round)

details.description: The bubbling tar churns and seethes, periodically bursting in a burning hot splatter of scalding sludge and gas.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:30] check to throw rocks or other bits of debris into areas within the tar lake that are about to burst, allowing the lake to vent harmlessly for that round (on a critical success, this prevents the lake from venting for 3 rounds); permanently disabling the tar pit would require powerful magic or long-term engineering projects (subject to the GM's discretion).
details.routine: (1 action) The bubbling lake of tar churns and sputters. One randomly determined target within 5 feet of the tar lake is targeted by a Scalding Burst.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to move through the tar

Effect A scalding burst of hot tar burns the creature, which must immediately attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:28] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[6d6[fire],1d6[persistent,fire]]{6d6 fire damage and 1d6 persistent fire damage}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[9d6[fire],2d6[persistent,fire]]{9d6 fire damage, 2d6 persistent fire damage} and takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speed as long as it continues to take the persistent fire damage from the hot tar clinging to it.

stealth.details: (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:29|traits:action:seek] (expert) to spot the control panel

details.description: Five scroll symbols shoot electricity bolts around the room.
details.disable:
@Check[thievery|dc:25|name:Counteract Magic|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (3rd rank; counteract DC 24)

@Check[thievery|dc:29|name:Disable Trap (Control Panel)|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to disable the entire trap from the hidden control panel beneath the central slab.
details.reset: The trap deactivates 1 round after it has no target creatures and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine:
(5 actions) Each disabled symbol reduces the trap's number of actions by 1.

For each of its actions, a different symbol shoots an electricity bolt at a random creature in the room. The trap doesn't take multiple attack penalties.

description.value:
Hardness 16

HP 64 (BT 32)

Resistances Electricity 15

Immunities Critical Hits, Object Immunities, Precision Damage

Hardness 18

HP 72 (BT 36)

Resistances Electricity 15

Immunities Critical Hits, Object Immunities, Precision Damage

Trigger A creature without an Aspis Consortium badge approaches within 5 feet of a slab

Effect Both double doors leading out of the room shut and lock. While the trap is active, the doors must be battered open (Hardness 18; Panel HP 72 [BT 36]; @Check[athletics|dc:29|name:Batter Down Doors|traits:action:force-open] to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open]). The closest symbol to the triggering creature shoots an electricity bolt attack at it. The trap then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: A heart of tumorous, shadowy tissue unleashes magic on nearby creatures.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:26] or @Check[occultism|dc:26] (expert) to counter the heart's offensive magic, or @Check[religion|dc:28] (trained) to purify the area containing the heart. Four successes are required to disable it; after two successes, the shadow heart can't use its shadow lash Strike.
details.reset: The heart deactivates if there are no creatures present in the room. It resets in 1 hour.
details.routine: The shadow heart uses Battering Vortex, and then uses shadow lash on a random creature within range.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the heart

Effect The heart gives a slow beat, and all creatures in the room must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:30] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} and unable to take reactions for 1 round (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3} on a critical failure). The dark heart then rolls initiative.

A swirling vortex of shadows forms around the heart, lifting all creatures in the room and slamming them forcefully to the ground, dealing @Damage[4d10[bludgeoning]] damage. Each creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save. Anyone adjacent to a wall may @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] with a successful @Check[reflex|dc:28] save; on a success at Grabbing an Edge, the creature uses a result one step better than the roll of their Fortitude save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Failure The creature takes full damage, is knocked prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage, is knocked prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 3} for 1 round.

details.description: Invisible runes are concealed within an elaborate light-woven floor mosaic.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (trained) to remove pieces from the mosaic without triggering the magic or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 26) to counteract the rune

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-19: Mean Streets of Shadow Absalom

description.value:
Trigger A creature walks upon the mosaic.

Effect The trap shoots a fusillade of shadow-wreathed spears out of the walls, making a Strike against each creature in area A1. Creatures who take damage from the spears must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:28] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1} as the spears tear off part of their shadow.

details.description: A magical rune is inscribed on the tarry earth at the entrance to the hut, buried beneath snow, with an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Invisible] sensor that detects creatures within a 10-foot-radius sphere.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30|name:Erase the Rune|traits:hazard] (master) to erase the rune without triggering the sensor or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 28) to dispel the rune

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature comes within 10 feet of the rune

Effect The rune unleashes a deafening peal of thunder that deals @Damage[10d8[sonic]] damage in a 20-foot burst (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic]).

details.description: Pressure plates in the floor, wall, and ceiling of this hall trigger silver blades to protrude from the ceiling, slashing anything passing through the hall.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28] (master) to disable the pressure plates; two successes are required to disable the trap

publication.title: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

details.reset: The trap deactivates after 1 minute and can be manually reset.
details.routine: (2 actions) The maze of blades continues to spin, jab, and slash, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[slashing]|traits:magical|options:damage:material:silver,area-damage] damage (magic, silver) to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on the floor or places pressure on the walls or ceiling of these two adjoining halls

Effect Silver blades erupt from the walls and wildly slash everything in the area, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[slashing]|traits:magical|options:damage:material:silver,area-damage] damage (magic, silver) to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Two hosts of shadowy spirits emerge from Hell to rip Oourax's soul in two, damaging all those who stand in their path.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:33] or @Check[deception|dc:33] to convince the spirits to ignore you, or @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] to weaken the haunt; four successes are required to disable it. After 5 rounds, Oourax's soul is sundered and the haunt ends.
details.routine: (1 action) Oourax's soul attempts to flee, moving 20 feet in a random direction. Her soul is pursued by two hosts of devilish spirits. Large or smaller creatures (or troops composed of Large or smaller creatures) within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] of Oourax's soul are trampled by the spirits (@Damage[(2d10+13)[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger Oourax dies

Effect Oourax's soul rises from her body. The haunt rolls initiative.

details.description: A cyclone of angry spirits surges around the area.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:30] (trained) or @Check[religion|dc:30] (trained) to weaken the haunt; three total successes in any combination are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: The spirit cyclone disperses after 1 minute and resets after 1 day.
details.routine: (3 actions) The spirit cyclone uses 3 actions to move, traveling up to 30 feet with each action and dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[void]] damage to each creature in its path (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save). A creature needs to attempt only one save during the cyclone's movement, even if the cyclone moves over its space more than once. On a critical failure, a creature is swept up into the cyclone, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] ([[/act escape dc=32]]). A creature grabbed by the cyclone moves along with the cyclone and takes @Damage[(1d10+6)[void]] damage at their beginning of its turn, and it must attempt a Reflex save against the cyclone on the cyclone's turn, no matter where the cyclone moves. A creature that successfully Escapes from the cyclone falls from a height of [[/r {1d12*5} #Falling Distance]]{1d12 x 5 feet}.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more creatures enter the area

Effect A spiraling column of spirits gather, becoming a whirling cyclone of souls @Template[burst|distance:5]{10 feet wide} and 60 feet tall. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: Four animal statues sit on pillars, one in each corner of the statuary, with decorated tiles between them in a seemingly random arrangement.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28] (expert) or @Check[stealth|dc:31] (expert) to disarm the statue while adjacent to it

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-20: The Rakshasa's Court

details.reset: Each statue resets immediately after being triggered.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on one of the plain silver tiles in the statuary

Effect A wave of cold passes through the target, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:26|basic] save).

Trigger A creature steps on one of the diamond-embedded silver tiles in the statuary

Effect A jolt of electricity arcs up from the tile and strikes the target, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save).

Trigger A creature steps on one of the plain copper tiles in the statuary

Effect The high-pitched yowl of an attacking cat echoes in the target's head, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:26|basic] save).

details.description: A field of warm mist covers an open plain. Pockets of highly pressurized steam lie @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] in the earth; pressure releases jets of steam.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:30] (expert) twice to navigate a safe path

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #1-24: Lightning Strikes, Stars Fall

description.value:
Trigger A creature fails a skill check to navigate through the steaming field

Effect Several jets of pressurized steam erupt, dealing @Damage[(3d6+10)[bludgeoning],(3d6+10)[fire]]{3d6+10 bludgeoning damage and 3d6+10 fire damage} to creatures within 30 feet of the ground (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic]).

details.description: A 15-foot-wide patch of tar covered with dirt and leaves attempts to capture creatures that step onto it.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:25|name:Reveal the Tar Pit|traits:environmental,hazard] (expert) to disturb the surface and reveal the tar pit
details.reset: Though the hazard still captures anyone who touches it, the surface doesn't become hidden again until it settles, which takes 24 hours.
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the tar pit pulls down each creature within it, thick tar adhering to the creature's body. A creature that was submerged up to its waist becomes submerged up to its neck, and a creature that was submerged up to its neck is pulled under and must hold its breath to avoid suffocation. A creature in the tar pit can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:33] check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Climb] to either raise itself by one step (if it's submerged to its neck or full submerged) or to move 5 feet if it's submerged only up to its waist. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down one step. Other creatures can Aid the creature, typically by using a rope or similar aid or by attempting to pull the creature out with their own @Check[athletics|dc:33] check, with the same results as if the creature attempted the check. In addition to the usual results, a character adjacent to the tar pit who critically fails while Aiding moves into the tar pit. A creature that Climbs out of the tar pit escapes the hazard and lands @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in an adjacent space.

description.value:
Trigger A Huge or smaller creature walks onto the tar pit

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the tar pit up to its waist. The tar pit rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

details.description: A raging storm covers the mountain lake as spring water levitates from the lake to fall back down in a driving spiral of steaming rain. In the depths of the storm, barely visible, is a tiny koi egg surrounded by three bands of water.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:29], @Check[religion|dc:29], @Check[nature|dc:29], or @Check[occultism|dc:29] to purify the magic corrupting the ascension ritual, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:32] or @Check[performance|dc:32] to soothe the spirit within the koi egg and give it the strength to fight off the corruption itself; four total successes are required to purify the ritual, each of the first three successes purifying one ring of water and the final one halting the ritual itself. If the cursed comb has not been removed from the ritual site, then succeeding on this final check merely halts the ritual; if the cursed comb has been removed from the ritual site, then succeeding on this final check completes the ascension ritual.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-11: The Godsrain and the Dragon

details.reset: The turbulent koi egg can attempt to hatch again, restarting the ascension ritual, after 1000 years of Xalreonshin's care.
details.routine: (1 action) Torrential water descends from the sky, taking the shape of a dragon that smashes into one creature within 60 feet of the egg. The dragon always targets the creature that is closest to the egg, dealing @Damage[2d12[bludgeoning],2d12[fire]]{2d12 bludgeoning damage and 2d12 fire} damage in a spray of boiling water (@Check[fortitude|dc:28] save). Creatures that fail the save are pushed 10 feet away. Creatures that have the earth trait, are carrying an item with the earth trait, or are otherwise strongly tied to elemental earth (such as an earth elemental sorcerer or kineticist) get the result one degree of success better than the outcome of their save, as the power of elemental earth overcomes water.

items.name: Immune to All Damage (Until Final Ring is Purified)

description.value:
Trigger Xalreonshin rolls initiative, three rounds pass after the PCs begin speaking with Xalreonshin, or a creature other than Xalreonshin comes within 40 feet of the egg

Effect The turbulent koi egg churns the lake surface, ejecting water into the sky. A 1-mile-radius area centered on the egg becomes filled with heavy rain and strong wind imposing a -2 circumstance penalty to Perception checks and requiring flying creatures to [[/act maneuver-in-flight dc=24]].

details.description: A mysterious portrait lies face down on the floor.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:31] (trained) to carefully dismantle the portrait without jostling it enough to trigger it or @Check[religion|dc:33] (expert) to exorcise the haunt; 3 successes are required to disable it.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to move the portrait

Effect Once someone flips the portrait over, they see a headless man portrayed in fine clothing, only to have the name "VELICARN" appear across the portrait in the form of bloody letters that run from the headless man's raw stump. If the triggering creature didn't attempt to look at the portrait, the painting wrenches and twists to present itself to the target, who must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:28] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). A moment later, a trio of shadowy forms that drip and splatter blood surge out from the portrait, leaving it a blank canvas. These shapes appear vaguely as headless humanoids made of red smoke and leave bloodstains wherever they touch, but they're otherwise treated as typical wraiths. The wraiths roll initiative and remain for 1 minute, after which they vanish, and the portrait becomes a blank but bloodstained canvas. The wraiths also vanish if someone disables the haunt before the duration expires.

details.description: Nearby creatures are caught in the memory of the struggle between Iron Taviah and the Benefactor.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:28] or @Check[religion|dc:28] (expert) in the room to drain away the haunt's spiritual energy. Each successful check reduces the number of actions in the haunt's routine; three successful checks are required to completely disable the haunt.
details.reset: Unless completely disabled or destroyed, the haunt resets at the next moonrise at full strength.
details.routine: (3 actions) The spectral Taviah uses 1 action to appear adjacent to a random creature within 30 feet if not already adjacent to a creature. It then uses its spectral claw Strike with its remaining actions.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the spilled goblet

Effect The desperation of the sudden fight fills the minds of all creatures in the dining room as the apparition of Iron Taviah at the north end of the table lunges. The haunt deals @Damage[4d10[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:30|basic]) to each creature in the room. On a failed save, the creature also takes @Damage[1d6[persistent,mental]] damage. The haunt then rolls for initiative.

details.description: The spectral figure of a handsome, well-dressed man flees down the stairs, clutching a wound in his side with one hand and making a shoving motion with the other. His shout drives creatures back.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to drain away the haunt's spiritual energy. This check can be attempted only from the top of the stairs behind the secret door.
details.reset: The haunt ends as soon as there are no creatures within 60 feet. It resets when the secret door is closed.
details.routine: (1 action) (divine, evocation, incapacitation, sonic) The spectral figure shouts, dealing @Damage[4d10[sonic]] damage to all creatures within 60 feet of the secret door (@Check[will|dc:28|basic|traits:divine,evocation,incapacitation,sonic]). A creature that fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger The secret door to the basement stairs opens

Effect The spectral figure's shout shoves every creature in the room away from the east wall. Creatures in the drawing room or the adjacent foyer must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:28] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature isn't moved.

Success The creature is pushed back 10 feet. If this movement would push it into a wall, it takes bludgeoning damage equal to the number of feet it was moved.

Failure As success, but 20 feet and the creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure As success, but 20 feet, and the creature is knocked prone and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2}.

details.description: Several ghostly mammoths arise from the floorboards, pawing at the ground in terror before stampeding toward the door.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:28] to exorcise the animals' spirits, or @Check[nature|dc:26] or @Check[stabling-lore|dc:24] to soothe them
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger The door to the stable opens

Effect The mammoth spirits activate in panic, materializing into physical forms as they stampede toward the door to escape. All creatures standing within 20 feet of the door take @Damage[(4d10+26)[force]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save).

details.description: The anger of the fallen Umbra Legion seeps into any creature entering combat.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:26] (expert) to exorcise the closest spirits or @Check[diplomacy|dc:30] (expert) to calm them down

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters combat while in Umbra's Last Stand

Effect The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save as the rage of the surrounding orcs threatens to overwhelm them.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is confused for 1 minute.

details.description: The PCs hear the sound of sobbing and feel an overwhelming sense of sorrow, as if they'd lost something more precious to them than their own life.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:32] or @Check[performance|dc:32] (master) to laugh away the sadness, @Check[occultism|dc:31] (expert) to banish the psychic imprint, @Check[thievery|dc:33] (master) to disperse the emotional energy
details.reset: The trap resets automatically after 24 hours.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches the statue

Effect The trap deals @Damage[(4d10+14)[mental]] damage to all creatures in the room (@Check[will|dc:28|basic] save). On a failed save, a creature additionally falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] and begins to weep, taking @Damage[1d10[persistent,mental]] damage.

stealth.details: (expert) to sense the terror of the spirits inside the vault

details.description: The spirits of the island's citizens manifest and panic as the vault fills with seawater.
details.disable: DC 33 Athletics (trained) twice to [[/act force-open dc=33]] the vault's door or @Check[religion|dc:30] (trained) three times to exorcise the spirits
details.reset: The waters recede and the spirits disappear once no living creatures remain inside the vault, but the haunt resets immediately, triggering again if a living creature steps inside.
details.routine:
(1 action) Water begins to fill the vault from a crack in the floor hidden beneath the bones. The spirits of the citizens panic and grasp at the living, begging them to act. Each creature in the vault takes @Damage[(2d10+13)[void]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] saving throw. On a failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} on a critical failure).

The first time the hazard takes this action, water fills the vault to knee-height, making the area difficult terrain. The second time the hazard takes this action, the vault fills halfway with water, and creatures inside must succeed at a @Check[athletics|dc:15] check each round to Swim. The third time the hazard takes this action, the vault fills entirely with water, and creatures inside must hold their breath.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature spends more than 1 minute inside the vault

Effect The vault's door slams shut, trapping any creatures inside. Meanwhile, the souls of the dozens of citizens who died when Pakilykos sank manifest throughout the vault. The haunt then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to notice the mound of gore is haunted; noticing the mound itself has a DC of 0

details.description: A hideous mound of flesh, shattered bone, tangles of hair, and what appears to be gray matter lies in a glistening, 2-foot-tall heap on the floor, only to suddenly start piling up on itself to gradually form an immense, hateful head.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to exorcise the haunt; three successes area required to disable it

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: Velicarn's head collapses back into a 2-foot-high mound of gore at the end of any round in which no non-fiend living creatures are in the library. It then resets 1 minute later.
details.routine: (1 action) On its turn, Velicarn's head continues to grow and expand as it becomes a monstrous distortion of his once-human appearance, with beady red eyes, sharp fangs, and two bat-like wings that unfurl from either side. The haunt screeches louder, and all creatures in the library take @Damage[4d6[persistent,bleed]] damage as blood pours from their ears, eyes, and mouths (@Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] save). If Velicarn's head isn't disabled or destroyed by the end of the third round, it rips out of the ground and joins the fight as a horrific monstrosity—a daqqanoenyent. At this point, the haunt is automatically disabled, and the PCs must face this new threat. As a daqqanoenyent, Velicarn's head focuses its attacks on the PCs and ignores the sahkil (who, if still alive, titters and cackles in delight at this development). It pursues PCs relentlessly and fights until it's destroyed, which permanently destroys the hazard.

description.value:
Trigger A living non-fiend creature approaches within 15 feet of the remains of Velicarn's head

Effect The mound of gore shudders and quakes, then starts to pile up on itself, slowly transforming into an immense, screaming head. All living creatures in area F1 must attempt a @Check[will|dc:28|options:inflicts:frightened] save as the gurgling birth-shriek of the awakening horror tears through the room. Those who fail become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: Faceless statues struggle against each other to stay above the black dust.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:27|name:Convince the Statues] (expert) to urge the statues to work together, @Check[intimidation|dc:30|traits:action:coerce|name:Coerce the Statues] (expert) to Coerce the statues away, or @Check[occultism|dc:33|name:Oppose Psychic Influence] (trained) to oppose the psychic influence. Each check to disable the haunt is a two-action activity; three total successes are required to disable the haunt.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #3-17: Dreams of a Dustbound Isle

details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: (1 action) On the haunt's initiative, creatures in the area sink deeper into the dust as statues grasp at them. Creatures in the area take @Damage[(2d10+13)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:28|basic|options:inflicts:confused] saving throw). On a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round. Creatures @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] by the haunt take a -1 circumstance penalty to their saving throw.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature spends 1 round in the area

Effect A whispered shout carries through the air: "I have a face! I have a name! My sisters may be forgotten, but I will reclaim what's mine" Sinking statues desperately grasp at creatures in the area, struggling to stay above the surface. The haunt attempts an [[/r 1d20+21 #Grapple]]{Athletics check (with a +21 modifier)} to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grapple] each creature in the area. On a success, a creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] ([[/act escape dc=31]]). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: This dangerous sooty black mold readily releases its toxic spores when disturbed. The entire swath of fungus in area B2 is a single organism. There are nine 10-foot-square patches to remove, but the whole organism dies once it takes enough damage.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:27|name:Remove Patch|traits:environmental,fungus] (expert) to remove a 10-foot-square patch of ash web without triggering the spores

publication.title: Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City

details.reset: This large patch of ash web resets automatically at the start of the round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the ash web's space or damages the web with any type of damage other than cold damage.

Requirements The ash web is not in direct sunlight.

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within 10 feet of that creature are exposed to ash web spores.

The enfeebled condition from ash web remains even after the poison's duration ends. The condition's value reduces by 1 per hour. Gugs are immune to this poison; instead, they have vivid and strange dreams when they sleep after ingesting ash web.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:29]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 3} (1 round).

details.description: A hard-to-reach rune is placed on the lock's tumbler. It emits a blast of force when the door is jostled.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32|name:Disarm the Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to disarm the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 28) to counteract the rune's magic.
details.reset: The rune resets 1 minute after it triggers.

description.value:
Trigger A non-undead creature opens the door or critically fails an attempt to disarm or dispel the rune

Effect A @Template[cone|distance:30] of force issues forth from the lock. Creatures within the cone must succeed a @Check[fortitude|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect] saving throw or take @Damage[8d12[force]|options:area-damage] damage. Those who fail the save are pushed 10 feet, and those who critically fail are pushed 20 feet and are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2}.

stealth.details: (master) to notice your vision tint red

details.description: A wailing spirit appears and gouges their own eyes. As they do, blood seeps from the eyes of those who witness the act, obscuring their vision.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:30|name:Talk to the spirit] (master) to talk the spirit out of gouging its eyes or @Check[religion|dc:32|name:Ease the spirit's sorrows] (trained) to ease the spirit's sorrows
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 15 feet

Effect Blood seeps from the eyes of all creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet}, dealing persistent bleed damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:33] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+3)[bleed]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d8+6)[bleed]] damage and is dazzled for as long as the persistent damage lasts.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(8d8+12)[bleed]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for as long as the persistent damage lasts.

details.description: An object haunted by the echoes of a vicious mind attempts to kill someone who comes near.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:29|name:Exorcise Spirit] (master) to exorcise the spirit or @Check[diplomacy|dc:31|name:Talk Down the Spirit] (expert) to talk it down

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the haunted object

Effect The haunt takes control of the triggering creature, forcing it to attack itself. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:29] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target makes a Strike against itself and automatically hits; the target also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target makes a Strike against itself and automatically scores a critical hit; the target also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The target attempts a Fortitude save. If the target succeeds, it is subject to the effects of a failure instead. If the target fails, it is reduced to 0 HP and dies

details.description: The head of the Granite Ox tosses back and forth as the creature charges toward Dhuraxilis.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:27] (master) to tug one of the ox's ears, @Check[arcana|dc:32], @Check[nature|dc:32], @Check[occultism|dc:32], or @Check[religion|dc:32] (expert) to distract the Ox with a display of magic, or @Check[acrobatics|dc:35] (expert) to dangle in front of the Ox's eyes and obscure its vision; three successes are required to disable the hazard
details.reset: The hazard ends automatically after 5 rounds or once all PCs are thrown from the Ox's back, whichever comes first.
details.routine: (1 action) The Granite Ox bucks. Each creature on the Ox's head must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:33] save. Prone creatures gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the save. On a failure, the creature falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. On a critical failure, the creature flies from the Ox's back, falling 120 feet to the ground or into Kardaji Bay.

description.value:
Trigger A creature reaches the Granite Ox's head or attempts a skill check to disable the hazard

Effect The Granite Ox tosses its head. Creatures in the area @Damage[(2d12+13)[bludgeoning]] damage must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] saving throw. The hazard rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is knocked prone, and takes @Damage[1d12[persistent,bleed]] damage as it scrapes across the Ox's scales.

details.description: A metal lattice forged from warshards feeds into a dark purplish crystal that pulses with energy. If the configuration of the lattice changes, so does the energy the device emits.
details.disable: Three [[/act disable-device dc=27]] (see Disabling the Focus under Event 1 on page 18).

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-17: The Devil in the Details

details.routine: (3 actions) The Nahyndrian Focus uses Select Condition to determine which condition it is going to inflict. It then uses Inflict twice, usually on two different targets. If the focus is broken, it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1}, and can only Inflict once per turn.

description.value:
Trigger Yollen activates the Nahyndrian Focus

Effect The Nahyndrian Focus shifts into attack mode and rolls initiative. All creatures without the unholy trait in within @Template[emanation|distance:60]{60 feet} must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:35|basic] save against @Damage[(4d12+26)[spirit]] damage.

The Nahyndrian Focus rolls a [[/gmr 1d4 #Condition]] to randomly decide whether it is going cause the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled], or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied] condition when it uses Inflict until the end of its turn. After the first round, it changes the random roll die to [[/gmr 1d6 #Condition]] as it does not pick the same condition it had in the previous round.

An unhealthy beam shoots out from the focus. One creature without the unholy trait within 60 feet of the Focus must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:31] save.

Critical Success The target becomes immune to Inflict for 1 minute.

Success The targe gains the condition from Select Condition with a value of 1 for 1 minute. If the creature is sickened from the Focus' reaction, the sickened condition increase by 1, up to a maximum of 3.

Failure As success, but with a value of 2.

Critical Failure As success, but with a value of 3.

details.description: The stairs' supports are rigged to collapse if the trip wires are triggered, sending a 10-foot section of stairs tumbling down the cliff.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:25] or Engineering Lore to shore up the supports, @Check[perception|dc:27] to notice the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] bypass lever in the stair railing, or @Check[thievery|dc:29] to disrupt the connecting mechanism

description.value:
Trigger The trip wires are pulled or severed

Effect The wires pull away the mechanisms supporting the stairs, causing them to collapse. Each creature standing on the affected area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:29] save.

Critical Success The creature jumps safely to the cliff and is unaffected.

Success The creature jumps to the cliff, but takes @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage from the leap.

Failure The creature falls to the next section of stairs, 100 feet down the cliff, and takes @Damage[50[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage. They can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling.

Critical Failure As failure, but the fall distance is 200 feet, all the way down the cliff, dealing @Damage[100[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage.

details.description: The anchor points in the platform's corners suddenly tear loose, causing the entire platform to collapse into the shaft below.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:32] (expert) or @Check[thievery|dc:35] (expert) to reinforce one of the anchor points; at least three of the four dangerous anchor points must be disabled to make the lift safe

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

description.value:
Trigger The lift is raised or lowered while at least one Small or larger creature stands on it

Effect Attempt a @Check[flat|dc:11]. This DC increases by 1 for each creature on the lift that's Medium, or by 4 for each Large creature. On a success, the lift operates normally that round, but on a failure, the anchor points give way, dropping the platform and all creatures on the lift into the shaft. A falling creature can arrest their fall by Grabbing an Edge to grab one of the swinging ropes (@Check[reflex|dc:33]), otherwise the creature takes normal falling damage plus an additional @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:33|basic]) from falling debris.

details.description: Fire burns Corazal and threatens to spread to the surrounding forest.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:30] or @Check[survival|dc:30] (expert) to douse flames or @Check[athletics|dc:32] (expert) to smother flames; on the sixth successful check, the flames go out. Casting a non-cantrip water spell on the flames counts as one success.
details.routine: (1 action; fire) On its turn, the fire deals @Damage[(2d8+13)[fire]] damage to creatures in the grove, plus an additional @Damage[6[fire]] damage to Corazal and any creature within 15 feet of them (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches Corazal

Effect The flames blaze higher, dealing @Damage[(2d12+13)[fire]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic] save). The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: The fog solidifies into tiny, gnawing creatures that devour everyone within before fading back into mist.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:28|name:Disrupt the Fog] (expert) to disrupt the fog before the hazard triggers or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 26) to counteract the magic.
details.reset: The hazard resets over the course of an hour as the daemonic stain reenergizes the fog.

description.value:
Trigger A creature crosses the cavern's midpoint (the dotted line on the map)

Effect The hazard deals @Damage[9d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:confused] save) to the triggering creature and all other non-fiend creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:20]{20 feet}. A creature that critically fails the saving throw is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

details.description: Ekriathe's talon releases a pulse of psychic rage throughout the area, transforming the lighthouse's lantern room into a pitched battlefield.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:27] (expert) twice to disrupt the divine mythic energies, or @Check[thievery|dc:32] (expert) twice to cut down the talon and remove it from the room without triggering the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates once no creatures remain in the lantern room, but it resets immediately.
details.routine: (3 actions) The haunt attacks creatures in the area, making spear and talon Strikes against targets determined at random. The haunt takes the multiple attack penalty only if it targets the same creature more than once on a turn. The haunt loses one action if its Hit Points are reduced below its Break Threshold.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than Dorlina touches Ekriathe's talon

Effect Each creature in the area sees the lantern room transform into a battlefield, where Ekriathe and her allies battle a force of human soldiers. Each creature takes @Damage[(4d12+26)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:33|basic] save). A creature that fails or critically fails the save believes it has been transported backward in time and can't willingly leave the lantern room until the haunt is disabled. An affected creature can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end this effect. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: An invisible rune creates an invisible, spherical magical sensor with a 20-foot radius.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32] (expert) to erase the rune without triggering the sensor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 27) to counteract the rune

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the sensor area

Effect The rune detonates a fireball centered on the triggering creature's square. This is a 6th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Fireball] spell that deals @Damage[12d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic|traits:fire|options:item:spell] save).

stealth.details: (trained); @Check[perception|dc:32] (expert) to find the precise location of the steam vents

details.description: Hidden vents emit scalding hot steam made from a particularly acrid solution, irritating the eyes and throat of anyone who inhales it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|name:disable|dc:33] (expert) to block a vent, or @Check[crafting|name:disable|dc:31] (expert) to disable the heating element within the vent that turns the liquid into steam

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-20: The Rakshasa's Court

details.reset: The steam vents cease to operate 1 minute after all creatures have left the area and go into a maintenance cycle for 1 hour. After this time, they can be triggered again.
details.routine: (4 actions) Each of the four steam vents uses a single action each to emit superheated steam, which spreads for 60 feet along the wall from its location and extends out from the wall by 30 feet, dealing @Damage[(2d12+13)[untyped]] damage to each creature in their range (@Check[fortitude|dc:29] save). For each disabled vent, the trap loses 1 action each turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area of the trap

Effect All four vents rapidly heat and let out steam for 5 seconds, filling room with a painfully acrid smell. Each creature in the room must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save, and the trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded].

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 3}, and, struck by a coughing fit, falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

details.description: While the roof is on fire and collapsing timbers crush those within the hall, demons fly down from the sky to pluck victims from the flames.
details.disable: Two @Check[athletics|dc:32] or @Check[diplomacy|dc:32] checks to douse the flames; Athletics to do the work yourself or Diplomacy to muster the ghostly soldiers. This reduces the hazard's actions by 1 and prevents it from using Burning Timbers. Banish the demons with up to two @Check[arcana|dc:35], @Check[occultism|dc:35], or @Check[religion|dc:35] checks; each success reduces the hazard's actions by 1, and two successes prevent it from using Demonic Abduction. When the hazard loses all 3 actions, Burning Timbers, and Demonic Abduction, it's disabled.
details.reset: The hall falls quiet for 24 hours, after which it can trigger again.
details.routine: (3 actions) The haunt spends 1 action to fill the hall with burning timbers falling from above, and 2 actions to pluck up random victims and drop them to their deaths.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the hall or ends its turn in the hall

Effect The hall bursts into flame, dealing @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage to each creature in the hall. The haunt then rolls initiative.
Creatures within the 30-foot by 90-foot area of the hall take @Damage[(4d6+12)[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic]).

Spectral demons lift a single creature 50 feet into the air and drop it. The haunt makes a Strike against the creature with a +23 attack bonus. On a success, the creature is lifted into the air and dropped, taking @Damage[25[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage], though it might negate some or all this damage using a spell, such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Gentle Landing]. On a critical hit, the creature also takes @Damage[(2d12+13)[slashing]] damage as the demons' claws tear through its flesh.

stealth.details: (expert) to detect the magical sensor; noticing the wheel has a DC of 0

details.description: An ornate wheel, divided into eight segments with a rune painted on each, is mounted on a pole and controlled by a lever that can be triggered manually or a sensor that detects creatures within 30 feet in front of it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:31|name:Flip the switch|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap] (master) on the wheel to flip the switch returning it to a harmless carnival game, @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Erase each rune|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap] (expert) to erase each rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 28) to counteract each rune.

publication.title: Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any creature moving within range of its sensor.
details.routine:
(2 actions) On its initiative, the trap uses its first action to spin, then stops. Roll [[/r 1d8]]{1d8} to determine which segment is topmost when the wheel stops spinning. The wheel uses its second action to replicate the spell listed for that segment (5th level, DC 27, spell attack roll +17). This spell's target is centered on or otherwise includes the nearest creature in the spell's area. This increases the spell's range to 30 feet if necessary. Any spell cast by this trap is occult.

1 - @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Black Tentacles] @Template[burst|distance:20|name:Black Tentacles|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap,conjuration,occult]{20-foot burst} [[/r 1d20+17]]{Spell Attack vs. Target's Fortitude DC}

2 - @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Blindness] @Check[fortitude|dc:27|name:Blindness|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap,incapacitation,necromancy,occult]

3 - @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Confusion] @Check[will|dc:27|name:Confusion|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap,emotion,enchantment,mental,occult]

4 - @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Death Ward] Range 30 feet

5 - @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Outcast's Curse] @Check[will|dc:27|name:Outcast's Curse|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap,curse,enchantment,mental,misfortune,occult] Range 30 feet

6 - @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Shadow Blast] @Template[line|distance:30|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap,evocation,occult,shadow|width:5]{30-foot line} @Check[reflex|dc:27|basic|name:Shadow Blast|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap,evocation,occult,shadow] or @Check[will|dc:27|basic|name:Shadow Blast|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap,evocation,occult,shadow] @Damage[5d8[force]] damage

7 - @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Noise Blast] @Template[burst|distance:10|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap,evocation,occult,sonic]{10-foot burst} @Check[fortitude|dc:27|name:Sound Burst|traits:hazard,complex,magical,mechanical,trap,evocation,occult,sonic] @Damage[2d10[sonic]] damage

8 - @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Gecko Grip] Range 30 feet

description.value:
Trigger A creature pulls the lever or enters the sensor's detection area

Effect The wheel begins to spin and rolls initiative.

details.description: The illusion of a normal, if dirty, pond is broken by clammy hands reaching up from the depths. While they never break the surface, travelers feel compelled to reach for the grasping fingers and willingly walk to their doom.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:32], @Check[diplomacy|dc:32] or @Check[religion|dc:32] (expert) to calm the spirits; two successes required, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (4th rank; counteract DC 32) to dispel the magic.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-14: Twice in Steel

details.reset: When there are no living humanoids within 30 feet of the pond, the hands recede into the depths and the pond resets.
details.routine: (1 action) The pond attempts to Grab all PCs in the water with a [[/gmr 1d20+22 #Athletics]]{+22} Athletics bonus. Any @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] PCs take @Damage[(4d12+26)[void]|traits:unholy] (unholy) damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:32|basic] save). All PCs within 30 feet must then make a save against Call of the Depths.

description.value:
Trigger A creature is within 30 feet of the Horrible Pond

Effect All PCs must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save, with results as described below. The pond then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The PC is unaffected

Success The PC feels compelled to reach for the grasping hands, but is otherwise unaffected.

Failure The PC is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} until the end of the pond's next turn and must use their first action on their next turn to move half their speed toward the pond.

Critical Failure The PC is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Controlled] and must use all their actions to walk into the pond, or swim in the pond if they are already in the water.

hp.details: to destroy a channel gate (this prevents that channel from being disabled and stops the trap from resetting); Floor Hardness 18; Floor HP 72 (BT 36)

details.description: Four gated channels carved into stone allow lava to flow into a 15-foot-tall room; the floor can withdraw to allow the hardened lava to fall into a chamber beneath.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:29|name:Block a Channel|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to block a channel, or @Check[thievery|dc:31|name:Release Floor Latch|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to release the floor latch and escape to the chamber below
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 hour by withdrawing the floor, cracking and dumping the hardened lava (and any creatures trapped inside) into the chamber. Creatures fall 40 feet, taking falling damage (typically @Damage[17[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage).
details.routine:
(4 actions) The trap loses 1 action per disabled channel each turn. On each action, a different channel spews lava, dealing @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage to each creature within 10 feet of the channel (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] save), and increasing the depth of the lava in the room by 1 foot (4 feet per round if all the channels are active).

A creature that starts its turn in lava takes @Damage[8d6[fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until it @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape]{Escapes} the hardening lava (DC 27). The creature might suffocate if covered in lava.

Lava from the previous round hardens fully at the start of the trap's turn, effectively raising the floor of the room. Once the room is full of lava, the trap stops taking actions, but creatures in the room remain stuck until the floor opens and the trap resets.

description.value:
Trigger A creature tries to leave the room.

Effect The exits seal instantly and the trap rolls initiative.

details.description: The skeleton suddenly appears to lurch forward, crying out in pain as a shaking voice begs for someone to take away the pain.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32] (master) to carefully remove the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Vile Remnant] from the skeleton's ribcage without triggering the haunt or @Check[religion|dc:35] (expert) to exorcise the haunt

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

description.value:
Trigger A creature attacks the skeleton or ends their turn adjacent to the remains

Effect The skeleton lurches to unlife just long enough to beg for peace, then exhales at the triggering creature, who must attempt a @Check[will|dc:33] save or be subjected to the haunt's curse. A cursed creature becomes racked with pain from what feels like hours spent under a torturer's knives and takes a –1 status penalty to Fortitude saves. In addition, each time the creature takes slashing or piercing damage, they must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:33] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} from the agony (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). The curse remains until removed by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cleanse Affliction] or similar magic.

details.description: Any attempt to open the door without first tracing the symbol of Yhidothrus (an hourglass) on the door's face with a fingertip causes a glowing green ward to seal the door shut and raise an alarm.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32] (expert) to bypass the magical triggers on the door, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank, counteract DC 29) to counteract the alarm.
details.reset: The locking alarm resets after 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to open or affect the door

Effect The religious symbol of Yhidothrus glows green on the door, sealing it shut while simultaneously shrieking like a tormented soul. This howl is audible in areas D1-D4 and is loud enough in area D2 to rouse the bodaks.

While the door remains locked, a @Check[athletics|dc:32] check can Force it Open, or a @Check[thievery|dc:32] check can Pick the Lock. The lock can also be removed with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank, counteract DC 29).

Once triggered, the alarm continues to shriek and the door remains locked until the hazard is disabled or the door is destroyed or opened. Tracing Yhidothrus's hourglass-shaped religious symbol on either side of the door with a fingertip disables the alarm and the locking ward.

stealth.details: (master) to recognize that the fulus function as magical wards; noticing the fulus that cover the manor walls is DC 0

details.description: Layers of fulus and strips of prayer-printed ribbons are plastered over the walls and entrances to the manor, demarcating a boundary around the mansion where no ghosts or spirits can pass.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:35] (master), @Check[occultism|dc:35] (master), or @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master) to compromise the magic to allow entry into the manor, @Check[athletics|dc:32] to force your way through the barrier and enter the manor (this doesn't allow other PCs to enter behind you, though!), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 28) to counteract the wards

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: The wards reset automatically, continuing to prevent entry and damaging those who attempt to bypass them until they're disabled.

description.value:
Trigger A creature without a nullifying seal script on their hand approaches within 5 feet of the manor or attempts to damage the manor

Effect An explosive blast of magic violently slams against the intruder, who must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:33] save. If the triggering creature isn't adjacent to the manor, it adjusts the result of this save up by one degree of success.

Critical Success The creature takes @Damage[1d12[force]] damage and is pushed 5 feet away from the manor.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d12+13)[force]] damage and is pushed 5 feet away from the manor.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d12+16)[force]] damage and is thrown 20 feet back and knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], taking @Damage[10[bludgeoning]] damage from the fall.

Critical Failure As failure, but 100 feet back and knocked prone, taking @Damage[50[bludgeoning]] damage from the fall.

details.description: Ten masks adorn twelve stone statues.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:29|name:Dismiss the magic|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap] (expert) or @Check[occultism|dc:29|name:Dismiss the magic|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap] or @Check[thievery|dc:31|name:Dismiss the magic|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap] (expert) to dismiss the magic on a single statue. Replacing the two missing masks (a mask made of or incorporating coins on the merchant statue and a mask made of any valuable fabric on the tailor statue) and succeeding at a @Check[religion|dc:27|name:Disarm the trap|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap] check disarms the trap.

publication.title: Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets 1 minute after it has no target creatures
details.routine: (3 actions) An unmasked statue casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantasmal Killer] (@Check[will|dc:27|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap,death,emotion,fear,illusion,mental] and @Check[fortitude|dc:27|traits:hazard,complex,magical,trap,death,emotion,fear,illusion,mental,incapacitation]) against a target in the room for each of the trap's actions, creating an image of the statue lunging forth. It doesn't affect the same creature more than once; if there are fewer targets than it has actions, the trap doesn't use its remaining actions. Unmasking an additional statue increases the hazard's actions by 1, and replacing a mask reduces its actions by 1.

description.value:
Trigger A creature removes the mask from a statue

Effect The trap casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantasmal Killer] (DC 27) against the creature, creating an image of the statue lunging forth. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: The puppets and marionettes in this room suddenly turn to face intruders and come to clattering, unsettling life as the echoing sound of mocking laughter fills the air.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:32] (master) to distract and entertain the haunted puppets and make them forget their anguish and despair, or @Check[religion|dc:35] (expert) to exorcise the haunt; four successes are required to disable it
details.reset: The haunt deactivates if no living creatures are in area C2, then resets 1 minute later.
details.routine: (1 action) The room's puppets and marionettes clatter to life and begin to swarm about the room, throwing rubble at living intruders, stabbing them in the ankles with sharp bits of broken metal, and slashing at them with lashes from long, thin marionette cords. At the same time, other puppets cackle, mock, and pantomime a chosen foe. The haunt uses its action to target one living creature in area C2; that creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:29|options:inflicts:slowed|traits:emotion,mental] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round by the mockery (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2} on a critical failure); this effect has the emotion and mental traits. At the same time, because the puppets constantly swarm about, they can use the Puppet Attack free action (see below) to attack targets during their turns.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature spends more than a minute in this room, or one of the geists in this room uses Terrifying Laugh

Effect The puppets clatter to life, and the haunt fills the room with derisive laughter. All living creatures in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:29|options:area-effect,inflicts:slowed] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round by the overwhelming self-doubt the laughter instills. The mocking puppets roll initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

Trigger A living creature ends their turn while in area C2

Effect The haunt makes a makeshift weapon Strike against the triggering creature.

details.description: Whether made up of water rushing through the streets that sweeps up dangerous debris along the way or a less natural substance bursting free of its container, this relentless flood batters everything in its path.
details.disable: Three @Check[athletics|dc:35|name:Move Barricade], @Check[crafting|dc:35|name:Construct Barricade], or @Check[survival|dc:35|name:Construct Barricade] checks to move or construct barricades strong enough to create a shelter from the flood. While this creates a safe place to stand, creatures outside of the barricaded area may still be in danger depending on the nature and the source of the flood.
details.routine:
(1 action) The flood advances forward 60 feet, crashing into all creatures within its area. Each creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save as the floodwaters pummel them and pull them downstream. The amount and type of damage dealt are based on the nature of the flood, and certain types of floods impose additional effects. The turbulent waters mean creatures within the area of the flood must attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Swim|traits:action:swim] check to Swim in order to move, and those who do not succeed at a check to Swim each round may drown.

This caustic flood dissolves flesh as it moves, dealing @Damage[1d12[bludgeoning],(1d12+8)[acid]]{1d12 bludgeoning damage and 1d12+8 acid damage}. Additionally, it deals @Damage[2d6[persistent,acid]] damage to creatures who critically fail their Fortitude saves.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is moved 10 feet along with the water.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is moved 20 feet along with the water.

description.value:
Trigger A creature or effect breaks the flood's containment.

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as the flood surges forth.

details.description: Whether made up of water rushing through the streets that sweeps up dangerous debris along the way or a less natural substance bursting free of its container, this relentless flood batters everything in its path.
details.disable: Three @Check[athletics|dc:35|name:Move Barricade], @Check[crafting|dc:35|name:Construct Barricade], or @Check[survival|dc:35|name:Construct Barricade] checks to move or construct barricades strong enough to create a shelter from the flood. While this creates a safe place to stand, creatures outside of the barricaded area may still be in danger depending on the nature and the source of the flood.
details.routine:
(1 action) The flood advances forward 60 feet, crashing into all creatures within its area. Each creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save as the floodwaters pummel them and pull them downstream. The amount and type of damage dealt are based on the nature of the flood, and certain types of floods impose additional effects. The turbulent waters mean creatures within the area of the flood must attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Swim|traits:action:swim] check to Swim in order to move, and those who do not succeed at a check to Swim each round may drown.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is moved 10 feet along with the water.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is moved 20 feet along with the water.

  • Acidic Runoff This caustic flood dissolves flesh as it moves, dealing @Damage[1d12[bludgeoning],(1d12+8)[acid]]{1d12 bludgeoning damage and 1d12+8 acid damage}. Additionally, it deals @Damage[2d6[persistent,acid]] damage to creatures who critically fail their Fortitude saves.
  • Battering Waves This flood of rushing water deals @Damage[(2d12+10)[bludgeoning]] damage.
  • Repulsive Refuse This flood has picked up tainted or disease-ridden objects like sewer runoff or rotting food. It deals @Damage[(2d12+8)[bludgeoning]] damage. Each creature exposed to the flood must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} on a failure or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure. Additionally, creatures who come into contact with the flood waters are exposed to filth fever (@Check[fortitude|dc:20]).
  • Sharp Debris The waters have picked up various objects, some of which are particularly sharp. The flood deals @Damage[1d12[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[(1d12+12)[piercing]] damage.
  • Sticky Goo The substance is particularly sticky. It deals @Damage[(2d12+6)[bludgeoning]] damage. Additionally, each creature in the flood must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:29] save at the beginning of their turn each round. On a failed saving throw, they take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all their Speeds for 1 round. On a critical failure, they are instead @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature or effect breaks the flood's containment.

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as the flood surges forth.

The sickened and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] conditions from filth fever don't improve on their own until the disease is cured.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:20]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect ([[/gmr 1d4 #hours]]{1d4 hours})

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 sickened 1 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} as long as it remains sickened (1 day)

Stage 4 unconscious (1 day)

Stage 5 dead

details.description: Three shadow tendrils emerge from the ground and seize trespassers.
details.disable:
@Check[arcana|dc:32] or @Check[occultism|dc:32] (expert) to banish one of the three the shadow tendrils or @Check[thievery|dc:34] (master) twice to confound the bowl's magical sensors

Bypass A creature who places an offering worth at least 10 gp into the bowl doesn't trigger the trap and isn't targeted by the trap.
details.reset: The trap resets after 10 minutes.
details.routine: (2 actions per shadow tendril) On its turn, each shadow tendril uses 1 action to make a lash Strike against a creature in the area, then uses 1 action to Constrict a creature it has @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed]. The shadow tendrils don't apply the trap's multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
