Trigger A living creature passes by the statue without placing an offering worth at least 10 gp in the bowl

Effect Shadow tendrils rise from the ground and make a lash Strike against the triggering creature. The trap then rolls initiative.

@Damage[2d12[bludgeoning]], @Check[fortitude|dc:33|basic]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Constrict]
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ImprovedGrab]

details.description: Pressure-sensitive panels in the floor connects to a metal plate holding a number of sewer pipes shut in the ceiling.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32] (expert) on the floor panels before the plate falls away

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is placed on the floor tiles

Effect The metal plate above gives way, unleashing a torrent of sewage and flesh-eating worms on everyone within 10 feet of the pressure plate. Anyone hit by the filth must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} and takes @Damage[2d12[piercing]] damage.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} and takes @Damage[4d12[piercing]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 4} and takes @Damage[4d12[piercing]] damage plus @Damage[2d12[persistent,piercing]] damage.

details.description: A cyclone of living shadows and planar magic surges around the area, attracted to living creatures.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:32] or @Check[occultism|dc:32] (expert) to weaken the tornado; three successes in any combination are required to disable the trap.
details.reset: The cyclone disperses after 1 minute and resets after 1 day.
details.routine: (3 actions) The cyclone uses 3 actions to move, traveling up to 30 feet with each action and dealing @Damage[(2d12+13)[slashing]] damage to each creature in its path (@Check[reflex|dc:33|basic] save). A creature needs to attempt only one save during the cyclone's movement, even if the cyclone moves over its space more than once. On a critical failure, a creature is swept up into the cyclone, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] ([[/act escape dc=33]]). A creature grabbed by the cyclone moves along with the cyclone and takes @Damage[(1d12+7)[slashing]]{1d12+7 additional slashing} damage at the beginning of its turn, and it must attempt the basic Reflex save against the cyclone on the cyclone's turn, no matter where the cyclone moves. A creature that successfully Escapes from the cyclone falls from a height of 1d20 × 5 feet.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more living creatures enter the area

Effect A spiraling column of shadows and crackling planar magic gathers 20 feet away, becoming a whirling cyclone of darkness 15 feet wide and 100 feet tall. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: The Granite Ox shakes off centuries of accumulated earth, flinging the PCs toward its hindquarters.

description.value:
Trigger The Granite Ox awakens from slumber

Effect Creatures in area F2 are thrown hundreds of feet from the peak of the Ox's horn onto its back. Each creature takes falling damage as if they fell 60 feet (@Damage[30[bludgeoning]] damage), with a @Check[reflex|dc:31] save. Creatures can arrest their fall with spells like @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Gentle Landing], as normal. In addition, the result determines how far the PC must travel in order to escape the Ox's tail in the Swatted Like Flies hazard.

Critical Success The PC must travel 20 feet.

Success The PC must travel 40 feet.

Failure The PC must travel 60 feet.

Critical Failure As failure, and the PC is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.

details.description: Motion-sensitive fungus tendrils cause spears to shoot out of the floor and ceiling, almost like teeth.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (expert) on the spear panels from any adjacent space, or @Check[nature|dc:32] (master) to slowly scrape away the motion-sensitive tendrils without triggering the trap
details.reset: The trap resets in 1 round after a creature leaves its space.

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature enters the square marked with an S

Effect Dozens of iron spears shoot from the floor and ceiling, impaling anyone in the space. The trap deals @Damage[(8d8+16)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save). Those that fail this save are also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until the start of their next turn when the spears retract.

details.description: The elemental-infused fountain spouts boiling water in a singular direction, carrying all non-aquatic creatures caught in it across the hallway and toward the perilous drop-off in the opposite tower!
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:25] (trained), @Check[nature|dc:25] (trained), or @Check[survival|dc:25] (trained) to divert the flow into weaker streams, or @Check[athletics|dc:27], @Check[crafting|dc:27] (trained), or @Check[thievery|dc:27] (trained) to break up the ground to weaken the flow; two successes (or one critical success) are needed to disable the hazard.
details.routine: (1 action) The hazard attempts an Athletics check with a +28 modifier to [[/act shove]] all creatures in the water-filled areas in areas E5 and E6 toward the drop-off in area E7; the boiling spring gains a +4 circumstance bonus to its Fortitude DC against this effect. Any creature ending its turn in the flow also takes @Damage[(2d12+12)[fire]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:29|basic] save.

description.value:
Trigger The boiling spring rolls initiative

Effect The steaming flow rolls initiative.

details.description: The pooling seawater burbles and hisses.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:32] or @Check[nature|dc:32] (master) to dissipate the fire-aligned energies in the water, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:28] or @Check[religion|dc:28] to beseech Sangeh and convince her to momentarily tamp down her mythic power; two successes are required to disable the hazard
details.routine: (2 actions) The hazard uses each of its actions to create a Blistering Plume centered on a space chosen by the GM. Each successful check to disable the hazard removes one action from this routine.

description.value:
Trigger A creature within the hazard's area rolls initiative

Effect A enormous column of superheated steam rises from the water, dealing @Damage[(4d12+26)[fire]] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save). On a critical failure, the creature also takes @Damage[1d12[persistent,fire]] damage as the water continues to burn their skin. The hazard then rolls initiative.

A 30-foot-tall geyser erupts from the boiling water. Creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] of the geyser take @Damage[(2d12+13)[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save).

stealth.details: (expert); @Check[perception|dc:29|name:Notice the Line of Cord|traits:complex,mechanical,trap,hazard] to notice the line of cord running from the lathe's trigger toward the door to area A2.

details.description: The powered lathe's springs have been critically overwound so that when jostled or triggered, the lathe unwinds violently, hurling blades, gears, and sharp shards of metal around the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:31|name:Release the Spring Tension|traits:complex,mechanical,trap,hazard] (expert) on the lathe releases the tension on its springs without unleashing its blades and gears.
details.routine: (4 actions) The lathe attempts four sharpened fragment attacks against creatures in the room, selecting a target randomly from all available targets in area A1. The trap does not take multiple attack penalties for any of its attacks. The trap loses 1 action each turn as its springs wind down, and becomes disabled when it has 0 actions.

description.value:
Trigger The lathe is jostled or damaged, or its trigger is remotely pulled.

Effect The trap makes a sharpened fragment Strike against a random target in area A1, then rolls for initiative.
The sharpened fragments deal @Damage[1d8[bleed]] on a critical hit.

details.description: The statue of the willow tree animates, its branches lashing out to try to grab anyone in area B2.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:25|name:Force Open a Branch|traits:complex,magical,trap,hazard,action:force-open] or @Check[thievery|dc:25|name:Lever Open a Branch|traits:complex,magical,trap,hazard] (trained) to force or lever open a single branch, disabling that branch and freeing any creature trapped within. @Check[thievery|dc:29|name:Disrupt the Tree's Magical Animation|traits:complex,magical,trap,hazard] (expert) to disrupt the tree's magical animation, shut it down, and free all trapped creatures. Placing a dreamstone in the tree's trunk takes 2 Interact actions and causes the trap to shut down, freeing all trapped creatures. Placing the cursed dreamstone from area B4 in the trunk instead increases the tree's actions per turn to 4 and gives it a +2 item bonus to all saving throws and attack rolls.
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if it has no creatures @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] and no creatures in the room to attack. If an uncursed dreamstone is placed in its trunk, the statue doesn't reactivate.
details.routine: (3 actions) The statue uses each action to attempt a branch Strike against a random creature in the room that it hasn't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed]. If there are no creatures for it to attack and it has at least one creature grabbed, it instead Constricts. The trap can have up to six creatures grabbed.

description.value:
@Damage[(1d10+12)[bludgeoning]] damage.

@Check[fortitude|dc:27|basic]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Constrict]

A creature that begins its turn grabbed by the trap experiences vivid, warped visions of true events and must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:31|traits:damaging-effect] save or take @Damage[4d8[mental]] damage. On a critical failure, the creature also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1}. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

The tree of dreadful dreams can attempt up to six Attacks of Opportunity each round.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.AttackOfOpportunity]
Any creature struck by the tree's Branch strike are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed]. Additionally the tree's Branch strikes do not suffer from multiple attack penalties.

details.description: A raging storm covers the mountain lake as spring water levitates from the lake to fall back down in a driving spiral of steaming rain. In the depths of the storm, barely visible, is a tiny koi egg surrounded by three bands of water.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:30], @Check[religion|dc:30], @Check[nature|dc:30], or @Check[occultism|dc:30] to purify the magic corrupting the ascension ritual, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:33] or @Check[performance|dc:33] to soothe the spirit within the koi egg and give it the strength to fight off the corruption itself; four total successes are required to purify the ritual, each of the first three successes purifying one ring of water and the final one halting the ritual itself. If the cursed comb has not been removed from the ritual site, then succeeding on this final check merely halts the ritual; if the cursed comb has been removed from the ritual site, then succeeding on this final check completes the ascension ritual.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-11: The Godsrain and the Dragon

details.reset: The turbulent koi egg can attempt to hatch again, restarting the ascension ritual, after 1000 years of Xalreonshin's care.
details.routine: (1 action) Torrential water descends from the sky, taking the shape of a dragon that smashes into one creature within 60 feet of the egg. The dragon always targets the creature that is closest to the egg, dealing @Damage[(2d12+2)[bludgeoning],2d12[fire]]{2d12+2 bludgeoning damage and 2d12 fire} damage in a spray of boiling water (@Check[fortitude|dc:28] save). Creatures that fail the save are pushed 10 feet away. Creatures that have the earth trait, are carrying an item with the earth trait, or are otherwise strongly tied to elemental earth (such as an earth elemental sorcerer or kineticist) get the result one degree of success better than the outcome of their save, as the power of elemental earth overcomes water.

items.name: Immune to All Damage (Until Final Ring is Purified)

description.value:
Trigger Xalreonshin rolls initiative, three rounds pass after the PCs begin speaking with Xalreonshin, or a creature other than Xalreonshin comes within 40 feet of the egg

Effect The turbulent koi egg churns the lake surface, ejecting water into the sky. A 1-mile-radius area centered on the egg becomes filled with heavy rain and strong wind imposing a -2 circumstance penalty to Perception checks and requiring flying creatures to [[/act maneuver-in-flight dc=24]].

details.description: Yagrekash appears to berate weak soldiers for not doing their part.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:28] (expert) or @Check[performance|dc:28] (expert) to feign joining the fray, or @Check[religion|dc:30] (master) to ward Yagrekash away; three total successes in any combination are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area of Fort Whiteskull but resets immediately. Tyrant's orders resets 1 day after being disabled if the ancestor storm isn't put to rest.

description.value:
Trigger A PC critically fails a skill check to navigate the ancestor storm

Effect Yagrekash barks orders at the PCs to join the battle. All PCs must attempt a @Check[will|dc:27] save; orc and dromaar PCs take a –1 penalty.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} for 1 minute.

Failure The creature is compelled to join the Umbra Legion in battle, taking @Damage[(2d12+13)[mental]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the damage is doubled.

details.description: An unsettling wave of malice and sinister energy ripples invisibly out from the manor, causing those who view the building to see a leering, evil face glaring at them from the structure's architecture.
details.disable:
@Check[occultism|dc:32] or @Check[religion|dc:32] to exorcise the haunt, @Check[society|dc:35] to approach the manor in a proper way that defers to its occupant's symbolic standing in the community; displaying an official pass that legally grants permission to enter the building reduces both of these DCs by 5 (provided the PCs approach the front door and not from another direction).

@Check[occultism|dc:27] @Check[religion|dc:27] @Check[society|dc:30]

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: The unwelcoming facade resets immediately if not disabled and can be triggered again the very next round.

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature approaches within 30 feet of the house's exterior

Effect A wave of malice fills the minds of all living creatures outside of the manor within 60 feet of it with overwhelming fear; each creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:29] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Horrifying Vision for 24 hours. In addition to this temporary immunity, the creature can attempt a @Check[occultism|dc:23] or @Check[religion|dc:23] check as a free action to notice that despite there being only one face in the facade, several spiritual echoes seem to infuse the building. On a critical success, the creature realizes that only the most powerful force infusing the manor is evil and that the other elements are non-hostile spirits who are overwhelmed and trapped by that singular force.

Success The creature experiences a brief vision of the manor's facade twisting into an immense, horrific face and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}. The creature can attempt the same @Check[occultism|dc:33] or @Check[religion|dc:33] check as above, but the DC increases to 33.

Failure As failure, but the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for as long as it remains frightened. The creature can attempt the Occultism or Religion check to Recall Knowledge normally (DC 33).

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 for 1 minute. In addition, the overwhelming fear causes the creature to become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1}; this condition persists until it's cured magically or the hazard is disabled.

stealth.details: (expert) to notice subtle vapors of magical energy seething across the doors

details.description: These heavy doors, carved with an image of Dahak, echo with a hatred so powerful that it can kill anyone who comes nearby, manifesting a vision of Dahak's head emerging from the doors to strike at a foe.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:29|name:Disrupt the Divine Magic|traits:complex,magical,trap,hazard] (expert) to disrupt the divine magic, @Check[religion|dc:29|name:Placate Wrathful Energies|traits:complex,magical,trap,hazard] (expert) to placate the wrathful energies, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 26).
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any creature being in range.
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the wrath of the destroyer targets the closest target in area C6 with Face of the Fatal Divine.

description.value:
Trigger A non-worshipper of Dahak touches either door leading to C7

Effect The hazard targets the creature with Face of the Fatal Divine, then rolls initiative.

The creature beholds the face of Dahak as it emerges to bite with its burning jaws, targeting the creature with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantasmal Killer] (5th level, @Check[will|dc:29|traits:complex,hazard,magical,trap]).

details.description: The Caul is a thin layer of transparent, moist webbing clinging to the manor's exterior surfaces. The stuff feels slippery and unnaturally cold to the touch, and it clings and stretches when a finger or other object used to touch the Caul is pulled away.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:32|name:Exorcise the Caul] (master) or @Check[religion|dc:34|name:Exorcise the Caul] (expert) to exorcise the caul
details.reset: 1 week; putting Xarwin's ghost to rest permanently destroys the haunt.

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens any door or window into Xarwin Manor, or any creature or object passes through the caul from outside into the manor

Effect The caul immediately bursts like an oily bubble, vanishing in an instant of foul-smelling smoke. A wave of susurration creeps through the area, and all sapient creatures experience a sudden sensation of utter hatred toward birdlife for the span of a single heartbeat. All natural avians within a @Template[emanation|distance:300] from Xarwin Manor (but not those within the manor or underground) take @Damage[15d6[mental]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:33|basic|name:Xarwin Caul] save).

details.description: Narseigus's worm-calling enchantments have attained imperfect control over Zogototaru, compelling her to aid him. She follows his directions loosely and with little finesse.
details.disable: Disable @Check[arcana|dc:28] or @Check[occultism|dc:28] (trained) to identify a loophole in mind-controlling magic, @Check[diplomacy|dc:30] to reason with Zogototaru, or @Check[deception|dc:31] or @Check[performance|dc:31] to speak as if the PC were Taargick
details.routine: (1 action) Zogototaru acts on Narseigus's most recent command, either Rescuing, Roaring, or Thrashing.

description.value: Zogototaru lunges near Narseigus, flips him atop her head, and recoils. This moves Narseigus up to 30 feet horizontally and positions him atop Zogototaru, treating her head as a 10-by-10-foot space that's 20 feet above the magma's surface. This movement triggers reactions based on the move trait, targeting Narseigus. Zogototaru continues supporting Narseigus until ordered to perform a different action, after which she deposits him in a safe location within 30 feet as a free action. A PC can Climb (@Check[athletics|dc:25]) Zogototaru's coarse hide to reach him.

Zogototaru looms and screeches ferociously. Creatures in a @Template[cone|distance:60] take @Damage[6d6[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:29|basic] save) and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:29] save.

Critical Success No effect.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for [[/gmr 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds} and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}.

Zogototaru lashes her body, rattling the ground and splashing magma in a @Template[cone|distance:30]. Creatures in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:29] save.

Critical Success No effect.

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage and @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage and @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] damage, and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Special The first time she Thrashes, Zogototaru destroys the pillars in area F2a. Anyone there can leap free as part of their Reflex saving throw, landing on the closest surface. For the remainder of combat, the rear half of the crimson worm's body floats in the magma there, creating an improvised bridge 10 feet wide and 30 feet long. Creatures on Zogototaru take a –2 circumstance penalty to their Reflex saves against her Thrashing.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Riding Zogototaru]

details.description: A ballista armed with a massive bolt fires on a creature approaching the Palace.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:36] (master) to sabotage the ballista by reaching through the firing slit from outside (this DC is reduced by 10 if the PCs are in the room with the ballista, @Check[thievery|dc:26]), or @Check[intimidation|dc:33] to frighten off the Pitax Warden operating the ballista.
details.reset: (1 day) If the PCs haven't reached 30 Liberation Points, additional ammunition for a ballista can be secured in a day, but if the PCs have reached 30 Liberation Points, a ballista that runs out of ammo cannot be reset.
details.routine: (3 actions) The ballista takes 1 action to fire a ballista bolt and 2 actions to reload. After ten rounds, the ballista is out of ammunition and can no longer function.

description.value:
Trigger The alarm is raised while the PCs are approaching the Palace

Effect The guards ready and load the ballista. The ballista defense then rolls Initiative.

stealth.details: +21 (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:31|traits:action:seek] (expert) to notice the hidden override mechanism in the north wall

details.description: Seven nozzles hidden within holes in the 15‑foot-high ceiling release a poison gas.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33|name:Disable Hidden Mechanism|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to disable the hidden mechanism on the north wall, immediately sucking the gas from the room, or @Check[thievery|dc:29|name:Plug One Nozzle|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to adequately plug one of the seven nozzles. When all seven nozzles are plugged or destroyed, the trap is deactivated.
details.reset: After an hour, the trap deactivates; the gas disperses slowly, and the doors can be opened again. After 24 hours, the gas builds up and the trap can be triggered again.
details.routine:
(1 action) The gas intensifies. Each creature in the room must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:30].

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune to the gas for 1 hour, though if the creature has already been stupefied by the trap, that condition remains for its normal duration.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours. If the target is already stupefied, the condition value increases by 1 (to a maximum of stupefied 4) and the target takes @Damage[8d6[mental]] damage.

Critical Effect As failure, except the target takes double the mental damage.

description.value:
Trigger Both secret doors are closed and at least one creature is in the room

Effect Gas fills the chamber. Creatures within the chamber must succeed on a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A churning pool of blood bubbles and seethes, periodically venting noxious vapors into the air.
details.disable: Three @Check[nature|dc:33] or @Check[occultism|dc:33] checks to draw from the blood its magical nature, causing it to clot and become inert, a @Check[religion|dc:36] check to offer prayers to sever the sacred link to Kabriri, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 29) to render the hazard inert for 10 minutes
details.reset: The hazard settles back down 1 round after all creatures leave the room and can trigger again at the start of the next round.
details.routine: (2 actions) The bubbles furiously sputter and burst on the hazard's first action, filling the air with toxic vapor that inflicts @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save) to all creatures in area J3b. A creature that's holding its breath gains a +4 circumstance bonus to this save. On the hazard's second action, the bubbles Vent Toxins at a random target in area J3b.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the room beyond the northern stairs

Effect The blood on the floor bubbles vigorously, and as the bubbles burst, waves of toxic gas vent up around the triggering creature. The creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save unless they are holding their breath or don't need to breathe; the hazard then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} by the foul smelling gas.

Failure As success, but the creature also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} by the poison gas.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2}.

details.description: Agitated aurochs trample through the streets.
details.disable: Four @Check[athletics|dc:27], @Check[intimidation|dc:27], @Check[nature|dc:27] (expert), or @Check[survival|dc:27] (expert) checks to guide the herd, find it paths through Stockside, or keep it in line.
details.routine:
(1 action) The stampede advances, trampling everything in its path. Each creature attempting to control the stampede must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:30] save or be caught up in the stampede, taking @Damage[(2d12+15)[bludgeoning]] damage; a creature who succeeded at a skill check to disable the hazard this round has a +1 circumstance bonus to the Reflex save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and cannot attempt to disable the hazard next round, as they must spend it extricating themselves from the stampede.

description.value:
Trigger A creature frees the bulls from the stable

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as the bulls stampede.

stealth.details: (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:30|traits:action:seek] (expert) to notice the three launching mechanisms built into the wall

details.description: Three launching mechanisms built into the mudbrick walls expel darts containing acidic chemicals. All three launching mechanisms must be disabled or destroyed to deactivate the trap.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32|name:Jam the tube|traits:trap,mechanical,complex] (master) to sufficiently jam the tubes to make the trap unable to attack creatures in a single 5-foot square in the room, or @Check[crafting|dc:34|name:Alter a launching mechanism|traits:trap,mechanical,complex] (master) to alter one of the three launching mechanisms.
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets 1 minute after no creatures are in area C7
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap uses each action for a caustic dart Strike. The trap loses 1 action for each launching mechanism that is broken or destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A creature reaches the midpoint of the hall

Effect The trap makes an acid dart against three different random creatures in the hall. The trap then rolls initiative.

hp.details: to ruin the nozzle (making it impossible to disable, in which case the only way to disarm the bomb is to destroy both its tanks); Tank Hardness 15; Tank HP 80 (BT 40) to destroy one of the tanks (both tanks must be destroyed to disarm the bomb

stealth.details: The bomb uses Oggvurm's initiative roll as its Stealth roll

details.description: A clockwork bomb releases gouts of poisonous smoke. The smoke issues forth from a single nozzle attached to two tanks of pressurized poison
details.disable: Three [[/act disable-device dc=31]]{DC 31 Thievery} (expert) checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Disable a Device] to disable the latches that lock the nozzle in place, then one [[/act disable-device dc=35]]{DC 35 Thievery} (master) check to Disable a Device to turn off the nozzle; DCs decrease by 2 if Oggvurm is immobilized, paralyzed, unconscious, or dead
details.reset: The trap issues smoke for 3 minutes before its tanks run dry. The tanks must be replaced before the bomb can be reactivated.
details.routine: (1 action) On its turn, the bomb spews forth a smoky gout of airborne @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Blackfinger Blight] poison. The cloud fills a 20-foot radius, or a 10-foot radius if only one tank remains intact. If the bomb is already in the center of a blackfinger blight cloud, the radius of that cloud increases by 20 feet (or 10 feet, if only one tank remains intact) instead.

description.value:
Trigger The bomb is Activated, which requires 3 actions

Effect The bomb releases a smoky cloud of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Blackfinger Blight] in a 20-foot radius. Creatures within the smoke are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed] from other creatures.

Any creature that starts its turn in the smoke must roll a @Check[fortitude|dc:32|name:Blackfinger Blight|traits:contact,virulent] saving throw to avoid being afflicted by blackfinger blight poison.

The cloud remains for 1 minute or until dispersed by strong winds.

details.description: The pain of over 300 souls crying out for recognition and revenge pierces through the room, as a spiritual whirlwind fills the space.
details.disable: @Check[medicine|dc:33] or @Check[society|dc:33] to determine 287's first or surname through the journal text or @Check[occultism|dc:35] or @Check[religion|dc:35] to connect with the spiritual energy bound to the journal to find her name. Two successes are needed, one for each part of 287's name.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-11: The Hidden Current

details.reset: The haunt vanishes permanently if 287's name is revealed.
details.routine: (1 action) The haunt makes a howling wail Strike against all living creatures in area A13 as 1 action.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs start investigating the journal for 287's name

Effect The victims all beg for attention and recognition, lashing out at the PCs when they remain focused. The haunt makes a howling wail Strike against all living creatures and then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: A violet crystal containing the body of a dead creature, grown from its dying agony, pulses as it exudes motes of flickering energy.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33] (master) or @Check[survival|dc:35] (master) to know where to Strike a specific crystal perfectly on a facet to cause it to shatter harmlessly
details.reset: A crystallized agony resets automatically and can be triggered again the next round.

description.value:
Trigger The crystallized agony is struck hard enough to take damage

Effect A shimmering mote of violet light flashes from the point of origin from the triggering action, unerringly striking the triggering creature. This bolt of agony deals @Damage[10d10[mental]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic]).

details.description: Doldrum heaps that have only started to grow have no intelligence, but are incredibly tenacious and can reflexively grab onto nearby prey. They obtain nutrients by drowning prey in the water they grow in.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:35] (expert) to rip the reflexive tendrils off the bud or @Check[thievery|dc:35] (expert) to trigger the tendrils beforehand and keep them occupied.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-17: The Devil in the Details

details.reset: 1 hour if the bud pile has something in its grasps. 1 round if the bud pile has nothing in its grasps.

description.value:
Trigger a creature moves into a square adjacent to the bud pile or into the square the bud pile occupies

Effect The doldrum bud pile whips out numerous tendrils, attempting to grab the creature. The target must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:31] save.

Critical Success The target dodges the tendril and is unaffected.

Success A couple of tendrils latches on to the target. The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed]. The target can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] by attempting an Athletics or Acrobatics skill check of DC 31. The first time they fail to Escape each round, however, the bud pile constricts, dealing @Damage[(2d12+20)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Failure Several tendrils grab the target. The target becomes grabbed and must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:33] save or fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. If the target cannot breathe in water, they begin to suffocate. The DC to Escape increases to 36.

Critical Failure As failure, except the DC to Escape increases to 38.

details.description: An enormous hammer at an edifice's entrance swings down in an attempt to damage a creature entering an area, push it back, and prevent it from going any further.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Prevent Hammer from Swinging|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) once on the hammer itself and once on its joint to prevent the hammer from swinging
details.reset: The trap resets over the course of the round, and is ready to swing again 1 round later.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to enter through the entrance.

Effect The hammer swings down, making a Strike against the triggering creature.

On a hit, the target is knocked back 10 feet and must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:30] or be unable to enter the edifice through any entrance for 24 hours (on a critical hit, the target automatically fails the Will save); no multiple attack penalty

details.description: Crates of knives and daggers shine with ill intent.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:26] (master) to exorcise the spirits, @Check[crafting|dc:33] (expert) to damage the knives, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 29) to counteract the crate
details.reset: The spirits in the hazard reactivate after 10 minutes.
details.routine: (3 actions) The knife collection identifies targets. It first moves to have as many living creatures in its space as possible within a single Fly action, then attempts dagger Strikes against living creatures in its space.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature touches one of the crates

Effect A cloud of knives rises from the boxes in a 10-foot radius around the touched crate and stabs all creatures in its area, dealing @Damage[10d4[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:34|basic] save). The hazard then rolls initiative.
Fly 15 feet

details.description: Mist coils from the ground, perhaps through a sewer grate or from cracks along a basement foundation, quickly forming into the ghostly image of Queen Ileosa.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:33|name:Exorcise the Manifestation] (expert) to exorcise the manifestation, or @Check[intimidation|dc:36|name:Stand your ground] (trained) to stand one's ground without showing fear.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature moves within 10 feet of where the manifestation will appear

Effect Ileosa laughs, becoming more substantial as bloody, spectral bodies appear to pile under her feet. Each living creature within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} of the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes @Damage[5d10[mental]] damage as false memories of being tormented and persecuted under Ileosa's rule tear through their mind.

Failure As above, but the creature takes @Damage[10d10[mental]] damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} until the next sunrise from the overwhelming nature of the false memories.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} instead. In addition, the creature continues to see dead, bloody bodies crawling from the ground during times of stress, causing them to treat all ground as difficult terrain during combat. This hallucination persists as long as the character remains stupefied from the hazard.

details.description: A robe-clad spirit rises out of the floor, pointing an accusing finger.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:36|name:Convince the Spirit], @Check[nature|dc:36|name:Convince the Spirit], @Check[occultism|dc:36|name:Convince the Spirit], or @Check[religion|dc:36|name:Convince the Spirit] (master) to convince the spirit that the target's magical knowledge is too great to be trifled with
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature that is currently affected by a beneficial spell approaches within 30 feet of the abjurer.

Effect Envying the fame and magical prowess of others, the jealous abjurer attempts to engineer a catastrophic failure in the highest-level beneficial spell currently affecting its target. It attempts a counteract check with a +26 modifier. If the counteract check succeeds, the spell is dispelled, and the creature it had been affecting takes @Damage[(4d12+30)[force]] damage as the spell violently implodes (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save).

details.description: A mass of petrified lichen takes the shape of a beast and lurches forward.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:33] or @Check[thievery|dc:33] (master) to scrape away lichen without being exposed, or @Check[medicine|dc:36] or @Check[nature|dc:36] (expert) to concoct a natural remedy and weaken the hazard; four total successes are required to disable it.
details.reset: The trap resets over the course of an hour.
details.routine:
(3 actions) The lichen monstrosity uses 3 actions to move, traveling up to 30 feet with each action and dealing @Damage[(2d10+10)[bludgeoning]] damage. Each creature in its path must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:31] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is exposed to leechlichen.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is exposed to leechlichen. The results of its saving throw against the leechlichen are treated as one step worse. Each successful check to disable this hazard reduces its movement by 30 feet, and the fourth success disables it completely.

description.value:
Trigger Three or more creatures not infected with leechlichen enter the area of the hazard;

Effect The mass of lichen lurches forward, taking up one 5-foot square and forming a beast-like shape. The hazard rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (expert) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Detect Magic] to note the presence of necromantic energies suffusing the crystals.

details.description: Sharp, bloodthirsty crystals make walking through this chamber treacherous.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:30|name:Lifeleech Crystal Patch Disable|traits:environmental,magical] (expert) to utter prayers that quell the crystals' necromantic compulsion to drink blood

publication.title: Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City

description.value:
Trigger A creature with blood moves through a patch of lifeleech crystals.

Effect The crystals twist and slash, animating and cutting into the creature as it walks, dealing @Damage[5d8[slashing]] damage. The creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:28|traits:damaging-effect,environmental,magical] saving throw.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2}.

Critical Failure As failure, plus the creature's blood causes fresh growth. A new 10-foot-square patch of lifeleech crystals grows in an adjacent space to the current patch of crystals.

stealth.details: (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:33] (master) to notice sparks dancing around normally non-conductive surfaces

details.description: Electricity suffuses an area where Agyra has used her lightning breath multiple times.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:35|name:Fashion Makeshift Lightning Rod] (master) to fashion a makeshift lightning rod, @Check[nature|dc:33|name:Dissipate Electrical Charge] (expert) to dissipate the electrical charge harmlessly into the air.
details.reset: The hazard deactivates and immediately resets if has no targets for 1 round; it also resets if Agyra uses her lightning breath multiple times in a single combat in the area.
details.routine: (2 actions) Bolts of lightning flash out. The hazard makes two lightning bolt Strikes at two different creatures within 120 feet of the hazard's center. If only one creature is in range, the hazard makes only one Strike.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the hazard's center

Effect Electricity wells up from the ground and nearby material. Each creature in the hazard's area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:33] save, and the hazard then rolls initiative. A character wearing metal armor or wielding a metal shield or weapon of at least 1 Bulk treats the result of their saving throw as one step worse.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} for 2 rounds.

Critical Failure The creature is clumsy 2 for 4 rounds and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} for 1 round.

hp.details: to break the spring under the step and make that step safe to stand on

details.description: This staircase has springs in each step, which send poisoned needles shooting up through tiny holes in the wood when stepped on. Each step, 6 in total, takes up one 5-foot square.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33|name:Disable Stair Spring|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) on each stair to disable its spring, or deactivate the switch in area C8 to deactivate the whole trap. Cutting the wire on the topmost stair ([[/act disable-device dc=35]]) that connects the staircase to the control switch prevents the control switch from turning the trap on or off.
details.reset: If deactivated via the switch, the trap can be reactivated with the switch in area C8.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap makes a separate poisoned needle Strike against each creature currently on an active step as 1 action. Because it is constantly jabbing needles up through any weight-bearing steps, the trap can also use its Bloody Feet free action to jab at a creature on one of the steps during that creature's turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature ends its movement on one of the stairs

Effect The trap makes a poisoned needle Strike against the triggering creature, then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

Trigger A creature moves onto an active step

Effect The trap makes a poisoned needle Strike against the triggering creature.

stealth.details: (trained) (decreases by 2 each round the hazard is active) to smell the smoke and hear the crackling flames

details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:27] to beat out the flames, @Check[crafting|dc:27] to rig up a firefighting device, or @Check[nature|dc:27] to cut off avenues of the fire's spread. On a success, one square of the fire is extinguished (two squares on a critical success). Dousing a square in water, such as with create water, automatically extinguishes that square. When all squares are extinguished, the hazard ends.
details.routine: (1+ actions) Each square of flames spreads to an adjacent square, taking one action per square. Creatures adjacent to the fire take @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage each round from the heat (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save); a creature who fails their save is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} by the smoke (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). If there are more than 10 active squares of flames, creatures adjacent to the fire instead take @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage each round (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save), and a creature who fails their save is sickened 2 (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 3} on a critical failure). A creature standing in the flames takes @Damage[12d6[fire]] damage each round (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save); a creature who fails their save also takes @Damage[4d6[persistent,fire]] damage.

description.value:
Trigger The arsonists set the fire

Effect The hay around the initial flames catches, setting two 5-foot squares in the affected room on fire. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: The sparrow on the scroll animates, speaking to the PCs in Noguchi's voice (one of the nosois from Event 11: Nosois' Brunch): "They're the ones! They're the blasphemers who can't even properly serve tea! Send them along to the Lady!"
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:36] (to quickly destroy the scroll without triggering the hazard), @Check[religion|dc:33] (to apologize to Pharasma and the nosois for the previous insult), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th level; counteract DC 29).

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

description.value:
Trigger One of the PCs enters the room and is noticed by the hazard

Effect The trap summons four @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Vanth]{Vanths}, who then roll initiative and remain for up to 1 minute before the spell ends and the vanths disappear. The summoned vanths can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: Clouds of buzzing flies and a carpet of vermin fill a room strewn with decaying pieces of cast-off body parts and gobbets of rancid meat.
details.disable: Three @Check[arcana|dc:33] or @Check[nature|dc:33] checks to command the vermin to be silent or to cleanse away the filth and decay to make the hazard harmless, a @Check[religion|dc:36] check to offer prayers to sever the sacred link to Kabriri, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 29) to render the hazard inert for 10 minutes
details.reset: The hazard settles back down 1 round after all creatures leave the room and can trigger again at the start of the next round.
details.routine: (2 actions) The clouds of vermin in the room fill the air, rain down from the walls and ceiling, and swarm up from the floor below. All creatures in the room are subjected to Swarming Bites on the hazard's first action. On the second action, the swarming vermin grow particularly dense and eager in their attentions to one randomly determined creature in area J3c. If that creature is suffering from ghast fever and is at stage 4 or lower, they must immediately attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save. On a failure, the stage of their ghast fever advances one step.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the room beyond the northern stairs

Effect A combination of biting flies and ravenous creepy-crawlies swarm the triggering creature. The creature takes @Damage[6d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save). If the creature took any damage, it's automatically exposed to ghast fever. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:30]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect (1 day)

Stage 2 @Damage[3d8[void]] damage and regains half as many Hit Points from all healing (1 day)

Stage 3 as stage 2 (1 day)

Stage 4 @Damage[3d8[void]] damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day)

Stage 5 as stage 4 (1 day)

Stage 6 dead, and rises as a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.Ghast] the next midnight

stealth.details: (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:30|traits:action:seek] (master) to detect the spiritual nexus

details.description: The restless spirit of Currew raves against his tormentors when someone enters his room.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:30|name:Quiet the spirit|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] (expert) to quiet the spirit for 1 round, or @Check[religion|dc:32|name:Exorcise spirit|traits:hazard,haunt,complex] (master) to exorcise it
details.reset: The haunt grows quiet and resets 1 minute after no creatures are in area B3.
details.routine: (2 actions) The haunts casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantasmal Killer] (@Check[will|dc:30|name:Phantasmal Killer|traits:hazard,haunt,complex,death,emotion,fear,mental] save and @Check[fortitude|dc:30|name:Phantasmal Killer|traits:hazard,haunt,complex,death,emotion,fear,mental,incapacitation] save for critical failure) on a random creature who can see Currew's bed (it can even target creatures outside of area B3, so long as the target can see the bed). Everyone affected by the phantasmal killer sees the same image: a sickly old man lies in the bed, screaming and ranting about his guilt, shame, and eventual death.

description.value:
Trigger A creature remains in area B3 for longer than 1 round

Effect Each creature in the sealed room is targeted by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Nightmare] (@Check[will|dc:30] save). The haunt counts as having met the targets. It then rolls initiative.

details.description: Swirling shadows that behave like animated pools of ink splash and slither up against the limbs of those who tread too far into this chamber.
details.disable: Three @Check[arcana|dc:33] or @Check[occultism|dc:33] checks to redirect the negative energy back in on itself and disrupt the hazard's source of power, a @Check[religion|dc:36] check to offer prayers to sever the sacred link to Kabriri, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 29) to render the hazard inert for 10 minutes
details.reset: The hazard settles back down 1 round after all creatures leave the room and can trigger again at the start of the next round.
details.routine: (1 action) The shadows writhing on the floor extrude a storm of inky tentacles of smoke and darkness that whip and writhe throughout the room, filling the air with unsettling sounds of agonizing cries and shrieks. All creatures in area J3a take @Damage[8d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the room beyond the northern stairs

Effect Shadowy tendrils wisp up from the darkness below to brush against the triggering creature's body. The creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save or their strength is sapped from their body. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature's speeds are reduced by 10 feet for 1 round.

Failure As success, but the creature also falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed]. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save.

stealth.details: (master) to notice tears beginning to run from the statue's eyes

details.description: The statue begins to weep, its tears running in rivulets down into the surrounding pool, filling all nearby with overwhelming shame and grief.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:29] (master) to offer prayers to Shelyn (a worshipper of Shelyn gains a +2 circumstance bonus to this check), or @Check[diplomacy|dc:33] (expert) to offer condolences and comforts to the haunt

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature enters the pool or touches the statue

Effect The waters of the pool and the statue become infused with divine power. The triggering creature and all other creatures within 20 feet of that creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save. If the save is attempted by a worshipper of Shelyn, the result is automatically improved one degree.

Critical Success Shelyn sees in the creature a chance to bring the shrine back to glory, and instead of filling them with despair, the haunt fills them with hope. In addition to being filled with an urge to defeat the evil that has infested the shrine, the creature has the urge to restore the shrine. Exact details as to the nature of the evil infesting the shrine aren't apparent other than to alert the character that there's certainly something dire within the shrine's walls. The creature need not act on these urges, but as long as they pursue either goal, they gain a +1 status bonus to all checks while remaining in the shrine's area. The GM determines whether or not a check counts toward this goal (at the very least, all combat checks made against foes in the rest of the adventure count).

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.campaign-effects.Item.Effect: Hope or Despair (Critical Success)]

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure Feelings of sadness and despair well up in the creature's mind. For the next hour, the creature takes a -1 penalty to all saving throws against mental effects; this penalty increases to a -2 penalty against emotion mental effects. This effect has the curse trait.

Critical Failure As failure, but the effects persist as long as the green hag Tulvak remains in control of the shrine.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.campaign-effects.Item.Effect: Hope or Despair (Failure or Critical Failure)]

Trigger the haunt is disabled;

Effect The creature that disabled the haunt, as well as all allies within 30 feet of that creature, gain the critical success result for Hope or Despair.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.campaign-effects.Item.Effect: Hope or Despair (Critical Success)]

details.description: An @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Invisible] rune on the floor summons a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Goliath Spider] when disturbed by someone who isn't wearing Norgorber's religious symbol.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:34|name:Erase the Rune|traits:uncommon,magical,trap,complex] (master) to erase the rune or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 36) to dispel the rune.
details.reset: The trap resets after 24 hours.

description.value:
Trigger A creature that isn't wearing a religious symbol of Norgorber enters the area above the rune

Effect The trap summons a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Goliath Spider]. The goliath spider rolls initiative and remains for [[/r 2d6 #rounds]]{2d6 rounds}, after which the spell ends and the spider disappears. The spider also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. Unlike most summoned creatures, the goliath spider can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions.

details.description: Four animal statues sit on pillars, one in each corner of the statuary, with decorated tiles between them in a seemingly random arrangement.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:31] (expert) or @Check[stealth|dc:32] (expert) to disarm the statue while adjacent to it

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #5-20: The Rakshasa's Court

details.reset: Each statue resets immediately after being triggered.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on one of the plain silver tiles in the statuary

Effect A wave of cold passes through the target, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:26|basic] save).

Trigger A creature steps on one of the diamond-embedded silver tiles in the statuary

Effect A jolt of electricity arcs up from the tile and strikes the target, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic] save).

Trigger A creature steps on one of the plain copper tiles in the statuary

Effect The high-pitched yowl of an attacking cat echoes in the target's head, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:29|basic] save).

details.description: A ghostly stinger appears in the air and jabs at anyone moving through the opening.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:36|name:Prevent Ghostly Stinger from Forming|traits:uncommon,magical,trap] (master) to prevent the ghostly stinger from forming or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 34).
details.reset: The trap resets after 24 hours.

description.value:
Trigger A creature who isn't wearing a religious symbol of Norgorber moves the trapdoor

Effect The trap targets the creature with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Cave Worm Venom]{Purple Worm} sting (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|traits:trap,uncommon,magical] save).

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[5d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)

details.description: A relentless storm assails the ship, battering those on board who don't secure themselves.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:29], Crafting, or Sailing Lore to securely lash one's self to the ship. Creatures tied to the ship are immune to the hazard's routine but are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized].
details.routine:
(1 action) The storm tosses the ship in a random direction (roll 1d8 to determine). All creatures on the ship that aren't lashed to it are moved 10 feet in that direction and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:30] save or Sailing Lore check as the waves pummel them.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and can avoid being moved 10 feet if it wishes.

Success The creature takes no damage.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] and takes @Damage[(1d12+15)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] and takes @Damage[(2d12+15)[bludgeoning]] damage.

description.value:
Trigger The storm builds for at least 10 minutes

Effect The ship pitches dangerously atop the waves. The hazard rolls initiative and performs its routine as a free action.

details.description: A manifestation of Tar-Baphon drains the essence of its divine target.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:33] (expert) to disrupt Tar-Baphon's magic, @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] (master) to suppress the shadow's energy; three total successes in any combination are required to disable the haunt unless the Carcass Man is destroyed, in which case it's automatically destroyed
details.routine: (1 action; death) The haunt drains Zagresh's divine energies, dealing @Damage[5d10[void]] damage.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the cavern

Effect The shadow latches onto the Carcass Man, unleashing a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] of necromantic energy. Creatures in the area except the Carcass Man must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save or take @Damage[2d12[void]] damage. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is pushed back 5 feet.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is pushed back 15 feet.

Critical Failure As failure and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} and knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].
The shadow is sustained by the power of Tar-Baphon, regaining 10 Hit Points at the beginning of every round, and is repaired by void damage.

details.description: The dreamwell plant shudders and pulses, emitting more potent odors before it bursts into swarms of ravenous ghostly spiders.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:33] (master) to use the dreamwell plant's own blooms to soothe the ghostly spiders or @Check[religion|dc:36] (trained) to exorcise the ghostly spiders; three successes are required to disable the haunt completely

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates at the end of any round in which there are no Small or larger living creatures in the room, then resets automatically.
details.routine:
(2 actions) An immense swarm of thumb-sized spiders boil out of the plant's planter box and its bulbous trunk, filling the air with the nauseating sound of tens of thousands of tiny clawed feet scuttling everywhere. The haunt uses its first action to spray webbing through the area so that every creature in the room must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:30] save to avoid taking a –5-foot status penalty to their Speed. These penalties stack with multiple failed Reflex saves, and a character whose penalties reduce their Speed to 0 becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] ([[/act escape dc=30]]).

On its second action, the phantasmal spiders begin to bite and gnaw at living flesh with fangs that are suddenly both very real and very toxic. Every creature in the room takes 2d10 piercing and @Damage[2d10[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger living creature spends more than a minute in the room, or anyone attempts to move or damage the plant

Effect The dreamwell plant's flowers bloom and fill the air in area F14 with soporific scents. All creatures in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30|options:inflicts:incapacitated,inflicts:slowed] save with the following effects; the haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The character is unaffected.

Success The character takes a –1 status penalty to their initiative roll at the start of the following round.

Failure As success, but the character is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} from sudden tiredness. The affected character can attempt a new @Check[will|dc:30] save at the end of a round to recover from this condition; otherwise, it lasts for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The character falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious]. If it's still unconscious (and alive) after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically.

details.description: Two portcullises drop down to block progress through this area.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33] (master) to disable the trigger that causes the portcullises to drop
details.reset: The trap can be manually reset with a 10-minute Manipulate activity performed in the hallways to the east or west of this area, as long as neither portcullis is broken or destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A character enters a square between the two portcullises

Effect Both portcullises drop from the ceiling; attempting to lift one requires a @Check[athletics|dc:30] check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open]. When the portcullises drop, creatures in adjacent squares must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:30|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is unharmed and can take a Step as a reaction to move to an adjacent square.

Success As critical success, but the creature takes a glancing blow from the falling portcullis for @Damage[(2d12+15)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Failure The creature is struck by the falling portcullis for @Damage[(4d12+30)[bludgeoning]] damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in their square.

Critical Failure The creature is struck by the falling portcullis for @Damage[(6d12+45)[bludgeoning]] damage, is knocked prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] by the portcullis as it pins them to the ground until they can [[/act escape dc=30]] or [[/act force-open dc=30]] the portcullis.

details.description: Snow and ice gathered at the peaks of the mountain come crashing down in an explosive wave.
details.disable: Three @Check[crafting|dc:34] or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:34] (expert) to create cover for a 10-foot square area and shelter from the avalanche; a flying creature can attempt a @Check[acrobatics|dc:29] check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Maneuver in Flight] to avoid the avalanche

description.value:
Trigger The zombie mammoth horde moves beneath the peaks

Effect An avalanche of snow and ice cascades down the mountainside at astonishing speed. All creatures on the ground within a 200-foot radius must attempt @Check[reflex|dc:30] saves.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[4d6[cold]] damage.

Failure The creature is swept away, moving 200 feet down the mountainside, and takes @Damage[8d6[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[8d6[cold]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is also buried, taking an additional @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]] damage and then @Damage[1d6[cold]] damage per minute of burial. Locating a buried creature requires a @Check[perception|dc:28] or @Check[survival|dc:28] check; once located, a buried creature can be extricated with a @Check[athletics|dc:28] check in one round or dug out with a @Check[athletics|dc:23] check with 4 minutes of work.

details.description: At the heart of the shrine hangs a massive bronze temple bell.
details.disable: Ringing the bell as an Interact action eight times in succession with the ceremonial ringer (carried by Mago Kai) over the course of three consecutive rounds automatically deactivates the hazard, @Check[religion|dc:36] to appeal to Pharasma to extinguish the flame, @Check[thievery|dc:36] to extinguish the flame within the bell, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 29) or a shimmerthief's Drain Light ability to disable the wards

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: Reactivating the warding bell requires a complicated rite taking several days.
details.routine:
(1 action; divine, emotion, enchantment, fear, mental) The warding bell emits a harrowing ring that can be heard only by undead or transmigrated PCs in area C11; affected characters must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30|traits:divine,emotion,enchantment,fear,mental] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to the warding bell's routine for 24 hours.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} and takes @Damage[6d6[mental]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}, takes @Damage[12d6[mental]] damage, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] as long as it remains frightened.

description.value: The warding bell's light increases from a faint flickering to luminous enough that the whole room is brightly lit. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: The weakened floor gives way with a tremendous crash, dropping everything in the room into a muddy morass of toxic mold.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:33] (master) three times to knock holes in the floor without triggering the spore cloud while simultaneously lightening the strain on the floor so it can be walked upon, @Check[stealth|dc:36] (expert) to move carefully through the room for the remainder of your turn without triggering the hazard, or @Check[nature|dc:36] (expert) twice once the trap is triggered to knock enough holes into the nearby walls to cause the spores to disperse

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

details.reset: One minute after nothing moves in the room, the spores settle down. The floor remains obvious and won't collapse further, but any creature that moves through the mold causes the spores to plume up again, re-triggering the trap.
details.routine: (1 action) Every round on its turn, the spores that now fill area A4 inflict @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage to all creatures in the room (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save) as the cloud burns at exposed flesh; a creature that holds its breath gains a +4 circumstance bonus to this saving throw.

description.value:
Trigger A round begins with a Small or larger creature standing on the floor in area A4

Effect The floor of the entire room collapses with a thunderous crash, dropping all creatures in the room 5 feet into a soupy morass of mud, mold, and toxic fungus while a cloud of gray and brown spores spews up into the air above. The room becomes greater difficult terrain. All creatures who were standing on the floor in the room fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in the mess below unless they succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:34] save, in which case they land on their feet. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: The collection of bottles at the bottom of one locker have been jostled. Their contents leak into a puddle, and their vapors burn through the open air.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:30] (master) to neutralize the alchemical substances, or @Check[thievery|dc:35] (expert) to carefully clean the spill
details.reset: If the reagents aren't cleaned up before closing the locker, the accumulating gases reset the trap after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature fully opens the locker containing the alchemical clutter

Effect A cloud of vapor deals @Damage[8d10[poison]] damage to all creatures within 10 feet (@Check[reflex|dc:36|basic] save). On a failure, a target takes an additional @Damage[2d10[persistent,poison]] damage.

details.description: The azure lilies expel a burst of toxic pollen.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:33] or @Check[survival|dc:33] (expert) to harvest the plant's pollen without disturbing the blossoms.
details.reset: 1 month

description.value:
Trigger The lily patch is disturbed

Effect The pollen erupts in a @Template[burst|distance:30]{30-foot-radius burst}. Any living creature in the burst must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save, though creatures with the plant trait are immune.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[6d6[mental]] damage as the pollen causes wracking headaches and jolts of pain.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[12d6[mental]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 minute. The creature can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end the confusion early.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[20d6[mental]] damage and is confused for 1 minute, with no save to end the confusion early.

details.description: Ghostly attackers knock the tables about, send dishes flying, and pull fleeing creatures into the room.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:34|name:Say an Iroran prayer|traits:haunt] (expert) to say an Iroran prayer and put the haunt temporarily to rest, @Check[occultism|dc:38|name:Ward Against Future Hauntings|traits:haunt] (master) to create a ward against future hauntings.
details.reset: The haunt deactivates after 1 minute. It re-forms at the same times every day, 7 a.m. and 7 p.m., which is when the monks had their morning and evening meals. If at least two people cook a meal in the kitchen, share a meal in the refectory, and utter a prayer to Irori before the meal, the haunt becomes permanently disabled and doesn't re-form.
details.routine: (1 action) The haunt sends a flurry of objects hurtling across the room. All creatures in the room are battered by the ghostly objects, taking @Damage[(3d10+14)[force]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:36|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger One round passes since a creature entered the refectory.

Effect A Broken Rebus monk grabs the creature and yanks them toward the room's center. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36|traits:haunt] save. The haunt can use this reaction any number of times per round, but only once per creature that tries to leave the room. The haunt then rolls initiative, if it hasn't already.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is pulled 10 feet into the room.

Failure The creature is pulled 15 feet into the room.

Critical Failure The creature is pulled 15 feet into the room, knocked prone, and takes @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]] damage.

stealth.details: (expert) to notice a slight tugging feeling

details.description: A supernatural compulsion to leap into the unknown exerts itself on anyone who approaches the ledge.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:34|name:Expel Magical Compulsion] or @Check[arcana|dc:36|name:Expel Magical Compulsion] to expel the magical compulsion from the current island and from any of the red ropes directly attached to it, darkening the rope in color. The Echo also automatically disables the hazard from any island he's standing on, and any of the red ropes directly attached to it, when he Reconstitutes from Thought on that island.
details.reset: The call of the void persists as long as the Echo exists, though it doesn't activate until the Echo has rolled initiative. The hazard resets each day.
details.routine: (1 action) The call targets all creatures adjacent to a ledge or on one of the red ropes and tugs at their minds. A creature who has already fallen this round is immune.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters a square adjacent to the ledge of an island or steps onto one of the red ropes

Effect The call of the void tugs at the mind of intruders, compelling them to leap off the ledge. The creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:34] save or @Check[acrobatics|dc:32|traits:action:balance] check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Balance] or else drop off the ledge. If the creature falls off, it drops 100 feet, loops through the closed space of the mindscape, and lands in the square it fell from, taking falling damage as normal (usually @Damage[50[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage). The call of the void then rolls initiative.

details.description: A ring of eight dragon pillar replicas mark the border of a dome of magical prismatic energy that entirely contains the Fortress of Sorrow.
details.disable: Each dragon pillar that is destroyed removes the corresponding color's effect from Dahak's shell; as long as even one color remains active, the pillars marking the shell's border cannot be damaged themselves, but a successful @Check[thievery|dc:32|name:Disable Color|traits:magical,trap,hazard] (master) check or a successful @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 30) against one of the eight pillars can cause a randomly determined active color in the shell defense (below) to become deactivated for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

description.value: The target suffers an effect corresponding to the eye beam's color. On a critical hit, the target's save result is one degree worse.

Trigger a creature attempts and fails to disable one of the shell colors via Thievery or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic]

Effect Dahak's shell Strikes the triggering creature with a random eye beam. Roll [[/r 1d8 #Eye Beam]] to determine the color:

  1. Red
  2. Orange
  3. Yellow
  4. Green
  5. Blue
  6. Indigo
  7. Violet
  8. Black

When a creature physically passes through the shell, the creature is affected simultaneously by all colors of energy currently active in the shell; apply these effects in the following order: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet, black. These effects are the same as that of the eye beams. Each effect requires its own save.

@Check[fortitude|dc:32|traits:hazard,trap] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 round.

Failure The target is blinded for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The target is blinded for 24 hours.

@Check[fortitude|dc:32|traits:trap,hazard] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Petrified] for 1 round.

Failure The target is petrified for 1 hour.

Critical Failure The target is petrified permanently.
@Damage[6d6[poison]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic|traits:custom,hazard,trap] save).

@Check[will|dc:32|traits:hazard,trap] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The target is controlled by the dragon pillar and remains within 60 feet of it at all times, defending the dragon pillar from all non-Cinderclaws. While a target is controlled, it is treated as a Cinderclaw ally by the dragon pillar. The dragon pillar can control up to 3 targets at a time; any targets in excess who critically fail this saving throw are instead confused for [[/gmr 1d4+1 #Confused Rounds]]{1d4+1} rounds. A controlled creature can attempt a new Will save once every 24 hours to escape control, but doing so automatically causes the dragon pillar to attack it with a new eye beam to attempt to re-establish control. The control ends if the pillar is destroyed.
@Damage[6d6[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic|traits:evocation,custom,hazard,trap] save).
@Damage[6d6[acid]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic|traits:custom,hazard,trap] save).
@Damage[6d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic|traits:hazard,trap] save).

@Check[will|dc:32|traits:hazard,trap] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 7}.

stealth.details: (master) to feel an odd sense of hunger

details.description: A skeletally thin spirit appears, weeping as it shoves earth and stones down its throat in an effort to stave off hunger.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:36|name:Convince the spirit it's not hungry] (master) to convince the spirit it's no longer hungry or @Check[occultism|dc:38|name:Feed the spirit] (trained) to ritually feed the spirit
details.reset: 1 day
