description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet

Effect Creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} are filled with painful hunger and must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save or feel their stomachs fill with rocks, dirt, and worse, dealing @Damage[(6d10+20)[piercing]] damage.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 4}, and takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds for as long as it's sickened.

details.description: Tendrils of infernal energy reach out from the Inferno Gate like tentacles, infusing the creatures that serve it, even tangentially, with vitality and protection from harm.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to pray for divine intervention in counterbalancing this infernal influence; @Check[arcana|dc:35] (expert), @Check[nature|dc:35] (expert), or @Check[occultism|dc:35] (expert) to exert a magical force of will greater than the gate's influence; @Check[athletics|dc:38] (expert) or Thievery (expert) to disrupt the pentagram surrounding the Inferno Gate; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 30) to cut off the magical energies. Magical checks to disable this hazard can be attempted from up to 30 feet away from the Inferno Gate, but physical checks require physically touching the pentagram; three successes are required to disrupt the diabolic vigor hazard.
details.routine: (1 action) On its turn, the diabolic vigor hazard empowers the merztorlegon and their bound armies within 200 feet of the Inferno Gate. It grants the siege devil regeneration 20 and reduces their weakness to holy from 20 to 10. The armies gain regeneration 10 and reduce their weakness to area damage and splash damage by 5. These effects last for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger Encounter E4 begins

Effect The Inferno Gate sends out beams of reddish energy to all devils and their bound armies within 200 feet, reducing all of their weaknesses by 10 for 1 round. The diabolic vigor hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: Eerie shapes manifest within the doorway as it's opened, conjuring a group of four @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.Animate Dream]{Animate Dreams} into being.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:30] (expert) to concentrate on the eldritch energies and diffuse them through meditation, @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master) to scratch warding runes along the door's hinges and block dreams from manifesting, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 30) to counteract the trap

description.value:
Trigger The door is opened

Effect A group of four @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.gatewalkers-bestiary.Actor.Animate Dream]{Animate Dreams} manifest in the area just beyond the door. These four animate dreams each appear as vaguely whale-shaped but human-sized masses of tendrils. They swim through the air and attack the intruders at once, fighting to the death and pursuing any who flee the temple.

details.description: The earth shakes violently side to side, opening a fissure in the ground, partially collapsing builds, and scattering debris in all directions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-19: What Walks Again

description.value: The ground shakes violently. All creatures in the area take @Damage[6d10[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save. On a critical failure, the creature falls into a 40-foot-deep fissure opened by the tremor. They can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] (DC 30 to prevent the fall, with a –2 circumstance penalty due to the moving ground). A creature that falls takes an additional @Damage[20[bludgeoning]]{20} falling damage, is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], and must be rescued from their predicament by another PC taking one action to pull them free. If any creatures are still in fissures due to previous tremors, they automatically get a Failure on this save.

details.description: Wiring keeping the furniture together unravels, causing the spring-loaded pieces of furniture and wire to shoot outward.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:34|name:Identify and Cut Wiring|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to identify and harmlessly cut the taut wiring

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 5 feet of the pile of broken furniture

Effect The pile of furniture and entangling wires explodes outward. The trap deals @Damage[(6d10+27)[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save). On a failed save, the target takes a –10-foot penalty to all its Speeds for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure).

details.description: A hidden needle delivers a magical poison to anyone trying to open the door.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32|name:Jam Needle in Housing|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to jam the needle in its housing or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank, counteract DC 30) to dispel the magical poison.

publication.title: Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the door

Effect The creature is targeted by purple worm sting (@Check[fortitude|dc:32] save).

Venom from enormous purple worms leaves a victim weakened.

Activate 2 Interact (Injury)

Saving Throw DC 32 Fortitude

n

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

n

Stage 1 @Damage[5d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

n

Stage 2 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)

Venom from enormous purple worms leaves a victim weakened.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:32|traits:alchemical,injury,poison]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[5d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)

details.description: The fetid material in this pit sloshes around in a disgusting feedback loop, filled with large pieces of solid trash that risk bludgeoning creatures inside it to death!
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:27] or @Check[thievery|dc:27] (trained) to move without causing a wave of filth, or @Check[athletics|dc:30] or @Check[crafting|dc:30] (trained) to stabilize the trash heap and prevent it from flowing; three successes are required to fully stabilize and disable the hazard.
details.routine:
(1 to 3 actions) The feedback flow hazard uses up to 3 actions to make flowing trash Strikes against living creatures that take move actions inside the refuse pit (area E7) but can only target an individual creature once per round. The ofalths, used to living in this pit, don't trigger these attacks. Additionally, any non-ofalth creature that ends its turn inside the refuse pit must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} until the end of its next turn, then is immune to this effect for 1 minute.

Failure The creature is sickened 1.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

description.value:
Trigger Any Medium or larger non-ofalth creature takes a move action inside area E7

Effect The feedback flow makes a flowing trash Strike against the triggering creature and then rolls initiative.

details.description: A whirling tornado of spectrally propelled glass and steel slices whatever it touches to ribbons.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:35|name:Adjust Blades|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to precisely adjust the blades so that they destroy each other, or @Check[religion|dc:38|name:Weaken the Haunt] (expert) to weaken the haunt; four successes are required to disable it
details.reset: The haunt draws jagged shards back into its area over the course of an hour, after which it can trigger again.
details.routine:
(3 actions) The tornado of blades uses 3 actions to move, traveling up to 30 feet with each action and dealing @Damage[(2d10+10)[slashing]] damage. Each creature in its path must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:33].

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage plus @Damage[1d10[bleed]].

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and @Damage[1d10[bleed]]. It also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Wounded]{Wounded 1} (or increases its wounded value by 1, if it is already wounded).

Each successful check to disable this hazard reduces the haunt's movement by 30 feet, and the fourth success disables it completely.

description.value:
Trigger Three or more creatures enter the area of the haunt

Effect Sharp fragments lift up from the ground and begin to spin in rapid circles taking up one 5-foot square. The haunt rolls initiative.

details.description: The stairs collapse into a downward spiral.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:35|name:Decouple Mechanisms] (master) to decouple several mechanisms barely accessible through gaps between the shifting risers, or @Check[perception|dc:38|name:Note Stairs Wear Patterns DC] to note which stairs have slightly different wear patterns to know which ones are safe to step on

publication.title: Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star

details.reset: The flensing chute resets automatically.

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature approaches within 20 feet of the top of the stairs

Reaction The stair risers shift downward, forming a steep ramp while exposing lines of razor-sharp blades.

All creatures on the stairs must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:32|name:Change to Chute DC|traits:damaging-effect] save or tumble down the chute, taking @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning],(4d12+26)[slashing]]{2d6 bludgeoning damage + 4d12+26 slashing damage}, and landing @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] in area A4.

details.description: Hinged boards prop the massive paving stones into place on the causeway.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:27] (expert) to reinforce the stones of this causeway or @Check[thievery|dc:29] (master) to jam the hinges on the boards

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves through a marked square and fails a secret @Check[flat|dc:5]

Effect The board's hinges release, causing the paving stones to drop—along with any creatures in the marked area—to the ground below. Affected creatures can attempt to [[/act grab-an-edge]] (DC 29). Creatures that fall take @Damage[(4d12+26)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic] save) as well as the typical falling damage.

stealth.details: (expert) to realize the illusory images conceal a magical trap (noticing the images has a DC of 0)

details.description: Psychically enhanced illusions flood the minds of creatures in the 40-foot-long, 15-foot-wide hallway (the white dotted box on the map) with memories of their past failures.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:32|name:Weaken the images] (master) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 26) to weaken the images; three successes are required to disable the trap
details.reset: The trap resets immediately and can affect the same creature multiple times on the creature's turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature ends a move action within the trap's area

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[(2d10+10)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:32|basic] save) as it recalls its past failures. A creature that takes damage hears a soft whisper offering, "Let me take something from you and I can stop the pain." A creature who agrees loses access to a random skill feat that isn't a prerequisite for another feat, and the creature doesn't take further damage from Echoes of Defeat. This effect lasts for 1 week and can be ended by effects that remove curses.

stealth.details: (expert) to realize the illusory images conceal a magical trap (noticing the images has a DC of 0)

details.description: Psychically-enhanced illusions flood the minds of creatures in the 95-foot-long, 15-foot-wide hallway (the white dotted box on the map) with visions of their failures yet to come.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:32|name:Weaken the images] (master) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (5th rank; counteract DC 26) to weaken the images; three successes are required to disable the trap
details.reset: The trap resets immediately and can affect the same creature multiple times on the creature's turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature ends a move action within the trap's area

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[(2d10+10)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:32|basic] save) as it considers its future failures. A creature that takes damage hears a soft whisper offering, "Let me take something from you and I can stop the pain." A creature who agrees loses access to a random class feat that isn't a prerequisite for another feat, and the creature doesn't take further damage from Flood of Despair. This effect lasts for 1 week and can be ended by effects that remove curses. The creature also immediately detects the secret door at the end of the hall, no matter how far away from the end of the hall they are.

details.description: A magical orb inflicts terrible visions of gruesome ends on anyone who approaches.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:27] (master) to infuse the locus with life force, @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to counteract the locus's energies, @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master) to extricate the orb from the altar
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area. After 1 hour, the haunt reactivates.
details.routine: (1 action; death, emotion, fear, mental) One living creature in the haunt's location (determined randomly) is targeted by a 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Vision of Death] (@Check[will|dc:32|traits:death,emotion,fear,mental]). A creature that critically fails their save doesn't physically flee but instead can't attempt to disable the hazard until they're no longer frightened. Once all creatures have been affected, the haunt deactivates.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the orb or attempts to disable the haunt

Effect Necromantic energy lashes out from the orb and the triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:32] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

Casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Breath of Life], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Death Ward], or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Raise Dead] in the orb's presence automatically destroys the locus; other spells or abilities that restore the dead to life might also do so, at GM discretion.
The locus is sustained by the power of Tar-Baphon. The orb regains 10 Hit Points at the beginning of every round and is repaired by void damage.

details.description: A magical orb drives intruders to commit acts of mindless destruction.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:27] (master) to reconfigure the locus, @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to counteract the locus's energies, @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master) to extricate the orb from the altar
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area. After 1 hour, the haunt reactivates.
details.routine:
(1 action; mental) The destructive impulses grow stronger. Affected creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:32|options:inflicts:confused|traits:mental] save; creatures who fail or critically fail can't attempt to disable the haunt that turn.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected this round.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round, targeting both creatures and objects.

Critical Failure As failure but the confusion lasts for 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the orb or attempts to disable the haunt

Effect A destructive fury fills the minds of all living creatures in the area, who must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:32|options:inflicts:stupefied] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure) for 1 minute. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Creation] or a creation domain spell (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Creative Splash] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Artistic Flourish]) in the orb's presence automatically destroys the locus; other spells or abilities that create things might also do so at GM discretion.
The locus is sustained by the power of Tar-Baphon. The orb regains @Damage[10[healing]] Hit Points at the beginning of every round and is repaired by void damage.
No multiple attack penalty

Trigger The orb is damaged by an attack

Effect The locus makes a destructive lash Strike against the triggering creature.

details.description: An arcane orb sits upon a sinister altar, filling creatures in the area with unimaginable pain.
details.disable: @Check[medicine|dc:27] (master) to soothe the locus's pain, @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] to counteract the locus's energies, @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master) to extricate the orb from the altar
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area. After 1 hour, the haunt reactivates.
details.routine: (1 action; mental, nonlethal) Creatures affected by the haunt's agony lash are wracked with pain, as 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantom Pain] (@Check[will|dc:32|traits:mental,nonlethal]). If a creature falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] due to the haunt, they don't continue taking damage.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the orb or attempts to disable the haunt

Effect All living creatures within 60 feet are overwhelmed by pain and must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

Casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Calm] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Soothe] in the orb's presence automatically destroys the locus; other spells or abilities that ease pain might also do so, at GM discretion.
The locus is sustained by the power of Tar-Baphon. The orb regains 10 Hit Points at the beginning of every round and is repaired by void damage.

details.description: An eerie machine made of levers, crystals, and glowing bits of metal that, as one draws near, causes unsettling aches and pains.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33] (master) to deactivate the machine, or @Check[perception|dc:38] (expert) to decipher the proper order in which the switches and dials should be adjusted in order to power the device down harmlessly
details.reset: If the augnagar still lives and at least one crystallized agony is still functional, the pain milker resets automatically in a number of rounds equal to 7 - the number of remaining crystallized agonies.

description.value:
Trigger The pain milker is struck hard enough to take damage, or any of its dials and levers are adjusted carelessly

Effect The pain milker emits a shrill whine and exudes a blast of violet light. All creatures in area B2 must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save. This DC is reduced by 2 if the machine is broken.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d10[vitality]] or @Damage[4d10[void]] damage (whichever is more harmful).

Failure The creature takes @Damage[8d10[vitality]] or @Damage[8d10[void]] damage (whichever is more harmful) and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1}.

Critical Failure As failure but @Damage[16d10[vitality]] or @Damage[16d10[void]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2}.

details.description: A Druidic glyph tries to transforms a trespasser into an animal.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32|name:Drain Glyph's Power|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to drain the glyph's power harmlessly, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 30) to counteract the glyph

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 30 feet of the glyph without speaking the passphrase in Druidic

Effect The creature is targeted by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cursed Metamorphosis] (@Check[will|dc:32] save).

details.description: A swirling portal leads to unwholesome places.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:35], @Check[occultism|dc:35], or @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master); must have studied Carlina's notes

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes through the doorway

Effect The creature is pulled apart and reassembled as they pass through multiple dimensions at once. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save and disappears for a number of rounds, reappearing at the end of their turn on the final round. Each round at the beginning of their turn, creatures trapped in the doorway must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save against a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cursed Metamorphosis] spell as their bodies are warped and rearranged. A creature can pass through each red-tinged doorway only once.

Critical Success The creature disappears for 1 round. Additionally, the creature gains 10 temporary Hit Points for 24 hours. These stack with temporary Hit Points gained from other red-tinged doorways and replenish each day at midnight as long as the creature is within 1 mile of Narona.

Success The creature disappears for 1 round.

Failure The creature disappears for 2 rounds.

Critical Failure The creature disappears for 3 rounds. If the creature critically fails its saving throw against a cursed metamorphosis spell, it's permanently transformed into an amalgamite.

details.description: Flames engulf the opera house's stage.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:27] to extinguish two adjacent squares of fire (with a critical success extinguishing four adjacent squares); any water spell (such as from @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Create Water] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Hydraulic Push]) cast at the fire extinguishes two squares automatically, or [[/r 2d4 #Squares]] adjacent squares if the caster of the spell achieves a DC 30 result with a spell attack roll; @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Quench] instantly extinguishes all fire within its 20‑foot burst.
details.routine:
On its turn, the stage fire expands inward and outward by 5 feet, filling all adjacent squares with fire. Creatures within these squares take @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic]), as do any creatures that enter a burning square. This fire damage can be inflicted on a creature only once per turn.

In addition, a creature that ends its turn in a burning square takes @Damage[8d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic]); on a failed save, that creature also takes @Damage[1d6[persistent,fire]] damage.

description.value: When this encounter begins, the fire erupts in a ring that encircles the entire stage, as shown by the dotted outline on the map. The stage fire then rolls initiative.

details.description: Freezing black fire bursts from the two quartz altars, filling the room with numbing black flames that scour away memories and emotions as swiftly as they sear flesh and bone.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (master) to sever the links between the door and the lanterns, @Check[religion|dc:35] (master) to use prayer to deactivate the divine magic, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 30) to counteract the trap
details.reset: The trap resets one minute after the doors to area E3 are closed.

description.value:
Trigger The doors to area E3 are opened without someone making blood offerings and lighting the lanterns.

Effect Every creature in area E2 must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:32|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(3d10+13)[cold]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(6d10+27)[cold]] damage and is stupefied 1.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(6d10+27)[cold]] damage, takes @Damage[1d10[persistent,cold]] damage, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2}.

details.description: Three innocuous decorations instilled with telekinetic magic pull objects and pieces of the room itself into spinning clouds of debris that attack all creatures in the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27|name:Take Apart Cloud|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to take apart a telekinetic cloud, @Check[thievery|dc:32|name:Disable Telekinetic Decoration|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to disable each telekinetic decoration, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 30) to counteract each telekinetic decoration
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets 10 minutes after it has no target creatures (because the creatures either moved too far away or died).
details.routine:
(9 actions) Each decoration uses 3 of the trap's actions each turn, and the trap loses 3 actions each turn for every decoration that is disabled. A decoration uses its first action to move its cloud of objects up to 200 feet, its second action to make the objects Strike, and its third action to add more objects to the cloud, increasing its damage by 1d12 (to a maximum of 4d12+10). If a decoration's cloud is already at maximum damage, it does nothing with its third action.

If a decoration's cloud has been destroyed, the decoration instead spends its first action to create a new cloud of objects inside the room (using the starting damage value) and then its second and third actions to have the cloud move and attack.

description.value:
Trigger A creature stays in the room for at least 6 seconds.

Effect Each telekinetic decoration constructs a cloud of objects in the room (three clouds total) and the trap rolls initiative. The creatures in the room when the trap is triggered become the trap's targets, regardless of whether they leave the room or other creatures later enter the room. Each decoration targets a different creature if possible. A target creature that moves at least 1 mile from the trap ceases being a target, at which point the decoration designates a new target.

details.description: The pool sucks in creatures that fall into it, making them sink into its endless depths.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:32|name:Calm the Pool] (master) to quell the fear and calm the pool or @Check[intimidation|dc:35|name:Scare the Pool] to give the pool something worse to fear; three successes required.
details.reset: The trap resets once there are no moving creatures in it.
details.routine: (3 actions) Water from the chalice increases the depth of the water by 10 feet for each action. Each time this happens, creatures in the pool are pushed 10 feet down and take @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage. As the pool has no bottom, creatures in it can fall down indefinitely; they must @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Swim] up to avoid drowning, but the water is especially choppy, so the Athletics DC is @Check[athletics|dc:25|traits:action:swim]. The pool never overflows. Each successful check to Disable reduces the pool's actions by 1, and once the pool is completely Disabled, the water becomes still and the Athletics DC to Swim becomes @Check[athletics|dc:10|traits:action:swim].

description.value:
Trigger A creature falls into the pool

Effect More water pours forth from the chalice. Creatures in the pool become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1}, and they can't decrease their clumsy condition so long as they remain in the pool. The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to notice the tension of a low vibration forming in the area from the imminent release of the Oliphaunt's trumpet

details.description: An overwhelming trumpeting sound vibrates and suffuses the area, causing everything to slow.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:38|name:Shore Up the Area] (expert), @Check[maelstrom-lore|dc:38|name:Shore Up the Area] (expert), @Check[occultism|dc:38|name:Shore Up the Area] (master), @Check[religion|dc:38|name:Shore Up the Area] (master) to recognize and shore up the area against the localized planar instability causing a connection to the Oliphaunt of Jandelay. Four successes are needed to strengthen the area enough against the instability to end the hazard.
details.reset: The area becomes unstable again after 24 hours.
details.routine:
(1 action) Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save. A creature with the slowed condition from this hazard takes a –2 circumstance penalty on further saves against this effect.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d6+6)[sonic]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 round.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d6+13)[sonic]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 2 rounds.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(8d6+26)[sonic]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2} for 2 rounds.

description.value:
Trigger Three or more characters enter the area of planar instability

Effect Pent-up magical energy from another world begins to release in waves of overwhelming sound. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: Countless fey with grisly visages and misshapen bodies cavort throughout the room, kicking up caustic debris and pulling other creatures into their dangerous dance.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:32] or @Check[occultism|dc:32] (expert) to perform a ritual to ward the area against fey intrusion, @Check[performance|dc:34] (master) to meet the Twisted King's demands with a disturbing dance, or @Check[athletics|dc:32] or @Check[intimidation|dc:32] (expert) to fend off or frighten the fey dancers. Three total successes are required to disable the haunt, and each check takes 2 actions.
details.reset: The haunt deactivates after 1 minute. It re-forms every new moon. If all of the glyphs in area B6 are restored—a process requiring 10 days of downtime and 10 successful @Check[nature|dc:36] or @Check[occultism|dc:36] checks—the haunt becomes permanently disabled and doesn't re-form.
details.routine: (1 action) Spectral fey cavort about the room. All creatures in the room are raked by ghostly claws and splashed with toxic water, taking @Damage[(1d10+14)[force],2d10[acid]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save). Creatures that critically fail their Reflex saves are pulled 10 feet toward the standing stones.

description.value:
Trigger 1 round passes since both isqulugs in area B6 are slain, or 10 rounds pass since they rolled initiative

Effect The isqulugs die horribly (if they're still alive), rent by clawed fey hands. Spectral fey pull at the other creatures in the caves, all of which must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is pulled 10 feet toward the standing stones.

Failure The creature is pulled 15 feet toward the standing stones.

Critical Failure The creature is pulled 15 feet toward the standing stones, knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], and takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage (in addition to any damage from the toxic water).

stealth.details: (master) (or +30 and expert if a PC critically succeeded at Recalling Knowledge about the campsite's condition)

details.description: Coils of pale yellow thorny fungal vines on which grow hundreds of brown puffballs suddenly whip into frenzied life.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:33] (master) twice to exorcise the unholy presence within the withermold, or @Check[survival|dc:35] (master) twice to safely remove the sensory tendrils growing in the mold

publication.title: Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower

details.reset: A withermold deactivates at the end of any round when there are no targets within reach to attack with thorny vine Strikes. It resets immediately unless it's used all its Spore Burst reactions for the day, in which case the withermold resets in 24 hours.
details.routine: (3 actions) A withermold makes three Strikes with its thorny vines on its turn, splitting its Strikes up among potential targets before striking a single target more than once.

description.value:
Frequency three times per day

Trigger A Small or larger creature touches or attempts to enter one of the three tents

Effect The thorny vines of the three withermolds growing on the tents suddenly whip into a frenzy, filling the air with clouds of toxic spores. All creatures within a tent or within 10 feet of a tent's exterior take @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:32|basic] save). The withermolds then roll initiative.

details.description: An orb of undeath draws creatures closer to a cursed existence.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:27] to negate the forces of undeath (master), @Check[occultism|dc:30] (expert) to channel vital energy, @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master) to extricate the orb from the altar
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area. After 1 hour, the haunt reactivates.
details.routine: (1 action; void) The wraiths attack all living creatures in the area, dealing @Damage[(3d10+14)[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:32|basic] save). Creatures who fail their saves are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2} on a critical failure) or increase their drained value by 1 (2 on a critical failure), up to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 4}.

description.value: The locus is sustained by the power of Tar-Baphon. The orb regains 10 Hit Points at the beginning of every round and is repaired by void damage.

Trigger A creature touches the orb or attempts to disable the haunt

Effect A cyclone of wraiths spirals from the orb, grasping at all living creatures in the area. Targeted creatures must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:32] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 2} on a critical failure).

A creature who dies in the presence of the orb immediately becomes undead. Casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Bind Undead] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Peaceful Rest] in the presence of the orb automatically destroys the locus; other spells or abilities that combat undead might also do so, at GM discretion.

hp.details: to destroy a nozzle; Tank Hardness 20; TankHP 100 (BT 50) to damage the tank enough to stop it from spewing gas

details.description: Five nozzles hidden across the apartment's ceiling spew thick clouds of acidic poison from a reinforced tank behind the wall.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:35|name:Block One Nozzle|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to block one of the nozzles or @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Shut Off the Tank|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to shut off the tank. The trap deactivates after 5 rounds, once the tank is empty.
details.routine: (1 action) When it's triggered, the trap has five active nozzles, but at the end of its turn each round, one random nozzle deactivates. On the trap's turn, the active nozzles emit a cloud of acidic poison gas that expands to fill the apartment. The cloud deals @Damage[1d6[acid]] damage and @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage per active nozzle to each creature and object in the room (@Check[fortitude|dc:33|basic|name:Spew Cloud|traits:poison] save). Unattended objects automatically fail saves to avoid the damage.

description.value:
Trigger Twelve seconds have passed since the door to the apartment was opened

Effect The trap rolls initiative.

details.description: A spike of stone bursts from the south wall and quickly retracts.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:33|name:Quell Rippling Stone] (master) to quell the rippling stone or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 31) to dispel the magical feedback.

publication.title: Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven

description.value:
Trigger A creature in area E1 casts an arcane spell or uses an arcane ability

Effect The trap makes a stone spike attack against the triggering creature. For the next 1 minute thereafter, the ripples are stronger, giving the trap a +2 circumstance bonus on stone spike Strikes during this time.

details.description: A snapped trip wire causes an alchemical bomb to detonate and blow up part of the tin tub, spilling boiling water all over the trap's victim.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32|name:Remove Trip Wire|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to safely remove the trip wire from the bomb

description.value:
Trigger The trip wire is pulled or severed

Effect An acid flask detonates, spraying acid at nearby creatures and causing the tub of boiling water to burst in the same direction.

All creatures in a @Template[cone|distance:5] east of the triggered boiling tub trap take @Damage[3d6[persistent,acid],(3[splash])[acid],(8d6+20)[fire]]{3d6 persistent acid damage, 3 acid splash damage, and 8d6+20 fire damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:31|basic|traits:acid,fire] save).

details.description: Three phantom hounds chase down intruders, damaging any they pass through.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:36|name:Calm Hound] (expert) to calm each hound, or @Check[occultism|dc:38|name:Banish Hound] (master) to banish each hound
details.reset: 1 hour
details.routine: (9 actions) The trap loses 3 actions each round for each hound disabled. Each hound uses three actions to Stride 40 feet after creatures in the room, passing through corporeal creatures during this movement if possible. Each creature the hound moves through during its movement takes @Damage[(2d8 + 7)[void]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:37|traits:damaging-effect] negates). A creature can only be damaged by each hound once each round, no matter how many times the hound moves through their space.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area

Effect The hounds manifest and bay loudly, dealing @Damage[(3d10 + 16)[sonic]] damage to all creatures within 30 feet of the hounds (@Check[fortitude|dc:33|basic]). On a failed check, a creature is additionally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Tiny black flames mirror living creatures' movements in the city above as scraps of shadow patrol the streets-flames that notice intruders in their "sky" below and reach out to the intruders "above" with a deafening violence that incidentally alerts several of the vault's denizens to the intruders' presence.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Adjust the model] (master) to adjust the model so its attention is focused inward, @Check[occultism|dc:37|name:Discharge the energy] (expert) to cause the accumulated psychic energy to discharge harmlessly, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 32). A character who sneaks through the room with a @Check[stealth|dc:32|name:Avoid the hazard] (expert) check avoids triggering the hazard, but this does not disable it.
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature without supernatural ties to the Shadow Plane enters the room (creatures like fetchlings or divine spellcasters who worship Zon-Kuthon represent two examples of creatures who won't trigger this hazard)

Effect The flickering dark flames on the city's streets flare up and the sound of an entire city populace screaming an alarm fills the room. All creatures in area C1 take @Damage[18d6[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:33|basic]), and all strigoi from Aliriel's Vault immediately know intruders have been spotted in this room. Any strigoi in area C2 quickly investigate and attack the PCs on sight.

details.description: An invisible rune on the door begins emitting a very loud buzzing sound, potentially paralyzing an intruder.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:34|name:Disrupt the Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to disrupt the rune without triggering it or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 31) to magically counteract the rune
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to open the door without using the key

Effect The trap makes a loud buzz and deals @Damage[5d10[electricity]] damage and @Damage[5d10[sonic]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save; this save applies to both the electricity damage and the sonic damage). Further, the creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save.

Critical Success No effect.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] for 1 round.

Failure The target is paralyzed for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. At the end of each of its turns, it can attempt a new @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round, or end it entirely on a critical success.

Critical Failure The target is paralyzed for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, it can attempt a new Fortitude save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round, or end it entirely on a critical success.

details.description: The looms spring to life, sending skeins of spidersilk yarn between them that attempt to ensnare nearby creatures and pull them into the machinery.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:46] (master) to shut down all eight looms at once by activating either of the bypass locks once either lock is located (this check is automatically successful if Fenton's keys are used), @Check[thievery|dc:37] (master) twice to shut down a single loom by deftly removing critical components, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 33) to counteract a single loom
details.reset: A loom deactivates if no Small or larger creatures are in area C6, resetting in 1 minute unless disabled or all looms are broken.
details.routine:
(1 action) Each loom takes one of two possible actions on its turn, depending on if it's currently restraining a creature or not.

Not Restraining a Creature: If a loom isn't restraining a creature, it launches a tangle of yarn at a random creature in the room that isn't currently @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained]. That creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:37] save. If there are no non-restrained targets for the loom to attempt to grapple, it takes no actions this round.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is entangled, and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} until the end of the looms' next turn.

Failure The creature is tangled in the yarn and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} until the end of the loom's next turn—or if it already has the clumsy condition, it becomes restrained (see critical failure).

Critical Failure The creature becomes restrained. A restrained creature can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open] the tangle of yarn or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] with a DC 37 check.

Restraining a Creature: If a loom has restrained a creature, it instead fires more lines of yarn at the restrained creature and then drags that creature (if the creature is Medium or smaller) 20 feet toward itself. (If it's restraining a Large or larger creature, it can only drag the creature 5 feet toward itself). If this dragging causes a creature to become adjacent to the loom, the loom pulls the creature into its grinding gears and inflicts @Damage[5d12[bludgeoning],5d12[slashing]] damage to the creature (@Check[reflex|dc:37|basic] save). The creature is then placed in an adjacent square and is still restrained by that loom.

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature ends their turn in area C6

Effect The looms suddenly begin to grind and churn, filling the room with the squeal of metal on metal. All eight looms roll initiative.

details.description: A magical glyph flashes before triggering a spell.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33] (trained) to disable the glyph, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 33) to counteract the glyph

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door between area B8 and B7

Effect A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Noise Blast], heightened to 6th level (@Check[fortitude|dc:33|traits:damaging-effect,spell,sonic,evocation], @Damage[6d10[sonic]] damage), is centered on the creature opening the door. The sound alerts all creatures in the tower.

details.description: An hourglass-shaped glyph flashes with mauve light, then seems to quickly drain its sand away, as if to indicate time has suddenly run out.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:34] (trained) to remove the glyph without triggering it, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 34) to counteract the glyph.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempting to open the desk drawer or the secret door to area D6 triggers the associated glyph

Effect The glyph of warding targets the creature with a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Spirit Blast] (@Check[fortitude|dc:34|basic] save).

details.description: Ileosa manifests as a crimson ghost and reaches out as if to receive a gift from the ritual's primary caster.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:37|name:Reverse the ritual's power] (expert) or @Check[occultism|dc:40|name:Reverse the ritual's power] (trained) to reverse the ritual's power.

description.value:
Trigger Someone attempts to contact Ileosa with call spirit and achieves any result other than a critical success to cast the ritual

Effect Ileosa closes her fingers and attempts to pull the primary caster's heart from their chest. The primary caster must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:33] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature feels Ileosa's hand grip and tug at their heart; they take @Damage[25[void]] damage, and then the manifestation vanishes.

Failure As success, but the creature takes @Damage[50[void]] damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} as they momentarily see their heart appear in Ileosa's hand before the manifestation vanishes.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[75[void]] damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2}. If this reduces the target to 0 Hit Points, the target dies instantly. For a brief moment, the victim's heart appears in Ileosa's hand before the manifestation vanishes. If the victim is slain by this hazard, their heart drops to the ground with a splat, pulsing its final beats after the manifestation vanishes.

details.description: Thassilonian runes set with onyx erupt with negative energy and thunderous roars at the presence of a living creature.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:35|name:Destroy the key rune] (master) to destroy the key rune, @Check[arcana|dc:37|name:Discharge the runes] (master) to harmlessly discharge the runes, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 31) to counteract the trap

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 5 feet of either double door

Effect All living creatures in the room take @Damage[9d6[void]] damage and @Damage[9d6[sonic]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:33|basic]).

details.description: Haunted coral transfers pain between creatures.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:33] or @Check[occultism|dc:37] or @Check[thievery|dc:37] to pinpoint and disable key synaptic nerves throughout the coral, @Check[religion|dc:37] to quell the coral's tortured memories; each success reduces the coral's number of actions per turn by 1, and the hazard is disabled when after four successes.
details.reset: The coral diffuses and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (4 actions) The coral casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantom Pain] as a 6th-rank spell on a target (DC 33), targeting creatures that aren't already taking persistent mental damage.

description.value:
Trigger A creature takes damage within area D4

Effect The brain coral casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Spirit Link] on Xendra. The hazard rolls initiative.

Trigger A creature within 30 feet of the coral takes persistent mental damage

Effect The coral grants an equal number of temporary Hit Points to a different creature within 30 feet.

details.description: A rift attempts to draw creatures into another plane (the GM chooses the specific plane).
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33|name:Assemble a Rift Seal|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to assemble a rift seal using objects strongly grounded to your plane, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 31) to counteract the rift

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the rift.

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within 30 feet of the rift are drawn into another plane. Each creature can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:33] save to avoid this fate.

stealth.details: (expert) to realize that the shattered runewell is dangerous; noticing the hazard has a DC of 0

details.description: Plumes of magenta and violet light and vapor shift and writhe out of a hole in reality surrounded by the fragments of a runewell suspended mid-explosion.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:37] (master) or @Check[occultism|dc:40] (master) to redirect the magic back in on itself and cancel out the danger, or @Check[crafting|dc:40] (legendary) to reposition runewell fragments so they're not dangerous; three total successes are required to disable the shattered runewell

publication.title: Pathfinder #219: Lord of the Trinity Star

details.routine: (3 actions) When the shattered runewell takes its first action, a towering, 30-foot-tall image of Runelord Xanderghul formed from scintillating magenta and violet light and fire looms up from the runewell. On each of the hazard's three actions, this manifestation of Runelord Xanderghul scowls down at the PCs, and tendrils of strangely colored fire lash out of the runewell, each targeting a different PC in the area. The effects of these tendrils vary according to their color; these ranged Strikes can target anyone in the Eye of Arrogance and accrue multiple attack penalties normally.

description.value:
Trigger Intruders enter the Eye of Arrogance

Effect All of the illusory peacocks in the demiplane emit loud shrieks of anger. All intruders to the Eye of Arrogance must attempt a @Check[will|dc:37] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The shattered runewell then rolls initiative.
plus the struck creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round if they fail a @Check[will|dc:37] save

details.description: Spring-loaded wedges of sharpened stone pop out of the wooden barricade.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33|name:Supress Spring Mechanism|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to suppress the spring mechanism for one 5-foot segment of the barricade
details.reset: The trap must be manually reset by pushing the wedges back into place.

description.value: No multiple attack penalty

Trigger A creature enters the area adjacent to a barrier

Effect Wedges of sharpened stone extend from the barricade in all directions and attack all creatures within 10 feet.

stealth.details: (master) to detect that the spore stomach is hazardous; noticing the spore stomach has a DC of 0

details.description: A puffball-like fungus periodically vents gray spores into the air.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:37] (master) three times to carefully prune away the fungal roots that connect to the garden below, or @Check[religion|dc:37] (master) three times to exorcise the demonic energies from the fungus

publication.title: Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower

details.reset: The spore stomach deactivates if it starts a turn and there are no non-fungus creatures in area E1. It then immediately resets.
details.routine: (1 action; divine, poison) On the first round, the spore stomach sprays a 40-foot-long cone of spores into the left half of area E1. On the second round, it sprays spores into the right half of area E1. On the third round, it takes no action. It repeats this pattern thereafter every 3 rounds. A creature in the area of spores takes @Damage[10d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:37|basic|traits:divine,poison] save).

description.value:
Trigger The spore stomach takes damage, an attempt to disable it fails, or combat breaks out in area E1

Effect The spore stomach clenches, exuding gray spores in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20]. Non-fungus creatures in this area take @Damage[10d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:37|basic] save). The spore stomach then rolls initiative.

details.description: The floor of this circular room pivots and shifts awkwardly when weight is placed upon its surface, pitching those who attempt to walk on it off their feet and potentially into one of two deep spiked pits.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:37] (expert) to wedge scraps of material into the edges of the room to hamper the floor's tilt (requires 4 successes).
details.reset: This trap resets instantly.
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative each turn, the floor tilts randomly, forcing all characters standing on the floor of the room to attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:33] save to avoid being pitched into one of the two pits.

description.value:
Trigger A creature or object of at least 3 bulk moves onto the floor; the trap can use this reaction multiple times in a round, but only once per triggering creature or object

Effect The floor pitches and rolls. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:33] save.

Critical Success The character can move across the room normally this turn, without having to attempt Acrobatics checks, but treats the unstable floor as difficult terrain.

Success The character treats the ground in this room as difficult terrain, but each time they move they must succeed at a @Check[acrobatics|dc:30] check or they fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] at the start of their turn. A character who falls prone must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:33] save or fall into one of the two spiked pits to the north or south of the room (see Failure).

Failure As success but the character automatically falls prone and must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:33] save to avoid being pitched into one of the two spiked pits to the north or south of the room. A character who is pitched into a pit can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] to avoid falling. The DC to Grab an Edge or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Climb] a pit's walls is 33. A character who falls into one of the pits plunges 50 feet onto a bed of spikes, taking @Damage[25[bludgeoning],2d6[piercing]]{25 points of bludgeoning damage and 2d6 piercing damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:33|basic]).

Critical Failure As failure, but the character automatically rolls into one of the two pits.

details.description: The room fills with swirling vortexes of freezing wind that swiftly freeze flesh and chill the bones.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33] (master) to adjust the wheel's mechanisms so that it won't trigger the Test of Endurance, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 31) to counteract the trap's magic.
details.reset: The trap resets automatically.

description.value:
Trigger A creature critically fails at an attempt to Rotate the Wheel or releases their grip on the wheel once they've started to rotate it

Effect A vortex of freezing wind howls through the room-all creatures in the room must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:33] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d6[cold]] damage for each revolution the wheel has been rotated (max 7d6).

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d6[cold]] damage for each revolution the wheel has been rotated (max 14d6) and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued].

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[3d6[cold]] damage for each revolution the wheel has been rotated (max 21d6), becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}, and becomes fatigued.

details.description: The steps angle downward into a slope, releasing all currently placed boulders to roll back down to the north.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:37] (expert) to disable the hidden hinges for one of the steps, thereby locking all four steps into place
details.reset: The trap Releases Boulders 24 hours after it Opens Doors. Once the trap Releases Boulders, it resets itself automatically.

description.value:
Trigger All four boulders are placed within six minutes;

Effect the doors to area D4 open.

Trigger All four boulders are not placed within six minutes

Effect All four steps angle downward, causing any boulders held within to roll toward area D1. Each rolling boulder inflicts @Damage[(3d6 + 6)[bludgeoning]] damage on all creatures in area D2(@Check[reflex|dc:37|basic]).

details.description: Obscure magical runes hidden within the runes of the 16 named warriors bind a pair of derghodaemons into the walls of this hallway.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:37] (expert) to remove a set of runes linked to one bound derghodaemon, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 31) to counteract the trap's magic.
details.reset: If at least one of the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.Derghodaemon]{Derghodaemons} survives, the trap resets after 1 hour, causing the surviving derghodaemons to be completely restored to full health and to merge with the floors.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves past the bend in the middle of the hall

Effect A pair of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.Derghodaemon] rise up from the floor to the north and southeast near the stairs; the daemons roll for initiative using Stealth, and fight to the death but do not pursue foes out of this area.

details.description: A sphere of concentrated agony harvested from a qlippoth begins pulsing with violet light as it blasts the room with otherworldly agony.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:37] (master) to dismantle the housing and refocus the agony inward so it consumes itself harmlessly, @Check[arcana|dc:33] or @Check[occultism|dc:33] (master) to vent the agony in a controlled way that causes the portalrender to become inert after 1 minute (during which it can still be triggered normally), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 31)
details.routine: The unfinished portalrender continues to flood area B4 with agony, and everyone in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:33] save against its Agonizing Pulse. At the end of its turn, the unfinished portalrender attempts a @Check[flat|dc:6]. On a failure, its stores of agony deplete and it deactivates, causing the central orb of agony to fade away permanently.

description.value:
Trigger The unfinished portalrender takes any damage or is moved (the portalrender is Bulk 5)

Effect The central sphere of concentrated agony flashes bright violet, filling the mind's of the room's inhabitants with overwhelming mental sensations of pain and hallucinations that the world around them is being torn away and replaced by a demon-haunted apocalypse. All creatures in area B4 must attempt a @Check[will|dc:33] save, then the trap rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[mental]] damage from the pain.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[8d6[mental]] damage from the pain and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] until the end of their next turn.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[16d6[mental]] damage from the pain, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1}, and is confused for 1 minute.

stealth.details: (master) or @Check[perception|dc:37|name:Notice Ghostly Faces|traits:hazard,complex,environmental,magical] (master) to notice what appear to be screaming ghostly faces in the crystals

details.description: Three clusters of gleaming, colorful crystals are corrupted with necromantic energy that causes them to reflect negative emotions. All three must be disabled or destroyed to negate the hazard.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:29|name:Fracture the Crystals|traits:hazard,complex,environmental,magical] (master) to carefully fracture the crystals or @Check[crafting|dc:27|name:Stop the Resonance|traits:hazard,complex,environmental,magical] (expert) to place objects to stop the resonance, both disabling one 5-foot patch of crystals.

publication.title: Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City

details.reset: The wailing crystals deactivate and reset once no living creatures are within 20 feet.
details.routine: (3 actions) The hazard uses each action for an Anguished Shriek. The wailing crystals lose 1 action for each 5-foot patch of crystals that is broken or destroyed.

items.name: HP is 72 for each 5 foot patch

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 20 feet of a wailing crystal.

Effect A despairing chorus of cries echoes off the crystals, working its way into the minds of all living creatures in Jewelgate Way Station, affecting them with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Wave of Despair] (7th level, @Check[will|dc:33|name:Crushing Despair|traits:mental,emotion,magical,spell] save).

The hazard rolls initiative.

A shrieking, distorted face composed of writhing necromantic energy lances out of the crystals to target a random living creature within 20 feet. The target takes @Damage[4d6[mental],4d6[sonic]]{4d6 mental damage and 4d6 sonic damage} (@Check[will|dc:33|basic] save).

details.description: Magic stored in runes painted in powdered bone and banshees' tears lash out at anyone other than Heh Shan-Bao who opens the secondary compartment.
details.disable: [[/act disable-device dc=37]] to scratch out the runes and dissipate their magic harmlessly, @Check[arcana|dc:40] to sabotage the spell and cause the runes to dry up and blow away as inert powder, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 31) to counteract the runes

publication.title: Pathfinder Season of Ghosts Hardcover Compilation

description.value:
Trigger Someone other than Heh Shan-Bao opens the secondary compartment

Effect A howling, spectral face manifests out of the chest and screeches, causing all within area C4 to attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:33] save; the sound also alerts the jiang-shi in areas C3 a and C6, who quickly come to investigate the sound.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[7d6[void]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[15d6[void]] damage. In addition, their flesh withers and weakens, leaving them to appear old and decrepit—the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} until the curse is removed.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[30d6[void]] damage and becomes cursed as failure above, but becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2}.

details.description: A monk's tormented spirit attacks intruders.
details.disable: @Check[irori-lore|dc:32|name:Restore the Prayer Items|traits:haunt]{Irori Lore} (trained) to clean the room and return the prayer items to their proper arrangement, @Check[religion|dc:37|name:Iroran Prayer of Peace] (expert) to state an Iroran prayer of peace and put the haunt to rest, or @Check[occultism|dc:40|name:Create a Ward Against Future Hauntings] (master) to create a ward against future hauntings
details.reset: The haunt re-forms after 1 hour. The haunt becomes disabled and doesn't re-form if a creature lights all of the candles in the room and offers a prayer to Irori.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the prayer room.

Effect The abbot's spirit appears and begins to channel powerful ki energy. One round later, the room fills with a powerful kinetic storm, dealing @Damage[(3d10+15)[electricity]] damage and @Damage[(3d10+15)[force]] damage to all creatures in the room. Creatures in the room must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:37|traits:haunt] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage, @Damage[2d8[persistent,electricity]] damage, and becomes deafened for 1 minute.

stealth.details: (master) to notice the sudden breeze in the theater

details.description: A portal to the Plane of Air rips open onstage, causing roaring winds to tear through the opera house
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:38|name:Air Rift Arcana|traits:hazard,environmental,magical,air] (master) to sew the magical threads of the rift shut, @Check[nature|dc:40|name:Air Rift Nature|traits:hazard,environmental,magical,air] or @Check[survival|dc:40|name:Air Rift Survival|traits:hazard,environmental,magical,air] (legendary) to open enough windows and doors to reduce its intensity, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 34) to counteract the air rift
details.routine:
(1 action) Winds blow through the area in a random direction, causing creatures and objects within 50 feet of the air rift to float up, down, north, south, east, or west (roll a d6 to determine which direction). Creatures and objects in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:37|traits:damaging-effect,air,environmental,magical,hazard] save. If a creature is pushed into a solid object or another creature by this effect, both take @Damage[(3d8+15)[bludgeoning]] damage on impact.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is pushed 10 feet, but takes only half damage from impact.

Failure The creature is pushed 20 feet, falls prone, and takes full damage from impact.

Critical Failure The creature is pushed 40 feet, falls prone, and takes double damage from impact.

items.name: AC 40 vs. Reactions and Ranged Weapon Attacks

description.value: The Elemental Plane of Air is opposed by the Plane of Earth. Any spell or magical effect with the earth trait that targets a creature or area within 50 feet of the rift deals 10 damage to the rift per spell ranks (or per 2 levels for magic items, feats, and so forth). For example, a 3rd-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Earthbind] spell cast on a creature near the air rift deals 30 points of damage to the rift

Trigger A creature comes within 50 feet of the rift

Effect The air rift creates a shroud of subjective gravity around the creature for 1 minute. The creature can use a free action, which has the concentrate trait, to choose any direction they wish for gravity to pull them. This allows a creature to Stride along any surface or Fly in a straight line by choosing a direction into midair. For unattended objects and mindless creatures, this is microgravity: they float in place unless pushed off a surface.

details.description: A shimmering field of force fills the carved wooden archway, beyond which a flight of stairs leads upward.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:30] (expert) to perform the four aria lines (requires four successes), @Check[thievery|dc:41] (master) to deface one of the four carved images (requires four successes), disintegrate of any rank, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 31) to counteract the barrier
details.reset: The harmonic barrier resets the instant after it is triggered.

description.value:
Trigger A creature interacts with the barrier

Effect The barrier blasts the triggering creature with devastating sound, ringing out like a shrill operatic cry; the triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:34] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[7d6[sonic]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[14d6[sonic]] damage and is pushed back 5 feet.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[28d6[sonic]] damage, is pushed back 10 feet, and falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

stealth.details: (master) to notice that clouds of debris are coalescing into swirling, faceless shapes held together by rust-red fog and spectral, dripping clots of gore

details.description: A low howl rises from the ground, gathering strength as scraps of ancient armor, bone fragments, and clumps of earth rise into the air. Crimson vapor holds the faceless shape together, never coalescing into an identifiable form, but merely writhing through ever-changing images of torment and destruction.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:38|name:Negate Psychic Resonances] (master) to temporarily negate the psychic resonances around each fragment, causing the agglomeration to fall apart, or @Check[religion|dc:33|name:Interrupt Unholy Power] (expert) to temporarily interrupt Verex-That-Was's unholy power with divine energy from another source. If a boneburst takes 40 points of damage from a single source and it isn't destroyed, it is rendered dormant for 1 round.
details.reset: If the boneburst spends 5 consecutive rounds without coalescing around a living creature, it falls dormant, sinking back into the earth. After it falls dormant or is disabled, the boneburst gathers energy over the course of the next hour, after which it can be triggered again.
details.routine: (2 actions) An awakened boneburst occupies a @Template[square|distance:20]; it is reduced to a @Template[square|distance:10] when it reaches its BT. It can occupy the same space as other creatures, but not other bonebursts. On its initiative, the boneburst Flies up to 30 feet toward the nearest living creature as its first action. As its second action, the boneburst then coalesces around any living creature within its space, invisible shards of bone tearing at their flesh. Each living creature in its space takes @Damage[(2d10+18)[slashing],1d10[spirit]|options:area-damage]{2d10+18 slashing damage and 1d10 spirit damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic|options:area-effect] save). If no living creatures are within its space, the boneburst can use its second action to Fly up to 30 feet.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 50 feet of a dormant boneburst

Effect The boneburst awakens, roiling up from the ground, and rolls initiative.

details.description: The pain and agony endured by Abbot Ildeben as the Gardener cut his throat lingers on in this room.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:38] check to appeal to the haunt and convince it the PCs aren't the source of its pain, or @Check[religion|dc:33] to rebuke the haunt in Cayden Cailean's name.
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 5 feet of the bloodstain

Effect A spectral sickle manifests behind the triggering creature, then slices around to cut at that creature's throat. The target must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:39|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes @Damage[4d10[void]] damage.

Failure The target takes @Damage[(4d10+35)[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}. An eerie white scar manifests on the target's neck; this scar persists as long as the drained effect caused by this haunt lasts.

Critical Failure As failure but @Damage[(4d10+70)[void]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2}. The eerie white scar periodically appears to drip with blood, but this is purely an unsettling cosmetic side-effect; it does no additional damage to the victim.

hp.details: The mirror can't be damaged while any mirror duplicate is alive

stealth.details: (master) to notice it isn't a regular mirror

details.description: A magic mirror replaces characters with malicious mirror duplicates from another dimension.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:34] (legendary) to retrieve a creature from the other dimension within 10 minutes of the switch (possible only if their mirror duplicate is dead), @Check[thievery|dc:39] (master) to permanently disable the mirror once all mirror duplicates are dead, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 32) to counteract the mirror for 1 minute and prevent additional replacements during that time.
details.reset: The mirror is always ready to absorb creatures into the other dimension. Ten minutes after a creature is sucked into the mirror, if an ally doesn't rescue the creature, it reaches the other dimension. In the mirror dimension, it counts as a mirror duplicate. These dimensions are alternate realities, not planes, so even spells like @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Interplanar Teleport] can't reach them.
details.routine: (1 action) The mirror absorbs another reflected creature and replaces it with a mirror duplicate. A mirror duplicate attacks on their own initiative, using the same statistics as the original creature, but they hate all other non-duplicate creatures. A mirror duplicate can spend 3 actions in contact with the mirror to return to their original dimension and release the duplicated creature.

description.value:
Trigger A creature is reflected in the mirror

Effect The mirror absorbs the creature, replacing it with a malicious mirror duplicate (@Check[reflex|dc:34] to avoid being absorbed into the mirror), and rolls initiative.

details.description: Fun-house mirrors distort a viewer's reflection, painfully reshaping their body to match what appears in the reflected images.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:38|name:Deface the Mirror|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to deface the mirror's pane, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 36) to dispel the mirror or counteract the transformation
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 day.

description.value:
Trigger A creature is reflected in the mirror

Effect The triggering creature's body is painfully squished, stretched, and distorted to match their reflection. The creature takes @Damage[(6d10 + 30)[force]] damage and must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:39|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} for 4 rounds.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is clumsy 2 and enfeebled 2 for 1 minute. As long as the creature is clumsy or enfeebled, it additionally takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds.

When the effects of this trap end, the triggering creature's transformation ends, and its body reverts to its natural form.

details.description: The earth shakes violently side to side, opening a fissure in the ground, partially collapsing builds, and scattering debris in all directions.

publication.title: Pathfinder Society Scenario #6-19: What Walks Again

description.value: The ground shakes violently. All creatures in the area take @Damage[8d10[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save. On a critical failure, the creature falls into a 60-foot-deep fissure opened by the tremor. They can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] (DC 30 to prevent the fall, with a –2 circumstance penalty due to the moving ground). A creature that falls takes an additional @Damage[30[bludgeoning]]{30} falling damage, is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], and must be rescued from their predicament by another PC taking one action to pull them free. If any creatures are still in fissures due to previous tremors, they automatically get a Failure on this save.

details.description: A mass of illusions floods the senses of creatures, plunging them into an ever-shifting maze of fluid realties.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:38] (legendary) to seize strands of a false reality and tug at them to unravel the surrounding illusions, @Check[arcana|dc:41] to take command of the illusions in a false reality and force them to consume themselves, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th-rank; counteract DC 39) to counteract one of the four realities the illusion creates

publication.title: Pathfinder #219: Lord of the Trinity Star

details.routine:
(1 action) The trap shifts from the current reality into a randomly determined one that affects all those lost within the illusion in a different way. The new reality restores @Damage[20[healing]] Hit Points to itself if it had previously taken damage. Each time a PC succeeds at a check or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] attempt to disable the trap or the current reality is reduced to 0 HP, the current illusory reality vanishes, leaving the PCs in an infinite haze of nothingness save for a three-tailed comet burning in the "sky" above (recognizable as the Trinity Star with a @Check[arcana|dc:25], @Check[nature|dc:25], @Check[occultism|dc:25], or @Check[religion|dc:25] check) until the trap takes its next action and randomly determines its new reality from the options that remain. If only one endless reality remains when it's disabled or destroyed, the PCs are returned to the real world and the trap is entirely disabled.

When the endless reality shifts, determine its effects and appearance by randomly selecting from the following (rerolling any results that have already been disabled or destroyed).

Each time a PC critically succeeds at a saving throw to resist a reality's damage, the trap degrades; it's disable DCs and hardness are reduced by 2 with each such critical success. If the trap's hardness is reduced to 0 in this way, the entire trap is disabled at once.
