Burning Reality (fire) Additional Immunity fire; Effect The world appears to catch on fire, while legions of burning monsters attack the party. Every PC takes @Damage[10d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:34|basic|traits:fire] save).

Frozen Reality (cold) Additional Immunity cold; Effect The world appears to crust over with ice, while armies of frozen ghosts assault the PCs. Every PC takes @Damage[10d6[cold]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:34|basic|traits:cold] save).

Impossible Reality (mental) Additional Immunity physical; Effect The world distorts into a reality of impossible geometry as the PCs feel their own bodies distort to match twisted reflections of unworkable configurations. Every PC takes @Damage[10d6[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:34|basic|traits:mental] save).

Melting Reality (acid) Additional Immunity acid; Effect The world appears to dissolve away, as slithering oozes swarm out of the surroundings to engulf the PCs. Every PC takes @Damage[10d6[acid]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:34|basic|traits:acid] save).

description.value:
Trigger A non-follower of Xanderghul attempts to enter the hallway or chambers to the east of this area

Effect A flood of illusions overwhelms in a rush of images and sounds, engulfing the triggering creature and all of their allies within the dawnsilver workshop. Affected creatures must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:34] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure) for 24 hours.

stealth.details: (master) to detect that the fungal heart is hazardous; noticing the fungal heart has a DC of 0

details.description: A pulsing green cluster of fungus suspended in the middle of the spherical room by thick lengths of fibrous stalks.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:33] (master) three times to carefully prune away specific tethers that keep the fungal heart active, or @Check[religion|dc:33] (master) three times to exorcise the demonic energies from the fungal heart.
details.reset: The fungal heart deactivates and resets if 3 rounds pass during which no attempts to disable or damage it take place. On subsequent activations of "Treerazer Notices," no telepathic message is sent—it's simply an overwhelming sensation of imminent doom.
details.routine: (1 action; divine, poison) The fungal heart clenches as if beating like a real heart, then exudes a churning cloud of toxic spores that fills areas D1 and D2. All creatures in these areas take @Damage[10d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:34|basic] save; creatures in area D1 gain a +4 circumstance bonus to this save due to the diffusion of the spores as they wash into the larger area).

description.value:
Trigger The fungal heart takes damage, or an attempt to disable it fails

Effect A sinister voice fills the minds of all creatures in area D1 or D2, speaking in Chthonian: "And so my whelp has failed, yet you have not found salvation—you have doomed Kyonin to fall. Will you be the first to die, or shall you linger long enough to see all you hold dear become mine?" The PCs immediately recognize this voice as belonging to Treerazer and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:39] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} (or doomed 1, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 4}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for as long as they remain frightened on a critical failure). The Fungal Heart then rolls initiative.

details.description: A magical glyph flashes before triggering a spell.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:34] (trained) to disable the glyph, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 34) to counteract the glyph

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door to area D6

Effect The creature is targeted by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Spirit Blast] spell (@Check[fortitude|dc:34|basic|traits:force,necromancy,spell], @Damage[16d6[force]] damage).

details.description: Every potential point of entry to the house, whether window or door, is covered with a barrier of sonic energy that prevents passage, visible as a faintly shimmering energy field.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:38] (expert) to remove the barrier from a single window or exterior door, @Check[occultism|dc:38] (master) to manipulate the harmonics in a way that disrupts the entire barrier (four successes are required to disable it), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 31) to counteract the barrier from a single window or exterior door
details.reset: The harmonic barrier resets the instant after it's triggered.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to enter or exit the manor through a window or exterior door

Effect The barrier blasts the triggering creature with devastating sound, ringing out like a thunderous low-pitched gong; the triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:34] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and can pass through the window or door without harm.

Success The creature takes @Damage[7d6[sonic]] damage but can pass through the window or door.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[14d6[sonic]] damage and is pushed back 5 feet.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[28d6[sonic]] damage, is pushed back 10 feet, and falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

hp.details: to destroy one of the forges (both must be destroyed to disable the haunt completely)

details.description: The rundown furnaces blaze to life, filling with metal and fire, while the wall of hardened lava to the east appears to revert to red hot molten rock.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:38] (master) to exorcise the spirits or @Check[intimidation|dc:41] to quell them with furious anger; three total successes are required to disable the haunt

publication.title: Pathfinder #219: Lord of the Trinity Star

details.reset: 1 hour.
details.routine:
(4 actions) The burning spirits shriek and howl, swooping in and out of the forges to the south, which roar into scorching life. The spirits scoop up gobs of phantasmal molten dawnsilver and hurl them at different targets in the room. Up to four randomly determined creatures are targeted in this way with molten dawnsilver Strikes (the spirits can target any creature in area B5). A single creature may be targeted only once per round, so if there are fewer than four different targets, the haunt's extra actions are lost. The haunt also loses two actions from its routine if one of the two forges are destroyed.

On a critical hit from a molten dawnsilver Strike, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round as the molten dawnsilver swiftly cools and encases the target. Molten dawnsilver produced by the haunt vanishes entirely 1 round after it's been thrown.

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 30 feet of either forge

Effect The eastern wall appears to transform into molten lava, and dozens of shrieking human spirits made of fire fly out of the lava to spiral and scorch through the room. Creatures in area B5 must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:34] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage

Failure The creature takes @Damage[8d6[fire]] damage, @Damage[1d6[persistent,fire]] damage, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[16d6[fire]] damage, @Damage[2d6[persistent,fire]] damage, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

details.description: A host of violent spirits possess three clockwork mannequins, and each lashes out at any creatures that dare walk near.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:37|name:Jam a mannequin's clockwork mechanisms|traits:clockwork,haunt,mechanical,hazard]{Thievery (master)} to jam a mannequin's clockwork mechanisms or a @Check[religion|dc:35|name:Exorcise a spirit from a mannequin|traits:clockwork,haunt,mechanical,hazard]{Religion (expert)} twice to exorcise a spirit from a mannequin.
details.reset: The haunt deactivates after it has no target creatures and resets, usually over the course of 1 round, as the mannequins move to their original positions. If a mannequin is more than 150 feet from its original position, the haunt takes longer to reset, as the mannequin moves back 150 feet each round.
details.routine: (9 actions) Each of the mannequins uses three of the haunt's actions on each turn, and the haunt loses three actions each turn for every disabled or destroyed mannequin. Each mannequin uses its first action to Stride up to 50 feet and its remaining two actions to make clockwork fist Strikes. Each has its own separate multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 30 feet of a mannequin

Effect The mannequins all lurch towards the triggering creature, Striding up to 50 feet, then roll initiative.

details.description: The night giant mummy unfolds its arms and leans forward to exhale a blast of gray mist and shrieking spirits into the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33] to "blind" the statue so that it cannot notice any who approach, @Check[occultism|dc:35] to redirect and expend the trap's occult energies harmlessly, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 32) to counteract the trap.
details.reset: The trap resets during the following round, after which it can be triggered again.

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature in area A2 moves within 15 feet of the secret door to the north or the double door to area A5

Effect The mummy exhales a blast of gray mist into the room that imparts an overwhelming sense that night itself is consuming all thought. The trap deals @Damage[12d6[mental]] damage to all creatures in area A2, who each must attempt a @Check[will|dc:36] saving throw.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for as long as they remain frightened.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}, and is fleeing for as long as they remain frightened.

details.description: All six sides of this adamantine-reinforced box are covered with Thassilonian runes. Touching a rune allows one to exchange its position with another.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:41] (expert) to feel the subtle vibrations when moving a rune to shift into the right position, or @Check[thassilon-lore|dc:33] (expert) or @Check[society|dc:38] (master) while being able to read Thassilonian to work out the anagram puzzle using traditional means, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 34)
details.reset: The paradox engine deactivates 1 minute, and its owb prophet returns to its interior after all creatures leave the area, but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: (3 actions) The slicing shadows congeal into the form of what appears to be the upper half of a skeletal, cowled humanoid: an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.Owb Prophet]. This owb prophet is a creation of the paradox engine and does not have a forsaken patron. While the owb prophet is active, the paradox engine no longer uses its Slicing Shadows reaction; instead, the owb prophet focuses its attacks on creatures that attempt to damage or deactivate the engine. If the owb prophet is destroyed, the paradox engine's disable DCs and Hardness are reduced by 5. It thereafter functions as a simple hazard with the Slicing Shadows reaction, and resets after 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger The paradox engine is damaged, or a creature fails a check to disable it

Effect Slats on the sides of the box open to release beams of razor-sharp freezing darkness that swipe throughout area C5 and extend 10 feet south into area C4; all creatures in this area who lack the Shadow trait take @Damage[5d6[slashing]] damage and @Damage[5d6[cold]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:34|basic]).

stealth.details: (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:39|traits:action:seek] (master) to notice the control panel. Anyone specifically searching the name plate of Abresha Tallow-Hands locates the control panel automatically.

details.description: Countless poison darts fire from holes in the statues and walls of this hall.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:34|name:Disable Trap|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) on the control panel deactivates the trap.

publication.title: Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap launches one dart against every creature in the hall as 1 action. Because it launches darts continuously, the trap can also use the Continuous Barrage free action (see below) to launch darts at each creature during that creature's turn. The trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

description.value:
Trigger A creature makes noise in the hall, which can be avoided with a successful @Check[stealth|dc:28|name:Avoid Notice|traits:action:sneak,move,secret] check

Effect The trap makes a poisoned dart Strike against the triggering creature, then rolls initiative.

Trigger A creature within the hallway finishes an action while the trap is active

Effect The trap makes a poisoned dart Strike against the triggering creature.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:28]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 4d6 poison damage and clumsy 2 (1 round)

Stage 2 5d6 poison damage, clumsy 2, and –5-foot status penalty to all Speeds (1 round)

Stage 3 7d6 poison damage, clumsy 2, and –10-foot status penalty to all Speeds (1 round)

details.description: Multiple tiny needles filled with poison are embedded in this secret door, spring-loaded to pop out.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:34|name:Break the Mechanism|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to break the mechanism, or @Check[perception|dc:39|traits:action:seek] (master) to identify the three stones that, when pressed, prevent the trap from triggering
details.reset: If disabled, the trap resets after 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature tries to open the door, whether or not it is still locked, without first depressing three specific stones on the door's surface

Effect Needles pop out of the door at the triggering creature.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:34]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[5d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round).

details.description: Mogaru's steady footsteps pick up speed, causing the earth around him to tremble as though from a powerful earthquake.
details.disable: Three @Check[athletics|dc:39|name:Brace a Small Structure or Surface] (trained), @Check[crafting|dc:39|name:Brace a Small Structure or Surface] (trained), or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:39|name:Brace a Small Structure or Surface] (trained) checks to brace a small structure or surface to cancel the effects of the quake in that area; @Check[performance|dc:41|name:Calm Mogaru] (legendary) to calm Mogaru momentarily
details.reset: Mogaru doesn't rush this way again for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

description.value:
Trigger Mogaru moves at least 60 feet

Effect The ground shakes in a @Template[emanation|distance:120] from Mogaru's space. This creates a variety of effects, depending on where the player characters are at the time of the quake.

In most parts of the city, the ground becomes difficult terrain, and creatures on it are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} for 1 round.

In parts of the city with existing underground spaces (like sewers, sinkholes, or catacombs), fissures open up in the ground. Creatures in the area tumble into the resulting 40-foot-deep hole unless they succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:40] save.

In bays, on beaches, and in other relatively substantial but shallow bodies of water, Mogaru's movement creates dangerous waves. Creatures in the water or within 60 feet of the waterline are struck by waves that deal @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic] save). On a critically failed save, a creature is instantly swept 60 feet out to sea and 60 feet under the water's surface.

details.description: Ebeshra slithers across the ground, causing the earth to tremble as though from a powerful earthquake.
details.disable: three @Check[athletics|dc:38|name:Brace for Earthquake (Athletics)|traits:environmental,kaiju,hazard] (trained), @Check[crafting|dc:38|name:Brace for Earthquake (Crafting)|traits:environmental,kaiju,hazard] (trained), or @Check[engineering|dc:38|name:Brace for Earthquake (Engineering)|traits:environmental,kaiju,hazard]{Engineering Lore} (trained) checks to brace a small structure or surface to cancel the effects of the quake in that area, or a single @Check[diplomacy|dc:41|name:Entreat With Ebeshra|traits:environmental,kaiju,hazard] (master) check to entreat Ebeshra to stand down

publication.title: Pathfinder Lost Omens Monsters of Myth

details.reset: Ebeshra doesn't rush this way again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Rounds]] rounds.

description.value:
Trigger Ebeshra moves at least 60 feet on land

Effect The ground shakes in a @Template[emanation|distance:120] from Ebeshra's space. This creates a variety of effects depending on the surrounding environment.

In an area without significant underground space, the ground becomes greater difficult terrain, and creatures on it are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} for 1 round.

In areas with existing underground spaces (like sewers, sinkholes, or catacombs), fissures open up in the ground. Creatures in the area tumble into the resulting 80-foot-deep hole unless they succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:40|name:Avoid Fissure|traits:environmental,kaiju,hazard] save.

In bays, on beaches, and in other relatively substantial but shallow bodies of water, the tremors create dangerous waves. Creatures in the water or within 60 feet of the waterline are struck by waves that deal @Damage[10d8[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic|name:Crashing Waves|traits:environmental,kaiju,hazard] save). On a critical failure, a creature is instantly swept 60 feet out to sea and 60 feet under the water's surface.

details.description: Buffeting winds swirl sharp grains of sand about the room.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:38|name:State an Iroran Koan|traits:haunt]{Religion} (expert) to state an Iroran koan about being complete only upon recognizing one's incompleteness, or @Check[occultism|dc:41|name:Ward Against Hauntings|traits:haunt] (master) to create a ward against future hauntings
details.reset: The haunt deactivates after 1 minute and re-forms after 1 hour. The haunt is disabled and doesn't re-form if at least two creatures spend 1 minute to create an image with the sand. Creating the image requires a successful @Check[crafting|dc:30|name:Create Image in Sand] or @Check[thievery|dc:30|name:Create Image in Sand] check.
details.routine: (1 action) The haunt's violent winds savagely buffet everyone in the room, dealing @Damage[(6d10+30)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to living creatures (@Check[reflex|dc:34|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:blinded|traits:haunt]). On a critically failed save, a creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to leave the chamber

Effect A powerful wind pulls the creature back into the room. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:39|traits:haunt] save. The haunt can use this reaction any number of times per round, but only once per creature that tries to leave the room. The haunt then rolls initiative, if it hasn't already.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is pulled back 10 feet into the room.

Failure The creature is pulled back 15 feet into the room and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is pulled back 15 feet into the room and is blinded for 1 minute.

details.description: Six statues rapidly slide around the room on rollers, slowing down intruders and striking at them with spring-loaded sword arms.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:34|name:Disable Statue Rollers|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to disable a specific statue's rollers, or @Check[thievery|dc:39|name:Disable Trap (Control Panel)|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) on the control panel in A4 deactivates the whole trap. For each statue disabled, the trap loses 1 action from its routine and the DC for its predictive impediment ability decreases by 4. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank, counteract DC 32) doesn't harm the statues but removes the predictive impediment ability. Breaking the control panel prevents the trap from resetting.

publication.title: Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets one minute after the room is empty.
details.routine: (6 actions) On the trap's initiative, each functioning statue slides up to 40 feet around the room and makes a statue shortsword Strike against an adjacent creature. The trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty, and the statues' movements don't trigger reactions.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters a square adjacent to a statue

Effect The trap makes a statue shortsword Strike against the triggering creature, activates its predictive impediment ability, and then rolls initiative.

The statues continually slide around to bump into creatures and block their passage through the room. The first time on its turn that a creature attempts to move within the room, it must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:36] save or treat the entire room as difficult terrain for 1 round. On a critical failure, the creature is also knocked prone.

details.description: The stink of bile suddenly grows as the myriad mouths carved into the walls begin to vomit forth a deluge of acidic slurry.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33] (master) to disrupt the trap's magical network of sensors and effectively "blind" it to targets to react to, @Check[religion|dc:38] (master) to recognize the carvings and recall one of the mantras needed to pass safely, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 32) to counteract the trap.
details.reset: The trap deactivates 1 round after no targets can be sensed, but resets immediately.
details.routine: On its turn, the trap Vomits as its action. As long as the Putrid Rise is active, the stairs in area D3 are difficult terrain.

description.value:
Trigger The trap's sensors notice a creature on the stairs north of the entrance to area D4

Effect The myriad mouths on the walls spew out a foul-smelling acidic slurry along the entire stairwell. The stairs in areas D3 become difficult terrain, and all creatures in area D3 must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:34|traits:damaging-effect] saving throw. A creature that is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued] by hunger takes a -2 circumstance penalty to this saving throw. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[acid]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[8d6[acid]] damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}, and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[16d6[acid]] damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 3}, is knocked prone, and then tumbles down the stairs. The fall ends once the creature reaches the next lowest landing (note that a creature falling at the lowest flight tumbles into the carrion pit at area D2, but can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] as detailed in that room). A creature that falls down the steps takes an additional @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic]) from the tumble, regardless of the total distance traveled.

details.description: An @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Invisible] rune imprinted on the door latch releases a powerful sonic discharge that automatically alerts the guards in area D6 that intruders are nearby.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:38] (master) to scratch out the rune without allowing it to activate, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 31) to counteract the rune

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door directly or with a tool without first touching the latch with a proper key (such as the one at area D4a)

Effect The trap deals @Damage[15d8[sonic]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:39|basic] save).

stealth.details: (master) to notice that the eerie manifestation is dangerous; noticing the ghostly image of the Arudora in question floating in the middle of the room has a DC of 0

details.description: A ghostly image of one of the Arudoras floats in the middle of the room, arms folded over their chest as if reclining in a coffin, yet they hover vertically and are shrouded by ribbons of green smoke.
details.disable:
@Check[religion|dc:41] to exorcise the haunt, or one of the following checks depending on the Arudora; four total successes are required to disable the haunt.

J3a (Jinnivere) @Check[nature|dc:38] to draw upon her love of horses

J3b (Dazerien) @Check[intimidation|dc:38] to frighten the cruel murderer

J3c (Ryhasphinea) @Check[deception|dc:38] to trick her into believing she's been forsaken by Urgathoa

J3d (Augsten) @Check[diplomacy|dc:38] to explain that abuse of the servants shows weakness of character

J3e (Alisendra) @Check[performance|dc:38] to give her one last entertainment

J3f (Raudltz) @Check[society|dc:38] to help him come to terms with the shame he feels for his infidelities

J3g (Irrokcis) @Check[occultism|dc:38] to use his own obscure theories against him to banish him

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates as soon as no living creatures are in the room, then immediately resets.
details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the haunt transforms into a physical manifestation of that Arudora's personality and lashes out, dealing @Damage[9d6[untyped]] damage to all creatures in the room and within 30 feet of any open doorway outside the room. A PC can attempt a DC 34 basic saving throw against the damage. The type of damage and saving throw to defend against it depend on the Arudora, as does the additional condition imparted on a critical failure. Conditions imparted by this haunt are curses that have unlimited durations.

J3a (Jinnivere) trampling horses (bludgeoning damage, Reflex save, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} on a critical failure)

J3b (Dazerien) complex torture devices (piercing damage, Reflex save, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} on a critical failure)

J3c (Ryhasphinea) skull-faced spirits (spirit damage, Will save, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} on a critical failure)

J3d (Augsten) barbed whip and scourge Strikes (slashing damage, Reflex save, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} on a critical failure)

J3e (Alisendra) shrieking spirits and discordant instruments (sonic damage, Fortitude save, stupefied 1 on a critical failure)

J3f (Raudltz) ghostly fangs that chew into your thoughts (mental damage, Will save, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} on a critical failure)

J3g (Irrokcis) decaying skeletal limbs (void damage, Fortitude save, drained 1 on a critical failure)

description.value:
Trigger A living creature ends their turn in the room

Effect The soul's eyes open, and it cries out in agony as its ghostly body twists and transforms into something monstrous. The triggering PC must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:34|options:inflicts:frightened,inflicts:fleeing] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for as long as they're frightened on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: Six eerie purple crystals react to the presence of life, pulsing with flashes of violet light that seem to match the heartbeats of living creatures in the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33] (master) or @Check[nature|dc:35] (master) to discern a hairline fracture that, when struck, shatters a crystal to dust
details.reset: The void garden deactivates 1 round after all living targets leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
details.routine: (6 actions) The void garden's crystals each use an action to fire a void ray, a beam of destructive dark purple energy, at a random living creature in the room or in visible reach through the open doorway (the crystals don't fire at living targets on the balcony in area D6c). A single creature can be targeted only once per round by a void ray. The void garden loses one action for each crystal that is disarmed or destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the circular portion of area D4, or a crystal takes damage from any source

Effect The void garden creates a pulse of void energy that fills the entire room and, if the doors to area D3 are open, extends 30 feet into the hallway beyond. All living creatures in this area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:34|traits:damaging-effect] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected

Success The creature takes @Damage[5d6[void]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[8d6[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[16d6[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2}.

Plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} (this drained value stacks with that caused by other void rays or the hazard's Negative Energy Pulse, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 5}).

details.description: The shaft walls vanish and are replaced by an endless field of stars in all direction, as if the viewer were suddenly lost in outer space.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:40] (master) to unweave the magic left in the wake of the Trinity Star's passage, @Check[thievery|dc:43] (expert) to etch temporary runes to siphon away the magic, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 33) to counteract the magic; three successes are required to disable the trap. Once the PCs are within the trap's overwhelming illusion, they can still attempt these checks to disable it, but the check DCs increase by 2.

publication.title: Pathfinder #219: Lord of the Trinity Star

details.reset: The trap resets automatically.
details.routine:
A Voyage Through the Infinite (1 action) Every round on its turn, the trap inflicts all creatures in the shaft with the perception that they have been hurtling at an unimaginable speed through the vastness of space. The stars are so distant that no indication of movement can be observed or felt beyond that overwhelming mental conviction. At the same time, the creatures feel as if something unimaginably powerful and hungry is chasing them... and gaining on them. Any PC who looks over their shoulder sees that the stars are indeed winking out behind them, as if an immense black object were growing closer or as if the stars themselves were vanishing. While the PC's body is somehow protected by the vastness of space, their minds are not—each PC must attempt a @Check[will|dc:40] save.

Critical Success As success below, but the creature also realizes they're reliving the memories of Runelord Xanderghul—like him, they're escaping some sort of horrific fate and are hurtling toward Golarion. The next time this creature achieves a critical success against this trap, they suddenly see Golarion loom large, and as they witness their "crash" into the Throne of the Troll King, they suddenly wake, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], in area C1 at the heart Xin-Cyrusian. If any other PCs are still trapped in the Falling Star's Memories, a PC in Xin-Cyrusian knows instinctively that they can call out to those trapped PCs—each PC in Xin-Cyrusian who does so reduces the save DC against A Voyage Through the Infinite for those still trapped by 5.

Success The creature realizes they're experiencing the path the Trinity Star took through the cosmos and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature's grip on reality fades as they experience what seems like years or even decades of hurtling adrift through a vast expanse. The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours or increases the value of their stupefied condition by 1 if they're already stupefied.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} (or increase the stupefied condition by 2).

Special If this trap would cause a creature's stupefied condition to exceed @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 5}, the creature instead "crashes" to the ground on the hilltop outside, about ten feet from the Throne of the Troll King. The creature takes @Damage[10d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save), and their stupefied condition now has an unlimited duration.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves (or falls) more than 60 feet down the shaft below the illusory throne

Effect All creatures in the shaft suddenly witness an endless night sky all around them, as if they were hurtling through outer space with no sign of any nearby planetary bodies. Each affected creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:40] save to resist being @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure) for 24 hours as the visual overwhelms them. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A mounted gun equipped with a magical targeting system and integrated ammo supply.
details.disable:
@Check[thievery|dc:35|name:Disconnect the Turret|traits:magical,trap,hazard] (master) to disconnect the turret from its ammo supply system, or @Check[arcana|dc:35|name:Neutralize the Magical Targeting System|traits:magical,trap,hazard] (master) to neutralize the magical targeting system.

Tan‑Takneh can also deactivate one or both auto‑turrets remotely with a special command word.

publication.title: Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star

details.routine:
(3 actions) With each action, the auto‑turret makes a longrifle Strike against a creature within 60 feet of Tan‑Takneh's campsite (other than Tan-Takneh). If there are no such creatures, the auto‑turret instead uses its action to sprout mechanical legs, totter 20 feet in the direction of its last target, and then plant itself into the ground again. Programmed to spread its fire around, the auto‑turret doesn't attack any creature it's already attacked in the round.

After making 15 Strikes, the auto‑turret depletes its ammunition and automatically deactivates.

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 60 feet of Tan-Takneh's campsite

Effect The turret attacks the triggering creature with a longrifle Strike.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (master) or @Check[perception|dc:38|traits:action:seek] (master) to spot the trap's control panel

details.description: Four large flaming chandeliers swing wildly around the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:36|name:Disable a Chandelier|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to disable a chandelier or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 33) to permanently drain the magic from a chandelier. Any amount of cold damage that overcomes a chandelier's cold resistance extinguishes its flames and removes its persistent fire damage and flame dart attack. @Check[thievery|dc:41|name:Disable Trap (Control Panel)|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to disable the entire trap from the hidden control panel in the southeast corner of the room.

publication.title: Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets 1 minute after there are no living creatures remaining in the room.
details.routine: (4 actions) For every chandelier disabled, the trap's actions are reduced by 1. For each of the trap's actions, a different chandelier attacks a random creature in the room. A chandelier uses its swinging chandelier attack if it can, but it can't make melee attacks against creatures in the cage, the closet, or the gap between the two double doors leading into the vault. Against a creature it can't reach with a melee attack, it uses a flame dart attack instead. The trap doesn't take multiple attack penalties.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the cage door, the closet door, or any wooden stand

Effect Both double doors leading out of the room shut and lock; while the trap is active, the key doesn't work to open these doors (although their locks can be picked normally). The chandeliers drop several feet on elongated chains, allowing them to swing around the room. The closest chandelier to the triggering creature makes a swinging chandelier Strike against it. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: The floating gem flares with inner radiance, sending out beams of light to captivate and drain the vitality from all who approach.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:35] (expert), @Check[occultism|dc:35] (expert), or @Check[religion|dc:35] (expert) to unravel the magical matrix within the gem, @Check[crafting|dc:40] or @Check[thievery|dc:40] check to unbalance the crystal's alignment, or a @Check[athletics|dc:43] check to damage one of the gem's facets and change its geometry; two successes are required to disable the hazard
details.routine: (3 actions) The captivating gem attempts uses all three of its actions to incapacitate creatures. It targets the closest creature with its Freezing Light ability, then a different creature with its facet flare Strike. If a creature has already been @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] by Freezing Light, it chooses a different target for that ability.

description.value:
The captivating gem shines intense light on a living creature, freezing it in place. The target must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] Save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} and takes @Damage[2d12[force]] damage.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] for 1 round and takes @Damage[4d12[force]] damage.

Critical Failure The target is paralyzed for 1 round and takes @Damage[8d12[force]] damage.

Trigger Any living creature approaches within 10 feet of the gem

Effect The captivating gem targets the triggering creature with a facet flare Strike and then rolls initiative.
The target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}

details.description: Four claw-tipped clockwork arms suspended from the ceiling pick up and deposit creatures into a nearby hatch.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43|name:Deactivate One Arm|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to jam and deactivate one of the arms or @Check[athletics|dc:46|name:Tear Arm Off Machine] (legendary) to tear an arm off the machine.

publication.title: Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale

details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action per arm destroyed or disabled. On its turn, the trap makes claw Strikes against creatures other than the Rumormonger or the Inkmaster. If an arm already has a creature grabbed, the trap spends 2 actions to use its Open Hatch ability.

description.value:
Trigger The Rumormonger or the Inkmaster enters the room

Effect The trap rolls initiative.

The metal hatch opens and the arms deposit a grabbed Medium or smaller creature into the hole. The creature becomes caught in the gears (see Caught in Gears) for 1 round before emerging in area K5. Reset Rewinding the gears for [[/gmr 1d4 #minutes]]{1d4 minutes} resets the trap.

details.description: An iron bridge's connectors give way to drop into the lake below.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Disable Quick Release Mechanism|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to disable one side of the bridge's quick release mechanisms

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature moves more than 20 feet onto the bridge

Effect The bridge chains detach from their connectors, dropping itself and anything on it into the lake 150 feet below. A creature that's adjacent to the ledges to the north or south can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:33] save to Grab the Edge, but creatures who can't reach the edge fall with the bridge into the toxic water below (unless one side of the bridge remains attached, in which case a creature can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] by clinging to the dangling bridge-requiring a successful @Check[athletics|dc:20|traits:action:climb] check to climb the hanging bridge to safety).

stealth.details: +20 (master) to notice cracks forming in the walls of the structure as Mogaru approaches

details.description: Mogaru brushes against a structure, severely compromising the building's stability and possibly causing it to collapse.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:43|name:Brace the Structure] (master), @Check[crafting|dc:43|name:Brace the Structure] (master), or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:43|name:Brace the Structure]{Engineering Lore} (master) to brace the structure to reduce the risk of collapse until the end of the creature's next turn. The DC of the flat check for this round (see Routine) is increased by 4 on a success, or by 8 on a critical success. Increasing the flat check DC to 21 or higher stabilizes the structure, ending this hazard.
details.reset: The building becomes susceptible to Shake Apart again [[/gmr 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds} after it is stabilized.
details.routine: (1 action) The GM rolls a @Check[flat|dc:9] to determine if the building collapses, increasing the DC as listed above. On a successful check, dust and debris fall within the building and 30 feet around it, providing @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed]{Concealment} and dealing @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] damage to [[/gmr 1d4 #targets]] randomly chosen targets (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic] save; on a critically failed save, the creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]). On a critical success, the effect is the same, but the debris deals @Damage[12d6[bludgeoning]] damage to [[/gmr 2d4 #targets]] randomly chosen targets instead (DC 35 basic Reflex save; on a critically failed Reflex save, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] by rubble until freed [[[/act force-open dc=38]], [[/act escape dc=35]]]).

description.value:
Trigger Mogaru applies any amount of force to the structure

Effect The building trembles. The floors of the building and the streets within 30 feet of the building become difficult terrain; creatures on this difficult terrain take a -2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls, AC, and skill checks. The hazard rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to notice cracks forming in the walls of the structure as Ebeshra approaches

details.description: Ebeshra brushes against a structure, possibly causing it to collapse.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:43|name:Brace Building (Athletics)|traits:complex,environmental,kaiju,hazard] (master), @Check[crafting|dc:43|name:Brace Building (Crafting)|traits:complex,environmental,kaiju,hazard] (master), or @Check[engineering|dc:43|name:Brace Building (Engineering)|traits:complex,environmental,kaiju,hazard]{Engineering Lore} (master) to brace the structure to reduce the risk of collapse until the end of the creature's next turn. The DC of the flat check for this round (see Routine) is increased by 4 on a success, or by 8 on a critical success. Increasing the flat check DC to 21 or higher stabilizes the structure, ending this hazard.

publication.title: Pathfinder Lost Omens Monsters of Myth

details.reset: The building becomes susceptible to Shake Apart again [[/gmr 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds} after it's stabilized.
details.routine: The GM rolls a @Check[flat|dc:9|showDC:owner] to determine if the building partially collapses, increasing the DC as listed above. On a successful check, dust and debris fall within the building and 30 feet around it, providing concealment and dealing @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] damage to [[/r 1d4 #Targets]] randomly chosen targets (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic|name:Falling Debris|traits:hazard,complex,environmental,kaiju] save; on a critical failure, the creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]). On a critical success, the effect is the same, but the debris deals @Damage[12d6[bludgeoning]] damage to [[/r 2d4 #Targets]] randomly chosen targets instead (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic|name:Falling Debris|traits:hazard,complex,environmental,kaiju] save; on a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] by rubble until freed [[[/act force-open dc=38]], [[/act escape dc=35]]]).

description.value:
Trigger Ebeshra applies any amount of force to the structure

Effect The building trembles. The floors of the building and the streets within 30 feet of the building become difficult terrain; creatures on this difficult terrain take a -2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls, AC, and skill checks. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: Sinister runes adorn the doorway, opening a portal to a realm of nightmares if anyone other than Dwandek opens the door.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Seal the Rift|traits:magical,trap,hazard] (master) to seal the rift using materials strongly grounded to the Material Plane, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th‑level, counteract DC 33) to counteract the portal.

publication.title: Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star

details.reset: The deathly dream trap resets automatically as long as it hasn't been disabled or destroyed. Each time it resets, the tiny pocket plane of nightmare it sends its victim into resets as well, so no two victims will ever share the same deathly dream.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than Dwandek opens the door

Effect The triggering creature is drawn into a pocket plane of pure nightmare (@Check[will|dc:38] save to resist). Each round at the start of their turn, the creature takes @Damage[4d8[mental]] damage as they are assaulted by otherworldly visions, and must succeed at a new Will save against the original DC to be ejected from the pocket plane and deposited in the center of area A5.

stealth.details: Performance +33 (master); creatures in the area automatically notice the eerily swaying shadows

details.description: A choir of ghostly shadows fill the benches in this room, chanting a haunting and unearthly melody.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:40|name:Exorcise the Spirits|traits:haunt,hazard] (master) to exorcise the spirits and break the power binding them into a single entity, or @Check[performance|dc:35|name:Counter the Choir|traits:haunt,hazard] (expert) to counter the choir and nullify the destructive elements of its song

publication.title: Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star

details.reset: The entropy choir resets automatically after 24 hours, even if it has been disabled or destroyed. It fades away forever once Dwandek and other members of the cult to which it sings its praises are destroyed.
details.routine:
(3 actions; auditory, divine, evocation, sonic) The choir continues to sing its grim dirge every round until it is disabled or destroyed. If there are any non‑slowed living creatures in area A3 when its turn starts, it first uses Call to Nothingness as a single action.

It uses its remaining actions (or all three actions if there are slowed living creatures in the room already) to focus soul‑shattering choruses onto individual targets.

The choir can target any living creature in area A3 with an action, but never more than once per round per creature. It prefers to attack slowed creatures over all others, but it targets non‑slowed creatures if they're the only viable targets. A targeted creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36|traits:auditory,divine,evocation,sonic] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes @Damage[5d6[sonic]] damage.

Failure The target takes @Damage[10d6[sonic]] damage.

Critical Failure The target takes @Damage[15d6[sonic]] damage and increases their @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained] condition by 1, to a maximum of drained 4.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature moves more than 10 feet into area A3

Effect The eerie call of the entropy choir saps the will of living creatures. A living creature who fails a @Check[will|dc:36] save becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} as long as they remain in area A3. The entropy choir then rolls initiative.

details.description: A vat of thick glass stands 10 feet high and is filled with green slime and fist-sized eyeballs.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:38|name:Acidify Slime|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) or @Check[crafting|dc:34|name:Acidify Slime] (expert) to acidify the slime inside the tank, destroying the eyeballs

publication.title: Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale

details.routine: (3 actions) Each round, the vat uses all three of its actions to shoot random eye rays at any living creatures (except Ixusoth) in range.

description.value:
Trigger Three or more living creatures remain within 20 feet of the trap for 1 round

Effect The trap rolls initiative.

attackEffects.custom: target is subjected to a crushing despair spell
target is subjected to a feeblemind spell (DC 36)
target is subjected to a spirit blast spell (DC 36)

description.value:
Trigger The tank reaches its Broken Threshold

Effect The glass shatters, spilling eyeballs onto the floor in a @Template[emanation|distance:15]{15-foot radius} and casting grease (DC 33) on the affected area. The eyeball tank is permanently destroyed and can't be reset.Reset The trap can be reset by adding 100 new eyeballs to the tank and allowing them to grow in the slime for 1 week.

details.description: Pressure plates in the floor cause crystal‑tipped spikes to rain down into the room and explode.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40] (master) to disable enough pressure plates so the trap won't trigger
details.reset: The trap's supply of spikes magically replenishes, and the trap resets after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger Each time a non-flying creature enters a square in this room, roll a secret @Check[flat|dc:6|traits:secret]. On a failure, the trap triggers as the character steps on a pressure plate—the trap automatically triggers if a creature steps on either square just west of the hallway leading to H12

Effect The trap makes a crystal spike Strike against all creatures in the room, then each creature in the room takes @Damage[6d10[piercing]] damage as the spikes explode (@Check[reflex|dc:36|basic]).

details.description: Four massive skulls of aurochs, enormous wild cattle, rise into the air, each trailing its ghostly body behind it.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:36] (master) to calm one of the four aurochs or @Check[religion|dc:40] (trained) to exorcise them
details.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area or after all four aurochs skulls are destroyed. After 1 hour, if at least one aurochs skull remains, the haunt reactivates.
details.routine: (4 actions) For each aurochs skull disabled or destroyed, the haunt has 1 fewer action. On each action, a different aurochs moves up to 60 feet and attempts a horn Strike against a different living creature. A creature critically hit by a horn Strike also takes 2d6 persistent bleed damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of an aurochs skull

Effect The skulls rise into the air, form ghostly bodies, and bellow in rage. Each creature within @Template[emanation|distance:60]{60 feet} of an aurochs skull must attempt a @Check[will|dc:36|options:area-effect,damaging-effect,inflicts:frightened] saving throw. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(1d12+6)[mental]|options:area-damage] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d12+12)[mental]|options:area-damage] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 4}.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: A section of ground thrusts up to form what looks like a mountain in which a sword has been impaled, and then the sword lashes out to stab at someone nearby. A successful @Check[religion|dc:15] check to Recall Knowledge is enough to note that the hazard looks very similar to Gorum's religious symbol; any character who is at least expert in Religion notes this automatically.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40] (master) to wipe away the faint runes scratched on the floor without activating a blade, or @Check[religion|dc:43] (master) to utter memorial prayers to Gorum to rob from the trap its violent energy
details.routine: (3 actions) If the Gorum's epitaph is adjacent to a creature, it uses its three actions to make greatsword Strikes on that creature (determining targets randomly if more than one creature is adjacent). Otherwise, it uses actions to Stride toward the closest visible target, then uses any remaining actions to Strike that target. Gray Master can flank with a Gorum's epitaph.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the square in which the trap is hidden

Effect The ground erupts upward in the shape of a mountain impaled by a +2 greater striking greater astral wounding greatsword. The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:36] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] until the start of their next turn (plus being knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] on a critical failure). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: One of the carvings on the mural suddenly animates and clambers out of the painting to become a real creature in the middle of the chamber.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:35] (to deface the mural in precisely the right way to render it inoperable), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 34) to counteract the mural
details.reset: 24 hours

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to open the door to area H7

Effect A living mukradi manifests in the 20-foot-square space indicated by the dotted outline on the map. Any other creatures in this area are knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] and pushed out of this square—those who succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:40] save can choose to be pushed north or south, while those who fail are pushed west, out of the room entirely down a 200-foot fall. These creatures can Grab the Edge with a successful @Check[reflex|dc:36] save. The mukradi then rolls initiative but vanishes completely after 1 minute.

stealth.details: (master) to notice runes marked on the floor.

details.description: Barely visible runes are etched into the stone floor in a 20-foot diameter circle.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:36|name:Approach Rune] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[occultism|dc:34|name:Erase the Rune] (expert) or @Check[thievery|dc:38|name:Erase the Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 34) to counteract the rune.
details.reset: The trap resets at the first high tide of each day.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the circle without uttering the passphrase, "Praise to the Water Lizard"

Effect The trap summons a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Mukradi]. The mukradi rolls initiative and remains for 1 minute, after which the spell ends and the mukradi disappears. The mukradi also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. The mukradi can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

details.description: The consumption of sentient, and even sapient creatures is widespread in Leng. Those who suffer such fates are as much the genesis of this nightmare as those who partake, and as such, this nightmare tends to manifest in places where meals were taken, from banquet halls to campsites.
details.disable: @Check[society|dc:43] (master) to fight these destructive urges by focusing your thoughts on traditions not so steeped in cruelty, or @Check[intimidation|dc:40] (legendary) to overwhelm the nightmare's influence with the strength of your own personality; three successes are required to disable the nightmare
details.reset: The nightmare deactivates as soon as a round begins with no creatures in its area, and resets in [[/gmr 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds}.
details.routine:
(1 action) The nightmare transitions from directly chewing on the creatures in the area to compelling them to feed for themselves. Each round on the nightmare's turn, all creatures within 30 feet of the nightmare's epicenter must attempt a @Check[will|dc:36] save with the following results.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The nightmare causes a momentary change to the creature's appearance, causing its jaws to take on a frightening, fanged appearance and filling the creature's mind with distracting, unsettling hunger that cause them to become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] until the start of the nightmare feast's next turn.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] until the start of the nightmare feast's next turn and gains a +30 bite (magical) that deals @Damage[(3d12+10)[slashing]] damage plus @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage on a hit. The creature favors this bite Strike when the confusion drives them to attack.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Quickened] and can use the extra action to make bite Strikes.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the remains of a previous feast

Effect Invisible attackers begin to bite and gnaw at the triggering creature and any other creatures within 30 feet. The creatures take @Damage[10d6[slashing]] damage from the biting (@Check[reflex|dc:36|basic] save), and then the nightmare rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to hear the echoes of a faraway object crashing into the ground

details.description: Four vengeful spirits grab interlopers and toss them off the edge of a nearby 120-foot-tall cliff.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:40|name:Forcefully Push Spirits Back] (trained) to push back so forcefully that the spirits fear being thrown off the cliff, or @Check[religion|dc:40|name:Seal the Spirits] (expert) to temporarily seal the spirits away
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 15 feet of the edge of the cliff.

Effect Each spirit attempts to throw a creature within 60 feet of the cliff's edge off the cliff by attempting a check with a +26 modifier against the target's Fortitude DC. If the haunt succeeds, the target is thrown over the edge of the cliff, where it then falls 120 feet to the ground. Until the haunt is defeated, each creature within 60 feet of the cliff's edge must attempt a @Check[will|dc:40] save each time they would spend an action or reaction to Arrest a Fall, Fly, Grab an Edge, or otherwise avoid falling; if they fail the save, the action is disrupted. The haunt automatically attempts to counteract spells that would slow a fall or mitigate the effects of falling, such as feather fall, with a counteract modifier of +32.

details.description: A seemingly innocent ramen cart is rigged to detonate if moved from its current location.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Disable the Detonation Trigger|traits:hazard,alchemical,mechanical,trap] (master) to disable the detonation trigger

publication.title: Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star

description.value:
Trigger The cart is moved, damaged, or physically searched

Effect The cart explodes in a @Template[burst|distance:40]{40-foot radius}, dealing @Damage[10d6[fire],10d6[piercing]]{10d6 fire damage and and 10d6 piercing damage} to all creatures within the area (@Check[reflex|dc:36|basic] save).

Creatures who fail their save against this effect are also covered in "noodles" of tar and other sticky alchemical substances that make the creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} until they spend at least an hour scrubbing the noodles off.

details.description: A roving sphere of distorted magic shimmers and floats through the air.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:38], @Check[nature|dc:38], @Check[occultism|dc:38], or @Check[religion|dc:38] (master) to cause the magic of the sphere to dissipate harmlessly, or @Check[thievery|dc:40] to cause the sphere to burst and implode safely, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 34)
details.reset: The sphere ceases moving when no creature remains to trigger Magic Drift. It then dissipates after an hour spent with no targets within 60 feet
details.routine: (2 actions) The sphere uses its first action to fly 30 feet toward the closest creature currently under the effects of a spell, or it uses both actions to fly 60 feet toward the closest creature if none are within 30 feet. The rogue sphere attempts to enter the target's space. If a rogue sphere shares a creature's space, it uses an action to Magic Drift the target. If the target has no magical effects or carries no magic items, the result of its Fortitude save to resist the force damage caused by Magic Drift is increased by one degree of success.

description.value:
Trigger A creature within 60 feet casts a spell or uses magical effect, or a creature within 60 feet is the target of an active spell that's 5th level or higher, carries a magic item of 10th level or higher, or is the target of an ongoing magical effect created by a creature who's 10th level or higher

Effect The sphere flies 30 feet toward the triggering creature. If the sphere would enter a creature's space, it stops moving in that creature's space, then attempts to counter the triggering magic effect (or alternately, the strongest magic effect on the creature), with a counteract modifier of [[/r 1d20+28 #Counteract]]{+28}. A counteracted magic item becomes a mundane item of its type for 10 minutes. If the target is a creature, or is a creature who's carrying the targeted item, the creature takes @Damage[10d6[force]] damage from this effect (@Check[fortitude|dc:36|basic] save). The rogue sphere then rolls initiative.

details.description: An oblong, puffball-like fungus shaped ominously like a bulging coffin in which Aravashnial's distorted face can be seen silently screaming.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:40] three times to exorcise Aravashnial's soul from a pod, or @Check[nature|dc:43] twice to pull the well-hidden filaments that connect the spore pod to the rest of the spires

publication.title: Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower

details.reset: A soul pod deactivates once no PCs are in the spires, then resets automatically.
details.routine:
(2 actions) The soul pod releases a soulrending shriek on its first action, then on its second action exhales a blast of spore-laden wind in a @Template[type:cone|distance:40]. Creatures in this area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save. This blast has the air and poison traits.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] by the wind.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[16d6[poison]] damage, is knocked prone, and is blown 10 feet backward directly away from the soul pod, possibly being blown off the edge of a platform or stairwell.

description.value:
Trigger A round begins after all demons in the spires have been slain

Effect Aravashnial's distorted face unleashes an overwhelming shriek of agony that threatens to completely warp the thoughts of all intruders. All creatures within 20 feet of a soul pod must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:36] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure). A confused creature can attempt a new save at the end of each of their turns to end the confusion early. The soul pods then roll initiative.

details.description: Transparent runes around the window's edge trigger a silent alarm shortly after being opened by an unauthorized person.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40] (master) to carefully scuff the faint runes around the window's edge

description.value:
Trigger The window is broken or is unlocked and opened without using one of the shop keys

Effect Each person carrying a shop key within 500 feet receives a mental image of the area 10 feet around the window for 1 minute.

details.description: A ceiling-mounted launcher rains resinous needles onto intruders, after which the needles melt into acid.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:36|name:Disable Firing Mechanism|traits:mechanical,trap,complex] to disable some firing mechanisms, making three adjacent squares in the trapped area safe to enter.
details.routine: (3 actions) The acidic needle launcher fires a needle at a random target in the area indicated on the map.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the area indicated on the map

Effect The trap shoots an acidic needle at a random target in the area indicated on the map, then rolls initiative.

details.description: A roiling sphere of chromatic energy floats above a pedestal marked with arcane symbols.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:42] (legendary) or @Check[crafting|dc:45] (master) to remove an immunity the prison currently has to one of the following damage types: acid, cold, electricity, or fire (this effect lasts until the prison uses its Energy Immunity reaction to regain that immunity); @Check[arcana|dc:42] (master) to fortify the pedestal's containment runes, preventing the prison from using its Chromatic Eruption action for 1 round and causing it to suffer feedback that inflicts @Damage[20[untyped]] damage (this damage bypasses the prison's hardness and immunities); @Check[thievery|dc:42] (master) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 34) to suppress the pedestal's containment runes for 1 round, preventing the prison from using its Self-Repair action and causing it to suffer feedback that inflicts @Damage[20[untyped]] damage (this damage bypasses the prison's hardness and immunities). Unlike most hazards, Aethusa's prison can't be disabled, but it can be destroyed by reducing it to 0 Hit Points.
details.reset: The trap deactivates and then resets automatically when it repairs to its maximum Hit Points.
details.routine: (2 actions) The prison uses its first action to Self-Repair. If the hazard has less than its maximum Hit Points, it then uses its Chromatic Eruption action.

description.value:
Trigger The prison takes any damage

Effect The prison uses its Chromatic Eruption action as part of this reaction, then gains immunity to the triggering damage type until the hazard resets. The hazard then rolls initiative.
The prison regains @Damage[30[healing]] Hit Points. Then, if the prison is at maximum Hit Points, the hazard deactivates and immediately resets.

Requirements The prison has less than its maximum Hit Points

Effect Tendrils of uncontained magical energy lash at creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20], dealing @Damage[10d6[untyped]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:37|basic] save) of a randomly determined type. Roll [[/gmr 1d4]]: 1–acid, 2–cold, 3–electricity, 4–fire.

Trigger The prison takes acid, cold, electricity, or fire damage

Effect The prison takes the damage but then gains immunity to the triggering damage type until the hazard resets.

stealth.details: (master) to sense something unusual lurking in the area's echoes

details.description: A pack of cannibalistic spirits swarm through the area, devouring living creatures.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:40|name:Drive off the spirits] (master) to drive the spirits off with a ferocious display or @Check[occultism|dc:42|name:Exorcise the spirits] (expert) to exorcise the spirits
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet

Effect The spirits bite and chew, dealing @Damage[(6d12+35)[piercing]] damage to each creature within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet}. Affected creatures must each attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:41] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage, @Damage[2d8[bleed]], and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, @Damage[4d8[bleed]], and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2}.

details.description: Metal supports twist and shear off the bridge, causing stretches of the structure to collapse.
details.disable: @Check[engineering-lore|dc:40] to identify and reinforce weak points, @Check[crafting|dc:43] (master) to construct secondary supports, or @Check[athletics|dc:45] (legendary) to reinforce the bridge with found materials; three successes are required to halt the collapse
details.routine: (1 action) The collapse radiates out from the damaged section of the bridge. All squares adjacent to a collapsed section also fall into the water.

description.value:
Trigger A Medium or larger creature steps onto the bridge's weak point

Effect The bridge collapses in the creature's square, then rolls initiative. The DC to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] on the bridge is 38. Once the bridge begins to collapse, the river's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Swim] DC increases to 36 as the water churns with the falling debris. A creature that starts its turn swimming within 15 feet of the bridge takes @Damage[10d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:41|basic] save).

details.description: A noisome mass of fungus at the center of the grove raises fungal guardians from the ground to defend itself.
details.disable: @Check[survival|dc:42] (master) six times to carefully remove all the tangled fungal clots that absorb Tanglebriar's ambient energies for nourishment or @Check[religion|dc:45] (legendary) on the grove three times by a character who has holy sanctification and who uses prayer to exorcise the unholy energies that sustain the corrupt grove

publication.title: Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower

details.reset: The corrupt grove resets 1 minute after no enemies are within the grove itself or attempting to damage the grove from beyond the crater's perimeter.
details.routine: (1 action; poison, primal) The corrupt grove pulses and exudes a blast of toxic spores in a @Template[type:cone|distance:60] that targets the area with the densest concentration of intruders. Creatures in this area take @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:37|basic|traits:poison,primal] save).

description.value:
Trigger A creature damages the corrupt grove, attempts but fails to disable it, or ends their turn within 10 feet of the central mass

Effect The corrupt grove suddenly contracts and then exudes an unsettling, trumpeting howl and rolls initiative.

stealth.details: @Check[perception|dc:42|traits:action:seek] (master) to spot the triggering rod beneath the black sand.

details.description: A hidden stone rod causes two immense stone doors to fall forward from their gate, crushing anything beneath them. The doors also fall forward if touched.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:42|name:Secure Door in Place|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to secure the doors in place without triggering the trap

publication.title: Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands

description.value:
Trigger The stone rod is moved or either door is touched

Effect The two falling doors deal @Damage[(6d12+35)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:37|basic] save) to all creatures within 15 feet of the gate. On a failure, a creature is also pinned beneath the door and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until the door is moved (@Check[athletics|dc:34|traits:action:force-open]) or destroyed; each door has the same AC, Fortitude save, Hardness, and Hit Points as the trap itself. On a critical failure, the creature is trapped in such a way that it can't attempt to free itself. Moving or destroying one door doesn't help free anyone trapped by the other door.

details.description: An eerie orchestra compels all who hear it to dance until they collapse from exhaustion.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:42|name:Frighten the Orchestra|traits:action:demoralize] (expert) three times to frighten dancers and spectral musicians alike away from participating in the deadly performance, @Check[performance|dc:40|name:Disrupt Compulsion] (master) twice to produce a tune discordant enough to disrupt the compulsion, or @Check[religion|dc:42|name:Banish Spirits] (master) three times to banish the spirits with prayers
details.reset: The eerie orchestra spends an hour retuning its phantasmal instruments, after which it is ready to begin its routine again.
details.routine:
(1 action; auditory, incapacitation, mental, occult) The orchestra performs a raucous tune, compelling all creatures that can hear it to spend actions dancing. Each round, creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:37] save; the results of this save modify the number of actions that the creature must spend dancing each round. If this would cause the creature to spend more actions dancing than it can use on its turn, the creature takes @Damage[10d6] damage (or double that on a critical failure) from moving faster than its body can manage.

Critical Success The creature decreases the number of actions it must spend dancing by 1.

Success No effect.

Failure The creature increases the actions it must spend dancing by 1.

Critical Failure The creature increases the actions it must spend dancing by 2.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 30 feet of the orchestra

Effect The orchestra compels all creatures that can hear it to begin dancing. Each creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:41] save, with the following effects. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] and cannot use reactions. Additionally, it must spend 1 of its actions each round dancing. Dancing is a move action that allows the creature to Stride up to half its Speed.

Failure As success, except the creature must spend 2 of its actions each round dancing.

Critical Failure As failure, except the creature must spend 3 of its actions each round dancing.

details.description: The disturbing sensation of being watched, of having attracted the unwanted attention of something unimaginably powerful, or knowing the eerie conviction that unseen stars in the sky above might be looking down upon this spot grows increasingly overwhelming.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:45] (master) to inscribe eldritch warding sigils on the stones of the tower (this is an activity that takes 1 hour to perform) or defeat the Thresholder influence in the chambers below.

description.value:
Trigger A creature ends its turn within the ruins of the tower or the chambers below while not currently being affected by the Dread Aura

Effect The creature feels as if its presence in the area has attracted the attention of something powerful and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:37] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is immune to the Dread Aura forever.

Success The creature is unaffected and is immune to the Dread Aura for 24 hours.

Failure The creature becomes unsettled and suffers a -2 penalty to all saves against fear effects as long as they remain within the hex containing Candlemere.

Critical Failure As failure, but each time the creature becomes frightened or flees, the fear is physically enervating, and the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} as long as they remain frightened or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing].

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Unsettling Attention]

stealth.details: (master) to hear the quiet murmurs of angry spirits

details.description: Malevolent spirits led by a ghostly image of Ludika rise and begin a deadly brawl. These spirits attempt to overwhelm living creatures, forcing them to join the battle.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:35|name:Exorcise|traits:haunt,complex] (master) three times to exorcise the spirits or @Check[diplomacy|dc:39|name:Talk Down|traits:haunt,complex] (expert) three times to talk them down.

publication.title: Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City

details.reset: After 24 hours, the haunt returns. The spirits are permanently put to rest only if a lawfully appointed judge from Saggorak (or Kovlar) comes into the prison, takes damage from the haunt, and survives the attack.
details.routine: (1 action) As 1 action, the haunt causes a barrage of ghostly fists to lash from the walls, floors, and ceiling. The haunt attempts a spectral fist Strike against each creature in area H10.

description.value:
Trigger At least three creatures enter area H10.

Effect The haunt takes control of all creatures in the prison, affecting them with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Confusion] (8th level, @Check[will|dc:37] save).
