On hit the creature must attempt a new @Check[will|dc:37|name:Spectral Fist Confusion] save against @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Confusion] if it's not already @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused].

details.description: The murky waters that fill the chamber grant false visions of the past.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:45] (master) to release alchemical reagents to neutralize the ink in the water, @Check[nature|dc:42] (legendary) to manipulate the primal magic in the region and negate the ink's effects, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 34) to counteract the magical ink in the water; four success are required to disable it (spells that purify or manipulate water, such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cleanse Cuisine] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Control Water], can affect this hazard as if the caster were using an equal rank dispel magic)
details.reset: The hazard remains disabled until Baolen refills the area with ink (typically once every 4 hours).
details.routine:
(2 actions) On the trap's first action, it attempts a water jet ranged Strike on all creatures outside of the water in this room or on the stairs leading up to area A5. On its second action, the primal magic infusing the waters attempts to seep into the minds of those within it, forcing each creature in the water to attempt a @Check[will|dc:39|options:area-effect,damaging-effect,inflicts:stupefied] save against the following effects.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[mental]|options:area-damage] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[8d6[mental]|options:area-damage] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 24 hours. While the creature is stupefied, it takes an additional –2 status penalty to checks made to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Recall Knowledge].

Critical Failure As failure, but @Damage[16d6[mental]|options:area-damage] damage, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 3}, and the stupefied condition has an unlimited duration.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves into the ink-fouled water

Effect The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:37|options:inflicts:stupefied] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 24 hours on a critical failure). The hazard then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: +23 (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:38|traits:action:seek] (master) to notice the pattern of the orb's trajectory

details.description: A floating skull of fire swoops around the arena, rotating as it weaves through the ring and releasing huge gouts of flame in random directions.
details.disable: three @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Disable an Elemental Core|traits:action:disable-a-device,hazard,magical,trap,fire] (legendary) checks or castings of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 40), one each to pull out or disable the elemental cores hidden in the floating skull's right eye, left eye, and mouth
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap loses 1 action per turn per successful check to disable. The floating flamethrower uses its first action to release a gout of flame in a @Template[cone|distance:15] that deals @Damage[(4d6+4)[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save), its second action to fly in a U-shape in a random direction, and its third action to release another gout of flame.

description.value:
Trigger Hao Jin signals the start of the bout

Effect The trap roars with fire and rolls initiative.

stealth.details: +23 (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:38|traits:action:seek] (master) to notice the four sensors

details.description: Four remote sensors at each corner of the arena cause bursts of freezing magic to erupt from random tiles on the arena and stop creatures in their tracks.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Disable One Sensor|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (6th rank; counteract DC 38) to disable one of the sensors
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action per disabled sensor. On each action, the trap causes a random 10-foot-by-10-foot space in the arena to suddenly freeze. Each creature that begins its turn in a frozen space takes @Damage[(2d6+2)[cold]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save). A creature that critically fails its save also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round; if it was already slowed, the creature instead becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] for 1 round. Any amount of fire damage dealt to a slowed or immobilized creature removes the condition. Frozen tiles become unfrozen at the beginning of the trap's next turn.

description.value:
Trigger Hao Jin signals the beginning of the match.

Effect The trap rolls initiative.

details.description: The murdered spirits of the slain priests manifest as churning, glowing spheres of floating, blood- red wine that drift and slither through the air. Within each can be glimpsed a silently screaming face.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:42] check to promise the restless spirits that justice will be served and the cause of their pain will be punished, or @Check[religion|dc:36] to invoke Cayden Cailean to calm the haunt.
details.reset: 1 hour
details.routine:
(3 actions) Up to four different creatures in area A12 are each targeted by one of the floating, churning spheres of wine drifting through the air; it hurtles toward them and douses them with wine that turns to life- draining blood as it strikes unerringly. The haunt cannot target the same creature more than once each round. After the haunt is reduced to its broken threshold of 53 HP, it can target no more than two different creatures. A creature targeted by the lights of the lost must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:37|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} from drunkenness.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[8d6[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

Critical Failure The takes @Damage[16d6[void]] damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 3}, and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1}. As long as the creature is doomed from this trap, they cannot reduce their sickened condition below 1, and they take a -1 circumstance penalty on all further saving throws made against fear effects as lingering traces of the terror the priests felt loom in their memories.

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens any of the doors into a priest's cell

Effect Waves of fear wash through area A12 as all creatures in the room realize that, one by one, their loved ones are being murdered and that they will be the last to be slain. Every creature in area A12 must attempt a @Check[will|dc:37] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3}.

details.description: A cloud of invisible sensors in a 10-foot-radius surrounds an invisible floor rune.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:40] to approach a rune without triggering it followed by @Check[thievery|dc:42] (master) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank, counteract DC 34) to counteract the rune.
details.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors

Effect The rune causes a chain reaction throughout the room, causing all remaining active summoning runes in area D2 to summon a Mask of Gray Master. The masks roll initiative and remain for 12 rounds, after which the effect ends and any remaining Masks disappear. A Mask also disappears if someone disables its linked trap before the duration expires. The Mask can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

stealth.details: (master) to notice the reflective field is dangerous; noticing the field has a DC of 0

details.description: A reflective field of energy fills a large archway in a gatehouse, in which viewers regard themselves dressed in fine clothes, bowing down before Runelord Xanderghul.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:37] (master) to disrupt one of the six anchor points that link the paradigm cube to the mirror, @Check[arcana|dc:34] (legendary) to use knowledge of magic that opposes pride to unravel the mirror's power, @Check[intimidation|dc:37] (master) to use one's force of personality to stand against the arrogance amplified by the mirror, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th-rank; counteract DC 36) to counteract one of the six faces of the paradigm cube; six successes are required to disable the Mirror of Glory

publication.title: Pathfinder #219: Lord of the Trinity Star

details.reset: The trap deactivates, then resets instantly.
details.routine:
(6 actions) On its turn, the Mirror of Glory targets random PCs in the area with distortions of their reality, spending an action to target one PC. If it has actions remaining after targeting the PCs, it spends any remaining actions repairing damage done to itself at the rate of 25 HP restored per action spent. If the mirror hasn't been damaged, then these unused actions are lost. Each time the Mirror of Glory targets a PC with a distortion, roll 1d6 and consult the entries below to determine what the PC experiences and any subsequent effects. The trap loses one action for each successful check made to disable it, but this doesn't reduce the number of distortions it's capable of creating. When a PC experiences a distortion, they take @Damage[10d6[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:37|basic] save), but on a failed saving throw, the PC suffers that distortion's additional effect.

1—Beauty: The PC regards Xanderghul as the most attractive figure they've ever seen. Additional effect: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

2—Cruelty: The PC sees Xanderghul as a demonic figure capable of unimaginable cruelty, who lashes out at the PC with dozens of blades and poisoned needles meant to carve away their strength. Additional effect: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} for 24 hours.

3—Disappointment: To the PC, Xanderghul suddenly seems to be an intimidating parental figure, commanding officer, or other person with authority whose disapproval induces tremors of nervousness. Additional effect: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 24 hours.

4—Jealousy: The PC watches as a glorious plume of peacock tail feathers spreads behind Xanderghul, filling them with jealousy and potentially driving them to lash out unpredictably against their allies in hopes of becoming the only one worthy of Xanderghul's attention. Additional effect: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round. 5—Despair: The PC feels their own flesh rotting away as they realize the Xanderghul who stands before them exists outside of the laws of time. Additional effect: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

6—Worship: The PC is filled with the conviction that Xanderghul is the one true god and feels the urge to pray to him for absolution. Additional effect: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3}.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the mirror or Seeks the reflection while being adjacent to the mirror

Effect The image of Xanderghul that appears behind the reflected PCs spreads his arms wide in glory, transforming the world presented in the reflection. Each PC reflected in the mirror (regardless of whether they're looking at the reflection or not) must attempt a @Check[will|dc:37] save to avoid being compelled to drop to their knees before Xanderghul's glory—on a failure, the PC becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], and the feelings of inferiority cause them to become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Horrific visions of a lumbering monstrosity whose face is nothing but a fanged mouth lurch out of the violet mist to attempt to eat the viewer alive.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:42|name:Focus the Portal's Energy|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to focus the portal's energies back in on itself to close it off, or @Check[occultism|dc:36|name:Invoke the Proper Chant] (master) to invoke the proper chant and make the proper hand gestures to untether the portal from the Dreamlands, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 34) to counteract the portal for 1 minute and prevent Orgesh's influence from reaching through during that time
details.reset: The nightmare portal resets 1 minute after it no longer detects targets.
details.routine: (2 actions) A vision of an immense lumbering figure with nothing but a circular mouth for a face lurches out of the portal. For each action, the nightmare portal casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantasmal Killer] (heightened to 8th level, @Check[will|dc:38|traits:death,emotion,fear,mental]), targeting a different creature both times. It can select targets in area E11, the secret alcove to the northeast, or up to 10 feet east from the door (although a closed door blocks it), choosing the closest two targets each round. The nightmare portal loses 1 action if it's broken and can thus only cast phantasmal killer once per round.

description.value:
Trigger At the start of a round after a creature who can dream and isn't immune to sleep effects enters the room, the portal starts to glow with purple mist. At this point, the portal triggers as soon as such a creature looks into it. A character who just passes by and doesn't specifically avert their gaze must attempt a @Check[flat|dc:11] to not accidently glance into the portal, while a character who Searches this room automatically looks into it unless they specify otherwise.

Effect The nightmare portal casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantasmal Calamity] (heightened to 8th level, @Check[will|dc:37|traits:mental]), making reality appear to crumble away to reveal a vast cavern filled with bones through which immense wormlike creatures burrow as they endlessly eat away at the world. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Sudden dark clouds gather in the sky above, and moments later, blasts of lightning and swirling tornadoes begin to rip the landscape apart.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:42] (master) to note the magical nature of the storm and to perform a quick ritual and prayer to the fey spirits of the region to cause the tempest to disperse, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank, counteract DC 36) to counteract the tempest immediately.
details.routine:
(4 actions) If the PCs successfully sought shelter with a Survival check, the storm takes no actions if the GM fails a @Check[flat|dc:16]. Otherwise, the storm takes four of the following actions, each determined by rolling a [[/gmr 1d4]], during the round. On each successive round, the number of actions the storm takes is reduced by 1; when the storm is reduced to 0 actions in this way, the hazard ends.

  • 1 Thunderclap A blast of sound rips through the region. Unsheltered armies must attempt a DC 30 Morale check or increase their shaken value by 1. Unsheltered PCs take @Damage[10d6[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:38|basic]).
  • 2 Lightning The tempest takes its Raging Storm reaction as a single action.
  • 3 Tornado A tornado funnel extends down from the storm to unerringly strike at a PC, who must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:38] save.

Critical Success The PC escapes unscathed.

Success The PC is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] and takes @Damage[8d6[bludgeoning]] damage.

Failure The PC is hurled through the air, taking @Damage[8d6[bludgeoning]] damage and then falling 1d6×10 feet for appropriate falling damage, landing prone.

Critical Failure As failure, but @Damage[8d6[bludgeoning]] damage and a fall of [[/r 10*(2d8) #Feet Fallen]]{2d8 x 10 feet}.

  • 4 Renewal Blasts of wind tear through the area but do no real damage. Attempt a @Check[flat|dc:11]; on a success, the number of actions the storm takes next round is not reduced as usual.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs fail an attempt to Disable the tempest, or 10 minutes pass without any attempt to leave the area (whichever comes first)

Effect Bolts of lightning lance down from above. If the PCs managed to succeed at a Survival check to prepare for the storm, their armies are protected and only one PC (determined randomly) is targeted by this bolt, otherwise all PCs and armies are targeted by bolts. Against PCs, the bolt inflicts @Damage[10d6[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:38|basic]). Against armies, the bolt inflicts 1 point of damage (DC 30 Mobility check negates). Then, whether or not the PCs managed to take shelter, roll initiative for Nyrissa's Tempest.

stealth.details: (master) to realize the water within the dome poses a supernatural threat

details.description: Shifting illusions disorient, entrap, and agonize those who attempt to pass through the shadowy maze.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:42] (master) to decouple faint strands of shadow essence from the magical matrix that holds the maze together, @Check[arcana|dc:45] or @Check[occultism|dc:45] to shunt the shadowy energy back into the Shadow Plane and render the maze inert, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 35) to counteract the shadow maze
details.reset: If disarmed, the trap resets in 1 hour as long as the two crystals in area C5 still function.
details.routine:
(1 action; illusion, incapacitation, mental) The shadow maze shifts and adjusts its appearance, affecting all creatures currently located within its area. Any creatures in the area at this time must attempt a @Check[will|dc:37] save.

Critical Success The creature sees through the illusions and can act normally.

Success The creature treats all squares within the trap's area as difficult terrain, and the trap fills the creature's mind with sensations and false memories that it has been wandering these alleys for nearly an hour. The creature takes @Damage[5d6[mental]] damage from the perceived strain of navigating the alien flooded alleys.

Failure As success, but the creature believes it has been wandering the alleys for several hours. It becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2} for 1 round and takes @Damage[10d6[mental]] damage from the perceived strain.

Critical Failure The creature believes it has been hopelessly lost in the alleys of the shadow maze for days, and it is incapacitated for 1 round and takes @Damage[20d6[mental]] damage from the perceived strain.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the 20-foot-wide interior of the shadow maze, either from area C2 or C4, or through a hole smashed in the dome

Effect Reality appears to shift around the creature, who perceives a sudden transformation in the surrounding terrain into a vast maze of underwater alleys and crumbling ruins. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:37] save, and the shadow maze then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature sees through the illusions and can move freely through squares within the shadow maze's effect. It's temporarily immune to the effects of the trap's routine for 1 minute.

Success The creature understands that what it's experiencing is an illusion, but it isn't immune to the effects of the trap's routine.

Failure The creature believes it has stepped into a vast maze. To those observing the creature outside of the shadow maze, the creature remains motionless. The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2} for 1 round, during which time all of its speeds are reduced to 5 feet.

Critical Failure The creature not only believes it has entered a vast maze, but is overwhelmed with the sensation of being hopelessly lost. The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until the end of its next turn.

details.description: A spiral shape suggesting the curling religious symbol of Pharasma is carved into the stone floor.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:37] (master) or @Check[religion|dc:40] (master) to unmake the Spiral Seal through occult ritual or prayer performed as a 10 minute activity with the Concentrate trait, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank, counteract DC 40) to counteract the Spiral Seal.

description.value:
Trigger A creature fails an attempt to disable the Spiral Seal or inflicts damage to it without successfully breaking it

Effect The magic stored within the Spiral Seal blasts outward in a concussive wave of power, surging in a spiraling vortex. All creatures within 60 feet of the Spiral Seal must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:41] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Failure The creature is knocked prone and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours.

Critical Failure The creature is knocked prone, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 3} for 24 hours, and increases their @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed] value by 1.

Frequency once per round

Trigger The Spiral Seal is damaged but is above its Broken Threshold

Effect The Spiral Seal repairs itself, regaining @Damage[30[healing]] Hit Points.

details.description: Three blue comets orbiting the room turn red and attack when intruders enter.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:42] (master) to command each comet to disperse with Pharasmin prayers, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 42) to counteract each comet
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour.
details.routine: (3 action) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each comet disabled, counteracted, or destroyed. Each comet uses 1 action to attack the nearest intruder with a comet Strike.

description.value:
Trigger An intruder enters the room

Effect The comets change from blue to red in color. The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (expert) to hear the echoed shouts of panicked xulgaths

details.description: The shadows of four xulgaths endlessly relive their deaths at the hands of the wendigos and their clutchmates.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:42|name:Calm a Spirit|traits:haunt] (master) or @Check[occultism|dc:42|name:Calm a Spirit|traits:haunt] (master) to calm each of the four spirits. On a character's first successful check, the character also learns that xulgath funerary rites typically involve cremation, which hints that fire is a viable way to put them to rest.
details.reset: The haunt is deactivated and resets 1 minute after all non-wendigo creatures leave the area.
details.routine: (4 actions) Each active spirit chooses a living non-wendigo creature within 120 feet and attempts to devour it, targeting different creatures if possible. Each calmed spirit reduces the hazard's actions by 1

description.value:
Trigger A living creature other than a wendigo gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened] condition while within 100 feet of the way station

Effect The haunt rolls for initiative.

hp.details: to destroy a tub and prevent it from making any further attacks; Door Seal Hardness 18; Door Seal HP 72 (BT 36) to destroy the wax on a sealed door and allow anyone to escape through the door

details.description: Four huge tubs containing congealed wax, enchanted to melt and envelop unwary intruders, each sit atop a cold fire.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43|name:Drain Wax from a Tub|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to drain the wax from a tub or @Check[athletics|dc:45|name:Push Through Wax|traits:action:force-open] (master) to push through the wax blocking a sealed door and escape (other creatures must push their own way through, as the wax reseals)

publication.title: Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 hour. At that time, the wax in the tubs cools and congeals, and any wax elsewhere in the room magically goes back into the tubs.
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn per drained or destroyed tub. On each action, a different tub spews hot wax at a random creature in the room, dealing @Damage[3d12[fire]|options:area-damage] damage to the target and all adjacent creatures (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic|options:area-effect] save). On a failure or critical failure, the creature is also encased in hot wax. A creature that starts its turn encased in wax takes @Damage[8d12[fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until it [[/act escape dc=35]]{Escapes} the hardening wax. Each turn it remains encased, the damage dealt by the hot wax decreases by 2d12 but the DC to Escape increases by 2 (minimum 0 damage, maximum DC 43). A creature that can't get free from the wax might suffocate.

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within 5 feet of a tub

Effect The fires beneath the vats blaze to life, magically melting the wax in the tubs. The tubs emit gouts of wax over the room's exits, sealing them shut. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: The room floods with harmful effects by pressing a magic sigil on a console. Each sigil causes different results. There are six sigils on the console.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:40] (master) to disenchant each sigil (can be done from either side of the reception desk), @Check[thievery|dc:43] (master) twice to disable the console (can only be done while behind the reception desk), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 40) to counteract each sigil; prevent Noph from pressing the buttons by gaining access to the reception desk or other methods
details.routine:
(2 actions) Noph frantically pecks and stomps on the console, using each action to press a random sigil. Roll [[/gmr 1d6 #Pressed Sigil]] to determine which sigil Noph presses. Psychopomps are immune to the effects of these sigils.

1 The area is flooded with holy light, dealing @Damage[(3d12+19)[vitality]] damage to each creature in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:43|basic]).

2 The area is flooded with unholy darkness, dealing @Damage[(3d12+19)[void]] damage to each creature in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:43|basic]).

3 The area is flooded with blue flames, dealing @Damage[(3d10+19)[fire]] damage to each creature in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:43|basic]). On a critical failure, a creature additionally takes @Damage[1d10[persistent,fire]] damage.

4 A comet streaks through the area, attacking the nearest creature to Noph with a comet Strike.

5 Force tethers sprout from the floor, grasping at intruders. Each creature in the area is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] for 1 round unless they succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:43] save.

6 Ghostly blue mist surrounds each psychopomp in the area, healing them @Damage[(3d8+19)[healing]]{3d8+19} HP and granting them @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed]{Concealment} for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger Noph or another creature arms the trap by pressing a sigil on the console

Effect A barrier of force forms from floor to ceiling surrounding the reception desk. The DC to counteract this barrier with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] is 43. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: The door slams shut, and poison gas fills the room.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:36|name:Exorcise the Spirits|traits:mechanical,trap] (master) or @Check[religion|dc:36|name:Exorcise the Spirits|traits:mechanical,trap] (master) to exorcise the spirits
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 day.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature other than an ooze spends at least 3 rounds in the room

Effect The trap activates and fills the area with caustic nightmare vapor, exposing all creatures within.

Oozes are immune to this poison

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:38]

Onset 1 round

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[4d6[acid]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[6d6[acid]] damage, confused, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[8d6[acid]] damage, confused, off-guard, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} (1 round)

details.description: Sheets of fire and vitality energy blast through the room in waves that obliterate the undead and scorch the living.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:41] (master) to bleed away the trap's energy, or @Check[religion|dc:39] (expert) to recite the appropriate prayers to Urgathoa to stop the trap (Pesabnet knows these prayers and automatically succeeds at checks to recite them). Disabling the trap requires three successful checks, in any combination of Thievery and Religion checks.
details.reset: The sacred fire disappears 1 minute after no creatures are in the room, and the trap immediately resets
details.routine:
(1 action) A wall of sacred fire washes back and forth throughout the room, bathing every inch of the space in holy power and dealing @Damage[3d12[fire],3d12[vitality]]{3d12 fire damage 3d12 vitality damage}. Each creature in the trap's area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:39] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes double damage.

Critical Failure If the creature has void healing, it is instantly destroyed. Otherwise, it takes double damage and @Damage[1d12[persistent,fire]] damage.

description.value:
Trigger A creature tries to open the door to area C6

Effect Magical fire and vitality energy flood the room with a harmonious roar. All creatures in the room take @Damage[3d12[fire],3d12[vitality]]{3d12 fire damage and 3d12 vitality damage} and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:39] save with the results below. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and @Damage[1d12[persistent,fire]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, @Damage[1d12[persistent,fire]] damage, and @Damage[1d12[persistent,vitality]] damage.

details.description: A loud aria in a soaring tenor issues from the door, sending soundwaves outward.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:38] (legendary) to cancel out the aria's soundwaves with another, @Check[thievery|dc:43] (master) to pick apart the arcane energies, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank, counteract DC 36) to counteract the trap

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the warehouse's double door without uttering the password

Effect All creatures within area C2 take @Damage[(6d12+37)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:38|basic] save); a creature that critically fails is permanently @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened].

hp.details: per square (6 squares must be destroyed to disable the haunt)

details.description: A haunted mural fascinates characters and swiftly drains their vitality.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:38|name:Calm the Restless Energies|traits:haunt,magical] (master) or @Check[religion|dc:38|name:Calm the Restless Energies|traits:haunt,magical] (master) to calm the restless energies and suppress the haunt for 1 hour; a critical success deactivates the haunt permanently.
details.reset: The haunt continues its routine until there are no targets within 30 feet of the room. It then resets over the course of 1 minute and is able to activate again.
details.routine: (2 actions) The haunt lures creatures into area A2 using Captivate. Any actions it hasn't used to Captivate are used to drain a living creature in the room with Live a Thousand Lives.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature examines the mural or enters the room

Effect The haunt activates and rolls initiative.

The faintly moving images compel one creature within 30 feet of the room to move into the room. The creature attempts a @Check[will|dc:38|traits:charm] save.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target must spend all its actions on its next turn moving into the room, and is then @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] until the end of its next turn.

Critical Failure As failure, and the target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 1 minute.

The haunt causes a living creature in the room to experience a full elven life, weathering every wound, misfortune, loss, and consequence of aging centuries in a matter of seconds. The target must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:38] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued].

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} (or its drained value increases by 1, to a maximum of drained 3), and it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] until the end of its next turn.

Critical Failure As failure, but the target also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1} (or its doomed value increases by 1).

details.description: Warding magic attempts to trap intruders or would-be thieves in a disrupted time flow.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:38|name:Disassemble Spell Components|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to rapidly disassemble the spell's myriad components in a single blink of an eye; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 36) to counteract the trap before it triggers or to counteract the effect on one creature after the trap is triggered

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the warded object or area.

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within 30 feet are trapped in a disrupted time flow (@Check[fortitude|dc:38] negates). The creatures' minds move so quickly that each round seems to last a century, but their bodies and magical energies move so slowly that they can't use any actions except Recall Knowledge. An affected creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:36] saving throw against a warp mind spell immediately and again for every minute of real time that passes while the creature is trapped in the frozen moment. This effect has an unlimited duration but can be counteracted.

details.description: These desperate spirits are the echoes of people who committed great atrocities in the name of an evil god. Now, they are left with only the knowledge that their souls have been damned, and the unwavering belief that they can better their fate by providing powerful sacrifices for their fiendish masters.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:46|name:Call For Divine Intervention] (master) to inspire a deity to intervene and counteract the ritual
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger Three or more sentient living creatures of 13th level or higher enter the haunt's area.

Effect The haunt deals @Damage[(6d12+35)[void]] damage to each creature in the haunt's area, and each creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:40|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and is doomed 1.

Success The creature takes half damage and is doomed 1.

Failure The creature takes full damage, becomes doomed 2, and is marked for damnation.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, becomes doomed 3, and is marked for damnation.

If a creature that is marked for damnation dies within the next 24 hours, including from the haunt's damage, its soul is immediately dragged away to the plane of the evil deity that the damned spirits served, where the creature's soul is held captive by one of that deity's powerful servitors. Only wish and similarly potent effects are able to recover the lost soul directly; however, it is also possible to recover the soul by journeying to the evil plane and defeating the soul's captor.

details.description: Apparitions of Aroden in his various 12 guises (the shepherd, soldier, tailor, thief, and more) emerge in pairs to defend this room.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:43] (master), @Check[intimidation|dc:43] (master), or @Check[religion|dc:43] (expert); three successes are required to convince the trap that Aroden is truly dead, causing the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Spiritual Guardian]{Spiritual Guardians} to hesitate and protest before fading away
details.reset: 1 day or after Kaisentia spends 10 minutes here in focused prayer
details.routine: (2 actions; spirit) The hazard Sustains both @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Spiritual Guardian]{spiritual guardians}, either attacking or protecting at Kaisentia's direction. Once per round, if fewer than two spiritual guardians are present, the hazard conjures one new spiritual guardian from one of the statues as an action. Each statue can create only one spiritual guardian in this way before needing to be reset.

description.value:
Trigger Kaisentia rolls initiative

Effect Two 9th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Spiritual Guardian] spells resembling ghostly simulacra of Aroden in two of his 12 guises emerge from their corresponding statues. One moves to an unoccupied space within the statue's line of effect and makes an attack against an adjacent creature hostile to Kaisentia (spell attack modifier of +33, @Damage[5d8[force]] damage). The other moves to an unoccupied space adjacent to Kaisentia and protects her (90 Hit Points). The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: Wave after wave of Hellish power ripples out from the pentagram surrounding the Inferno Gate, bathing all of the battling armies around it in its dark energies. Some are slain on the spot, while others fall into cracks that open beneath them in the earth, and still others are consumed by ignited gasses.
details.disable:
@Check[Devil Lore|dc:34] or @Check[HellLore|dc:34] to give the proper incantations and invoke the name of Visperthul to ward off the magical defenses from the Inferno Gate; @Check[arcana|dc:38] (expert), @Check[nature|dc:38] (expert), @Check[occultism|dc:38] (expert), or @Check[religion|dc:38] (expert) to erect a magical barrier to shield against the power emanating from the gate; @Check[survival|dc:43] to use the natural terrain as a shield to survive the onslaught of magical energies; @Check[will|dc:48] save to simply push back against the Hellish storm surge and refute them; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 34) to blast the bow shock with magic to stem its flow; three successes are required to dissipate the hazard's energies.

The first time a PC successfully uses Devil Lore or Hell Lore against this hazard, all PCs gain a +2 status bonus to their saving throws against the hazard's effects.
details.routine: (2 actions) The infernal bow shocks release a different effect each round for 3 rounds, centered on a randomly-chosen PC each time and never the same PC twice in a row (though creatures standing near each other might just be unlucky). In the first round, it unleashes Flaming Fumes. In the second round, it uses an 8th-rank whispers of the void spell (DC 38), and in the third round, it emulates the effect of an earthquake spell (DC 38), with each of the three piles of tower rubble collapsing as though they were shoddy structures. Notably, no fissures extend into the former inner bailey of Fort Arego (area E4, as outlined by the remaining rectangular battlements). The hazard concludes for the PCs when it's disabled, or when the third round ends.

description.value:
Trigger Encounter E3 begins

Effect A randomly chosen PC is targeted at the center of a @Template[type:burst|distance:20] of acrid-smelling (but invisible) fumes. The hazard unleashes Flaming Fumes on the same target as Shock Wave. A creature must succeed at a Perception check against the hazard's Stealth DC to visually detect the fumes. The infernal bow shocks then roll initiative.

A @Template[type:burst|distance:20] of acrid-smelling fumes ignites in gouts of hellfire. All creatures in the area take @Damage[7d6[fire],7d6[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:38|basic|options:area-effect] save.

details.description: A hidden glyph suddenly lights up and dominates the mind of any creature not bearing the sigil of Kharnas the Angel-Binder.
details.disable: Disable @Check[thievery|dc:46|name:Destroy Glyph|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to destroy the glyph, @Check[arcana|dc:42|name:Divert Glyph's Power] (master) to harmlessly divert the glyph's power, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (7th rank; counteract DC 38) to counteract the glyph

publication.title: Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege

details.reset: The glyph can affect two creatures before it deactivates for 5 minutes and automatically resets.

description.value:
Trigger A creature who doesn't bear the sigil of Kharnas attempts to open the door

Effect A surge of the Radiant Spark's latent power courses through the glyph and washes over the triggering creature. If the creature is undead, it takes @Damage[10d10[vitality]] damage. If it's living, it instead regains @Damage[10d10[healing]] Hit Points. It then must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:40|traits:incapacitation,mental] save or be affected by a 6th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dominate] spell.

The spell commands the affected creature to attack the nearest creature who doesn't bear the sigil of Kharnas.

details.description: An invisible magic rune emits a burst of psychic static and sends extraplanar creatures to Pharasma's realm.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:42|name:Discharge Glyph|traits:uncommon,trap,magical] (master) or @Check[thievery|dc:42|name:Discharge Glyph|traits:uncommon,trap,magical] (master) to discharge the glyph's power harmlessly, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 40) to counteract the glyph

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Night of the Gray Death

description.value:
Trigger A creature that isn't openly wearing a religious symbol of Pharasma passes over the glyph at the midpoint of the landing

Effect The glyph flares and discharges its energy. Each creature within 30 feet of the glyph takes @Damage[(8d12+24)[mental]] damage and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:40|traits:magical,trap,uncommon] saving throw.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage and, if not on its home plane, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is stupefied 1 for 1 minute. If not on its home plane, the creature is sent to a random location in the Boneyard.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 1 minute. If not on its home plane, the creature is sent to a random location in the Boneyard and can't return to the Material Plane until it receives permission to do so from a psychopomp of a higher level than the creature's level.

details.description: Arcs of lightning arc flash from the eyes of the floating sentries.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:45] (legendary) to turn the lightning back against the sentry, causing the head to plummet, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 36) to counteract the magic that empowers the sentry, causing it to plummet. Each sentry must be counteracted or destroyed separately.
details.routine: (6 actions) Each Rune Sentry grants the hazard 1 action; a Rune Sentry that's plummeted or been disabled or destroyed reduces the hazard's available actions by 1. On the hazard's turn, all active Rune Sentries spend their action to use Lightning Arc.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs fail the Trial of Runes

Effect Each sentry whose question the PCs answered correctly crashes to the ground. A creature directly below a head when it falls takes @Damage[(6d12+37)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic] save). The 10-foot area beneath the head becomes difficult terrain. The hazard then rolls initiative.

(electricity) One creature (other than the peacock phoenix) within 60 feet of the sentry takes @Damage[(3d12+19)[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic] save). A target wearing metal armor or made of metal takes a –1 circumstance penalty to its save; if damaged, they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round. A creature may only be targeted once by a lightning arc each round—any lightning arcs that go unused as a result of there not being enough targets instead restore 20 HP each to the peacock phoenix.

details.description: A spirit steps out of a covered mirror, pulling off and becoming shrouded in the cloth, which reveals the mirror. The shrouded figure attacks the living until the cloth is yanked off, which banishes the formless spirit.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43|name:Steal the Cloth|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to steal the cloth before the spirit manifests, or @Check[athletics|dc:47|name:Pull the Cloth Off] to pull the cloth off the spirit after it manifests
details.reset: The haunt is reset when the mirror is covered again.
details.routine: (3 actions) The spirit uses 3 actions to Step, fly 60 feet, or Strike, in any order. On a successful Strike the spirit's target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}, and on a critical success, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area

Effect The spirit steps out of the mirror, wearing the cloth as a shroud and revealing the mirror. The triggering creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} unless they succeed at a @Check[will|dc:38] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: An arcane sigil forces creatures to confront their greatest fear.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43] (master) to drain the sigil's power harmlessly, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 42) to counteract the sigil
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the sigil

Effect All creatures within 120 feet of the sigil are affected by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantasmagoria] spell (@Check[will|dc:40] save).

details.description: An arcane sigil forces creatures to confront their greatest fear.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43|name:Drain the Sigil's Power|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to drain the sigil's power harmlessly, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 42) to counteract the sigil
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 day.

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the sigil

Effect All creatures within 120 feet of the sigil are affected by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantasmagoria] spell (@Check[will|dc:40|options:area-effect,damaging-effect] save).

details.description: Smoke billows out from the chimney and fills the room.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:40] (expert) to pull the blockage from the chimney, @Check[thievery|dc:46] (master) to clear the chimney by removing a crucial object and cause everything to fall out, or create water (or similar effect) to extinguish the fire
details.reset: The smoking chimney resets when 10 Bulk of objects are placed in it.
details.routine: (1 action; inhaled, poison) The smoking chimney billows out smoke. If the door from area A6 is open, the smoke expands 5 feet into the corridor. All creatures in the smoke take @Damage[11d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:43|basic|traits:inhaled,poison] save). On a failure, the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than Ivirno enters area A6

Effect Smoke fills the room, concealing all creatures within area A6. All creatures in the area other than Ivirno take @Damage[11d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:43|basic] save), then the smoking chimney rolls initiative.

details.description: Four steel pillars emit pulsing energy to slow souls' processes.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:43] or Religion (master) to reweave the magic within each pillar, @Check[thievery|dc:46] (master) to disable each pillar, or dispel magic (8th level; counteract DC 43) to counteract each pillar
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any souls in the area.
details.routine: (4 actions) This trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled pillar. Each pillar uses 1 action to emit a @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30-foot-radius burst} of inhibiting energy that slows souls. Each non-psychopomp creature with a soul in the area is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} unless they succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:43] save.

description.value:
Trigger A soul or creature with a soul enters the area

Effect The steel pillars activate with an audible hum that resonates with spirits. Each non-psychopomp creature with a soul is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3} unless they succeed at a @Check[will|dc:43] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A spectral cast of singers in opera costumes emerges from the walls, keening a mournful and terrifying chorus.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:46] (master) to disrupt the song with another tune, or @Check[religion|dc:43] (expert) to calm the spirits

description.value:
Trigger A nonworshipper of Norgorber enters the room

Effect The tormented spirits rise, and their chorus deals @Damage[(6d12+33)[mental]] damage to any within the room who don't worship Norgorber. Affected creatures must each attempt a @Check[will|dc:37] save.

Critical Success The creature is unharmed.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage. It becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for 1 round.

stealth.details: (master) to notice the hallway is constructed to tilt on a hinge point halfway down its length

details.description: Images of excessive feasts decorate the walls of the hallway.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43] (expert) to jam the hallway so it can't tilt; a character who succeeds at a @Check[acrobatics|dc:46] or @Check[athletics|dc:46] check can balance or sprint the length of the hallway before it has a chance to tilt. Flying creatures and Tiny creatures don't trigger the trap. Creative solutions (such as loading the hallway's western half with an appropriate counterweight) might also disable the trap.
details.reset: Once the weight in the eastern end of the hallway is removed, the ramp rises back to its original position and the trap immediately resets.

description.value:
Trigger The number of PCs in the eastern half of the hallway equals or exceeds the number in the western half

Effect The eastern side of the hallway floor tilts at a 45-degree angle, its end descending. Any creature standing in this section of the hall must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:43] save to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge]. On a failure, the creature falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], tumbles eastward down the length of the ramp, and falls 30 feet into a chamber beneath the hall (area B4b), taking @Damage[(3d12+15)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:43|basic] save). A PC within 10 feet of the hallway's eastern end when it tilts can instead attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:40] save to scramble into area B5. A PC who fails this check instead tumbles and rolls all the way down the steeply sloped ramp before plummeting into area B4b.

details.description: A shaft of light shines from the ceiling to the floor in a ring of slowly churning smoke.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43] (master) to use tools to reflect and focus the light shining from above into the surrounding smoke to disperse it and the hazard's magical energies, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 36) to counteract the trial
details.reset: Once the room is empty of living creatures, the trial deactivates and then resets after 1 minute has passed.
details.routine:
(1 or 2 actions; emotion, fear, mental) If a creature in area B4 is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] as a result of A Quick Death, the trial's first action is to Sustain that paralysis or unconscious effect; the paralyzed or unconscious creature can attempt a new @Check[will|dc:38|traits:emotion,fear,mental] save at this time against this effect with the same potential results as detailed above for A Quick Death, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save. The Trial of Finality's second action is to manifest horrifying ghostly shapes in the smoke that shriek and telepathically threaten everyone in area B4, promising them their deaths are imminent. All creatures other than the paralyzed or unconscious creature currently enduring A Quick Death must attempt a @Check[will|dc:38|traits:emotion,fear,mental] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] as long as they remain frightened.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature steps into the central 15-foot area of the room directly illuminated by light

Effect The triggering creature momentarily becomes convinced they have been dead for a long time, and that the events of this adventure are but a hallucination. If the triggering creature has come close to death earlier in this adventure, they briefly believe that they didn't actually survive that encounter. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:38] save.

Critical Success The creature shakes off the conviction that they are dead and realizes it was a magically induced hallucination. The Trial of Finality deactivates.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed], and the conviction that they are dead and now watching, as a ghost, their companions from the middle of the room. The Trial of Finality rolls initiative, and the creature remains paralyzed until the end of the hazard's next turn.

Failure As success, but the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] until the end of the trial's next turn instead of paralyzed. While unconscious, the creature experiences a dreamlike vision of being carried through the River of Souls toward the Boneyard, where their soul shall be judged.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1}, or increases the value of an existing doomed condition by 1. If the creature dies as a result of this, the Trial of Finality deactivates.

details.description: A convincing illusion of a bustling market scene fills the empty room, confronting the viewers with potentially frightening diversity and unexpected encounters.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43] (master) to scratch out well-hidden key runes etched into the room's floor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 36) to counteract the trial
details.reset: Once the room is empty of creatures, the trial deactivates and then resets after 1 minute has passed.
details.routine: (1 action; emotion, fear, illusion, mental) An unexpectedly aggressive or frightening looking figure, such as a Hellknight, demon, pirate, or cloaked assassin suddenly emerges from the crowd and quickly advances toward a randomly determined character in the room, then stands menacingly in their path until the end of the round before they appear to lose interest and drift back into the crowd. If the target has a type of fear or hatred toward a specific kind of creature, this unexpected figure epitomizes those fears and hatreds. The target must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:46|traits:emotion,fear,illusion,mental] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). If any creature in the room takes any hostile action against the intimidating figure, the Trial of Tolerance uses its Reflective Violence reaction. Once three rounds pass without the Trial of Tolerance using Reflective Violence, the hazard deactivates.

description.value:
Trigger A creature begins its turn in area B2

Effect A convincing illusion of an Ilizmagorti city bazaar appears, complete with a cloudless blue sky above. The surrounding crowd is dense, and the smells and sights and sounds are quite realistic even as no one in the crowd actually bumps against the PCs as they mill about. The Trial of Tolerance then rolls initiative.

Trigger A creature uses a hostile action against an illusory figure created by the Trial of Tolerance or fails at an attempt to disable the trap

Effect The illusory figure effortlessly avoids the triggering action, then appears to attack the triggering creature using its most harrowing and violent option. Regardless of what this attack appears as, the triggering creature takes @Damage[(3d12+19)[mental]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:38|basic] save).

details.description: What appears to be an eye opens within the glowing orb near the ceiling.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43] (master) to deactivate the glowing orb and prevent it from observing the room, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 36) to counteract the trial, @Check[religion|dc:45] (master) to perform a complex 5-round candle-lighting ritual, or placing General Ordulf's religious symbol on an empty hook on any wall as an Interact action
details.reset: Once the room is empty of creatures, the trial deactivates and then resets after 1 minute has passed.
details.routine: (1 action; death, emotion, fear, mental) The red eye glances at a randomly determined creature in area B3 that it can see and is not engaged in a candle-lighting ritual, then casts a 9th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Vision of Death] on that target, causing them to experience the sight of Achaekek rising up from the ground to smite the transgressor for failing to honor the gods.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Pick a Lock]{Pick the Lock} on the eastern door, or ends a turn in this room while not performing the candle-lighting ritual

Effect A glowing red eye manifests in the ceiling mounted orb to stare angrily at the triggering creature, who must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:46] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The Trial of the Gods then rolls initiative.

details.description: A trail of over-sized footprints crosses the path, markings of the apparently recent passage of a dangerous and mind-shattering entity whose stride is enough to be mistaken for depressions in the natural terrain, and promising destruction to those compelled to follow the tracks.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:46] (master) to distract those (including yourself) compelled to follow footprints, or @Check[survival|dc:43] (legendary) to quickly obscure the trail
details.reset: The unspeakable impressions fade once they are triggered, but reform somewhere else in the vicinity 24 hours later unless they are successfully disabled.

description.value:
Trigger A creature crosses over the footprints' trail

Effect The triggering creature suddenly realizes the nature of the immense trail they just stepped into and feels a compulsion to follow the trail to their destination; the triggering creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:35] save with the following results.

Critical Success The creature Strides once down the trail before coming to their senses—this movement does not put the creature in any peril or danger.

Success The creature takes its three actions to Stride down the trail. While this movement doesn't put the creature in any direct peril, when they end the movement, they're overwhelmed with the knowledge that had they gone further, something horrible would have happened; the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 hour by the close call.

Failure As success, but the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1} and also stupefied 1 for 24 hours.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2}. In addition, at the end of the three Strides, the creature vanishes from view, instantly teleporting to some dangerous area or hidden prison. This might be a nearby location that the creature's allies can discover and rescue them from, or it may be somewhere further away that will require more effort to reach before the victim can be rescued.

details.description: Swirling ashes fill the room and form into the semblance of a massive predatory bird that swoops upon those in the chamber, draining their life force away.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:44] (master) to disrupt and counteract the shadow magic animating the ashes, or @Check[religion|dc:42] (master) to exorcise the animating spirit. Disabling the trap requires three successful checks, in any combination of Occultism and Religion checks.
details.reset: The haunt resets immediately when there are no creatures in area F9. Any previous successes from attempts to Disable the haunt persist when it's later triggered. After three successes, the haunt doesn't reset.
details.routine:
(1 action) The shadowy shape sends streams of scouring ash toward the mouth, eyes, and nose of each creature in the room, dealing @Damage[(3d12+20)[slashing]] damage and requiring a @Check[fortitude|dc:42] save. Creatures that don't need breathe can't be sickened by the haunt and creatures that don't see can't be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] by it.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and is blinded for 1 round.

Success The creature takes half damage, is blinded for 1 round, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is blinded for 1 minute, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage, is blinded for 1 minute, is sickened 2, and is unable to use any spells or abilities with the verbal trait while it is sickened.

description.value:
Trigger Two or more creatures enter area F9

Effect A terrifying shriek of pain and suffering deals @Damage[(3d12+40)[mental]] damage to each creature within area F9, and each creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:42] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Success The creature takes half damage and is frightened 1.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is frightened 2, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1}.

details.description: Hundreds of panicked whippoorwills swarm through the area.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:43] (master) to calm the birds, @Check[occultism|dc:45] (master) to alter your aura, or @Check[performance|dc:45] (master) to imitate the living
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any undead or creatures with void healing in the garden.
details.routine: (1 action) The birds swarm wildly through the garden, pecking and slashing at all undead creatures and creatures with void healing in the garden, dealing @Damage[(3d10+20)[slashing]] damage and @Damage[1d10[bleed]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:44|basic]).

description.value:
Trigger An undead or a creature with void healing enters the garden

Effect The birds screech in panic, dealing @Damage[(3d12+20)[sonic]] damage to all creatures in the garden (@Check[fortitude|dc:44|basic]). On a failure, the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3} on a critical failure). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A magically contagious wail of the banshee spell is trapped in the larynx of an invisible, mummified elf.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:42|name:Pierce Larynx|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to pierce the invisible larynx so precisely that the magic releases in a trickle before the trap activates, @Check[thievery|dc:44|name:Deconstruct Larynx|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) three times to deconstruct the larynx while the trap is active in such a way that it tears the spell apart, or spell DC 38 (9th level) to counteract the wail of the banshee before the trap activates
details.reset: The trap ends when the trap is unable to make a creature scream (usually because no creature failed its save on the previous turn or because all creatures have critically succeeded in the past). It then resets over 24 hours, as a new scream builds up in the mummified larynx.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap uses its action to force one random creature that failed its save against wail of the banshee last turn to emit a wail of the banshee with the same statistics as the initial one. The creature wails even if it is dead or unable to speak, no matter how far away from the trap it is. Unlike a casting of the spell, the drained condition from this trap's wail of the banshee spells increases the targets' drained condition values. A creature that critically succeeds at any of its saves can still be affected by the trap on future rounds, but can't be forced to wail by the trap.

description.value:
Trigger Three or more living creatures remain within 100 feet of the trap for 6 seconds or longer.

Effect The trap releases an arcane wail of the banshee (DC 40) with a 100-foot-radius emanation instead of 40 feet, targeting all living creatures in the area, and rolls initiative.

details.description: The many iron bars of this iron mask fits tightly around Javownir's head and snout, looking almost as if it was built in place to conform specifically to the shape of his skull with no apparent latch or hinge for it to be removed.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:45] (expert) twice to find the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] latches, @Check[athletics|dc:48] (master) to wrench the mask open quickly enough to prevent it from triggering before it's destroyed, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th-rank; counteract DC 35), or automatic with Spleen's key
details.reset: The mask resets automatically at the start of the round after it is triggered.

description.value: Whenever the mask takes damage, Javownir takes the same damage (@Check[reflex|dc:44|basic] save)

Trigger The mask takes damage, or an Athletics check to wrench it free fails or critically fails

Effect The mask creates a cloud of poison gas in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] around Javownir. Creatures in the area take @Damage[(6d12+41)[poison]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:44|basic] save; Javownir treats the result of this save as one degree of success worse than the rolled result).

details.description: Spores explode onto nearby creatures, leaving welts as the mold dissolves exposed flesh.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:42] (legendary) to neutralize the void energy that animates the mold, or @Check[survival|dc:36] (master) to remove the mold without triggering the spores

description.value:
Trigger A living creature moves adjacent to the mold or damages the mold

Effect All creatures within 15 feet of the mold are exposed to the flesh-eating spores.

The flesh-eating spores leave red rings on the skin as they dissolve the target's flesh

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:44]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[4d10[void]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[6d10[void]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[8d10[void]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 3} (1 round)

details.description:
This apocalypse represents the effects of numerous planar rifts forming and exploding in a region, with the risk of the planar fabric being compromised enough to tear a city-sized (if not larger) hole in reality. These rifts are likely to eject inhabitants of the other plane into the Universe, as well as serve as gateways for furious defenders to pour through to punish anyone they think might be responsible for the problem. Any additional encounters that result from these incursions should award XP as normal for encounters of their difficulty. While the rifts are likely to all lead to the same plane, particularly far-reaching disruptions might create gateways to multiple planes at once, with each rift spewing forth its own defenders. These groups would each have their own aims and agendas, clashing, collaborating, or causing chaos wherever they go.

Anticipate DC 40 Lore (expert) for the plane's Lore skill to notice pinpoint incursions from the other plane or @Check[arcana|dc:45] (master), @Check[nature|dc:45] (master), @Check[occultism|dc:45] (master), or @Check[religion|dc:45] (master) to detect subtle instabilities in the fabric of the Universe

Prepare DC 42 Lore (expert) with the plane's Lore skill, @Check[arcana|dc:45] (master), @Check[nature|dc:45] (master), @Check[occultism|dc:45] (master), @Check[religion|dc:45] (master) or @Check[performance|dc:48] (legendary) to perform a planar stabilization rite to minimize the damage
details.routine:
(1+ hours, @Check[flat|dc:18]) Rifts detonate with raw planar essence. As the hazard progresses, the ruptures grow more frequent until they merge together into a gaping portal (this final stage might be prevented with good fortune or successful efforts to Prepare). Ruptures occur once per 10 minutes for the first hour and once per minute for the second hour. The frequency with which each unprepared creature in the area is hit by a rupture depends upon their proximity to the hazard's epicenter. If the hazard proceeds into a third hour, all creatures and objects in the area take the hazard's damage one final time before being swallowed up by the burgeoning portal and deposited on the other plane.

The planar explosions deal 2d12+18 damage, with a DC 42 basic saving throw, and inflict a condition on a failed saving throw. The damage type, saving throw type, condition, and condition duration depend upon the exploding plane, as detailed in the table below.

d20PlaneDamage and SaveCondition
1AbaddonFortitude vs. poison damagefrightened 2 (1 hour, The condition's value can't be reduced by the typical means for 1 hour)
2Astral or Ethereal PlaneEach time a new rift appears, roll again on this table, using the listed effects.Each time a new rift appears, roll again on this table, using the listed effects.
3AxisFortitude vs. force damageclumsy 2 (1 hour)
4BoneyardWill vs. spirit damagedrained 2 (1 hour)
5Creation's ForgeWill vs. vitality damagedazzled (1 hour)
6ElysiumFortitude vs. sonic damagedeafened (24 hours)
7First WorldWill vs. mental damageconfused (1 minute)
8HeavenReflex vs. fire damagefascinated by the rift (10 minutes)
9HellReflex vs. fire damagedoomed 1 (1 hour)
10Maelstromvaries 2varies 2
11NirvanaFortitude vs. bludgeoning damagestupefied 2 (1 hour)
12The NetherworldFortitude vs. cold damageblinded (10 minutes)
13Outer RiftsReflex vs. acid damagesickened 1 (1 hour) 1
14Plane of AirReflex vs. electricity damagestunned 1 (1 minute)
15Plane of EarthFortitude vs. bludgeoning damagefatigued
16Plane of FireReflex vs. fire damage2d6 persistent fire damage
17Plane of MetalReflex vs. slashing damage2d6 persistent bleed damage
18Plane of WaterReflex vs. bludgeoning damageoff-guard (10 minutes)
19Plane of WoodReflex vs. piercing damage–15-foot circumstance penalty to Speeds (10 minutes)
20The VoidWill vs. void damageenfeebled 2 (1 hour)

details.description: The hall's entry and exit seal with a force barrier, then waves of force surge through the hall.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48] (master) to deface the script without triggering, @Check[arcana|dc:47] (master) to redirect the magical energy, or @Check[religion|dc:46] (master) to quote Pharasmin prayers
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (1 action) A wave of force flows down the hall from north to south, dealing @Damage[(3d10+20)[force]] damage to each creature in the corridor. Each creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:40|basic]. On a failure, a creature is pushed back 5 feet (10 feet on a critical failure). If this movement would cause a creature to hit another creature, both creatures take @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage. If this movement would cause a creature to hit a force barrier, the creature takes @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]] damage. Animals, plants, and worshippers of Pharasma are immune to this force damage.

description.value:
Trigger A creature reaches the hall's midpoint

Effect Force barriers seal the hall's entrance and exit for 1 minute. The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to hear or feel the energy coalescing

details.description: A wave of magic passes through the area, causing crystal shards to grow and stab intruders.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:45] (master) to chip away at the crystal and disrupt its resonance, or @Check[thievery|dc:42] (master) to carefully detach the crystals and then safely smash them
details.reset: Once the cluster heart uses its reaction, it automatically resets after 1 minute. As long as the cluster heart has 150 or more Hit Points, it recovers 50 per day.

description.value: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Disintegrate] deals double damage, ignoring Hardness, rather than destroying the cluster heart outright

Trigger A non-elemental creature ends its turn adjacent to the cluster heart

Effect The heart extends shards of crystal imbued with force, like a jabbing blade, and deals @Damage[(3d10+20)[force]] damage and @Damage[(3d10+20)[piercing]] damage to all creatures in a 20-foot radius (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save). A creature who takes piercing damage from the surge is exposed to crystal corruption. On a critical failure, a creature also takes @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Creatures afflicted by this curse slowly turn to solid crystal. This affliction's sickened, slowed, and paralyzed conditions can't be removed until the affliction itself is removed. Creatures with both the earth and elemental traits instead become carriers and suffer no ill effects aside from gaining the weakness to sonic.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:40]

Stage 1 sickened 1 (1 hour)

Stage 2 weakness 5 to sonic and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 weakness 5 to sonic and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} (1 day)

Stage 4 weakness 10 to sonic and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] (1 day)

Stage 5 weakness 10 to sonic, and the creature is permanently @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Petrified].

stealth.details: (expert) to notice cracks forming in the walls of the structure

details.description: Tremors from the changing landscape could cause a building to collapse.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:45] (master), @Check[crafting|dc:45] (master), or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:45] (master) to brace the structure against collapse until the end of the creature's next turn. The DC of the flat check for this round (see Routine) decreases by 4 on a success (or by 8 on a critical success). Decreasing the flat check DC to 0 stabilizes the structure, ending this hazard.
details.reset: The building becomes susceptible to Shake to Pieces again [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} after it's stabilized.
details.routine: (1 action) The GM rolls a @Check[flat|dc:11], decreasing the DC as noted above. If the check fails, dust and debris fall within the building and 30 feet around it, providing concealment for 1 round and dealing @Damage[10d6[bludgeoning]] damage to 1d4 randomly chosen targets in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save); a target that critically fails this save is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. If the flat check critically fails, the effect is the same, but damage from the debris increases to 16d6, it affects 2d4 randomly chosen targets, and a creature that critically fails its save is knocked prone and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] by the rubble ([[/act escape dc=40]], [[/act force-open dc=44]]).

description.value:
Trigger The Hellfire tornado moves adjacent to the building

Effect The building trembles. The floors of the building and the streets within 30 feet of the building become difficult terrain; creatures on this difficult terrain take a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls, AC, and skill checks. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: Statues depicting the runelords of envy steal magic from intruders.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:45] (master) to unweave the magic surrounding a statue, @Check[athletics|dc:48] (expert) to wrest a statue's implement from its hands, @Check[thievery|dc:48] (expert) to slip an implement free, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 36) to counteract the magic that empowers the statue. Increase the DCs to disable the Belimarius statue by 2.
