Each statue must be disabled or counteracted separately, and disabling the Belimarius statue or all nine other statues permanently disables the hazard.
details.reset: The hazard resets automatically if not disabled.
details.routine: (4 actions) The hazard uses Steal Magic twice. Once the implements of at least three runelord statues are charged, the hazard uses its next available action to Transfer Magic. If the hazard starts its turn with the Belimarius statue charged, the hazard uses its reaction to Empower Barrier. A disabled runelord statue can't use Steal Magic. If the Belimarius statue is disabled, the hazard can't use Transfer Magic or Empower Barrier.

description.value:
Trigger A magic item or active spell effect enters the corridor

Effect A randomly determined statue uses the hazard's Steal Magic action. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Roll a [[/gmr d10]] to determine which statue activates, rerolling a result of 10 or any result that would indicate a disabled statue or a statue that is already charged. The indicated statue casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Detonate Magic] with a [[/gmr 1d20+32 #Counteract]]{+32} bonus to its counteract check. The spell targets the closest magic item within 120 feet, prioritizing the highest-ranking magic item and targeting spell effects only if no magic item is within range. On a success or critical success, the statue gathers the dispersed energy into its implement, charging it.

While the statue's implement remains charged, any creature who is at least an expert in @Check[arcana|dc:44], @Check[nature|dc:44], @Check[occultism|dc:44], or @Check[religion|dc:44] can use a 2-action activity with the concentrate trait to attempt a DC 44 check using that skill. On a success or critical success, they return the magic to its original source, causing it to reactivate. Removing an implement from its statue prevents the statue from using Transfer Magic but doesn't prevent a creature from restoring the charge to its source.

Requirements The implements of at least three runelord statues are charged

Effect Three statues with charged implements transfer their charges to the Belimarius statue. While the Belimarius statue holds at least one charge, a creature can transfer charges from its implement as described in Steal Magic, except the DC of the skill check increases to DC 46; success returns the stolen magic to the appropriate items.

@Check[arcana|dc:46], @Check[nature|dc:46], @Check[occultism|dc:46], or @Check[religion|dc:46]

Requirements The hazard starts its turn, and the Belimarius statue holds at least one charge

Effect The Belimarius statue transfers all charges from its implement into the force barrier separating the corridor from area B3. For every charge the statue transfers, the force barrier gains 10 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 hour. (If the force barrier has been destroyed, the statue instead expends the first charge to cast a 6th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Wall of Force], with any remaining charges granting the barrier temporary Hit Points.) Charges transferred to the force barrier are lost forever and can't be returned to items they were stolen from.

stealth.details: (master) or DC 48 (master) to find the control panel

details.description: A spinning metal pole covered in deadly blades is hidden in the ceiling, connected to several trigger plates around the room and a hidden control panel built into the bed frame.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:39] (master) three times on a pillar, or @Check[thievery|dc:37] (master) twice on the control panel deactivates the whole trap. Breaking the control panel prevents anyone from disabling the trap using the control panel and prevents the trap from deactivating automatically (see Reset below)
details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (3 actions) The trap uses its first action to make a spinning blade Strike against each adjacent creature. Its second action is to move in a random direction (roll 1d4 to determine the direction), and its third action to make a spinning blade Strike against each adjacent creature.

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps on one of the marked trigger plates

Effect The pillar descends from the ceiling at the marked grid intersection and makes a spinning blade Strike against one adjacent creature (if any). The trap then rolls initiative.
10 feet

Panel Hardness 15;

Panel HP 40 (BT 20)

details.description: A massive storm stirs in the skies above Breachill and Citadel Altaerein. As specific events begin during the three waves of the manifestation's retribution, the dragonstorm wreaks localized havoc.
details.disable: The dragonstorm can't be disabled-only endured. It ends only once the attack on Breachill and Citadel Altaerein is over.

description.value:
Trigger A new wave begins

Effect Roll [[/gmr 1d10 #Localized Dragonstorm]] to determine which effect manifests from the following possibilities. The placement of these effects is guided by Dahak's will, and they should manifest in the most detrimental locations possible to the PCs.

A torrent of acid drenches creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:20]. Creatures in the area take @Damage[6d10[acid]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic|options:area-effect] save). Creatures that critically fail also take @Damage[5[persistent,acid]] damage.

A barrage of freezing wind and hail assaults all creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:20]. Creatures in the area take @Damage[3d10[cold],3d10[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:slowed] save). Creatures that critically fail are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

Several bolts of lightning strike all creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:20]. Creatures in the area take @Damage[6d10[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:blinded,inflicts:deafened] save). Creatures that critically fail their saves become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Deafened] for 1 round.

Gouts of flame fall on all creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:20]. Creatures in the area take @Damage[6d10[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic|options:area-effect] save). Creatures that critically fail their saves also take @Damage[5[persistent,fire]] damage.

Poisonous mist rolls forth, covering a @Template[burst|distance:20] for 1 round. Creatures within the mist become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed], and creatures outside it become concealed to creatures within it. Creatures within the mist take @Damage[6d10[poison]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:42|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:enfeebled] save). Creatures that critically fail their saves become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2}.

details.description: Long saw-toothed blades slash up along the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] grooves in the floor, their edges caked with what looks like blood but is in fact a deadly poison.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:45] (master) once per weapon rack to detach the triggers from the trap mechanism
details.reset: Manual.
details.routine: (10 actions) The trap rolls a sawtooth saber Strike for each action, divided targets evenly among creatures in the trapped area. If there aren't enough targets in area B7 to divide these actions evenly, additional targets are randomly chosen. The trap loses one action for each destroyed blade. The trap ignores multiple attack penalties.

description.value:
Trigger Either of the weapons stored on either weapon rack is disturbed

Effect Both doors into this room slam shut and lock (DC 40). The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (trained) to hear the flood of spirits coalescing

details.description: A wave of spirits fly through the area, passing right through the living.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:48|name:Divert the spirits] (expert) or @Check[religion|dc:48|name:Divert the spirits] (master) to divert the spirits
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes through the area

Effect The haunt deals @Damage[(3d12+20)[void],(3d12+20)[cold]]{3d12+20 void damage and 3d12+20 cold damage} to creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save). On a failure, a creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 1 minute, and on a critical failure, it's also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 minute.

details.description: A gauntlet of magical force clutches creatures trying to steal gear from the armory.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:45], @Check[nature|dc:45], @Check[occultism|dc:45], or @Check[religion|dc:45] to trick the hazard's magic into nullifying itself, @Check[athletics|dc:41] to wrestle the gauntlet into submission, @Check[thievery|dc:39] to slide an object into the gauntlet's grasp, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 41)
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the warded area

Effect A gauntleted hand reaches out from the ward and attempts a gauntlet Strike. The hazard has an Athletics modifier of +37 for its [[/act grapple dc=37]]{Grab} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] [[/act escape dc=47]]{DC 47}).
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ImprovedGrab]

details.description: A portcullis drops from the ceiling, landing with a heavy thunk. Seconds later, a cloud of acid rises from a metal grate in the floor.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:45] to prevent the portcullis from dropping, @Check[crafting|dc:48] (master) to create a compound to neutralize the cloud of acid, or @Check[thievery|dc:35] (expert) to jam the pressure plate
details.reset: Once triggered, the trap must be manually reset by raising the portcullis. Once all the vials are smashed, the trap can't use its routine again until the vials are replaced.
details.routine: (1 action) A cloud of acidic vapor rises from the grate beneath the portcullis in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5]. Any creature that enters the area or starts its turn inside the cloud takes @Damage[(3d12+20)[acid]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:40|basic] save). A creature that critically fails the save also takes @Damage[1d12[persistent,acid]] damage. Each time the hazard uses this routine beyond the first, increase the radius of the emanation by 5 feet.

description.value:
Trigger A creature Strides beneath the portcullis while it is raised

Effect The portcullis slams down, driving its sharpened ends through a grate in the floor. Creatures directly beneath the portcullis take @Damage[(3d12+20)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save). The portcullis is made of adamantine and has Hardness 31 and 120 HP (BT 60). The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master)

details.description: The very air catches fire from the heat of a dragon's breath, causing a burning tornado.
details.disable: The Hellfire tornado can't be disabled—only endured. It ends after 5 rounds.
details.reset: Once 5 rounds pass, the Hellfire tornado dissipates and doesn't reset.
details.routine:
(1 action; air, fire) The Hellfire tornado—a 15-footdiameter column 80 feet tall—moves 60 feet down the street. It can move through creatures' spaces, and creatures can move into its space, though the tornado's space is difficult terrain. Each creature the tornado moves through or each creature that ends its turn in the tornado's area takes @Damage[(4d10)[bludgeoning],(4d6)[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic|traits:air,fire] save). A creature can be affected by the tornado only once per round.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and rises 10 feet into the air as it's engulfed by the tornado ([[/act escape dc=40]]). While engulfed, a creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1}, and if it doesn't have a fly Speed, it gains a 10-foot fly Speed until it either reaches the ground or ceases to be in the tornado's area, potentially falling when the hazard ends or when it leaves the tornado's area. A creature engulfed by the tornado moves along with it on the tornado's turn.

Critical Failure As failure, except the creature takes double damage and rises 20 feet into the air.

description.value:
Trigger Kandarlu breathes flame onto the streets below

Effect The back draft from the dragon's breath washes over the PCs. Each creature on the street must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:44] save. The Hellfire tornado then forms at one end of the street, and the hazard rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] from the wind.

Failure The creature is knocked prone from the wind and gains weakness 10 to fire damage caused by the Hellfire tornado.

Critical Failure As failure, but the weakness is 15. The creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued] from the heat.

details.description: A host of angry spirits inhabiting the ruined house attempts to drag living creatures closer and crush them to death.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:43|name:Exorcise Spirits] (master) to exorcise the spirits or @Check[deception|dc:45|name:Trick the Spirits] (master) to trick the spirits into departing

publication.title: Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands

details.reset: The haunt ends 1 round after no creatures are within 50 feet of the house. The haunt resets after 1 day.
details.routine: 3 For each of its actions, the trap attempts a ghostly hand Strike against a different creature within 50 feet of the ruins of the house (if there are fewer than 3 creatures, the haunt doesn't use its remaining actions).

description.value:
Trigger A creature that has angered the haunt begins its turn within 50 feet of the ruins of the house

Effect The haunt attempts a ghostly hand Strike against the target creature, and then rolls initiative.

A creature outside the house that takes damage from the host of spirits' ghostly hand Strike must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:42] save or be pulled directly into the house. If there is a wall in the creature's path, that section of wall is destroyed, and the creature takes an additional @Damage[3d12[bludgeoning]] damage. The haunt's ghostly hand Strike also deals an additional @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] damage to a target already inside the house (@Check[fortitude|dc:42|basic] save).

details.description: Steel hatch doors automatically lock shut in each of the four vats labeled J2. Four ink spouts (one per vat) jut through a narrow aperture in the ceiling of each vat, 20 feet above.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Seal an Ink Spout|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to seal an ink spout, @Check[thievery|dc:48|name:Unlock a Door|traits:action:pick-a-lock] (master) to unlock a steel hatch door, or @Check[athletics|dc:45|name:Force Open a Door|traits:action:force-open] (master) to Force Open a steel hatch door

publication.title: Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale

details.reset: The trap can be reset by refilling the ink storage tanks above the vats.
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action for each disabled ink spout. On each of the trap's actions, a spout pours ink, increasing the depth of the ink in that vat by 2 feet. Creatures standing in the ink are exposed to a dose of poison ink. Once the vat is full of ink, the trap stops using actions, but creatures in the vat might start drowning.

description.value:
Trigger The switch outside the vats is pulled

Effect The trap rolls initiative.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:38]

Maximum Duration 10 rounds

Stage 1 3d6 poison damage and sickened 1 (1 round)

Stage 2 4d6 poison damage and sickened 2 (1 round)

details.description: A vision of a terrifying presence (the King in Yellow, the Crawling Chaos, or some other horror beyond imagining) overwhelms an unfortunate explorer, putting them into waking stupor that quickly turns into a homicidal fugue.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:48] (master) to talk the affected individual out of the killing fugue, @Check[occultism|dc:45] (legendary) to recognize the spiritual attack taking place and help the victim to resist its effects; four successes are required to disable the nightmare
details.reset: As long as a creature remains under the effects of the nightmare, it cannot reset. Once a creature has either recovered from the effects or died, the nightmare resets in [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.
details.routine:
(3 actions; curse) The killing fugue attempts to force a creature under the stupefying effects of deathly vision into taking violent actions against their allies. The affected creature can still take its normal turn of actions on its own turn, but on the killing fugue's turn they perform actions as decided upon by the GM. The killing fugue then spends the appropriate amount of actions to perform those actions and activities. The affected creature can attempt a @Check[will|dc:40|traits:curse] save each time the killing fugue attempts to force them to take actions in this way with the following results.

Critical Success The character throws off the killing fugue's influence, ending the nightmare.

Success The character prevents the killing fugue from taking the attempted action or activity.

Failure The character is forced to take the action or activity chosen by the killing fugue.

Critical Failure As failure, but the character can no longer attempt Will saves to resist the killing fugue.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of a site associated with the terrifying presence (such as a cairn, an altar, a cave, a strange skeleton, a waterfall, a pit, a statue, etc.)

Effect The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:40] save, with the following effects. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} as long as the Killing Fugue remains active.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] for 1 round and is stupefied 1 but is capable of whispering to anyone nearby that if anyone touches them, they will die.

Critical Failure As failure but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2}.

details.description: A burst of radiance explodes from the door.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to disable the wards or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 38) to counteract the ward
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the door

Effect All creatures within 20 feet of the door are scorched with brilliant light, taking @Damage[20d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save). A creature that fails this save is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 hour (or permanently on a critical failure).

stealth.details: (the maggots are slow and impossible to miss)

details.description: Countless human-faced, fanged maggots wriggle and squirm in this chamber.
details.disable: Three @Check[religion|dc:45] checks within the room to utter prayers, which calm and quell the maggots with invocations to the gods
details.reset: The pit functions continuously. If it's disabled or destroyed, it resets automatically after 24 hours if the crystal stele in area M2 still functions, otherwise it doesn't reset.
details.routine: (2 actions; death, divine, necromancy) On its initiative, the pit uses its first action to use Damning Swarm against any creature within 5 feet of the pit floor. It then takes its second action to form a long, thrashing tendril composed of thousands of maggots to lash at a single target within area M1.

description.value:
Trigger a creature appears in area M1

Effect The swarming maggots squirm into a biting frenzy, and all creatures on the ground in area M1 must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:40|traits:damaging-effect] save. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(1d10+5)[piercing]] damage, @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+10)[piercing]] damage, @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage, is sickened 1, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} by the maggots' bites.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d10+20)[piercing]] damage, @Damage[12d6[poison]] damage, is sickened 1, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} by the maggots' bites.
On a critical hit, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1}; this has the poison trait

details.description:
A tsunami can be a devastating disaster but pales in comparison to the daunting wall of water created by a megatsunami. When a megatsunami begins, ocean water often recedes far away from the shore, much more dramatically than it would during low tide. This warning sign might occur only seconds or minutes before the full force of the megatsunami crashes onto the land in the form of a wave whose height can exceed a hundred feet.

Anticipate @Check[ocean-lore|dc:36] (expert), @Check[nature|dc:38] (expert), or @Check[survival|dc:40] (master) to notice anomalies in wave patterns

Prepare @Check[ocean-lore|dc:36] (expert) or @Check[nature|dc:38] or @Check[survival|dc:38] (master) to chart the flood path, @Check[crafting|dc:38] (master) to reinforce a shelter, or @Check[athletics|dc:40] (expert) to quickly ferry people or supplies to higher ground
details.routine:
(5+ minutes, @Check[flat|dc:18]) The megatsunami's wave overtakes and crushes everything in its path. It rips buildings off their foundations and tears up trees by their roots. Prepared creatures have managed to reach an area of high ground or another form of safety, but unprepared creatures in the area are caught up in the flood and battered by a combination of water and debris.

Unprepared creatures take @Damage[(5d12+30)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:43|basic] save) when the megatsunami hits and are thereafter surrounded by water. If they can't breathe water, they must hold their breath or begin to drown. Each round, a creature can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:40] check to Swim up near the surface or to keep their head above water for the remainder of the minute once they reach the surface.

At the start of the next four minutes, the megatsunami has carried each unprepared character 250 feet inland. At this time, each unprepared character takes @Damage[(3d12+20)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save). A character who managed that minute to remain near the surface gains a result one degree of success better than their actual rolled result, with a critical success indicating they've managed to clamber out of the surge onto high ground and are now prepared.

At the end of the five minutes, attempt a @Check[flat|dc:18] to determine if a new wave repeats the routine. Characters already swept inland only become affected by these following waves once the rushing water reaches them, but until then they can Swim in the choppy floodwaters with a successful @Check[athletics|dc:30] check, potentially reaching safety or high ground before the next wave hits. Once a region is flooded by a megatsunami, it typically remains flooded for several hours.

stealth.details: (master) to realize that the glimmering orbs signal a dangerous trap.

details.description: Five globes shoot bolts of force up and down the hall when a second creature reaches its midpoint.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48] (master) to deactivate the trap entirely before it triggers, @Check[thievery|dc:45] (expert) to deactivate one of the glimmering orbs after the trap rolls initiative, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 41) to deactivate the trap entirely.
details.reset: The trap deactivates 1 round after it has no creatures to target and resets automatically after 1 minute.
details.routine: (5 actions) Five energized orbs each take one action to make a force bolt attack against a different target in the hallway (a single target cannot be fired at more than once per round by the trap). The trap doesn't take multiple attack penalties. Each orb that is disabled or destroyed reduces the trap's actions by 1.

description.value:
Trigger A second creature crosses a magical sensor at the midpoint of the hall.

Effect The doors at either end of the hall slam shut and lock in place while the trap is active (the doors open automatically if the trap is disabled or destroyed). The doors (Hardness 14, HP 56 [28 BT]) can be Forced Open with a @Check[athletics|dc:40|traits:action:force-open] check, or via a @Check[thievery|dc:35|traits:action:pick-a-lock] check to Pick the Lock. The trap rolls initiative.
(can target any creature in area A8)

details.description: Spirits of long-dead soldiers appear in the mist and attack intruders.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:45] (legendary) to scare off the soldiers or @Check[deception|dc:46] (legendary) to order the soldiers to stand down
details.reset: 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area

Effect Spirit soldiers lunge out of the mist and attack intruders. The spirit soldiers attempt a spear Strike against each creature within 30 feet.

details.description: A spirit locked in memories of its death flies down the hall, forcing intruders to experience the agony of its death.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:48] (master) to calm the spirit or @Check[occultism|dc:46] (master) to ward off the spirit
details.reset: 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area

Effect Creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} take @Damage[(6d12+41)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:40|basic]) as they experience the spirit's death.

details.description: A terrified soul releases an earsplitting scream.
details.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:44] (master) to comfort the spirit or @Check[occultism|dc:45] (master) to ward off the spirit
details.reset: 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area

Effect The spirit screams, dealing @Damage[(6d12+41)[sonic]] damage to all creatures in a @Template[cone|distance:30] (@Check[fortitude|dc:40|basic]).

details.description: A supernatural exhaustion fills creatures who climb the stairs.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:45] (legendary) to wedge crumbling stones into cracks to jam the collapsing stairs, @Check[thievery|dc:48] (master) to deface the hidden magical runes that power the trap; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank, counteract DC 36) to counteract a rune; three total successes are required to disable the trap
details.reset: The trap continues taking Leaden Limbs actions (and the void at the bottom of the stairwell remains open) for as long as creatures remain in the area. Once no creatures remain, the stairwell rebuilds itself, the void closes, and the trap resets.
details.routine: (2 actions) The haunt uses Leaden Limbs once, then Crumbling Stairs once.

description.value:
Trigger A creature inspects the wall at the top of the stairs or begins to descend the stairs after reaching the top

Effect The floor at the bottom of the stairwell disappears, revealing a seemingly bottomless pit. Meanwhile, the stairwell begins to collapse, starting from the top and working its way down. Any PC standing on the lower few steps falls into the pit unless they Grab the Edge with a successful @Check[reflex|dc:40] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

The trap drains vigor from creatures in the stairwell, making their limbs feel as heavy as lead. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] saving throw with the following results.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} until the end of their next turn.

Failure The creature's clumsy condition value increases by 1, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 4}. The creature is slowed 1 as long as they remain clumsy. The clumsy condition ends as soon as the creature exits the stairwell.

Critical Failure As failure, but the value increases by 2, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2}.

A stretch of stairs crumbles, dropping into the void at the bottom of the stairwell. The first time the trap takes this action, the upper third of the stairwell collapses. The second time, the middle third collapses. The third time, the entire stairwell collapses. A creature on a collapsing section of stairs can try to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] with a @Check[reflex|dc:40] save to avoid falling into the void. Flying creatures avoid the fall automatically but might tumble into the void anyway if their slowed condition prevents them from taking the Fly action on their turn.

Though it appears to stretch forever, the void at the bottom of the stairwell functions as a portal to the Crypt of Sloth. Creatures who plummet into the void experience a fall that seems to take hours, but are eventually deposited in the middle of area B10, taking @Damage[80[bludgeoning]] damage from the fall.

stealth.details: (master) to see the skull's lower jaws start to open

details.description: The smith's skull gazes with fiery light and breathes in a deep breath.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:48] (master) to hold the skull's jaw shut, or @Check[occultism|dc:45] or @Check[religion|dc:45] (expert) to quiet the Smith's spirit
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature who can see the Smith's skull approaches within 20 feet

Effect The skull's eyes flare. Each non-storykin creature within 60 feet who can see the skull is subjected to an overwhelming feeling of being sucked into it, and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:40|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature feels pain across its body, as if the skull's inhalation were pulling the skin from its bones. The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} and takes @Damage[8d6[mental]] damage.

Failure The creature is convinced that the skull just "breathed in" its skin, and it takes @Damage[16d6[mental]] damage. The overwhelming pain leaves the creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round as well.

Critical Failure As failure, but @Damage[24d6[mental]] damage and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3} from the pain.

details.description: A petrified creature momentarily animates to attack an adjacent creature.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Remove Animating Magic|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to remove the animating magic without triggering the trap, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 38) to counteract the animating magic

publication.title: Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands

details.reset: The magical energy that animates the statue builds up again over 24 hours and the trap resets.

description.value:
Trigger A living or undead creature moves within 5 feet of the stabbing sentinel

Effect The stabbing sentinel animates, making one Strike against the triggering creature.

On a critical hit, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Petrified] for 1 round. Additionally, if another stabbing sentinel has already been triggered, that sentinel immediately resets. If no other stabbing sentinels have been triggered, then another random statue in the room becomes infused with animating magic and becomes another stabbing sentinel hazard (to a maximum of four at a time).

details.description: Ebeshra aligns its crystalline form to produce a blast of lightning that charges the surrounding area with electrical energy.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:45|name:Balance the Electrical Charges|traits:divine,electricity,environmental,evocation,kaiju,hazard] (master) to balance the electrical charges in the area or @Check[deception|dc:48|name:Divert Ebeshra's Attention|traits:divine,electricity,environmental,evocation,kaiju,hazard] (expert) to momentarily divert Ebeshra's attention

publication.title: Pathfinder Lost Omens Monsters of Myth

details.reset: Ebeshra waits [[/gmr 1d4 #Lightning Blast Wait]] rounds before another blast.

description.value:
Trigger A creature takes a hostile action against Ebeshra, produces a visible area effect, or flies within 120 feet of Ebeshra

Effect Ebeshra unleashes a @Template[line|distance:240] of electricity toward the triggering creature. Creatures in the line take @Damage[16d8[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic|options:area-effect] save). The electricity creates a static field in all squares that the line passed through and all adjacent squares. For 1 round, any creatures that enter a space in the static field take @Damage[4d8[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:38|basic|name:Static Field|options:area-effect] save).

details.description: Mogaru's tail sweeps forth, causing an arc of devastation in a @Template[cone|distance:60].
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:45|name:Divert Mogaru's Attention] (legendary) or @Check[deception|dc:48|name:Divert Mogaru's Attention] (legendary) to momentarily divert Mogaru's attention
details.reset: Mogaru doesn't swat his tail again for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

description.value:
Trigger A creature takes a hostile action against Mogaru, produces a visible area effect, or flies within 120 feet of Mogaru

Effect Creatures in the area must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:40] save or take @Damage[(6d10+28)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is pushed 20 feet away. If flying, it descends 20 feet.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is pushed 40 feet, and descends 60 feet (if flying) or is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is pushed 60 feet away, descends 120 feet (if flying) or is knocked prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2}.

details.description: A blazing comet with three tails—one red, one black, and one with rainbow hues—careens throughout the area.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:48] (legendary) to grab one of the comet's tails and rip it free; @Check[occultism|dc:45] or @Check[religion|dc:45] (master) to exorcise the divine energy of the red tail (which is infused with Gorum's legacy) or the black tail (infused with the nihilism of Abaddon's Eclipse); @Check[arcana|dc:45] (master) to unweave the magic of the rainbow tail (Xanderghul's mythic power); or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 36) to counteract one of the tails. Each tail must be disabled separately to disable the hazard.
details.reset:
(1 action) The Trinity Star flies at a speed of 60 feet, moving through the spaces of as many creatures as possible. Each creature the Trinity Star moves through must make a @Check[reflex|dc:42] save with the following results.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[6d6[force]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[11d6[force]] damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[22d6[force]] damage, is knocked prone, and if the creature is mythic, they lose 1 Mythic Point. If the Trinity Star has lost any of its tails, it regrows one of them (chosen at random if it's lost two tails).

description.value:
Trigger Dream Aethusa has been dead for 10 minutes

Effect Dream Aethusa's body is consumed with mythic energy, transforming into a comet with three blazing tails. All creatures within 120 feet must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] save to resist becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 minute (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] permanently on a critical failure). The hazard then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (legendary) to distinguish between the hazard and the oozes within it; @Check[perception|dc:0|traits:hazard,complex,environmental] to notice the pool

details.description: A shallow, foul-smelling puddle of toxic acid blocks a narrow hall (marked with a dotted line on the map), and poison bursts from it when disturbed.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:40|name:Neutralize the acid|traits:hazard,complex,environmental] (master) or @Check[survival|dc:42|name:Neutralize the acid|traits:hazard,complex,environmental] (legendary) to neutralize the acid while within reach of it. Three successes using either skill (or a combination of both) destroys the hazard.
details.reset: The hazard ends after 1 minute, contracting to its original size and keeping still until disturbed again.
details.routine: (2 actions) The hazard uses its first action to spread 10 feet down the hallway in both directions. Once out of the hall, each side spreads in a random direction each round, but they don't go through doors. The hazard uses its second action to move the center of the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Toxic Cloud] up to 20 feet in any direction to which any part of the pool has line of effect (normally, to encompass the greatest number of characters). If the cloudkill isn't present, it instead uses its second action to produce a new cloudkill, as Poison Puff.

description.value: The area of the venom pool is difficult terrain, and each creature moving through it takes @Damage[10[acid]]{10 acid} for every square of its area that it moves into.

Trigger A creature moves through any square in the hazard's space

Effect The pool produces a 9th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Toxic Cloud]; (@Check[fortitude|dc:40|traits:death,necromancy,poison]) at its center. It then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to recognize the bars aren't just a passive defense; noticing the bars themselves has a DC of 0

details.description: Bars made of pure Void energy block access to the far side of the cell.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:45] (master) three times to deface the well-hidden focusing runes etched along the walls, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank; counteract DC 38) to counteract the void bars

publication.title: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

details.reset: The trap deactivates at the end of a round in which no living creatures are in the cell, then immediately resets at the start of the next round.
details.routine: (1 action) Each set of void bars manifests a long arm made of darkness that reaches out and attempts to Strike a creature in the room, favoring targets that previously damaged the bars or attempted to disable them, but otherwise choosing a target randomly from those available. This void talon can extend to attack any creature in the room or within 20 feet of the cell door in the outer hall, but targets in the outer hall gain the benefit of cover against this attack.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature comes within five feet of the void bars, or the void bars take damage (the Python does not trigger this hazard, and his presence suppresses this trigger)

Effect Both sets of void bars pulse with dark energy, blasting waves unlife into the area. All living creatures inside the cell or within 20 feet of the cell's entrance in the outer hall must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 2} on a critical failure; creatures in the outer hall gain a +2 circumstance bonus on this save). If at least one PC becomes drained by a void pulse, psychic alerts ripple through the Oubliette and the party accrues 1 Awareness Point. The void bars then roll initiative.

details.description: Anyone who opens a secret compartment is subjected to a powerful curse.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:39|name:Remove Bone Fetish|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to carefully remove the bone fetish that holds the curse.

publication.title: Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands

details.reset: The trap resets if both secret compartments are shut.

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens a secret compartment

Effect The creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:38] save or be subjected to the witch-priests' curse. Whenever a cursed creature takes at least 10 piercing or slashing damage from a single attack, it also takes @Damage[3d6[bleed]]. In addition, the cursed creature regains only half as many Hit Points as normal from all healing. The effect is permanent unless removed by magic.

stealth.details: (expert) to sense Zutha's hungry spirit before it manifests

details.description: The scowling visage of Runelord Zutha appears above the altar. Meanwhile, mask-like versions of Zutha's face manifest on nearby rubble and vomit swarms of maggots. Once the masks are destroyed, the air fills with spectral duplicates of Zutha's life-draining bone scythe.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:45] (legendary) or Religion (legendary) to exorcise Zutha's spirit, or @Check[intimidation|dc:48] (master) or @Check[performance|dc:48] (master) to recite the details of Zutha's defeat; six total successes are required to disable the haunt.
details.reset: The haunt deactivates once no living creatures remain nearby. The haunt resets 1 hour later.
details.routine: (4 actions) The haunt uses Vomit Maggot three times, then Poisonous Belch once. For each success at Disabling the haunt, the haunt loses one Vomit Maggot action. Destroying a mask also removes one of the haunt's Vomit Maggot actions. Once the haunt can no longer use Vomit Maggot, it immediately regains three actions and replaces Vomit Maggot with Spectral Scythe. Destroying a scythe also removes one of the haunt's Spectral Scythe actions. Once the haunt loses all three of its Spectral Scythe actions, the haunt is destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature attempts to descend the stairwell at area A1 a into the islet's interior

Effect A ghostly version of Zutha's face appears above the island. Meanwhile, the rubble on the islet warps into smaller versions of Zutha's face. The faces howl in rage as one. All creatures on the islet must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:42] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

One of Zutha's rubble-masks vomits a swarm of maggots. (The masks appear everywhere on the islet, and destroyed masks reform moments later, making nowhere on the islet safe.) The maggots burrow into the flesh of a creature of the GM's choice. The creature takes @Damage[2d12[persistent,piercing]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:42|basic] save) and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for as long as they continue taking this damage. When the persistent damage ends, the maggots dissolve into foul-smelling oil.

One of Zutha's scythes swipes at a creature of the GM's choice. The creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:42] save with the following results.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(3d10+10)[void]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(6d10+10)[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(12d10+10)[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2}.

The ghostly image of Zutha's face belches a toxic cloud that reeks of decaying flesh. The cloud fills a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] centered anywhere on the island and lasts until the haunt's next turn. Any creature that enters the area or that starts its turn in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:44] save with the following results.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[5d8[poison]|options:area-damage] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[10d8[poison]|options:area-damage] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[20d8[poison]|options:area-damage] damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2}, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for as long as it remains sickened.

details.description: An endless void stretches in all directions. Visions of Golarion's destruction roll like storm clouds across the space.
details.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:47] to Fly through the space without becoming stuck in the webs, @Check[athletics|dc:50] to Climb the webs and make progress forward through the infinite, @Check[nature|dc:47] or @Check[occultism|dc:47] or @Check[arcana|dc:50] or @Check[religion|dc:50] (legendary) to collapse the extradimensional space, or @Check[perception|dc:52] to peer through the visions of Golarion to notice discrepancies that help keep the overwhelming despair from setting in; six successes disables the hazard, causing it to vanish forever and shunting all creatures to Liralarue's Prison.
details.reset: The hazard resets automatically once no creatures are in the area.
details.routine:
(1 action) (arcane, emotion, illusion, mental) A stormfront filled with illusory scenes of Golarion's destruction rolls through the extradimensional space. Each creature in the hazard's area takes @Damage[6d12[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:42|basic|traits:arcane,emotion,illusion,mental] save). On a failed save, a creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Doomed]{Doomed 2} on a critical failure). A PC reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage is shunted back into area C7.

A creature that takes damage while Climbing the webs must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] save or fall. A creature can try to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] (@Check[reflex|dc:40]). On a success, they lose one success they've earned to escape the hazard but otherwise arrest their fall. On a failure, they plummet into the void, losing all Athletics successes they've earned to escape the hazard. Each round on their turn, the character can try to Grab an Edge again, but each time they fail to do so, the party must earn an additional success to escape the hazard. Characters who fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] or repeatedly fail to Grab an Edge might effectively become lost in the void, though once the nightmare hazard is disabled, they appear along with the rest of the PCs in Liralarue's Prison.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the shaft leading to area C8

Effect Storm clouds roiling with gouts of fire, bolts of lightning, and the wails of uncountable souls form in the distance. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: The trapdoor's metal is infused with a deadly toxin that seeps out when the trap is triggered.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:50|name:Gum Up Trapdoor|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to gum up the trapdoor's porous metal surface, @Check[crafting|dc:45|name:Render Toxin Inert] (master) to render the toxin inert, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 45) to counteract the trap's magic

publication.title: Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege

description.value:
Trigger A creature opens the door without first whispering the name Blackfingers

Effect The metal trapdoor emits toxic vapors. Each creature within 30 feet must save against a potent strain of Blackfinger Blight.

Each round, at the beginning of their turn, a creature affected by blackfinger blight must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] check or drop one random item they're holding

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:40]

Onset 1 round

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[10d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] (1 round)

stealth.details: (master) or @Check[perception|dc:50] (legendary) to notice the bypass button hidden in Urgathoa's skeletal feet

details.description: Magic forces those who pass by the statue to the ground with sudden force; pressing the bypass button deactivates the trap for 1 minute.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:47] (legendary) to deactivate the trap through prayer, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 47) to counteract the trap, or press the bypass button
details.reset: The trap resets automatically if triggered, after 1 minute if the bypass button is pressed, or after 15 minutes if disabled or counteracted.

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes by the statue

Effect The trap deals @Damage[(7d12+40)[force]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:46|basic]). On a failed save, the creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], and on a critical failure, the creature is knocked prone and takes an additional @Damage[1d12[bludgeoning]] damage from the force of the fall.

details.description: Deadbolts inside the doors clunk as three glowing runes appear on the ceiling.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:42], @Check[nature|dc:42], @Check[occultism|dc:42], or @Check[religion|dc:42] to erase the runes, or @Check[crafting|dc:46] or @Check[thievery|dc:46] to destroy the runes, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank, counteract DC 41); each rune requires a success to remove (three runes total)
details.reset: 1 minute after the hazard activates, the deadbolts unlock and the hazard resets.
details.routine: (3 actions) The cleansing suite activates one of its runes. The hazard can't activate the same rune more than once per turn. Disabling a rune removes one of the hazard's actions and prevents the hazard from activating that rune.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the cleansing suite's eastern doors

Effect The doors to the chamber slam shut and seal with deadbolts. The hazard rolls initiative.
The hazard fills the chamber with holy energy that deals @Damage[(6d6+20)[spirit]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:41|basic] save) to creatures with the unholy trait.
The hazard casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank, counteract [[/r 1d20+31 #Counteract]]{+31}) that targets a spell or effect on each creature in the chamber.

The hazard casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Detect Scrying]. If it detects a scrying effect in the chamber, it casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Hidden Mind] on each affected creature and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] on the effect (9th rank, counteract [[/r 1d20+31 #Counteract]]{+31}).

stealth.details: (legendary) or DC 47 to notice the dagger left in the pool start to move on its own.

details.description: An elven woman wearing a robe stands in the water. She beckons anyone who enters the room to join her in the pool.
details.disable: @Check[deception|dc:45] (legendary) to pretend to be Erveniss and ask for forgiveness, @Check[diplomacy|dc:45] (legendary) to convince Damurdiel to stand down (elves who are masters in Diplomacy can attempt the check as if they were legendary instead), or @Check[religion|dc:40] (legendary) to call upon Calistria to calm Damurdiel's spirit.
details.reset: Damurdiel's spirit subsides once she is destroyed or if she manages to reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points. She returns after 1 minute.
details.routine: (3 actions) The haunt attempts to Strike with the dagger.

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 5 feet of the pool's edge

Effect The ghostly elf causes a dagger to rise out of the waters and then fling toward the triggering creature. If the attack is a hit, it is a critical hit instead. The haunt then rolls initiative.

hp.details: The mirror can't be damaged while any mirror duplicate is alive

stealth.details: (master) to notice it isn't a regular mirror

details.description:
A magic mirror replaces characters with evil mirror duplicates from another dimension.

The mirror can't be damaged while any mirror duplicate is alive
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Retrieve a Creature] (legendary) to retrieve a creature from the other dimension within 10 minutes of the switch (possible only if their mirror duplicate is dead), @Check[thievery|dc:45|name:Disable the Mirror] (master) to permanently disable the mirror once all mirror duplicates are dead, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 37) to counteract the mirror for 1 minute and prevent additional replacements during that time
details.reset: The mirror is always ready to absorb creatures into the other dimension. Ten minutes after a creature is sucked into the mirror, if an ally doesn't rescue the creature with Thievery, it reaches the other dimension, where it might be captured or killed. In the mirror dimension, it counts as a mirror duplicate, so the denizens of the other dimension can't destroy the mirror on their side while the absorbed creature is there. These dimensions are alternate realities, not planes, so even rituals like plane shift can't reach them.
details.routine: (1 action) The mirror absorbs another reflected creature into the mirror and replaces it with a mirror duplicate. Mirror duplicates attack on their own initiative, using the same statistics as the original creature, but with an evil alignment (changing only abilities that shift with the alignment change). A mirror duplicate can spend 3 actions in contact with the mirror to return to its original dimension and release the creature it duplicated, but most mirror duplicates prefer not to.

description.value:
Trigger A non-evil creature is reflected in the mirror

Effect The mirror absorbs the creature into the mirror, replacing it with an evil mirror duplicate (@Check[reflex|dc:41] to avoid being absorbed into the mirror), and rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (expert) to notice the softly glowing magic circle shimmering from under the moldering sheets; noticing the circle if the sheets are pulled aside has a DC of 0

details.description: A softly flickering circle of runes pulses and periodically distorts the air above it before a raw red wound in reality splits open to disgorge demons and lash out with tendrils of acidic blood.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:47] to erase key runes from the circle, defeat all four summoned demons in combat, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank, counteract DC 36)
details.reset: The distortion circle deactivates (and any summoned demons vanish) at the end of any round in which no viable targets for the tongue to attack are in area C10. The trap then immediately resets (and is ready to summon a new batch of four demons).
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the trap extends a tongue-like appendage covered with sharp teeth. The tongue snaps like a whip, firing a volley of sharp teeth at a non-demon target in the room. The trap can precisely sense the location of all viable targets in the room, and teeth fired from the tongue penetrate the many wooden shelves (although a character with at least one shelf between it and the trap gains greater cover).

description.value:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the distortion circle

Effect The distortion circle begins glowing with red light, shining through any cloth covering it might still have over it, and then summons four seraptis demons into the room. The demons appear in pairs at the entrances to the alcoves to the left and right of the distortion circle's alcove, and they then roll initiative. The demons do not pursue foes from this room, and vanish if the trap resets or if the trap is disabled. The summoned demons can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures. The distortion circle then appears to open into a bloody toothy mouth and rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) or @Check[perception|dc:50|traits:secret] (legendary) to notice the bypass button hidden in a skull's eye socket

details.description: A warded metal gate electrocutes anyone who touches the gate without pressing the bypass button.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:47] (legendary) to destroy the ward, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 47) to counteract the ward, or press the bypass button
details.reset: The trap resets automatically if triggered, after 1 minute if the bypass button is pressed, or after 15 minutes if disabled or counteracted.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the gate directly or with a tool

Effect The trap deals @Damage[(8d10+40)[electricity]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:46|basic]).

stealth.details: (master) to realize the fungus will harm its prisoner if it is tampered with; noticing the fungus itself has a DC of 0

details.description: A mass of hundreds of long thin fang-shaped fungal growths protruding from the wall encase and pierce the flesh of an elven prisoner.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:41] three times to delicately cut the elf free without causing the fungus to react, or @Check[medicine|dc:47] two times to gently manipulate the fungus loose from where it's tendrils have penetrated flesh, or @Check[athletics|dc:50] to swiftly wrench the elf free from the cage before the fungus can react

publication.title: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

details.reset: A fang fungus cage resets instantly.

description.value:
Trigger A fungus cage takes damage, or any magical effect targets a caged elf

Effect The tendrils constricting and growing through an elf swiftly lash, constrict, and burrow deep. An @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious] elf is killed by this, while a conscious elf is rendered unconscious.

details.description: A ring of dead trees covered in dry fungus suddenly shakes and rattles as if something inside some of the trees were about to explode.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:45] twice to appeal to spirits of the natural world still lingering in the area and ask them to neutralize the infestation, @Check[survival|dc:39] four times to gently break open four key tree trunks in the colony to allow the spores to vent harmlessly, or @Check[thievery|dc:48] to trigger a chain reaction among the logs that forces the hazard to implode dramatically but harmlessly. Once the hazard is triggered, only the Nature check can disable it.

publication.title: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

details.reset: 24 hours after the fungal colonies erupt, the hazard reactivates and can trigger again. If the warsworns are activated and destroyed, they do not appear again on a subsequent triggering.
details.routine:
(1 action; poison, primal) The 120-foot-tall plume of spores turn toxic. Each round, the spore cloud's highest point drops by 40 feet, and it expands outward from the initial perimeter at A2 by 20 feet on the ground and at a height of 10 feet. At the end of the third round, the spore cloud stabilizes as a ten-foot-high ring with a width of 45 feet, which completely fills the clearing with ease. This spore cloud doesn't obscure vision, but creatures that end their turn in it take @Damage[5d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:41|basic|traits:poison,primal] save).

On round 4, the spore cloud settles to the ground on the hazard's action. If the hazard hasn't yet been disabled, the ground between areas A1 and A2 erupts, and a trio of fungus-encrusted warsworns, their bodies composed of ancient elven skeletons bound together by glistening filaments of mold, rises up to attack all intruders in the area. At this point, the hazard deactivates, but the warsworns fight until they're destroyed.

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature enters the hazardous ring or attempts to fly over it at a height of less than 120 feet

Effect The ring of fallen trees explodes upward to a height of 120 feet. All creatures in the ring or within the area 120 feet above it take @Damage[8d6[piercing]] damage and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:41] save. The fungal colony then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The target takes full damage, is sickened 1, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 minute as spores settle on their bodies and quickly grow into rigid, interlocking plates. A creature can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] the shells (DC 41) to end the slowed 1 effect early.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] as they become almost completely encased in a hard fungal shell until they roll to Escape (DC 41), at which point they are slowed 1 for an additional minute until they Escape a second time.

stealth.details: (legendary) to notice the fungi is potentially hazardous and that the two mushrooms in the middle are load bearing and that destroying those with damage can disable the hazard; noticing the engine itself has a DC of 0

details.description: A thick mass of pulsing fungi and mold infested with ghostly shapes cakes the walls of the room.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:41] seven times to seek out and remove seven specific nodes of fungi that govern the intake and dispersal of energy, or @Check[religion|dc:47] three times to exorcise the souls out of the engine and force it to shut down

publication.title: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

details.reset: The fungal engine deactivates and resets after 1 minute, but any demons called in remain in the area for an hour to ensure the intruders don't return.
details.routine:
(1 action) The fungal engine expels clouds of toxic spores on its turn, causing all non-fungi and non-demon creatures in the room to take @Damage[10d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:41|basic] save); any creature that takes damage is exposed to grayshroud.

Grayshroud

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:40]

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued] (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} and fatigued (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 2} and fatigued (1 day)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 3}, fatigued, and can't speak (1 day)

Stage 5 death

description.value:
Trigger any damage is inflicted on the engine, or any attempt to disable it critically fails

Effect The entire mass of fungi shudders, then emits a piercing psychic scream as the trapped souls within cry out. All non-fungi creatures in area E3 take @Damage[10d6[spirit]] damage (@Check[will|dc:41|basic] save), and four vansidieth demons waiting for this precise alarm use translocate to teleport into the room to attack the intruders. The hazard then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: A fifteen-foot diameter spherical fungus with a five-foot-diameter circular aperture facing into the room; inside the fungus lie partially decayed humanoid remains.

publication.title: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

details.reset: A fungal forge resets immediately at the start of every round, and immediately triggers again if there are still sapient creatures within it.

description.value:
Trigger A sapient living creature enters or is placed inside the fungal forge, a fungal forge takes damage, or an attempt to disable a forge critically fails

Effect The fungal forge floods its interior with sickly green demonic energy that looks like a mix of fluid and fog. A creature within the fungal forge takes 5d8 variable damage and 5d8 void damage as their soul withers and their flesh decays, while a creature within 20 feet of the forge is exposed to vented unholy energy and only takes the void damage as their flesh rots—in either case, an affected creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:41] save. The type of non-void damage a fungal forge inflicts on those within varies. The green forge (D6a) deals cold damage, the yellow one (D6b) poison damage, the orange one (D6c) acid damage, the black one (D6d) electricity damage, and the purple one (D6e) mental damage. Demons and fungi are immune to this effect entirely.

Green: @Damage[5d8[cold],5d8[void]]

Yellow: @Damage[5d8[poison],5d8[void]]

Orange: @Damage[5d8[acid],5d8[void]]

Black: @Damage[5d8[electricity],5d8[void]]

Purple: @Damage[5d8[mental],5d8[void]]

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage. If the creature is inside the fungal forge, they also become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} with an unlimited duration.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage. If the creature is inside the fungal forge, they also become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} with an unlimited duration.

hp.details: Hardness 10 in the guthallath's erosion aura

stealth.details: (master) to notice the odd aging of the stones, seemingly far older and more weathered than the surrounding rocks

details.description: The rock walls nearby begin to crumble and collapse.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:48|name:Buttress the Rocks|traits:environmental] (legendary) to buttress the collapsing rocks in three different locations. Using large and durable materials (or applicable spells) to support the rocks reduces the check to @Check[crafting|dc:45|name:Buttress the Rocks|traits:environmental] (master).
details.reset: The guthallath can reset the hazard with a few days of work stacking the fallen rocks.
details.routine:
(2 actions) On the hazard's first action, any areas of crumbling rocks from a previous round collapse, making a falling rocks Strike against each creature in the area.

On its second action, the hazard crumbles rocks in a 20-foot radius around the previous collapse. A character can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Seek] (DC 40) to determine where the rocks will fall on the following round, but on a critical failure, they incorrectly identify where the rocks will fall.

description.value:
Trigger The guthallath rolls initiative

Effect The rocks of the area begin to crumble. The hazard rolls for initiative.

Trigger The hazard takes 30 or more damage

Effect Move the hazard's initiative to immediately follow the creature that damaged the hazard.

hp.details: The mirror can't be damaged while any mirror duplicate is alive

stealth.details: (legendary) to notice that the mirror isn't a regular mirror

details.description: This magic mirror replaces characters with evil duplicates from another dimension.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:42|name:Retrieve a Creture from the Mirror|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to retrieve a creature from the mirror within 10 minutes of the switch (possible only if their mirror duplicate is dead), @Check[thievery|dc:47|name:Permanently Disable the Mirror|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to permanently disable the mirror once all mirror duplicates are dead, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 40) to counteract the mirror for 1 minute and prevent additional replacements from appearing during that time.

publication.title: Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands

details.reset: The mirror is always ready to absorb creatures into another dimension. 10 minutes after a creature is sucked into the mirror, if an ally doesn't rescue the creature using the Thievery skill, the creature reaches the mirror dimension, where it could be captured or killed. In the mirror dimension, it counts as a mirror duplicate, so the denizens of the other dimension can't destroy the mirror on their side while the absorbed creature is there. These dimensions are alternate realities, not planes, so even rituals like @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Interplanar Teleport] can't reach them.
details.routine:
(1 action) The mirror absorbs another reflected creature into the mirror (@Check[reflex|dc:42]) and replaces it with a mirror duplicate. Mirror duplicates act on their own initiative, using the same statistics as the original creature, but with an evil alignment (changing only abilities that shift with the alignment change).

Mirror duplicates are legendary in Intimidation and are permanently @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Quickened], but they can use this additional action only to Demoralize.

A mirror duplicate can spend 3 actions in contact with the mirror to return to its original dimension and release the creature it duplicated, but most mirror duplicates prefer not to.

description.value:
Trigger A non-evil creature is reflected in the mirror

Effect The mirror absorbs the creature into the mirror, replacing it with an evil mirror duplicate (@Check[reflex|dc:42] to avoid being absorbed into the mirror), and rolls initiative.

details.description: A mass of brushes use magical paint to convert creatures into art.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:41|name:Crafting Disable] (master) to mix the paints into bland, useless colors, @Check[thievery|dc:41|name:Thievery Disable] (legendary) to dismantle the animated brushes, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 41) to counteract the magic within the paint.

publication.title: Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege

description.value:
Trigger A creature steps into the same space as the paints

Effect The brushes animate and attempt to paint all creatures within 30 feet. The creatures must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:41] save to avoid the brushes and splashes from paint cans or be converted into living artwork for 1 hour.

A creature that becomes living artwork is immediately bound to the nearest flat surface of its size or larger within 30 feet. The creature can move only along flat surfaces such as floors and walls. This allows it to end its movement wrapped around corners when on a continuous surface.

While bound to a surface, the creature can attack from its space as normal, including against other creatures bound to the same surface.

If the surface it's on is destroyed (such as a portrait hit by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Fireball] spell or a wall being demolished), the creature takes @Damage[10d6[untyped]] damage and is shunted to the nearest flat surface. If there is no flat surface within 5 feet, the creature is destroyed.

details.description: A psychic scream disorients creatures in the area.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:41|name:Suppress the Magic|traits:magical,trap] to suppress the magic or @Check[thievery|dc:41|name:Scratch Out the Ward|traits:magical,trap] to scratch out the ward

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the marked area

Effect A psychic wail affects all creatures within 10 feet of the marked area, requiring them to attempt a @Check[will|dc:41] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] for 1 round, then stunned 2 when the paralysis ends.

Critical Failure The target is paralyzed for 4 rounds. At the end of each of its turns, it can attempt a new Will save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round, or end it entirely on a critical success.

details.description:
Impact from a sufficiently large asteroid would be powerful enough to wipe out a region or destroy a planet, but smaller-scale impact events, like from a meteor shower, present hazards that could be survived. This shower might arise naturally, or it might be the result of the PCs or other forces preventing a greater calamity by breaking a world-rending asteroid into smaller pieces.

Anticipate @Check[astronomy-lore|dc:41] (master) or @Check[nature|dc:45] (legendary) to notice signs of the approach of unusual celestial bodies

Prepare @Check[astronomy-lore|dc:40] (master) or @Check[nature|dc:50] (legendary) to predict the trajectory of the falling asteroids and determine safe zones, @Check[athletics|dc:45] (legendary) or @Check[crafting|dc:47] (master) to dig underground shelters, @Check[diplomacy|dc:45] (master) or @Check[intimidation|dc:47] (master) to coordinate an orderly evacuation
details.routine: (1+ minutes, @Check[flat|dc:17]) Small asteroids rain down across the sky, battering and incinerating those caught in their path before carving craters. Over the course of a minute, enough asteroids smash into the ground and explode to inflict @Damage[6d10[bludgeoning],14d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage to any unprepared characters in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:44|basic] save). The @Template[type:burst|distance:40]{40-foot-radius} craters left behind are greater difficult terrain.

details.description: Sudden invasive emotions of fear, paranoia, and anger well up in the mind, causing companions to take on sinister casts to their no-longer familiar features.
details.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:47] (legendary) to shout down the emotional energies and exert mental dominance over them, or @Check[thievery|dc:44] (master) to cause the trap's energies to discharge harmlessly

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature ends its turn inside of area B7

Effect The trap releases its built-up emotional energy in a silent blast of power. All creatures in area B7 or within 20 feet of the shed's exterior walls must attempt a @Check[will|dc:41] save (creatures within the shed take a -2 circumstance penalty to this save).

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success For 1 hour, the creature treats no other creatures as allies and can't take concentrate actions that don't have the rage trait.

Failure As success, but the effect is permanent.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature must attack any creature it detects, preferring to attack former friends or allies when possible.

stealth.details: (legendary) to recognize the symbols as a magical trap

details.description: Magical letters reveal phrases in Aklo that, if not properly spoken aloud, inflict numbing cold.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:47] (master) or @Check[religion|dc:49] (master) three times in a row to successfully speak aloud the esoteric phrases as they appear (this causes the trap to deactivate for 1 hour, although the phrases continue to appear during this time), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank, counteract DC 38).
details.reset: The trap deactivates 1 round after it has no target creatures and resets after 1 minute.
details.routine: (1 action) The trap fills the entirety of area B4 with additional blasts of cold. All creatures in area B4 take @Damage[12d6[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:41|basic], plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] for 1 minute on a critical failure).

description.value:
Trigger A creature spends more than 1 round in the room without speaking aloud the phrases appearing in the alcove.

Effect The creature takes @Damage[12d6[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:41|basic], plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] for 1 minute on a critical failure). The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (legendary) to notice the network is hazardous; noticing the rifts and mushrooms themselves has a DC of 0

details.description: A six-foot-tall green mushroom with red spots on its cap is connected to seven rifts in surrounding alcoves by tendrils of vapor.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:50] once to delicately cut out or destroy the seven points on the mushroom that connect to the rifts, @Check[religion|dc:41] seven times (once per rift) to exorcise the demonic influence, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Planar Seal] (10th rank, counteract DC 39) to counteract the planar link in this room

publication.title: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

details.reset: The network deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any creature moving in area D4 and without any attempt to disable it.
details.routine: (7 actions) Each round, each of the seven rifts fires a beam of demonic energy into the room, targeting one creature (regardless of alliance to Tanglebriar) at random. A single creature cannot be targeted by more than one rift beam per round, so if there are fewer than seven potential targets, the rift portal network loses any remaining actions for that round.

description.value:
Trigger the mushroom takes damage, any of the seven rifts are touched, or any attempt to disable the network critically fails

Effect The demonic energies within each of the seven rifts take note, and the cacophonous sound of howling fiends fills the room. All creatures in area D4 must attempt a @Check[will|dc:41|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The hazard then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: Lines of searing lava lance through the area, causing targets to shake as if caught in an earthquake and potentially become petrified.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43|name:Disable the Wards|traits:magical,trap] (master) to disable the wards or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 38) to counteract the ward.
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the marked area

Effect Fiery spears make one Strike against each living creature within 5 feet of the marked area.

A creature struck by a seismic spear is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 3} for 1 round as their body shakes uncontrollably. On a critical hit, a target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:39|options:inflicts:petrified] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Petrified] for 1 minute, or permanently on a critical failure.
No multiple attack penalty

details.description: A formless spirit drains life from the living, becoming progressively visible as its victims weaken.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:48] (expert) or @Check[religion|dc:48] (expert) to weaken the spirit; four total successes are required to disable the haunt
