details.reset: After 1 minute, the spirit is satiated, and the haunt deactivates as it returns to a resting state. Over the course of 1 hour, the spirit's siphoned life energy disperses and the haunt resets.
details.routine:
(1 action; death, divine, necromancy, void) The spirit inhales, siphoning the souls of the living and becoming more visible. Each creature within @Template[burst|distance:60]{60 feet} takes @Damage[(4d10+20)[void]] damage, with a @Check[fortitude|dc:41|traits:death,divine,necromancy,void,damaging-effect] save. If no creatures are within the area, the haunt moves up to 120 feet into the largest concentration of living creatures and then inhales.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and increases its @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained] value by 1, to a maximum of drained 4.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and increases its drained value by 2, to a maximum of drained 4. If the creature was already drained 4, it dies.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature passes within 15 feet of the spirit

Effect The invisible spirit latches onto the life essence of the living, dealing @Damage[(4d10+20)[void]] damage to all living creatures within @Template[burst|distance:60]{60 feet} (@Check[fortitude|dc:41|basic] save). The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: A dozen spears hidden within sockets in the walls thrust into the hall when the pressure plate is triggered.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:47] (master) to disable the pressure plate

description.value:
Trigger Pressure is applied to the pressure plate

Effect A dozen spears extend into the hall, making one spear Strike against each creature in the hall, up to a total of 12 spear Strikes.

details.description: Blood sprays from clock's straight razor hands, forming a large set of oversized blood red razors that slice out to hew at flesh and bone.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:47] once on the clock's hands and once on its face to decouple both of the magical sensors in the trap from each other, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank, counteract DC 39) to counteract the trap
details.reset: The Skinclock resets automatically.

description.value:
Trigger An attempt to Pick the Lock on the Skinclock fails, or any attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Force Open] the wall or to damage it occurs

Effect A pair of bloody straight razors form and then slash forward to scissor at the triggering creature. The blades attempt to Strike the triggering creature before they vanish.

details.description: Four spirit collectors drain the souls from nearby creatures.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:47] (legendary) to sever the connection between Urgathoa and each spirit collector, @Check[thievery|dc:50] (legendary) to deactivate each spirit collector, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 50) to counteract each spirit collector
details.routine: (4 actions) This trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled spirit collector. Each spirit collector uses 1 action to drain the souls of all creatures within 30 feet, dealing @Damage[(4d10+20)[force]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:46|basic]). On a failed save, a creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} or increases their drained and stupefied conditions by 1 (by 2 on a critical failure).

description.value:
Trigger A creature damages a spirit collector

Effect The damaged spirit collector releases a wave of force to repel the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[(4d10+20)[force]] damage to the triggering creature, who must attempt a Reflex save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is pushed back 5 feet.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is pushed back 10 feet, and lands @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is pushed back 20 feet, and lands prone.

details.description:
Volcanic eruptions are dramatic events in which magma erupts from a planet's mantle onto its surface. They're often preceded by earthquakes and the opening of fissures in the ground that vent toxic gases. These initial signs can occur months or even years in advance, making it difficult to predict the eruption's timing. While the intensity, features, and progression of eruptions vary, this hazard represents the most dangerous variety of eruption, one that can swiftly overtake cities with rapid tides of superheated gas and rock and spew out enough ash to affect the climate for years. When these sorts of eruptions are also supervolcanos, eruptions with the highest degree of explosive force, they cause unparalleled transformation to the landscape and alter climate on a planetary scale.

If you're looking for something more supernatural, it's a relatively simple change to adjust the energy damage caused by a supervolcano's pyroclastic flow to something else—for example, a cloud of ghosts erupting from a long-lost city might inflict mental, spirit, or void damage instead of the fire or electricity damage a normal pyroclastic flow brings. Just be sure to give the new event a suitable description that matches the new type of damage!

Anticipate @Check[mountain-lore|dc:40] (master), @Check[survival|dc:42] (master), or @Check[nature|dc:45] (legendary) to predict the eruption's arrival

Prepare @Check[mountain-lore|dc:42] (master), @Check[survival|dc:45] (master), or @Check[nature|dc:48] (legendary) to determine evacuation paths, @Check[medicine|dc:45] (master) to safely treat and relocate people poisoned by toxic gases in the preceding days, @Check[athletics|dc:48] (master) to relocate people and essential supplies, or @Check[crafting|dc:48] (legendary) to create filtration systems and barricades capable of withstanding a pyroclastic flow
details.routine:
(24 hours) When the hazard begins, toxic gas vents open throughout the affected region, followed by clouds of ash. As the eruption column collapses, material rushes forth from the volcano in a pyroclastic flow, which is followed by a dense ash cloud that persists for several minutes. Ash and toxic gas persist for up to 24 hours.

Ash limits visibility like a thick fog, deals @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage per minute, and creates difficult terrain as it collects on the ground. The ash also generates bolts of ash lightning. Once per minute, one unprepared PC takes @Damage[3d12[electricity]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:46|basic] save). Pockets of dense ash are particularly dangerous. Unprepared creatures in areas of dense ash must hold their breath or begin to suffocate.

When the pyroclastic flow first hits a region, creatures in its path must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:43] save with the following results (prepared creatures increase their roll by one degree of success).

Critical Success The creature avoids most of the pyroclastic flow, but still takes @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage and treats all movement in the area as greater difficult terrain for the next 10 minutes.

Success The creature takes @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage, and is buried in ash and debris. While buried, they can't breathe and take @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage each round at the start of their turn until they succeed at a DC 40 check to Escape, in addition to the normal @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage each minute for being buried.

Failure As success, but @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage, and [[/act escape dc=42]].

Critical Failure As success, but @Damage[12d6[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[12d6[fire]] damage, and [[/act escape dc=46]].

details.description: The House at the Edge of Time folds time and space back on the target, who teleports painfully into their own body an instant before they travel.
details.disable: This trap cannot be disabled as long as Nyrissa lives, but if she is slain or forgiven, the teleport trap vanishes.

description.value:
Trigger A non-native of Thousandbreaths attempts to take a teleportation action that either begins or ends in any area within the House at the Edge of Time

Effect The House redirects the magic to cause the creature that attempted the Teleportation action to instead teleport into themselves a split second before they attempted the action in the first place. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:41] save. A PC under the protection of the Helix Theorem gains their +4 status bonus to this saving throw, and treats all Critical Failure results as Failure results instead.

Critical Success The action functions normally.

Success The action fails but doesn't harm anyone.

Failure The action fails, and the creature that attempted it (but not any other creature targeted by the action) teleports into themselves, suffering a sudden and agonizing pain, taking @Damage[(4d10 + 40)[bludgeoning]] damage, @Damage[4d10[mental]] damage, and becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.

Critical Failure As failure, but affecting all creatures that would have been affected by the attempted action, and the creature that attempted the action instead takes @Damage[80[bludgeoning]] damage, @Damage[40[mental]] damage, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 4}.

details.description: The Avalanche rests as a gem on a stone pedestal, continually unleashing natural disasters in the nearby area.
details.disable: Two @Check[athletics|dc:41] or @Check[survival|dc:43] checks to approach the pedestal, resisting the quaking earth and other hazards and eventually holding strong enough to take the gem from the pedestal; a creature who can approach the pedestal without touching the ground, such as by jumping or flying, can simply snatch the gem and end the Avalanche's assault

publication.title: Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds

details.routine:
(1 action) The Avalanche releases its fury at a point within 120 feet, affecting all creatures within its area. Each creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:41] save to hold their ground or avoid the incoming danger. The amount and type of damage dealt are based on the nature of the natural disaster the Avalanche unleashes, and certain types of disasters impose additional effects. The Avalanche can't unleash the same type of disaster twice in a row. The sturzstromer is immune to these effects.

Earthquake The ground in a @Template[burst|distance:20] violently shakes and launches rubble in the area, dealing @Damage[(3d12+20)[bludgeoning]] damage. On a failure, the creature also stumbles back 5 feet, away from the Avalanche. On a critical failure, the creature stumbles back 10 feet and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone].

Fire Rain A hail of burning rock rains down in a @Template[burst|distance:20], dealing @Damage[(3d12+20)[fire]] damage. Creatures that fail their save catch on fire and take @Damage[2d6[persistent,fire]] damage (double on a critical failure).

Landslide A mass of rubble falls in a @Template[burst|distance:20], dealing @Damage[9d8[bludgeoning]] damage. Creatures that critically fail their save become trapped under this rubble as if it were an avalanche, except a creature can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] from beneath the rubble (DC 40).

Lava Channel A burst of molten rock violently spews forth in a @Template[line|distance:30|width:10]{30‑foot‑long and 10‑foot‑wide line}, dealing @Damage[9d8[fire]] damage. Creatures that fail their Reflex save are swept 15 feet away by the lava (30 feet on a critical failure). The lava cools almost instantly, allowing creatures to walk over its path immediately after resolving the effects of the disaster.

Powerful Winds A massive burst of wind travels in a @Template[line|distance:60], kicking up dust and dealing @Damage[(3d12+20)[slashing]] damage. Creatures that fail their Reflex save become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round and also fall prone on a critical failure.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs reach the area depicted on the Trial of the Avalanche map

Effect The Avalanche shudders, causing a thunderous boom to fill the area. All creatures without the Earth trait in the area must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:39] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3} on a critical failure). The Avalanche then rolls initiative.

details.description: The Eclipse is located high in the sky, manifesting as an eclipsed sun.
details.disable: Reduce the Eclipse to 0 HP, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank; counteract DC 39) to counteract the Eclipse (once it's in range)

publication.title: Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds

details.routine: (1 action) The Eclipse heals @Damage[50[healing]] points of damage, retreats upward from PCs if any have approached (as determined by its Distance Score), then unleashes a meteor down on the island below. The meteor explodes upon striking the island, creating a @Template[burst|distance:30] that inflicts @Damage[8d6[fire],4d6[bludgeoning]]{8d6 fire damage and 4d6 bludgeoning damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:41|basic|traits:damaging-effect] save) to all creatures caught in the blast. The eclipse targets these strikes to catch as many PCs in the blast as it can each time—while it doesn't seek to damage a bul‑gae, it won't worry about catching one or both in the area of effect if that spot is the most advantageous to striking the most possible PCs.

description.value:
Trigger The PCs arrive at the island

Effect A fiery point of light appears in the sky at the center of the Eclipse, swiftly growing in size as it becomes apparent that a meteor is hurtling toward the island. The Eclipse rolls initiative.

details.description: A large metallic arm, 20 feet long, is set into the floor. It rotates around the room to wind up nearby clockwork creatures.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:50|name:Deactivate System|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to deactivate the system, or @Check[athletics|dc:40|name:Jam Gears] (legendary) to jam the gears for 1 round

publication.title: Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale

details.reset: The Winder resets as soon as no creatures remain in the room.
details.routine: (1, 2, or 4 actions, depending on the encounter's threat level) The Winder uses each of its actions to Wind clockwork assassins in the room, starting with inactive ones.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than the Rumormonger enters the room, or the Rumormonger commands the device to activate

Effect The Winder rolls initiative.

The Winder winds a clockwork creature in the room. If the creature is currently inactive, this activates the creature, allowing it to act normally for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. If already active, this instead increases the number of rounds the clockwork creature remains active by 1d4.

details.description: A wickedly sharp saw blade descends and travels along grooves in a complex path throughout the room, attempting to decapitate everyone within.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:41|name:Jam Saw Blade|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) at four different junctions to jam all the saw blade's possible paths, preventing it from traveling through the room
details.reset: The trap resets over the course of the round and can be triggered again 1 round later.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to exit the room.

Effect The saw blade travels along its path, making one Strike against each creature in the room, twisting and varying its height for a maximum chance of beheading its targets.
No multiple attack penalty
On a critical hit, a target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:39|traits:damaging-effect] save or be decapitated, dying instantly unless it can survive without a head.

stealth.details: (legendary) to notice the sphere is hazardous; noticing the sphere itself has a DC of 0

details.description: A sphere of tangled branches and roots, encrusted with fungus and mold, suddenly animates and reaches out several claw-like limbs.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:50] to exorcise the corrupted spirit trapped within the Witchbole's Heart, or @Check[thievery|dc:47] three times to deactivate the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] runes scribed in the sphere that allow the corrupted spirit to animate limbs (each one of these successful Thievery checks reduce the hazard's routine by 1 action)

publication.title: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

details.reset: The Witchbole's Heart deactivates and resets automatically 1 minute after no targets remain.
details.routine: (3 actions) The Witchbole's Heart forms several long, thin, spiky limbs tipped with poisonous talons. On each of its actions, it attempts to Strike one non-demon creature in reach of the central sphere. The Witchbole's Heart does can't attempt to Strike a single target more than once per round.

description.value:
Trigger a non-demon creature ends their turn in area D3, or a demon in area D3 issues a command to the Witchbole's Heart to awaken

Effect A distorted arboreal's face forms on the side of the Witchbole's Heart and issues a horrific shriek that sounds as if it were issued through an immense throat made of grinding wood. All non-demon creatures in area D3 or within 30 feet of any entrance to area D3 take @Damage[10d6[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[will|dc:41|basic|options:area-effect] save) as their own souls twist and writhe in pain. The trap then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: @Check[perception|dc:42|traits:action:seek] (master) to notice massive buds beneath the other foliage before they open

details.description: Five large flowers in the weed-choked field unfurl, each dusting pollen onto vines that animate into @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.Wemmuth]{Wemmuths}.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:48|name:Calm a Flower|traits:environmental] (master) or @Check[performance|dc:48|name:Calm a Flower|traits:environmental] (legendary) to calm a flower, closing it
details.reset: Wemmuths collapse into ordinary vines and the trap resets if the area is left alone for 1 minute.
details.routine:
2 If there are fewer wemmuths than open flowers, the flowers create more wemmuths (so there as many wemmuths as open flowers) as their first action.

For their second action, the flowers produce a mind-addling pollen. Each non-plant creature within 30 feet of a flower must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:41|name:Mindaddling Pollen|traits:emotion,mental,olfactory] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round, on a critical failure). The pollen is an emotion, mental, and olfactory effect.

description.value:
Trigger A non-plant creature enters the garden

Effect The blooms open, spawning a single @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.Wemmuth] that acts on the hazard's initiative, and the hazard rolls for initiative.

stealth.details: (master)

details.description: A spiky iron cage pulls in a victim with animated chains before slamming shut and locking tight.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:45] (master) to Pick the Lock, @Check[athletics|dc:48] (master) to break the lock or bend the bars; five total successes are required to disable the trap
details.reset: The trap deactivates 1 minute after there are no longer any potential prisoners in the room and then activates 1 round after that.
details.routine: (3 actions) As 1 action, the trap makes a chain Strike against a target in its reach; its Athletics modifier for Improved Grab is +36. The trap can have any number of creatures @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] by its chains. If the trap has any creatures grabbed or restrained, it attempts to pull them closer as 1 action with an Athletics check against the creature's Fortitude DC; on a success, it pulls the creature 5 feet closer (10 feet on a critical success). A Large or smaller creature can be pulled inside the cage, after which the cage door slams shut. The cage can only contain one creature, and that creature is restrained while inside ([[/act escape dc=50]]). As 1 action, the trap can Constrict the creature inside the cage with more chains.

description.value:
Trigger A creature that isn't a member of the inquisition or an authorized ally comes within 15 feet of the cage

Effect The trap makes a chain Strike against the triggering creature. If the trap damages and successfully grapples the target with Improved Grab, the trap pulls the target 5 feet closer (or into the cage if the Strike was a critical hit). The trap then rolls initiative.

@Damage[(3d8+20)[bludgeoning]], @Check[fortitude|dc:45|basic]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Constrict]
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ImprovedGrab]

details.description: A Utopian glyph transforms trespassers into aeons.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48|name:Drain the Glyph's Power|traits:magical,trap] (master) to drain the glyph's power harmlessly, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 42) to counteract the glyph
details.reset: 1 hour

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the entryway to the Hall of Wonders (area N4) without speaking the passphrase ("The History and Future of Humanity") in Utopian

Effect Each creature within 20 feet of the door is targeted by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cursed Metamorphosis] (@Check[will|dc:42|name:Baleful Polymorph|traits:incapacitation,polymorph,magical] save), except the effect transforms creatures into the form of an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Arbiter] aeon rather than that of an animal.

details.description:
This apocalypse represents the effects of numerous planar rifts forming and exploding in a region, with the risk of the planar fabric being compromised enough to tear a city-sized (if not larger) hole in reality. These rifts are likely to eject inhabitants of the other plane into the Universe, as well as serve as gateways for furious defenders to pour through to punish anyone they think might be responsible for the problem. Any additional encounters that result from these incursions should award XP as normal for encounters of their difficulty. While the rifts are likely to all lead to the same plane, particularly far-reaching disruptions might create gateways to multiple planes at once, with each rift spewing forth its own defenders. These groups would each have their own aims and agendas, clashing, collaborating, or causing chaos wherever they go.

Anticipate DC 43 Lore (expert) for the plane's Lore skill to notice pinpoint incursions from the other plane or @Check[arcana|dc:48] (master), @Check[nature|dc:48] (master), @Check[occultism|dc:48] (master), or @Check[religion|dc:48] (master) to detect subtle instabilities in the fabric of the Universe

Prepare DC 45 Lore (expert) with the plane's Lore skill, @Check[arcana|dc:48] (master), @Check[nature|dc:48] (master), @Check[occultism|dc:48] (master), @Check[religion|dc:48] (master) or @Check[performance|dc:51] (legendary) to perform a planar stabilization rite to minimize the damage
details.routine:
(1+ hours, @Check[flat|dc:18]) Rifts detonate with raw planar essence. As the hazard progresses, the ruptures grow more frequent until they merge together into a gaping portal (this final stage might be prevented with good fortune or successful efforts to Prepare). Ruptures occur once per 10 minutes for the first hour and once per minute for the second hour. The frequency with which each unprepared creature in the area is hit by a rupture depends upon their proximity to the hazard's epicenter. If the hazard proceeds into a third hour, all creatures and objects in the area take the hazard's damage one final time before being swallowed up by the burgeoning portal and deposited on the other plane.

The planar explosions deal 3d12+18 damage, with a DC 45 basic saving throw, and inflict a condition on a failed saving throw. The damage type, saving throw type, condition, and condition duration depend upon the exploding plane, as detailed in the table below.

d20PlaneDamage and SaveCondition
1AbaddonFortitude vs. poison damagefrightened 2 (1 hour, The condition's value can't be reduced by the typical means for 1 hour)
2Astral or Ethereal PlaneEach time a new rift appears, roll again on this table, using the listed effects.Each time a new rift appears, roll again on this table, using the listed effects.
3AxisFortitude vs. force damageclumsy 2 (1 hour)

12The NetherworldFortitude vs. cold damageblinded (10 minutes)
