details.description: Three shadow tethers form when a creature reaches the hall's midpoint; each lashes, grabs, and crushes creatures in the hall.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48] (legendary) to disable each tether or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank, counteract DC 48) to counteract each tether
details.routine: (six actions) This trap loses 2 actions each turn for each disabled shadow tether. Each shadow tether has 2 actions it can use to either Strike a creature or Constrict a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed] creature, in any combination.

description.value:
@Damage[(4d10+22)[bludgeoning]], @Check[fortitude|dc:47|basic]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Constrict]

Trigger A creature reaches the hall's midpoint

Effect The trap attempts a shadow tether Strike against the triggering creature. On a hit, the triggering creature is automatically @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed]. The trap then rolls initiative.
Plus the creature is automatically @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed]

details.description: The westernmost 5 feet of the building gives way, dropping rubble and anyone in the area into the depths of the Lantern Kingdom below.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:51|traits:action:disable-a-device|name:Jury Rig the Supports] to quickly jury-rig key supports against weak wall sections to prevent the collapse

description.value:
Trigger A Small or larger creature walks into either 5-foot-square at the westernmost end of the building

Effect The triggering creature falls and takes @Damage[50[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage, then an additional @Damage[10d6[bludgeoning]] damage as they and the building's falling timbers "land" in a devastating crash at area A1. The creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Grab an Edge] reaction to avoid falling.

details.description:
Monsters of titanic proportions awaken and stampede across the land, crushing everything in their wake. These might be a group of battling kaiju, an army of titanic constructs, or another onslaught of oversized creatures. This hazard works best when combined with combat against one or more massive monsters. These creatures might notice the PCs' strength and decide to get rid of them before the PCs have a chance to interfere with their onslaught.

Anticipate DC 32 (master) skill check with a skill typically used to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Recall Knowledge] about the rampaging creatures to notice subtle signs of their proximity in the surrounding landscape (DC 30 if it's a Lore skill) or @Check[survival|dc:38] (master) to notice the ground trembling from immense footfalls
Prepare @Check[crafting|dc:48] (master) or Thievery (master) to lay traps to hamper the approaching rampage; DC 50 (master) skill check with a skill typically used to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Recall Knowledge] about the creature's type to make targeted preparations (DC 46 if it's a Lore skill); or @Check[warfare-lore|dc:46] (master), @Check[intimidation|dc:48] (master), or @Check[diplomacy|dc:50] (master) to rally nearby troops to prepare for battle
details.routine:
(10+ minutes, @Check[flat|dc:15]) The colossus rampage affects the entire area, but whether or not the affected area remains static, moves, expands, or otherwise changes is left to you to determine as the story needs. At the start of each minute, unprepared creatures in the area take @Damage[(3d10+10)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:47|basic] save). A creature that fails the save is jostled enough that they're @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] for that minute, while a creature that critically fails is pinned under fallen debris and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] until they [[/act escape dc=47]].

Two additional effects, based on the nature of the creatures and the battlefield, take place during that minute. Roll [[/gmr 1d10]] for each to determine on which round during that minute each effect takes place (rerolling duplicate rounds). You can create alternative abilities to better fit the nature of the creatures in the stampede, but in general, consider selecting one ability that imposes a condition or battlefield effect and one that deals damage. Note that these effects can affect prepared and unprepared creatures in the area, but when a prepared creature attempts a save against any of these effects, they increase their result by one degree of success.

description.value:
The stampede's approach inflicts overwhelming fear upon those who can't escape its path. This ability might represent exposure to frightful presence auras, terrifying howls, or simply the sheer presence of the advancing army. All creatures that start their turn in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:42] save, with the following effects. An affected creature can't reduce the value of its frightened condition while it remains in the aura.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to deathly terror for 10 minutes.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 4}.

The stampede fills the air with an impenetrable cloud, blocking all vision. This cloud might arise from a dust storm churned up by massive stomping feet, debris from countless ruined buildings, or a magical ability to summon cloying mist. The cloud blocks all vision, making unprepared creatures @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] until they leave the area, and imparting a –4 circumstance penalty to vision-based Perception checks for prepared creatures. Creatures outside the area can't see into the cloud.

Overlapping blasts of energy or other effects carpet the area. This ability might represent the energy breaths of creatures like dragons, or the overlapping auras of creatures wreathed in fire or other elements. It might also represent the combined effects of numerous spellcasters to cover the region, whether they are part of the stampede itself or defenders attempting to slow the stampede with indiscriminate magic. All creatures in the area take 4d10+12 acid, cold, electricity, fire, sonic, or void damage (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic|options:area-effect] save). The damage type depends upon the nature of the magic (or can be randomly determined).

@Damage[(4d10+12)[acid]|options:area-damage]

@Damage[(4d10+12)[cold]|options:area-damage]

@Damage[(4d10+12)[electricity]|options:area-damage]

@Damage[(4d10+12)[fire]|options:area-damage]

@Damage[(4d10+12)[sonic]|options:area-damage]

@Damage[(4d10+12)[void]|options:area-damage]

The stampede crushes through buildings and rends the ground in its wake. As it does, rocks, trees, metal fragments, and all manner of other dangerous objects soar through the air at perilous speeds. All creatures in the area take 4d10+12 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:42|basic|options:area-effect] save). The damage type depends upon the nature of what the stampede is currently destroying (or can be randomly determined).

@Damage[(4d10+12)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage]

@Damage[(4d10+12)[piercing]|options:area-damage]

@Damage[(4d10+12)[slashing]|options:area-damage]

Poison fills the air, infiltrating the bodies of all who are unfortunate enough to enter its reach. This ability most often represents the effect of a congregation of poisonous monsters, but it could also come about from more unusual circumstances like the destruction of a site used to mass-produce toxic compounds. By default, the miasma exposes all creatures in the area to cerulean scourge, with Fortitude DC of 42. If the creatures in the stampede have their own specific poison with no onset, you can replace cerulean scourge with their poison effect. Use a Fortitude DC of 42 or the poison's normal DC, whichever is higher.

Cerulean Scourge This poison is infamous for making the victim's blood vessels glow with a bright blue light before painfully bursting.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:42|traits:alchemical,injury,poison]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[10d6[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[12d6[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[14d6[poison]] damage (1 round)

details.description: This shadow tunnel compresses when creatures pass through.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:46] (legendary) twice to push back the shadows or @Check[thievery|dc:48] (legendary) three times to pierce through the shadows
details.routine: (1 action) The tunnel compresses, crushing all creatures in it and dealing @Damage[(4d10+22)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:42|basic]). On a failed save, a creature additionally becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized] ([[/act escape dc=42]]).

description.value:
Trigger A creature reaches the tunnel's midpoint

Effect The tunnel shudders, knocking creatures @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] unless they succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:42] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to realize the lattice is a complex clockwork before it begins to move; noticing the lattice itself is DC 0.

details.description: A complex clockwork of bright metal and delicately carved gemstones, the lattice's interlocking gears begin to whir as golden runes glow across its surface.
details.disable:
@Check[thievery|dc:40|name:Obliterate a Key Rune|traits:magical,trap] (master) or @Check[religion|dc:35|name:Obliterate a Key Rune|traits:magical,trap] (master) four times to obliterate each of the four key runes.

A @Check[thievery|dc:45|name:Stop the Clockwork's Movement|traits:magical,trap] (legendary) check (or breaking the lattice) stops the clockwork's movement and prevents it from resetting, but doesn't stop its current activation.
details.reset: The convergence lattice deactivates and resets after there are no uncontrolled targets within line of sight for 1 minute.
details.routine:
(3 actions) The convergence lattice uses its first action to call for aid. Each convergent creature within 100 feet of the lattice Strides toward the lattice as a reaction. Creatures thus called continue to move toward the lattice on their turn each round until they are adjacent to it, at which point they defend it with their lives.

The lattice uses its second action to deal @Damage[16d6[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:42|basic] save) to a non-convergent creature within 100 feet. This damage is nonlethal; a creature knocked out by this damage doesn't gain the dying condition and is automatically @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Controlled] by the convergence lattice when they awaken.

The lattice uses its third action to make the same attack against a second non-convergent creature within 100 feet; if there isn't such a second creature, it doesn't take its third action.

description.value:
Trigger A creature that is not @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Controlled] by the convergence lattice sees it

Effect The lattice's clockworks begin to move and it rolls for initiative.

stealth.details: (legendary); @Check[perception|dc:20] to notice the warning carved in the door frame

details.description: A warning carved into the door frame magically crushes any who pass through the doorway without Kemnebi's permission.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48] (legendary) to scratch out the warning or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank; counteract DC 48) to counteract the magic in the warning

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes through the doorway without Kemnebi's permission

Effect The trap casts implosion on the triggering creature. This is a 10th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Implosion] spell that deals @Damage[85[untyped]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:47|basic]).

details.description: Ebeshra fires a crystal at a creature to pin them onto the Material Plane.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:48|name:Earn Ebeshra's Favor (Occultism)|traits:abjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,hazard] (legendary) or @Check[religion|dc:48|name:Earn Ebeshra's Favor (Religion)|traits:abjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,hazard] (legendary) to earn Ebeshra's favor and allow safe travel or @Check[deception|dc:51|name:Divert Ebeshra's Attention|traits:abjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,hazard] (master) to momentarily divert Ebeshra's attention

publication.title: Pathfinder Lost Omens Monsters of Myth

details.reset: Ebeshra waits [[/gmr 1d4 #Rounds]] rounds to break off another crystal.

description.value:
Trigger A creature Ebeshra can see begins to use a teleportation effect or an effect that would move any number of targets to a different plane

Effect Ebeshra hurls a crystal at the triggering creature. The triggering creature and all creatures within 50 feet of the creature take @Damage[16d8[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:45|basic|traits:abjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,hazard] save) and additional effects based on the result of their save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The effect is counteracted, and the creature can't use teleportation effects or planar travel effects for 1 minute.

Failure As success, but for 1 day instead of 1 minute.

Critical Failure As success, but for 1 week instead of 1 minute. Additionally, the creature becomes pinned under the crystal and must succeed at 3 total DC 45 checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Escape] from under the crystal.

stealth.details: (legendary) to notice the low vibration forming in the area surrounding the apocalypse seed; noticing the apocalypse seed itself has a DC of 0

details.description: An overwhelming trumpeting fills the area as the apocalypse seed vibrates and shakes off the encrusted bone that's accreted around it to reveal a large tooth fragment from some immense monster.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:50] (master), @Check[occultism|dc:50] (legendary), or @Check[religion|dc:50] (legendary) to recognize and shore up the area against the localized planar instability causing a connection between the apocalypse seed and Jandelay, or @Check[athletics|dc:53] (legendary) to grasp the apocalypse seed and muffle its vibrations with your embrace. Four successes are needed to muffle the aura enough against the planar instability and end the hazard.
details.reset: The apocalypse seed creates another area of instability after 24 hours.
details.routine:
(1 action) Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:44] save. A creature with the slowed condition from this hazard takes a –2 penalty to this save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(2d10+10)[sonic]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(4d10+20)[sonic]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} until the start of the hazard's next turn.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(8d10+40)[sonic]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2} for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters area C6

Effect Pent-up magical energy from another world begins to release in waves of overwhelming sound. The hazard rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to realize that the strange faint sounds could be dangerous.

details.description: Those who listen to the sounds for too long become convinced that these forces can be defeated by lashing out at them, even though the forces themselves are not apparent.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:42|name:Play a Virtuoso Act] (master) to counter the eerie sounds by exceeding their influence with a virtuoso act, or @Check[intimidation|dc:48|name:Display of Anger and Rage] (master) to startle the echoes into silence with a display of anger and rage.
details.routine:
(1 action; emotion, enchantment, mental, primal) The trap starts scrambling senses and forcing creatures to perceive threats to their very place in time and space where no such threats actually exist. Every creature in area K13 must attempt a @Check[will|dc:42|traits:emotion,enchantment,mental,primal] saving throw.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure As success, but the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] as long as it remains stupefied.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} and confused. The confused condition persists until the creature recovers normally, but the stupefaction effect is permanent until removed.

description.value:
Trigger At least two creatures who aren't native to Thousandbreaths end their turn in area K13.

Effect Those in the room begin "hearing" the sounds in the room telepathically and start to have difficulty telling the difference between these sounds and those of their allies; the trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: An invisible rune causes the animated bear to explode if it is destroyed or if it finishes its dance while anyone is in the room.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48|traits:action:disable-a-device|name:Erase the Rune] (legendary) to erase the rune without triggering the explosion (the DC is increased to @Check[thievery|dc:51|traits:action:disable-a-device|name:Erase the Rune] while the bear is moving), @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank, counteract DC 39) to counteract the rune, or Nyrissa says the words "Good bear" to the trap before it finishes its dance.

description.value:
Trigger The animated bear is reduced below its BT, or it finishes its three-round dance while there are still people in the room

Effect The rune detonates into a blast of fire centered on the bear. All creatures within a 20-foot radius of the center of area K6 take @Damage[16d10[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic]).

details.description: A distortion in space spawns an aggressive, malevolent shadow duplicate of any characters who pass between Terimor's faces.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:42|name:Disable Thievery] (legendary) to permanently disable the planar distortion (DC increases by 2 for each duplicate still extant), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank; counteract DC 42) to destroy the planar distortion

publication.title: Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves between the two glass faces of Olansa Terimor

Effect The planar rift disgorges an evil mirror duplicate of the creature, and the duplicate rolls initiative.

The field of opposition can use this reaction once per round but can't spawn a duplicate of a creature that already has a mirror duplicate in existence.

A mirror duplicate rolls initiative with a +45 modifier. It uses the same statistics as the original creature, but with a +2 status bonus to AC, attacks, and saving throws. The duplicate is evil, and any abilities reliant on alignment change accordingly. The duplicate is also aggressive, attacking and taking other hostile actions available to it against anyone other than mirror duplicates.

It has three actions and one reaction and can use any of the original creature's abilities. Any limited-use abilities (such as spell slots) are based on what the original had when duplicated and are used up separately from the original creature's. The duplicate also has duplicates of the creature's worn and held items, but not consumables.

A duplicate is destroyed when reduced to 0 HP, when the trap is disabled or destroyed, or when the duplicate leaves area E2. Its duplicated items disappear when they leave its grip, though they last long enough for ranged Strikes.

details.description: Four invisible runes on a large crate conjure jets of flame and whips of force when someone tries to open the container.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:45] (master) to erase each rune, @Check[arcana|dc:48] (master) to channel energy from one rune into another, destroying both, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 45) to counteract each rune; a PC can choose to disable a fire rune or a force rune (or choose randomly)
details.reset: The runes deactivate after 1 minute, but if at least one rune remains, the trap reactivates 1 round after that.
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap has 1 fewer action for each rune that's been disabled or destroyed; whether that rune was a fire rune or a force rune affects the actions the trap can take. On the trap's initiative, the two fire runes each blast @Template[type:cone|distance:30]s of flame as an action in opposite directions that deal @Damage[12d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:44|basic] save). Then the two force runes each make a force whip Strike as an action against different targets.

description.value:
Trigger A creature tries to open the crate without removing the runes

Effect The four runes become active with a flash of red light. Each creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:42] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is slightly dazed by the flash, taking a –2 circumstance penalty to their initiative.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Blinded] for 1 round and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to their initiative.

Critical Failure The target is blinded for 1 minute and takes a –4 circumstance penalty to their initiative.

details.description: Miniature snowflake-shaped glyphs on the ground cause a layer of ice to ripple over the floor of the room and sound a silent alarm.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:53|name:Disable Arcana] (legendary) to draw a fiery counteracting glyph over each snowflake, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank, counteract DC 48)

publication.title: Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege

details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters one of the three squares marked with a T on the map

Effect The floor of the entire 10-foot-by-15-foot antechamber freezes over, sending deadly magical frost creeping up the legs of the intruders. Any creatures standing in the room must roll a @Check[reflex|dc:45] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[(3d10+5)[cold]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(6d10+10)[cold]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(12d10+20)[cold]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 minute.

Additionally, the golems in area B10 become aware of the intruders. The golems activate and come to this room to attack, if they haven't been destroyed already.

stealth.details: (legendary) to realize the fungus is hazardous; noticing the fungus itself has a DC of 0

details.description: A glowing wall of moss, lichens, mold, and stringy mushrooms weaves into a towering violet wall of shuddersome matter.
details.disable: @Check[nature|dc:42] (master) six times to identify and prune away the fungal curtain's six sensory bulbs (this doesn't remove the curtain; it must still be hacked through to allow access to the area beyond), or @Check[religion|dc:48] (legendary) twice to exorcise the fiendish energies that empower the fungal curtain and cause it to wilt into sludge

publication.title: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

details.reset: The fungal curtain deactivates at the end of any round where there were no foes in area E5, then resets at the start of the next round. It restores 10 Hit Points to each damaged section every hour.
details.routine: (3 actions) The fungal curtain takes the following actions each round in this order; the fungal curtain can perceive targets via darkvision and has a Perception modifier of +38. On its first action, it extends a thorny tentacle to attempt a melee Strike on the closest target in reach—if there's no target in reach, the fungal curtain loses this action. On its second action, it fires a volley of thorny spores into area E5, attempting a ranged thorny spore Strike against all potential targets in the chamber. On its third action, it emits another whistling decay effect.

description.value:
Trigger The fungal curtain takes damage or an attempt to disable it critically fails

Effect Pod like protrusions along the fungal curtain clench and then expel a shrill whistling sound along with clouds of flesh-eating spores. The whistling sound alerts the occupants of area E6. Any non-fungus, non-demon creature within Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} of the fungal curtain is exposed to the flesh-eating spores and takes @Damage[8d6[poison],4d6[persistent,void]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:42|basic|options:area-effect] save). The fungal curtain then rolls initiative.
No multiple attack penalty

stealth.details: (expert) to notice the words on the tombstone waver

details.description: A tombstone bears the name of those who look upon it, causing their hearts to seize.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:45|name:Bless the tombstone] (legendary) to bless the tombstone or @Check[occultism|dc:50|name:Ward off the spirits] (trained) to ward off spirits
details.reset: 1 day

description.value:
Trigger A creature reads or touches the tombstone

Effect The haunt deals @Damage[(8d10+44)[void]] damage (@Check[will|dc:47|basic]) to all creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:60]{60 feet} who can see the tombstone and can read any language. On a critical failure, a creature dies.

details.description: Six giant tentacles reach up from the ink lake, grabbing any foes in reach and dragging them underwater.
details.disable:
@Check[athletics|dc:48|name:Disable Athletics] (legendary) or @Check[acrobatics|dc:45|name:Disable Acrobatics] (master) to tie one tentacle into a knot, rendering it useless, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (8th rank; counteract DC 44) to counteract one tentacle.

The death of the Daemonic Rumormonger permanently dispels all the inky tendrils.

publication.title: Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege

details.routine: (6 actions) The hazard loses 1 action for every tentacle that is disabled or destroyed. The tentacles can combine their actions to use the hazard's Inky Imitator ability. A broken tentacle can still Strike, but it can't Grab. This hazard takes no multiple attack penalty.

description.value: No multiple attack penalty

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:38]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] (1 round)

The inky tendrils cast a 10th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Duplicate Foe] spell (@Check[fortitude|dc:40|name:Duplicate Foe]) on the target; on a failed save, a simulacrum of the target made from dripping ink and clockwork scraps emerges from any other spot (tendrils' choice) in the lake.

The tendrils don't need to Sustain the Spell; the duplicate exists for 1 minute or until it is destroyed before collapsing into a puddle of ink and scrap metal.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Grab]

@Damage[(2d8+10)[bludgeoning]] plus black ink delirium, @Check[fortitude|dc:42|basic]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Constrict]

Trigger A non-daemonic creature moves within 15 feet of the shoreline

Effect The hazard makes a tentacle Strike against the triggering creature and the hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: Four magic keystones repel creatures when one is damaged or tampered with.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48] (legendary) to remove each keystone or @Check[occultism|dc:48] (legendary) to manipulate the magic to isolate each keystone
details.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each removed or isolated keystone. Each keystone spends 1 action to release a wave of force, dealing @Damage[(4d10+22)[force]] damage in a @Template[cone|distance:15] (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic]) and pushing each creature back 5 feet. On a failed save, the distance a creature is pushed increases to 10 feet.

description.value:
Trigger A creature damages or tampers with a keystone

Effect The triggering creature is moved back 10 feet by a wave of force. A successful @Check[fortitude|dc:42] save reduces the distance moved to 5 feet. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A small-scale version of a dragonstorm-a churning vortex of wind infused with fire, acid, lightning, poison, and ice-fills Alseta's Ring.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:40|name:Call for Divine Intervention] (legendary) to call out to a non-evil deity for direct intervention to disperse the lesser dragonstorm (prayers to Apsu allow characters who are masters in Religion to attempt the check as if they were legendary instead), @Check[thievery|dc:42|name:Deactivate Vengegate|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to deactivate Vengegate while the manifestation is forcing it open, or a successful @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank; DC 40) to counteract the lesser dragonstorm.
details.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the lesser dragonstorm reaches out and blasts all creatures within Alseta's Ring. Affected creatures take @Damage[2d8[acid],2d8[cold],2d8[electricity],2d8[fire],2d8[poison]|options:area-damage]{10d8 damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:46|basic|options:area-effect,forced-movement,inflicts:prone]), evenly split between acid, cold, fire, electricity, and poison. Creatures that fail their save are pulled 20 feet toward the center of Alseta's Ring (or are knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] and pulled 30 feet on a critical failure). After 10 rounds, the lesser dragonstorm is destroyed, but a much greater dragonstorm manifests in the surrounding region-see The Dragonstorm.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to activate Vengegate

Effect The chamber within Alseta's Ring fills with a dragonstorm. Creatures within this area take @Damage[2d8[acid],2d8[cold],2d8[electricity],2d8[fire],2d8[poison]|options:area-damage]{10d8 damage}, equally split between acid, cold, electricity, fire, and poison.

details.description: Magic sigils @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] in the grain of the wooden door trigger a magic trap that damages the mind of anyone attempting to pick the door's lock.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48] (legendary) to deface the symbols or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank, counteract DC 48) to counteract the trap.
details.reset: The trap resets automatically if triggered and doesn't reset if disabled.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to Pick the Lock

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[(8d10+44)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:42|basic]). On a failed save, it's additionally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 minute.

details.description:
Impact from a sufficiently large asteroid would be powerful enough to wipe out a region or destroy a planet, but smaller-scale impact events, like from a meteor shower, present hazards that could be survived. This shower might arise naturally, or it might be the result of the PCs or other forces preventing a greater calamity by breaking a world-rending asteroid into smaller pieces.

Anticipate @Check[astronomy-lore|dc:41] (master) or @Check[nature|dc:45] (legendary) to notice signs of the approach of unusual celestial bodies

Prepare @Check[astronomy-lore|dc:40] (master) or @Check[nature|dc:50] (legendary) to predict the trajectory of the falling asteroids and determine safe zones, @Check[athletics|dc:45] (legendary) or @Check[crafting|dc:47] (master) to dig underground shelters, @Check[diplomacy|dc:45] (master) or @Check[intimidation|dc:47] (master) to coordinate an orderly evacuation
details.routine:
(1+ minutes, @Check[flat|dc:17]) Small asteroids rain down across the sky, battering and incinerating those caught in their path before carving craters. Over the course of a minute, enough asteroids smash into the ground and explode to inflict @Damage[7d10[bludgeoning],14d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage to any unprepared characters in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:46|basic] save). The @Template[type:burst|distance:40]{40-foot-radius} craters left behind are greater difficult terrain.

The material raining down from outer space includes chunks of adamantine. Adamantine's ability to break through the Hardness of materials is particularly lethal at terminal velocity. The falling adamantine has Hardness 20, allowing the asteroid's bludgeoning damage to overcome the Hardness of all materials with less Hardness, including shelters that would otherwise provide cover. Cover that's destroyed by the bludgeoning damage provides no protection against the fire damage.

stealth.details: (legendary) to realize the chamber is a complex rotating trap before it triggers. No check is necessary to notice the platforms and the pit.

details.description: Several swinging pendulums of force cleave through the room across the platforms, knocking people into the pit.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:48|name:Deactivate a Pendulum Blade|traits:complex,magical,mechanical,trap] (legendary), @Check[thievery|dc:44|name:Deactivate a Pendulum Blade|traits:complex,magical,mechanical,trap] (master), or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 44) on a platform to deactivate its pendulum blade; once all five platforms have been deactivated, the trap is disabled and the room rotates back into position along the exits. @Check[thievery|dc:48|name:Rotate the Room Open|traits:complex,magical,mechanical,trap] (legendary) from any blocked exit to activate the rotating mechanism and rotate the room back into position before the trap has been disabled.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure: Night of the Gray Death

details.reset: The trap resets after 1 hour. If the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Darkness] throughout the room has been dispelled, the darkness in the room returns when the trap resets.
details.routine: (5 actions) For each active platform, the trap uses an action to create a pendulum of force to swing down along a straight line between the platform and one other creature in the room that isn't on the platform (if any). The trap makes a pendulum blade attack against any creature in that line. For each platform deactivated, reduce the number of actions the trap takes in its routine by 1.

description.value:
Trigger A creature disturbs any of the platforms, or anything falls into the pit

Effect The room rotates 45 degrees, closing off the room by shifting its exits to face bare walls. A pendulum of force swings across the platform with the armor on it, knocking the armor into the pit and making a pendulum blade Strike against a creature on that platform (if any). The trap then rolls for initiative.
No multiple attack penalty
A creature hit by a pendulum blade must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:44|options:forced-movement] save or be moved 5 feet along the line of the pendulum's swing. This is forced movement.

stealth.details: (master) to notice Ebeshra twisting its shape in preparation of creating a planar rift

details.description: Ebeshra opens a massive rift in the Material Plane that pulls nearby creatures into another plane entirely. The rift is typically to a random plane in the multiverse, though Ebeshra can have the rift pull creatures into a plane of Ebeshra's choosing. Additionally, Ebeshra can choose any number of creatures to be immune to the effects of the planar rift, usually due to the kaiju recognizing the innocence of the creatures.
details.disable: DC 47 Lore (master) related to the specific plane beyond the rift to disrupt the planar frequency and close the rift, @Check[arcana|dc:52|name:Undo the Rift's Manifestation (Arcana)|traits:complex,conjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,teleportation,hazard] (legendary) or @Check[occultism|dc:52|name:Undo the Rift's Manifestation (Occultism)|traits:complex,conjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,teleportation,hazard] (legendary) to undo the magical manifestation of the rift, @Check[performance|dc:55|name:Counteract the Rift's Frequency|traits:complex,conjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,teleportation,hazard] (legendary) to play a tone that counteracts the rift's harmonic frequency, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:55|name:Plead With Ebeshra (Diplomacy)|traits:complex,conjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,teleportation,hazard] (legendary) or @Check[religion|dc:55|name:Plead With Ebeshra (Religion)|traits:complex,conjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,teleportation,hazard] (legendary) to plead with Ebeshra to close the rift

publication.title: Pathfinder Lost Omens Monsters of Myth

details.reset: Ebeshra vanishes 1 minute after opening the rift, which closes the rift. Ebeshra must wait 1 week before it can open another planar rift.
details.routine:
The rift pulls creatures within 500 feet toward it. The rift can pull any creature, regardless of its size. Creatures in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:45|name:Planar Rift|traits:complex,conjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,teleportation,hazard] or @Check[reflex|dc:45|name:Planar Rift|traits:complex,conjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,teleportation,hazard] save (the creature's choice) to resist the effects of this pull or attempt to grab something to avoid getting pulled.

Critical Success The creature resists the pull and doesn't move toward the rift.

Success The creature is moved 25 feet toward the rift.

Failure The creature is moved 50 feet toward the rift.

Critical Failure The creature is moved 100 feet toward the rift. The harsh pull causes the creature to take @Damage[10d6[bludgeoning]] damage.

If a creature is directly beneath the rift when the rift pulls it, the creature instead attempts a @Check[fortitude|dc:50|name:Pull Into the Rift|traits:complex,conjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,teleportation,hazard] or @Check[reflex|dc:50|name:Pull Into the Rift|traits:complex,conjuration,divine,environmental,kaiju,teleportation,hazard] save (the creature's choice) to avoid being pulled into the rift. On a failure, the creature is pulled up into the rift and thrown to a random point on the plane on the other side.

description.value:
Trigger Ebeshra recognizes a nearby threat as needing an indisputable resolution, or a deity tasks Ebeshra with creating a planar rift

Effect Ebeshra twists its body, aligning its segmented sections into a fine blade, and cuts the fabric of the Material Plane. It creates an enormous rift in a point in the sky within 1 mile. The rift is 100 feet long and 50 feet wide. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: Four pillars manifest shadow lashes that attack intruders.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:46] (legendary) to drain the magic from each shadow lash or @Check[thievery|dc:48] (legendary) to disable each shadow lash
details.routine: (8 actions) This trap loses 2 actions each turn for each disabled shadow lash. Each shadow lash has 2 actions it can use to perform the following actions.

description.value: The lash lengthens, increasing its reach to 60 feet, and makes a lash Strike. The lash's length then reverts to normal.

Trigger A creature other than Kemnebi enters the chamber

Effect Four pillars manifest a writhing whip composed of solidified shadows. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Spirits trapped inside a haunted window harm those who touch the window.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:48|name:Free the Spirits] (legendary) to free the spirits from the mirror, or @Check[religion|dc:48|name:Banish the Spirits] (legendary) to banish the spirits
details.reset: The trap resets after 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the window

Effect The triggering creature takes @Damage[(8d10+44)[void]] damage with a @Check[will|dc:42|basic] save as the window attempts to steal their soul. If this damage would kill the target, the target's soul is trapped inside the window with the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Seize Soul].

details.description: Subtle but deadly magical runes link this mural to Kharnas's master. Good- or neutral-aligned creatures who examine the mural witness the castle's drawbridge silently descend, revealing an endless darkness.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:44|name:Disable Thievery] (legendary) to obscure or mar the magical runes set into the mural, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 42) to counteract the mural

publication.title: Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege

details.reset: 1 week

description.value:
Trigger A creature capable of casting divine magic examines the mural

Effect The mural casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Weird] (@Check[will|dc:43|traits:death,emotion,fear,illusion,mental,damaging-effect] save) on all creatures in the gallery. A snarl fills the creatures' minds.

stealth.details: (master) to notice that the hairline cracks on the passage walls actually form runes.

details.description: Nearly imperceptible runes set off an explosion at the point a thief is most unable to receive aid.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:45|name:Counteract the Runes|traits:magical,trap] (master) or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank, counteract DC 50) to counteract the runes
details.reset: The trap resets in 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature crosses a rune

Effect The runes unleash a gout of fire roaring up the chute. Anyone in the chute and within 5 feet of its openings is dealt @Damage[(8d10+44)[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic] save).

Immediately thereafter, the runes emanate a 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Silence] effect for 1 minute, so anyone injured in the chute can't call out for help.

details.description: A time rift focused on the sundial causes time to pass erratically for all creatures in the area.
details.disable: Three @Check[thievery|dc:48] (legendary) or @Check[occultism|dc:48] (master) checks to mend the time rift.
details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the time rift alters each creature's timeline. Each creature in the area rolls a [[/gmr 1d4 #Time Rift Effect]]{1d4} to randomly determine the effect on them.

1 The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

2 The creature is unaffected.

3 The creature is affected erratically by time; they're @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed] for 1 round.

4 The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Quickened] 1 for 1 round.

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area

Effect The triggering creature becomes temporarily unmoored from time, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3}. If the creature succeeds at a @Check[will|dc:47] save, they reduce this to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: This banquet table is covered with a wide range of delicious-looking snacks and a mix of chilled and piping hot beverages.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:48] to offer prayers to neutralize the sour reputation of the food and render it safe to eat
details.reset: The unfulfilling banquet resets instantly.

description.value:
Trigger A creature tastes any of the food or beverages on the banquet table

Effect The flavor blooming in the triggering creature's mouth simultaneously delights and frustrates the triggering creature, who must attempt a @Check[will|dc:47] save.

Critical Success The triggering creature is unsatisfied by the taste, but is otherwise temporarily immune to the Unfulfilling Banquet for 24 hours.

Success The triggering creature is momentarily distracted by their unsatisfying taste, and until the start of their next turn takes a –2 status penalty to Perception checks.

Failure The triggering creature becomes obsessed with dissatisfaction and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} (or increases their existing stupefied condition value from this hazard by 1, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 4}) for an unlimited duration.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is compelled to taste something else from the table on their next turn and won't willingly move away from the table until they do so. They take a –2 status penalty on their Will save to resist the hazard's effects on their next taste.

details.description: The Fiendflame Cage is uncontrolled, releasing holy light and hellfire in the area.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:48], @Check[nature|dc:48], @Check[occultism|dc:48], or @Check[religion|dc:48] (master) four times on the Fiendflame Cage to calm it through ritual incantations
details.reset: The trap resets after 24 hours.
details.routine: (2 actions) On its initiative, the Fiendflame Cage uses 1 action to release a wave of hellfire, dealing @Damage[(2d10+12)[spirit],(2d10+12)[fire]|traits:unholy]{2d10+12 spirit damage and 2d10+12 fire damage} to all creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:15]{15-foot-radius burst} (@Check[reflex|dc:47|basic]). The Fiendflame Cage uses its second action to release a wave of holy light, dealing @Damage[(2d10+12)[spirit],(2d10+12)[fire]|traits:holy]{2d10+12 spirit damage and 2d10+12 fire damage} to all creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:15]{15-foot-radius burst} (@Check[reflex|dc:47|basic]).

description.value:
Trigger A creature enters the area

Effect The triggering creature is exposed to the Fiendflame Cage's clashing energies. The triggering creature takes @Damage[8d6[spirit],4d6[spirit],4d6[spirit]]{8d6 spirit damage, 4d6 spirit damage, and 4d6 spirit damage} (@Check[fortitude|dc:47|basic]). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: A statue of Urgathoa fires eye beams at intruders.
details.disable: @Check[religion|dc:48] (legendary) to disenchant each eye through prayer or @Check[thievery|dc:48] (legendary) to disable each eye
details.routine: (1 action) The trap shoots a concentrated beam of fire at a random intruder. The trap makes an eye beam Strike. If one of Urgathoa's eyes has been disabled, this Strike deals half damage.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than Kemnebi enters the chamber

Effect The trap shoots a concentrated beam of fire at the triggering creature. The trap makes an eye beam Strike. If one of Urgathoa's eyes has been disabled, this Strike deals half damage. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: An ice-caked pit with no bottom looks out into the depths of space.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48] (legendary) to deactivate the pit's magical link to outer space or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (10th rank, counteract DC 44)
details.reset: The Void Pit resets instantly.

description.value:
Trigger A creature within the pit takes three consecutive Interact actions to push their way through the barrier in the pit

Effect The creature is pulled through the portal into the depths of space. It can attempt to [[/act grab-an-edge dc=42]] with a DC 42 Reflex save, otherwise it discovers the portal is one-way—no return route to Golarion exists. The creature takes @Damage[10d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:42|basic]) at the start of each turn and begins to suffocate. Without powerful magic like @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Interplanar Teleport] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Wish], even a creature that can survive the depths of space will be lost forever—as the Void Pit deposits them in the unimaginably vast gulf between galaxies in which sound cannot exist, where they cannot cast spells with verbal components.

stealth.details: (master) to realize the mountain is dangerous (DC 0 to see the towering statue in the first place)

details.description: A massive sculpture of a wolflike form as tall as a mountain guards passage to the Cold Wastes at the northern end of Leng and seems almost ready to come to life at the approach of any creatures.
details.disable: @Check[stealth|dc:51] (master) to avoid the initial notice of the gigantic form, or @Check[occultism|dc:48] (legendary) to know the proper posture and hand sign to make to turn away the watcher's attention
details.reset: The nightmare resets immediately.

description.value:
Trigger A creature comes within a mile of the mountain-sized statue's base

Effect While the Watcher does not actually animate, it fills the triggering creature's mind with the dread conviction that it might do so at any moment. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:42] save, after which they are temporarily immune to that particular Watcher's Dreadful Notice for 24 hours.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 3} (unlimited duration).

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed] with fear with an unlimited duration; as long as the creature remains paralyzed, they need not drink or eat. If they are brought to a location where they no longer have line of sight to the watcher, the paralyzed creature can attempt a new Will save every day to recover from the paralysis, but during this time they are subject to thirst and starvation as normal.

stealth.details: (legendary) to detect the dangerous and sealed entrance to the Eye of Desire; noticing the anima focus itself has a DC of 0

details.description: Three pitchers pour water in an endless cascade, each decorated with a symbol of fertility, fecundity, and desire. The pitchers never empty, and the fountain below never overflows; the entire thing flickers with eerie red energy.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:50] (legendary) or @Check[society|dc:50] (legendary) to decipher the symbols and runes scribed across on the runewell and pinpoint which runes can be used to deactivate it, @Check[thievery|dc:53] (legendary) to temporarily disrupt the anima focus's connection to the Eye of Desire or to destroy a phantom of desire; three total successes are required to disable the hazard (destroying a phantom of desire counts as a single success)
details.reset: The animus of longing deactivates as soon as no foes remain in the atrium, then immediately resets.
details.routine: (1 action) On its turn, the animus of longing sustains the three phantoms of desire. The three creatures each take their turn.

description.value:
Trigger A creature attempts to use the anima focus to open the way into the Eye of Desire

Effect The Runewell of Lust's magic reaches out of the Eye of Desire and into the minds of all nearby creatures, creating vivid, dreamlike hallucinations fulfilling their greatest desires. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:44] save with the following effects. The hazard then manifests three phantoms of desire—incarnations of Runelord Sorshen empowered by certain beliefs that she hasn't actually turned over a proverbial new leaf and is playing a long-con game to take over the world. The phantoms and the hazard then roll initiative.

Critical Success The creature understands the false nature of the hallucination but gleans useful insights from what they see, gaining a +2 status bonus to one Perception check, saving throw, or skill check made before the end of their next turn.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dazzled] for 1 round, then recognizes the hallucination for what it is and blocks it from their mind.

Failure The creature becomes lost in the hallucination, struggling to tell it apart from reality. The creature becomes dazzled and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} as long as they remain in Sorshen's atrium. At the end of each of the creature's turns, it may attempt a new @Check[will|dc:44] save to end this effect.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 2}, and the Will save to end the effect is DC 48.

details.description: Contact with the doors, pillars, or statue causes a shower of acid.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:48] (master) to ground the charged features or @Check[thievery|dc:50] (legendary) to disable the contact sensors

description.value:
Trigger A creature touches the doors, pillars, or statue directly or with a tool

Effect A shower of acid rains down from the ceiling, dealing @Damage[(8d10+48)[acid]] damage to all creatures in the chamber (@Check[reflex|dc:44|basic]). On a critical failure, a creature additionally takes @Damage[1d10[persistent,acid]] damage.

details.description: A ragged planar rift drags things into its depths and hurls creatures through from its terminal end.
details.disable: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dispel Magic] (9th rank; counteract DC 46) to seal the rift

publication.title: Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege

description.value:
Trigger A creature moves within 5 feet of the rift

Effect The creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:44] save or be drawn into the rift. A creature so drawn in becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3} and immediately teleports to a different random area in Kharnas's ruins.

details.description: Mogaru exhales a blast of intense flames to eradicate any meddlesome pests in the area.
details.disable: @Check[performance|dc:50|name:Divert Mogaru's Attention] (legendary) or @Check[deception|dc:53|name:Divert Mogaru's Attention] (legendary) to momentarily divert Mogaru's attention
details.reset: Mogaru must wait [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} before letting loose another gout of fire.

description.value:
Trigger A creature takes a hostile action against Mogaru, produces a visible area effect, or flies within 120 feet of Mogaru

Effect Mogaru breathes a @Template[cone|distance:120] of fire toward the object of his ire. Flammable objects in the area immediately combust, and stone or metal surfaces warp into difficult terrain. Creatures in the area take @Damage[20d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:44|basic] save). On a failed or critically failed save, a creature also takes @Damage[3d6[persistent,fire]] damage.

details.description: A wave of psychic energy ripples through the room, violently prying into the minds of creatures in the area and alerting Kemnebi to the presence of intruders.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:50] (legendary) to stymie the psychic energy before it manifests
details.reset: 1 hour.

description.value:
Trigger A creature has been in the room for more than 1 round

Effect A wave of psychic energy slams into the minds of creatures in the chamber, dealing @Damage[(8d10+48)[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:44|basic]). On a failed save, a creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 3}.

details.description: Powerful warding magic tied to an object or location tries to regress the ages of a creature and its allies.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:46|name:Dismantle Spell|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to take apart the spell one tiny piece at a time, with eyes closed, while recalling every vivid life memory in order, starting from the earliest memory

description.value:
Trigger A creature tries to steal the object or intrude upon the location. If someone uses a proxy or dupe for the theft or intrusion, the trap unerringly targets the true perpetrator or perpetrators at any distance—even across planes.

Effect The triggering creature and up to five coconspirators instantly revert to infants, losing all memories, class abilities, and other skills acquired during their lives (@Check[fortitude|dc:44] negates). Reversing this effect is nearly impossible, requiring powerful magic such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Wish].

details.description: Hooks of solidified shadow extend from the walls, hook into creatures, and pull, tearing them open.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:50] (legendary) to disable the trap's sensors

description.value:
Trigger A creature reaches the hall's midpoint

Effect The trap attempts a shadow hook Strike against each creature in the hall. On a critical hit, a creature additionally takes @Damage[1d10[bleed]] damage.

details.description: A looming illusory image of the face of Asmodeus appears within the room, eyes glinting with malice at any who refuse to worship him.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:50] (master) to disrupt the rune projecting Asmodeus's face or @Check[religion|dc:48] (master) to refute the Asmodean faith; four total successes are required to disable the hazard
details.reset: The trap deactivates 1 round after all creatures who don't worship Asmodeus either leave the room or are dead. It resets 1 round after that.
details.routine:
(1 action; mental, visual) The illusory face of Asmodeus gazes at one creature in the area who doesn't worship Asmodeus. His eyes glint with diabolic power as that creature is overwhelmed by visions of the enormity of Hell. That creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:44|traits:mental,visual] saving throw. The hazard tends to target creatures who are attempting to disable the hazard. Taking damage from this hazard doesn't grant a flat check to end the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] condition.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[5d8[mental]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[10d8[mental]] damage and is confused for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[20d8[mental]] damage and is confused for 1 minute.

description.value:
Trigger A creature who doesn't worship Asmodeus enters the room

Effect The booming voice of Asmodeus laughs cruelly at nonbelievers who would dare enter his highest temple on Golarion. Each creature who doesn't worship Asmodeus within 100 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:48|traits:auditory,emotion,fear,mental] save. The hazard then rolls initiative as Asmodeus' face appears in the middle of the vestry.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for as long as they're frightened.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 4}.

details.description: A torrent of souls pulled from Hell pours through an archway, inflicting their personal suffering on all they touch.
details.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:50] (master) or @Check[religion|dc:50] (master) to quell the souls' suffering; these checks must be attempted while adjacent to the arch, and four total successes are required to disable the haunt
details.reset: After 1 minute, the wave of souls dries up, and the haunt deactivates. Over the course of 1 hour, planar energy rebuilds in the area, and the haunt resets.
details.routine:
(1 action; divine, void) A seemingly endless flow of damned spirits emerges from the arch and washes down the street. Each creature within a @Template[type:line|distance:240] from the arch down the street takes @Damage[(4d10+24)[void]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:44|traits:divine,void] save. This line affects everyone within 20 feet of the ground.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and increases its doomed value by 1.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and increases its doomed value by 2.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature comes within 15 feet of the arch

Effect The sounds of screams from the damned intensify as the arch begins to glow with planar energy. Each living creature within 60 feet of the arch must attempt a @Check[will|dc:48] saving throw. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 for the haunt's duration.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 4} and can't reduce its frightened condition below 2 for the duration of the haunt.

details.description: The edges of the extraplanar tunnel seem to writhe with malevolence, as if preventing unwanted souls from passing through.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:50] (master) or @Check[religion|dc:50] (master) to allow the group to traverse along the portal tunnel; six total successes are required to complete the journey; a PC who succeeds at a @Check[acrobatics|dc:48], @Check[athletics|dc:48], or @Check[survival|dc:48] check as a 2-action activity grants themselves a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and saves against the hazard's routine for 1 round (on a critical success, they can also grant this bonus to one other PC)
details.reset: Once the PCs pass through the portal tunnel and arrive in Hell, the Avernal vortex ends and doesn't reset.
details.routine:
(1 action; divine) The Avernal vortex churns across the portal tunnel, stopping the PCs from reaching their ultimate destination. In the first round, the vortex takes an action to attempt to dispel all beneficial spells on all creatures in the tunnel, scouring them with Hellfire. The vortex has a counteract modifier of [[/r 1d20+43 #Counteract]]{+43} and a counteract rank of 10, and rolls separately for each spell. On a success, the target creature takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:45|basic] save (this has the divine, fire, and unholy traits).

In the second round, the vortex attempts to instill a dread of tumbling forever through the tunnel to those within it; each creature takes @Damage[6d6[persistent,mental]] damage with a @Check[will|dc:45|basic] save (this is an emotion and mental effect). Once a creature ends its persistent mental damage (or if they avoided the persistent damage), they can reach and escape the tunnel.

In all subsequent rounds, the vortex releases bolts of acid, cold, fire, and electricity. It makes one vortex bolt Strike against each creature in the tunnel.

description.value:
Trigger A living creature enters the portal tunnel

Effect As the PCs begin to pierce planar boundaries, they feel they've caught the attention of the archdevil Barbatos, ruler of Avernus. Each creature in the tunnel must attempt a @Check[will|dc:45|traits:divine,emotion,fear,fire,mental,unholy] save. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 2} for 1 minute and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 3} for 1 minute and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}.
acid, cold, fire, or electricity; the damage is chosen randomly, and the Strike gains the appropriate trait; no multiple attack penalty

details.description:
Variant @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.revenge-of-the-runelords-bestiary.Actor.Megatsunami] and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.revenge-of-the-runelords-bestiary.Actor.Meteor Shower]

Anticipate @Check[astronomy-lore|dc:42] (master) or @Check[nature|dc:45] (legendary) to chart the meteor's trajectory, @Check[society|dc:50] (master) to recall information on Xin-Bakrakhan and how it was destroyed during Earthfall

Prepare @Check[ocean-lore|dc:42] (expert) or @Check[nature|dc:45] (master) or @Check[survival|dc:45] (master) to chart the flood paths of the coming waves, @Check[crafting|dc:50] (legendary) to reinforce a shelter
details.routine:
(5 minutes) Only the first five minutes of this disaster directly impact the PCs, but during that time, they must contend with the combination of a megatsunami and meteor shower.

Meteor Shower: As @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.revenge-of-the-runelords-bestiary.Actor.Meteor Shower], but @Damage[7d10[bludgeoning],15d6[fire]]{7d10 bludgeoning damage and 15d6 fire damage} to all characters (@Check[reflex|dc:42|basic] save). If at least half the PCs succeeded at Prepare checks, only one randomly determined PC is affected by the meteor shower rather than the whole party.

Tsunami: If at least half the PCs succeed on Prepare checks, they avoid all effects of the megatsunami until the last minute, at which point they're affected as if they were unprepared. Many of Xin-Bakrakhan's citizens are powerful wizards or otherwise have access to flight, be it by spell, wings, or flying minions, but their retreat into the skies above is chaotic. A PC capable of flight (or who is carried aloft by a flying ally) for an entire minute can avoid the tsunami's effects if they succeed at a @Check[acrobatics|dc:35] check to Maneuver in Flight to avoid mid-air collisions with others or falling meteors—on a failure they're forced back into the dangerous waters below and take a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against the tsunami's effects.

The tsunami floods into the lowest elevation area on the 2nd minute, then proceeds up into the higher regions as shown on the map on each of the following minutes. A PC in the area is affected as the standard megatsunami, but @Damage[(6d12+35)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:42|basic] save) when they're first struck and then @Damage[(4d12+25)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic]) each minute they're carried along by the wave.

stealth.details: (legendary) to notice that the wall undulates

details.description: The passage closes around intruders. Cavities open in its soft wood and try to envelope anything within it.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:50] (legendary) three times to scrape away @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] sensors on the floor and walls, or @Check[nature|dc:55] (legendary) to call upon lingering elements of primal energy in the Witchbole to render the trap inert

publication.title: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

details.reset: The trap deactivates as soon as a round begins with no living targets in area E4, and then resets at the start of the following round.
details.routine: (2 actions) The trap's first action on its turn is to heave and jolt, causing the entire length of area E4 to become difficult terrain until the start of the gorging passage's next turn. It then grinds at those within while simultaneously exuding acid from its walls. All creatures in area E4 take @Damage[4d10[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[8d6[acid]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:45|basic] save).

description.value:
Trigger A non-demon and non-fungus creature proceeds beyond the halfway point into the tunnel (indicated by the dotted line)

Effect The passage constricts tightly down. All creatures in area E4 take creatures take @Damage[(4d10+26)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:45|basic] save). The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: The magically and mechanically automated tools Sorshen used to aid in the crafting of perfume on the long, eastern workbench have interacted in unexpected ways, transforming the workshop into a dangerous peril.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:52] (legendary) to quickly introduce neutralizing agents into the alchemical machinery, @Check[thievery|dc:55] (master) to disable the mechanized portions of the devices, or @Check[arcana|dc:55] (master) to counteract magical elements; four total successes are required to disable the hazard (destroying one of the four workbenches counts as one success)
details.routine:
(5 actions) On its initiative, the malfunctioning perfumery takes the following five actions, in order. For each success at disabling it, the hazard loses one action. The targets for actions 2–5 are randomly chosen from creatures in the room, with no one creature being targeted more than once before all other creatures in the room have been targeted. For example, a party of 4 PCs will each be targeted by one of these actions. If just a single PC is in the room, they will be targeted by all four.

  • 1: Crushing Notes 1 (olfactory, poison) The malfunctioning machinery exudes a blast of concentrated and overwhelming perfume notes into area B11. Creatures in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:45|options:inflicts:stunned|traits:olfactory,poison] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 2} on a critical failure).

  • 2: Nozzle Spray 1 (poison) Nozzles built into the machinery extend out to spray toxic, overwhelmingly cloying perfume in multiple directions. One randomly determined target in area B11 takes @Damage[14d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:45|basic|traits:poison] save).

  • 3: Sillage 1 (emotion, mental) A larger, more intimidating nozzle than the one utilized in the previous action extends forward and violently sprays a staggering array of different scents at a random target in the room. The overwhelming mixture of lingering scent (the perfume's sillage) causes the creature to take @Damage[10d6[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:45|basic|options:inflicts:slowed|traits:emotion,mental] save); on a failed save, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} for 1 round by the overwhelming scents.

  • 4: Oil Spill 1 (fire) Sparks from grinding machinery ignite a patch of spilled oil somewhere in the room—a random creature in area B11 takes @Damage[10d6[fire],4d6[persistent,fire]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:45|basic|traits:fire] save).

  • 5: Boiling Beaker 1 (emotion, mental) A pressurized beaker containing boiling fluids springs a leak, and a dislodged tube sprays disorienting, psychotropic fumes at a random creature in the room, who must then succeed at a @Check[will|dc:44|options:inflicts:confused|traits:emotion,mental] save to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Confused] for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure).

description.value:
Trigger The door to area B11 is opened or a creature enters the area, or any open flame is brought into area B11

Effect The musky fog filling the perfume laboratory billows out into the hall, spreading 5 feet from the doorway and expanding by another 5 feet each round the door remains open. All creatures within the fog become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed], and all creatures outside the fog become concealed to creatures within it. Creatures in the fog must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:45|traits:poison|options:area-effect,inflicts:sickened] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Sickened]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure); this is a poison effect. If the pungent cloud was triggered by open flame, chemicals in the air ignite into a flash of fire, dealing @Damage[6d6[fire],6d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:45|basic|options:area-effect] save), after which the pungent cloud disperses. The hazard then rolls initiative.

details.description: The magically and mechanically automated tools Sorshen used to aid in the crafting of perfume on the long, eastern workbench have interacted in unexpected ways, transforming the workshop into a dangerous peril.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:52] (legendary) to quickly introduce neutralizing agents into the alchemical machinery, @Check[thievery|dc:55] (master) to disable the mechanized portions of the devices, or @Check[arcana|dc:55] (master) to counteract magical elements; four total successes are required to disable the hazard (destroying one of the four workbenches counts as one success)

The hazard then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: +45 for initiative; noticing her has a DC of 0

details.description: Queen Sorshen is a beautiful Thassilonian woman wielding a shimmering guisarme. Her violet eyes are fierce and focused, but the corner of her lips twist up in a small, lopsided smile.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:55] to unravel the energies allowing Sorshen to continue to vex Xanderghul; 4 successes are required to disable the hazard.
details.routine:
(3 actions) On her initiative each round, Sorshen can spend her three actions on any of the following options, following PC suggestions if requested. She cannot use any action from the following list more than once per round.

Aid Ally 1 Sorshen uses her magic to empower one of the PCs, granting that PC a +4 circumstance bonus to attack rolls until the start of her next turn. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Queen Sorshen's Aid (Aid Ally)]

Dispel Pride 1 Sorshen attempts to counteract a magic effect put in place by Xanderghul, with a counteract rank of 9th and a [[/gmr 1d20+40 #Counteract]]{+40} modifier for the roll.

Distracting Presence 1 Sorshen uses her power to distract Xanderghul, causing him to lose his resistance to mental damage until the start of her next turn and also to attempt a @Check[will|dc:50] save with the following results.

Critical Success Xanderghul takes no further penalty, and is temporarily immune to Distracting Presence for 24 hours.

Success Xanderghul is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Off-Guard] until the start of his turn.

Failure Xanderghul is off-guard until the start of Sorshen's next turn.

Critical Failure As failure, but Xanderghul is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Slowed]{Slowed 1} as long as he remains off-guard.

Indulgent Arcana 1 Sorshen empowers a PC's magic, so that the next spell with the mental trait they cast before Sorshen's next turn increases its save DC and attack roll by 2 and removes the spell's incapacitation trait, if any. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Effect: Queen Sorshen's Aid (Indulgent Arcana)]

Sinuous Strike 1 Sorshen swings her guisarme, usurping some of Xanderghul's own power to manifest an illusory duplicate that attempts a guisarme Strike against him.

description.value:
Trigger Xanderghul rolls initiative

Effect Sorshen casts telepathy, allowing her to communicate with the PCs; she can inform them of her options during her routine and follows their advice and requests if given. She then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to notice the buildup of divine energy and anticipate its discharge.

details.description: Seldeg's ritual nears its conclusion, causing magical energy to arc erratically between the ritual circles.
details.disable: @Check[arazni-lore|dc:44] or @Check[iomedae-lore|dc:44] to recount a tale from history that grounds that deity in the current reality, @Check[crafting|dc:50] (legendary) to create a device to siphon energy from the ritual circle, or @Check[religion|dc:46] (legendary) to discharge the escalating divine energy; six successes (three successes per circle) are required to disarm the hazard.
details.reset: While atop the floating platform, Seldeg Bhedlis can use a 3-action activity with the auditory trait to reenergize the ritual. Doing so removes one success to disable the hazard and immediately triggers the hazard's Reorder Reality reaction.
details.routine:
(1 action; divine) The divine energy gathered by the ritual spikes. One of the eleven stained-glass windows (each depicting one of her Eleven Acts) transforms to feature Arazni instead. Any creature that can see the window change must attempt a @Check[will|dc:50] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is immune to this effect for 10 minutes.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[(3d12+20)[mental]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[(6d12+40)[mental]] damage. If the creatures' next d20 roll is a critical success, they earn a critical failure instead. This is a misfortune effect. If all 11 windows transform, Seldeg's ritual finishes ahead of schedule. The stained-glass windows shatter, dealing @Damage[(8d10+52)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:50] save) to each creature in the room. The hazard then becomes inactive.

description.value:
Trigger A creature other than Seldeg enters the area contained by the ritual circles

Effect Arcs of divine energy travel between the two circles. Each creature within the circles takes @Damage[(4d10+26)[spirit]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:45|basic] save). If one of the ritual circles has been disabled, the hazard instead affects all creature in the room, but each creature gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their save. The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: A roiling red orb, forged from a drop of the god Rovagug's blood, rains fire from the sky when a specified condition is met.
details.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48|name:Imbue Thieves' Tools|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to imbue a thieves' toolkit with aspects representing Asmodeus and Sarenrae and use them to drain away the orb's power over 10 minutes; the character attempting this check takes @Damage[5[fire]] damage each round until the orb is depleted

description.value:
Trigger A special condition set by the trap's creator occurs, typically the event of their death.

Effect Fire rains from the sky in a 100-mile radius, dealing @Damage[10d6[fire]] damage to creatures and objects in the area. Each creature or object can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:46|basic] save. Any creature reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage dies instantly. This is not enough damage to completely burn away a forest or level an entire mountain or city, but it typically kills most creatures in the area.
